name: isCancelAdd_iff
statement: theorem isCancelAdd_iff  : IsCancelAdd αᵐᵒᵖ ↔ IsCancelAdd α
proof:
simp_rw [isCancelAdd_iff, isLeftCancelAdd_iff, isRightCancelAdd_iff]

@[to_additive] instance instSMul [SMul α β] : SMul α βᵐᵒᵖ where smul c x := op (c • unop x)

@[simp] lemma op_zero [Zero α] : op (0 : α) = 0 := rfl

@[simp] lemma unop_zero [Zero α] : unop (0 : αᵐᵒᵖ) = 0 := rfl

@[to_additive (attr := simp)] lemma op_one [One α] : op (1 : α) = 1 := rfl

@[to_additive (attr := simp)] lemma unop_one [One α] : unop (1 : αᵐᵒᵖ) = 1 := rfl

@[simp] lemma op_add [Add α] (x y : α) : op (x + y) = op x + op y := rfl

@[simp] lemma unop_add [Add α] (x y : αᵐᵒᵖ) : unop (x + y) = unop x + unop y := rfl

@[simp] lemma op_neg [Neg α] (x : α) : op (-x) = -op x := rfl

@[simp] lemma unop_neg [Neg α] (x : αᵐᵒᵖ) : unop (-x) = -unop x := rfl

@[to_additive (attr := simp)] lemma op_mul [Mul α] (x y : α) : op (x * y) = op y * op x := rfl

@[to_additive (attr := simp)]

name: not_isPrimePow_zero
statement: theorem not_isPrimePow_zero  : ¬IsPrimePow (0 : R)
proof:
simp only [isPrimePow_def, not_exists, not_and', and_imp]
  intro x n _hn hx
  rw [eq_zero_of_pow_eq_zero hx]
  simp

name: isPrimePow_nat_iff
statement: theorem isPrimePow_nat_iff (n : ℕ) : IsPrimePow n ↔ ∃ p k : ℕ, Nat.Prime p ∧ 0 < k ∧ p ^ k = n
proof: simp only [isPrimePow_def, Nat.prime_iff]

name: isPrimePow_nat_iff_bounded
statement: theorem isPrimePow_nat_iff_bounded (n : ℕ) : IsPrimePow n ↔ ∃ p : ℕ, p ≤ n ∧ ∃ k : ℕ, k ≤ n ∧ p.Prime ∧ 0 < k ∧ p ^ k = n
proof:
rw [isPrimePow_nat_iff]
  refine Iff.symm ⟨fun ⟨p, _, k, _, hp, hk, hn⟩ => ⟨p, k, hp, hk, hn⟩, ?_⟩
  rintro ⟨p, k, hp, hk, rfl⟩
  refine ⟨p, ?_, k, (Nat.lt_pow_self hp.one_lt).le, hp, hk, rfl⟩
  conv => {lhs; rw [← (pow_one p)]}
  exact Nat.pow_le_pow_right hp.one_lt.le hk

name: eqvGen_rel_eq
statement: theorem eqvGen_rel_eq (r : R → R → Prop) : Relation.EqvGen (Rel r) = RingConGen.Rel r
proof:
ext x₁ x₂
  constructor
  · intro h
    induction h with
    | rel _ _ h => induction h with
      | of => exact RingConGen.Rel.of _ _ ‹_›
      | add_left _ h => exact h.add (RingConGen.Rel.refl _)
      | mul_left _ h => exact h.mul (RingConGen.Rel.refl _)
      | mul_right _ h => exact (RingConGen.Rel.refl _).mul h
    | refl => exact RingConGen.Rel.refl _
    | symm => exact RingConGen.Rel.symm ‹_›
    | trans => exact RingConGen.Rel.trans ‹_› ‹_›
  · intro h
    induction h with
    | of => exact Relation.EqvGen.rel _ _ (Rel.of ‹_›)
    | refl => exact (RingQuot.ringCon r).refl _
    | symm => exact (RingQuot.ringCon r).symm ‹_›
    | trans => exact (RingQuot.ringCon r).trans ‹_› ‹_›
    | add => exact (RingQuot.ringCon r).add ‹_› ‹_›
    | mul => exact (RingQuot.ringCon r).mul ‹_› ‹_›

end RingQuot

/-- The quotient of a ring by an arbitrary relation. -/

name: mkRingHom_surjective
statement: theorem mkRingHom_surjective (r : R → R → Prop) : Function.Surjective (mkRingHom r)
proof:
simp only [mkRingHom_def, RingHom.coe_mk, MonoidHom.coe_mk, OneHom.coe_mk]
  rintro ⟨⟨⟩⟩
  simp

@[ext 1100]

name: mkAlgHom_coe
statement: theorem mkAlgHom_coe (s : A → A → Prop) : (mkAlgHom S s : A →+* RingQuot s) = mkRingHom s
proof:
simp_rw [mkAlgHom_def, mkRingHom_def]
  rfl

name: mkAlgHom_surjective
statement: theorem mkAlgHom_surjective (s : A → A → Prop) : Function.Surjective (mkAlgHom S s)
proof:
suffices Function.Surjective fun x ↦ (⟨.mk (Rel s) x⟩ : RingQuot s) by
    simpa [mkAlgHom_def, mkRingHom_def]
  rintro ⟨⟨a⟩⟩
  use a

variable {B : Type u₄} [Semiring B] [Algebra S B]

@[ext 1100]

name: is_sol_mkSol
statement: theorem is_sol_mkSol (init : Fin E.order → R) : E.IsSolution (E.mkSol init)
proof:
intro n
  rw [mkSol]
  simp

/-- `E.mkSol init`'s first `E.order` terms are `init`. -/

name: mkSol_eq_init
statement: theorem mkSol_eq_init (init : Fin E.order → R) : ∀ n : Fin E.order, E.mkSol init n = init n
proof:
intro n
  rw [mkSol]
  simp only [n.is_lt, dif_pos, Fin.mk_val]

/-- If `u` is a solution to `E` and `init` designates its first `E.order` values,
  then `∀ n, u n = E.mkSol init n`. -/

name: solSpace_rank
statement: theorem solSpace_rank  : Module.rank R E.solSpace = E.order
proof:
simp [rank_eq_card_basis E.basis]

end StrongRankCondition

section CommRing

variable {R : Type*} [CommRing R] (E : LinearRecurrence R)

/-- The characteristic polynomial of `E` is
`X ^ E.order - ∑ i : Fin E.order, (E.coeffs i) * X ^ i`. -/

name: charPoly_degree_eq_order
statement: theorem charPoly_degree_eq_order  : (charPoly E).degree = E.order
proof:
rw [charPoly, degree_sub_eq_left_of_degree_lt]
    <;> rw [degree_monomial E.order one_ne_zero]
  simp_rw [← C_mul_X_pow_eq_monomial]
  exact degree_sum_fin_lt E.coeffs

name: charPoly_monic
statement: theorem charPoly_monic  : charPoly E |>.Monic
proof:
nontriviality R
  rw [Monic, leadingCoeff, natDegree_eq_of_degree_eq_some <| charPoly_degree_eq_order _, charPoly,
    coeff_sub, coeff_monomial_same, finsetSum_coeff, sub_eq_self]
  refine sum_eq_zero fun _ _ ↦ coeff_eq_zero_of_degree_lt ?_
  grw [degree_monomial_le]
  simp

/-- The geometric sequence `q^n` is a solution of `E` iff
  `q` is a root of `E`'s characteristic polynomial. -/

name: geom_sol_iff_root_charPoly
statement: theorem geom_sol_iff_root_charPoly (q : R) : (E.IsSolution fun n ↦ q ^ n) ↔ E.charPoly.IsRoot q
proof:
rw [charPoly, Polynomial.IsRoot.def, Polynomial.eval]
  simp only [Polynomial.eval₂_finsetSum, one_mul, RingHom.id_apply, Polynomial.eval₂_monomial,
    Polynomial.eval₂_sub]
  constructor
  · intro h
    simpa [sub_eq_zero] using h 0
  · intro h n
    simp only [pow_add, sub_eq_zero.mp h, mul_sum]
    exact sum_congr rfl fun _ _ ↦ by ring

end CommRing

end LinearRecurrence

name: discrim_eq_zero_iff
statement: theorem discrim_eq_zero_iff (ha : a ≠ 0) : discrim a b c = 0 ↔ (∃! x, a * (x * x) + b * x + c = 0)
proof:
refine ⟨fun hd => ?_, discrim_eq_zero_of_existsUnique ha⟩
  simp_rw [quadratic_eq_zero_iff_of_discrim_eq_zero ha hd, existsUnique_eq]

end Field

section LinearOrderedField

variable {K : Type*} [Field K] [LinearOrder K] [IsStrictOrderedRing K] {a b c : K}

/-- If a polynomial of degree 2 is always nonnegative, then its discriminant is nonpositive -/

name: cardinalMk_lift_le_mul
statement: theorem cardinalMk_lift_le_mul  : Cardinal.lift.{u} #{ x : A // IsAlgebraic R x } ≤ Cardinal.lift.{v} #R[X] * ℵ₀
proof:
rw [← mk_uLift, ← mk_uLift]
  choose g hg₁ hg₂ using fun x : { x : A | IsAlgebraic R x } => x.coe_prop
  refine lift_mk_le_lift_mk_mul_of_lift_mk_preimage_le g fun f => ?_
  rw [lift_le_aleph0, le_aleph0_iff_set_countable]
  suffices MapsTo (↑) (g ⁻¹' {f}) (f.rootSet A) from
    this.countable_of_injOn Subtype.coe_injective.injOn (f.rootSet_finite A).countable
  rintro x (rfl : g x = f)
  exact mem_rootSet.2 ⟨hg₁ x, hg₂ x⟩

name: countable
statement: theorem countable  : Set.Countable { x : A | IsAlgebraic R x }
proof:
rw [← le_aleph0_iff_set_countable, ← lift_le_aleph0]
  apply (cardinalMk_lift_le_max R A).trans
  simp

@[simp]

name: cardinalMk_le_mul
statement: theorem cardinalMk_le_mul  : #{ x : A // IsAlgebraic R x } ≤ #R[X] * ℵ₀
proof:
rw [← lift_id #_, ← lift_id #R[X]]
  exact cardinalMk_lift_le_mul R A

@[stacks 09GK]

name: cardinalMk_le_max
statement: theorem cardinalMk_le_max  : #{ x : A // IsAlgebraic R x } ≤ max #R ℵ₀
proof:
rw [← lift_id #_, ← lift_id #R]
  exact cardinalMk_lift_le_max R A

@[simp]

name: act_act_self_eq
statement: theorem act_act_self_eq (x y : S) : (x ◃ y) ◃ x = x ◃ y
proof:
have h : (x ◃ y) ◃ x = (x ◃ y) ◃ (x ◃ 1) := by rw [act_one]
  rw [h, ← Shelf.self_distrib, act_one]

name: act_self_act_eq
statement: theorem act_self_act_eq (x y : S) : x ◃ (x ◃ y) = x ◃ y
proof:
have h : x ◃ (x ◃ y) = (x ◃ 1) ◃ (x ◃ y) := by rw [act_one]
  rw [h, ← Shelf.self_distrib, one_act]

/--
The associativity of a unital shelf comes for free.
-/

name: assoc
statement: theorem assoc (x y z : S) : (x ◃ y) ◃ z = x ◃ y ◃ z
proof:
rw [self_distrib, self_distrib, act_act_self_eq, act_self_act_eq]

end UnitalShelf

namespace Rack

variable {R : Type*} [Rack R]

export Shelf (self_distrib)

/-- A rack acts on itself by equivalences. -/

name: exact_subtype_mkQ
statement: theorem exact_subtype_mkQ (Q : Submodule R N) : Exact (Submodule.subtype Q) (Submodule.mkQ Q)
proof: rw [exact_iff, Submodule.ker_mkQ, Submodule.range_subtype Q]

name: ker_eq_bot_range_liftQ_iff
statement: theorem ker_eq_bot_range_liftQ_iff (h : range f ≤ ker g) : ker ((range f).liftQ g h) = ⊥ ↔ ker g = range f
proof:
simp only [Submodule.ext_iff, mem_ker, Submodule.mem_bot, mem_range]
  constructor
  · intro hfg x
    simpa using hfg (Submodule.Quotient.mk x)
  · intro hfg x
    obtain ⟨x, rfl⟩ := Submodule.Quotient.mk_surjective _ x
    simpa using hfg x

name: injective_range_liftQ_of_exact
statement: theorem injective_range_liftQ_of_exact (h : Function.Exact f g) : Function.Injective ((range f).liftQ g (h · |>.mpr))
proof:
simpa only [← LinearMap.ker_eq_bot, ker_eq_bot_range_liftQ_iff, exact_iff] using h

end LinearMap

/-- The linear equivalence `(N ⧸ LinearMap.range f) ≃ₗ[A] P` associated to
an exact sequence `M → N → P → 0` of `R`-modules. -/
@[simps! apply]
noncomputable def Function.Exact.linearEquivOfSurjective (h : Function.Exact f g)
    (hg : Function.Surjective g) : (N ⧸ LinearMap.range f) ≃ₗ[R] P :=
  LinearEquiv.ofBijective ((LinearMap.range f).liftQ g (h · |>.mpr))
    ⟨LinearMap.injective_range_liftQ_of_exact h, LinearMap.surjective_range_liftQ _ hg⟩

@[simp]

name: eps_pow_two
statement: theorem eps_pow_two  : (ε : R[ε]) ^ 2 = 0
proof:
simp [pow_two]

@[simp]

name: range_inlAlgHom_sup_adjoin_eps
statement: theorem range_inlAlgHom_sup_adjoin_eps  : (inlAlgHom R A A).range ⊔ Algebra.adjoin R {ε} = ⊤
proof:
refine top_unique fun x hx => ?_; clear hx
  rw [← x.inl_fst_add_inr_snd_eq, inr_eq_smul_eps, ← inl_mul_eq_smul]
  refine add_mem ?_ (mul_mem ?_ ?_)
  · exact le_sup_left (α := Subalgebra R _) <| Set.mem_range_self x.fst
  · exact le_sup_left (α := Subalgebra R _) <| Set.mem_range_self x.snd
  · refine le_sup_right (α := Subalgebra R _) <| subset_adjoin <| Set.mem_singleton ε

@[simp]

name: rank_eq_four
statement: theorem rank_eq_four  : Module.rank R ℍ[R,c₁,c₂,c₃] = 4
proof:
rw [rank_eq_card_basis (basisOneIJK c₁ c₂ c₃), Fintype.card_fin]
  norm_num

name: finrank_eq_four
statement: theorem finrank_eq_four  : Module.finrank R ℍ[R,c₁,c₂,c₃] = 4
proof:
rw [Module.finrank, rank_eq_four, Cardinal.toNat_ofNat]

/-- There is a natural equivalence when swapping the first and third coefficients of a
  quaternion algebra if `c₂` is 0. -/
@[simps]

name: mul_star_eq_coe
statement: theorem mul_star_eq_coe  : a * star a = (a * star a).re
proof:
rw [← star_comm_self']
  exact a.star_mul_eq_coe

open MulOpposite

/-- Quaternion conjugate as an `AlgEquiv` to the opposite ring. -/

name: re_star
statement: theorem re_star  : (star a).re = a.re
proof:
rw [QuaternionAlgebra.re_star, zero_mul, add_zero]

@[simp] theorem imI_star : (star a).imI = -a.imI := rfl

@[simp] theorem imJ_star : (star a).imJ = -a.imJ := rfl

@[simp] theorem imK_star : (star a).imK = -a.imK := rfl

@[simp] theorem im_star : (star a).im = -a.im := QuaternionAlgebra.im_star a

name: self_add_star
statement: theorem self_add_star  : a + star a = 2 * a.re
proof: simpa using QuaternionAlgebra.self_add_star a

name: star_add_self
statement: theorem star_add_self  : star a + a = 2 * a.re
proof: simpa using QuaternionAlgebra.star_add_self a

name: star_eq_two_re_sub
statement: theorem star_eq_two_re_sub  : star a = ↑(2 * a.re) - a
proof:
simpa using QuaternionAlgebra.star_eq_two_re_sub a

@[simp, norm_cast]

name: normSq_coe
statement: theorem normSq_coe  : normSq (x : ℍ[R]) = x ^ 2
proof:
rw [normSq_def, star_coe, ← coe_mul, re_coe, sq]

@[simp]

name: normSq_natCast
statement: theorem normSq_natCast (n : ℕ) : normSq (n : ℍ[R]) = (n : R) ^ 2
proof:
rw [← coe_natCast, normSq_coe]

@[norm_cast]

name: normSq_intCast
statement: theorem normSq_intCast (z : ℤ) : normSq (z : ℍ[R]) = (z : R) ^ 2
proof:
rw [← coe_intCast, normSq_coe]

@[simp]

name: im_sq
statement: theorem im_sq  : a.im ^ 2 = -normSq a.im
proof: simp_rw [sq, ← star_mul_self, star_im, neg_mul, neg_neg]

name: coe_normSq_add
statement: theorem coe_normSq_add  : normSq (a + b) = normSq a + a * star b + b * star a + normSq b
proof: simp only [star_add, ← self_mul_star, mul_add, add_mul, add_assoc, add_left_comm]

name: normSq_eq_zero
statement: theorem normSq_eq_zero  : normSq a = 0 ↔ a = 0
proof:
refine ⟨fun h => ?_, fun h => h.symm ▸ normSq.map_zero⟩
  rw [normSq_def', add_eq_zero_iff_of_nonneg, add_eq_zero_iff_of_nonneg, add_eq_zero_iff_of_nonneg]
    at h
  · apply ext a 0 <;> apply eq_zero_of_pow_eq_zero
    exacts [h.1.1.1, h.1.1.2, h.1.2, h.2]
  all_goals apply_rules [sq_nonneg, add_nonneg]

name: normSq_nonneg
statement: theorem normSq_nonneg  : 0 ≤ normSq a
proof:
rw [normSq_def']
  apply_rules [sq_nonneg, add_nonneg]

@[simp]

name: sq_eq_normSq
statement: theorem sq_eq_normSq  : a ^ 2 = normSq a ↔ a = a.re
proof:
rw [← star_eq_self, ← star_mul_self, sq, mul_eq_mul_right_iff, eq_comm]
  exact or_iff_left_of_imp fun ha ↦ ha.symm ▸ star_zero _

name: sq_eq_neg_normSq
statement: theorem sq_eq_neg_normSq  : a ^ 2 = -normSq a ↔ a.re = 0
proof:
simp_rw [← star_eq_neg]
  obtain rfl | hq0 := eq_or_ne a 0
  · simp
  · rw [← star_mul_self, ← mul_neg, ← neg_sq, sq, mul_left_inj' (neg_ne_zero.mpr hq0), eq_comm]

end LinearOrderedCommRing

section Field

variable [Field R] (a b : ℍ[R])

instance instNNRatCast : NNRatCast ℍ[R] where nnratCast q := (q : R)
instance instRatCast : RatCast ℍ[R] where ratCast q := (q : R)

@[simp, norm_cast] lemma re_nnratCast (q : ℚ≥0) : (q : ℍ[R]).re = q := rfl
@[simp, norm_cast] lemma im_nnratCast (q : ℚ≥0) : (q : ℍ[R]).im = 0 := rfl
@[simp, norm_cast] lemma imI_nnratCast (q : ℚ≥0) : (q : ℍ[R]).imI = 0 := rfl
@[simp, norm_cast] lemma imJ_nnratCast (q : ℚ≥0) : (q : ℍ[R]).imJ = 0 := rfl
@[simp, norm_cast] lemma imK_nnratCast (q : ℚ≥0) : (q : ℍ[R]).imK = 0 := rfl
@[simp, norm_cast] lemma re_ratCast (q : ℚ) : (q : ℍ[R]).re = q := rfl
@[simp, norm_cast] lemma im_ratCast (q : ℚ) : (q : ℍ[R]).im = 0 := rfl
@[simp, norm_cast] lemma imI_ratCast (q : ℚ) : (q : ℍ[R]).imI = 0 := rfl
@[simp, norm_cast] lemma imJ_ratCast (q : ℚ) : (q : ℍ[R]).imJ = 0 := rfl
@[simp, norm_cast] lemma imK_ratCast (q : ℚ) : (q : ℍ[R]).imK = 0 := rfl

@[norm_cast] lemma coe_nnratCast (q : ℚ≥0) : ↑(q : R) = (q : ℍ[R]) := rfl

@[norm_cast] lemma coe_ratCast (q : ℚ) : ↑(q : R) = (q : ℍ[R]) := rfl

variable [LinearOrder R] [IsStrictOrderedRing R] (a b : ℍ[R])

@[simps -isSimp]
instance instInv : Inv ℍ[R] :=
  ⟨fun a => (normSq a)⁻¹ • star a⟩

instance instGroupWithZero : GroupWithZero ℍ[R] :=
  { Quaternion.instNontrivial with
    inv_zero := by rw [inv_def, star_zero, smul_zero]
    mul_inv_cancel := fun a ha => by
      rw [inv_def, Algebra.mul_smul_comm (normSq a)⁻¹ a (star a), self_mul_star, smul_coe,
        inv_mul_cancel₀ (normSq_ne_zero.2 ha), coe_one] }

@[norm_cast, simp]

name: normSq_ratCast
statement: theorem normSq_ratCast (q : ℚ) : normSq (q : ℍ[R]) = (q : ℍ[R]) ^ 2
proof:
rw [← coe_ratCast, normSq_coe, coe_pow]

end Field

end Quaternion

namespace Cardinal

open Quaternion

section QuaternionAlgebra

variable {R : Type*} (c₁ c₂ c₃ : R)

private theorem pow_four [Infinite R] : #R ^ 4 = #R :=
  power_nat_eq (aleph0_le_mk R) <| by decide

/-- The cardinality of a quaternion algebra, as a type. -/

name: mk_quaternionAlgebra
statement: theorem mk_quaternionAlgebra  : #(ℍ[R,c₁,c₂,c₃]) = #R ^ 4
proof:
rw [mk_congr (QuaternionAlgebra.equivProd c₁ c₂ c₃)]
  simp only [mk_prod, lift_id]
  ring

@[simp]

name: mk_quaternionAlgebra_of_infinite
statement: theorem mk_quaternionAlgebra_of_infinite  : #(ℍ[R,c₁,c₂,c₃]) = #R
proof:
rw [mk_quaternionAlgebra, pow_four]

/-- The cardinality of a quaternion algebra, as a set. -/

name: mk_univ_quaternionAlgebra
statement: theorem mk_univ_quaternionAlgebra  : #(Set.univ : Set ℍ[R,c₁,c₂,c₃]) = #R ^ 4
proof: rw [mk_univ, mk_quaternionAlgebra]

name: liftAux_eq
statement: theorem liftAux_eq (f : X → A) : liftAux R f = lift R f
proof:
rw [lift]
  rfl

@[simp]

name: lift_symm_apply
statement: theorem lift_symm_apply (F : FreeAlgebra R X →ₐ[R] A) : (lift R).symm F = F ∘ ι R
proof:
rw [lift]
  rfl

variable {R}

@[simp]

name: ι_comp_lift
statement: theorem ι_comp_lift (f : X → A) : (lift R f : FreeAlgebra R X → A) ∘ ι R = f
proof:
ext
  rw [Function.comp_apply, ι_def, lift]
  rfl

@[simp]

name: lift_comp_ι
statement: theorem lift_comp_ι (g : FreeAlgebra R X →ₐ[R] A) : lift R ((g : FreeAlgebra R X → A) ∘ ι R) = g
proof:
rw [← lift_symm_apply]
  exact (lift R).apply_symm_apply g

/-- See note [partially-applied ext lemmas]. -/
@[ext high]

name: adjoin_range_ι
statement: theorem adjoin_range_ι  : Algebra.adjoin R (Set.range (ι R : X → FreeAlgebra R X)) = ⊤
proof:
set S := Algebra.adjoin R (Set.range (ι R : X → FreeAlgebra R X))
  refine top_unique fun x hx => ?_; clear hx
  induction x with
  | grade0 => exact S.algebraMap_mem _
  | add x y hx hy => exact S.add_mem hx hy
  | mul x y hx hy => exact S.mul_mem hx hy
  | grade1 x => exact Algebra.subset_adjoin (Set.mem_range_self _)

variable {A : Type*} [Semiring A] [Algebra R A]

/-- Noncommutative version of `Algebra.adjoin_range_eq_range_aeval`. -/

name: i_mul_k
statement: theorem i_mul_k  : q.i * q.k = c₁ • q.j + c₂ • q.k
proof:
rw [← i_mul_j, ← mul_assoc, i_mul_i, add_mul, smul_mul_assoc, one_mul, smul_mul_assoc]

@[simp]

name: k_mul_i
statement: theorem k_mul_i  : q.k * q.i = -c₁ • q.j
proof:
rw [← i_mul_j, mul_assoc, j_mul_i, mul_sub, i_mul_k, neg_smul, mul_smul_comm, i_mul_j]
  linear_combination (norm := module)

@[simp]

name: k_mul_j
statement: theorem k_mul_j  : q.k * q.j = c₃ • q.i
proof:
rw [← i_mul_j, mul_assoc, j_mul_j, mul_smul_comm, mul_one]

@[simp]

name: j_mul_k
statement: theorem j_mul_k  : q.j * q.k = (c₂ * c₃) • 1 - c₃ • q.i
proof:
rw [← i_mul_j, ← mul_assoc, j_mul_i, sub_mul, smul_mul_assoc, j_mul_j, ← smul_assoc, k_mul_j]
  rfl

@[simp]

name: k_mul_k
statement: theorem k_mul_k  : q.k * q.k = -((c₁ * c₃) • (1 : A))
proof:
rw [← i_mul_j, mul_assoc, ← mul_assoc q.j _ _, j_mul_i, ← i_mul_j, ← mul_assoc, mul_sub, ←
    mul_assoc, i_mul_i, add_mul, smul_mul_assoc, one_mul, sub_mul, smul_mul_assoc, mul_smul_comm,
    smul_mul_assoc, mul_assoc, j_mul_j, add_mul, smul_mul_assoc, j_mul_j, smul_smul,
    smul_mul_assoc, mul_assoc, j_mul_j]
  linear_combination (norm := module)


/-- Intermediate result used to define `QuaternionAlgebra.Basis.liftHom`. -/

name: lift_add
statement: theorem lift_add (x y : ℍ[R,c₁,c₂,c₃]) : q.lift (x + y) = q.lift x + q.lift y
proof:
simp only [lift, re_add, map_add, imI_add, add_smul, imJ_add, imK_add]
  abel

name: lift_mul
statement: theorem lift_mul (x y : ℍ[R,c₁,c₂,c₃]) : q.lift (x * y) = q.lift x * q.lift y
proof:
simp only [lift, Algebra.algebraMap_eq_smul_one]
  simp_rw [add_mul, mul_add, smul_mul_assoc, mul_smul_comm, one_mul, mul_one, smul_smul]
  simp only [i_mul_i, j_mul_j, i_mul_j, j_mul_i, i_mul_k, k_mul_i, k_mul_j, j_mul_k, k_mul_k]
  simp only [smul_smul, smul_neg, sub_eq_add_neg, ← add_assoc, neg_smul]
  simp only [mul_right_comm _ _ (c₁ * c₃), mul_comm _ (c₁ * c₃)]
  simp only [mul_comm _ c₁]
  simp only [mul_right_comm _ _ c₃]
  simp only [← mul_assoc]
  simp only [re_mul, sub_eq_add_neg, add_smul, neg_smul, imI_mul, ← add_assoc, imJ_mul, imK_mul]
  linear_combination (norm := module)

name: range_liftHom
statement: theorem range_liftHom (B : Basis A c₁ c₂ c₃) : (liftHom B).range = Algebra.adjoin R {B.i, B.j, B.k}
proof:
apply le_antisymm
  · rintro x ⟨y, rfl⟩
    refine add_mem (add_mem (add_mem ?_ ?_) ?_) ?_
    · exact algebraMap_mem _ _
    all_goals
      exact Subalgebra.smul_mem _ (Algebra.subset_adjoin <| by simp) _
  · rw [Algebra.adjoin_le_iff]
    rintro x (rfl | rfl | rfl)
      <;> [use (Basis.self R).i; use (Basis.self R).j; use (Basis.self R).k]
    all_goals simp [lift]

/-- Transform a `QuaternionAlgebra.Basis` through an `AlgHom`. -/
@[simps i j k]

name: length_mul
statement: theorem length_mul (x y : FreeSemigroup α) : (x * y).length = x.length + y.length
proof:
simp [length, Nat.add_right_comm]

@[to_additive (attr := simp)]

name: of_a_eq_zero
statement: theorem of_a_eq_zero (ha : P.a = 0) : P.toPoly = C P.b * X ^ 2 + C P.c * X + C P.d
proof: rw [toPoly, ha, C_0, zero_mul, zero_add]

name: toPoly_eq_zero_iff
statement: theorem toPoly_eq_zero_iff (P : Cubic R) : P.toPoly = 0 ↔ P = 0
proof:
rw [← zero, toPoly_injective]

private theorem ne_zero (h0 : P.a ≠ 0 ∨ P.b ≠ 0 ∨ P.c ≠ 0 ∨ P.d ≠ 0) : P.toPoly ≠ 0 := by
  contrapose! h0
  rw [(toPoly_eq_zero_iff P).mp h0]
  exact ⟨rfl, rfl, rfl, rfl⟩

name: monic_of_a_eq_one
statement: theorem monic_of_a_eq_one (ha : P.a = 1) : P.toPoly.Monic
proof:
nontriviality R
  rw [Monic, leadingCoeff_of_a_ne_zero (ha ▸ one_ne_zero), ha]

name: degree_of_a_eq_zero
statement: theorem degree_of_a_eq_zero (ha : P.a = 0) : P.toPoly.degree ≤ 2
proof: simpa only [of_a_eq_zero ha] using degree_quadratic_le

name: natDegree_of_a_eq_zero
statement: theorem natDegree_of_a_eq_zero (ha : P.a = 0) : P.toPoly.natDegree ≤ 2
proof: simpa only [of_a_eq_zero ha] using natDegree_quadratic_le

name: map_toPoly
statement: theorem map_toPoly  : (map φ P).toPoly = Polynomial.map φ P.toPoly
proof:
simp only [map, toPoly, map_C, map_X, Polynomial.map_add, Polynomial.map_mul, Polynomial.map_pow]

end Map

end Basic

section Roots

open Multiset

/-! ### Roots over an extension -/


section Extension

variable {P : Cubic R} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] {φ : R →+* S}

/-- The roots of a cubic polynomial. -/

name: map_roots
statement: theorem map_roots  : (map φ P).roots = (Polynomial.map φ P.toPoly).roots
proof: rw [roots, map_toPoly]

name: splits_iff_card_roots
statement: theorem splits_iff_card_roots (ha : P.a ≠ 0) : Splits (P.toPoly.map φ) ↔ (map φ P).roots.card = 3
proof:
replace ha : (map φ P).a ≠ 0 := (map_ne_zero φ).mpr ha
  rw [roots, ← map_toPoly, Polynomial.splits_iff_card_roots,
    ← ((degree_eq_iff_natDegree_eq <| ne_zero_of_a_ne_zero ha).1 <| degree_of_a_ne_zero ha : _ = 3)]

name: splits_iff_roots_eq_three
statement: theorem splits_iff_roots_eq_three (ha : P.a ≠ 0) : Splits (P.toPoly.map φ) ↔ ∃ x y z : K, (map φ P).roots = {x, y, z}
proof: rw [splits_iff_card_roots ha, card_eq_three]

name: toFinsupp_toFreeAbelianGroup
statement: theorem toFinsupp_toFreeAbelianGroup (f : X →₀ ℤ) : FreeAbelianGroup.toFinsupp (Finsupp.toFreeAbelianGroup f) = f
proof:
rw [← AddMonoidHom.comp_apply, toFinsupp_comp_toFreeAbelianGroup, AddMonoidHom.id_apply]

variable (X)

/-- The additive equivalence between `FreeAbelianGroup X` and `(X →₀ ℤ)`. -/
@[simps!]

name: support_zero
statement: theorem support_zero  : support (0 : FreeAbelianGroup X) = ∅
proof:
simp only [support, Finsupp.support_zero, map_zero]

@[simp]

name: support_of
statement: theorem support_of (x : X) : support (of x) = {x}
proof:
rw [support, toFinsupp_of, Finsupp.support_single _ one_ne_zero]

@[simp]

name: support_neg
statement: theorem support_neg (a : FreeAbelianGroup X) : support (-a) = support a
proof:
simp only [support, map_neg, Finsupp.support_neg]

@[simp]

name: support_add
statement: theorem support_add (a b : FreeAbelianGroup X) : support (a + b) ⊆ a.support ∪ b.support
proof:
simp only [support, map_add]
  apply Finsupp.support_add

end FreeAbelianGroup

name: mulSupport_eq_iff
statement: theorem mulSupport_eq_iff  : mulSupport f = s ↔ (∀ x, x ∈ s → f x ≠ 1) ∧ ∀ x, x ∉ s → f x = 1
proof:
simp +contextual only [Set.ext_iff, mem_mulSupport, ne_eq, iff_def,
    not_imp_comm, and_comm, forall_and]

@[to_additive]

name: mulSupport_disjoint_iff
statement: theorem mulSupport_disjoint_iff  : Disjoint (mulSupport f) s ↔ EqOn f 1 s
proof:
simp_rw [← subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right, mulSupport_subset_iff', notMem_compl_iff, EqOn,
    Pi.one_apply]

@[to_additive]

name: disjoint_mulSupport_iff
statement: theorem disjoint_mulSupport_iff  : Disjoint s (mulSupport f) ↔ EqOn f 1 s
proof:
rw [disjoint_comm, mulSupport_disjoint_iff]

@[to_additive (attr := simp)]

name: mulSupport_eq_empty_iff
statement: theorem mulSupport_eq_empty_iff  : mulSupport f = ∅ ↔ f = 1
proof:
rw [← subset_empty_iff, mulSupport_subset_iff', funext_iff]
  simp

@[to_additive (attr := simp)]

name: mulSupport_nonempty_iff
statement: theorem mulSupport_nonempty_iff  : (mulSupport f).Nonempty ↔ f ≠ 1
proof:
rw [nonempty_iff_ne_empty, Ne, mulSupport_eq_empty_iff]

@[to_additive]

name: range_subset_insert_image_mulSupport
statement: theorem range_subset_insert_image_mulSupport (f : ι → M) : range f ⊆ insert 1 (f '' mulSupport f)
proof:
simpa only [range_subset_iff, mem_insert_iff, or_iff_not_imp_left] using
    fun x (hx : x ∈ mulSupport f) ↦ mem_image_of_mem f hx

@[to_additive]

name: mulSupport_curry
statement: theorem mulSupport_curry (f : ι × κ → M) : (mulSupport f.curry) = (mulSupport f).image Prod.fst
proof:
simp [mulSupport, funext_iff, image]

@[to_additive]

name: image_inter_mulSupport_eq
statement: theorem image_inter_mulSupport_eq  : g '' s ∩ mulSupport f = g '' (s ∩ mulSupport (f ∘ g))
proof:
rw [mulSupport_comp_eq_preimage f g, image_inter_preimage]

end Set

namespace Pi
variable [DecidableEq ι] [One M] {i j : ι} {a b : M}

@[to_additive]

name: subsingleton_mulSupport_mulSingle
statement: theorem subsingleton_mulSupport_mulSingle  : (mulSupport (mulSingle i a)).Subsingleton
proof:
classical
  rw [mulSupport_mulSingle]
  split_ifs with h <;> simp

@[to_additive]

name: mulIndicator_eq_self
statement: theorem mulIndicator_eq_self  : s.mulIndicator f = f ↔ mulSupport f ⊆ s
proof:
simp only [funext_iff, subset_def, mem_mulSupport, mulIndicator_apply_eq_self, not_imp_comm]

@[to_additive]

name: mulIndicator_eq_one
statement: theorem mulIndicator_eq_one  : (mulIndicator s f = fun _ => 1) ↔ Disjoint (mulSupport f) s
proof:
simp only [funext_iff, mulIndicator_apply_eq_one, Set.disjoint_left, mem_mulSupport,
    not_imp_not]

@[to_additive (attr := simp)]

name: mulIndicator_one_preimage
statement: theorem mulIndicator_one_preimage (s : Set M) : t.mulIndicator 1 ⁻¹' s ∈ ({Set.univ, ∅} : Set (Set α))
proof:
classical
  rw [mulIndicator_one', Pi.one_def, Set.preimage_const]
  split_ifs <;> simp

@[to_additive]

name: toFreeMonoid_injective
statement: theorem toFreeMonoid_injective  : Function.Injective (@toFreeMonoid α)
proof:
rintro ⟨x, xs⟩ ⟨y, ys⟩ h
  simp only [toFreeMonoid_mk_eq_cons, Equiv.apply_eq_iff_eq] at h
  simpa using h

@[to_additive (attr := simp, grind .)]

name: toFreeMonoid_ne_one
statement: theorem toFreeMonoid_ne_one (x : FreeSemigroup α) : toFreeMonoid x ≠ 1
proof:
induction x with simp

@[to_additive]

name: range_toFreeMonoid
statement: theorem range_toFreeMonoid  : Set.range (@toFreeMonoid α) = {1}ᶜ
proof:

/--
The free monoid on `α` is isomorphic to the free semigroup on `α` with a `1` added.
-/
@[expose, to_additive (attr := simps) /-- The free additive monoid on `α` is isomorphic to
the free additive semigroup on `α` with a `0` added. -/]

name: countP_of
statement: theorem countP_of (x : α) : countP p (of x) = if p x then 1 else 0
proof:
rw [countP_apply, toList_of, List.countP_singleton]
  simp only [decide_eq_true_eq]

/-- `List.count` as a bundled additive monoid homomorphism. -/
-- Porting note: was (x = ·)

name: of_ne_one
statement: theorem of_ne_one (a : α) : of a ≠ 1
proof:
intro h
  have := congrArg FreeMonoid.length h
  simp only [length_of, length_one, Nat.succ_ne_self] at this

@[to_additive (attr := simp)]

name: stabilizer_univ
statement: theorem stabilizer_univ  : stabilizer G (Set.univ : Set α) = ⊤
proof:
ext
  simp

@[to_additive (attr := simp)]

name: stabilizer_mul_self
statement: theorem stabilizer_mul_self (s : Set G) : (stabilizer G s : Set G) * s = s
proof:
ext
  refine ⟨?_, fun h ↦ ⟨_, (stabilizer G s).one_mem, _, h, one_mul _⟩⟩
  rintro ⟨a, ha, b, hb, rfl⟩
  rw [← mem_stabilizer_iff.1 ha]
  exact smul_mem_smul_set hb

@[to_additive]

name: smul_set_stabilizer_subset
statement: theorem smul_set_stabilizer_subset (ha : a ∈ s) : a • (stabilizer G s : Set G) ⊆ s
proof:
simpa using op_smul_set_stabilizer_subset ha

end CommGroup
end Set

variable [Group G] [Group H] [MulAction G α] {a : G}

/-! ### Stabilizer of a subgroup -/

section Subgroup

-- TODO: Is there a lemma that could unify the following three very similar lemmas?

@[to_additive (attr := simp)]

name: stabilizer_subgroup
statement: theorem stabilizer_subgroup (s : Subgroup G) : stabilizer G (s : Set G) = s
proof:
simp_rw [SetLike.ext_iff, mem_stabilizer_set]
  refine fun a ↦ ⟨fun h ↦ ?_, fun ha b ↦ s.mul_mem_cancel_left ha⟩
  simpa only [smul_eq_mul, SetLike.mem_coe, mul_one] using (h 1).2 s.one_mem

@[to_additive (attr := simp)]

name: stabilizer_op_subgroup
statement: theorem stabilizer_op_subgroup (s : Subgroup G) : stabilizer Gᵐᵒᵖ (s : Set G) = s.op
proof:
simp_rw [SetLike.ext_iff, mem_stabilizer_set]
  simp only [smul_eq_mul_unop, SetLike.mem_coe, Subgroup.mem_op, «forall», unop_op]
  refine fun a ↦ ⟨fun h ↦ ?_, fun ha b ↦ s.mul_mem_cancel_right ha⟩
  simpa only [op_smul_eq_mul, SetLike.mem_coe, one_mul] using (h 1).2 s.one_mem

@[to_additive (attr := simp)]

name: stabilizer_subgroup_op
statement: theorem stabilizer_subgroup_op (s : Subgroup Gᵐᵒᵖ) : stabilizer G (s : Set Gᵐᵒᵖ) = s.unop
proof:
simp_rw [SetLike.ext_iff, mem_stabilizer_set]
  refine fun a ↦ ⟨fun h ↦ ?_, fun ha b ↦ s.mul_mem_cancel_right ha⟩
  have : 1 * MulOpposite.op a ∈ s := (h 1).2 s.one_mem
  simpa only [op_smul_eq_mul, SetLike.mem_coe, one_mul] using this

end Subgroup

/-! ### Stabilizer of a finset -/

section Finset
variable [DecidableEq α]

@[to_additive (attr := simp, norm_cast)]

name: stabilizer_coe_finset
statement: theorem stabilizer_coe_finset (s : Finset α) : stabilizer G (s : Set α) = stabilizer G s
proof:
ext; simp [← Finset.coe_inj]

@[to_additive (attr := simp)]

name: stabilizer_finset_univ
statement: theorem stabilizer_finset_univ  : stabilizer G (Finset.univ : Finset α) = ⊤
proof:
ext
  simp

@[to_additive (attr := simp)]

name: stabilizer_finset_singleton
statement: theorem stabilizer_finset_singleton (b : α) : stabilizer G ({b} : Finset α) = stabilizer G b
proof:
ext; simp

@[to_additive]

name: stabilizer_image_coe_quotient
statement: theorem stabilizer_image_coe_quotient  : stabilizer Q (q '' s) = ⊥
proof:
ext a
  induction a using QuotientGroup.induction_on with | _ a
  simp only [mem_stabilizer_iff, Subgroup.mem_bot, QuotientGroup.eq_one_iff]
  have : q a • q '' s = q '' (a • s) :=
    (image_smul_distrib (QuotientGroup.mk' <| stabilizer G s) _ _).symm
  rw [this]
  refine ⟨fun h ↦ ?_, fun h ↦ by rw [h]⟩
  rwa [QuotientGroup.image_coe_inj, mul_smul_comm, stabilizer_mul_self] at h

end MulAction

name: mem_span_support
statement: theorem mem_span_support (f : k[G]) : f ∈ Submodule.span k (of k G '' (f.support : Set G))
proof:
simp only [of, MonoidHom.coe_mk, OneHom.coe_mk]
  rw [← Finsupp.supported_eq_span_single, Finsupp.mem_supported]

end Span

end MonoidAlgebra

namespace AddMonoidAlgebra

open Finset Finsupp MulOpposite

variable {k : Type u₁} {G : Type u₂} [Semiring k]

section Span

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
/-- An element of `k[G]` is in the submodule generated by its support. -/

name: mem_span_support
statement: theorem mem_span_support (f : k[G]) : f ∈ Submodule.span k (of' k G '' f.support)
proof:
simp only [of']; rw [← Finsupp.supported_eq_span_single, Finsupp.mem_supported]

@[deprecated (since := "2025-12-08")] alias mem_span_support' := mem_span_support

end Span

end AddMonoidAlgebra

name: divOf_zero
statement: theorem divOf_zero (x : k[G]) : x /ᵒᶠ 0 = x
proof:
ext
  simp only [AddMonoidAlgebra.divOf_apply, zero_add]

name: mul_def
statement: theorem mul_def (x y : R[M]) : x * y = x.sum fun m₁ r₁ ↦ y.sum fun m₂ r₂ ↦ single (m₁ * m₂) (r₁ * r₂)
proof:
with_unfolding_all rfl

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
@[to_additive (dont_translate := R)]
instance nonUnitalNonAssocSemiring : NonUnitalNonAssocSemiring R[M] where
  zero_mul := by simp [mul_def]
  mul_zero := by simp [mul_def]
  left_distrib := by classical simp [mul_def]; simp [MonoidAlgebra, sum_add_index, mul_add]
  right_distrib := by classical simp [mul_def]; simp [MonoidAlgebra, sum_add_index, add_mul]

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
@[to_additive (dont_translate := R) mul_apply]

name: mul_single_apply_aux
statement: theorem mul_single_apply_aux (H : ∀ m' ∈ x.support, m' * m = m₁ ↔ m' = m₂) : (x * single m r) m₁ = x m₂ * r
proof:
classical
  calc
    (x * single m r) m₁
    _ = x.sum fun m' r' ↦ if m' * m = m₁ then r' * r else 0 := by simp [mul_apply]
    _ = x.sum fun m' r' ↦ if m' = m₂ then r' * r else 0 := by
      dsimp [Finsupp.sum]; congr! 2; simp [*]
    _ = x m₂ * r := by simp +contextual [Finsupp.sum_eq_single m₂]

@[to_additive (dont_translate := R) single_mul_apply_aux]

name: single_mul_apply_aux
statement: theorem single_mul_apply_aux (H : ∀ m' ∈ x.support, m * m' = m₁ ↔ m' = m₂) : (single m r * x) m₁ = r * x m₂
proof:
classical
  calc
    (single m r * x) m₁
    _ = x.sum fun m' r' ↦ if m * m' = m₁ then r * r' else 0 := by simp [mul_apply]
    _ = x.sum fun m' r' ↦ if m' = m₂ then r * r' else 0 := by
      dsimp [Finsupp.sum]; congr! 2; simp [*]
    _ = r * x m₂ := by simp +contextual [Finsupp.sum_eq_single m₂]

@[to_additive (attr := simp) (dont_translate := R) mul_single_apply_of_not_exists_add]

name: cardinalMk_eq_lift_of_fintype
statement: theorem cardinalMk_eq_lift_of_fintype  : #R[M'] = lift.{v} #R ^ card M'
proof:
simp [MonoidAlgebra]

@[deprecated (since := "2026-03-26")]
alias cardinalMk_lift_of_fintype := cardinalMk_eq_lift_of_fintype

@[to_additive]

name: supported_eq_span_single
statement: theorem supported_eq_span_single  : supported R R s = .span R ((fun m ↦ single m 1) '' s)
proof:
simp [supported_eq_map, Finsupp.supported_eq_span_single R s, Submodule.map_span,
    ← Set.image_comp, ofCoeff]

@[to_additive (attr := gcongr)]

name: sup_support_add_le
statement: theorem sup_support_add_le  : (f + g).support.sup degb ≤ f.support.sup degb ⊔ g.support.sup degb
proof:
classical
  exact (Finset.sup_mono Finsupp.support_add).trans_eq Finset.sup_union

name: supDegree_mem_range
statement: theorem supDegree_mem_range (hp : p ≠ 0) : p.supDegree D ∈ Set.range D
proof:
obtain ⟨a, -, he⟩ := exists_supDegree_mem_support D hp; exact ⟨a, he.symm⟩

variable {ι : Type*} {s : Finset ι} {i : ι} (hi : i ∈ s) {f : ι → R[A]}

name: supDegree_add_eq_left
statement: theorem supDegree_add_eq_left (h : q.supDegree D < p.supDegree D) : (p + q).supDegree D = p.supDegree D
proof:
apply (supDegree_add_le.trans <| sup_le le_rfl h.le).antisymm
  obtain ⟨a, ha, he⟩ := exists_supDegree_mem_support D (ne_zero_of_not_supDegree_le h.not_ge)
  rw [he] at h ⊢
  apply Finset.le_sup
  rw [mem_support_iff, add_apply, apply_eq_zero_of_not_le_supDegree h.not_ge, add_zero]
  exact mem_support_iff.mp ha

name: supDegree_add_eq_right
statement: theorem supDegree_add_eq_right (h : p.supDegree D < q.supDegree D) : (p + q).supDegree D = q.supDegree D
proof:
rw [add_comm, supDegree_add_eq_left h]

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in

name: leadingCoeff_add_eq_left
statement: theorem leadingCoeff_add_eq_left (h : q.supDegree D < p.supDegree D) : (p + q).leadingCoeff D = p.leadingCoeff D
proof:
obtain ⟨a, he⟩ := supDegree_mem_range D (ne_zero_of_not_supDegree_le h.not_ge)
  rw [leadingCoeff, supDegree_add_eq_left h, Finsupp.add_apply, ← leadingCoeff,
    apply_eq_zero_of_not_le_supDegree (D := D), add_zero]
  rw [← he, Function.apply_invFun_apply (f := D), he]; exact h.not_ge

name: leadingCoeff_add_eq_right
statement: theorem leadingCoeff_add_eq_right (h : p.supDegree D < q.supDegree D) : (p + q).leadingCoeff D = q.leadingCoeff D
proof: rw [add_comm, leadingCoeff_add_eq_left h]

name: leadingCoeff_eq_zero
statement: theorem leadingCoeff_eq_zero (hD : D.Injective) : p.leadingCoeff D = 0 ↔ p = 0
proof:
refine ⟨(fun h => ?_).mtr, fun h => h ▸ leadingCoeff_zero⟩
  rw [leadingCoeff, ← Ne, ← Finsupp.mem_support_iff]
  exact supDegree_mem_support hD h

name: mapDomainAlgHom_id
statement: theorem mapDomainAlgHom_id  : mapDomainAlgHom R A (.id M) = .id R A[M]
proof:
ext; simp [MonoidHom.id, ← Function.id_def]

@[to_additive (attr := simp)]

name: intrinsicStar_mulRight
statement: theorem intrinsicStar_mulRight (x : E) : star (toConv (mulRight R x)) = toConv (mulLeft R (star x))
proof: rw [star_eq_iff_star_eq, intrinsicStar_mulLeft, star_star]

name: intrinsicStar_single
statement: theorem intrinsicStar_single (i : n) : star (toConv (single R B i)) = toConv (single R B i)
proof:
aesop (add simp [Pi.single, Function.update])

variable [Fintype n]

name: gc
statement: theorem gc  : GaloisConnection (adjoin R : Set A → NonUnitalStarSubalgebra R A) (↑)
proof:
intro s S
  rw [← toNonUnitalSubalgebra_le_iff, adjoin_toNonUnitalSubalgebra,
    NonUnitalAlgebra.adjoin_le_iff, coe_toNonUnitalSubalgebra]
  exact ⟨fun h => Set.subset_union_left.trans h,
    fun h => Set.union_subset h fun x hx => star_star x ▸ star_mem (show star x ∈ S from h hx)⟩

/-- Galois insertion between `adjoin` and `SetLike.coe`. -/
protected def gi : GaloisInsertion (adjoin R : Set A → NonUnitalStarSubalgebra R A) (↑) where
  choice s hs := (adjoin R s).copy s <| le_antisymm (NonUnitalStarAlgebra.gc.le_u_l s) hs
  gc := NonUnitalStarAlgebra.gc
  le_l_u S := (NonUnitalStarAlgebra.gc (S : Set A) (adjoin R S)).1 <| le_rfl
  choice_eq _ _ := NonUnitalStarSubalgebra.copy_eq _ _ _

name: adjoin_eq_span
statement: theorem adjoin_eq_span (s : Set A) : (adjoin R s).toSubmodule = Submodule.span R (Subsemigroup.closure (s ∪ star s))
proof:
rw [adjoin_toNonUnitalSubalgebra, NonUnitalAlgebra.adjoin_eq_span]

@[simp]

name: toNonUnitalSubalgebra_bot

proof:
ext x
  simp only [mem_bot, NonUnitalStarSubalgebra.mem_toNonUnitalSubalgebra, NonUnitalAlgebra.mem_bot]

@[simp, norm_cast]

name: coe_bot

proof:
simp only [Set.ext_iff, NonUnitalStarAlgebra.mem_bot, SetLike.mem_coe, Set.mem_singleton_iff,
    forall_const]

name: coe_centralizer_centralizer
statement: theorem coe_centralizer_centralizer (s : Set A) : (centralizer R (centralizer R s : Set A)) = (s ∪ star s).centralizer.centralizer
proof:
rw [coe_centralizer, StarMemClass.star_coe_eq, Set.union_self, coe_centralizer]

end Centralizer

end NonUnitalStarSubalgebra

namespace NonUnitalStarAlgebra

open NonUnitalStarSubalgebra

variable [CommSemiring R] [StarRing R]
variable [NonUnitalSemiring A] [StarRing A] [Module R A]
variable [IsScalarTower R A A] [SMulCommClass R A A] [StarModule R A]

name: adjoin_le_centralizer_centralizer
statement: theorem adjoin_le_centralizer_centralizer (s : Set A) : adjoin R s ≤ centralizer R (centralizer R s)
proof:
rw [← toNonUnitalSubalgebra_le_iff, centralizer_toNonUnitalSubalgebra,
    adjoin_toNonUnitalSubalgebra]

  rw [StarMemClass.star_coe_eq]
  simp

name: sqrt_two_inv_mul_self
statement: theorem sqrt_two_inv_mul_self  : (√2)⁻¹ * (√2)⁻¹ = (2⁻¹ : ℝ)
proof:
rw [← mul_inv]
  simp

end TsirelsonInequality

open TsirelsonInequality

/-- In a noncommutative ordered \*-algebra over ℝ,
Tsirelson's bound for a CHSH tuple (A₀, A₁, B₀, B₁) is
`A₀ * B₀ + A₀ * B₁ + A₁ * B₀ - A₁ * B₁ ≤ 2^(3/2) • 1`.

We prove this by providing an explicit sum-of-squares decomposition
of the difference.

(We could work over `ℤ[2^(1/2), 2^(-1/2)]` if we really wanted to!)
-/

name: add_star_self
statement: theorem add_star_self (x : R) : IsSelfAdjoint (x + star x)
proof:
simp only [isSelfAdjoint_iff, add_comm, star_add, star_star]

@[simp]

name: star_add_self
statement: theorem star_add_self (x : R) : IsSelfAdjoint (star x + x)
proof:
simp only [isSelfAdjoint_iff, add_comm, star_add, star_star]

end AddCommMonoid

section Semigroup

variable [Semigroup R] [StarMul R]

@[aesop safe apply]

name: invOf_iff
statement: theorem invOf_iff (x : R) : IsSelfAdjoint ⅟x ↔ IsSelfAdjoint x
proof:

alias ⟨_, invOf⟩ := invOf_iff

@[grind =]

name: inv_iff
statement: theorem inv_iff (x : R) : IsSelfAdjoint x⁻¹ ↔ IsSelfAdjoint x
proof:

@[aesop safe apply]

name: inv₀_iff
statement: theorem inv₀_iff (x : R) : IsSelfAdjoint x⁻¹ ↔ IsSelfAdjoint x
proof:

name: image_star
statement: theorem image_star  : Star.star '' s = s⋆
proof:

name: coe_centralizer_centralizer
statement: theorem coe_centralizer_centralizer (s : Set A) : (centralizer R (centralizer R s : Set A)) = (s ∪ star s).centralizer.centralizer
proof:

/-! ### The star closure of a subalgebra -/
namespace Subalgebra

open scoped Pointwise

variable {F R A B : Type*} [CommSemiring R] [StarRing R]
variable [Semiring A] [Algebra R A] [StarRing A] [StarModule R A]
variable [Semiring B] [Algebra R B] [StarRing B] [StarModule R B]

/-- The pointwise `star` of a subalgebra is a subalgebra. -/
instance involutiveStar : InvolutiveStar (Subalgebra R A) where
  star S :=
    { carrier := star S.carrier
      mul_mem' := fun {x y} hx hy => by
        simp only [Set.mem_star, Subalgebra.mem_carrier] at *
        exact (star_mul x y).symm ▸ mul_mem hy hx
      one_mem' := Set.mem_star.mp ((star_one A).symm ▸ one_mem S : star (1 : A) ∈ S)
      add_mem' := fun {x y} hx hy => by
        simp only [Set.mem_star, Subalgebra.mem_carrier] at *
        exact (star_add x y).symm ▸ add_mem hx hy
      zero_mem' := Set.mem_star.mp ((star_zero A).symm ▸ zero_mem S : star (0 : A) ∈ S)
      algebraMap_mem' := fun r => by
        simpa only [Set.mem_star, Subalgebra.mem_carrier, ← algebraMap_star_comm] using
          S.algebraMap_mem (star r) }
  star_involutive S :=
    Subalgebra.ext fun x =>
      ⟨fun hx => star_star x ▸ hx, fun hx => ((star_star x).symm ▸ hx : star (star x) ∈ S)⟩

@[simp]

name: gc
statement: theorem gc  : GaloisConnection (adjoin R : Set A → StarSubalgebra R A) (↑)
proof:
intro s S
  rw [← toSubalgebra_le_iff, adjoin_toSubalgebra, Algebra.adjoin_le_iff, coe_toSubalgebra]
  exact
    ⟨fun h => Set.subset_union_left.trans h, fun h =>
      Set.union_subset h fun x hx => star_star x ▸ star_mem (show star x ∈ S from h hx)⟩

/-- Galois insertion between `adjoin` and `coe`. -/
protected def gi : GaloisInsertion (adjoin R : Set A → StarSubalgebra R A) (↑) where
  choice s hs := (adjoin R s).copy s <| le_antisymm (StarAlgebra.gc.le_u_l s) hs
  gc := StarAlgebra.gc
  le_l_u S := (StarAlgebra.gc (S : Set A) (adjoin R S)).1 <| le_rfl
  choice_eq _ _ := StarSubalgebra.copy_eq _ _ _

name: adjoin_eq_span
statement: theorem adjoin_eq_span (s : Set A) : Subalgebra.toSubmodule (adjoin R s).toSubalgebra = span R (Submonoid.closure (s ∪ star s))
proof:
rw [adjoin_toSubalgebra, Algebra.adjoin_eq_span]

open Submodule in

name: adjoin_nonUnitalStarSubalgebra_eq_span
statement: theorem adjoin_nonUnitalStarSubalgebra_eq_span (s : NonUnitalStarSubalgebra R A) : (adjoin R (s : Set A)).toSubalgebra.toSubmodule = span R {1} ⊔ s.toSubmodule
proof:
rw [adjoin_eq_span, Submonoid.closure_eq_one_union, span_union,
    ← NonUnitalStarAlgebra.adjoin_eq_span, NonUnitalStarAlgebra.adjoin_eq]

name: adjoin_le_centralizer_centralizer
statement: theorem adjoin_le_centralizer_centralizer (s : Set A) : adjoin R s ≤ centralizer R (centralizer R s)
proof:
rw [← toSubalgebra_le_iff, centralizer_toSubalgebra, adjoin_toSubalgebra]
  convert Algebra.adjoin_le_centralizer_centralizer R (s ∪ star s)
  rw [StarMemClass.star_coe_eq]
  simp

/-- If all elements of `s : Set A` commute pairwise and with elements of `star s`, then `adjoin R s`
is commutative. -/

name: inf_toSubalgebra
statement: theorem inf_toSubalgebra (S T : StarSubalgebra R A) : (S ⊓ T).toSubalgebra = S.toSubalgebra ⊓ T.toSubalgebra
proof:
ext; simp
-- Porting note: Lean can no longer prove this by `rfl`, it times out

@[simp, norm_cast]

name: algebraMap_star_comm
statement: theorem algebraMap_star_comm (r : R) : algebraMap R A (star r) = star (algebraMap R A r)
proof:
simp only [Algebra.algebraMap_eq_smul_one, star_smul, star_one]

variable (A) in
protected lemma IsSelfAdjoint.algebraMap {r : R} (hr : IsSelfAdjoint r) :
    IsSelfAdjoint (algebraMap R A r) := by
  simpa using congr(algebraMap R A $(hr.star_eq))

name: toUnits_comp_map
statement: theorem toUnits_comp_map (f : R →⋆* S) : toUnits.comp (map f).toMonoidHom = (Units.map f.toMonoidHom).comp toUnits
proof:
ext; rfl

/-- The star monoid isomorphism between unitary subgroups induced by a star monoid isomorphism of
the underlying star monoids. -/
@[simps]

name: coe_div
statement: theorem coe_div (U₁ U₂ : unitary R) : ↑(U₁ / U₂) = (U₁ / U₂ : R)
proof:
simp only [div_eq_mul_inv, coe_inv, Submonoid.coe_mul]

@[norm_cast]

name: conjStarAlgAut_symm
statement: theorem conjStarAlgAut_symm (u : unitary R) : (conjStarAlgAut S R u).symm = conjStarAlgAut S R (star u)
proof: ext; simp [conjStarAlgAut_symm_apply]

name: star_algebraMap
statement: theorem star_algebraMap (r : R) : star (algebraMap R (FreeAlgebra R X) r) = algebraMap R _ r
proof:
simp [star, Star.star]

/-- `star` as an `AlgEquiv` -/

name: conjneg_eq_zero
statement: theorem conjneg_eq_zero  : conjneg f = 0 ↔ f = 0
proof:
rw [← conjneg_inj, conjneg_conjneg, conjneg_zero]

@[simp] lemma conjneg_eq_one : conjneg f = 1 ↔ f = 1 := by
  rw [← conjneg_inj, conjneg_conjneg, conjneg_one]

name: support_conjneg
statement: theorem support_conjneg (f : G → R) : support (conjneg f) = -support f
proof:
ext; simp [starRingEnd_apply]

/-- `conjneg` bundled as a ring homomorphism. -/
@[simps] def conjnegRingHom : (G → R) →+* (G → R) where
  toFun := conjneg
  map_zero' := conjneg_zero
  map_one' := conjneg_one
  map_add' := conjneg_add
  map_mul' := conjneg_mul

end CommSemiring

section CommRing
variable [CommRing R] [StarRing R]

@[simp] lemma conjneg_sub (f g : G → R) : conjneg (f - g) = conjneg f - conjneg g := by ext; simp
@[simp] lemma conjneg_neg (f : G → R) : conjneg (-f) = -conjneg f := by ext; simp

end CommRing

name: snd_comp_inl
statement: theorem snd_comp_inl  : (snd ..).comp (inl G₀ H₀) = 1
proof:
ext x
  obtain rfl | ⟨_, rfl⟩ := GroupWithZero.eq_zero_or_unit x <;>
  simp_all [WithZero.withZeroUnitsEquiv, snd, inl]

name: fst_comp_inr
statement: theorem fst_comp_inr  : (fst ..).comp (inr G₀ H₀) = 1
proof:
ext x
  obtain rfl | ⟨_, rfl⟩ := GroupWithZero.eq_zero_or_unit x <;>
  simp_all [WithZero.withZeroUnitsEquiv, fst, inr]

name: fst_surjective
statement: theorem fst_surjective  : Function.Surjective (fst G₀ H₀)
proof:
classical
  exact Function.HasRightInverse.surjective ⟨inl .., fun _ ↦ by simp⟩

name: snd_surjective
statement: theorem snd_surjective  : Function.Surjective (snd G₀ H₀)
proof:
classical
  exact Function.HasRightInverse.surjective ⟨inr .., fun _ ↦ by simp⟩

variable [DecidablePred fun x : G₀ ↦ x = 0] [DecidablePred fun x : H₀ ↦ x = 0]

name: indicator_eq_zero_iff_notMem
statement: theorem indicator_eq_zero_iff_notMem  : indicator s 1 i = (0 : M₀) ↔ i ∉ s
proof: classical simp [indicator_apply, imp_false]

name: indicator_eq_one_iff_mem
statement: theorem indicator_eq_one_iff_mem  : indicator s 1 i = (1 : M₀) ↔ i ∈ s
proof: classical simp [indicator_apply, imp_false]

name: support_div
statement: theorem support_div (f g : ι → G₀) : support (fun a ↦ f a / g a) = support f ∩ support g
proof:
simp [div_eq_mul_inv]

@[simp] lemma support_div' (f g : ι → G₀) : support (f / g) = support f ∩ support g :=
  support_div _ _

end GroupWithZero

variable [One R]

name: notMem_nonZeroDivisorsLeft_iff
statement: theorem notMem_nonZeroDivisorsLeft_iff  : x ∉ nonZeroDivisorsLeft M₀ ↔ {y | x * y = 0 ∧ y ≠ 0}.Nonempty
proof:
simpa [mem_nonZeroDivisorsLeft_iff] using Set.nonempty_def.symm

/-- The collection of elements of a `MonoidWithZero` that are not right zero divisors form a
`Submonoid`. -/

name: notMem_nonZeroDivisorsRight_iff
statement: theorem notMem_nonZeroDivisorsRight_iff  : x ∉ nonZeroDivisorsRight M₀ ↔ {y | y * x = 0 ∧ y ≠ 0}.Nonempty
proof: simpa [mem_nonZeroDivisorsRight_iff] using Set.nonempty_def.symm

name: nonZeroDivisorsLeft_eq_right
statement: theorem nonZeroDivisorsLeft_eq_right (M₀ : Type*) : nonZeroDivisorsLeft M₀ = nonZeroDivisorsRight M₀
proof:
ext x; simp [mul_comm x]

@[simp] lemma coe_nonZeroDivisorsLeft_eq [NoZeroDivisors M₀] [Nontrivial M₀] :
    nonZeroDivisorsLeft M₀ = {x : M₀ | x ≠ 0} := by
  ext x
  simp only [SetLike.mem_coe, mem_nonZeroDivisorsLeft_iff, mul_eq_zero, Set.mem_setOf_eq]
  refine ⟨fun h ↦ ?_, fun hx y hx' ↦ by simp_all⟩
  contrapose! h
  exact ⟨1, Or.inl h, one_ne_zero⟩

@[simp] lemma coe_nonZeroDivisorsRight_eq [NoZeroDivisors M₀] [Nontrivial M₀] :
    nonZeroDivisorsRight M₀ = {x : M₀ | x ≠ 0} := by
  ext x
  simp only [SetLike.mem_coe, mem_nonZeroDivisorsRight_iff, mul_eq_zero, forall_eq_or_imp, true_and,
    Set.mem_setOf_eq]
  refine ⟨fun h ↦ ?_, fun hx y hx' ↦ by contradiction⟩
  contrapose! h
  exact ⟨1, h, one_ne_zero⟩

end

/-- The submonoid of non-zero-divisors of a `MonoidWithZero` `M₀`. -/

name: notMem_nonZeroDivisors_iff
statement: theorem notMem_nonZeroDivisors_iff  : r ∉ M₀⁰ ↔ {s | r * s = 0 ∧ s ≠ 0}.Nonempty ∨ {s | s * r = 0 ∧ s ≠ 0}.Nonempty
proof: simp [-not_and, not_and_or, mem_nonZeroDivisors_iff, Set.nonempty_def]

name: nonZeroDivisorsLeft_eq_nonZeroDivisors
statement: theorem nonZeroDivisorsLeft_eq_nonZeroDivisors  : nonZeroDivisorsLeft M₀ = nonZeroDivisors M₀
proof: rw [nonZeroDivisors, nonZeroDivisorsLeft_eq_right, inf_idem]

name: nonZeroDivisorsRight_eq_nonZeroDivisors
statement: theorem nonZeroDivisorsRight_eq_nonZeroDivisors  : nonZeroDivisorsRight M₀ = nonZeroDivisors M₀
proof: rw [← nonZeroDivisorsLeft_eq_right, nonZeroDivisorsLeft_eq_nonZeroDivisors]

name: nonZeroDivisorsRight_eq_left
statement: theorem nonZeroDivisorsRight_eq_left  : nonZeroDivisorsRight M₀ = nonZeroDivisorsLeft M₀
proof: rw [nonZeroDivisorsLeft_eq_right]

name: mem_nonZeroDivisors_iff_left
statement: theorem mem_nonZeroDivisors_iff_left  : r ∈ M₀⁰ ↔ ∀ x, r * x = 0 → x = 0
proof: rw [← nonZeroDivisorsLeft_eq_nonZeroDivisors]; rfl

name: mem_nonZeroDivisors_iff_right
statement: theorem mem_nonZeroDivisors_iff_right  : r ∈ M₀⁰ ↔ ∀ x, x * r = 0 → x = 0
proof: rw [← nonZeroDivisorsRight_eq_nonZeroDivisors]; rfl

name: notMem_nonZeroDivisors_iff_left
statement: theorem notMem_nonZeroDivisors_iff_left  : r ∉ M₀⁰ ↔ {s | r * s = 0 ∧ s ≠ 0}.Nonempty
proof: simp [mem_nonZeroDivisors_iff_left, Set.nonempty_def]

name: notMem_nonZeroDivisors_iff_right
statement: theorem notMem_nonZeroDivisors_iff_right  : r ∉ M₀⁰ ↔ {s | s * r = 0 ∧ s ≠ 0}.Nonempty
proof: simp [mem_nonZeroDivisors_iff_right, Set.nonempty_def]

name: mul_left_mem_nonZeroDivisors_eq_zero_iff
statement: theorem mul_left_mem_nonZeroDivisors_eq_zero_iff (hr : r ∈ M₀⁰) : r * x = 0 ↔ x = 0
proof:
rw [mul_comm, mul_right_mem_nonZeroDivisors_eq_zero_iff hr]

@[simp]

name: mk_mem_nonZeroDivisors_associates
statement: theorem mk_mem_nonZeroDivisors_associates  : Associates.mk a ∈ (Associates M₀)⁰ ↔ a ∈ M₀⁰
proof:
rw [mem_nonZeroDivisors_iff_right, mem_nonZeroDivisors_iff_right]
  contrapose!
  constructor
  · rintro ⟨⟨x⟩, hx₁, hx₂⟩
    refine ⟨x, ?_, ?_⟩
    · rwa [← Associates.mk_eq_zero, ← Associates.mk_mul_mk, ← Associates.quot_mk_eq_mk]
    · rwa [← Associates.mk_ne_zero, ← Associates.quot_mk_eq_mk]
  · refine fun ⟨b, hb₁, hb₂⟩ ↦ ⟨Associates.mk b, ?_, by rwa [Associates.mk_ne_zero]⟩
    rw [Associates.mk_mul_mk, hb₁, Associates.mk_zero]

/-- The non-zero divisors of associates of a monoid with zero `M₀` are isomorphic to the associates
of the non-zero divisors of `M₀` under the map `⟨⟦a⟧, _⟩ ↦ ⟦⟨a, _⟩⟧`. -/

name: inv_right₀
statement: theorem inv_right₀ (h : SemiconjBy a x y) : SemiconjBy a x⁻¹ y⁻¹
proof:
by_cases ha : a = 0
  · simp only [ha, zero_left]
  by_cases hx : x = 0
  · subst x
    simp only [SemiconjBy, mul_zero, @eq_comm _ _ (y * a), mul_eq_zero] at h
    simp [h.resolve_right ha]
  · have := mul_ne_zero ha hx
    rw [h.eq, mul_ne_zero_iff] at this
    exact @units_inv_right _ _ _ (Units.mk0 x hx) (Units.mk0 y this.1) h

@[simp]

name: associated_eq_eq
statement: theorem associated_eq_eq  : (Associated : M → M → Prop) = Eq
proof:
ext
  rw [associated_iff_eq]

name: decompositionMonoid_iff
statement: theorem decompositionMonoid_iff  : DecompositionMonoid (Associates M) ↔ DecompositionMonoid M
proof:
simp_rw [_root_.decompositionMonoid_iff, forall_associated, isPrimal_mk]

instance instDecompositionMonoid [DecompositionMonoid M] : DecompositionMonoid (Associates M) :=
  decompositionMonoid_iff.mpr ‹_›

@[simp]

name: irreducible_iff_prime_iff
statement: theorem irreducible_iff_prime_iff  : (∀ a : M, Irreducible a ↔ Prime a) ↔ ∀ a : Associates M, Irreducible a ↔ Prime a
proof:
simp_rw [forall_associated, irreducible_mk, prime_mk]

end CommMonoidWithZero

section CancelCommMonoidWithZero

variable [CommMonoidWithZero M] [IsCancelMulZero M]

instance instPartialOrder : PartialOrder (Associates M) where
  le_antisymm := mk_surjective.forall₂.2 fun _a _b hab hba => mk_eq_mk_iff_associated.2 <|
    associated_of_dvd_dvd (dvd_of_mk_le_mk hab) (dvd_of_mk_le_mk hba)

instance instIsCancelMulZero : IsCancelMulZero (Associates M) :=
  @IsLeftCancelMulZero.to_isCancelMulZero _ _ _
  { mul_left_cancel_of_ne_zero := by
      rintro ⟨a⟩ ha ⟨b⟩ ⟨c⟩ h
      rcases Quotient.exact' h with ⟨u, hu⟩
      have hu : a * (b * ↑u) = a * c := by rwa [← mul_assoc]
      exact Quotient.sound' ⟨u, mul_left_cancel₀ (mk_ne_zero.1 ha) hu⟩ }

name: mul_eq_mul_left_iff
statement: theorem mul_eq_mul_left_iff  : a * b = a * c ↔ b = c ∨ a = 0
proof:
by_cases ha : a = 0 <;> [simp only [ha, zero_mul, or_true]; simp [mul_right_inj', ha]]

end IsLeftCancelMulZero

/-- A mixin for right cancellative multiplication by nonzero elements. -/
@[mk_iff] class IsRightCancelMulZero (M₀ : Type u) [Mul M₀] [Zero M₀] : Prop where
  /-- Multiplication by a nonzero element is right cancellative. -/
  protected mul_right_cancel_of_ne_zero : ∀ {a : M₀}, a ≠ 0 → IsRightRegular a

section IsRightCancelMulZero
section Mul
variable [Mul M₀] [Zero M₀] [IsRightCancelMulZero M₀] {a b c : M₀}

name: mul_eq_mul_right_iff
statement: theorem mul_eq_mul_right_iff  : a * c = b * c ↔ a = b ∨ c = 0
proof:
by_cases hc : c = 0 <;> [simp only [hc, mul_zero, or_true]; simp [mul_left_inj', hc]]

end IsRightCancelMulZero

/-- A mixin for cancellative multiplication by nonzero elements. -/
@[mk_iff] class IsCancelMulZero (M₀ : Type u) [Mul M₀] [Zero M₀] : Prop
  extends IsLeftCancelMulZero M₀, IsRightCancelMulZero M₀

name: noZeroDivisors_iff_right_eq_zero_of_mul
statement: theorem noZeroDivisors_iff_right_eq_zero_of_mul  : NoZeroDivisors M₀ ↔ ∀ x : M₀, x ≠ 0 → ∀ y, x * y = 0 → y = 0
proof:
simp only [noZeroDivisors_iff, or_iff_not_imp_left]
  exact ⟨fun h a ha b eq ↦ h eq ha, fun h a b eq ha ↦ h a ha b eq⟩

name: noZeroDivisors_iff_left_eq_zero_of_mul
statement: theorem noZeroDivisors_iff_left_eq_zero_of_mul  : NoZeroDivisors M₀ ↔ ∀ x : M₀, x ≠ 0 → ∀ y, y * x = 0 → y = 0
proof:
simp only [noZeroDivisors_iff, or_iff_not_imp_right]
  exact ⟨fun h b hb a eq ↦ h eq hb, fun h a b eq hb ↦ h b hb a eq⟩

name: noZeroDivisors_iff_eq_zero_of_mul
statement: theorem noZeroDivisors_iff_eq_zero_of_mul  : NoZeroDivisors M₀ ↔ ∀ x : M₀, x ≠ 0 → (∀ y, x * y = 0 → y = 0) ∧ (∀ y, y * x = 0 → y = 0)
proof:
simp only [forall_and, ← noZeroDivisors_iff_right_eq_zero_of_mul,
    ← noZeroDivisors_iff_left_eq_zero_of_mul, and_self]

variable [NoZeroDivisors M₀] {a b : M₀}

/-- If `α` has no zero divisors, then the product of two elements equals zero iff one of them
equals zero. -/
@[simp]

name: not_isLeftRegular_zero_iff
statement: theorem not_isLeftRegular_zero_iff  : ¬IsLeftRegular (0 : R) ↔ Nontrivial R
proof:
rw [nontrivial_iff, not_iff_comm, isLeftRegular_zero_iff_subsingleton, subsingleton_iff]
  push Not
  exact Iff.rfl

/-- The element `0` is right-regular if and only if `R` is trivial. -/

name: not_isRightRegular_zero_iff
statement: theorem not_isRightRegular_zero_iff  : ¬IsRightRegular (0 : R) ↔ Nontrivial R
proof:
rw [nontrivial_iff, not_iff_comm, isRightRegular_zero_iff_subsingleton, subsingleton_iff]
  push Not
  exact Iff.rfl

/-- The element `0` is regular if and only if `R` is trivial. -/

name: embedding_restrict₀
statement: theorem embedding_restrict₀ (a : A) : ValueGroup₀.embedding (restrict₀ f a) = f a
proof:
simp only [restrict₀_apply, embedding_apply]
  aesop

end ValueGroup₀

end Restrict

noncomputable section GroupWithZero

variable [GroupWithZero A] [GroupWithZero B] [MonoidWithZeroHomClass F A B] {f}

/- When the *domain* is itself a group with zero, the `valueMonoid` and the `valueGroup` coincide.-/

name: valueMonoid_eq_valueGroup
statement: theorem valueMonoid_eq_valueGroup  : (valueMonoid f) = (valueGroup f).toSubmonoid
proof:
rw [valueGroup_def, Subgroup.closure_toSubmonoid, Eq.comm]
  apply Submonoid.closure_eq_of_le
  · simp only [union_subset_iff, subset_refl, true_and]
    intro _ ⟨y, hy⟩
    use y⁻¹
    simp [hy]
  · simp [Submonoid.closure_union]

variable (f)

name: valueGroup_eq_range
statement: theorem valueGroup_eq_range  : Units.val '' (valueGroup f) = (range f \ {0})
proof:
ext x
  simp only [mem_diff, mem_range, mem_singleton_iff, ← valueMonoid_eq_valueGroup' f, mem_image,
    SetLike.mem_coe, mem_valueMonoid_iff, mem_preimage, mem_range]
  constructor
  · rintro ⟨y, hy, rfl⟩
    simp only [Units.ne_zero, not_false_eq_true, and_true, hy]
  · rintro ⟨⟨y, hy⟩, hx₀⟩
    refine ⟨Units.mk0 x hx₀, ?_, rfl⟩
    simpa [Units.val_mk0, mem_range] using ⟨y, hy⟩

@[simp]

name: zero_or_exists_mk
statement: theorem zero_or_exists_mk (x : ValueGroup₀ f) : x = 0 ∨ ∃ r s hr hs, x = valueGroup.mk f r s hr hs
proof:
obtain _ | ⟨x, hx⟩ := x
  · left; rfl
  · rw [mem_valueGroup_iff_of_comm'] at hx
    obtain ⟨r, hr, s, hs, hrs⟩ := hx
    refine .inr ⟨r, s, hr, hs, Option.some_inj.mpr <| by
      simp only [valueGroup.mk, Subtype.mk.injEq]
      rw [eq_inv_mul_iff_mul_eq]
      simp [← hrs, mul_comm]⟩

name: dvd_antisymm
statement: theorem dvd_antisymm  : a ∣ b → b ∣ a → a = b
proof:
rintro ⟨c, rfl⟩ ⟨d, hcd⟩
  rw [mul_assoc, eq_comm, mul_right_eq_self₀, mul_eq_one] at hcd
  obtain ⟨rfl, -⟩ | rfl := hcd <;> simp

name: isConj_iff₀
statement: theorem isConj_iff₀  : IsConj a b ↔ ∃ c : α, c ≠ 0 ∧ c * a * c⁻¹ = b
proof:
rw [IsConj, Units.exists_iff_ne_zero (p := (SemiconjBy · a b))]
  congr! 2 with c
  exact and_congr_right (mul_inv_eq_iff_eq_mul₀ · |>.symm)

name: zero_pow_eq
statement: theorem zero_pow_eq (n : ℕ) : (0 : M₀) ^ n = if n = 0 then 1 else 0
proof:
split_ifs with h
  · rw [h, pow_zero]
  · rw [zero_pow h]

name: zero_pow_eq_one₀
statement: theorem zero_pow_eq_one₀  : (0 : M₀) ^ n = 1 ↔ n = 0
proof: rw [zero_pow_eq, one_ne_zero.ite_eq_left_iff]

name: inv_eq_of_mul
statement: theorem inv_eq_of_mul (h : a * b = 1) : a⁻¹ = b
proof:
rw [← inv_mul_cancel_left₀ (left_ne_zero_of_mul_eq_one h) b, h, mul_one]

-- See note [lower instance priority]
instance (priority := 100) GroupWithZero.toDivisionMonoid : DivisionMonoid G₀ where
  inv_inv a := by
    by_cases h : a = 0
    · simp [h]
    · exact left_inv_eq_right_inv (inv_mul_cancel₀ <| inv_ne_zero h) (inv_mul_cancel₀ h)
  mul_inv_rev a b := by
    by_cases ha : a = 0
    · simp [ha]
    by_cases hb : b = 0
    · simp [hb]
    apply inv_eq_of_mul
    simp [mul_assoc, ha, hb]
  inv_eq_of_mul _ _ := by exact inv_eq_of_mul

-- see Note [lower instance priority]
instance (priority := 10) : IsCancelMulZero G₀ where
  mul_left_cancel_of_ne_zero {x} hx y z h := by
    dsimp only at h; rw [← inv_mul_cancel_left₀ hx y, h, inv_mul_cancel_left₀ hx z]
  mul_right_cancel_of_ne_zero {x} hx y z h := by
    dsimp only at h; rw [← mul_inv_cancel_right₀ hx y, h, mul_inv_cancel_right₀ hx z]

end GroupWithZero

section GroupWithZero

variable [GroupWithZero G₀] {a : G₀}

@[simp]

name: mul_self_mul_inv
statement: theorem mul_self_mul_inv (a : G₀) : a * a * a⁻¹ = a
proof:
by_cases h : a = 0
  · rw [h, inv_zero, mul_zero]
  · rw [mul_assoc, mul_inv_cancel₀ h, mul_one]


/-- Multiplying `a` by its inverse and then by itself results in `a`
(whether or not `a` is zero). -/
@[simp]

name: mul_inv_mul_cancel
statement: theorem mul_inv_mul_cancel (a : G₀) : a * a⁻¹ * a = a
proof:
by_cases h : a = 0
  · rw [h, inv_zero, mul_zero]
  · rw [mul_inv_cancel₀ h, one_mul]


/-- Multiplying `a⁻¹` by `a` twice results in `a` (whether or not `a`
is zero). -/
@[simp]

name: inv_mul_mul_self
statement: theorem inv_mul_mul_self (a : G₀) : a⁻¹ * a * a = a
proof:
by_cases h : a = 0
  · rw [h, inv_zero, mul_zero]
  · rw [inv_mul_cancel₀ h, one_mul]


/-- Multiplying `a` by itself and then dividing by itself results in `a`, whether or not `a` is
zero. -/
@[simp]

name: div_div_self
statement: theorem div_div_self (a : G₀) : a / (a / a) = a
proof:
rw [div_div_eq_mul_div]
  exact mul_self_div_self a

name: zero_zpow_eq
statement: theorem zero_zpow_eq (n : ℤ) : (0 : G₀) ^ n = if n = 0 then 1 else 0
proof:
split_ifs with h
  · rw [h, zpow_zero]
  · rw [zero_zpow _ h]

name: div_mul_eq_mul_div₀
statement: theorem div_mul_eq_mul_div₀ (a b c : G₀) : a / c * b = a * b / c
proof: simp_rw [div_eq_mul_inv, mul_assoc, mul_comm c⁻¹]

name: div_sq_cancel
statement: theorem div_sq_cancel (a b : G₀) : a ^ 2 * b / a = a * b
proof:
obtain rfl | ha := eq_or_ne a 0
  · simp
  · rw [sq, mul_assoc, mul_div_cancel_left₀ _ ha]

end CommGroupWithZero

name: center_units_subset
statement: theorem center_units_subset  : center G₀ˣ ⊆ ((↑) : G₀ˣ → G₀) ⁻¹' center G₀
proof:
simp_rw [subset_def, mem_preimage, _root_.Semigroup.mem_center_iff]
  intro u hu a
  obtain rfl | ha := eq_or_ne a 0
  · rw [zero_mul, mul_zero]
  · exact congr_arg Units.val <| hu <| Units.mk0 a ha

/-- In a group with zero, the center of the units is the preimage of the center. -/

name: inv_mem_centralizer₀
statement: theorem inv_mem_centralizer₀ (ha : a ∈ centralizer s) : a⁻¹ ∈ centralizer s
proof:
obtain rfl | ha₀ := eq_or_ne a 0
  · rw [inv_zero]
    exact zero_mem_centralizer
  · rintro c hc
    rw [mul_inv_eq_iff_eq_mul₀ ha₀, mul_assoc, eq_inv_mul_iff_mul_eq₀ ha₀, ha c hc]

@[simp] lemma div_mem_centralizer₀ (ha : a ∈ centralizer s) (hb : b ∈ centralizer s) :
    a / b ∈ centralizer s := by
  simpa only [div_eq_mul_inv] using mul_mem_centralizer ha (inv_mem_centralizer₀ hb)

end GroupWithZero
end Set

name: cardinalMk_eq_lift
statement: theorem cardinalMk_eq_lift  : #(FreeAlgebra R X) = Cardinal.lift.{v} #R
proof:
have := lift_mk_eq'.2 ⟨show (FreeMonoid X →₀ R) ≃ R from Finsupp.uniqueEquiv 1⟩
  rw [lift_id'.{u, v}, lift_umax] at this
  rwa [equivMonoidAlgebraFreeMonoid.toEquiv.cardinal_eq, MonoidAlgebra]

@[nontriviality]

name: cardinalMk_eq_one
statement: theorem cardinalMk_eq_one  : #(FreeAlgebra R X) = 1
proof: rw [equivMonoidAlgebraFreeMonoid.toEquiv.cardinal_eq, MonoidAlgebra, mk_eq_one]

name: cardinalMk_le_max_lift
statement: theorem cardinalMk_le_max_lift  : #(FreeAlgebra R X) ≤ Cardinal.lift.{v} #R ⊔ Cardinal.lift.{u} #X ⊔ ℵ₀
proof:
cases subsingleton_or_nontrivial R
  · exact (cardinalMk_eq_one R X).trans_le (le_max_of_le_right one_le_aleph0)
  cases isEmpty_or_nonempty X
  · exact (cardinalMk_eq_lift R X).trans_le (le_max_of_le_left <| le_max_left _ _)
  · exact (cardinalMk_eq_max_lift R X).le

variable (X : Type u)

name: cardinalMk_eq
statement: theorem cardinalMk_eq  : #(FreeAlgebra R X) = #R
proof: simp

name: cardinalMk_le_max
statement: theorem cardinalMk_le_max  : #(FreeAlgebra R X) ≤ #R ⊔ #X ⊔ ℵ₀
proof:
simpa using cardinalMk_le_max_lift R X

end FreeAlgebra

namespace Algebra

name: dvd_mul_left
statement: theorem dvd_mul_left  : a ∣ u * b ↔ a ∣ b
proof:
rw [mul_comm]
  apply dvd_mul_right

/-- In a commutative monoid, an element `a` divides an element `b` iff all
  left associates of `a` divide `b`. -/

name: mul_left_dvd
statement: theorem mul_left_dvd  : ↑u * a ∣ b ↔ a ∣ b
proof:
rw [mul_comm]
  apply mul_right_dvd

end CommMonoid

end Units

namespace IsUnit

section Monoid

variable [Monoid α] {a b u : α}

/-- Units of a monoid divide any element of the monoid. -/
@[simp]

name: dvd
statement: theorem dvd (hu : IsUnit u) : u ∣ a
proof:
rcases hu with ⟨u, rfl⟩
  apply Units.coe_dvd

@[simp]

name: dvd_mul_right
statement: theorem dvd_mul_right (hu : IsUnit u) : a ∣ b * u ↔ a ∣ b
proof:
rcases hu with ⟨u, rfl⟩
  apply Units.dvd_mul_right

/-- In a monoid, an element a divides an element b iff all associates of `a` divide `b`. -/
@[simp]

name: mul_right_dvd
statement: theorem mul_right_dvd (hu : IsUnit u) : a * u ∣ b ↔ a ∣ b
proof:
rcases hu with ⟨u, rfl⟩
  apply Units.mul_right_dvd

name: dvd_mul_left
statement: theorem dvd_mul_left (hu : IsUnit u) : a ∣ u * b ↔ a ∣ b
proof:
rcases hu with ⟨u, rfl⟩
  apply Units.dvd_mul_left

/-- In a commutative monoid, an element `a` divides an element `b` iff all
  left associates of `a` divide `b`. -/
@[simp]

name: mul_left_dvd
statement: theorem mul_left_dvd (hu : IsUnit u) : u * a ∣ b ↔ a ∣ b
proof:
rcases hu with ⟨u, rfl⟩
  apply Units.mul_left_dvd

end CommMonoid

end IsUnit

section CommMonoid

variable [CommMonoid α]

name: isRelPrime_mul_unit_left_right
statement: theorem isRelPrime_mul_unit_left_right  : IsRelPrime y (x * z) ↔ IsRelPrime y z
proof: rw [isRelPrime_comm, isRelPrime_mul_unit_left_left hu, isRelPrime_comm]

name: isRelPrime_mul_unit_left
statement: theorem isRelPrime_mul_unit_left  : IsRelPrime (x * y) (x * z) ↔ IsRelPrime y z
proof: rw [isRelPrime_mul_unit_left_left hu, isRelPrime_mul_unit_left_right hu]

name: isRelPrime_mul_unit_right_left
statement: theorem isRelPrime_mul_unit_right_left  : IsRelPrime (y * x) z ↔ IsRelPrime y z
proof: rw [mul_comm, isRelPrime_mul_unit_left_left hu]

name: isRelPrime_mul_unit_right_right
statement: theorem isRelPrime_mul_unit_right_right  : IsRelPrime y (z * x) ↔ IsRelPrime y z
proof: rw [mul_comm, isRelPrime_mul_unit_left_right hu]

name: isRelPrime_mul_unit_right
statement: theorem isRelPrime_mul_unit_right  : IsRelPrime (y * x) (z * x) ↔ IsRelPrime y z
proof:
rw [isRelPrime_mul_unit_right_left hu, isRelPrime_mul_unit_right_right hu]

end IsUnit

name: algebraMap_mono
statement: theorem algebraMap_mono  : Monotone (algebraMap α β)
proof: simpa [Algebra.smul_def] using smul_one_mono (α := α) β

name: algebraMap_strictMono
statement: theorem algebraMap_strictMono  : StrictMono (algebraMap α β)
proof: simpa [Algebra.smul_def] using smul_one_strictMono (α := α) β

name: neg_add_cancel_of_ne_top
statement: theorem neg_add_cancel_of_ne_top (ha : a ≠ ⊤) : -a + a = 0
proof: simp [add_comm, add_neg_cancel_of_ne_top ha]

name: top_ne_zero
statement: theorem top_ne_zero  : (⊤ : α) ≠ 0
proof:
intro h
  obtain ⟨a, ha⟩ := exists_ne (0 : α)
  rw [← zero_add a] at ha
  simp [LinearOrderedAddCommGroupWithTop.top_add', -zero_add, ← h] at ha

@[simp] lemma zero_ne_top : 0 ≠ (⊤ : α) := top_ne_zero.symm

@[simp] lemma top_pos : (0 : α) < ⊤ := lt_top_iff_ne_top.2 top_ne_zero.symm

@[simp] lemma isAddUnit_iff : IsAddUnit a ↔ a ≠ ⊤ where
  mp := by rintro ⟨⟨b, c, hbc, -⟩, rfl⟩ rfl; simp [LinearOrderedAddCommGroupWithTop.top_add'] at hbc
  mpr ha := .of_add_eq_zero (-a) <| by simp [ha, add_neg_cancel_of_ne_top]

instance : LinearOrderedAddCommMonoidWithTop α where
  top_add' := LinearOrderedAddCommGroupWithTop.top_add'
  isAddLeftRegular_of_ne_top _a ha := (isAddUnit_iff.2 ha).isAddRegular.1

name: add_eq_top
statement: theorem add_eq_top  : a + b = ⊤ ↔ a = ⊤ ∨ b = ⊤
proof:
rw [← not_iff_not, not_or]; exact add_ne_top

@[simp] lemma add_lt_top : a + b < ⊤ ↔ a < ⊤ ∧ b < ⊤ := by simp [lt_top_iff_ne_top]

@[simp] lemma neg_eq_top : -a = ⊤ ↔ a = ⊤ where
  mp h := by simpa [h] using add_neg_cancel_of_ne_top (x := a)
  mpr h := by simp [h]

@[simp] lemma sub_top : a - ⊤ = ⊤ := by simp [sub_eq_add_neg]

instance (priority := 100) toSubtractionMonoid : SubtractionMonoid α where
  neg_neg a := by
    obtain rfl | ha := eq_or_ne a ⊤
    · simp
    · apply left_neg_eq_right_neg (a := -a) <;> simp [add_comm, add_neg_cancel_of_ne_top, ha]
  neg_add_rev a b := by
    obtain rfl | ha := eq_or_ne a ⊤
    · simp
    obtain rfl | hb := eq_or_ne b ⊤
    · simp
    · exact left_neg_eq_right_neg (a := a + b) (by simp [neg_add_cancel_of_ne_top, *])
        (by simp [add_assoc, add_neg_cancel_of_ne_top, add_neg_cancel_left_of_ne_top, *])
  neg_eq_of_add a b h := by
    have ha : a ≠ ⊤ := by rintro rfl; simp at h
    exact left_neg_eq_right_neg (a := a) (by simp [neg_add_cancel_of_ne_top, *]) h

@[deprecated (since := "2025-12-27")]
alias injective_add_left_of_ne_top := add_left_injective_of_ne_top

@[deprecated (since := "2025-12-27")]
alias injective_add_right_of_ne_top := add_right_injective_of_ne_top

name: sub_left_injective_of_ne_top
statement: theorem sub_left_injective_of_ne_top (h : b ≠ ⊤) : Function.Injective fun x ↦ x - b
proof: simpa [sub_eq_add_neg] using add_left_injective_of_ne_top (-b) (by simpa)

name: sub_right_injective_of_ne_top
statement: theorem sub_right_injective_of_ne_top (h : b ≠ ⊤) : Function.Injective fun x ↦ b - x
proof:
simpa [sub_eq_add_neg] using (add_right_injective_of_ne_top b h).comp neg_injective

@[simp]

name: sub_left_strictMono_of_ne_top
statement: theorem sub_left_strictMono_of_ne_top (h : b ≠ ⊤) : StrictMono fun x ↦ x - b
proof:
simpa [sub_eq_add_neg] using add_left_strictMono_of_ne_top (b := -b) (by simpa)

@[simp]

name: sub_self_eq_zero_iff_ne_top
statement: theorem sub_self_eq_zero_iff_ne_top  : a - a = 0 ↔ a ≠ ⊤
proof:
rw [sub_eq_add_neg, add_neg_cancel_iff_ne_top]

alias ⟨_, sub_self_eq_zero_of_ne_top⟩ := sub_self_eq_zero_iff_ne_top

name: sub_pos
statement: theorem sub_pos  : 0 < a - b ↔ b < a ∨ b = ⊤
proof:
obtain rfl | hb := eq_or_ne b ⊤
  · simp
  · simp [← sub_self_eq_zero_of_ne_top hb, hb]

@[simp]

name: neg_pos
statement: theorem neg_pos  : 0 < -a ↔ a < 0 ∨ a = ⊤
proof:
simpa using sub_pos (a := 0) (b := a)

@[simp]

name: sub_self_nonneg
statement: theorem sub_self_nonneg  : 0 ≤ a - a
proof:
obtain rfl | ha := eq_or_ne a ⊤
  · simp
  · rw [sub_self_eq_zero_of_ne_top ha]

@[simp]

name: sub_eq_zero
statement: theorem sub_eq_zero (ha : a ≠ ⊤) : b - a = 0 ↔ b = a
proof:
rw [← sub_self_eq_zero_of_ne_top ha, sub_left_inj_of_ne_top ha]

end LinearOrderedAddCommGroupWithTop

namespace WithTop

instance linearOrderedAddCommMonoidWithTop [AddCancelCommMonoid α] [LinearOrder α]
    [IsOrderedAddMonoid α] : LinearOrderedAddCommMonoidWithTop (WithTop α) where
  top_add' := WithTop.top_add
  isAddLeftRegular_of_ne_top _a ha _b _c := WithTop.add_left_cancel ha

namespace LinearOrderedAddCommGroup
variable [AddCommGroup G] {x y : WithTop G}

instance instNeg : Neg (WithTop G) where
  neg := .map fun a ↦ -a

/-- If `G` has subtraction, we can extend the subtraction to `WithTop G`, by setting `x - ⊤ = ⊤` and
`⊤ - x = ⊤`. This definition is only registered as an instance on additive commutative groups, to
avoid conflicting with the instance `WithTop.instSub` on types with a bottom element. -/
instance instSub : Sub (WithTop G) where
  sub
  | _, ⊤ => ⊤
  | ⊤, (b : G) => ⊤
  | (a : G), (b : G) => (a - b : G)

@[simp, norm_cast] lemma coe_neg (a : G) : (↑(-a) : WithTop G) = -a := rfl
@[simp, norm_cast] lemma coe_sub (a b : G) : (↑(a - b) : WithTop G) = ↑a - ↑b := rfl

@[simp] lemma neg_top : -(⊤ : WithTop G) = ⊤ := rfl

@[simp] lemma top_sub (x : WithTop G) : ⊤ - x = ⊤ := by cases x <;> rfl
@[simp] lemma sub_top (x : WithTop G) : x - ⊤ = ⊤ := by cases x <;> rfl

@[simp] lemma sub_eq_top_iff : x - y = ⊤ ↔ x = ⊤ ∨ y = ⊤ := by
  cases x <;> cases y <;> simp [← coe_sub]

instance [LinearOrder G] [IsOrderedAddMonoid G] : LinearOrderedAddCommGroupWithTop (WithTop G) where
  __ := WithTop.linearOrderedAddCommMonoidWithTop
  sub_eq_add_neg a b := by cases a <;> cases b <;> simp [← coe_sub, ← coe_neg, sub_eq_add_neg]
  neg_top := WithTop.map_top _
  zsmul := zsmulRec
  add_neg_cancel_of_ne_top | (a : G), _ => mod_cast add_neg_cancel a

end WithTop.LinearOrderedAddCommGroup

name: mul_iSup_distrib
statement: theorem mul_iSup_distrib  : x * ⨆ i, f i = ⨆ i, x * f i
proof:
rw [iSup, mul_sSup_distrib, iSup_range]

@[to_additive]

name: iSup_mul_distrib
statement: theorem iSup_mul_distrib  : (⨆ i, f i) * x = ⨆ i, f i * x
proof:
rw [iSup, sSup_mul_distrib, iSup_range]

@[to_additive]

name: mul_sup_distrib
statement: theorem mul_sup_distrib  : x * (y ⊔ z) = (x * y) ⊔ (x * z)
proof:
rw [← iSup_pair, ← sSup_pair, mul_sSup_distrib]

@[to_additive]

name: sup_mul_distrib
statement: theorem sup_mul_distrib  : (x ⊔ y) * z = (x * z) ⊔ (y * z)
proof:
rw [← (@iSup_pair _ _ _ (fun _? => _? * z) _ _), ← sSup_pair, sSup_mul_distrib]

@[to_additive]
instance : MulLeftMono α where
  elim := by
    intro _ _ _; simp only; intro
    rwa [← left_eq_sup, ← mul_sup_distrib, sup_of_le_left]

@[to_additive]
instance : MulRightMono α where
  elim := by
    intro _ _ _; simp only; intro
    rwa [← left_eq_sup, ← sup_mul_distrib, sup_of_le_left]

@[to_additive]

name: bot_mul
statement: theorem bot_mul  : ⊥ * x = ⊥
proof:
rw [← sSup_empty, sSup_mul_distrib]
  simp only [Set.mem_empty_iff_false, not_false_eq_true, iSup_neg, iSup_bot, sSup_empty]

@[to_additive (attr := simp)]

name: mul_bot
statement: theorem mul_bot  : x * ⊥ = ⊥
proof:
rw [← sSup_empty, mul_sSup_distrib]
  simp only [Set.mem_empty_iff_false, not_false_eq_true, iSup_neg, iSup_bot, sSup_empty]

end Zero

end Quantale

name: cutMap_self
statement: theorem cutMap_self (a : α) : cutMap α a = Iio a ∩ range (Rat.cast : ℚ → α)
proof:
grind [mem_cutMap_iff]

end DivisionRing

variable (β) [IsStrictOrderedRing α] [Field β] [LinearOrder β] [IsStrictOrderedRing β]
  {a a₁ a₂ : α} {b : β} {q : ℚ}

name: cutMap_coe
statement: theorem cutMap_coe (q : ℚ) : cutMap β (q : α) = Rat.cast '' {r : ℚ | (r : β) < q}
proof:
simp_rw [cutMap, Rat.cast_lt]

variable [Archimedean α]

omit [LinearOrder β] [IsStrictOrderedRing β] in

name: cutMap_bddAbove
statement: theorem cutMap_bddAbove (a : α) : BddAbove (cutMap β a)
proof:
obtain ⟨q, hq⟩ := exists_rat_gt a
  exact ⟨q, forall_mem_image.2 fun r hr => mod_cast (hq.trans' hr).le⟩

name: cutMap_add
statement: theorem cutMap_add (a b : α) : cutMap β (a + b) = cutMap β a + cutMap β b
proof:
refine (image_subset_iff.2 fun q hq => ?_).antisymm ?_
  · rw [mem_setOf_eq, ← sub_lt_iff_lt_add] at hq
    obtain ⟨q₁, hq₁q, hq₁ab⟩ := exists_rat_btwn hq
    refine ⟨q₁, by rwa [coe_mem_cutMap_iff], q - q₁, ?_, add_sub_cancel _ _⟩
    norm_cast
    rw [coe_mem_cutMap_iff]
    exact mod_cast sub_lt_comm.mp hq₁q
  · rintro _ ⟨_, ⟨qa, ha, rfl⟩, _, ⟨qb, hb, rfl⟩, rfl⟩
    -- After https://github.com/leanprover/lean4/pull/2734, `norm_cast` needs help with beta reduction.
    refine ⟨qa + qb, ?_, by beta_reduce; norm_cast⟩
    rw [mem_setOf_eq, cast_add]
    exact add_lt_add ha hb

end CutMap

end LinearOrderedField

namespace ConditionallyCompleteLinearOrderedField

open LinearOrderedField

/-!
### Induced map

`LinearOrderedField.cutMap` spits out a `Set β`. To get something in `β`, we now take the supremum.
-/


section InducedMap

variable (α β γ) [Field α] [LinearOrder α] [IsStrictOrderedRing α]
  [Field β] [ConditionallyCompleteLinearOrder β] [IsStrictOrderedRing β]
  [Field γ] [ConditionallyCompleteLinearOrder γ] [IsStrictOrderedRing γ]

/-- The induced order-preserving function from a linear ordered field to a conditionally complete
linear ordered field, defined by taking the Sup in the codomain of all the rationals less than the
input. -/

name: inducedMap_rat
statement: theorem inducedMap_rat (q : ℚ) : inducedMap α β (q : α) = q
proof:
refine csSup_eq_of_forall_le_of_forall_lt_exists_gt
    (cutMap_nonempty β (q : α)) (fun x h => ?_) fun w h => ?_
  · rw [cutMap_coe] at h
    obtain ⟨r, h, rfl⟩ := h
    exact le_of_lt h
  · obtain ⟨q', hwq, hq⟩ := exists_rat_btwn h
    rw [cutMap_coe]
    exact ⟨q', ⟨_, hq, rfl⟩, hwq⟩

@[simp]

name: coe_lt_inducedMap_iff
statement: theorem coe_lt_inducedMap_iff  : (q : β) < inducedMap α β a ↔ (q : α) < a
proof:
refine ⟨fun h => ?_, fun hq => ?_⟩
  · rw [← inducedMap_rat α] at h
    exact (inducedMap_mono α β).reflect_lt h
  · obtain ⟨q', hq, hqa⟩ := exists_rat_btwn hq
    apply lt_csSup_of_lt (cutMap_bddAbove β a) (coe_mem_cutMap_iff.mpr hqa)
    exact mod_cast hq

name: inducedMap_inv_self
statement: theorem inducedMap_inv_self (b : β) : inducedMap γ β (inducedMap β γ b) = b
proof: rw [inducedMap_inducedMap, inducedMap_self]

name: inducedMap_add
statement: theorem inducedMap_add (x y : α) : inducedMap α β (x + y) = inducedMap α β x + inducedMap α β y
proof:
rw [inducedMap, cutMap_add]
  exact csSup_add (cutMap_nonempty β x) (cutMap_bddAbove β x) (cutMap_nonempty β y)
    (cutMap_bddAbove β y)

variable {α β}

/-- Preparatory lemma for `inducedOrderRingHom`. -/

name: add_one_le_of_lt
statement: theorem add_one_le_of_lt (h : x < y) : x + 1 ≤ y
proof:
rw [← succ_eq_add_one]
  exact succ_le_of_lt h

name: add_one_le_iff_of_not_isMax
statement: theorem add_one_le_iff_of_not_isMax (hx : ¬ IsMax x) : x + 1 ≤ y ↔ x < y
proof:
rw [← succ_eq_add_one, succ_le_iff_of_not_isMax hx]

@[simp]

name: wcovBy_add_one
statement: theorem wcovBy_add_one (x : α) : x ⩿ x + 1
proof:
rw [← succ_eq_add_one]
  exact wcovBy_succ x

@[simp]

name: le_sub_one_of_lt
statement: theorem le_sub_one_of_lt (h : x < y) : x ≤ y - 1
proof:
rw [← pred_eq_sub_one]
  exact le_pred_of_lt h

name: le_sub_one_iff_of_not_isMin
statement: theorem le_sub_one_iff_of_not_isMin (hy : ¬ IsMin y) : x ≤ y - 1 ↔ x < y
proof:
rw [← pred_eq_sub_one, le_pred_iff_of_not_isMin hy]

@[simp]

name: sub_one_wcovBy
statement: theorem sub_one_wcovBy (x : α) : x - 1 ⩿ x
proof:
rw [← pred_eq_sub_one]
  exact pred_wcovBy x

@[simp]

name: le_of_lt_add_one
statement: theorem le_of_lt_add_one (h : x < y + 1) : x ≤ y
proof:
rw [← succ_eq_add_one] at h
  exact le_of_lt_succ h

name: lt_add_one_iff_of_not_isMax
statement: theorem lt_add_one_iff_of_not_isMax (hy : ¬ IsMax y) : x < y + 1 ↔ x ≤ y
proof:
rw [← succ_eq_add_one, lt_succ_iff_of_not_isMax hy]

@[simp]

name: add_one_inj
statement: theorem add_one_inj  : x + 1 = y + 1 ↔ x = y
proof:
simp [← succ_eq_add_one]

end Add

section AddMonoidWithOne
variable [AddMonoidWithOne α] [NoMaxOrder α] [SuccAddOrder α]

@[simp]

name: lt_one_iff
statement: theorem lt_one_iff  : x < 1 ↔ x = 0
proof: rw [← zero_add 1, lt_add_one_iff, nonpos_iff_eq_zero]

name: le_one_iff
statement: theorem le_one_iff  : x ≤ 1 ↔ x = 0 ∨ x = 1
proof:
rw [le_iff_lt_or_eq, lt_one_iff]

@[simp]

name: Iio_one
statement: theorem Iio_one  : Set.Iio (1 : α) = {0}
proof: ext; simp

name: Iic_one
statement: theorem Iic_one  : Set.Iic (1 : α) = {0, 1}
proof:
ext; simp [le_one_iff]

@[simp]

name: lt_two_iff
statement: theorem lt_two_iff  : x < 2 ↔ x ≤ 1
proof: rw [← one_add_one_eq_two, lt_add_one_iff]

name: le_two_iff
statement: theorem le_two_iff  : x ≤ 2 ↔ x = 0 ∨ x = 1 ∨ x = 2
proof: rw [le_iff_lt_or_eq, lt_two_iff, le_one_iff, or_assoc]

name: Iio_two
statement: theorem Iio_two  : Set.Iio (2 : α) = {0, 1}
proof: ext; simp [le_one_iff]

name: Iic_two
statement: theorem Iic_two  : Set.Iic (2 : α) = {0, 1, 2}
proof:
ext; simp [le_two_iff]

end AddMonoidWithOne

section Sub

variable [Sub α] [One α] [PredSubOrder α]

name: le_of_sub_one_lt
statement: theorem le_of_sub_one_lt (h : x - 1 < y) : x ≤ y
proof:
rw [← pred_eq_sub_one] at h
  exact le_of_pred_lt h

name: sub_one_lt_iff_of_not_isMin
statement: theorem sub_one_lt_iff_of_not_isMin (hx : ¬ IsMin x) : x - 1 < y ↔ x ≤ y
proof:
rw [← pred_eq_sub_one, pred_lt_iff_of_not_isMin hx]

@[simp]

name: monotoneOn_of_le_add_one
statement: theorem monotoneOn_of_le_add_one (hs : s.OrdConnected) : (∀ a, ¬ IsMax a → a ∈ s → a + 1 ∈ s → f a ≤ f (a + 1)) → MonotoneOn f s
proof: simpa [Order.succ_eq_add_one] using monotoneOn_of_le_succ hs (f := f)

name: antitoneOn_of_add_one_le
statement: theorem antitoneOn_of_add_one_le (hs : s.OrdConnected) : (∀ a, ¬ IsMax a → a ∈ s → a + 1 ∈ s → f (a + 1) ≤ f a) → AntitoneOn f s
proof: simpa [Order.succ_eq_add_one] using antitoneOn_of_succ_le hs (f := f)

name: strictMonoOn_of_lt_add_one
statement: theorem strictMonoOn_of_lt_add_one (hs : s.OrdConnected) : (∀ a, ¬ IsMax a → a ∈ s → a + 1 ∈ s → f a < f (a + 1)) → StrictMonoOn f s
proof: simpa [Order.succ_eq_add_one] using strictMonoOn_of_lt_succ hs (f := f)
