name: strictAntiOn_of_add_one_lt
statement: theorem strictAntiOn_of_add_one_lt (hs : s.OrdConnected) : (∀ a, ¬ IsMax a → a ∈ s → a + 1 ∈ s → f (a + 1) < f a) → StrictAntiOn f s
proof: simpa [Order.succ_eq_add_one] using strictAntiOn_of_succ_lt hs (f := f)

name: monotone_of_le_add_one
statement: theorem monotone_of_le_add_one  : (∀ a, ¬ IsMax a → f a ≤ f (a + 1)) → Monotone f
proof: simpa [Order.succ_eq_add_one] using monotone_of_le_succ (f := f)

name: antitone_of_add_one_le
statement: theorem antitone_of_add_one_le  : (∀ a, ¬ IsMax a → f (a + 1) ≤ f a) → Antitone f
proof: simpa [Order.succ_eq_add_one] using antitone_of_succ_le (f := f)

name: strictMono_of_lt_add_one
statement: theorem strictMono_of_lt_add_one  : (∀ a, ¬ IsMax a → f a < f (a + 1)) → StrictMono f
proof: simpa [Order.succ_eq_add_one] using strictMono_of_lt_succ (f := f)

name: strictAnti_of_add_one_lt
statement: theorem strictAnti_of_add_one_lt  : (∀ a, ¬ IsMax a → f (a + 1) < f a) → StrictAnti f
proof:
simpa [Order.succ_eq_add_one] using strictAnti_of_succ_lt (f := f)

end SuccAddOrder

section PredSubOrder
variable [Sub α] [One α] [PredSubOrder α] [IsPredArchimedean α] {s : Set α} {f : α → β}

name: monotoneOn_of_sub_one_le
statement: theorem monotoneOn_of_sub_one_le (hs : s.OrdConnected) : (∀ a, ¬ IsMin a → a ∈ s → a - 1 ∈ s → f (a - 1) ≤ f a) → MonotoneOn f s
proof: simpa [Order.pred_eq_sub_one] using monotoneOn_of_pred_le hs (f := f)

name: antitoneOn_of_le_sub_one
statement: theorem antitoneOn_of_le_sub_one (hs : s.OrdConnected) : (∀ a, ¬ IsMin a → a ∈ s → a - 1 ∈ s → f a ≤ f (a - 1)) → AntitoneOn f s
proof: simpa [Order.pred_eq_sub_one] using antitoneOn_of_le_pred hs (f := f)

name: strictMonoOn_of_sub_one_lt
statement: theorem strictMonoOn_of_sub_one_lt (hs : s.OrdConnected) : (∀ a, ¬ IsMin a → a ∈ s → a - 1 ∈ s → f (a - 1) < f a) → StrictMonoOn f s
proof: simpa [Order.pred_eq_sub_one] using strictMonoOn_of_pred_lt hs (f := f)

name: strictAntiOn_of_lt_sub_one
statement: theorem strictAntiOn_of_lt_sub_one (hs : s.OrdConnected) : (∀ a, ¬ IsMin a → a ∈ s → a - 1 ∈ s → f a < f (a - 1)) → StrictAntiOn f s
proof: simpa [Order.pred_eq_sub_one] using strictAntiOn_of_lt_pred hs (f := f)

name: monotone_of_sub_one_le
statement: theorem monotone_of_sub_one_le  : (∀ a, ¬ IsMin a → f (a - 1) ≤ f a) → Monotone f
proof: simpa [Order.pred_eq_sub_one] using monotone_of_pred_le (f := f)

name: antitone_of_le_sub_one
statement: theorem antitone_of_le_sub_one  : (∀ a, ¬ IsMin a → f a ≤ f (a - 1)) → Antitone f
proof: simpa [Order.pred_eq_sub_one] using antitone_of_le_pred (f := f)

name: strictMono_of_sub_one_lt
statement: theorem strictMono_of_sub_one_lt  : (∀ a, ¬ IsMin a → f (a - 1) < f a) → StrictMono f
proof: simpa [Order.pred_eq_sub_one] using strictMono_of_pred_lt (f := f)

name: strictAnti_of_lt_sub_one
statement: theorem strictAnti_of_lt_sub_one  : (∀ a, ¬ IsMin a → f a < f (a - 1)) → StrictAnti f
proof:
simpa [Order.pred_eq_sub_one] using strictAnti_of_lt_pred (f := f)

end PredSubOrder
end Monotone

name: toIcoDiv_zsmul_sub_self
statement: theorem toIcoDiv_zsmul_sub_self (a b : α) : toIcoDiv hp a b • p - b = -toIcoMod hp a b
proof:
rw [toIcoMod, neg_sub]

@[simp]

name: toIocDiv_zsmul_sub_self
statement: theorem toIocDiv_zsmul_sub_self (a b : α) : toIocDiv hp a b • p - b = -toIocMod hp a b
proof:
rw [toIocMod, neg_sub]

@[simp]

name: toIcoMod_sub_self
statement: theorem toIcoMod_sub_self (a b : α) : toIcoMod hp a b - b = -toIcoDiv hp a b • p
proof:
rw [toIcoMod, sub_sub_cancel_left, neg_smul]

@[simp]

name: toIocMod_sub_self
statement: theorem toIocMod_sub_self (a b : α) : toIocMod hp a b - b = -toIocDiv hp a b • p
proof:
rw [toIocMod, sub_sub_cancel_left, neg_smul]

@[simp]

name: self_sub_toIcoMod
statement: theorem self_sub_toIcoMod (a b : α) : b - toIcoMod hp a b = toIcoDiv hp a b • p
proof:
rw [toIcoMod, sub_sub_cancel]

@[simp]

name: self_sub_toIocMod
statement: theorem self_sub_toIocMod (a b : α) : b - toIocMod hp a b = toIocDiv hp a b • p
proof:
rw [toIocMod, sub_sub_cancel]

@[simp]

name: toIcoMod_add_toIcoDiv_zsmul
statement: theorem toIcoMod_add_toIcoDiv_zsmul (a b : α) : toIcoMod hp a b + toIcoDiv hp a b • p = b
proof:
rw [toIcoMod, sub_add_cancel]

@[simp]

name: toIocMod_add_toIocDiv_zsmul
statement: theorem toIocMod_add_toIocDiv_zsmul (a b : α) : toIocMod hp a b + toIocDiv hp a b • p = b
proof:
rw [toIocMod, sub_add_cancel]

@[simp]

name: toIcoDiv_zsmul_sub_toIcoMod
statement: theorem toIcoDiv_zsmul_sub_toIcoMod (a b : α) : toIcoDiv hp a b • p + toIcoMod hp a b = b
proof:
rw [add_comm, toIcoMod_add_toIcoDiv_zsmul]

@[simp]

name: toIocDiv_zsmul_sub_toIocMod
statement: theorem toIocDiv_zsmul_sub_toIocMod (a b : α) : toIocDiv hp a b • p + toIocMod hp a b = b
proof: rw [add_comm, toIocMod_add_toIocDiv_zsmul]

name: toIcoMod_eq_iff
statement: theorem toIcoMod_eq_iff  : toIcoMod hp a b = c ↔ c ∈ Set.Ico a (a + p) ∧ ∃ z : ℤ, b = c + z • p
proof:
refine
    ⟨fun h =>
      ⟨h ▸ toIcoMod_mem_Ico hp a b, toIcoDiv hp a b, h ▸ (toIcoMod_add_toIcoDiv_zsmul _ _ _).symm⟩,
      ?_⟩
  simp_rw [← @sub_eq_iff_eq_add]
  rintro ⟨hc, n, rfl⟩
  rw [← toIcoDiv_eq_of_sub_zsmul_mem_Ico hp hc, toIcoMod]

name: toIocMod_eq_iff
statement: theorem toIocMod_eq_iff  : toIocMod hp a b = c ↔ c ∈ Set.Ioc a (a + p) ∧ ∃ z : ℤ, b = c + z • p
proof:
refine
    ⟨fun h =>
      ⟨h ▸ toIocMod_mem_Ioc hp a b, toIocDiv hp a b, h ▸ (toIocMod_add_toIocDiv_zsmul hp _ _).symm⟩,
      ?_⟩
  simp_rw [← @sub_eq_iff_eq_add]
  rintro ⟨hc, n, rfl⟩
  rw [← toIocDiv_eq_of_sub_zsmul_mem_Ioc hp hc, toIocMod]

@[simp]

name: toIcoMod_apply_left
statement: theorem toIcoMod_apply_left (a : α) : toIcoMod hp a a = a
proof:
rw [toIcoMod_eq_iff hp, Set.left_mem_Ico]
  exact ⟨lt_add_of_pos_right _ hp, 0, by simp⟩

@[simp]

name: toIocMod_apply_left
statement: theorem toIocMod_apply_left (a : α) : toIocMod hp a a = a + p
proof:
rw [toIocMod_eq_iff hp, Set.right_mem_Ioc]
  exact ⟨lt_add_of_pos_right _ hp, -1, by simp⟩

name: toIcoMod_apply_right
statement: theorem toIcoMod_apply_right (a : α) : toIcoMod hp a (a + p) = a
proof:
rw [toIcoMod_eq_iff hp, Set.left_mem_Ico]
  exact ⟨lt_add_of_pos_right _ hp, 1, by simp⟩

name: toIocMod_apply_right
statement: theorem toIocMod_apply_right (a : α) : toIocMod hp a (a + p) = a + p
proof:
rw [toIocMod_eq_iff hp, Set.right_mem_Ioc]
  exact ⟨lt_add_of_pos_right _ hp, 0, by simp⟩

@[simp]

name: toIcoDiv_add_right
statement: theorem toIcoDiv_add_right (a b : α) : toIcoDiv hp a (b + p) = toIcoDiv hp a b + 1
proof:
simpa only [one_zsmul] using toIcoDiv_add_zsmul hp a b 1

@[simp]

name: toIocDiv_add_right
statement: theorem toIocDiv_add_right (a b : α) : toIocDiv hp a (b + p) = toIocDiv hp a b + 1
proof:
simpa only [one_zsmul] using toIocDiv_add_zsmul hp a b 1

@[simp]

name: toIcoDiv_add_left
statement: theorem toIcoDiv_add_left (a b : α) : toIcoDiv hp a (p + b) = toIcoDiv hp a b + 1
proof:
rw [add_comm, toIcoDiv_add_right]

@[simp]

name: toIocDiv_add_left
statement: theorem toIocDiv_add_left (a b : α) : toIocDiv hp a (p + b) = toIocDiv hp a b + 1
proof:
rw [add_comm, toIocDiv_add_right]

@[simp]

name: toIcoDiv_sub
statement: theorem toIcoDiv_sub (a b : α) : toIcoDiv hp a (b - p) = toIcoDiv hp a b - 1
proof:
simpa only [one_zsmul] using toIcoDiv_sub_zsmul hp a b 1

@[simp]

name: toIocDiv_sub
statement: theorem toIocDiv_sub (a b : α) : toIocDiv hp a (b - p) = toIocDiv hp a b - 1
proof:
simpa only [one_zsmul] using toIocDiv_sub_zsmul hp a b 1

@[simp]

name: toIcoDiv_sub_eq_toIcoDiv_add
statement: theorem toIcoDiv_sub_eq_toIcoDiv_add (a b c : α) : toIcoDiv hp a (b - c) = toIcoDiv hp (a + c) b
proof:
apply toIcoDiv_eq_of_sub_zsmul_mem_Ico
  rw [← sub_right_comm, Set.sub_mem_Ico_iff_left, add_right_comm]
  exact sub_toIcoDiv_zsmul_mem_Ico hp (a + c) b

name: toIocDiv_sub_eq_toIocDiv_add
statement: theorem toIocDiv_sub_eq_toIocDiv_add (a b c : α) : toIocDiv hp a (b - c) = toIocDiv hp (a + c) b
proof:
apply toIocDiv_eq_of_sub_zsmul_mem_Ioc
  rw [← sub_right_comm, Set.sub_mem_Ioc_iff_left, add_right_comm]
  exact sub_toIocDiv_zsmul_mem_Ioc hp (a + c) b

name: toIcoDiv_neg
statement: theorem toIcoDiv_neg (a b : α) : toIcoDiv hp a (-b) = -(toIocDiv hp (-a) b + 1)
proof:
suffices toIcoDiv hp a (-b) = -toIocDiv hp (-(a + p)) b by
    rwa [neg_add, ← sub_eq_add_neg, toIocDiv_sub_eq_toIocDiv_add', toIocDiv_add_right] at this
  rw [← neg_eq_iff_eq_neg, eq_comm]
  apply toIocDiv_eq_of_sub_zsmul_mem_Ioc
  obtain ⟨hc, ho⟩ := sub_toIcoDiv_zsmul_mem_Ico hp a (-b)
  rw [← neg_lt_neg_iff, neg_sub' (-b), neg_neg, ← neg_smul] at ho
  rw [← neg_le_neg_iff, neg_sub' (-b), neg_neg, ← neg_smul] at hc
  refine ⟨ho, hc.trans_eq ?_⟩
  rw [neg_add, neg_add_cancel_right]

name: toIocDiv_neg
statement: theorem toIocDiv_neg (a b : α) : toIocDiv hp a (-b) = -(toIcoDiv hp (-a) b + 1)
proof: rw [← neg_neg b, toIcoDiv_neg, neg_neg, neg_neg, neg_add', neg_neg, add_sub_cancel_right]

name: toIcoMod_add_right
statement: theorem toIcoMod_add_right (a b : α) : toIcoMod hp a (b + p) = toIcoMod hp a b
proof:
simpa only [one_zsmul] using toIcoMod_add_zsmul hp a b 1

@[simp]

name: toIocMod_add_right
statement: theorem toIocMod_add_right (a b : α) : toIocMod hp a (b + p) = toIocMod hp a b
proof:
simpa only [one_zsmul] using toIocMod_add_zsmul hp a b 1

@[simp]

name: toIcoMod_add_left
statement: theorem toIcoMod_add_left (a b : α) : toIcoMod hp a (p + b) = toIcoMod hp a b
proof:
rw [add_comm, toIcoMod_add_right]

@[simp]

name: toIocMod_add_left
statement: theorem toIocMod_add_left (a b : α) : toIocMod hp a (p + b) = toIocMod hp a b
proof:
rw [add_comm, toIocMod_add_right]

@[simp]

name: toIcoMod_sub
statement: theorem toIcoMod_sub (a b : α) : toIcoMod hp a (b - p) = toIcoMod hp a b
proof:
simpa only [one_zsmul] using toIcoMod_sub_zsmul hp a b 1

@[simp]

name: toIocMod_sub
statement: theorem toIocMod_sub (a b : α) : toIocMod hp a (b - p) = toIocMod hp a b
proof:
simpa only [one_zsmul] using toIocMod_sub_zsmul hp a b 1

@[simp]

name: toIcoMod_sub_eq_sub
statement: theorem toIcoMod_sub_eq_sub (a b c : α) : toIcoMod hp a (b - c) = toIcoMod hp (a + c) b - c
proof: simp_rw [toIcoMod, toIcoDiv_sub_eq_toIcoDiv_add, sub_right_comm]

name: toIocMod_sub_eq_sub
statement: theorem toIocMod_sub_eq_sub (a b c : α) : toIocMod hp a (b - c) = toIocMod hp (a + c) b - c
proof: simp_rw [toIocMod, toIocDiv_sub_eq_toIocDiv_add, sub_right_comm]

name: toIcoMod_add_right_eq_add
statement: theorem toIcoMod_add_right_eq_add (a b c : α) : toIcoMod hp a (b + c) = toIcoMod hp (a - c) b + c
proof: simp_rw [toIcoMod, toIcoDiv_sub_eq_toIcoDiv_add', sub_add_eq_add_sub]

name: toIocMod_add_right_eq_add
statement: theorem toIocMod_add_right_eq_add (a b c : α) : toIocMod hp a (b + c) = toIocMod hp (a - c) b + c
proof: simp_rw [toIocMod, toIocDiv_sub_eq_toIocDiv_add', sub_add_eq_add_sub]

name: toIcoMod_neg
statement: theorem toIcoMod_neg (a b : α) : toIcoMod hp a (-b) = p - toIocMod hp (-a) b
proof:
simp_rw [toIcoMod, toIocMod, toIcoDiv_neg, neg_smul, add_smul]
  abel

name: toIocMod_neg
statement: theorem toIocMod_neg (a b : α) : toIocMod hp a (-b) = p - toIcoMod hp (-a) b
proof:
simp_rw [toIocMod, toIcoMod, toIocDiv_neg, neg_smul, add_smul]
  abel

name: toIcoMod_eq_toIcoMod
statement: theorem toIcoMod_eq_toIcoMod  : toIcoMod hp a b = toIcoMod hp a c ↔ ∃ n : ℤ, c - b = n • p
proof:
refine ⟨fun h => ⟨toIcoDiv hp a c - toIcoDiv hp a b, ?_⟩, fun h => ?_⟩
  · conv_lhs => rw [← toIcoMod_add_toIcoDiv_zsmul hp a b, ← toIcoMod_add_toIcoDiv_zsmul hp a c]
    rw [h, sub_smul]
    abel
  · rcases h with ⟨z, hz⟩
    rw [sub_eq_iff_eq_add] at hz
    rw [hz, toIcoMod_zsmul_add]

name: toIocMod_eq_toIocMod
statement: theorem toIocMod_eq_toIocMod  : toIocMod hp a b = toIocMod hp a c ↔ ∃ n : ℤ, c - b = n • p
proof:
refine ⟨fun h => ⟨toIocDiv hp a c - toIocDiv hp a b, ?_⟩, fun h => ?_⟩
  · conv_lhs => rw [← toIocMod_add_toIocDiv_zsmul hp a b, ← toIocMod_add_toIocDiv_zsmul hp a c]
    rw [h, sub_smul]
    abel
  · rcases h with ⟨z, hz⟩
    rw [sub_eq_iff_eq_add] at hz
    rw [hz, toIocMod_zsmul_add]

/-! ### Links between the `Ico` and `Ioc` variants applied to the same element -/


section IcoIoc

namespace AddCommGroup

name: modEq_iff_toIocMod_eq_right
statement: theorem modEq_iff_toIocMod_eq_right  : a ≡ b [PMOD p] ↔ toIocMod hp a b = a + p
proof:
refine modEq_iff_eq_add_zsmul.trans ⟨?_, fun h => ⟨toIocDiv hp a b + 1, ?_⟩⟩
  · rintro ⟨z, rfl⟩
    rw [toIocMod_add_zsmul, toIocMod_apply_left]
  · rwa [add_one_zsmul, add_left_comm, ← sub_eq_iff_eq_add']

alias ⟨ModEq.toIcoMod_eq_left, _⟩ := modEq_iff_toIcoMod_eq_left

alias ⟨ModEq.toIcoMod_eq_right, _⟩ := modEq_iff_toIocMod_eq_right

variable (a b)

open List in

name: not_modEq_iff_toIcoDiv_eq_toIocDiv
statement: theorem not_modEq_iff_toIcoDiv_eq_toIocDiv  : ¬a ≡ b [PMOD p] ↔ toIcoDiv hp a b = toIocDiv hp a b
proof:
rw [not_modEq_iff_toIcoMod_eq_toIocMod hp, toIcoMod, toIocMod, sub_right_inj,
    zsmul_left_inj hp]

name: modEq_iff_toIcoDiv_eq_toIocDiv_add_one
statement: theorem modEq_iff_toIcoDiv_eq_toIocDiv_add_one  : a ≡ b [PMOD p] ↔ toIcoDiv hp a b = toIocDiv hp a b + 1
proof:
rw [modEq_iff_toIcoMod_add_period_eq_toIocMod hp, toIcoMod, toIocMod, ← eq_sub_iff_add_eq,
    sub_sub, sub_right_inj, ← add_one_zsmul, zsmul_left_inj hp]

end AddCommGroup

open AddCommGroup

/-- If `a` and `b` fall within the same cycle w.r.t. `c`, then they are congruent modulo `p`. -/
@[simp]

name: Ico_eq_locus_Ioc_eq_iUnion_Ioo
statement: theorem Ico_eq_locus_Ioc_eq_iUnion_Ioo  : { b | toIcoMod hp a b = toIocMod hp a b } = ⋃ z : ℤ, Set.Ioo (a + z • p) (a + p + z • p)
proof:
ext1
  simp_rw [Set.mem_setOf, Set.mem_iUnion, ← Set.sub_mem_Ioo_iff_left, ←
    not_modEq_iff_toIcoMod_eq_toIocMod, modEq_iff_forall_notMem_Ioo_mod hp, not_forall,
    Classical.not_not]

name: toIocDiv_wcovBy_toIcoDiv
statement: theorem toIocDiv_wcovBy_toIcoDiv (a b : α) : toIocDiv hp a b ⩿ toIcoDiv hp a b
proof:
suffices toIocDiv hp a b = toIcoDiv hp a b ∨ toIocDiv hp a b + 1 = toIcoDiv hp a b by
    rwa [wcovBy_iff_eq_or_covBy, ← Order.succ_eq_iff_covBy]
  rw [eq_comm, ← not_modEq_iff_toIcoDiv_eq_toIocDiv, eq_comm, ←
    modEq_iff_toIcoDiv_eq_toIocDiv_add_one]
  exact em' _

name: toIcoMod_le_toIocMod
statement: theorem toIcoMod_le_toIocMod (a b : α) : toIcoMod hp a b ≤ toIocMod hp a b
proof:
rw [toIcoMod, toIocMod, sub_le_sub_iff_left]
  exact zsmul_left_mono hp.le (toIocDiv_wcovBy_toIcoDiv _ _ _).le

name: toIocMod_le_toIcoMod_add
statement: theorem toIocMod_le_toIcoMod_add (a b : α) : toIocMod hp a b ≤ toIcoMod hp a b + p
proof:
rw [toIcoMod, toIocMod, sub_add, sub_le_sub_iff_left, sub_le_iff_le_add, ← add_one_zsmul,
    (zsmul_left_strictMono hp).le_iff_le]
  apply (toIocDiv_wcovBy_toIcoDiv _ _ _).le_succ

end IcoIoc

open AddCommGroup

name: toIcoMod_eq_self
statement: theorem toIcoMod_eq_self  : toIcoMod hp a b = b ↔ b ∈ Set.Ico a (a + p)
proof:
rw [toIcoMod_eq_iff, and_iff_left]
  exact ⟨0, by simp⟩

name: toIocMod_eq_self
statement: theorem toIocMod_eq_self  : toIocMod hp a b = b ↔ b ∈ Set.Ioc a (a + p)
proof:
rw [toIocMod_eq_iff, and_iff_left]
  exact ⟨0, by simp⟩

@[simp]

name: toIcoMod_zero_sub_comm
statement: theorem toIcoMod_zero_sub_comm (a b : α) : toIcoMod hp 0 (a - b) = p - toIocMod hp 0 (b - a)
proof: rw [← neg_sub, toIcoMod_neg, neg_zero]

name: toIocMod_zero_sub_comm
statement: theorem toIocMod_zero_sub_comm (a b : α) : toIocMod hp 0 (a - b) = p - toIcoMod hp 0 (b - a)
proof: rw [← neg_sub, toIocMod_neg, neg_zero]

name: toIcoDiv_eq_sub
statement: theorem toIcoDiv_eq_sub (a b : α) : toIcoDiv hp a b = toIcoDiv hp 0 (b - a)
proof: rw [toIcoDiv_sub_eq_toIcoDiv_add, zero_add]

name: toIocDiv_eq_sub
statement: theorem toIocDiv_eq_sub (a b : α) : toIocDiv hp a b = toIocDiv hp 0 (b - a)
proof: rw [toIocDiv_sub_eq_toIocDiv_add, zero_add]

name: toIcoMod_eq_sub
statement: theorem toIcoMod_eq_sub (a b : α) : toIcoMod hp a b = toIcoMod hp 0 (b - a) + a
proof: rw [toIcoMod_sub_eq_sub, zero_add, sub_add_cancel]

name: toIocMod_eq_sub
statement: theorem toIocMod_eq_sub (a b : α) : toIocMod hp a b = toIocMod hp 0 (b - a) + a
proof: rw [toIocMod_sub_eq_sub, zero_add, sub_add_cancel]

name: toIcoMod_add_toIocMod_zero
statement: theorem toIcoMod_add_toIocMod_zero (a b : α) : toIcoMod hp 0 (a - b) + toIocMod hp 0 (b - a) = p
proof: rw [toIcoMod_zero_sub_comm, sub_add_cancel]

name: toIocMod_add_toIcoMod_zero
statement: theorem toIocMod_add_toIcoMod_zero (a b : α) : toIocMod hp 0 (a - b) + toIcoMod hp 0 (b - a) = p
proof:
rw [_root_.add_comm, toIcoMod_add_toIocMod_zero]

end Zero

/-- `toIcoMod` as an equiv from the quotient. -/
@[simps symm_apply]

name: self_sub_toIcoDiv_mul
statement: theorem self_sub_toIcoDiv_mul (a b : R) : b - toIcoDiv hp a b * p = toIcoMod hp a b
proof:
simpa using self_sub_toIcoDiv_zsmul hp a b

@[simp]

name: self_sub_toIocDiv_mul
statement: theorem self_sub_toIocDiv_mul (a b : R) : b - toIocDiv hp a b * p = toIocMod hp a b
proof:
simpa using self_sub_toIocDiv_zsmul hp a b

@[simp]

name: toIcoDiv_mul_sub_self
statement: theorem toIcoDiv_mul_sub_self (a b : R) : toIcoDiv hp a b * p - b = -toIcoMod hp a b
proof:
simpa using toIcoDiv_zsmul_sub_self hp a b

@[simp]

name: toIocDiv_mul_sub_self
statement: theorem toIocDiv_mul_sub_self (a b : R) : toIocDiv hp a b * p - b = -toIocMod hp a b
proof: simpa using toIocDiv_zsmul_sub_self hp a b

name: toIcoMod_sub_self_eq_mul
statement: theorem toIcoMod_sub_self_eq_mul (a b : R) : toIcoMod hp a b - b = -toIcoDiv hp a b * p
proof: simp

name: toIocMod_sub_self_eq_mul
statement: theorem toIocMod_sub_self_eq_mul (a b : R) : toIocMod hp a b - b = -toIocDiv hp a b * p
proof: simp

name: self_sub_toIcoMod_eq_mul
statement: theorem self_sub_toIcoMod_eq_mul (a b : R) : b - toIcoMod hp a b = toIcoDiv hp a b * p
proof: simp

name: self_sub_toIocMod_eq_mul
statement: theorem self_sub_toIocMod_eq_mul (a b : R) : b - toIocMod hp a b = toIocDiv hp a b * p
proof:
simp

@[simp]

name: toIcoMod_add_toIcoDiv_mul
statement: theorem toIcoMod_add_toIcoDiv_mul (a b : R) : toIcoMod hp a b + toIcoDiv hp a b * p = b
proof:
simpa using toIcoMod_add_toIcoDiv_zsmul hp a b

@[simp]

name: toIocMod_add_toIocDiv_mul
statement: theorem toIocMod_add_toIocDiv_mul (a b : R) : toIocMod hp a b + toIocDiv hp a b * p = b
proof:
simpa using toIocMod_add_toIocDiv_zsmul hp a b

@[simp]

name: toIcoDiv_mul_sub_toIcoMod
statement: theorem toIcoDiv_mul_sub_toIcoMod (a b : R) : toIcoDiv hp a b * p + toIcoMod hp a b = b
proof:
rw [add_comm, toIcoMod_add_toIcoDiv_mul]

@[simp]

name: toIocDiv_mul_sub_toIocMod
statement: theorem toIocDiv_mul_sub_toIocMod (a b : R) : toIocDiv hp a b * p + toIocMod hp a b = b
proof:
rw [add_comm, toIocMod_add_toIocDiv_mul]

@[simp]

name: toIcoDiv_eq_floor
statement: theorem toIcoDiv_eq_floor (a b : α) : toIcoDiv hp a b = ⌊(b - a) / p⌋
proof:
refine toIcoDiv_eq_of_sub_zsmul_mem_Ico hp ?_
  rw [Set.mem_Ico, zsmul_eq_mul, ← sub_nonneg, add_comm, sub_right_comm, ← sub_lt_iff_lt_add,
    sub_right_comm _ _ a]
  exact ⟨Int.sub_floor_div_mul_nonneg _ hp, Int.sub_floor_div_mul_lt _ hp⟩

name: toIocDiv_eq_neg_floor
statement: theorem toIocDiv_eq_neg_floor (a b : α) : toIocDiv hp a b = -⌊(a + p - b) / p⌋
proof:
refine toIocDiv_eq_of_sub_zsmul_mem_Ioc hp ?_
  rw [Set.mem_Ioc, zsmul_eq_mul, Int.cast_neg, neg_mul, sub_neg_eq_add, ← sub_nonneg,
    sub_add_eq_sub_sub]
  refine ⟨?_, Int.sub_floor_div_mul_nonneg _ hp⟩
  rw [← add_lt_add_iff_right p, add_assoc, add_comm b, ← sub_lt_iff_lt_add, add_comm (_ * _), ←
    sub_lt_iff_lt_add]
  exact Int.sub_floor_div_mul_lt _ hp

name: toIcoDiv_zero_one
statement: theorem toIcoDiv_zero_one (b : α) : toIcoDiv (zero_lt_one' α) 0 b = ⌊b⌋
proof: simp [toIcoDiv_eq_floor]

name: toIcoMod_eq_add_fract_mul
statement: theorem toIcoMod_eq_add_fract_mul (a b : α) : toIcoMod hp a b = a + Int.fract ((b - a) / p) * p
proof:
rw [toIcoMod, toIcoDiv_eq_floor, Int.fract]
  simp [field, -Int.self_sub_floor]
  ring

name: toIcoMod_eq_fract_mul
statement: theorem toIcoMod_eq_fract_mul (b : α) : toIcoMod hp 0 b = Int.fract (b / p) * p
proof: simp [toIcoMod_eq_add_fract_mul]

name: toIocMod_eq_sub_fract_mul
statement: theorem toIocMod_eq_sub_fract_mul (a b : α) : toIocMod hp a b = a + p - Int.fract ((a + p - b) / p) * p
proof:
rw [toIocMod, toIocDiv_eq_neg_floor, Int.fract]
  simp [field, -Int.self_sub_floor]
  ring

name: toIcoMod_zero_one
statement: theorem toIcoMod_zero_one (b : α) : toIcoMod (zero_lt_one' α) 0 b = Int.fract b
proof:
simp [toIcoMod_eq_add_fract_mul]

end LinearOrderedField

/-! ### Lemmas about unions of translates of intervals -/


section Union

open Set Int

section LinearOrderedAddCommGroup

variable {α : Type*} [AddCommGroup α] [LinearOrder α] [IsOrderedAddMonoid α] [Archimedean α]
  {p : α} (hp : 0 < p) (a : α)
include hp

name: iUnion_Ioc_add_zsmul
statement: theorem iUnion_Ioc_add_zsmul  : ⋃ n : ℤ, Ioc (a + n • p) (a + (n + 1) • p) = univ
proof:
refine eq_univ_iff_forall.mpr fun b => mem_iUnion.mpr ?_
  rcases sub_toIocDiv_zsmul_mem_Ioc hp a b with ⟨hl, hr⟩
  refine ⟨toIocDiv hp a b, ⟨lt_sub_iff_add_lt.mp hl, ?_⟩⟩
  rw [add_smul, one_smul, ← add_assoc]
  convert sub_le_iff_le_add.mp hr using 1; abel

name: iUnion_Ico_add_zsmul
statement: theorem iUnion_Ico_add_zsmul  : ⋃ n : ℤ, Ico (a + n • p) (a + (n + 1) • p) = univ
proof:
refine eq_univ_iff_forall.mpr fun b => mem_iUnion.mpr ?_
  rcases sub_toIcoDiv_zsmul_mem_Ico hp a b with ⟨hl, hr⟩
  refine ⟨toIcoDiv hp a b, ⟨le_sub_iff_add_le.mp hl, ?_⟩⟩
  rw [add_smul, one_smul, ← add_assoc]
  convert sub_lt_iff_lt_add.mp hr using 1; abel

name: iUnion_Icc_add_zsmul
statement: theorem iUnion_Icc_add_zsmul  : ⋃ n : ℤ, Icc (a + n • p) (a + (n + 1) • p) = univ
proof:
simpa only [iUnion_Ioc_add_zsmul hp a, univ_subset_iff] using
    iUnion_mono fun n : ℤ => (Ioc_subset_Icc_self : Ioc (a + n • p) (a + (n + 1) • p) ⊆ Icc _ _)

name: iUnion_Ioc_zsmul
statement: theorem iUnion_Ioc_zsmul  : ⋃ n : ℤ, Ioc (n • p) ((n + 1) • p) = univ
proof: simpa only [zero_add] using iUnion_Ioc_add_zsmul hp 0

name: iUnion_Ico_zsmul
statement: theorem iUnion_Ico_zsmul  : ⋃ n : ℤ, Ico (n • p) ((n + 1) • p) = univ
proof: simpa only [zero_add] using iUnion_Ico_add_zsmul hp 0

name: iUnion_Icc_zsmul
statement: theorem iUnion_Icc_zsmul  : ⋃ n : ℤ, Icc (n • p) ((n + 1) • p) = univ
proof:
simpa only [zero_add] using iUnion_Icc_add_zsmul hp 0

end LinearOrderedAddCommGroup

section LinearOrderedRing

variable {α : Type*} [Ring α] [LinearOrder α] [IsStrictOrderedRing α] [Archimedean α] (a : α)

name: iUnion_Ioc_add_intCast
statement: theorem iUnion_Ioc_add_intCast  : ⋃ n : ℤ, Ioc (a + n) (a + n + 1) = Set.univ
proof:
simpa only [zsmul_one, Int.cast_add, Int.cast_one, ← add_assoc] using
    iUnion_Ioc_add_zsmul zero_lt_one a

name: iUnion_Ico_add_intCast
statement: theorem iUnion_Ico_add_intCast  : ⋃ n : ℤ, Ico (a + n) (a + n + 1) = Set.univ
proof:
simpa only [zsmul_one, Int.cast_add, Int.cast_one, ← add_assoc] using
    iUnion_Ico_add_zsmul zero_lt_one a

name: iUnion_Icc_add_intCast
statement: theorem iUnion_Icc_add_intCast  : ⋃ n : ℤ, Icc (a + n) (a + n + 1) = Set.univ
proof:
simpa only [zsmul_one, Int.cast_add, Int.cast_one, ← add_assoc] using
    iUnion_Icc_add_zsmul zero_lt_one a

variable (α)

name: iUnion_Ioc_intCast
statement: theorem iUnion_Ioc_intCast  : ⋃ n : ℤ, Ioc (n : α) (n + 1) = Set.univ
proof: simpa only [zero_add] using iUnion_Ioc_add_intCast (0 : α)

name: iUnion_Ico_intCast
statement: theorem iUnion_Ico_intCast  : ⋃ n : ℤ, Ico (n : α) (n + 1) = Set.univ
proof: simpa only [zero_add] using iUnion_Ico_add_intCast (0 : α)

name: iUnion_Icc_intCast
statement: theorem iUnion_Icc_intCast  : ⋃ n : ℤ, Icc (n : α) (n + 1) = Set.univ
proof:
simpa only [zero_add] using iUnion_Icc_add_intCast (0 : α)

end LinearOrderedRing

end Union

name: monovaryOn_inv_left
statement: theorem monovaryOn_inv_left  : MonovaryOn f⁻¹ g s ↔ AntivaryOn f g s
proof:
simp [MonovaryOn, AntivaryOn]

@[to_additive (attr := simp)]

name: antivaryOn_inv_left
statement: theorem antivaryOn_inv_left  : AntivaryOn f⁻¹ g s ↔ MonovaryOn f g s
proof:
simp [MonovaryOn, AntivaryOn]

@[to_additive (attr := simp)] lemma monovary_inv_left : Monovary f⁻¹ g ↔ Antivary f g := by
  simp [Monovary, Antivary]

@[to_additive (attr := simp)] lemma antivary_inv_left : Antivary f⁻¹ g ↔ Monovary f g := by
  simp [Monovary, Antivary]

@[to_additive] lemma MonovaryOn.mul_left (h₁ : MonovaryOn f₁ g s) (h₂ : MonovaryOn f₂ g s) :
    MonovaryOn (f₁ * f₂) g s := fun _i hi _j hj hij ↦ mul_le_mul' (h₁ hi hj hij) (h₂ hi hj hij)

@[to_additive] lemma AntivaryOn.mul_left (h₁ : AntivaryOn f₁ g s) (h₂ : AntivaryOn f₂ g s) :
    AntivaryOn (f₁ * f₂) g s := fun _i hi _j hj hij ↦ mul_le_mul' (h₁ hi hj hij) (h₂ hi hj hij)

@[to_additive] lemma MonovaryOn.div_left (h₁ : MonovaryOn f₁ g s) (h₂ : AntivaryOn f₂ g s) :
    MonovaryOn (f₁ / f₂) g s := fun _i hi _j hj hij ↦ div_le_div'' (h₁ hi hj hij) (h₂ hi hj hij)

@[to_additive] lemma AntivaryOn.div_left (h₁ : AntivaryOn f₁ g s) (h₂ : MonovaryOn f₂ g s) :
    AntivaryOn (f₁ / f₂) g s := fun _i hi _j hj hij ↦ div_le_div'' (h₁ hi hj hij) (h₂ hi hj hij)

@[to_additive] lemma MonovaryOn.pow_left (hfg : MonovaryOn f g s) (n : ℕ) :
    MonovaryOn (f ^ n) g s := fun _i hi _j hj hij ↦ pow_le_pow_left' (hfg hi hj hij) _

@[to_additive] lemma AntivaryOn.pow_left (hfg : AntivaryOn f g s) (n : ℕ) :
    AntivaryOn (f ^ n) g s := fun _i hi _j hj hij ↦ pow_le_pow_left' (hfg hi hj hij) _

@[to_additive]

name: monovaryOn_inv_right
statement: theorem monovaryOn_inv_right  : MonovaryOn f g⁻¹ s ↔ AntivaryOn f g s
proof:
simpa [MonovaryOn, AntivaryOn] using forall₂_comm

@[to_additive (attr := simp)]

name: antivaryOn_inv_right
statement: theorem antivaryOn_inv_right  : AntivaryOn f g⁻¹ s ↔ MonovaryOn f g s
proof:
simpa [MonovaryOn, AntivaryOn] using forall₂_comm

@[to_additive (attr := simp)] lemma monovary_inv_right : Monovary f g⁻¹ ↔ Antivary f g := by
  simpa [Monovary, Antivary] using forall_comm

@[to_additive (attr := simp)] lemma antivary_inv_right : Antivary f g⁻¹ ↔ Monovary f g := by
  simpa [Monovary, Antivary] using forall_comm
end

section
variable [CommGroup α] [PartialOrder α] [IsOrderedMonoid α]
  [CommGroup β] [PartialOrder β] [IsOrderedMonoid β]
  {s : Set ι} {f f₁ f₂ : ι → α} {g : ι → β}

@[to_additive] lemma monovaryOn_inv : MonovaryOn f⁻¹ g⁻¹ s ↔ MonovaryOn f g s := by simp
@[to_additive] lemma antivaryOn_inv : AntivaryOn f⁻¹ g⁻¹ s ↔ AntivaryOn f g s := by simp

@[to_additive] lemma monovary_inv : Monovary f⁻¹ g⁻¹ ↔ Monovary f g := by simp
@[to_additive] lemma antivary_inv : Antivary f⁻¹ g⁻¹ ↔ Antivary f g := by simp

end

@[to_additive] alias ⟨MonovaryOn.of_inv_left, AntivaryOn.inv_left⟩ := monovaryOn_inv_left
@[to_additive] alias ⟨AntivaryOn.of_inv_left, MonovaryOn.inv_left⟩ := antivaryOn_inv_left
@[to_additive] alias ⟨MonovaryOn.of_inv_right, AntivaryOn.inv_right⟩ := monovaryOn_inv_right
@[to_additive] alias ⟨AntivaryOn.of_inv_right, MonovaryOn.inv_right⟩ := antivaryOn_inv_right
@[to_additive] alias ⟨MonovaryOn.of_inv, MonovaryOn.inv⟩ := monovaryOn_inv
@[to_additive] alias ⟨AntivaryOn.of_inv, AntivaryOn.inv⟩ := antivaryOn_inv
@[to_additive] alias ⟨Monovary.of_inv_left, Antivary.inv_left⟩ := monovary_inv_left
@[to_additive] alias ⟨Antivary.of_inv_left, Monovary.inv_left⟩ := antivary_inv_left
@[to_additive] alias ⟨Monovary.of_inv_right, Antivary.inv_right⟩ := monovary_inv_right
@[to_additive] alias ⟨Antivary.of_inv_right, Monovary.inv_right⟩ := antivary_inv_right
@[to_additive] alias ⟨Monovary.of_inv, Monovary.inv⟩ := monovary_inv
@[to_additive] alias ⟨Antivary.of_inv, Antivary.inv⟩ := antivary_inv

end OrderedCommGroup

section LinearOrderedCommGroup
variable [Preorder α] [CommGroup β] [LinearOrder β] [IsOrderedMonoid β] {s : Set ι} {f : ι → α}
  {g g₁ g₂ : ι → β}

@[to_additive] lemma MonovaryOn.mul_right (h₁ : MonovaryOn f g₁ s) (h₂ : MonovaryOn f g₂ s) :
    MonovaryOn f (g₁ * g₂) s :=
  fun _i hi _j hj hij ↦ (lt_or_lt_of_mul_lt_mul hij).elim (h₁ hi hj) <| h₂ hi hj

@[to_additive] lemma AntivaryOn.mul_right (h₁ : AntivaryOn f g₁ s) (h₂ : AntivaryOn f g₂ s) :
    AntivaryOn f (g₁ * g₂) s :=
  fun _i hi _j hj hij ↦ (lt_or_lt_of_mul_lt_mul hij).elim (h₁ hi hj) <| h₂ hi hj

@[to_additive] lemma MonovaryOn.div_right (h₁ : MonovaryOn f g₁ s) (h₂ : AntivaryOn f g₂ s) :
    MonovaryOn f (g₁ / g₂) s :=
  fun _i hi _j hj hij ↦ (lt_or_lt_of_div_lt_div hij).elim (h₁ hi hj) <| h₂ hj hi

@[to_additive] lemma AntivaryOn.div_right (h₁ : AntivaryOn f g₁ s) (h₂ : MonovaryOn f g₂ s) :
    AntivaryOn f (g₁ / g₂) s :=
  fun _i hi _j hj hij ↦ (lt_or_lt_of_div_lt_div hij).elim (h₁ hi hj) <| h₂ hj hi

@[to_additive] lemma MonovaryOn.pow_right (hfg : MonovaryOn f g s) (n : ℕ) :
    MonovaryOn f (g ^ n) s := fun _i hi _j hj hij ↦ hfg hi hj <| lt_of_pow_lt_pow_left' _ hij

@[to_additive] lemma AntivaryOn.pow_right (hfg : AntivaryOn f g s) (n : ℕ) :
    AntivaryOn f (g ^ n) s := fun _i hi _j hj hij ↦ hfg hi hj <| lt_of_pow_lt_pow_left' _ hij

@[to_additive] lemma Monovary.mul_right (h₁ : Monovary f g₁) (h₂ : Monovary f g₂) :
    Monovary f (g₁ * g₂) :=
  fun _i _j hij ↦ (lt_or_lt_of_mul_lt_mul hij).elim (fun h ↦ h₁ h) fun h ↦ h₂ h

@[to_additive] lemma Antivary.mul_right (h₁ : Antivary f g₁) (h₂ : Antivary f g₂) :
    Antivary f (g₁ * g₂) :=
  fun _i _j hij ↦ (lt_or_lt_of_mul_lt_mul hij).elim (fun h ↦ h₁ h) fun h ↦ h₂ h

@[to_additive] lemma Monovary.div_right (h₁ : Monovary f g₁) (h₂ : Antivary f g₂) :
    Monovary f (g₁ / g₂) :=
  fun _i _j hij ↦ (lt_or_lt_of_div_lt_div hij).elim (fun h ↦ h₁ h) fun h ↦ h₂ h

@[to_additive] lemma Antivary.div_right (h₁ : Antivary f g₁) (h₂ : Monovary f g₂) :
    Antivary f (g₁ / g₂) :=
  fun _i _j hij ↦ (lt_or_lt_of_div_lt_div hij).elim (fun h ↦ h₁ h) fun h ↦ h₂ h

@[to_additive] lemma Monovary.pow_right (hfg : Monovary f g) (n : ℕ) : Monovary f (g ^ n) :=
  fun _i _j hij ↦ hfg <| lt_of_pow_lt_pow_left' _ hij

@[to_additive] lemma Antivary.pow_right (hfg : Antivary f g) (n : ℕ) : Antivary f (g ^ n) :=
  fun _i _j hij ↦ hfg <| lt_of_pow_lt_pow_left' _ hij

end LinearOrderedCommGroup

section OrderedSemiring
variable [Semiring α] [PartialOrder α] [IsOrderedRing α] [PartialOrder β]
  {s : Set ι} {f f₁ f₂ : ι → α} {g : ι → β}

name: monovaryOn_iff_forall_smul_nonneg
statement: theorem monovaryOn_iff_forall_smul_nonneg  : MonovaryOn f g s ↔ ∀ ⦃i⦄, i ∈ s → ∀ ⦃j⦄, j ∈ s → 0 ≤ (f j - f i) • (g j - g i)
proof:
simp_rw [smul_nonneg_iff_pos_imp_nonneg, sub_pos, sub_nonneg, forall_and]
  exact (and_iff_right_of_imp MonovaryOn.symm).symm

name: monovaryOn_iff_forall_mul_nonneg
statement: theorem monovaryOn_iff_forall_mul_nonneg  : MonovaryOn f g s ↔ ∀ ⦃i⦄, i ∈ s → ∀ ⦃j⦄, j ∈ s → 0 ≤ (f j - f i) * (g j - g i)
proof: simp only [smul_eq_mul, monovaryOn_iff_forall_smul_nonneg]

name: antivaryOn_iff_forall_mul_nonpos
statement: theorem antivaryOn_iff_forall_mul_nonpos  : AntivaryOn f g s ↔ ∀ ⦃i⦄, i ∈ s → ∀ ⦃j⦄, j ∈ s → (f j - f i) * (g j - g i) ≤ 0
proof: simp only [smul_eq_mul, antivaryOn_iff_forall_smul_nonpos]

name: monovary_iff_forall_mul_nonneg
statement: theorem monovary_iff_forall_mul_nonneg  : Monovary f g ↔ ∀ i j, 0 ≤ (f j - f i) * (g j - g i)
proof: simp only [smul_eq_mul, monovary_iff_forall_smul_nonneg]

name: antivary_iff_forall_mul_nonpos
statement: theorem antivary_iff_forall_mul_nonpos  : Antivary f g ↔ ∀ i j, (f j - f i) * (g j - g i) ≤ 0
proof:
simp only [smul_eq_mul, antivary_iff_forall_smul_nonpos]

/-- Two functions monovary iff the rearrangement inequality holds. -/

name: monovary_iff_mul_rearrangement
statement: theorem monovary_iff_mul_rearrangement  : Monovary f g ↔ ∀ i j, f i * g j + f j * g i ≤ f i * g i + f j * g j
proof:
simp only [smul_eq_mul, monovary_iff_smul_rearrangement]

/-- Two functions antivary iff the rearrangement inequality holds. -/

name: antivary_iff_mul_rearrangement
statement: theorem antivary_iff_mul_rearrangement  : Antivary f g ↔ ∀ i j, f i * g i + f j * g j ≤ f i * g j + f j * g i
proof:
simp only [smul_eq_mul, antivary_iff_smul_rearrangement]

alias ⟨MonovaryOn.sub_mul_sub_nonneg, _⟩ := monovaryOn_iff_forall_mul_nonneg
alias ⟨AntivaryOn.sub_mul_sub_nonpos, _⟩ := antivaryOn_iff_forall_mul_nonpos
alias ⟨Monovary.sub_mul_sub_nonneg, _⟩ := monovary_iff_forall_mul_nonneg
alias ⟨Antivary.sub_mul_sub_nonpos, _⟩ := antivary_iff_forall_mul_nonpos
alias ⟨Monovary.mul_add_mul_le_mul_add_mul, _⟩ := monovary_iff_mul_rearrangement
alias ⟨Antivary.mul_add_mul_le_mul_add_mul, _⟩ := antivary_iff_mul_rearrangement
alias ⟨MonovaryOn.mul_add_mul_le_mul_add_mul, _⟩ := monovaryOn_iff_mul_rearrangement
alias ⟨AntivaryOn.mul_add_mul_le_mul_add_mul, _⟩ := antivaryOn_iff_mul_rearrangement

end LinearOrderedRing

name: round_eq_div
statement: theorem round_eq_div (x : α) : round x = (⌊2 * x⌋ + 1) / 2
proof:
rw [← floor_add_fract x, round, fract_intCast_add, fract_fract, floor_intCast_add, mul_add,
    ← Int.cast_ofNat, ← Int.cast_mul, floor_intCast_add, ceil_intCast_add, add_assoc,
    Int.mul_add_ediv_left _ _ two_ne_zero, Int.cast_ofNat]
  split_ifs with h <;> congr 1
  · rw [Int.floor_eq_zero_iff.mpr, Int.floor_eq_zero_iff.mpr]
    · simp
    · simp [h]
    · suffices fract x < 1 by simpa
      refine lt_of_le_of_lt ?_ h
      apply le_mul_of_one_le_left <;> simp
  · have H : ⌊2 * fract x⌋ = 1 := by simpa [floor_eq_iff, ← two_mul, fract_lt_one] using h
    suffices 0 < fract x by simp [this, H, ceil_eq_iff, (fract_lt_one _).le]
    contrapose! h
    grw [h]
    simp

@[simp]

name: round_add_one
statement: theorem round_add_one (a : α) : round (a + 1) = round a + 1
proof:
rw [← round_add_intCast a 1, cast_one]

@[simp]

name: round_sub_one
statement: theorem round_sub_one (a : α) : round (a - 1) = round a - 1
proof:
rw [← round_sub_intCast a 1, cast_one]

@[simp]

name: abs_sub_round_eq_min
statement: theorem abs_sub_round_eq_min (x : α) : |x - round x| = min (fract x) (1 - fract x)
proof:
simp_rw [round, min_def_lt, two_mul, ← lt_tsub_iff_left]
  rcases lt_or_ge (fract x) (1 - fract x) with hx | hx
  · rw [if_pos hx, if_pos hx, self_sub_floor, abs_fract]
  · have : 0 < fract x := by
      replace hx : 0 < fract x + fract x := lt_of_lt_of_le zero_lt_one (tsub_le_iff_left.mp hx)
      simpa only [← two_mul, mul_pos_iff_of_pos_left, zero_lt_two] using hx
    rw [if_neg (not_lt.mpr hx), if_neg (not_lt.mpr hx), abs_sub_comm, ceil_sub_self_eq this.ne.symm,
      abs_one_sub_fract]

name: round_eq
statement: theorem round_eq (x : α) : round x = ⌊x + 1 / 2⌋
proof:
rw [← cast_mul_floor_div_cancel_of_pos two_pos, round_eq_div]
  simp [mul_add]

name: abs_sub_round
statement: theorem abs_sub_round (x : α) : |x - round x| ≤ 1 / 2
proof:
rw [round_eq, abs_sub_le_iff]
  have := floor_le (x + 1 / 2)
  have := lt_floor_add_one (x + 1 / 2)
  constructor <;> linarith

name: sub_half_lt_round
statement: theorem sub_half_lt_round (x : α) : x - 1 / 2 < round x
proof:
rw [round_eq x, show x - 1 / 2 = x + 1 / 2 - 1 by linarith]
  exact Int.sub_one_lt_floor (x + 1 / 2)

@[bound]

name: round_le_add_half
statement: theorem round_le_add_half (x : α) : round x ≤ x + 1 / 2
proof:
rw [round_eq x]
  exact Int.floor_le (x + 1 / 2)

end LinearOrderedField

end round

namespace Int

variable [Field α] [LinearOrder α] [IsStrictOrderedRing α]
  [Field β] [LinearOrder β] [IsStrictOrderedRing β] [FloorRing α] [FloorRing β]
variable [FunLike F α β] [RingHomClass F α β] {a : α} {b : β}

name: mul_upperClosure
statement: theorem mul_upperClosure  : s * upperClosure t = upperClosure (s * t)
proof:
simp_rw [← smul_eq_mul, ← Set.iUnion_smul_set, upperClosure_iUnion, upperClosure_smul,
    UpperSet.coe_iInf₂]
  rfl

@[to_additive]

name: mul_lowerClosure
statement: theorem mul_lowerClosure  : s * lowerClosure t = lowerClosure (s * t)
proof:
simp_rw [← smul_eq_mul, ← Set.iUnion_smul_set, lowerClosure_iUnion, lowerClosure_smul,
    LowerSet.coe_iSup₂]
  rfl

@[to_additive]

name: upperClosure_mul
statement: theorem upperClosure_mul  : ↑(upperClosure s) * t = upperClosure (s * t)
proof:
simp_rw [mul_comm _ t]
  exact mul_upperClosure _ _

@[to_additive]

name: lowerClosure_mul
statement: theorem lowerClosure_mul  : ↑(lowerClosure s) * t = lowerClosure (s * t)
proof:
simp_rw [mul_comm _ t]
  exact mul_lowerClosure _ _

@[to_additive (attr := simp)]

name: mulSingle_le_mulSingle
statement: theorem mulSingle_le_mulSingle  : mulSingle i a ≤ mulSingle i b ↔ a ≤ b
proof:
simp [mulSingle]

@[to_additive (attr := simp) single_nonneg]

name: kstar_le_of_mul_le_left
statement: theorem kstar_le_of_mul_le_left (hb : 1 ≤ b) : b * a ≤ b → a∗ ≤ b
proof: simpa using mul_kstar_le hb

name: kstar_le_of_mul_le_right
statement: theorem kstar_le_of_mul_le_right (hb : 1 ≤ b) : a * b ≤ b → a∗ ≤ b
proof:
simpa using kstar_mul_le hb

@[simp]

name: one_add_mul_kstar
statement: theorem one_add_mul_kstar  : 1 + a * a∗ = a∗
proof:
have h : 1 + a * a∗ ≤ a∗ := by
    rw [add_le_iff]
    exact ⟨one_le_kstar, mul_kstar_le_kstar⟩
  apply le_antisymm h
  suffices 1 + a * (1 + a * a∗) ≤ 1 + a * a∗ by
    rw [add_le_iff] at this
    nth_rw 1 [← mul_one a∗]
    exact (mul_right_mono this.1).trans (kstar_mul_le_self this.2)
  apply add_le_add_right (mul_right_mono h)

name: one_add_kstar_mul
statement: theorem one_add_kstar_mul  : 1 + a∗ * a = a∗
proof:
have h : 1 + a∗ * a ≤ a∗ := by
    rw [add_le_iff]
    exact ⟨one_le_kstar, kstar_mul_le_kstar⟩
  apply le_antisymm h
  suffices 1 + (1 + a∗ * a) * a ≤ 1 + a∗ * a by
    rw [add_le_iff] at this
    nth_rw 1 [← one_mul a∗]
    exact (mul_left_mono this.1).trans (mul_kstar_le_self this.2)
  apply add_le_add_right (mul_left_mono h)

end KleeneAlgebra

namespace Prod

instance instIdemSemiring [IdemSemiring α] [IdemSemiring β] : IdemSemiring (α × β) where
  add_eq_sup _ _ := Prod.ext (add_eq_sup _ _) (add_eq_sup _ _)

instance [IdemCommSemiring α] [IdemCommSemiring β] : IdemCommSemiring (α × β) where
  __ := Prod.instCommSemiring
  __ := Prod.instIdemSemiring

variable [KleeneAlgebra α] [KleeneAlgebra β]

instance : KleeneAlgebra (α × β) where
  kstar a := (a.1∗, a.2∗)
  one_le_kstar _ := ⟨one_le_kstar, one_le_kstar⟩
  mul_kstar_le_kstar _ := ⟨mul_kstar_le_kstar, mul_kstar_le_kstar⟩
  kstar_mul_le_kstar _ := ⟨kstar_mul_le_kstar, kstar_mul_le_kstar⟩
  mul_kstar_le_self _ _ := And.imp mul_kstar_le_self mul_kstar_le_self
  kstar_mul_le_self _ _ := And.imp kstar_mul_le_self kstar_mul_le_self

name: sq_sum_le_card_mul_sum_sq
statement: theorem sq_sum_le_card_mul_sum_sq  : (∑ i ∈ s, f i) ^ 2 ≤ #s * ∑ i ∈ s, f i ^ 2
proof:
simp_rw [sq]
  exact (monovaryOn_self _ _).sum_mul_sum_le_card_mul_sum

variable [Fintype ι]

/-- **Chebyshev's Sum Inequality**: When `f` and `g` monovary together (e.g. they are both
monotone/antitone), the product of their sum is less than the size of the set times their scalar
product. -/

name: sum_div_card_sq_le_sum_sq_div_card
statement: theorem sum_div_card_sq_le_sum_sq_div_card  : ((∑ i ∈ s, f i) / #s) ^ 2 ≤ (∑ i ∈ s, f i ^ 2) / #s
proof:
obtain rfl | hs := s.eq_empty_or_nonempty
  · simp
  rw [div_pow, div_le_div_iff₀ (by positivity) (by positivity), sq (#s : α), mul_left_comm,
    ← mul_assoc]
  gcongr
  exact sq_sum_le_card_mul_sum_sq

name: isIdempotentElem_idempotent
statement: theorem isIdempotentElem_idempotent (i : I) : IsIdempotentElem (idempotent V i : R)
proof:
rw [IsIdempotentElem, ← decompose_eq_mul_idempotent, idempotent, decompose_coe, of_eq_same]

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
/-- If a semiring can be decomposed into direct sum of finite left ideals `Vᵢ`
  where `1 = e₁ + ... + eₙ` and `eᵢ ∈ Vᵢ`, then `eᵢ` is a family of complete
  orthogonal idempotents. -/

name: one_mul
statement: theorem one_mul (x : ⨁ i, A i) : 1 * x = x
proof:
suffices mulHom A One.one = AddMonoidHom.id (⨁ i, A i) from DFunLike.congr_fun this x
  apply addHom_ext; intro i xi
  simp only [One.one]
  rw [mulHom_of_of]
  exact of_eq_of_gradedMonoid_eq (one_mul <| GradedMonoid.mk i xi)

private nonrec theorem mul_one (x : ⨁ i, A i) : x * 1 = x := by
  suffices (mulHom A).flip One.one = AddMonoidHom.id (⨁ i, A i) from DFunLike.congr_fun this x
  apply addHom_ext; intro i xi
  simp only [One.one]
  rw [flip_apply, mulHom_of_of]
  exact of_eq_of_gradedMonoid_eq (mul_one <| GradedMonoid.mk i xi)

private theorem mul_assoc (a b c : ⨁ i, A i) : a * b * c = a * (b * c) := by
  -- (`fun a b c => a * b * c` as a bundled hom) = (`fun a b c => a * (b * c)` as a bundled hom)
  suffices AddMonoidHom.mulLeft₃ = AddMonoidHom.mulRight₃ by
      simpa only [AddMonoidHom.mulLeft₃_apply, AddMonoidHom.mulRight₃_apply] using
        DFunLike.congr_fun (DFunLike.congr_fun (DFunLike.congr_fun this a) b) c
  ext ai ax bi bx ci cx : 6
  dsimp only [coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, AddMonoidHom.mulLeft₃_apply,
    AddMonoidHom.mulRight₃_apply]
  simp_rw [of_mul_of]
  exact of_eq_of_gradedMonoid_eq (_root_.mul_assoc (GradedMonoid.mk ai ax) ⟨bi, bx⟩ ⟨ci, cx⟩)

instance instNatCast : NatCast (⨁ i, A i) where
  natCast := fun n => of _ _ (GSemiring.natCast n)

/-- The `Semiring` structure derived from `GSemiring A`. -/
instance semiring : Semiring (⨁ i, A i) where
  one_mul := private one_mul A
  mul_one := private mul_one A
  mul_assoc := private mul_assoc A
  toNatCast := instNatCast _
  natCast_zero := by simp only [NatCast.natCast, GSemiring.natCast_zero, map_zero]
  natCast_succ := fun n => by
    simp_rw [NatCast.natCast, GSemiring.natCast_succ]
    rw [map_add]
    rfl

name: mul_comm
statement: theorem mul_comm (a b : ⨁ i, A i) : a * b = b * a
proof:
suffices mulHom A = (mulHom A).flip by
    rw [← mulHom_apply, this, AddMonoidHom.flip_apply, mulHom_apply]
  apply addHom_ext; intro ai ax; apply addHom_ext; intro bi bx
  rw [AddMonoidHom.flip_apply, mulHom_of_of, mulHom_of_of]
  exact of_eq_of_gradedMonoid_eq (GCommSemiring.mul_comm ⟨ai, ax⟩ ⟨bi, bx⟩)

/-- The `CommSemiring` structure derived from `GCommSemiring A`. -/
instance commSemiring : CommSemiring (⨁ i, A i) where
  mul_comm := private mul_comm A

end CommSemiring

/-- The `Ring` derived from `GSemiring A`. -/
instance ring : Ring (⨁ i, A i) where
  toIntCast.intCast z := of A 0 <| (GRing.intCast z)
  intCast_ofNat _ := congrArg (of A 0) <| GRing.intCast_ofNat _
  intCast_negSucc _ :=
    (congrArg (of A 0) <| GRing.intCast_negSucc_ofNat _).trans <| map_neg _ _

/-- The `CommRing` derived from `GCommSemiring A`. -/
instance commRing : CommRing (⨁ i, A i) where

The various `G*` instances are enough to promote the `AddCommMonoid (A 0)` structure to various
types of multiplicative structure.
-/


section GradeZero

section One

variable [Zero ι] [GradedMonoid.GOne A] [∀ i, AddCommMonoid (A i)]

@[simp]

name: of_intCast
statement: theorem of_intCast (n : ℤ) : of A 0 n = n
proof:
rfl

/-- The `Ring` derived from `GSemiring A`. -/
instance (priority := 900) GradeZero.ring : Ring (A 0) :=
  Function.Injective.ring (of A 0) DFinsupp.single_injective (of A 0).map_zero (of_zero_one A)
    (of A 0).map_add (of_zero_mul A) (of A 0).map_neg (of A 0).map_sub (fun _ _ ↦ map_nsmul _ _ _)
    (fun _ _ ↦ map_zsmul _ _ _) (fun _ _ => of_zero_pow _ _ _) (of_natCast A) (of_intCast A)

section CommRing

variable [∀ i, AddCommGroup (A i)] [AddCommMonoid ι] [GCommRing A]

/-- The `CommRing` derived from `GCommSemiring A`. -/
instance (priority := 900) GradeZero.commRing : CommRing (A 0) :=
  Function.Injective.commRing (of A 0) DFinsupp.single_injective (of A 0).map_zero (of_zero_one A)
    (of A 0).map_add (of_zero_mul A) (of A 0).map_neg (of A 0).map_sub (fun _ _ ↦ map_nsmul _ _ _)
    (fun _ _ ↦ map_zsmul _ _ _) (fun _ _ => of_zero_pow _ _ _) (of_natCast A) (of_intCast A)

end CommRing

end GradeZero

section ToSemiring

variable {R : Type*} [∀ i, AddCommMonoid (A i)] [AddMonoid ι] [GSemiring A] [Semiring R]
variable {A}

/-- If two ring homomorphisms from `⨁ i, A i` are equal on each `of A i y`,
then they are equal.

See note [partially-applied ext lemmas]. -/
@[ext]

name: lmap_injective
statement: theorem lmap_injective  : Function.Injective (lmap f) ↔ ∀ i, Function.Injective (f i)
proof: classical exact DFinsupp.mapRange_injective (hf := fun _ ↦ map_zero _)

name: lmap_surjective
statement: theorem lmap_surjective  : Function.Surjective (lmap f) ↔ (∀ i, Function.Surjective (f i))
proof: classical exact DFinsupp.mapRange_surjective (hf := fun _ ↦ map_zero _)

name: directSum_injective
statement: theorem directSum_injective  : Injective (directSum : (∀ i, AddChar (G i) R) → AddChar (⨁ i, G i) R)
proof:
refine toAddMonoidHomEquiv.symm.injective.comp <| DirectSum.toAddMonoid_injective.comp ?_
  rintro ψ χ h
  simpa [funext_iff] using h

end DirectSum
end AddChar

name: map_injective
statement: theorem map_injective  : Function.Injective (map f) ↔ ∀ i, Function.Injective (f i)
proof: classical exact DFinsupp.mapRange_injective (hf := fun _ ↦ map_zero _)

name: map_surjective
statement: theorem map_surjective  : Function.Surjective (map f) ↔ (∀ i, Function.Surjective (f i))
proof: classical exact DFinsupp.mapRange_surjective (hf := fun _ ↦ map_zero _)

name: map_eq_iff
statement: theorem map_eq_iff (x y : ⨁ i, α i) : map f x = map f y ↔ ∀ i, f i (x i) = f i (y i)
proof:
simp_rw [DirectSum.ext_iff, map_apply]

end map

end DirectSum

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
/-- The canonical isomorphism of a finite direct sum of additive commutative monoids
and the corresponding finite product. -/

name: vsub_self
statement: theorem vsub_self (p : P) : p -ᵥ p = (0 : G)
proof:
rw [← zero_add (p -ᵥ p), ← vadd_vsub_assoc, vadd_vsub]

/-- If subtracting two points produces 0, they are equal. -/

name: vsub_add_vsub_cancel
statement: theorem vsub_add_vsub_cancel (p₁ p₂ p₃ : P) : p₁ -ᵥ p₂ + (p₂ -ᵥ p₃) = p₁ -ᵥ p₃
proof:
apply vadd_right_cancel p₃
  rw [add_vadd, vsub_vadd, vsub_vadd, vsub_vadd]

/-- Subtracting two points in the reverse order produces the negation
of subtracting them. -/
@[simp]

name: neg_vsub_eq_vsub_rev
statement: theorem neg_vsub_eq_vsub_rev (p₁ p₂ : P) : -(p₁ -ᵥ p₂) = p₂ -ᵥ p₁
proof:
refine neg_eq_of_add_eq_zero_right (vadd_right_cancel p₁ ?_)
  rw [vsub_add_vsub_cancel, vsub_self]

name: vsub_sub_vsub_cancel_right
statement: theorem vsub_sub_vsub_cancel_right (p₁ p₂ p₃ : P) : p₁ -ᵥ p₃ - (p₂ -ᵥ p₃) = p₁ -ᵥ p₂
proof:
rw [← vsub_vadd_eq_vsub_sub, vsub_vadd]

/-- Convert between an equality with adding a group element to a point
and an equality of a subtraction of two points with a group
element. -/

name: pointReflection_vsub_right
statement: theorem pointReflection_vsub_right (x y : P) : pointReflection x y -ᵥ y = 2 • (x -ᵥ y)
proof:
simp [pointReflection, two_nsmul, vadd_vsub_assoc]

@[simp]

name: singleton_vsub_self
statement: theorem singleton_vsub_self (p : P) : ({p} : Set P) -ᵥ {p} = {(0 : G)}
proof:
rw [Set.singleton_vsub_singleton, vsub_self]

end Set

/-- If the same point subtracted from two points produces equal
results, those points are equal. -/

name: vsub_sub_vsub_cancel_left
statement: theorem vsub_sub_vsub_cancel_left (p₁ p₂ p₃ : P) : p₃ -ᵥ p₂ - (p₃ -ᵥ p₁) = p₁ -ᵥ p₂
proof:
rw [sub_eq_add_neg, neg_vsub_eq_vsub_rev, add_comm, vsub_add_vsub_cancel]

@[simp]

name: vsub_vadd_comm
statement: theorem vsub_vadd_comm (p₁ p₂ p₃ : P) : (p₁ -ᵥ p₂ : G) +ᵥ p₃ = (p₃ -ᵥ p₂) +ᵥ p₁
proof:
rw [← @vsub_eq_zero_iff_eq G, vadd_vsub_assoc, vsub_vadd_eq_vsub_sub]
  simp

name: vsub_sub_vsub_comm
statement: theorem vsub_sub_vsub_comm (p₁ p₂ p₃ p₄ : P) : p₁ -ᵥ p₂ - (p₃ -ᵥ p₄) = p₁ -ᵥ p₃ - (p₂ -ᵥ p₄)
proof:
rw [← vsub_vadd_eq_vsub_sub, vsub_vadd_comm, vsub_vadd_eq_vsub_sub]

namespace Set

@[simp] lemma vadd_set_vsub_vadd_set (v : G) (s t : Set P) : (v +ᵥ s) -ᵥ (v +ᵥ t) = s -ᵥ t := by
  ext; simp [mem_vsub, mem_vadd_set]

end Set

end comm

namespace Prod

variable {G G' P P' : Type*} [AddGroup G] [AddGroup G'] [AddTorsor G P] [AddTorsor G' P']

instance instAddTorsor : AddTorsor (G × G') (P × P') where
  vadd v p := (v.1 +ᵥ p.1, v.2 +ᵥ p.2)
  zero_vadd _ := Prod.ext (zero_vadd _ _) (zero_vadd _ _)
  add_vadd _ _ _ := Prod.ext (add_vadd _ _ _) (add_vadd _ _ _)
  vsub p₁ p₂ := (p₁.1 -ᵥ p₂.1, p₁.2 -ᵥ p₂.2)
  vsub_vadd' _ _ := Prod.ext (vsub_vadd _ _) (vsub_vadd _ _)
  vadd_vsub' _ _ := Prod.ext (vadd_vsub _ _) (vadd_vsub _ _)

@[simp]

name: gcd_zero_left
statement: theorem gcd_zero_left (a : R) : gcd 0 a = a
proof:
rw [gcd]
  exact if_pos rfl

/-- An implementation of the extended GCD algorithm.
At each step we are computing a triple `(r, s, t)`, where `r` is the next value of the GCD
algorithm, to compute the greatest common divisor of the input (say `x` and `y`), and `s` and `t`
are the coefficients in front of `x` and `y` to obtain `r` (i.e. `r = s * x + t * y`).
The function `xgcdAux` takes in two triples, and from these recursively computes the next triple:
```
xgcdAux (r, s, t) (r', s', t') = xgcdAux (r' % r, s' - (r' / r) * s, t' - (r' / r) * t) (r, s, t)
```
-/

name: gcd_zero_right
statement: theorem gcd_zero_right (a : R) : gcd a 0 = a
proof:
rw [gcd]
  split_ifs with h <;> simp only [h, zero_mod, gcd_zero_left]

name: gcd_val
statement: theorem gcd_val (a b : R) : gcd a b = gcd (b % a) a
proof:
rw [gcd]
  split_ifs with h <;> [simp only [h, mod_zero, gcd_zero_right]; rfl]

name: xgcdAux_val
statement: theorem xgcdAux_val (x y : R) : xgcdAux x 1 0 y 0 1 = (gcd x y, xgcd x y)
proof:
rw [xgcd, ← xgcdAux_fst x y 1 0 0 1]

set_option backward.privateInPublic true in
private def P (a b : R) : R × R × R → Prop
  | (r, s, t) => (r : R) = a * s + b * t

set_option backward.privateInPublic true in
set_option backward.privateInPublic.warn false in

name: gcd_eq_gcd_ab
statement: theorem gcd_eq_gcd_ab (a b : R) : (gcd a b : R) = a * gcdA a b + b * gcdB a b
proof:
have :=
    @xgcdAux_P _ _ _ a b a b 1 0 0 1 (by dsimp [P]; rw [mul_one, mul_zero, add_zero])
      (by dsimp [P]; rw [mul_one, mul_zero, zero_add])
  rwa [xgcdAux_val, xgcd_val] at this

-- see Note [lower instance priority]
instance (priority := 70) (R : Type*) [e : EuclideanDomain R] : IsDomain R :=
  haveI := Classical.decEq R
  have : NoZeroDivisors R :=
  { eq_zero_or_eq_zero_of_mul_eq_zero {a b} h :=
      or_iff_not_and_not.2 fun h0 ↦ h0.1 <| by rw [← mul_div_cancel_right₀ a h0.2, h, zero_div] }
  { e, NoZeroDivisors.to_isDomain R with }

name: gcd_mul_lcm
statement: theorem gcd_mul_lcm (x y : R) : gcd x y * lcm x y = x * y
proof:
rw [lcm]; by_cases h : gcd x y = 0
  · rw [h, zero_mul]
    rw [EuclideanDomain.gcd_eq_zero_iff] at h
    rw [h.1, zero_mul]
  rcases gcd_dvd x y with ⟨⟨r, hr⟩, ⟨s, hs⟩⟩
  generalize gcd x y = g at h hr ⊢; subst hr
  rw [mul_assoc, mul_div_cancel_left₀ _ h]

end LCM

section Div

name: lcm_ndunion
statement: theorem lcm_ndunion (s₁ s₂ : Multiset α) : (ndunion s₁ s₂).lcm = GCDMonoid.lcm s₁.lcm s₂.lcm
proof:
rw [← lcm_dedup, dedup_ext.2, lcm_dedup, lcm_add]
  simp

@[simp]

name: lcm_union
statement: theorem lcm_union (s₁ s₂ : Multiset α) : (s₁ ∪ s₂).lcm = GCDMonoid.lcm s₁.lcm s₂.lcm
proof:
rw [← lcm_dedup, dedup_ext.2, lcm_dedup, lcm_add]
  simp

@[simp]

name: gcd_eq_zero_iff
statement: theorem gcd_eq_zero_iff (s : Multiset α) : s.gcd = 0 ↔ ∀ x ∈ s, x = 0
proof:
constructor
  · intro h x hx
    apply eq_zero_of_zero_dvd
    rw [← h]
    apply gcd_dvd hx
  · refine s.induction_on ?_ ?_
    · simp
    intro a s sgcd h
    simp [h a (mem_cons_self a s), sgcd fun x hx ↦ h x (mem_cons_of_mem hx)]

name: gcd_ne_zero_iff
statement: theorem gcd_ne_zero_iff (s : Multiset α) : s.gcd ≠ 0 ↔ ∃ x ∈ s, x ≠ 0
proof: simp [gcd_eq_zero_iff]

name: gcd_ndunion
statement: theorem gcd_ndunion (s₁ s₂ : Multiset α) : (ndunion s₁ s₂).gcd = GCDMonoid.gcd s₁.gcd s₂.gcd
proof:
rw [← gcd_dedup, dedup_ext.2, gcd_dedup, gcd_add]
  simp

@[simp]

name: gcd_union
statement: theorem gcd_union (s₁ s₂ : Multiset α) : (s₁ ∪ s₂).gcd = GCDMonoid.gcd s₁.gcd s₂.gcd
proof:
rw [← gcd_dedup, dedup_ext.2, gcd_dedup, gcd_add]
  simp

@[simp]

name: abs_eq_normalize
statement: theorem abs_eq_normalize (z : ℤ) : |z| = normalize z
proof:
cases le_total 0 z <;>
  simp [abs_of_nonneg, abs_of_nonpos, normalize_of_nonneg, normalize_of_nonpos, *]

name: exists_unit_of_abs
statement: theorem exists_unit_of_abs (a : ℤ) : ∃ (u : ℤ) (_ : IsUnit u), (Int.natAbs a : ℤ) = u * a
proof:
rcases natAbs_eq a with h | h
  · use 1, isUnit_one
    rw [← h, one_mul]
  · use -1, isUnit_one.neg
    rw [← neg_eq_iff_eq_neg.mpr h]
    simp only [neg_mul, one_mul]

name: normUnit_mul_normUnit
statement: theorem normUnit_mul_normUnit (a : α) : normUnit (a * normUnit a) = 1
proof:
nontriviality α using Subsingleton.elim a 0
  obtain rfl | h := eq_or_ne a 0
  · rw [normUnit_zero, zero_mul, normUnit_zero]
  · rw [normUnit_mul h (Units.ne_zero _), normUnit_coe_units, mul_inv_eq_one]

@[simp]

name: out_zero
statement: theorem out_zero  : (0 : Associates α).out = 0
proof:
simp

end Associates

/-- GCD monoid: a cancellative `CommMonoidWithZero` with `gcd` (greatest common divisor) and
`lcm` (least common multiple) operations, determined up to a unit. The type class focuses on `gcd`
and we derive the corresponding `lcm` facts from `gcd`.
-/

name: lcm_eq_zero_iff
statement: theorem lcm_eq_zero_iff  : s.lcm f = 0 ↔ ∃ x ∈ s, f x = 0
proof: simp only [lcm_def, Multiset.lcm_eq_zero_iff, Multiset.mem_map, mem_val]

name: lcm_ne_zero_iff
statement: theorem lcm_ne_zero_iff  : s.lcm f ≠ 0 ↔ ∀ x ∈ s, f x ≠ 0
proof:
simp [lcm_eq_zero_iff]

end lcm

/-! ### gcd -/


section gcd

/-- Greatest common divisor of a finite set -/

name: gcd_eq_zero_iff
statement: theorem gcd_eq_zero_iff  : s.gcd f = 0 ↔ ∀ x ∈ s, f x = 0
proof:
induction s using Finset.cons_induction_on with
  | empty => simp
  | cons a s h ih => grind [gcd_cons, _root_.gcd_eq_zero_iff]

name: gcd_ne_zero_iff
statement: theorem gcd_ne_zero_iff  : s.gcd f ≠ 0 ↔ ∃ x ∈ s, f x ≠ 0
proof: simp [gcd_eq_zero_iff]

name: gcd_eq_gcd_filter_ne_zero
statement: theorem gcd_eq_gcd_filter_ne_zero  : s.gcd f = {x ∈ s | f x ≠ 0}.gcd f
proof:
classical
    trans ({x ∈ s | f x = 0} ∪ {x ∈ s | f x ≠ 0}).gcd f
    · rw [filter_union_filter_not_eq]
    rw [gcd_union]
    refine Eq.trans (?_ : _ = GCDMonoid.gcd (0 : α) ?_) (?_ : GCDMonoid.gcd (0 : α) _ = _)
    · exact gcd {x ∈ s | f x ≠ 0} f
    · refine congr (congr rfl <| s.induction_on ?_ ?_) (by simp)
      · simp
      · intro a s _ h
        rw [filter_insert]
        split_ifs with h1 <;> simp [h, h1]
    simp only [gcd_zero_left, normalize_gcd]

nonrec theorem gcd_mul_left {a : α} : (s.gcd fun x ↦ a * f x) = normalize a * s.gcd f := by
  classical
    refine s.induction_on ?_ ?_
    · simp
    · intro b t _ h
      rw [gcd_insert, gcd_insert, h, ← gcd_mul_left]
      apply ((normalize_associated a).mul_right _).gcd_eq_right

nonrec theorem gcd_mul_right {a : α} : (s.gcd fun x ↦ f x * a) = s.gcd f * normalize a := by
  classical
    refine s.induction_on ?_ ?_
    · simp
    · intro b t _ h
      rw [gcd_insert, gcd_insert, h, ← gcd_mul_right]
      apply ((normalize_associated a).mul_left _).gcd_eq_right

name: contsAux_stable_step_of_terminated
statement: theorem contsAux_stable_step_of_terminated (terminatedAt_n : g.TerminatedAt n) : g.contsAux (n + 2) = g.contsAux (n + 1)
proof:
rw [terminatedAt_iff_s_none] at terminatedAt_n
  simp only [contsAux, terminatedAt_n]

name: partNum_none_iff_s_none
statement: theorem partNum_none_iff_s_none  : g.partNums.get? n = none ↔ g.s.get? n = none
proof: cases s_nth_eq : g.s.get? n <;> simp [partNums, s_nth_eq]

name: terminatedAt_iff_partNum_none
statement: theorem terminatedAt_iff_partNum_none  : g.TerminatedAt n ↔ g.partNums.get? n = none
proof: rw [terminatedAt_iff_s_none, partNum_none_iff_s_none]

name: partDen_none_iff_s_none
statement: theorem partDen_none_iff_s_none  : g.partDens.get? n = none ↔ g.s.get? n = none
proof: cases s_nth_eq : g.s.get? n <;> simp [partDens, s_nth_eq]

name: terminatedAt_iff_partDen_none
statement: theorem terminatedAt_iff_partDen_none  : g.TerminatedAt n ↔ g.partDens.get? n = none
proof: rw [terminatedAt_iff_s_none, partDen_none_iff_s_none]

name: zeroth_conv_eq_h
statement: theorem zeroth_conv_eq_h  : g.convs 0 = g.h
proof: simp [conv_eq_num_div_den, num_eq_conts_a, den_eq_conts_b, div_one]

name: squashSeq_succ_n_tail_eq_squashSeq_tail_n
statement: theorem squashSeq_succ_n_tail_eq_squashSeq_tail_n  : (squashSeq s (n + 1)).tail = squashSeq s.tail n
proof:
cases s_succ_succ_nth_eq : s.get? (n + 2) with
  | none =>
    cases s_succ_nth_eq : s.get? (n + 1) <;>
      simp only [squashSeq, Stream'.Seq.get?_tail, s_succ_nth_eq, s_succ_succ_nth_eq]
  | some gp_succ_succ_n =>
    obtain ⟨gp_succ_n, s_succ_nth_eq⟩ : ∃ gp_succ_n, s.get? (n + 1) = some gp_succ_n :=
      s.ge_stable (n + 1).le_succ s_succ_succ_nth_eq
    -- apply extensionality with `m` and continue by cases `m = n`.
    ext1 m
    rcases Decidable.em (m = n) with m_eq_n | m_ne_n
    · simp [*, squashSeq]
    · cases s_succ_mth_eq : s.get? (m + 1)
      · simp only [*, squashSeq, Stream'.Seq.get?_tail, Stream'.Seq.get?_zipWith,
          Option.map₂_none_right]
      · simp [*, squashSeq]

/-- The auxiliary function `convs'Aux` returns the same value for a sequence and the
corresponding squashed sequence at the squashed position. -/

name: squashGCF_eq_self_of_terminated
statement: theorem squashGCF_eq_self_of_terminated (terminatedAt_n : TerminatedAt g n) : squashGCF g n = g
proof:
cases n with
  | zero =>
    change g.s.get? 0 = none at terminatedAt_n
    simp only [squashGCF, terminatedAt_n]
  | succ =>
    cases g
    simp only [squashGCF, mk.injEq, true_and]
    exact squashSeq_eq_self_of_terminated terminatedAt_n

/-- The values before the squashed position stay the same. -/

name: prod_ofFn
statement: theorem prod_ofFn (f : Fin n → M) : (List.ofFn f).prod = ∏ i, f i
proof:
simp [prod_eq_multiset_prod]

@[to_additive]

name: prod_univ_def
statement: theorem prod_univ_def (f : Fin n → M) : ∏ i, f i = ((List.finRange n).map f).prod
proof:
rw [← List.ofFn_eq_map, prod_ofFn]

/-- A product of a function `f : Fin 0 → M` is `1` because `Fin 0` is empty -/
@[to_additive /-- A sum of a function `f : Fin 0 → M` is `0` because `Fin 0` is empty -/]

name: prod_univ_getElem
statement: theorem prod_univ_getElem (l : List M) : ∏ i : Fin l.length, l[i.1] = l.prod
proof:
simp [Finset.prod_eq_multiset_prod]

@[to_additive (attr := simp)]

name: prod_univ_two
statement: theorem prod_univ_two (f : Fin 2 → M) : ∏ i, f i = f 0 * f 1
proof:
simp [prod_univ_succ]

@[to_additive]

name: prod_univ_three
statement: theorem prod_univ_three (f : Fin 3 → M) : ∏ i, f i = f 0 * f 1 * f 2
proof:
rw [prod_univ_castSucc, prod_univ_two]
  rfl

@[to_additive]

name: prod_univ_four
statement: theorem prod_univ_four (f : Fin 4 → M) : ∏ i, f i = f 0 * f 1 * f 2 * f 3
proof:
rw [prod_univ_castSucc, prod_univ_three]
  rfl

@[to_additive]

name: prod_univ_five
statement: theorem prod_univ_five (f : Fin 5 → M) : ∏ i, f i = f 0 * f 1 * f 2 * f 3 * f 4
proof:
rw [prod_univ_castSucc, prod_univ_four]
  rfl

@[to_additive]

name: prod_univ_six
statement: theorem prod_univ_six (f : Fin 6 → M) : ∏ i, f i = f 0 * f 1 * f 2 * f 3 * f 4 * f 5
proof:
rw [prod_univ_castSucc, prod_univ_five]
  rfl

@[to_additive]

name: prod_univ_seven
statement: theorem prod_univ_seven (f : Fin 7 → M) : ∏ i, f i = f 0 * f 1 * f 2 * f 3 * f 4 * f 5 * f 6
proof:
rw [prod_univ_castSucc, prod_univ_six]
  rfl

@[to_additive]

name: prod_univ_eight
statement: theorem prod_univ_eight (f : Fin 8 → M) : ∏ i, f i = f 0 * f 1 * f 2 * f 3 * f 4 * f 5 * f 6 * f 7
proof:
rw [prod_univ_castSucc, prod_univ_seven]
  rfl

@[to_additive]

name: sum_range_id
statement: theorem sum_range_id (n : ℕ) : ∑ i ∈ range n, i = n * (n - 1) / 2
proof:
rw [← sum_range_id_mul_two n, Nat.mul_div_cancel _ Nat.zero_lt_two]

end GaussSum

@[to_additive]

name: prod_Ico_div
statement: theorem prod_Ico_div (hmn : m ≤ n) : ∏ i ∈ Ico m n, f (i + 1) / f i = f n / f m
proof:
rw [prod_Ico_eq_div _ hmn, prod_range_div, prod_range_div, div_div_div_cancel_right]

@[to_additive]

name: dens_biUnion_le
statement: theorem dens_biUnion_le  : (s.biUnion t).dens ≤ ∑ a ∈ s, (t a).dens
proof:
simp only [dens, ← sum_div]
  gcongr
  exact mod_cast card_biUnion_le

name: balance_add
statement: theorem balance_add (f g : ι → G) : balance (f + g) = balance f + balance g
proof:
simp only [balance, expect_add_distrib, ← const_add, add_sub_add_comm, Pi.add_apply]

@[simp] lemma balance_sub (f g : ι → G) : balance (f - g) = balance f - balance g := by
  simp only [balance, expect_sub_distrib, const_sub, sub_sub_sub_comm, Pi.sub_apply]

@[simp] lemma balance_neg (f : ι → G) : balance (-f) = -balance f := by
  simp only [balance, expect_neg_distrib, const_neg, neg_sub', Pi.neg_apply]

@[simp] lemma sum_balance (f : ι → G) : ∑ x, balance f x = 0 := by
  cases isEmpty_or_nonempty ι <;> simp [balance_apply]

@[simp] lemma expect_balance (f : ι → G) : 𝔼 x, balance f x = 0 := by simp [expect]

@[simp] lemma balance_idem (f : ι → G) : balance (balance f) = balance f := by
  cases isEmpty_or_nonempty ι <;> ext x <;> simp [balance, expect_sub_distrib, univ_nonempty]

@[simp] lemma map_balance [FunLike F G H] [LinearMapClass F ℚ≥0 G H] (g : F) (f : ι → G) (a : ι) :
    g (balance f a) = balance (g ∘ f) a := by simp [balance, map_expect]

end AddCommGroup
end Fintype

name: finprod_one
statement: theorem finprod_one  : (∏ᶠ _ : α, (1 : M)) = 1
proof:
have : (mulSupport fun x : PLift α => (fun _ => 1 : α → M) x.down) ⊆ (∅ : Finset (PLift α)) :=
    fun x h => by simp at h
  rw [finprod_eq_prod_plift_of_mulSupport_subset this, Finset.prod_empty]

@[to_additive]

name: finprod_mem_mulSupport
statement: theorem finprod_mem_mulSupport (f : α → M) : ∏ᶠ a ∈ mulSupport f, f a = ∏ᶠ a, f a
proof:
rw [finprod_mem_def, mulIndicator_mulSupport]

@[to_additive]

name: finprod_def
statement: theorem finprod_def (f : α → M) : ∏ᶠ i : α, f i = if h : HasFiniteMulSupport f then ∏ i ∈ h.toFinset, f i else 1
proof:
split_ifs with h
  · exact finprod_eq_prod_of_mulSupport_toFinset_subset _ h (Finset.Subset.refl _)
  · rw [finprod, dif_neg]
    rw [HasFiniteMulSupport, mulSupport_comp_eq_preimage]
    exact mt (fun hf => hf.of_preimage Equiv.plift.surjective) h

@[to_additive]

name: finprod_mem_of_eqOn_one
statement: theorem finprod_mem_of_eqOn_one (hf : s.EqOn f 1) : ∏ᶠ i ∈ s, f i = 1
proof:
rw [← finprod_mem_one s]
  exact finprod_mem_congr rfl hf

/-- If the product of `f i` over `i ∈ s` is not equal to `1`, then there is some `x ∈ s` such that
`f x ≠ 1`. -/
@[to_additive
      /-- If the sum of `f i` over `i ∈ s` is not equal to `0`, then there is some `x ∈ s`
      such that `f x ≠ 0`. -/]

name: exists_ne_one_of_finprod_mem_ne_one
statement: theorem exists_ne_one_of_finprod_mem_ne_one (h : ∏ᶠ i ∈ s, f i ≠ 1) : ∃ x ∈ s, f x ≠ 1
proof:
by_contra! h'
  exact h (finprod_mem_of_eqOn_one h')

/-- Given a finite set `s`, the product of `f i * g i` over `i ∈ s` equals the product of `f i`
over `i ∈ s` times the product of `g i` over `i ∈ s`. -/
@[to_additive
      /-- Given a finite set `s`, the sum of `f i + g i` over `i ∈ s` equals the sum of `f i`
      over `i ∈ s` plus the sum of `g i` over `i ∈ s`. -/]

name: finprod_mem_singleton
statement: theorem finprod_mem_singleton  : (∏ᶠ i ∈ ({a} : Set α), f i) = f a
proof:
rw [← Finset.coe_singleton, finprod_mem_coe_finset, Finset.prod_singleton]

@[to_additive (attr := simp)]

name: finprod_mem_insert_of_eq_one_if_notMem
statement: theorem finprod_mem_insert_of_eq_one_if_notMem (h : a ∉ s → f a = 1) : ∏ᶠ i ∈ insert a s, f i = ∏ᶠ i ∈ s, f i
proof:
refine finprod_mem_inter_mulSupport_eq' _ _ _ fun x hx => ⟨?_, Or.inr⟩
  rintro (rfl | hxs)
  exacts [not_imp_comm.1 h hx, hxs]

/-- If `f a = 1`, then the product of `f i` over `i ∈ insert a s` equals the product of `f i` over
`i ∈ s`. -/
@[to_additive
      /-- If `f a = 0`, then the sum of `f i` over `i ∈ insert a s` equals the sum of `f i`
      over `i ∈ s`. -/]

name: finprod_mem_pair
statement: theorem finprod_mem_pair (h : a ≠ b) : (∏ᶠ i ∈ ({a, b} : Set α), f i) = f a * f b
proof:
rw [finprod_mem_insert, finprod_mem_singleton]
  exacts [h, finite_singleton b]

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
/-- The product of `f y` over `y ∈ g '' s` equals the product of `f (g i)` over `s`
provided that `g` is injective on `s ∩ mulSupport (f ∘ g)`. -/
@[to_additive
      /-- The sum of `f y` over `y ∈ g '' s` equals the sum of `f (g i)` over `s` provided that
      `g` is injective on `s ∩ support (f ∘ g)`. -/]

name: finprod_set_coe_eq_finprod_mem
statement: theorem finprod_set_coe_eq_finprod_mem (s : Set α) : ∏ᶠ j : s, f j = ∏ᶠ i ∈ s, f i
proof:
rw [← finprod_mem_range, Subtype.range_coe]
  exact Subtype.coe_injective

@[to_additive]

name: expect_univ
statement: theorem expect_univ  : 𝔼 i, f i = (∑ i, f i) /ℚ Fintype.card ι
proof:
rw [expect, card_univ]

@[simp] lemma expect_empty (f : ι → M) : 𝔼 i ∈ ∅, f i = 0 := by simp [expect]

@[simp] lemma expect_singleton (f : ι → M) (i : ι) : 𝔼 j ∈ {i}, f j = f i := by simp [expect]

@[simp] lemma expect_const_zero (s : Finset ι) : 𝔼 _i ∈ s, (0 : M) = 0 := by simp [expect]

@[congr]

name: exists_ne_zero_of_expect_ne_zero
statement: theorem exists_ne_zero_of_expect_ne_zero (h : 𝔼 i ∈ s, f i ≠ 0) : ∃ i ∈ s, f i ≠ 0
proof: contrapose! h; exact expect_eq_zero h

name: listProd_map
statement: theorem listProd_map (h : ∀ x ∈ l, f x ≡ g x [MOD n]) : (l.map f).prod ≡ (l.map g).prod [MOD n]
proof: induction l <;> aesop (add unsafe ModEq.mul)

name: listSum_map
statement: theorem listSum_map (h : ∀ x ∈ l, f x ≡ g x [MOD n]) : (l.map f).sum ≡ (l.map g).sum [MOD n]
proof: induction l <;> aesop (add unsafe ModEq.add)

name: listSum_map_zero
statement: theorem listSum_map_zero (h : ∀ x ∈ l, f x ≡ 0 [MOD n]) : (l.map f).sum ≡ 0 [MOD n]
proof: simpa using listSum_map h

name: listProd_map
statement: theorem listProd_map (h : ∀ x ∈ l, f x ≡ g x [ZMOD n]) : (l.map f).prod ≡ (l.map g).prod [ZMOD n]
proof: induction l <;> aesop (add unsafe ModEq.mul)

name: listSum_map
statement: theorem listSum_map (h : ∀ x ∈ l, f x ≡ g x [ZMOD n]) : (l.map f).sum ≡ (l.map g).sum [ZMOD n]
proof: induction l <;> aesop (add unsafe ModEq.add)

name: listSum_map_zero
statement: theorem listSum_map_zero (h : ∀ x ∈ l, f x ≡ 0 [ZMOD n]) : (l.map f).sum ≡ 0 [ZMOD n]
proof: simpa using listSum_map h

name: sum_eq_top
statement: theorem sum_eq_top  : ∑ i ∈ s, f i = ⊤ ↔ ∃ i ∈ s, f i = ⊤
proof:
induction s using Finset.cons_induction <;> simp [*]

/-- A sum is finite iff all terms are finite. -/

name: sum_lt_top
statement: theorem sum_lt_top  : ∑ i ∈ s, f i < ⊤ ↔ ∀ i ∈ s, f i < ⊤
proof:
simp [WithTop.lt_top_iff_ne_top]

end AddCommMonoid

section CommMonoidWithZero
variable [CommMonoidWithZero M₀] [NoZeroDivisors M₀] [Nontrivial M₀] [DecidableEq M₀]
  {s : Finset ι} {f : ι → WithTop M₀} {i : ι}

/-- A product of finite terms is finite. -/

name: prod_eq_top_ex_top
statement: theorem prod_eq_top_ex_top (h : ∏ j ∈ s, f j = ⊤) : ∃ i ∈ s, f i = ⊤
proof:
contrapose! h
  exact WithTop.prod_ne_top h

/-- A product is infinite iff each factor is nonzero and some factor is infinite -/

name: prod_eq_top_iff
statement: theorem prod_eq_top_iff  : ∏ j ∈ s, f j = ⊤ ↔ (∃ i ∈ s, f i = ⊤) ∧ (∀ i ∈ s, f i ≠ 0)
proof:
constructor
  · exact fun h ↦ ⟨prod_eq_top_ex_top h, fun _ ih ↦ prod_eq_top_ne_zero ih h⟩
  · exact fun ⟨h, h'⟩ ↦ prod_eq_top (Exists.choose_spec h).1 (Exists.choose_spec h).2 h'

end CommMonoidWithZero
end WithTop

namespace WithBot
section AddCommMonoid
variable [AddCommMonoid M] {s : Finset ι} {f : ι → WithBot M}

@[simp, norm_cast] lemma coe_sum (s : Finset ι) (f : ι → M) :
    ∑ i ∈ s, f i = ∑ i ∈ s, (f i : WithBot M) := map_sum addHom f s

/-- A sum is infinite iff one term is infinite. -/

name: sum_eq_bot_iff
statement: theorem sum_eq_bot_iff  : ∑ i ∈ s, f i = ⊥ ↔ ∃ i ∈ s, f i = ⊥
proof:
induction s using Finset.cons_induction <;> simp [*]

variable [LT M]

/-- A sum is finite iff all terms are finite. -/

name: bot_lt_sum_iff
statement: theorem bot_lt_sum_iff  : ⊥ < ∑ i ∈ s, f i ↔ ∀ i ∈ s, ⊥ < f i
proof:
simp only [WithBot.bot_lt_iff_ne_bot, ne_eq, sum_eq_bot_iff, not_exists, not_and]

/-- A sum of finite terms is finite. -/

name: normalizer_eq_self_of_isCartanSubalgebra
statement: theorem normalizer_eq_self_of_isCartanSubalgebra (H : LieSubalgebra R L) : H.toLieSubmodule.normalizer = H.toLieSubmodule
proof:
rw [← LieSubmodule.toSubmodule_inj, coe_normalizer_eq_normalizer,
    IsCartanSubalgebra.self_normalizing, coe_toLieSubmodule]

@[simp]

name: isCartanSubalgebra_iff_isUcsLimit
statement: theorem isCartanSubalgebra_iff_isUcsLimit  : H.IsCartanSubalgebra ↔ H.toLieSubmodule.IsUcsLimit
proof:
constructor
  · intro h
    have h₁ : LieRing.IsNilpotent H := by infer_instance
    obtain ⟨k, hk⟩ := H.toLieSubmodule.isNilpotent_iff_exists_self_le_ucs.mp h₁
    replace hk : H.toLieSubmodule = LieSubmodule.ucs k ⊥ :=
      le_antisymm hk
        (LieSubmodule.ucs_le_of_normalizer_eq_self H.normalizer_eq_self_of_isCartanSubalgebra k)
    refine ⟨k, fun l hl => ?_⟩
    rw [← Nat.sub_add_cancel hl, LieSubmodule.ucs_add, ← hk,
      LieSubalgebra.ucs_eq_self_of_isCartanSubalgebra]
  · rintro ⟨k, hk⟩
    exact
      { nilpotent := by
          dsimp only [LieRing.IsNilpotent]
          -- The instance for the second `H` in the goal is `lieRingSelfModule`
          -- but `rw` expects it to be `H.toLieSubmodule.instLieRingModuleSubtypeMem`,
          -- and these are not reducibly defeq.
          erw [H.toLieSubmodule.isNilpotent_iff_exists_lcs_eq_bot]
          use k
          rw [_root_.eq_bot_iff, LieSubmodule.lcs_le_iff, hk k (le_refl k)]
        self_normalizing := by
          have hk' := hk (k + 1) k.le_succ
          rw [LieSubmodule.ucs_succ, hk k (le_refl k)] at hk'
          rw [← LieSubalgebra.toSubmodule_inj, ← LieSubalgebra.coe_normalizer_eq_normalizer,
            hk', LieSubalgebra.coe_toLieSubmodule] }

name: atomistic
statement: theorem atomistic  : ∀ I : LieIdeal K L, sSup {J : LieIdeal K L | IsAtom J ∧ J ≤ I} = I
proof:
intro I
  apply le_antisymm
  · apply sSup_le
    rintro J ⟨-, hJ'⟩
    exact hJ'
  by_cases hI : I = ⊥
  · exact hI.le.trans bot_le
  obtain ⟨J, hJ, hJI⟩ := (eq_bot_or_exists_atom_le I).resolve_left hI
  let J' := orthogonal Φ hΦ_inv J
  suffices I ≤ J ⊔ (J' ⊓ I) by
    refine this.trans ?_
    apply sup_le
    · exact le_sSup ⟨hJ, hJI⟩
    rw [← atomistic (J' ⊓ I)]
    apply sSup_le_sSup
    simp only [le_inf_iff, Set.setOf_subset_setOf, and_imp]
    tauto
  suffices J ⊔ J' = ⊤ by rw [← sup_inf_assoc_of_le _ hJI, this, top_inf_eq]
  exact (orthogonal_isCompl Φ hΦ_nondeg hΦ_inv hΦ_refl hL J hJ).codisjoint.eq_top
termination_by I => finrank K I
decreasing_by
  apply finrank_lt_finrank_of_lt
  suffices ¬I ≤ J' by simpa
  intro hIJ'
  apply hJ.1
  rw [eq_bot_iff]
  exact orthogonal_disjoint Φ hΦ_nondeg hΦ_inv hL J hJ le_rfl (hJI.trans hIJ')

open LieSubmodule in
/--
A finite-dimensional Lie algebra over a field is semisimple
if it does not have non-trivial abelian ideals and it admits a
non-degenerate reflexive invariant bilinear form.
Here a form is *invariant* if it is compatible with the Lie bracket: `Φ ⁅x, y⁆ z = Φ x ⁅y, z⁆`.
-/

name: isSemisimple_of_nondegenerate
statement: theorem isSemisimple_of_nondegenerate  : IsSemisimple K L
proof:
refine ⟨?_, ?_, hL⟩
  · simpa using atomistic Φ hΦ_nondeg hΦ_inv hΦ_refl hL ⊤
  intro I hI
  apply (orthogonal_disjoint Φ hΦ_nondeg hΦ_inv hL I hI).mono_right
  apply sSup_le
  simp only [Set.mem_diff, Set.mem_setOf_eq, Set.mem_singleton_iff, and_imp]
  intro J hJ hJI
  rw [← lie_eq_self_of_isAtom_of_nonabelian J hJ (hL J hJ), lieIdeal_oper_eq_span, lieSpan_le]
  rintro _ ⟨x, y, rfl⟩
  simp only [orthogonal_carrier, LinearMap.IsOrtho, Set.mem_setOf_eq]
  intro z hz
  rw [← neg_eq_zero, ← hΦ_inv]
  suffices ⁅(x : L), z⁆ = 0 by simp only [this, map_zero, LinearMap.zero_apply]
  rw [← LieSubmodule.mem_bot (R := K) (L := L), ← (hJ.disjoint_of_ne hI hJI).eq_bot]
  apply lie_le_inf
  exact lie_mem_lie x.2 hz

end field

end InvariantForm

end LieAlgebra

name: lie_h_e_smul
statement: theorem lie_h_e_smul (t : IsSl2Triple h e f) : ⁅h, e⁆ = (2 : R) • e
proof: simp [t.lie_h_e_nsmul, two_smul]

name: lie_lie_smul_f
statement: theorem lie_lie_smul_f (t : IsSl2Triple h e f) : ⁅h, f⁆ = -((2 : R) • f)
proof: simp [t.lie_h_f_nsmul, two_smul]

name: e_ne_zero
statement: theorem e_ne_zero (t : IsSl2Triple h e f) : e ≠ 0
proof:
have := t.h_ne_zero
  contrapose this
  simpa [this] using t.lie_e_f.symm

name: f_ne_zero
statement: theorem f_ne_zero (t : IsSl2Triple h e f) : f ≠ 0
proof:
have := t.h_ne_zero
  contrapose this
  simpa [this] using t.lie_e_f.symm

variable {R}

/-- Given a representation of a Lie algebra with distinguished `sl₂` triple, a vector is said to be
primitive if it is a simultaneous eigenvector for the action of both `h` and `e`, and the eigenvalue
for `e` is zero. -/

name: lie_h_pow_toEnd_f
statement: theorem lie_h_pow_toEnd_f (n : ℕ) : ⁅h, ψ n⁆ = (μ - 2 * n) • ψ n
proof:
induction n with
  | zero => simpa using P.lie_h
  | succ n ih =>
    rw [pow_succ', Module.End.mul_apply, toEnd_apply_apply, Nat.cast_add, Nat.cast_one,
      leibniz_lie h, t.lie_lie_smul_f R, ← neg_smul, ih, lie_smul, smul_lie, ← add_smul]
    congr
    ring

name: lie_e_pow_succ_toEnd_f
statement: theorem lie_e_pow_succ_toEnd_f (n : ℕ) : ⁅e, ψ (n + 1)⁆ = ((n + 1) * (μ - n)) • ψ n
proof:
induction n with
  | zero =>
      simp only [zero_add, pow_one, toEnd_apply_apply, Nat.cast_zero, sub_zero, one_mul,
        pow_zero, Module.End.one_apply, leibniz_lie e, t.lie_e_f, P.lie_e, P.lie_h, lie_zero,
        add_zero]
  | succ n ih =>
    rw [pow_succ', Module.End.mul_apply, toEnd_apply_apply, leibniz_lie e, t.lie_e_f,
      lie_h_pow_toEnd_f P, ih, lie_smul, lie_f_pow_toEnd_f P, ← add_smul,
      Nat.cast_add, Nat.cast_one]
    congr
    ring

/-- The eigenvalue of a primitive vector must be a natural number if the representation is
finite-dimensional. -/

name: lie_e_pow_toEnd_e
statement: theorem lie_e_pow_toEnd_e (n : ℕ) : ⁅e, φ n⁆ = φ (n + 1)
proof: simp [pow_succ']

name: exists_hasPrimitiveVectorWith
statement: theorem exists_hasPrimitiveVectorWith  : ∃ (μ : R) (m : M), m ≠ 0 ∧ HasPrimitiveVectorWith t m μ
proof:
obtain ⟨μ₀, hμ₀⟩ := IsTriangularizable.exists_hasEigenvalue R L M h
  obtain ⟨m₀, hm₀⟩ := hμ₀.exists_hasEigenvector
  let evals (n : ℕ) : R := μ₀ + 2 * (n : R)
  let e_vecs (n : ℕ) : M := ((toEnd R L M e) ^ n) m₀
  have h_exists_zero : ∃ k, e_vecs k = 0 := by
    by_contra! contra
    have h_inj : Function.Injective evals := fun a b hab ↦ by
      simpa [evals, add_right_inj, mul_eq_mul_left_iff, Nat.cast_inj] using hab
    have aux (μ : range evals) : (toEnd R L M h).HasEigenvector μ (e_vecs μ.property.choose) := by
      set n := μ.property.choose
      refine ⟨?_, contra n⟩
      rw [Module.End.mem_eigenspace_iff, toEnd_apply_apply, ← μ.property.choose_spec]
      exact t.lie_h_pow_toEnd_e hm₀.apply_eq_smul n
    have _i := ((toEnd R L M h).eigenvectors_linearIndependent (range evals) _ aux).finite
    exact (Set.infinite_range_of_injective h_inj) (Set.toFinite _)
  obtain ⟨n, hn_ne, hn_zero⟩ := Nat.exists_not_and_succ_of_not_zero_of_exists hm₀.2 h_exists_zero
  exact ⟨evals n, e_vecs n, hn_ne,
    { ne_zero := hn_ne
      lie_h := t.lie_h_pow_toEnd_e hm₀.apply_eq_smul n
      lie_e := by rwa [lie_e_pow_toEnd_e n] }⟩

end CharZero

end IsSl2Triple

name: ker_killingForm_eq_bot
statement: theorem ker_killingForm_eq_bot  : LinearMap.ker (killingForm R L) = ⊥
proof: simp [← LieIdeal.coe_killingCompl_top, killingCompl_top_eq_bot]

name: killingForm_nondegenerate
statement: theorem killingForm_nondegenerate  : (killingForm R L).Nondegenerate
proof:
refine (LieModule.traceForm_isSymm R L L).isRefl.nondegenerate_iff_separatingLeft.mpr ?_
  simp [LinearMap.separatingLeft_iff_ker_eq_bot]

variable {R L} in

name: exists_smul_add_of_span_sup_eq_top
statement: theorem exists_smul_add_of_span_sup_eq_top (y : L) : ∃ t : R, ∃ z ∈ I, y = t • x + z
proof:
have hy : y ∈ (⊤ : Submodule R L) := Submodule.mem_top
  simp only [← hxI, Submodule.mem_sup, Submodule.mem_span_singleton] at hy
  obtain ⟨-, ⟨t, rfl⟩, z, hz, rfl⟩ := hy
  exact ⟨t, z, hz, rfl⟩

name: isSolvable_of_killingForm_apply_lie_eq_zero
statement: theorem isSolvable_of_killingForm_apply_lie_eq_zero (h : ∀ x, ∀ y ∈ derivedSeries R L 1, killingForm R L x y = 0) : IsSolvable L
proof:
suffices IsSolvable (⊤ : LieIdeal R L) by
    rwa [← solvable_iff_equiv_solvable LieSubalgebra.topEquiv (R := R)]
  apply LieIdeal.isSolvable_of_killingForm_apply_lie_eq_zero
  aesop

variable (R L)

/-- The Killing radical of a finite-dimensional Lie algebra is contained in the solvable radical. -/
public lemma killingCompl_top_le_radical :
    LieIdeal.killingCompl R L ⊤ ≤ radical R L := by
  rw [← LieIdeal.solvable_iff_le_radical]
  refine LieIdeal.isSolvable_of_killingForm_apply_lie_eq_zero _ ?_
  intro x hx y _
  rw [LieModule.traceForm_comm]
  exact (LieIdeal.mem_killingCompl R L ⊤).mp hx y (LieSubmodule.mem_top y)

/-- **Cartan's criterion for semisimplicity**: if a finite-dimensional Lie algebra has trivial
solvable radical, then its Killing form is non-degenerate.

See also `LieAlgebra.hasTrivialRadical_iff_isKilling`. -/
public instance HasTrivialRadical.instIsKilling [HasTrivialRadical R L] : IsKilling R L where
  killingCompl_top_eq_bot := by simpa using killingCompl_top_le_radical R L

public lemma hasTrivialRadical_iff_isKilling [IsPrincipalIdealRing R] :
    HasTrivialRadical R L ↔ IsKilling R L :=
  ⟨fun _ ↦ inferInstance, fun _ ↦ inferInstance⟩

name: traceForm_apply_lie_apply
statement: theorem traceForm_apply_lie_apply (x y z : L) : traceForm R L M ⁅x, y⁆ z = traceForm R L M x ⁅y, z⁆
proof:
calc traceForm R L M ⁅x, y⁆ z
      = trace R _ (φ ⁅x, y⁆ ∘ₗ φ z) := by simp only [traceForm_apply_apply]
    _ = trace R _ ((φ x * φ y - φ y * φ x) * φ z) := ?_
    _ = trace R _ (φ x * (φ y * φ z)) - trace R _ (φ y * (φ x * φ z)) := ?_
    _ = trace R _ (φ x * (φ y * φ z)) - trace R _ (φ x * (φ z * φ y)) := ?_
    _ = traceForm R L M x ⁅y, z⁆ := ?_
  · simp only [LieHom.map_lie, Ring.lie_def, ← Module.End.mul_eq_comp]
  · simp only [sub_mul, map_sub, mul_assoc]
  · simp only [LinearMap.trace_mul_cycle' R (φ x) (φ z) (φ y)]
  · simp only [traceForm_apply_apply, LieHom.map_lie, Ring.lie_def, mul_sub, map_sub,
      ← Module.End.mul_eq_comp]

/-- Given a representation `M` of a Lie algebra `L`, the action of any `x : L` is skew-adjoint
w.r.t. the trace form. -/

name: traceForm_lieInvariant
statement: theorem traceForm_lieInvariant  : (traceForm R L M).lieInvariant L
proof:
intro x y z
  rw [← lie_skew, map_neg, LinearMap.neg_apply, LieModule.traceForm_apply_lie_apply R L M]

/-- This lemma justifies the terminology "invariant" for trace forms. -/
@[simp] lemma lie_traceForm_eq_zero (x : L) : ⁅x, traceForm R L M⁆ = 0 := by
  ext y z
  rw [LieHom.lie_apply, LinearMap.sub_apply, Module.Dual.lie_apply, LinearMap.zero_apply,
    LinearMap.zero_apply, traceForm_apply_lie_apply', sub_self]

@[simp] lemma traceForm_eq_zero_of_isNilpotent [IsReduced R] [IsNilpotent L M] :
    traceForm R L M = 0 := by
  ext x y
  simp only [traceForm_apply_apply, LinearMap.zero_apply, ← isNilpotent_iff_eq_zero]
  apply LinearMap.isNilpotent_trace_of_isNilpotent
  exact isNilpotent_toEnd_of_isNilpotent₂ R L M x y

open scoped TensorProduct in
@[simp] lemma traceForm_baseChange [Module.Free R M] [Module.Finite R M]
    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [Algebra R A] :
    traceForm A (A ⊗[R] L) (A ⊗[R] M) = (traceForm R L M).baseChange A := by
  ext; simp [traceForm_apply_apply, ← LinearMap.baseChange_comp, Algebra.algebraMap_eq_smul_one]

variable {R L M} in

name: traceForm_eq_zero_of_isTrivial
statement: theorem traceForm_eq_zero_of_isTrivial  : traceForm R L M = 0
proof:
ext x y
  suffices φ x ∘ₗ φ y = 0 by simp [traceForm_apply_apply, this]
  ext m
  simp [trivial_lie_zero]

/-- Given a bilinear form `B` on a representation `M` of a nilpotent Lie algebra `L`, if `B` is
invariant (in the sense that the action of `L` is skew-adjoint w.r.t. `B`) then components of the
Fitting decomposition of `M` are orthogonal w.r.t. `B`. -/

name: traceForm_eq_of_le_idealizer
statement: theorem traceForm_eq_of_le_idealizer  : traceForm R I N = (traceForm R L M).restrict I
proof:
ext ⟨x, hx⟩ ⟨y, hy⟩
  change _ = trace R M (φ x ∘ₗ φ y)
