      (IsScalarTower.toAlgHom R (MvPolynomial S₂ R) _) = mapAlgHom (Algebra.ofId _ _) by
    exact DFunLike.congr_fun this p
  ext x : 1
  simp [commAlgEquiv]

name: optionEquivLeft_X_some
statement: theorem optionEquivLeft_X_some (x : S₁) : optionEquivLeft R S₁ (X (some x)) = Polynomial.C (X x)
proof:
simp [optionEquivLeft_apply, aeval_X]

@[simp]

name: optionEquivLeft_X_none
statement: theorem optionEquivLeft_X_none  : optionEquivLeft R S₁ (X none) = Polynomial.X
proof:
simp [optionEquivLeft_apply, aeval_X]

@[simp]

name: optionEquivLeft_C
statement: theorem optionEquivLeft_C (r : R) : optionEquivLeft R S₁ (C r) = Polynomial.C (C r)
proof: simp only [optionEquivLeft_apply, aeval_C, Polynomial.algebraMap_apply, algebraMap_eq]

name: optionEquivLeft_symm_C_X
statement: theorem optionEquivLeft_symm_C_X (x : S₁) : (optionEquivLeft R S₁).symm (.C (X x)) = .X (.some x)
proof:
simp [optionEquivLeft]

@[simp]

name: optionEquivRight_X_some
statement: theorem optionEquivRight_X_some (x : S₁) : optionEquivRight R S₁ (X (some x)) = X x
proof: simp [optionEquivRight_apply, aeval_X]

name: optionEquivRight_X_none
statement: theorem optionEquivRight_X_none  : optionEquivRight R S₁ (X none) = C Polynomial.X
proof: simp [optionEquivRight_apply, aeval_X]

name: optionEquivRight_C
statement: theorem optionEquivRight_C (r : R) : optionEquivRight R S₁ (C r) = C (Polynomial.C r)
proof:
simp only [optionEquivRight_apply, aeval_C, algebraMap_apply, Polynomial.algebraMap_eq]

variable (n : ℕ)

/-- The algebra isomorphism between multivariable polynomials in `Fin (n + 1)` and
polynomials over multivariable polynomials in `Fin n`.
-/

name: comap_id_apply
statement: theorem comap_id_apply (x : σ → R) : comap (AlgHom.id R (MvPolynomial σ R)) x = x
proof:
funext i
  simp only [comap, AlgHom.id_apply, aeval_X]

variable (σ R)

name: comap_id
statement: theorem comap_id  : comap (AlgHom.id R (MvPolynomial σ R)) = id

name: bind₁_X_left
statement: theorem bind₁_X_left  : bind₁ (X : σ → MvPolynomial σ R) = AlgHom.id R _
proof:
ext1 i
  simp

variable (f : σ → MvPolynomial τ R)

name: map_comp_C
statement: theorem map_comp_C (f : R →+* S) : (map f).comp (C : R →+* MvPolynomial σ R) = C.comp f
proof:
ext1
  apply map_C

-- mixing the two monad structures

name: degrees_zero
statement: theorem degrees_zero  : degrees (0 : MvPolynomial σ R) = 0
proof:
rw [← C_0]
  exact degrees_C 0

@[simp]

name: degrees_eq_zero_iff_support_subset_zero
statement: theorem degrees_eq_zero_iff_support_subset_zero  : p.degrees = 0 ↔ p.support ⊆ {0}
proof:
rw [Finset.subset_singleton_iff', Multiset.eq_zero_iff_forall_notMem]
  refine ⟨fun h s hs ↦ ?_, fun h i hi ↦ ?_⟩
  · rw [← Finsupp.support_eq_empty]
    simp only [mem_degrees] at h
    grind
  rcases mem_degrees.mp hi with ⟨s, hs1, hs2⟩
  have := Finsupp.support_eq_empty.mpr (h s <| mem_support_iff.mpr hs1) ▸ hs2
  grind

name: le_degrees_add_left
statement: theorem le_degrees_add_left (h : Disjoint p.degrees q.degrees) : p.degrees ≤ (p + q).degrees
proof:
classical
  apply Finset.sup_le
  intro d hd
  rw [Multiset.disjoint_iff_ne] at h
  obtain rfl | h0 := eq_or_ne d 0
  · rw [toMultiset_zero]; apply Multiset.zero_le
  · refine Finset.le_sup_of_le (b := d) ?_ le_rfl
    rw [mem_support_iff, coeff_add]
    suffices q.coeff d = 0 by rwa [this, add_zero, coeff, ← Finsupp.mem_support_iff]
    rw [Ne, ← Finsupp.support_eq_empty, ← Ne, ← Finset.nonempty_iff_ne_empty] at h0
    obtain ⟨j, hj⟩ := h0
    contrapose! h
    rw [mem_support_iff] at hd
    refine ⟨j, ?_, j, ?_, rfl⟩
    all_goals rw [mem_degrees]; refine ⟨d, ?_, hj⟩; assumption

name: le_degrees_add_right
statement: theorem le_degrees_add_right (h : Disjoint p.degrees q.degrees) : q.degrees ≤ (p + q).degrees
proof: simpa [add_comm] using le_degrees_add_left h.symm

name: degreeOf_zero
statement: theorem degreeOf_zero (n : σ) : degreeOf n (0 : MvPolynomial σ R) = 0
proof:
classical simp only [degreeOf_def, degrees_zero, Multiset.count_zero]

@[simp]

name: degreeOf_one
statement: theorem degreeOf_one (n : σ) : degreeOf n (1 : MvPolynomial σ R) = 0
proof:
classical simp [degreeOf_def, degrees_one]

@[simp]

name: totalDegree_eq
statement: theorem totalDegree_eq (p : MvPolynomial σ R) : p.totalDegree = p.support.sup fun m => Multiset.card (toMultiset m)
proof:
rw [totalDegree]
  congr; funext m
  exact (Finsupp.card_toMultiset _).symm

name: totalDegree_le_degrees_card
statement: theorem totalDegree_le_degrees_card (p : MvPolynomial σ R) : p.totalDegree ≤ Multiset.card p.degrees
proof:
classical
  rw [totalDegree_eq]
  exact Finset.sup_le fun s hs => Multiset.card_le_card <| Finset.le_sup hs

name: totalDegree_zero
statement: theorem totalDegree_zero  : (0 : MvPolynomial σ R).totalDegree = 0
proof:
rw [← C_0]; exact totalDegree_C (0 : R)

@[simp]

name: degreesLE_add
statement: theorem degreesLE_add  : degreesLE R σ (s + t) = degreesLE R σ s * degreesLE R σ t
proof:
classical
  rw [le_antisymm_iff, Submodule.mul_le]
  refine ⟨fun x hx ↦ x.as_sum ▸ sum_mem fun i hi ↦ ?_,
    fun x hx y hy ↦ degrees_mul_le.trans (add_le_add hx hy)⟩
  replace hi : i.toMultiset ≤ s + t := (Finset.le_sup hi).trans hx
  let a := (i.toMultiset - t).toFinsupp
  let b := (i.toMultiset ⊓ t).toFinsupp
  have : a + b = i := Multiset.toFinsupp.symm.injective (by simp [a, b, Multiset.sub_add_inter])
  have ha : a.toMultiset ≤ s := by simpa [a, add_comm (a := t)] using hi
  have hb : b.toMultiset ≤ t := by simp [b, Multiset.inter_le_right]
  rw [show monomial i (x.coeff i) = monomial a (x.coeff i) * monomial b 1 by simp [this]]
  exact Submodule.mul_mem_mul ((degrees_monomial _ _).trans ha) ((degrees_monomial _ _).trans hb)

@[simp] lemma degreesLE_zero : degreesLE R σ 0 = 1 := by
  refine le_antisymm (fun x hx ↦ ?_) (by simp)
  simp only [mem_degreesLE, nonpos_iff_eq_zero] at hx
  have := (totalDegree_eq_zero_iff_eq_C (p := x)).mp
    (Nat.eq_zero_of_le_zero (x.totalDegree_le_degrees_card.trans (by simp [hx])))
  exact ⟨x.coeff 0, by simp [Algebra.smul_def, ← this]⟩

variable (s) in

name: eval₂_add
statement: theorem eval₂_add  : (p + q).eval₂ f g = p.eval₂ f g + q.eval₂ f g
proof:
classical exact Finsupp.sum_add_index (by simp [f.map_zero]) (by simp [add_mul, f.map_add])

@[simp]

name: eval₂_C
statement: theorem eval₂_C (a) : (C a).eval₂ f g = f a
proof:
rw [C_apply, eval₂_monomial, prod_zero_index, mul_one]

@[simp]

name: eval₂_X
statement: theorem eval₂_X (n) : (X n).eval₂ f g = g n
proof: simp [eval₂_monomial, f.map_one, X, prod_single_index, pow_one]

name: eval₂_mul_monomial
statement: theorem eval₂_mul_monomial  : ∀ {s a}, (p * monomial s a).eval₂ f g = p.eval₂ f g * f a * s.prod fun n e => g n ^ e
proof:
classical
  apply MvPolynomial.induction_on p
  · intro a' s a
    simp [C_mul_monomial, eval₂_monomial, f.map_mul]
  · intro p q ih_p ih_q
    simp [add_mul, eval₂_add, ih_p, ih_q]
  · intro p n ih s a
    exact
      calc (p * X n * monomial s a).eval₂ f g
        _ = (p * monomial (Finsupp.single n 1 + s) a).eval₂ f g := by
          rw [monomial_single_add, pow_one, mul_assoc]
        _ = (p * monomial (Finsupp.single n 1) 1).eval₂ f g * f a * s.prod fun n e => g n ^ e := by
          simp [ih, prod_single_index, prod_add_index, pow_one, pow_add, mul_assoc, mul_left_comm,
            f.map_one]

name: eval₂_mul
statement: theorem eval₂_mul  : ∀ {p}, (p * q).eval₂ f g = p.eval₂ f g * q.eval₂ f g
proof:
apply MvPolynomial.induction_on q
  · simp [eval₂_C, eval₂_mul_C]
  · simp +contextual [mul_add, eval₂_add]
  · simp +contextual [X, eval₂_mul_monomial, ← mul_assoc]

name: eval₂_mul_eq_zero_of_left
statement: theorem eval₂_mul_eq_zero_of_left (hp : p.eval₂ f g = 0) : (p * q).eval₂ f g = 0
proof: simp [eval₂_mul f g, hp]

name: eval₂_mul_eq_zero_of_right
statement: theorem eval₂_mul_eq_zero_of_right (hq : q.eval₂ f g = 0) : (p * q).eval₂ f g = 0
proof:
simp [eval₂_mul f g, hq]

@[simp]

name: eval₂_eta
statement: theorem eval₂_eta (p : MvPolynomial σ R) : eval₂ C X p = p
proof:
apply MvPolynomial.induction_on p <;>
    simp +contextual [eval₂_add, eval₂_mul]

name: coeff_map
statement: theorem coeff_map (p : MvPolynomial σ R) : ∀ m : σ →₀ ℕ, coeff m (map f p) = f (coeff m p)
proof:
classical
  apply MvPolynomial.induction_on p <;> clear p
  · intro r m
    simp_rw [map_C, coeff_C, apply_ite f, f.map_zero]
  · intro p q hp hq m
    simp only [hp, hq, (map f).map_add, coeff_add, f.map_add]
  · intro p i hp m
    simp only [(map f).map_mul, map_X, hp, coeff_mul_X', f.map_zero, apply_ite f]

name: map_injective
statement: theorem map_injective (hf : Function.Injective f) : Function.Injective (map f : MvPolynomial σ R → MvPolynomial σ S₁)
proof:
intro p q h
  simp only [MvPolynomial.ext_iff, coeff_map] at h ⊢
  intro m
  exact hf (h m)

name: support_map_subset
statement: theorem support_map_subset (p : MvPolynomial σ R) : (map f p).support ⊆ p.support
proof:
simp only [Finset.subset_iff, mem_support_iff]
  intro x hx
  contrapose hx
  rw [coeff_map, hx, map_zero]

name: aeval_unique
statement: theorem aeval_unique (φ : MvPolynomial σ R →ₐ[R] S₁) : φ = aeval (φ ∘ X)
proof:
ext i
  simp

name: aeval_range
statement: theorem aeval_range  : (aeval f).range = Algebra.adjoin R (Set.range f)
proof:
apply le_antisymm
  · rintro x ⟨p, rfl⟩
    simp only [AlgHom.toRingHom_eq_coe, RingHom.coe_coe]
    induction p using induction_on with
    | C a => exact aeval_C f a ▸ Subsemiring.subset_closure (Or.inl (Set.mem_range_self a))
    | add p q hp hq => rw [map_add]; exact Subalgebra.add_mem _ hp hq
    | mul_X p n h =>
      simp only [map_mul, aeval_X]
      exact Subalgebra.mul_mem _ h (Algebra.subset_adjoin (Set.mem_range_self n))
  · rw [Algebra.adjoin_le_iff]
    rintro x ⟨i, rfl⟩
    use X i, by aesop

@[simp]

name: eval₂Hom_zero
statement: theorem eval₂Hom_zero (f : R →+* S₂) : eval₂Hom f (0 : σ → S₂) = f.comp constantCoeff
proof:
ext <;> simp

@[simp]

name: aevalTower_id
statement: theorem aevalTower_id  : aevalTower (AlgHom.id S S) = (aeval : (σ → S) → MvPolynomial σ S →ₐ[S] S)
proof:
ext
  simp only [aevalTower_X, aeval_X]

@[simp]

name: aevalTower_ofId
statement: theorem aevalTower_ofId  : aevalTower (Algebra.ofId S A) = (aeval : (σ → A) → MvPolynomial σ S →ₐ[S] A)
proof:
ext
  simp only [aeval_X, aevalTower_X]

end AevalTower

section EvalMem

variable {S subS : Type*} [CommSemiring S] [SetLike subS S] [SubsemiringClass subS S]

name: supported_eq_range_rename
statement: theorem supported_eq_range_rename (s : Set σ) : supported R s = (rename ((↑) : s → σ)).range
proof:
rw [supported, Set.image_eq_range, adjoin_range_eq_range_aeval, rename]
  congr

/-- The isomorphism between the subalgebra of polynomials supported by `s` and
`MvPolynomial s R`. -/
noncomputable def supportedEquivMvPolynomial (s : Set σ) : supported R s ≃ₐ[R] MvPolynomial s R :=
  (Subalgebra.equivOfEq _ _ (supported_eq_range_rename s)).trans
    (AlgEquiv.ofInjective (rename ((↑) : s → σ)) (rename_injective _ Subtype.val_injective)).symm

@[simp]

name: mem_supported
statement: theorem mem_supported  : p ∈ supported R s ↔ ↑p.vars ⊆ s
proof:
classical
  rw [supported_eq_range_rename, AlgHom.mem_range]
  constructor
  · rintro ⟨p, rfl⟩
    refine _root_.trans (Finset.coe_subset.2 (vars_rename _ _)) ?_
    simp
  · intro hs
    exact exists_rename_eq_of_vars_subset_range p ((↑) : s → σ) Subtype.val_injective (by simpa)

name: mem_supported_vars
statement: theorem mem_supported_vars (p : MvPolynomial σ R) : p ∈ supported R (↑p.vars : Set σ)
proof:
rw [mem_supported]

variable (s)

name: supported_univ
statement: theorem supported_univ  : supported R (Set.univ : Set σ) = ⊤
proof:
simp [Algebra.eq_top_iff, mem_supported]

@[simp]

name: supported_le_supported_iff
statement: theorem supported_le_supported_iff  : supported R s ≤ supported R t ↔ s ⊆ t
proof:
constructor
  · intro h i
    simpa using @h (X i)
  · exact supported_mono

name: C_mul_monomial
statement: theorem C_mul_monomial  : C a * monomial s a' = monomial s (a * a')
proof:
have := single_mul_single 0 s a a'
  rw [zero_add] at this
  exact this

@[simp]

name: C_eq_coe_nat
statement: theorem C_eq_coe_nat (n : ℕ) : (C ↑n : MvPolynomial σ R) = n
proof: induction n <;> simp [*]

name: C_eq_smul_one
statement: theorem C_eq_smul_one  : (C a : MvPolynomial σ R) = a • (1 : MvPolynomial σ R)
proof: rw [← C_mul', mul_one]

name: X_pow_eq_monomial
statement: theorem X_pow_eq_monomial  : X n ^ e = monomial (Finsupp.single n e) (1 : R)
proof: simp [X, monomial_pow]

name: monomial_add_single
statement: theorem monomial_add_single  : monomial (s + Finsupp.single n e) a = monomial s a * X n ^ e
proof: rw [X_pow_eq_monomial, monomial_mul, mul_one]

name: monomial_single_add
statement: theorem monomial_single_add  : monomial (Finsupp.single n e + s) a = X n ^ e * monomial s a
proof: rw [X_pow_eq_monomial, monomial_mul, one_mul]

name: monomial_eq
statement: theorem monomial_eq  : monomial s a = C a * (s.prod fun n e => X n ^ e : MvPolynomial σ R)
proof:
simp only [X_pow_eq_monomial, ← monomial_finsupp_sum_index, Finsupp.sum_single]

@[simp]

name: prod_X_pow_eq_monomial
statement: theorem prod_X_pow_eq_monomial  : ∏ x ∈ s.support, X x ^ s x = monomial s (1 : R)
proof: simp only [monomial_eq, map_one, one_mul, Finsupp.prod]

name: adjoin_range_X
statement: theorem adjoin_range_X  : Algebra.adjoin R (range (X : σ → MvPolynomial σ R)) = ⊤
proof:
set S := Algebra.adjoin R (range (X : σ → MvPolynomial σ R))
  refine top_unique fun p hp => ?_; clear hp
  induction p using MvPolynomial.induction_on with
  | C => exact S.algebraMap_mem _
  | add p q hp hq => exact S.add_mem hp hq
  | mul_X p i hp => exact S.mul_mem hp (Algebra.subset_adjoin <| mem_range_self _)

@[ext]

name: support_monomial
statement: theorem support_monomial  : (monomial s a).support = if a = 0 then ∅ else {s}
proof:
rw [← Subsingleton.elim (Classical.decEq R a 0) h]
  rfl

name: support_X
statement: theorem support_X  : (X n : MvPolynomial σ R).support = {Finsupp.single n 1}
proof: classical rw [X, support_monomial, if_neg]; exact one_ne_zero

name: support_one
statement: theorem support_one  : (1 : MvPolynomial σ R).support = {0}
proof:
classical
  simp [show support (1 : MvPolynomial σ R) = if (1 : R) = 0 then ∅ else {0} from rfl]

name: coeff_X
statement: theorem coeff_X (i : σ) : coeff (Finsupp.single i 1) (X i : MvPolynomial σ R) = 1
proof:
classical rw [coeff_X', if_pos rfl]

@[simp]

name: isRegular_X
statement: theorem isRegular_X  : IsRegular (X n : MvPolynomial σ R)
proof:
suffices IsLeftRegular (X n : MvPolynomial σ R) from
    ⟨this, this.right_of_commute <| Commute.all _⟩
  intro P Q (hPQ : (X n) * P = (X n) * Q)
  ext i
  rw [← coeff_X_mul i n P, hPQ, coeff_X_mul i n Q]

@[simp] lemma isRegular_X_pow (k : ℕ) : IsRegular (X n ^ k : MvPolynomial σ R) := isRegular_X.pow k

@[simp] lemma isRegular_prod_X (s : Finset σ) :
    IsRegular (∏ n ∈ s, X n : MvPolynomial σ R) :=
  IsRegular.prod fun _ _ ↦ isRegular_X

/-- The finset of nonzero coefficients of a multivariate polynomial. -/

name: coeffs_one
statement: theorem coeffs_one  : coeffs (1 : MvPolynomial σ R) ⊆ {1}
proof:
classical
    rw [coeffs, Finset.image_subset_iff]
    simp_all [coeff_one]

@[nontriviality]

name: coeffs_one_of_nontrivial
statement: theorem coeffs_one_of_nontrivial  : coeffs (1 : MvPolynomial σ R) = {1}
proof:
apply Finset.Subset.antisymm coeffs_one
  simp only [coeffs, Finset.singleton_subset_iff, Finset.mem_image]
  exact ⟨0, by simp⟩

name: zero_notMem_coeffs
statement: theorem zero_notMem_coeffs (p : MvPolynomial σ R) : 0 ∉ p.coeffs
proof:
intro hz
  obtain ⟨n, hnsupp, hn⟩ := mem_coeffs_iff.mp hz
  exact (mem_support_iff.mp hnsupp) hn.symm

name: constantCoeff_C
statement: theorem constantCoeff_C (r : R) : constantCoeff (C r : MvPolynomial σ R) = r
proof:
classical simp [constantCoeff_eq]

variable (R) in
@[simp]

name: constantCoeff_X
statement: theorem constantCoeff_X (i : σ) : constantCoeff (X i : MvPolynomial σ R) = 0
proof:
simp [constantCoeff_eq]

@[simp]

name: constantCoeff_comp_C
statement: theorem constantCoeff_comp_C  : constantCoeff.comp (C : R →+* MvPolynomial σ R) = RingHom.id R
proof:
ext x
  exact constantCoeff_C σ x

name: monomial_mem_coeffsIn
statement: theorem monomial_mem_coeffsIn  : monomial i x ∈ coeffsIn σ M ↔ x ∈ M
proof:
classical
  simp only [mem_coeffsIn, coeff_monomial]
  exact ⟨fun h ↦ by simpa using h i, fun hs j ↦ by split <;> simp [hs]⟩

@[simp]

name: mul_monomial_mem_coeffsIn
statement: theorem mul_monomial_mem_coeffsIn  : p * monomial i 1 ∈ coeffsIn σ M ↔ p ∈ coeffsIn σ M
proof:
classical
  simp only [mem_coeffsIn, coeff_mul_monomial']
  constructor
  · rintro hp j
    simpa using hp (j + i)
  · rintro hp i
    split <;> simp [hp]

@[simp]

name: monomial_mul_mem_coeffsIn
statement: theorem monomial_mul_mem_coeffsIn  : monomial i 1 * p ∈ coeffsIn σ M ↔ p ∈ coeffsIn σ M
proof:
simp [mul_comm]

@[simp]

name: mul_X_mem_coeffsIn
statement: theorem mul_X_mem_coeffsIn  : p * X s ∈ coeffsIn σ M ↔ p ∈ coeffsIn σ M
proof:
simpa [-mul_monomial_mem_coeffsIn] using mul_monomial_mem_coeffsIn (i := .single s 1)

@[simp]

name: coeffsIn_eq_span_monomial
statement: theorem coeffsIn_eq_span_monomial  : coeffsIn σ M = .span R {monomial i m | (m ∈ M) (i : σ →₀ ℕ)}
proof:
classical
  refine le_antisymm ?_ <| Submodule.span_le.2 ?_
  · rintro p hp
    rw [p.as_sum]
    exact sum_mem fun i hi ↦ Submodule.subset_span ⟨_, hp i, _, rfl⟩
  · rintro _ ⟨m, hm, s, n, rfl⟩ i
    simp
    split <;> simp [hm]

name: mem_coeffsIn_iff_coeffs_subset
statement: theorem mem_coeffsIn_iff_coeffs_subset  : p ∈ coeffsIn σ M ↔ (p.coeffs : Set S) ⊆ M
proof:
simp only [mem_coeffsIn, coeffs, Finset.coe_image, image_subset_iff]
  refine ⟨fun h x _ ↦ h x, fun h i ↦ ?_⟩
  by_cases hp : i ∈ p.support
  · exact h hp
  · convert M.zero_mem
    simpa using hp

end Module

section Algebra
variable [Algebra R S] {M : Submodule R S}

name: coeffsIn_mul
statement: theorem coeffsIn_mul (M N : Submodule R S) : coeffsIn σ (M * N) = coeffsIn σ M * coeffsIn σ N
proof:
classical
  refine le_antisymm (coeffsIn_le.2 ?_) ?_
  · intro r hr s
    induction hr using Submodule.mul_induction_on' with
    | mem_mul_mem m hm n hn =>
      rw [← add_zero s, ← monomial_mul]
      apply Submodule.mul_mem_mul <;> simpa
    | add x _ y _ hx hy =>
      simpa [map_add] using add_mem hx hy
  · rw [Submodule.mul_le]
    intro x hx y hy k
    rw [MvPolynomial.coeff_mul]
    exact sum_mem fun c hc ↦ Submodule.mul_mem_mul (hx _) (hy _)

name: degrees_neg
statement: theorem degrees_neg (p : MvPolynomial σ R) : (-p).degrees = p.degrees
proof: rw [degrees, support_neg]; rfl

name: vars_sub_subset
statement: theorem vars_sub_subset  : (p - q).vars ⊆ p.vars ∪ q.vars
proof:
convert vars_add_subset p (-q) using 2 <;> simp [sub_eq_add_neg]

@[simp]

name: totalDegree_neg
statement: theorem totalDegree_neg (a : MvPolynomial σ R) : (-a).totalDegree = a.totalDegree
proof: simp only [totalDegree, support_neg]

name: isNilpotent_iff_of_fintype
statement: theorem isNilpotent_iff_of_fintype  : IsNilpotent P ↔ ∀ i, IsNilpotent (P.coeff i)
proof:
classical
  -- Note: including `Fintype.ofFinite σ` in the entire context interferes with the `rw` below.
  refine have := Fintype.ofFinite σ; Fintype.induction_empty_option ?_ ?_ ?_ σ P
  · intro α β _ e h₁ P
    rw [← IsNilpotent.map_iff (rename_injective _ e.symm.injective), h₁,
      (Finsupp.equivCongrLeft e).forall_congr_left]
    simp [Finsupp.equivMapDomain_eq_mapDomain, coeff_rename_mapDomain _ e.symm.injective]
  · simp [Unique.forall_iff, ← IsNilpotent.map_iff (isEmptyRingEquiv R PEmpty).injective,
      -isEmptyRingEquiv_apply, isEmptyRingEquiv_eq_coeff_zero]
  · intro α _ H P
    obtain ⟨P, rfl⟩ := (optionEquivLeft _ _).symm.surjective P
    simp [IsNilpotent.map_iff (optionEquivLeft _ _).symm.injective,
      Polynomial.isNilpotent_iff, H, Finsupp.optionEquiv.forall_congr_left,
      ← optionEquivLeft_coeff_some_coeff_none, Finsupp.coe_update]

name: isNilpotent_iff
statement: theorem isNilpotent_iff  : IsNilpotent P ↔ ∀ i, IsNilpotent (P.coeff i)
proof:
obtain ⟨n, f, hf, P, rfl⟩ := P.exists_fin_rename
  rw [IsNilpotent.map_iff (rename_injective _ hf), MvPolynomial.isNilpotent_iff_of_fintype]
  lift f to Fin n ↪ σ using hf
  refine ⟨fun H i ↦ ?_, fun H i ↦ by simpa using H (i.embDomain f)⟩
  by_cases H : i ∈ Set.range (Finsupp.embDomain f)
  · aesop
  · rw [coeff_rename_eq_zero] <;> aesop (add simp Finsupp.embDomain_eq_mapDomain)

instance [IsReduced R] : IsReduced (MvPolynomial σ R) := by
  simp [isReduced_iff, isNilpotent_iff, MvPolynomial.ext_iff]

name: isUnit_iff
statement: theorem isUnit_iff  : IsUnit P ↔ IsUnit (P.coeff 0) ∧ ∀ i ≠ 0, IsNilpotent (P.coeff i)
proof:
classical
  refine ⟨fun H ↦ ⟨H.map constantCoeff, ?_⟩, fun ⟨h₁, h₂⟩ ↦ ?_⟩
  · intro n hn
    obtain ⟨i, hi⟩ : ∃ i, n i ≠ 0 := by simpa [Finsupp.ext_iff] using hn
    let e := (optionEquivLeft _ _).symm.trans (renameEquiv R (Equiv.optionSubtypeNe i))
    have H := (Polynomial.coeff_isUnit_isNilpotent_of_isUnit (H.map e.symm)).2 (n i) hi
    simp only [ne_eq, isNilpotent_iff] at H
    convert ← H (n.equivMapDomain (Equiv.optionSubtypeNe i).symm).some
    refine (optionEquivLeft_coeff_some_coeff_none _ _ _ _).trans ?_
    simp [Finsupp.equivMapDomain_eq_mapDomain,
      coeff_rename_mapDomain _ (Equiv.optionSubtypeNe i).symm.injective]
  · have : IsNilpotent (P - C (P.coeff 0)) := by
      simp +contextual [isNilpotent_iff, apply_ite, eq_comm, h₂]
    simpa using this.isUnit_add_right_of_commute (h₁.map C) (.all _ _)

instance : IsLocalHom (C : _ →+* MvPolynomial σ R) where
  map_nonunit := by classical simp +contextual [isUnit_iff, coeff_C]

instance : IsLocalHom (algebraMap R (MvPolynomial σ R)) :=
  inferInstanceAs (IsLocalHom C)

name: isUnit_iff_totalDegree_of_isReduced
statement: theorem isUnit_iff_totalDegree_of_isReduced  : IsUnit P ↔ IsUnit (P.coeff 0) ∧ P.totalDegree = 0
proof:
convert isUnit_iff (P := P)
  rw [totalDegree_eq_zero_iff]
  simp [not_imp_comm (a := _ = (0 : R)), Finsupp.ext_iff]

name: isUnit_iff_eq_C_of_isReduced
statement: theorem isUnit_iff_eq_C_of_isReduced  : IsUnit P ↔ ∃ r, IsUnit r ∧ P = C r
proof:
rw [isUnit_iff_totalDegree_of_isReduced, totalDegree_eq_zero_iff_eq_C]
  refine ⟨fun H ↦ ⟨_, H⟩, ?_⟩
  rintro ⟨r, hr, rfl⟩
  simpa

end MvPolynomial

name: rename_id_apply
statement: theorem rename_id_apply (p : MvPolynomial σ R) : rename id p = p
proof: simp

name: eval₂_rename
statement: theorem eval₂_rename  : (rename k p).eval₂ f g = p.eval₂ f (g ∘ k)
proof:
apply MvPolynomial.induction_on p <;>
    · intros
      simp [*]

name: rename_eval₂
statement: theorem rename_eval₂ (g : τ → MvPolynomial σ R) : rename k (p.eval₂ C (g ∘ k)) = (rename k p).eval₂ C (rename k ∘ g)
proof:
apply MvPolynomial.induction_on p <;>
    · intros
      simp [*]

name: exists_finset_rename
statement: theorem exists_finset_rename (p : MvPolynomial σ R) : ∃ (s : Finset σ) (q : MvPolynomial { x // x ∈ s } R), p = rename (↑) q
proof:
classical
  apply induction_on p
  · intro r
    exact ⟨∅, C r, by rw [rename_C]⟩
  · rintro p q ⟨s, p, rfl⟩ ⟨t, q, rfl⟩
    refine ⟨s ∪ t, ⟨?_, ?_⟩⟩
    · refine rename (Subtype.map id ?_) p + rename (Subtype.map id ?_) q <;>
        simp +contextual only [id, true_or, or_true,
          Finset.mem_union, forall_true_iff]
    · simp only [rename_rename, map_add]
      rfl
  · rintro p n ⟨s, p, rfl⟩
    refine ⟨insert n s, ⟨?_, ?_⟩⟩
    · refine rename (Subtype.map id ?_) p * X ⟨n, s.mem_insert_self n⟩
      simp +contextual only [id, or_true, Finset.mem_insert, forall_true_iff]
    · simp only [rename_rename, rename_X, map_mul]
      rfl

/-- `exists_finset_rename` for two polynomials at once: for any two polynomials `p₁`, `p₂` in a
  polynomial semiring `R[σ]` of possibly infinitely many variables, `exists_finset_rename₂` yields
  a finite subset `s` of `σ` such that both `p₁` and `p₂` are contained in the polynomial semiring
  `R[s]` of finitely many variables. -/

name: exists_finset_rename₂
statement: theorem exists_finset_rename₂ (p₁ p₂ : MvPolynomial σ R) : ∃ (s : Finset σ) (q₁ q₂ : MvPolynomial s R), p₁ = rename (↑) q₁ ∧ p₂ = rename (↑) q₂
proof:
obtain ⟨s₁, q₁, rfl⟩ := exists_finset_rename p₁
  obtain ⟨s₂, q₂, rfl⟩ := exists_finset_rename p₂
  classical
    use s₁ ∪ s₂
    use rename (fun x ↦ ⟨x, Finset.subset_union_left x.2⟩) q₁
    use rename (fun x ↦ ⟨x, Finset.subset_union_right x.2⟩) q₂
    constructor <;> simp [Function.comp_def]

/-- Every polynomial is a polynomial in finitely many variables. -/

name: exists_fin_rename
statement: theorem exists_fin_rename (p : MvPolynomial σ R) : ∃ (n : ℕ) (f : Fin n → σ) (_hf : Injective f) (q : MvPolynomial (Fin n) R), p = rename f q
proof:
obtain ⟨s, q, rfl⟩ := exists_finset_rename p
  let n := Fintype.card { x // x ∈ s }
  let e := Fintype.equivFin { x // x ∈ s }
  refine ⟨n, (↑) ∘ e.symm, Subtype.val_injective.comp e.symm.injective, rename e q, ?_⟩
  rw [← rename_rename, rename_rename e]
  simp only [Function.comp_def, Equiv.symm_apply_apply, rename_rename]

end Rename

name: expand_zero_apply
statement: theorem expand_zero_apply (f : MvPolynomial σ R) : expand 0 f = .C (MvPolynomial.eval 1 f)
proof:
simp

@[simp]

name: expand_one
statement: theorem expand_one  : expand 1 = AlgHom.id R (MvPolynomial σ R)
proof:
ext1 i
  simp

name: support_expand_subset
statement: theorem support_expand_subset  : (expand p φ).support ⊆ φ.support.image (p • ·)
proof:
conv_lhs => rw [φ.as_sum]
  simp only [map_sum, expand_monomial]
  refine MvPolynomial.support_sum.trans ?_
  aesop (add simp Finset.subset_iff)

name: totalDegree_expand
statement: theorem totalDegree_expand (f : MvPolynomial σ R) : (expand p f).totalDegree = f.totalDegree * p
proof:
classical
  rcases p.eq_zero_or_pos with hp | hp
  · simp [hp]
  by_cases hf : f = 0
  · rw [hf, map_zero, totalDegree_zero, zero_mul]
  simp_rw [totalDegree_eq, support_expand _ (p.ne_zero_iff_zero_lt.mpr hp)]
  simp only [Finsupp.card_toMultiset, Finset.sup_image, Finset.sup_mul₀, Function.comp_def]
  congr! 2 with d
  rw [Finsupp.sum_of_support_subset _ Finsupp.support_smul _ (by simp)]
  simp [Finsupp.sum, Finset.sum_mul, mul_comm p]

end

end CommSemiring

section CommRing

variable (R σ : Type*) [CommRing R]

name: irreducible_sq_not_dvd_iff_eq_zero_and_no_irreducibles_or_squarefree
statement: theorem irreducible_sq_not_dvd_iff_eq_zero_and_no_irreducibles_or_squarefree (r : R) : (∀ x : R, Irreducible x → ¬x * x ∣ r) ↔ (r = 0 ∧ ∀ x : R, ¬Irreducible x) ∨ Squarefree r
proof:
refine ⟨fun h ↦ ?_, ?_⟩
  · rcases eq_or_ne r 0 with (rfl | hr)
    · exact .inl (by simpa using h)
    · exact .inr ((squarefree_iff_no_irreducibles hr).mpr h)
  · rintro (⟨rfl, h⟩ | h)
    · simpa using h
    intro x hx t
    exact hx.not_isUnit (h x t)

name: subtypeCongrHom_injective
statement: theorem subtypeCongrHom_injective (p : α → Prop) : Function.Injective (subtypeCongrHom p)
proof:
rintro ⟨⟩ ⟨⟩ h
  rw [Prod.mk_inj]
  constructor <;> ext i <;> simpa using Equiv.congr_fun h i

/-- If `e` is also a permutation, we can write `permCongr`
completely in terms of the group structure. -/
@[simp]

name: ofSubtype_injective
statement: theorem ofSubtype_injective  : Function.Injective (ofSubtype : Perm (Subtype p) → Perm α)
proof:
intro x y h
  rw [Perm.ext_iff] at h ⊢
  intro a
  specialize h a
  rwa [ofSubtype_apply_coe, ofSubtype_apply_coe, SetCoe.ext_iff] at h

@[simp]

name: pow_mulLeft
statement: theorem pow_mulLeft (n : ℕ) : Equiv.mulLeft a ^ n = Equiv.mulLeft (a ^ n)
proof:
ext; simp [Perm.coe_pow]

@[to_additive (attr := simp)]

name: pow_mulRight
statement: theorem pow_mulRight (n : ℕ) : Equiv.mulRight a ^ n = Equiv.mulRight (a ^ n)
proof:
ext; simp [Perm.coe_pow]

@[to_additive (attr := simp)]

name: isCancelMul_iff_forall_isRegular
statement: theorem isCancelMul_iff_forall_isRegular  : IsCancelMul R ↔ ∀ r : R, IsRegular r
proof:
rw [isCancelMul_iff, isLeftCancelMul_iff, isRightCancelMul_iff, ← forall_and]
  exact forall_congr' fun _ ↦ isRegular_iff.symm

/-- If all multiplications cancel on the left then every element is left-regular. -/
@[to_additive /-- If all additions cancel on the left then every element is add-left-regular. -/]

name: isLeftRegular_iff_isRegular
statement: theorem isLeftRegular_iff_isRegular  : IsLeftRegular a ↔ IsRegular a
proof:
simp [isRegular_iff, IsLeftRegular, IsRightRegular, mul_comm]

@[to_additive (attr := simp)]

name: isRightRegular_iff_isRegular
statement: theorem isRightRegular_iff_isRegular  : IsRightRegular a ↔ IsRegular a
proof:
simp [isRegular_iff, IsLeftRegular, IsRightRegular, mul_comm]

/-- Any `CommMagma G` that satisfies `IsRightCancelMul G` also satisfies `IsLeftCancelMul G`. -/
@[to_additive AddCommMagma.IsRightCancelAdd.toIsLeftCancelAdd /-- Any `AddCommMagma G` that
satisfies `IsRightCancelAdd G` also satisfies `IsLeftCancelAdd G`. -/]

name: npowRec_add
statement: theorem npowRec_add  : npowRec (m + n) a = npowRec m a * npowRec n a
proof:
obtain _ | n := n; · exact (hn rfl).elim
  induction n with
  | zero => simp only [npowRec, ha]
  | succ n ih => rw [← Nat.add_assoc, npowRec, ih n.succ_ne_zero]; simp only [npowRec, mul_assoc]

@[to_additive] theorem npowRec_succ : npowRec (n + 1) a = a * npowRec n a := by
  rw [Nat.add_comm, npowRec_add 1 n hn a ha, npowRec, npowRec, ha]

end

library_note «forgetful inheritance» /--
Suppose that one can put two mathematical structures on a type, a rich one `R` and a poor one
`P`, and that one can deduce the poor structure from the rich structure through a map `F` (called a
forgetful functor) (think `R = MetricSpace` and `P = TopologicalSpace`). A possible
implementation would be to have a type class `rich` containing a field `R`, a type class `poor`
containing a field `P`, and an instance from `rich` to `poor`. However, this creates diamond
problems, and a better approach is to let `rich` extend `poor` and have a field saying that
`F R = P`.

To illustrate this, consider the pair `MetricSpace` / `TopologicalSpace`. Consider the topology
on a product of two metric spaces. With the first approach, it could be obtained by going first from
each metric space to its topology, and then taking the product topology. But it could also be
obtained by considering the product metric space (with its sup distance) and then the topology
coming from this distance. These would be the same topology, but not definitionally, which means
that from the point of view of Lean's kernel, there would be two different `TopologicalSpace`
instances on the product. This is not compatible with the way instances are designed and used:
there should be at most one instance of a kind on each type. This approach has created an instance
diamond that does not commute definitionally.

The second approach solves this issue. Now, a metric space contains both a distance, a topology, and
a proof that the topology coincides with the one coming from the distance. When one defines the
product of two metric spaces, one uses the sup distance and the product topology, and one has to
give the proof that the sup distance induces the product topology. Following both sides of the
instance diamond then gives rise (definitionally) to the product topology on the product space.

Another approach would be to have the rich type class take the poor type class as an instance
parameter. It would solve the diamond problem, but it would lead to a blow up of the number
of type classes one would need to declare to work with complicated classes, say a real inner
product space, and would create exponential complexity when working with products of
such complicated spaces, that are avoided by bundling things carefully as above.

Note that this description of this specific case of the product of metric spaces is oversimplified
compared to mathlib, as there is an intermediate typeclass between `MetricSpace` and
`TopologicalSpace` called `UniformSpace`. The above scheme is used at both levels, embedding a
topology in the uniform space structure, and a uniform structure in the metric space structure.

Note also that, when `P` is a proposition, there is no such issue as any two proofs of `P` are
definitionally equivalent in Lean.

To avoid boilerplate, there are some designs that can automatically fill the poor fields when
creating a rich structure if one doesn't want to do something special about them. For instance,
in the definition of metric spaces, default tactics fill the uniform space fields if they are
not given explicitly. One can also have a helper function creating the rich structure from a

name: npowRec_eq_npowBinRec
statement: theorem npowRec_eq_npowBinRec  : @npowRecAuto = @npowBinRecAuto
proof:
funext M _ _ k m
  rw [npowBinRecAuto, npowRecAuto, npowBinRec]
  match k with
  | 0 => rw [npowRec, npowBinRec.go, Nat.binaryRec_zero]
  | k + 1 => rw [npowBinRec.go_spec, npowRec_eq]

@[to_additive] theorem npowBinRec_zero {M : Type*} [Mul M] [One M] (m : M) :
    npowBinRec 0 m = 1 := rfl

@[to_additive] theorem npowBinRec_succ {M : Type*} [Semigroup M] [One M] (n : ℕ) (m : M) :
    npowBinRec (n + 1) m = npowBinRec n m * m := by
  iterate 2 rw [← npowBinRecAuto, ← npowRec_eq_npowBinRec]
  rfl

/-- An `AddMonoid` is an `AddSemigroup` with an element `0` such that `0 + a = a + 0 = a`. -/

name: mul_div_assoc
statement: theorem mul_div_assoc (a b c : G) : a * b / c = a * (b / c)
proof:
rw [div_eq_mul_inv, div_eq_mul_inv, mul_assoc _ _ _]

@[to_additive (attr := simp)]

name: inv_eq_of_mul_eq_one_left
statement: theorem inv_eq_of_mul_eq_one_left (h : a * b = 1) : b⁻¹ = a
proof:
rw [← inv_eq_of_mul_eq_one_right h, inv_inv]

@[to_additive]

name: mul_inv_cancel
statement: theorem mul_inv_cancel (a : G) : a * a⁻¹ = 1
proof:
rw [← inv_mul_cancel a⁻¹, inv_eq_of_mul (inv_mul_cancel a)]

@[to_additive (attr := simp) sub_self]

name: inv_mul_cancel_left
statement: theorem inv_mul_cancel_left (a b : G) : a⁻¹ * (a * b) = b
proof:
rw [← mul_assoc, inv_mul_cancel, one_mul]

@[to_additive (attr := simp)]

name: mul_inv_cancel_left
statement: theorem mul_inv_cancel_left (a b : G) : a * (a⁻¹ * b) = b
proof:
rw [← mul_assoc, mul_inv_cancel, one_mul]

@[to_additive (attr := simp)]

name: mul_inv_cancel_right
statement: theorem mul_inv_cancel_right (a b : G) : a * b * b⁻¹ = a
proof:
rw [mul_assoc, mul_inv_cancel, mul_one]

@[to_additive (attr := simp)]

name: mul_div_cancel_right
statement: theorem mul_div_cancel_right (a b : G) : a * b / b = a
proof:
rw [div_eq_mul_inv, mul_inv_cancel_right a b]

@[to_additive (attr := simp)]

name: inv_mul_cancel_right
statement: theorem inv_mul_cancel_right (a b : G) : a * b⁻¹ * b = a
proof:
rw [mul_assoc, inv_mul_cancel, mul_one]

@[to_additive (attr := simp)]

name: div_mul_cancel
statement: theorem div_mul_cancel (a b : G) : a / b * b = a
proof:
rw [div_eq_mul_inv, inv_mul_cancel_right a b]

@[to_additive]
instance (priority := 100) Group.toDivisionMonoid : DivisionMonoid G where
  inv_inv a := by exact inv_eq_of_mul (inv_mul_cancel a)
  mul_inv_rev a b := by
    apply inv_eq_of_mul
    rw [mul_assoc, mul_inv_cancel_left, mul_inv_cancel]
  inv_eq_of_mul _ _ := by exact inv_eq_of_mul

-- see Note [lower instance priority]
@[to_additive]
instance (priority := 100) Group.toCancelMonoid : CancelMonoid G where
  mul_right_cancel := fun a b c h ↦ by
    rw [← mul_inv_cancel_right b a, show b * a = c * a from h, mul_inv_cancel_right]
  mul_left_cancel := fun a {b c} h ↦ by
    rw [← inv_mul_cancel_left a b, show a * b = a * c from h, inv_mul_cancel_left]

end Group

/-- An additive commutative group is an additive group with commutative `(+)`. -/

name: inv_mul_cancel_comm
statement: theorem inv_mul_cancel_comm (a b : G) : a⁻¹ * b * a = b
proof:
rw [mul_comm, mul_inv_cancel_left]

@[to_additive (attr := simp)]

name: inv_mul_cancel_comm_assoc
statement: theorem inv_mul_cancel_comm_assoc (a b : G) : a⁻¹ * (b * a) = b
proof:
rw [mul_comm, mul_inv_cancel_right]

@[to_additive (attr := simp)] lemma mul_inv_cancel_comm_assoc (a b : G) : a * (b * a⁻¹) = b := by
  rw [mul_comm, inv_mul_cancel_right]

end CommGroup

namespace IsMulCommutative

/-- A magma which `IsMulCommutative` is a `CommMagma`.

This is primarily used to deduce the bundled version from the unbundled one for commutative
subobjects in a noncommutative ambient type. As such this is only available inside the
`IsMulCommutative` scope so as to avoid deleterious effects to type class synthesis for bundled
commutativity.

See note [commutative subobjects]. -/

/-- An additive magma which `IsMulCommutative` is a `AddCommMagma`.

This is primarily used to deduce the bundled version from the unbundled one for commutative

commutativity.

See note [commutative subobjects]. -/ ]
scoped instance (priority := 50) {M : Type*} [Mul M] [IsMulCommutative M] : CommMagma M where
  mul_comm := mul_comm'

argument order of these projections doesn't match the argument order of `^`.
The `nsmul`/`zsmul` lemmas will be correct. -/

name: coe_fwdDiffₗ_pow
statement: theorem coe_fwdDiffₗ_pow (n : ℕ) : ↑(fwdDiffₗ M G h ^ n) = (fwdDiff h)^[n]
proof:
ext; rw [Module.End.pow_apply, coe_fwdDiffₗ]

variable (M G) in
/-- Linear-endomorphism version of the shift-by-1 operator. -/

name: fwdDiff_choose
statement: theorem fwdDiff_choose (j : ℕ) : Δ_[1] (fun x ↦ x.choose (j + 1) : ℕ → ℤ) = fun x ↦ x.choose j
proof:
ext n
  simp only [fwdDiff, choose_succ_succ' n j, cast_add, add_sub_cancel_right]

name: fwdDiff_iter_choose
statement: theorem fwdDiff_iter_choose (j k : ℕ) : Δ_[1]^[k] (fun x ↦ x.choose (k + j) : ℕ → ℤ) = fun x ↦ x.choose j
proof:
induction k generalizing j with
  | zero => simp only [zero_add, iterate_zero, id_eq]
  | succ k IH =>
    simp only [iterate_succ_apply', add_assoc, add_comm 1 j, IH, fwdDiff_choose]

name: fwdDiff_iter_choose_zero
statement: theorem fwdDiff_iter_choose_zero (m n : ℕ) : Δ_[1]^[n] (fun x ↦ x.choose m : ℕ → ℤ) 0 = if n = m then 1 else 0
proof:
rcases lt_trichotomy m n with hmn | rfl | hnm
  · rcases Nat.exists_eq_add_of_lt hmn with ⟨k, rfl⟩
    simp_rw [hmn.ne', if_false, (by ring : m + k + 1 = k + 1 + m), iterate_add_apply,
      add_zero m ▸ fwdDiff_iter_choose 0 m, choose_zero_right, iterate_one, cast_one, fwdDiff_const,
      fwdDiff_iter_eq_sum_shift, smul_zero, sum_const_zero]
  · simp only [if_true, add_zero m ▸ fwdDiff_iter_choose 0 m, choose_zero_right, cast_one]
  · rcases Nat.exists_eq_add_of_lt hnm with ⟨k, rfl⟩
    simp_rw [hnm.ne, if_false, add_assoc n k 1, fwdDiff_iter_choose, choose_zero_succ, cast_zero]

end choose

name: pow_eq_one_iff_left
statement: theorem pow_eq_one_iff_left (hn : n ≠ 0) : a ^ n = 1 ↔ a = 1
proof:
rw [← pow_left_inj (a := a) hn, one_pow]

-- We want to use `IsAddTorsion.nsmul_eq_zero_iff` earlier than `smul_eq_zero`.
@[to_additive (attr := simp high)]

name: pow_eq_one_iff
statement: theorem pow_eq_one_iff  : a ^ n = 1 ↔ a = 1 ∨ n = 0
proof:
obtain rfl | hn := eq_or_ne n 0 <;> simp [pow_eq_one_iff_left, *]

@[to_additive nsmul_eq_zero_iff_left]

name: isSquare_inv
statement: theorem isSquare_inv  : IsSquare a⁻¹ ↔ IsSquare a
proof:
constructor <;> intro h <;> simpa using (isSquare_op_iff.mpr h).map (MulEquiv.inv' α).symm

@[to_additive] alias ⟨_, IsSquare.inv⟩ := isSquare_inv

@[to_additive (attr := aesop unsafe 80%) Even.zsmul_right]

name: sum_eq_ite
statement: theorem sum_eq_ite (ψ : AddChar A R) : ∑ a, ψ a = if ψ = 0 then ↑(card A) else 0
proof:
split_ifs with h
  · simp [h]
  obtain ⟨x, hx⟩ := ne_one_iff.1 h
  refine eq_zero_of_mul_eq_self_left hx ?_
  rw [Finset.mul_sum]
  exact Fintype.sum_equiv (Equiv.addLeft x) _ _ fun y ↦ (map_add_eq_mul ..).symm

variable [CharZero R]

name: sum_eq_zero_iff_ne_zero
statement: theorem sum_eq_zero_iff_ne_zero  : ∑ x, ψ x = 0 ↔ ψ ≠ 0
proof:
classical
  rw [sum_eq_ite, Ne.ite_eq_right_iff]; exact Nat.cast_ne_zero.2 Fintype.card_ne_zero

name: inv_mulShift
statement: theorem inv_mulShift (ψ : AddChar R M) : ψ⁻¹ = mulShift ψ (-1)
proof:
ext
  rw [inv_apply, mulShift_apply, neg_mul, one_mul]

/-- If `n` is a natural number, then `mulShift ψ n x = (ψ x) ^ n`. -/

name: mulShift_zero
statement: theorem mulShift_zero (ψ : AddChar R M) : mulShift ψ 0 = 1
proof:
ext; rw [mulShift_apply, zero_mul, map_zero_eq_one, one_apply]

@[simp]

name: mulShift_one
statement: theorem mulShift_one (ψ : AddChar R M) : mulShift ψ 1 = ψ
proof: ext; rw [mulShift_apply, one_mul]

name: comp_mul_left
statement: theorem comp_mul_left (x y : α) : (x * ·) ∘ (y * ·) = (x * y * ·)
proof:
ext z
  simp [mul_assoc]

/-- Composing two multiplications on the right by `y` and `x`
is equal to a multiplication on the right by `y * x`.
-/
@[to_additive (attr := simp) /-- Composing two additions on the right by `y` and `x`
is equal to an addition on the right by `y + x`. -/]

name: comp_mul_right
statement: theorem comp_mul_right (x y : α) : (· * x) ∘ (· * y) = (· * (y * x))
proof:
ext z
  simp [mul_assoc]

end Semigroup

section MulOneClass

instance Semigroup.to_isLawfulIdentity : Std.LawfulIdentity (α := M) (· * ·) 1 where

  right_id := mul_one

@[to_additive]

name: mul_left_comm
statement: theorem mul_left_comm (a b c : G) : a * (b * c) = b * (a * c)
proof:

@[to_additive]

name: mul_right_comm
statement: theorem mul_right_comm (a b c : G) : a * b * c = a * c * b
proof:

@[to_additive]

name: mul_mul_mul_comm
statement: theorem mul_mul_mul_comm (a b c d : G) : a * b * (c * d) = a * c * (b * d)
proof:

@[to_additive]

name: mul_rotate
statement: theorem mul_rotate (a b c : G) : a * b * c = b * c * a
proof:

@[to_additive]

name: mul_one_div
statement: theorem mul_one_div (x y : G) : x * (1 / y) = x / y
proof:
rw [div_eq_mul_inv, one_mul, div_eq_mul_inv]

@[to_additive]

name: eq_one_div_of_mul_eq_one_left
statement: theorem eq_one_div_of_mul_eq_one_left (h : b * a = 1) : b = 1 / a
proof:
rw [eq_inv_of_mul_eq_one_left h, one_div]

@[to_additive]

name: eq_one_div_of_mul_eq_one_right
statement: theorem eq_one_div_of_mul_eq_one_right (h : a * b = 1) : b = 1 / a

@[to_additive]

name: mul_zpow_neg_one
statement: theorem mul_zpow_neg_one (a b : α) : (a * b) ^ (-1 : ℤ) = b ^ (-1 : ℤ) * a ^ (-1 : ℤ)
proof:
simp only [zpow_neg, zpow_one, mul_inv_rev]

@[to_additive zsmul_neg]

name: eq_of_one_div_eq_one_div
statement: theorem eq_of_one_div_eq_one_div (h : 1 / a = 1 / b) : a = b
proof:
rw [← one_div_one_div a, h, one_div_one_div]

-- Note that `mul_zsmul` and `zpow_mul` have the primes swapped
-- when additivised since their argument order,
-- and therefore the more "natural" choice of lemma, is reversed.
@[to_additive mul_zsmul'] lemma zpow_mul (a : α) : ∀ m n : ℤ, a ^ (m * n) = (a ^ m) ^ n
  | (m : ℕ), (n : ℕ) => by
    rw [zpow_natCast, zpow_natCast, ← pow_mul, ← zpow_natCast]
    rfl
  | (m : ℕ), .negSucc n => by
    rw [zpow_natCast, zpow_negSucc, ← pow_mul, Int.ofNat_mul_negSucc, zpow_neg, inv_inj,
      ← zpow_natCast]
  | .negSucc m, (n : ℕ) => by
    rw [zpow_natCast, zpow_negSucc, ← inv_pow, ← pow_mul, Int.negSucc_mul_ofNat, zpow_neg, inv_pow,
      inv_inj, ← zpow_natCast]
  | .negSucc m, .negSucc n => by
    rw [zpow_negSucc, zpow_negSucc, Int.negSucc_mul_negSucc, inv_pow, inv_inv, ← pow_mul, ←
      zpow_natCast]
    rfl

@[to_additive mul_zsmul]

name: div_mul_eq_div_div_swap
statement: theorem div_mul_eq_div_div_swap  : a / (b * c) = a / c / b
proof:

@[to_additive eq_sub_of_add_eq']

name: mul_div_cancel
statement: theorem mul_div_cancel (a b : G) : a * (b / a) = b
proof:
rw [← mul_div_assoc, mul_div_cancel_left]

@[to_additive (attr := simp)]

name: mul_mul_div_cancel
statement: theorem mul_mul_div_cancel (a b c : G) : a * c * (b / c) = a * b
proof:

@[to_additive (attr := simp)]

name: div_mul_mul_cancel
statement: theorem div_mul_mul_cancel (a b c : G) : a / c * (b * c) = a * b
proof:
rw [mul_left_comm, div_mul_cancel, mul_comm]

@[to_additive (attr := simp)]

name: mul_div_div_cancel
statement: theorem mul_div_div_cancel (a b c : G) : a * b / (a / c) = b * c
proof:
rw [← div_mul, mul_div_cancel_left]

@[to_additive (attr := simp)]

name: div_div_div_cancel_left
statement: theorem div_div_div_cancel_left (a b c : G) : c / a / (c / b) = b / a
proof:
rw [← inv_div b c, div_inv_eq_mul, mul_comm, div_mul_div_cancel]

@[to_additive]

name: div_eq_div_iff_mul_eq_mul
statement: theorem div_eq_div_iff_mul_eq_mul  : a / b = c / d ↔ a * d = c * b
proof:
rw [div_eq_iff_eq_mul, div_mul_eq_mul_div, eq_comm, div_eq_iff_eq_mul']
  simp only [mul_comm, eq_comm]

@[to_additive]

name: mul_inv_eq_mul_inv_iff_mul_eq_mul
statement: theorem mul_inv_eq_mul_inv_iff_mul_eq_mul  : a * b⁻¹ = c * d⁻¹ ↔ a * d = c * b
proof:
rw [← div_eq_mul_inv, ← div_eq_mul_inv, div_eq_div_iff_mul_eq_mul]

@[to_additive]

name: inv_mul_eq_inv_mul_iff_mul_eq_mul
statement: theorem inv_mul_eq_inv_mul_iff_mul_eq_mul  : b⁻¹ * a = d⁻¹ * c ↔ a * d = c * b
proof:
rw [← div_eq_inv_mul, ← div_eq_inv_mul, div_eq_div_iff_mul_eq_mul]

@[to_additive]

name: div_eq_div_iff_div_eq_div
statement: theorem div_eq_div_iff_div_eq_div  : a / b = c / d ↔ a / c = b / d
proof:
rw [div_eq_iff_eq_mul, div_mul_eq_mul_div, div_eq_iff_eq_mul', mul_div_assoc]

@[to_additive (attr := simp)]

name: modEq_iff_nsmul
statement: theorem modEq_iff_nsmul  : a ≡ b [PMOD p] ↔ ∃ m n : ℕ, m • p + a = n • p + b
proof:
rfl

@[refl, simp]

name: add_iff_left
statement: theorem add_iff_left (h : a ≡ b [PMOD p]) : a + c ≡ b + d [PMOD p] ↔ c ≡ d [PMOD p]
proof:
refine ⟨fun hadd ↦ ?_, h.add⟩
  rw [modEq_iff_nsmul] at *
  rcases h with ⟨k, l, h⟩
  rcases hadd with ⟨m, n, hadd⟩
  use m + l, n + k
  apply add_right_cancel (b := a)
  rw [add_assoc, add_comm c, add_nsmul, add_right_comm, hadd, ← add_assoc, add_right_comm _ b,
    add_right_comm _ b, add_assoc, ← h, add_add_add_comm, add_nsmul, ← add_assoc]

@[simp]
protected theorem add_iff_right (h : c ≡ d [PMOD p]) :
    a + c ≡ b + d [PMOD p] ↔ a ≡ b [PMOD p] := by
  simpa only [add_comm c, add_comm d] using h.add_iff_left

protected alias ⟨add_left_cancel, _⟩ := ModEq.add_iff_left

protected alias ⟨add_right_cancel, _⟩ := ModEq.add_iff_right

protected theorem add_left_cancel' (c : M) : c + a ≡ c + b [PMOD p] → a ≡ b [PMOD p] :=
  modEq_rfl.add_left_cancel

protected theorem add_right_cancel' (c : M) : a + c ≡ b + c [PMOD p] → a ≡ b [PMOD p] :=
  modEq_rfl.add_right_cancel

end ModEq

@[simp]

name: add_modEq_left
statement: theorem add_modEq_left  : a + b ≡ a [PMOD p] ↔ b ≡ 0 [PMOD p]
proof:
simpa using (modEq_refl a).add_iff_left (d := 0)

@[simp]

name: modEq_iff_zsmul
statement: theorem modEq_iff_zsmul  : a ≡ b [PMOD p] ↔ ∃ m : ℤ, m • p = b - a
proof:
rw [modEq_iff_nsmul]
  constructor
  · rintro ⟨m, n, h⟩
    use m - n
    rw [sub_zsmul, ← sub_eq_add_neg, sub_eq_sub_iff_add_eq_add, add_comm b]
    exact mod_cast h
  · rintro ⟨m, h⟩
    use m.toNat, (-m).toNat
    rwa [add_comm _ b, ← sub_eq_sub_iff_add_eq_add, ← natCast_zsmul, ← natCast_zsmul,
      sub_eq_add_neg, ← sub_zsmul, m.toNat_sub_toNat_neg]

name: of_zsmul
statement: theorem of_zsmul (h : a ≡ b [PMOD z • p]) : a ≡ b [PMOD p]
proof:
rw [modEq_iff_zsmul] at *
  rcases h with ⟨m, h⟩
  simp [← h, ← mul_zsmul]

protected theorem zsmul (h : a ≡ b [PMOD p]) : z • a ≡ z • b [PMOD z • p] := by
  rw [modEq_iff_zsmul] at *
  rcases h with ⟨m, h⟩
  use m
  rw [← zsmul_sub, ← h, ← mul_zsmul, ← mul_zsmul']

end ModEq

@[simp]

name: sub_iff_left
statement: theorem sub_iff_left (h : a₁ ≡ b₁ [PMOD p]) : a₁ - a₂ ≡ b₁ - b₂ [PMOD p] ↔ a₂ ≡ b₂ [PMOD p]
proof:
simp [sub_eq_add_neg, h]

@[simp]
protected theorem sub_iff_right (h : a₂ ≡ b₂ [PMOD p]) :
    a₁ - a₂ ≡ b₁ - b₂ [PMOD p] ↔ a₁ ≡ b₁ [PMOD p] := by
  simp [h, sub_eq_add_neg]

protected alias ⟨sub_left_cancel, sub⟩ := ModEq.sub_iff_left

protected alias ⟨sub_right_cancel, _⟩ := ModEq.sub_iff_right

protected theorem sub_left (c : G) (h : a ≡ b [PMOD p]) : c - a ≡ c - b [PMOD p] :=
  modEq_rfl.sub h

protected theorem sub_right (c : G) (h : a ≡ b [PMOD p]) : a - c ≡ b - c [PMOD p] :=
  h.sub modEq_rfl

protected theorem sub_left_cancel' (c : G) : c - a ≡ c - b [PMOD p] → a ≡ b [PMOD p] :=
  modEq_rfl.sub_left_cancel

protected theorem sub_right_cancel' (c : G) : a - c ≡ b - c [PMOD p] → a ≡ b [PMOD p] :=
  modEq_rfl.sub_right_cancel

end ModEq

name: modEq_iff_eq_add_zsmul
statement: theorem modEq_iff_eq_add_zsmul  : a ≡ b [PMOD p] ↔ ∃ z : ℤ, b = a + z • p
proof:
simp_rw [modEq_iff_zsmul', sub_eq_iff_eq_add']

-- this roughly matches `Int.modEq_zero_iff_dvd`

name: modEq_zero_iff_eq_zsmul
statement: theorem modEq_zero_iff_eq_zsmul  : a ≡ 0 [PMOD p] ↔ ∃ z : ℤ, a = z • p
proof:
rw [modEq_comm, modEq_iff_eq_add_zsmul]
  simp_rw [zero_add]

name: not_modEq_iff_ne_add_zsmul
statement: theorem not_modEq_iff_ne_add_zsmul  : ¬a ≡ b [PMOD p] ↔ ∀ z : ℤ, b ≠ a + z • p
proof:
rw [modEq_iff_eq_add_zsmul, not_exists]

/-- If `a ≡ b [PMOD p]`, then mod `n • p` there are `n` cases. -/

name: isDedekindFiniteMonoid_iff
statement: theorem isDedekindFiniteMonoid_iff  : IsDedekindFiniteMonoid αᵐᵒᵖ ↔ IsDedekindFiniteMonoid α
proof:
simp_rw [isDedekindFiniteMonoid_iff, ← opEquiv.forall_congr_right]
  simpa [← op_one, ← op_mul] using forall_comm

@[to_additive] instance [MulOne α] [IsDedekindFiniteMonoid α] : IsDedekindFiniteMonoid αᵐᵒᵖ :=
  MulOpposite.isDedekindFiniteMonoid_iff.mpr ‹_›

end MulOpposite

/-!
### Multiplicative structures on `αᵃᵒᵖ`
-/


namespace AddOpposite

instance instSemigroup [Semigroup α] : Semigroup αᵃᵒᵖ := unop_injective.semigroup _ fun _ _ ↦ rfl

instance instLeftCancelSemigroup [LeftCancelSemigroup α] : LeftCancelSemigroup αᵃᵒᵖ :=
  unop_injective.leftCancelSemigroup _ fun _ _ => rfl

instance instRightCancelSemigroup [RightCancelSemigroup α] : RightCancelSemigroup αᵃᵒᵖ :=
  unop_injective.rightCancelSemigroup _ fun _ _ => rfl

instance instCommSemigroup [CommSemigroup α] : CommSemigroup αᵃᵒᵖ :=
  unop_injective.commSemigroup _ fun _ _ => rfl

instance instMulOneClass [MulOneClass α] : MulOneClass αᵃᵒᵖ :=
  unop_injective.mulOneClass _ (by exact rfl) fun _ _ => rfl

instance pow {β} [Pow α β] : Pow αᵃᵒᵖ β where pow a b := op (unop a ^ b)

@[simp]

name: centralizer_union
statement: theorem centralizer_union  : centralizer (S ∪ T) = centralizer S ∩ centralizer T
proof:
simp [centralizer, or_imp, forall_and, setOf_and]

@[to_additive (attr := gcongr) addCentralizer_subset]

name: centralizer_centralizer_centralizer
statement: theorem centralizer_centralizer_centralizer (S : Set M) : S.centralizer.centralizer.centralizer = S.centralizer
proof:
refine Set.Subset.antisymm ?_ Set.subset_centralizer_centralizer
  exact fun x hx y hy ↦ hx y <| Set.subset_centralizer_centralizer hy

@[to_additive decidableMemAddCentralizer]
instance decidableMemCentralizer [∀ a : M, Decidable <| ∀ b ∈ S, b * a = a * b] :
    DecidablePred (· ∈ centralizer S) := fun _ ↦ decidable_of_iff' _ mem_centralizer_iff

@[to_additive addCentralizer_addCentralizer_comm_of_comm]

name: inv_mem_center
statement: theorem inv_mem_center (ha : a ∈ Set.center M) : a⁻¹ ∈ Set.center M
proof:
rw [_root_.Semigroup.mem_center_iff]
  intro _
  rw [← inv_inj, mul_inv_rev, inv_inv, ha.comm, mul_inv_rev, inv_inv]

@[to_additive (attr := simp) sub_mem_addCenter]

name: coeff_inj
statement: theorem coeff_inj  : Function.Injective (coeff : HVertexOperator Γ R V W →ₗ[R] Γ → (V →ₗ[R] W))
proof:
intro _ _ h
  ext v n
  exact congrFun (congrArg DFunLike.coe (congrFun h n)) v

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
/-- Given a coefficient function valued in linear maps satisfying a partially well-ordered support
condition, we produce a heterogeneous vertex operator. -/
@[simps]

name: compHahnSeries_add
statement: theorem compHahnSeries_add (u v : U) : compHahnSeries A B (u + v) = compHahnSeries A B u + compHahnSeries A B v
proof:
ext
  simp only [compHahnSeries_coeff, map_add, coeff_apply_apply, HahnSeries.coeff_add', Pi.add_apply]
  rw [← HahnSeries.coeff_add]

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
@[simp]

name: coeff_comp
statement: theorem coeff_comp (g : Γ' ×ₗ Γ) : (comp A B).coeff g = A.coeff (ofLex g).2 ∘ₗ B.coeff (ofLex g).1
proof:
rfl

end Products

end HVertexOperator

name: le_one_toAddSubmonoid
statement: theorem le_one_toAddSubmonoid  : 1 ≤ (1 : Submodule R A).toAddSubmonoid
proof:
rintro x ⟨n, rfl⟩
  exact ⟨n, show (n : R) • (1 : A) = n by rw [Nat.cast_smul_eq_nsmul, nsmul_one]⟩

@[simp]

name: one_smul
statement: theorem one_smul  : (1 : Submodule R A) • N = N
proof:
refine le_antisymm (smul_le.mpr fun r hr m hm ↦ ?_) fun m hm ↦ ?_
  · obtain ⟨r, rfl⟩ := hr
    rw [LinearMap.toSpanSingleton_apply, smul_one_smul]; exact N.smul_mem r hm
  · rw [← one_smul A m]; exact smul_mem_smul (one_le.mp le_rfl) hm

name: pow_one
statement: theorem pow_one  : M ^ 1 = M
proof:
rw [Submodule.pow_succ, Submodule.pow_zero, Submodule.one_mul]

/-- `Submodule.pow_succ` with the right-hand side commuted. -/
protected theorem pow_succ' {n : ℕ} (h : n ≠ 0) : M ^ (n + 1) = M * M ^ n := by
  rw [add_comm, M.pow_add h, Submodule.pow_one]

@[simp]

name: one_eq_range
statement: theorem one_eq_range  : (1 : Submodule R A) = LinearMap.range (Algebra.linearMap R A)
proof:
rw [one_eq_span, LinearMap.span_singleton_eq_range,
    LinearMap.toSpanSingleton_one_eq_algebraLinearMap]

name: algebraMap_mem
statement: theorem algebraMap_mem (r : R) : algebraMap R A r ∈ (1 : Submodule R A)
proof:
simp [one_eq_range]

@[simp]

name: smul_one_eq_span
statement: theorem smul_one_eq_span (x : A) : x • (1 : Submodule R A) = span R {x}
proof: rw [one_eq_span, smul_span, smul_set_singleton, smul_eq_mul, mul_one]

name: map_op_one
statement: theorem map_op_one  : map (↑(opLinearEquiv R : A ≃ₗ[R] Aᵐᵒᵖ) : A →ₗ[R] Aᵐᵒᵖ) (1 : Submodule R A) = 1
proof:
ext x
  induction x
  simp

@[simp]

name: comap_op_one
statement: theorem comap_op_one  : comap (↑(opLinearEquiv R : A ≃ₗ[R] Aᵐᵒᵖ) : A →ₗ[R] Aᵐᵒᵖ) (1 : Submodule R Aᵐᵒᵖ) = 1
proof:
ext
  simp

@[simp]

name: map_unop_one
statement: theorem map_unop_one  : map (↑(opLinearEquiv R : A ≃ₗ[R] Aᵐᵒᵖ).symm : Aᵐᵒᵖ →ₗ[R] A) (1 : Submodule R Aᵐᵒᵖ) = 1
proof:
rw [← comap_equiv_eq_map_symm, comap_op_one]

@[simp]

name: comap_unop_one
statement: theorem comap_unop_one  : comap (↑(opLinearEquiv R : A ≃ₗ[R] Aᵐᵒᵖ).symm : Aᵐᵒᵖ →ₗ[R] A) (1 : Submodule R A) = 1
proof: rw [← map_equiv_eq_comap_symm, map_op_one]

name: span_mul_span
statement: theorem span_mul_span  : span R S * span R T = span R (S * T)
proof: rw [mul_eq_map₂]; apply map₂_span_span

name: mul_one
statement: theorem mul_one  : M * 1 = M
proof:
conv_lhs => rw [one_eq_span, ← span_eq M]
  rw [span_mul_span]
  simp

protected theorem map_mul {A'} [Semiring A'] [Algebra R A'] (f : A →ₐ[R] A') :
    map f.toLinearMap (M * N) = map f.toLinearMap M * map f.toLinearMap N :=
  calc
    map f.toLinearMap (M * N) = ⨆ i : M, (N.map (LinearMap.mul R A i)).map f.toLinearMap := by
      rw [mul_eq_map₂]; apply map_iSup
    _ = map f.toLinearMap M * map f.toLinearMap N := by
      rw [mul_eq_map₂]
      apply congr_arg sSup
      ext S
      constructor <;> rintro ⟨y, hy⟩
      · use ⟨f y, mem_map.mpr ⟨y.1, y.2, rfl⟩⟩
        refine Eq.trans ?_ hy
        ext
        simp
      · obtain ⟨y', hy', fy_eq⟩ := mem_map.mp y.2
        use ⟨y', hy'⟩
        refine Eq.trans ?_ hy
        rw [f.toLinearMap_apply] at fy_eq
        ext
        simp [fy_eq]

name: mul_eq_span_mul_set
statement: theorem mul_eq_span_mul_set (s t : Submodule R A) : s * t = span R ((s : Set A) * (t : Set A))
proof: rw [mul_eq_map₂]; exact map₂_eq_span_image2 _ s t

name: pow_eq_span_pow_set
statement: theorem pow_eq_span_pow_set (n : ℕ) : M ^ n = span R ((M : Set A) ^ n)
proof: rw [← span_pow, span_eq]

name: mker_spanSingleton
statement: theorem mker_spanSingleton  : MonoidHom.mker (Submodule.spanSingleton R) = (IsUnit.submonoid R).map (algebraMap R A)
proof:
ext; simp_rw [Submonoid.mem_map, IsUnit.mem_submonoid_iff, IsUnit, existsAndEq, true_and, eq_comm]
  exact span_singleton_eq_one_iff

/-- Exactness of the sequence `1 → Rˣ → Aˣ → (Submodule R A)ˣ → Pic R → Pic A` at `Aˣ`.
See Exercise I.3.7(iv) in [Weibel2013] or Theorem 2.4 in [RobertsSingh1993]. -/
/- Note: `assert_not_exists Submodule.hasQuotient` in `Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.Operations`
forbids importing `Function.MulExact` into this file. -/

name: isEpi_iff_forall_one_tmul_eq
statement: theorem isEpi_iff_forall_one_tmul_eq  : Algebra.IsEpi R A ↔ ∀ a : A, 1 ⊗ₜ[R] a = a ⊗ₜ[R] 1
proof:
refine ⟨fun h a ↦ IsEpi.injective_lift_mul <| by simp, fun h ↦ ⟨fun x y hxy ↦ ?_⟩⟩
  have h' (x : A ⊗[R] A) : ∃ a : A, x = a ⊗ₜ 1 := by
    induction x using TensorProduct.induction_on with
    | zero => exact ⟨0, by simp⟩
    | tmul u v =>
      use u * v
      calc u ⊗ₜ[R] v = u ⊗ₜ[R] 1 * 1 ⊗ₜ[R] v := by simp
                   _ = u ⊗ₜ[R] 1 * v ⊗ₜ[R] 1 := by rw [h]
                   _ = (u * v) ⊗ₜ[R] 1 := by simp
    | add u v hu hv =>
      obtain ⟨u, rfl⟩ := hu
      obtain ⟨v, rfl⟩ := hv
      exact ⟨u  + v, by simp [add_tmul]⟩
  obtain ⟨a, rfl⟩ := h' x
  obtain ⟨b, rfl⟩ := h' y
  aesop

/-- See also `Algebra.isEpi_iff_surjective_algebraMap_of_finite`. -/

name: isEpi_iff_surjective_algebraMap_of_finite
statement: theorem isEpi_iff_surjective_algebraMap_of_finite  : Algebra.IsEpi R A ↔ Surjective (algebraMap R A)
proof:
refine ⟨fun h ↦ ?_, isEpi_of_surjective_algebraMap R A⟩
  let R' := (Algebra.linearMap R A).range
  rcases subsingleton_or_nontrivial (A ⧸ R') with h | _
  · rwa [Submodule.Quotient.subsingleton_iff, LinearMap.range_eq_top] at h
  have : Subsingleton ((A ⧸ R') ⊗[R] (A ⧸ R')) := by
    refine subsingleton_of_forall_eq 0 fun y ↦ ?_
    induction y with
    | zero => rfl
    | add a b e₁ e₂ => rwa [e₁, zero_add]
    | tmul x y =>
      obtain ⟨x, rfl⟩ := R'.mkQ_surjective x
      obtain ⟨y, rfl⟩ := R'.mkQ_surjective y
      obtain ⟨s, hs⟩ : ∃ s, 1 ⊗ₜ[R] s = x ⊗ₜ[R] y := by
        use x * y
        trans x ⊗ₜ 1 * 1 ⊗ₜ y
        · simp [(isEpi_iff_forall_one_tmul_eq R A).mp]
        · simp
      have : R'.mkQ 1 = 0 := (Submodule.Quotient.mk_eq_zero R').mpr ⟨1, map_one (algebraMap R A)⟩
      rw [← map_tmul R'.mkQ R'.mkQ, ← hs, map_tmul, this, zero_tmul]
  cases false_of_nontrivial_of_subsingleton ((A ⧸ R') ⊗[R] (A ⧸ R'))

@[deprecated (since := "2026-01-13")]
alias _root_.RingHom.surjective_of_tmul_eq_tmul_of_finite :=
  isEpi_iff_surjective_algebraMap_of_finite

end Ring

section CommSemiring

variable (R A : Type*) [CommSemiring R] [CommSemiring A] [Algebra R A] [Algebra.IsEpi R A]

variable {A} in

name: tmul_comm
statement: theorem tmul_comm (a b : A) : a ⊗ₜ[R] b = b ⊗ₜ[R] a
proof:
have (a b : A) := calc a ⊗ₜ[R] b
      = a • (1 ⊗ₜ[R] b) := by rw [tmul_eq_smul_one_tmul]
    _ = a • (b ⊗ₜ[R] 1) := by rw [(isEpi_iff_forall_one_tmul_eq R A).mp inferInstance b]
    _ = a • (b • (1 ⊗ₜ[R] 1)) := by rw [tmul_eq_smul_one_tmul]
  rw [this a b, this b a, smul_comm]

section Module

variable (M : Type*) [AddCommMonoid M] [Module R M] [Module A M] [IsScalarTower R A M]

/-- If an `R`-algebra `A` is epi, then the scalar multiplication `A ⊗[R] M → M` is injective, for
any `A`-module `M`. -/

name: injective_lift_lsmul
statement: theorem injective_lift_lsmul  : Injective (lift <| LinearMap.restrictScalars₁₂ R R (LinearMap.lsmul A M))
proof:
/- Morally the proof is to recognise that we can construct the map `A ⊗[R] M → M` as a
  composition of (`A`-linear) equivalences:
  ```
  A ⊗[R] M ≃  A ⊗[R] (A ⊗[A] M)
           ≃ (A ⊗[R] A) ⊗[A] M
           ≃ A ⊗[A] M
           ≃ M
  ```
  However the second equivalence above requires a version of heterogeneous tensor product
  associativity which is problematic in Mathlib because `TensorProduct.leftModule` prioritises the
  left factor in any tensor product. We therefore formalise a slightly lower level proof below. -/
  suffices ∀ (a : A) (m : M), 1 ⊗ₜ[R] (a • m) = a ⊗ₜ[R] m by
    let f : M →ₗ[R] A ⊗[R] M :=
      { toFun m := 1 ⊗ₜ m
        map_add' m n := tmul_add _ _ _
        map_smul' r m := tmul_smul _ _ _ }
    have aux : f ∘ₗ (lift <| LinearMap.restrictScalars₁₂ R R (LinearMap.lsmul A M)) = .id := by
      ext a m; simpa using this a m
    exact HasLeftInverse.injective ⟨f, fun x ↦ congr($aux x)⟩
  intro a m
  let f : A ⊗[R] A →ₗ[R] A ⊗[R] M := lift
    { toFun x :=
      { toFun y := x ⊗ₜ (y • m)
        map_add' := by simp [add_smul, tmul_add]
        map_smul' := by simp }
      map_add' := by intros; ext; simp [add_tmul]
      map_smul' := by intros; ext; simp [smul_tmul'] }
  simpa [f] using congr_arg f (tmul_comm R 1 a)

/-- A heterogeneous variant of `TensorProduct.lid` when `R → A` is epi. -/

name: coe_range
statement: theorem coe_range (φ : F) : ((NonUnitalAlgHom.range φ : NonUnitalSubalgebra R B) : Set B) = Set.range (φ : A → B)
proof:
ext
  rw [SetLike.mem_coe, mem_range, Set.mem_range]

name: span_eq_toSubmodule
statement: theorem span_eq_toSubmodule (s : NonUnitalSubalgebra R A) : Submodule.span R (s : Set A) = s.toSubmodule
proof:
simp [SetLike.ext'_iff, Submodule.coe_span_eq_self]

variable [NonUnitalNonAssocSemiring B] [Module R B]
variable [FunLike F A B] [NonUnitalAlgHomClass F R A B]

section IsScalarTower

variable [IsScalarTower R A A] [SMulCommClass R A A]

/-- The minimal non-unital subalgebra that includes `s`. -/

name: adjoin_eq_span
statement: theorem adjoin_eq_span (s : Set A) : (adjoin R s).toSubmodule = span R (Subsemigroup.closure s)
proof:
apply le_antisymm
  · intro x hx
    induction hx using adjoin_induction with
    | mem x hx => exact subset_span <| Subsemigroup.subset_closure hx
    | add x y _ _ hpx hpy => exact add_mem hpx hpy
    | zero => exact zero_mem _
    | mul x y _ _ hpx hpy =>
      apply span_induction₂ ?Hs (by simp) (by simp) ?Hadd_l ?Hadd_r ?Hsmul_l ?Hsmul_r hpx hpy
      case Hs => exact fun x y hx hy ↦ subset_span <| mul_mem hx hy
      case Hadd_l => exact fun x y z _ _ _ hxz hyz ↦ by simpa [add_mul] using add_mem hxz hyz
      case Hadd_r => exact fun x y z _ _ _ hxz hyz ↦ by simpa [mul_add] using add_mem hxz hyz
      case Hsmul_l => exact fun r x y _ _ hxy ↦ by simpa [smul_mul_assoc] using smul_mem _ _ hxy
      case Hsmul_r => exact fun r x y _ _ hxy ↦ by simpa [mul_smul_comm] using smul_mem _ _ hxy
    | smul r x _ hpx => exact smul_mem _ _ hpx
  · apply span_le.2 _
    change Subsemigroup.closure s ≤ (adjoin R s).toSubsemigroup
    exact Subsemigroup.closure_le.2 (subset_adjoin R)

variable (R A)

@[simp]

name: toSubmodule_bot
statement: theorem toSubmodule_bot  : (⊥ : NonUnitalSubalgebra R A).toSubmodule = ⊥
proof:
ext
  simp only [mem_bot, NonUnitalSubalgebra.mem_toSubmodule, Submodule.mem_bot]

@[simp, norm_cast]

name: coe_bot
statement: theorem coe_bot  : ((⊥ : NonUnitalSubalgebra R A) : Set A) = {0}
proof: simp [Set.ext_iff, NonUnitalAlgebra.mem_bot]

name: coe_injective
statement: theorem coe_injective  : @Function.Injective (A →ₛₙₐ[φ] B) (A → B) (↑)
proof:
rintro ⟨⟨⟨f, _⟩, _⟩, _⟩ ⟨⟨⟨g, _⟩, _⟩, _⟩ h; congr
instance : FunLike (A →ₛₙₐ[φ] B) A B where
  coe f := f.toFun
  coe_injective' := coe_injective

instance : NonUnitalAlgSemiHomClass (A →ₛₙₐ[φ] B) φ A B where
  map_add f := f.map_add'
  map_zero f := f.map_zero'
  map_mul f := f.map_mul'
  map_smulₛₗ f := f.map_smul'

@[ext]

name: comp_symm
statement: theorem comp_symm (e : A₁ ≃ₐ[R] A₂) : AlgHom.comp (e : A₁ →ₐ[R] A₂) ↑e.symm = AlgHom.id R A₂
proof:
ext
  simp

@[simp]

name: symm_comp
statement: theorem symm_comp (e : A₁ ≃ₐ[R] A₂) : AlgHom.comp ↑e.symm (e : A₁ →ₐ[R] A₂) = AlgHom.id R A₁
proof:
ext
  simp

name: algebraMapSubmonoid_map_eq
statement: theorem algebraMapSubmonoid_map_eq (f : A →ₐ[R] B) : (algebraMapSubmonoid A M).map f = algebraMapSubmonoid B M
proof:
ext x
  constructor
  · rintro ⟨a, ⟨r, hr, rfl⟩, rfl⟩
    simp only [AlgHom.commutes]
    use r
  · rintro ⟨r, hr, rfl⟩
    simp only [Submonoid.mem_map]
    use (algebraMap R A r)
    simp only [AlgHom.commutes, and_true]
    use r

name: algebraMapSubmonoid_le_comap
statement: theorem algebraMapSubmonoid_le_comap (f : A →ₐ[R] B) : algebraMapSubmonoid A M ≤ (algebraMapSubmonoid B M).comap f.toRingHom
proof:
rw [← algebraMapSubmonoid_map_eq M f]
  exact Submonoid.le_comap_map (Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid A M)

end Algebra

namespace MulSemiringAction

variable {M G : Type*} (R A : Type*) [CommSemiring R] [Semiring A] [Algebra R A]
variable [Monoid M] [MulSemiringAction M A] [SMulCommClass M R A]

/-- Each element of the monoid defines an algebra homomorphism.

This is a stronger version of `MulSemiringAction.toRingHom` and
`DistribSMul.toLinearMap`. -/
@[simps]

name: starMap_id
statement: theorem starMap_id  : starMap (NonUnitalStarAlgHom.id R B) = StarAlgHom.id R (Unitization R B)
proof:
ext; all_goals simp

end StarMap

section StarNormal

variable {R A : Type*} [Semiring R]
variable [StarAddMonoid R] [Star A] {a : A}


@[simp]

name: isSelfAdjoint_inr
statement: theorem isSelfAdjoint_inr  : IsSelfAdjoint (a : Unitization R A) ↔ IsSelfAdjoint a
proof:
simp only [isSelfAdjoint_iff, ← inr_star, inr_injective.eq_iff]

alias ⟨_root_.IsSelfAdjoint.of_inr, _⟩ := isSelfAdjoint_inr

variable (R) in

name: isStarNormal_inr
statement: theorem isStarNormal_inr  : IsStarNormal (a : Unitization R A) ↔ IsStarNormal a
proof:
simp only [isStarNormal_iff, commute_iff_eq, ← inr_star, ← inr_mul, inr_injective.eq_iff]

alias ⟨_root_.IsStarNormal.of_inr, _⟩ := isStarNormal_inr

variable (R a) in
instance instIsStarNormal (a : A) [IsStarNormal a] :
    IsStarNormal (a : Unitization R A) :=
  isStarNormal_inr.mpr ‹_›

@[simp]

name: algebraMap_apply
statement: theorem algebraMap_apply (x : R) : algebraMap R A x = algebraMap S A (algebraMap R S x)
proof:
rw [algebraMap_eq R S A, RingHom.comp_apply]

@[ext]

name: algebraMapSubmonoid_powers
statement: theorem algebraMapSubmonoid_powers (r : R) : Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid S (.powers r) = Submonoid.powers (algebraMap R S r)
proof: simp [Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid]

name: algebraMapSubmonoid_isUnit_le
statement: theorem algebraMapSubmonoid_isUnit_le  : algebraMapSubmonoid S (IsUnit.submonoid R) ≤ IsUnit.submonoid S
proof:
rintro x ⟨y, hy, rfl⟩
  exact hy.map _

section CommSemiring

variable [CommSemiring R]

name: faithfulSMul_iff_algebraMap_injective
statement: theorem faithfulSMul_iff_algebraMap_injective  : FaithfulSMul R A ↔ Injective (algebraMap R A)
proof:
rw [faithfulSMul_iff_injective_smul_one, Algebra.algebraMap_eq_smul_one']

name: isTorsionFree_iff_algebraMap_injective
statement: theorem isTorsionFree_iff_algebraMap_injective  : IsTorsionFree R A ↔ Injective (algebraMap R A)
proof:

@[deprecated (since := "2026-01-21")]

name: commute_mulLeft_right
statement: theorem commute_mulLeft_right (a b : A) : Commute (mulLeft R a) (mulRight R b)
proof:
ext c
  exact (mul_assoc a c b).symm

/-- A `LinearMap` preserves multiplication if pre- and post- composition with `LinearMap.mul` are
equivalent. By converting the statement into an equality of `LinearMap`s, this lemma allows various
specialized `ext` lemmas about `→ₗ[R]` to then be applied.

This is the `LinearMap` version of `AddMonoidHom.map_mul_iff`. -/

name: embedding_injective
statement: theorem embedding_injective  : Injective (embedding M)
proof:
simpa [embedding] using mk_left_injective 0

end AffineAddMonoid

name: irreducible_mem_submonoidClosure_subset
statement: theorem irreducible_mem_submonoidClosure_subset  : {p ∈ Submonoid.closure S | Irreducible p} ⊆ S
proof:
refine fun x hx ↦
      Submonoid.closure_induction (s := S) (motive := fun x _ ↦ (Irreducible x → x ∈ S))
      (fun _ hx _ ↦ hx) (by simp) (fun a b _ _ ha hb h ↦ ?_) hx.1 hx.2
  obtain rfl | rfl := h.eq_one_or_eq_one <;> simp_all

/-- In a monoid with a single unit, irreducible elements lie in all generating sets. -/
@[to_additive
/-- In an additive monoid with a single unit, irreducible elements lie in all generating sets. -/]

name: irreducible_subset_of_submonoidClosure_eq_top
statement: theorem irreducible_subset_of_submonoidClosure_eq_top (hS : Submonoid.closure S = ⊤) : {p | Irreducible p} ⊆ S
proof:
simpa [hS] using irreducible_mem_submonoidClosure_subset (S := S)

/-- A finitely generated submonoid of a monoid with a single unit has finitely many irreducible
elements. -/
@[to_additive
/-- A finitely generated submonoid of an additive monoid with a single unit has finitely many
irreducible elements. -/]

name: finite_irreducible
statement: theorem finite_irreducible  : {p : M | Irreducible p}.Finite
proof:
simpa using Monoid.FG.fg_top.finite_irreducible_mem_submonoidClosure

end CommMonoid

section CancelCommMonoid
variable [CancelCommMonoid M] [Subsingleton Mˣ]

/-- A finitely generated cancellative monoid with a single unit is generated by its (finitely many)
irreducible elements. -/
@[to_additive (attr := simp)
/-- A finitely generated cancellative additive monoid with a single unit is generated by its
(finitely many) irreducible elements. -/]

name: not_zero_iff
statement: theorem not_zero_iff  : ¬IsSMulRegular M (0 : R) ↔ Nontrivial M
proof:
rw [nontrivial_iff, not_iff_comm, zero_iff_subsingleton, subsingleton_iff]
  push Not
  exact Iff.rfl

/-- The element `0` is `M`-regular when `M` is trivial. -/

name: mul_iff
statement: theorem mul_iff  : IsSMulRegular M (a * b) ↔ IsSMulRegular M a ∧ IsSMulRegular M b
proof:
rw [← mul_and_mul_iff]
  exact ⟨fun ab => ⟨ab, by rwa [mul_comm]⟩, fun rab => rab.1⟩

end CommSemigroup

end IsSMulRegular

section Group

variable {G : Type*} [Group G]

/-- An element of a group acting on a Type is regular. This relies on the availability
of the inverse given by groups, since there is no `LeftCancelSMul` typeclass. -/

name: isRegular_mul_and_mul_iff
statement: theorem isRegular_mul_and_mul_iff  : IsRegular (a * b) ∧ IsRegular (b * a) ↔ IsRegular a ∧ IsRegular b
proof:
refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
  · rintro ⟨ab, ba⟩
    exact
      ⟨⟨IsLeftRegular.of_mul ba.left, IsRightRegular.of_mul ab.right⟩,
        ⟨IsLeftRegular.of_mul ab.left, IsRightRegular.of_mul ba.right⟩⟩
  · rintro ⟨ha, hb⟩
    exact ⟨ha.mul hb, hb.mul ha⟩

/-- The "most used" implication of `mul_and_mul_iff`, with split hypotheses, instead of `∧`. -/
@[to_additive /-- The "most used" implication of `add_and_add_iff`, with split
hypotheses, instead of `∧`. -/]

name: isRegular_mul_iff
statement: theorem isRegular_mul_iff  : IsRegular (a * b) ↔ IsRegular a ∧ IsRegular b
proof:
refine Iff.trans ?_ isRegular_mul_and_mul_iff
  exact ⟨fun ab => ⟨ab, by rwa [mul_comm]⟩, fun rab => rab.1⟩

end CommSemigroup

section Monoid

variable [Monoid R] {a b : R} {n : ℕ}

/-- An element admitting a left inverse is left-regular. -/
@[to_additive /-- An element admitting a left additive opposite is add-left-regular. -/]

name: inv_inl
statement: theorem inv_inl (r : R) : (inl r)⁻¹ = (inl (r⁻¹ : R) : tsze R M)
proof:
ext
  · rw [fst_inv, fst_inl, fst_inl]
  · rw [snd_inv, fst_inl, snd_inl, snd_inl, smul_zero, smul_zero, neg_zero]

@[simp]

name: inv_inr
statement: theorem inv_inr (m : M) : (inr m)⁻¹ = (0 : tsze R M)
proof:
ext
  · rw [fst_inv, fst_inr, fst_zero, inv_zero]
  · rw [snd_inv, snd_inr, fst_inr, inv_zero, op_zero, zero_smul, snd_zero, neg_zero]

@[simp]
protected theorem inv_zero : (0 : tsze R M)⁻¹ = (0 : tsze R M) := by
  rw [← inl_zero, TrivSqZeroExt.inv_inl, inv_zero]

@[simp]
protected theorem inv_one : (1 : tsze R M)⁻¹ = (1 : tsze R M) := by
  rw [← inl_one, TrivSqZeroExt.inv_inl, inv_one]

protected theorem inv_mul_cancel {x : tsze R M} (hx : fst x ≠ 0) : x⁻¹ * x = 1 := by
  convert mul_left_eq_one _ _ (_root_.inv_mul_cancel₀ hx) using 2
  ext <;> simp

variable [SMulCommClass R Rᵐᵒᵖ M]

@[simp] theorem invOf_eq_inv (x : tsze R M) [Invertible x] : ⅟x = x⁻¹ := by
  letI := invertibleFstOfInvertible x
  ext <;> simp [fst_invOf, snd_invOf]

protected theorem mul_inv_cancel {x : tsze R M} (hx : fst x ≠ 0) : x * x⁻¹ = 1 := by
  have : Invertible x.fst := Units.invertible (.mk0 _ hx)
  have := invertibleOfInvertibleFst x
  rw [← invOf_eq_inv, mul_invOf_self]

protected theorem mul_inv_rev (a b : tsze R M) :
    (a * b)⁻¹ = b⁻¹ * a⁻¹ := by
  ext
  · rw [fst_inv, fst_mul, fst_mul, mul_inv_rev, fst_inv, fst_inv]
  · simp only [snd_inv, snd_mul, fst_mul, fst_inv]
    simp only [smul_neg, smul_add]
    simp_rw [mul_inv_rev, smul_comm (_ : R), op_smul_op_smul, smul_smul, add_comm, neg_add]
    obtain ha0 | ha := eq_or_ne (fst a) 0
    · simp [ha0]
    obtain hb0 | hb := eq_or_ne (fst b) 0
    · simp [hb0]
    rw [inv_mul_cancel_right₀ ha, mul_inv_cancel_left₀ hb]

protected theorem inv_inv {x : tsze R M} (hx : fst x ≠ 0) : x⁻¹⁻¹ = x :=
  -- adapted from `Matrix.nonsing_inv_nonsing_inv`
  calc
    x⁻¹⁻¹ = 1 * x⁻¹⁻¹ := by rw [one_mul]
    _ = x * x⁻¹ * x⁻¹⁻¹ := by rw [TrivSqZeroExt.mul_inv_cancel hx]
    _ = x := by
      rw [mul_assoc, TrivSqZeroExt.mul_inv_cancel, mul_one]
      rw [fst_inv]
      apply inv_ne_zero hx

@[simp]

name: range_inlAlgHom_sup_adjoin_range_inr
statement: theorem range_inlAlgHom_sup_adjoin_range_inr  : (inlAlgHom S R M).range ⊔ Algebra.adjoin S (Set.range inr) = (⊤ : Subalgebra S (tsze R M))
proof:
refine top_unique fun x hx => ?_; clear hx
  rw [← x.inl_fst_add_inr_snd_eq]
  refine add_mem ?_ ?_
  · exact le_sup_left (α := Subalgebra S _) <| Set.mem_range_self x.fst
  · exact le_sup_right (α := Subalgebra S _) <| Algebra.subset_adjoin <| Set.mem_range_self x.snd

@[simp]

name: map_id
statement: theorem map_id  : map (LinearMap.id : M →ₗ[R'] M) = AlgHom.id R' _
proof:
apply algHom_ext
  simp only [map_inr, LinearMap.id_coe, id_eq, AlgHom.coe_id, forall_const]

name: mem_kerIdeal_iff_inr
statement: theorem mem_kerIdeal_iff_inr (x : TrivSqZeroExt R M) : x ∈ kerIdeal R M ↔ x = inr x.snd
proof:
obtain ⟨r, m⟩ := x
  simp only [kerIdeal, RingHom.mem_ker, fstHom_apply, fst_mk]
  exact ⟨fun hr => by rw [hr]; rfl, fun hrm => by rw [← fst_mk r m, hrm, fst_inr]⟩

@[simp] theorem kerIdeal_sq : kerIdeal R M ^ 2 = ⊥ := by
  simp only [pow_two, eq_bot_iff, mul_le, mem_kerIdeal_iff_inr]
  rintro x hx y hy
  rw [hx, hy, mem_bot, inr_mul_inr]

end TrivSqZeroExt

end

name: hasseDeriv_apply
statement: theorem hasseDeriv_apply  : hasseDeriv k f = f.sum fun i r => monomial (i - k) (↑(i.choose k) * r)
proof:
dsimp [hasseDeriv]
  simp

name: hasseDeriv_coeff
statement: theorem hasseDeriv_coeff (n : ℕ) : (hasseDeriv k f).coeff n = (n + k).choose k * f.coeff (n + k)
proof:
rw [hasseDeriv_apply, coeff_sum, sum_def, Finset.sum_eq_single (n + k), coeff_monomial]
  · simp
  · #adaptation_note
    /-- Prior to nightly-2025-08-14, this was working as
    `grind [coeff_monomial, Nat.choose_eq_zero_of_lt, Nat.cast_zero, zero_mul]` -/
    intro i _hi hink
    rw [coeff_monomial]
    by_cases hik : i < k
    · simp only [Nat.choose_eq_zero_of_lt hik, ite_self, Nat.cast_zero, zero_mul]
    · grind
  · intro h
    simp only [notMem_support_iff.mp h, monomial_zero_right, mul_zero, coeff_zero]

name: hasseDeriv_apply_one
statement: theorem hasseDeriv_apply_one (hk : 0 < k) : hasseDeriv k (1 : R[X]) = 0
proof: rw [← C_1, hasseDeriv_C k _ hk]

name: hasseDeriv_X
statement: theorem hasseDeriv_X (hk : 1 < k) : hasseDeriv k (X : R[X]) = 0
proof:
rw [← monomial_one_one_eq_X, hasseDeriv_monomial, Nat.choose_eq_zero_of_lt hk, Nat.cast_zero,
    zero_mul, monomial_zero_right]

name: factorial_smul_hasseDeriv
statement: theorem factorial_smul_hasseDeriv  : ⇑(k ! • @hasseDeriv R _ k) = (@derivative R _)^[k]
proof:
induction k with
  | zero => rw [hasseDeriv_zero, factorial_zero, iterate_zero, one_smul, LinearMap.id_coe]
  | succ k ih => ?_
  ext f n : 2
  rw [iterate_succ_apply', ← ih]
  simp only [LinearMap.smul_apply, coeff_smul, LinearMap.map_smul_of_tower, coeff_derivative,
    hasseDeriv_coeff, ← @choose_symm_add _ k]
  simp only [nsmul_eq_mul, factorial_succ, mul_assoc, succ_eq_add_one, ← add_assoc,
    add_right_comm n 1 k, ← cast_succ]
  rw [← (cast_commute (n + 1) (f.coeff (n + k + 1))).eq]
  simp only [← mul_assoc]
  norm_cast
  congr 2
  rw [mul_comm (k + 1) _, mul_assoc, mul_assoc]
  congr 1
  have : n + k + 1 = n + (k + 1) := by apply add_assoc
  rw [← choose_symm_of_eq_add this, choose_succ_right_eq, mul_comm]
  congr
  rw [add_assoc, add_tsub_cancel_left]

name: hasseDeriv_comp
statement: theorem hasseDeriv_comp (k l : ℕ) : (@hasseDeriv R _ k).comp (hasseDeriv l) = (k + l).choose k • hasseDeriv (k + l)
proof:
ext i : 2
  simp only [LinearMap.smul_apply, comp_apply, LinearMap.coe_comp, smul_monomial, hasseDeriv_apply,
    mul_one, monomial_eq_zero_iff, sum_monomial_index, mul_zero, ←
    tsub_add_eq_tsub_tsub, add_comm l k]
  rw_mod_cast [nsmul_eq_mul]
  rw [← Nat.cast_mul]
  congr 2
  by_cases! hikl : i < k + l
  · rw [choose_eq_zero_of_lt hikl, mul_zero]
    by_cases! hil : i < l
    · rw [choose_eq_zero_of_lt hil, mul_zero]
    · rw [← tsub_lt_iff_right hil] at hikl
      rw [choose_eq_zero_of_lt hikl, zero_mul]
  apply @cast_injective ℚ
  have h1 : l ≤ i := le_of_add_le_right hikl
  have h2 : k ≤ i - l := le_tsub_of_add_le_right hikl
  have h3 : k ≤ k + l := le_self_add
  push_cast
  rw [cast_choose ℚ h1, cast_choose ℚ h2, cast_choose ℚ h3, cast_choose ℚ hikl]
  rw [show i - (k + l) = i - l - k by rw [add_comm]; apply tsub_add_eq_tsub_tsub]
  simp only [add_tsub_cancel_left]
  field

name: hasseDeriv_natDegree_eq_C
statement: theorem hasseDeriv_natDegree_eq_C  : f.hasseDeriv f.natDegree = C f.leadingCoeff
proof:
have : _ ≤ 0 := Nat.sub_self f.natDegree ▸ natDegree_hasseDeriv_le ..
  rw [eq_C_of_natDegree_le_zero this, hasseDeriv_coeff, zero_add, Nat.choose_self,
    Nat.cast_one, one_mul, leadingCoeff]

name: hasseDeriv_mul
statement: theorem hasseDeriv_mul (f g : R[X]) : hasseDeriv k (f * g) = ∑ ij ∈ antidiagonal k, hasseDeriv ij.1 f * hasseDeriv ij.2 g
proof:
let D k := (@hasseDeriv R _ k).toAddMonoidHom
  let Φ := @AddMonoidHom.mul R[X] _
  change
    (compHom (D k)).comp Φ f g =
      ∑ ij ∈ antidiagonal k, ((compHom.comp ((compHom Φ) (D ij.1))).flip (D ij.2) f) g
  simp only [← finsetSum_apply]
  congr 2
  clear f g
  ext m r n s : 4
  simp only [Φ, D, finsetSum_apply, coe_mulLeft, coe_comp, flip_apply, Function.comp_apply,
             hasseDeriv_monomial, LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom_coe, compHom_apply_apply,
             coe_mul, monomial_mul_monomial]
  have aux :
    ∀ x : ℕ × ℕ,
      x ∈ antidiagonal k →
        monomial (m - x.1 + (n - x.2)) (↑(m.choose x.1) * r * (↑(n.choose x.2) * s)) =
          monomial (m + n - k) (↑(m.choose x.1) * ↑(n.choose x.2) * (r * s)) := by
    intro x hx
    rw [mem_antidiagonal] at hx
    subst hx
    by_cases! hm : m < x.1
    · simp only [Nat.choose_eq_zero_of_lt hm, Nat.cast_zero, zero_mul,
                 monomial_zero_right]
    by_cases! hn : n < x.2
    · simp only [Nat.choose_eq_zero_of_lt hn, Nat.cast_zero, zero_mul,
                 mul_zero, monomial_zero_right]
    rw [tsub_add_eq_add_tsub hm, ← add_tsub_assoc_of_le hn, ← tsub_add_eq_tsub_tsub,
      add_comm x.2 x.1, mul_assoc, ← mul_assoc r, ← (Nat.cast_commute _ r).eq, mul_assoc, mul_assoc]
  rw [Finset.sum_congr rfl aux]
  rw [← map_sum, ← Finset.sum_mul]
  congr
  rw_mod_cast [← Nat.add_choose_eq]

end

end Polynomial

name: mem_lifts
statement: theorem mem_lifts (p : S[X]) : p ∈ lifts f ↔ ∃ q : R[X], map f q = p
proof: simp only [coe_mapRingHom, lifts, RingHom.mem_rangeS]

name: lifts_iff_set_range
statement: theorem lifts_iff_set_range (p : S[X]) : p ∈ lifts f ↔ p ∈ Set.range (map f)
proof: simp only [coe_mapRingHom, lifts, Set.mem_range, RingHom.mem_rangeS]

name: lifts_iff_ringHom_rangeS
statement: theorem lifts_iff_ringHom_rangeS (p : S[X]) : p ∈ lifts f ↔ p ∈ (mapRingHom f).rangeS
proof: simp only [coe_mapRingHom, lifts, RingHom.mem_rangeS]

name: lifts_iff_coeff_lifts
statement: theorem lifts_iff_coeff_lifts (p : S[X]) : p ∈ lifts f ↔ ∀ n : ℕ, p.coeff n ∈ Set.range f
proof:
rw [lifts_iff_ringHom_rangeS, mem_map_rangeS f]
  rfl

name: lifts_iff_coeffs_subset_range
statement: theorem lifts_iff_coeffs_subset_range (p : S[X]) : p ∈ lifts f ↔ (p.coeffs : Set S) ⊆ Set.range f
proof:
rw [lifts_iff_coeff_lifts]
  constructor
  · intro h _ hc
    obtain ⟨n, ⟨-, hn⟩⟩ := mem_coeffs_iff.mp hc
    exact hn ▸ h n
  · intro h n
    by_cases hn : p.coeff n = 0
    · exact ⟨0, by simp [hn]⟩
    · exact h <| coeff_mem_coeffs hn

name: lifts_iff_liftsRing
statement: theorem lifts_iff_liftsRing (p : S[X]) : p ∈ lifts f ↔ p ∈ liftsRing f
proof:
