simp only [lifts, liftsRing, RingHom.mem_range, RingHom.mem_rangeS]

end Ring

section Algebra

variable {R : Type u} [CommSemiring R] {S : Type v} [Semiring S] [Algebra R S]

/-- A polynomial `p` lifts if and only if it is in the image of `mapAlg`. -/

name: mkDerivation_one_eq_derivative
statement: theorem mkDerivation_one_eq_derivative (f : R[X]) : mkDerivation R (1 : R[X]) f = derivative f
proof:
rw [mkDerivation_one_eq_derivative']
  rfl

/-- `Polynomial.mkDerivation` as a linear equivalence. -/

name: mkDerivationEquiv_apply
statement: theorem mkDerivationEquiv_apply (a : A) : mkDerivationEquiv R a = mkDerivation R a
proof:
rfl

@[simp] lemma mkDerivationEquiv_symm_apply (D : Derivation R R[X] A) :
    (mkDerivationEquiv R).symm D = D X := rfl

end CommSemiring
end Polynomial

namespace Derivation

variable {R A M : Type*} [CommSemiring R] [CommSemiring A] [Algebra R A] [AddCommMonoid M]
  [Module A M] [Module R M] [IsScalarTower R A M] (d : Derivation R A M) (a : A)

open Polynomial Module

set_option linter.style.whitespace false in -- manual alignment is not recognised
/--
For a derivation `d : A → M` and an element `a : A`, `d.compAEval a` is the
derivation of `R[X]` which takes a polynomial `f` to `d(aeval a f)`.

This derivation takes values in `Module.AEval R M a`, which is `M`, regarded as an
`R[X]`-module, with the action of a polynomial `f` defined by `f • m = (aeval a f) • m`.
-/
/-
Note: `compAEval` is not defined using `Derivation.compAlgebraMap`.
This because `A` is not an `R[X]` algebra and it would be messy to create an algebra instance
within the definition.
-/
@[simps]

name: normUnit_X
statement: theorem normUnit_X  : normUnit (X : Polynomial R) = 1
proof:
have := coe_normUnit (R := R) (p := X)
  rwa [leadingCoeff_X, normUnit_one, Units.val_one, map_one, Units.val_eq_one] at this

name: X_eq_normalize
statement: theorem X_eq_normalize  : (X : Polynomial R) = normalize X
proof:
simp only [normalize_apply, normUnit_X, Units.val_one, mul_one]

end NormalizationMonoid

end IsDomain

section DivisionRing

variable [DivisionRing R] {p q : R[X]}

name: quotient_mul_add_remainder_eq_aux
statement: theorem quotient_mul_add_remainder_eq_aux (p q : R[X]) : q * div p q + mod p q = p
proof:
by_cases h : q = 0
  · simp only [h, zero_mul, mod, modByMonic_zero, zero_add]
  · conv =>
      rhs
      rw [← modByMonic_add_div p (q * C q.leadingCoeff⁻¹)]
    rw [div, mod, add_comm, mul_assoc]

private theorem remainder_lt_aux (p : R[X]) (hq : q ≠ 0) : degree (mod p q) < degree q := by
  rw [← degree_mul_leadingCoeff_inv q hq]
  exact degree_modByMonic_lt p (monic_mul_leadingCoeff_inv hq)

instance : Div R[X] :=
  ⟨div⟩

instance : Mod R[X] :=
  ⟨mod⟩

name: degree_div_le
statement: theorem degree_div_le (p q : R[X]) : degree (p / q) ≤ degree p
proof:
by_cases hq : q = 0
  · simp [hq]
  · rw [div_def, mul_comm, degree_mul_leadingCoeff_inv _ hq]; exact degree_divByMonic_le _ _

name: add_mod
statement: theorem add_mod (p₁ p₂ q : R[X]) : (p₁ + p₂) % q = p₁ % q + p₂ % q
proof: simp [Polynomial.mod_def, Polynomial.add_modByMonic]

name: sub_mod
statement: theorem sub_mod (p₁ p₂ q : R[X]) : (p₁ - p₂) % q = p₁ % q - p₂ % q
proof: simp [Polynomial.mod_def, Polynomial.sub_modByMonic]

name: mul_mod
statement: theorem mul_mod (p₁ p₂ q : R[X]) : (p₁ * p₂) % q = (p₁ % q) * (p₂ % q) % q
proof:
simp_rw [Polynomial.mod_def]
  apply Polynomial.mul_modByMonic

section

open EuclideanDomain

name: roots_degree_eq_one
statement: theorem roots_degree_eq_one (h : degree p = 1) : p.roots = {-((p.coeff 1)⁻¹ * p.coeff 0)}
proof:
rw [eq_X_add_C_of_degree_le_one (show degree p ≤ 1 by rw [h])]
  have : p.coeff 1 ≠ 0 := coeff_ne_zero_of_eq_degree h
  simp [roots_C_mul_X_add_C _ this]

name: leadingCoeff_div
statement: theorem leadingCoeff_div (hpq : q.degree ≤ p.degree) : (p / q).leadingCoeff = p.leadingCoeff / q.leadingCoeff
proof:
by_cases hq : q = 0
  · simp [hq]
  rw [div_def, leadingCoeff_mul, leadingCoeff_C,
    leadingCoeff_divByMonic_of_monic (monic_mul_leadingCoeff_inv hq) _, mul_comm,
    div_eq_mul_inv]
  rwa [degree_mul_leadingCoeff_inv q hq]

name: div_C_mul
statement: theorem div_C_mul  : p / (C a * q) = C a⁻¹ * (p / q)
proof:
by_cases ha : a = 0
  · simp [ha]
  simp only [div_def, leadingCoeff_mul, mul_inv, leadingCoeff_C, C.map_mul, mul_assoc]
  congr 3
  rw [mul_left_comm q, ← mul_assoc, ← C.map_mul, mul_inv_cancel₀ ha, C.map_one, one_mul]

name: div_C
statement: theorem div_C  : p / C a = p * C a⁻¹
proof: simpa [mul_comm] using div_C_mul (q := 1)

name: C_div
statement: theorem C_div  : C (a / b) = C a / C b
proof: rw [div_C, ← C_mul, div_eq_mul_inv]

name: degree_normalize
statement: theorem degree_normalize  : degree (normalize p) = degree p
proof: simp [normalize_apply]

name: prime_of_degree_eq_one
statement: theorem prime_of_degree_eq_one (hp1 : degree p = 1) : Prime p
proof:
classical
  have : Prime (normalize p) :=
    Monic.prime_of_degree_eq_one (hp1 ▸ degree_normalize)
      (monic_normalize fun hp0 ↦ absurd hp1 (by simp [hp0]))
  exact (normalize_associated _).prime this

name: not_irreducible_C
statement: theorem not_irreducible_C (x : R) : ¬Irreducible (C x)
proof:
by_cases H : x = 0
  · rw [H, C_0]
    exact not_irreducible_zero
  · exact fun hx => hx.not_isUnit <| isUnit_C.2 <| isUnit_iff_ne_zero.2 H

name: degree_pos
statement: theorem degree_pos (h : Irreducible f) : 0 < f.degree
proof:
rw [← natDegree_pos_iff_degree_pos]
  exact h.natDegree_pos

end Irreducible

name: taylor_coeff_zero
statement: theorem taylor_coeff_zero  : (taylor r f).coeff 0 = f.eval r
proof:
rw [taylor_coeff, hasseDeriv_zero, LinearMap.id_apply]

@[simp]

name: taylor_coeff_one
statement: theorem taylor_coeff_one  : (taylor r f).coeff 1 = f.derivative.eval r
proof:
rw [taylor_coeff, hasseDeriv_one]

@[simp]

name: coeff_taylor_natDegree
statement: theorem coeff_taylor_natDegree  : (taylor r f).coeff f.natDegree = f.leadingCoeff
proof:
by_cases hf : f = 0
  · rw [hf, map_zero, coeff_natDegree]
  · rw [taylor_coeff, hasseDeriv_natDegree_eq_C, eval_C]

@[simp]

name: leadingCoeff_taylor
statement: theorem leadingCoeff_taylor  : (taylor r f).leadingCoeff = f.leadingCoeff
proof:
rw [leadingCoeff, leadingCoeff, natDegree_taylor, coeff_taylor_natDegree, leadingCoeff]

@[simp]

name: taylor_eq_zero
statement: theorem taylor_eq_zero  : taylor r f = 0 ↔ f = 0
proof:
rw [← leadingCoeff_eq_zero, ← leadingCoeff_eq_zero, leadingCoeff_taylor]

@[simp]

name: degree_le_zero_iff_eq_one
statement: theorem degree_le_zero_iff_eq_one (hp : p.Monic) : p.degree ≤ 0 ↔ p = 1
proof: rw [← hp.natDegree_eq_zero, natDegree_eq_zero_iff_degree_le_zero]

name: monic_X_sub_C
statement: theorem monic_X_sub_C (x : R) : Monic (X - C x)
proof: simpa only [sub_eq_add_neg, C_neg] using monic_X_add_C (-x)

name: monic_of_isUnit_leadingCoeff_inv_smul
statement: theorem monic_of_isUnit_leadingCoeff_inv_smul (h : IsUnit p.leadingCoeff) : Monic (h.unit⁻¹ • p)
proof:
rw [Monic.def, leadingCoeff_smul_of_smul_regular _ (isSMulRegular_of_group _), Units.smul_def]
  simp

name: trailingDegree_mul
statement: theorem trailingDegree_mul  : (p * q).trailingDegree = p.trailingDegree + q.trailingDegree
proof:
by_cases hp : p = 0
  · rw [hp, zero_mul, trailingDegree_zero, top_add]
  by_cases hq : q = 0
  · rw [hq, mul_zero, trailingDegree_zero, add_top]
  · rw [trailingDegree_eq_natTrailingDegree hp, trailingDegree_eq_natTrailingDegree hq,
    trailingDegree_eq_natTrailingDegree (mul_ne_zero hp hq), natTrailingDegree_mul hp hq]
    apply WithTop.coe_add

end NoZeroDivisors


section CommRing

variable [CommRing R]

name: degree_eq_degree_of_associated
statement: theorem degree_eq_degree_of_associated (h : Associated p q) : degree p = degree q
proof:
let ⟨u, hu⟩ := h
  simp [hu.symm]

name: prime_X
statement: theorem prime_X  : Prime (X : R[X])
proof:
convert prime_X_sub_C (0 : R)
  simp

name: reflect_monomial
statement: theorem reflect_monomial (N n : ℕ) : reflect N ((X : R[X]) ^ n) = X ^ revAt N n
proof:
rw [← one_mul (X ^ n), ← one_mul (X ^ revAt N n), ← C_1, reflect_C_mul_X_pow]

@[simp] lemma reflect_one_X : reflect 1 (X : R[X]) = 1 := by
  simpa using reflect_monomial 1 1 (R := R)

name: reflect_one
statement: theorem reflect_one (n : ℕ) : (1 : R[X]).reflect n = Polynomial.X ^ n
proof: rw [← C.map_one, reflect_C, map_one, one_mul]

name: coeff_zero_reverse
statement: theorem coeff_zero_reverse (f : R[X]) : coeff (reverse f) 0 = leadingCoeff f
proof:
rw [coeff_reverse, revAt_le zero_le, tsub_zero, leadingCoeff]

@[simp]

name: reverse_natDegree_le
statement: theorem reverse_natDegree_le (f : R[X]) : f.reverse.natDegree ≤ f.natDegree
proof:
rw [natDegree_le_iff_degree_le, degree_le_iff_coeff_zero]
  intro n hn
  rw [Nat.cast_lt] at hn
  rw [coeff_reverse, revAt, Function.Embedding.coeFn_mk, if_neg (not_le_of_gt hn),
    coeff_eq_zero_of_natDegree_lt hn]

name: natDegree_eq_reverse_natDegree_add_natTrailingDegree
statement: theorem natDegree_eq_reverse_natDegree_add_natTrailingDegree (f : R[X]) : f.natDegree = f.reverse.natDegree + f.natTrailingDegree
proof:
by_cases hf : f = 0
  · rw [hf, reverse_zero, natDegree_zero, natTrailingDegree_zero]
  apply le_antisymm
  · refine tsub_le_iff_right.mp ?_
    apply le_natDegree_of_ne_zero
    rw [reverse, coeff_reflect, ← revAt_le f.natTrailingDegree_le_natDegree, revAt_invol]
    exact trailingCoeff_nonzero_iff_nonzero.mpr hf
  · rw [← le_tsub_iff_left f.reverse_natDegree_le]
    apply natTrailingDegree_le_of_ne_zero
    have key := mt leadingCoeff_eq_zero.mp (mt reverse_eq_zero.mp hf)
    rwa [leadingCoeff, coeff_reverse, revAt_le f.reverse_natDegree_le] at key

name: reverse_natDegree
statement: theorem reverse_natDegree (f : R[X]) : f.reverse.natDegree = f.natDegree - f.natTrailingDegree
proof: rw [f.natDegree_eq_reverse_natDegree_add_natTrailingDegree, add_tsub_cancel_right]

name: reverse_leadingCoeff
statement: theorem reverse_leadingCoeff (f : R[X]) : f.reverse.leadingCoeff = f.trailingCoeff
proof:
rw [leadingCoeff, reverse_natDegree, ← revAt_le f.natTrailingDegree_le_natDegree,
    coeff_reverse, revAt_invol, trailingCoeff]

name: natTrailingDegree_reverse
statement: theorem natTrailingDegree_reverse (f : R[X]) : f.reverse.natTrailingDegree = 0
proof:
rw [natTrailingDegree_eq_zero, reverse_eq_zero, coeff_zero_reverse, leadingCoeff_ne_zero]
  exact eq_or_ne _ _

name: reverse_trailingCoeff
statement: theorem reverse_trailingCoeff (f : R[X]) : f.reverse.trailingCoeff = f.leadingCoeff
proof: rw [trailingCoeff, natTrailingDegree_reverse, coeff_zero_reverse]

name: coeff_one_reverse
statement: theorem coeff_one_reverse (f : R[X]) : coeff (reverse f) 1 = nextCoeff f
proof:
rw [coeff_reverse, nextCoeff]
  split_ifs with hf
  · have : coeff f 1 = 0 := coeff_eq_zero_of_natDegree_lt (by simp only [hf, zero_lt_one])
    simp [*, revAt]
  · rw [revAt_le]
    exact Nat.succ_le_iff.2 (pos_iff_ne_zero.2 hf)

@[simp] lemma reverse_C (t : R) :
    reverse (C t) = C t := by
  simp [reverse]

@[simp] lemma reverse_mul_X (p : R[X]) : reverse (p * X) = reverse p := by
  nontriviality R
  rcases eq_or_ne p 0 with rfl | hp
  · simp
  · simp [reverse, hp]

@[simp] lemma reverse_X_mul (p : R[X]) : reverse (X * p) = reverse p := by
  rw [commute_X p, reverse_mul_X]

@[simp] lemma reverse_mul_X_pow (p : R[X]) (n : ℕ) : reverse (p * X ^ n) = reverse p := by
  induction n with
  | zero => simp
  | succ n ih => rw [pow_succ, ← mul_assoc, reverse_mul_X, ih]

@[simp] lemma reverse_X_pow_mul (p : R[X]) (n : ℕ) : reverse (X ^ n * p) = reverse p := by
  rw [commute_X_pow p, reverse_mul_X_pow]

@[simp] lemma reverse_add_C (p : R[X]) (t : R) :
    reverse (p + C t) = reverse p + C t * X ^ p.natDegree := by
  simp [reverse]

@[simp] lemma reverse_C_add (p : R[X]) (t : R) :
    reverse (C t + p) = C t * X ^ p.natDegree + reverse p := by
  rw [add_comm, reverse_add_C, add_comm]

section Eval₂

variable {S : Type*} [CommSemiring S]

name: reverse_neg
statement: theorem reverse_neg (f : R[X]) : reverse (-f) = -reverse f
proof:
rw [reverse, reverse, reflect_neg, natDegree_neg]

end Ring

end Polynomial

name: derivative_C_mul_X
statement: theorem derivative_C_mul_X (a : R) : derivative (C a * X) = C a
proof: simp [C_mul_X_eq_monomial, mul_one]

name: derivative_C_mul_X_sq
statement: theorem derivative_C_mul_X_sq (a : R) : derivative (C a * X ^ 2) = C (a * 2) * X
proof: rw [derivative_C_mul_X_pow, Nat.cast_two, pow_one]

name: derivative_X_pow
statement: theorem derivative_X_pow (n : ℕ) : derivative (X ^ n : R[X]) = C (n : R) * X ^ (n - 1)
proof:
convert derivative_C_mul_X_pow (1 : R) n <;> simp

@[simp]

name: derivative_X_pow_succ
statement: theorem derivative_X_pow_succ (n : ℕ) : derivative (X ^ (n + 1) : R[X]) = C (n + 1 : R) * X ^ n
proof: simp [derivative_X_pow]

name: derivative_X_sq
statement: theorem derivative_X_sq  : derivative (X ^ 2 : R[X]) = C 2 * X
proof:
rw [derivative_X_pow, Nat.cast_two, pow_one]

@[simp]

name: derivative_X_add_C
statement: theorem derivative_X_add_C (c : R) : derivative (X + C c) = 1
proof: rw [derivative_add, derivative_X, derivative_C, add_zero]

name: natDegree_derivative_le
statement: theorem natDegree_derivative_le (p : R[X]) : p.derivative.natDegree ≤ p.natDegree - 1
proof:
by_cases p0 : p.natDegree = 0
  · simp [p0, derivative_of_natDegree_zero]
  · exact Nat.le_sub_one_of_lt (natDegree_derivative_lt p0)

name: derivativeFinsupp_C
statement: theorem derivativeFinsupp_C (r : R) : derivativeFinsupp (C r : R[X]) = .single 0 (C r)
proof:
ext i : 1
  match i with
  | 0 => simp
  | i + 1 => simp

@[simp]

name: derivativeFinsupp_one
statement: theorem derivativeFinsupp_one  : derivativeFinsupp (1 : R[X]) = .single 0 1
proof:
simpa using derivativeFinsupp_C (1 : R)

@[simp]

name: derivativeFinsupp_X
statement: theorem derivativeFinsupp_X  : derivativeFinsupp (X : R[X]) = .single 0 X + .single 1 1
proof:
ext i : 1
  match i with
  | 0 => simp
  | 1 => simp
  | (n + 2) => simp

name: derivative_sq
statement: theorem derivative_sq (p : R[X]) : derivative (p ^ 2) = C 2 * p * derivative p
proof: rw [derivative_pow_succ, Nat.cast_one, one_add_one_eq_two, pow_one]

name: iterate_derivative_X_pow_eq_natCast_mul
statement: theorem iterate_derivative_X_pow_eq_natCast_mul (n k : ℕ) : derivative^[k] (X ^ n : R[X]) = ↑(Nat.descFactorial n k : R[X]) * X ^ (n - k)
proof:
induction k with
  | zero =>
    rw [Function.iterate_zero_apply, tsub_zero, Nat.descFactorial_zero, Nat.cast_one, one_mul]
  | succ k ih =>
    rw [Function.iterate_succ_apply', ih, derivative_natCast_mul, derivative_X_pow, C_eq_natCast,
      Nat.descFactorial_succ, Nat.sub_sub, Nat.cast_mul]
    simp [mul_assoc, mul_left_comm]

name: iterate_derivative_X_pow_eq_C_mul
statement: theorem iterate_derivative_X_pow_eq_C_mul (n k : ℕ) : derivative^[k] (X ^ n : R[X]) = C (Nat.descFactorial n k : R) * X ^ (n - k)
proof: rw [iterate_derivative_X_pow_eq_natCast_mul n k, C_eq_natCast]

name: derivative_X_add_C_sq
statement: theorem derivative_X_add_C_sq (c : R) : derivative ((X + C c) ^ 2) = C 2 * (X + C c)
proof: rw [derivative_sq, derivative_X_add_C, mul_one]

name: derivative_comp
statement: theorem derivative_comp (p q : R[X]) : derivative (p.comp q) = derivative q * p.derivative.comp q
proof:
induction p using Polynomial.induction_on'
  · simp [*, mul_add]
  · simp only [derivative_pow, derivative_mul, monomial_comp, derivative_monomial, derivative_C,
      zero_mul, C_eq_natCast, zero_add, map_mul]
    ring

/-- Chain rule for formal derivative of polynomials. -/

name: derivative_X_sub_C
statement: theorem derivative_X_sub_C (c : R) : derivative (X - C c) = 1
proof: rw [derivative_sub, derivative_X, derivative_C, sub_zero]

name: derivative_X_sub_C_sq
statement: theorem derivative_X_sub_C_sq (c : R) : derivative ((X - C c) ^ 2) = C 2 * (X - C c)
proof: rw [derivative_sq, derivative_X_sub_C, mul_one]

name: mapAlg_comp
statement: theorem mapAlg_comp (p : A[X]) : (mapAlg A C) p = (mapAlg B C) (mapAlg A B p)
proof: simp [mapAlg_eq_map, map_map, IsScalarTower.algebraMap_eq A B C]

name: coeff_zero_of_isScalarTower
statement: theorem coeff_zero_of_isScalarTower (p : A[X]) : (algebraMap B C) ((algebraMap A B) (p.coeff 0)) = (mapAlg A C p).coeff 0
proof:
rw [mapAlg_eq_map, coeff_map, IsScalarTower.algebraMap_eq A B C, RingHom.comp_apply]

end IsScalarTower

/-- Two polynomials `p` and `q` such that `p(q(X))=X` and `q(p(X))=X`
  induces an automorphism of the polynomial algebra. -/
@[simps!]

name: coeff_zero_eq_aeval_zero
statement: theorem coeff_zero_eq_aeval_zero (p : R[X]) : p.coeff 0 = aeval 0 p
proof: simp [coeff_zero_eq_eval_zero]

name: aevalTower_id
statement: theorem aevalTower_id  : aevalTower (AlgHom.id S S) = aeval
proof:
ext s
  simp only [eval_X, aevalTower_X, coe_aeval_eq_eval]

@[simp]

name: aevalTower_ofId
statement: theorem aevalTower_ofId  : aevalTower (Algebra.ofId S A') = aeval
proof:
ext
  simp only [aeval_X, aevalTower_X]

end aevalTower

open LinearMap TensorProduct in

name: dvd_comp_neg_X_iff
statement: theorem dvd_comp_neg_X_iff (p q : R[X]) : p ∣ q.comp (-X) ↔ p.comp (-X) ∣ q
proof:
let _ := invertibleOne (α := R)
  let _ := invertibleNeg (R := R) 1
  simpa using dvd_comp_C_mul_X_add_C_iff p q (-1) 0

variable [IsDomain R]

name: card_support_mul_le
statement: theorem card_support_mul_le  : #(p * q).support ≤ #p.support * #q.support
proof:
calc #(p * q).support
    _ = #(p.toFinsupp * q.toFinsupp).support := by rw [← support_toFinsupp, toFinsupp_mul]
    _ ≤ #(p.toFinsupp.support + q.toFinsupp.support) :=
      Finset.card_le_card (AddMonoidAlgebra.support_mul p.toFinsupp q.toFinsupp)
    _ ≤ #p.support * #q.support := Finset.card_image₂_le ..

/-- `Polynomial.sum` as a linear map. -/
@[simps]

name: mul_coeff_one
statement: theorem mul_coeff_one (p q : R[X]) : coeff (p * q) 1 = coeff p 0 * coeff q 1 + coeff p 1 * coeff q 0
proof:
rw [coeff_mul, Nat.antidiagonal_eq_map]
  simp [sum_range_succ]

/-- `constantCoeff p` returns the constant term of the polynomial `p`,
  defined as `coeff p 0`. This is a ring homomorphism. -/
@[simps]

name: coeff_C_mul
statement: theorem coeff_C_mul (p : R[X]) : coeff (C a * p) n = a * coeff p n
proof:
rcases p with ⟨p⟩
  simp_rw [← monomial_zero_left, ← ofFinsupp_single, ← ofFinsupp_mul, coeff]
  exact AddMonoidAlgebra.single_zero_mul_apply p a n

name: coeff_X_pow
statement: theorem coeff_X_pow (k n : ℕ) : coeff (X ^ k : R[X]) n = if n = k then 1 else 0
proof: simp only [one_mul, map_one, ← coeff_C_mul_X_pow]

name: isRegular_X_pow
statement: theorem isRegular_X_pow (n : ℕ) : IsRegular (X ^ n : R[X])
proof:
suffices IsLeftRegular (X ^ n : R[X]) from
    ⟨this, this.right_of_commute (fun p => commute_X_pow p n)⟩
  intro P Q (hPQ : X ^ n * P = X ^ n * Q)
  ext i
  rw [← coeff_X_pow_mul P n i, hPQ, coeff_X_pow_mul Q n i]

@[simp] theorem isRegular_X : IsRegular (X : R[X]) := pow_one (X : R[X]) ▸ isRegular_X_pow 1

name: card_roots
statement: theorem card_roots (hp0 : p ≠ 0) : (Multiset.card (roots p) : WithBot ℕ) ≤ degree p
proof:
classical
  unfold roots
  rw [dif_neg hp0]
  exact (Classical.choose_spec (exists_multiset_roots hp0)).1

name: roots_eq_zero_iff_isRoot_eq_bot
statement: theorem roots_eq_zero_iff_isRoot_eq_bot (hp0 : p ≠ 0) : p.roots = 0 ↔ p.IsRoot = ⊥
proof:
refine ⟨fun h ↦ ?_, fun h ↦ eq_zero_of_forall_notMem fun x hx ↦ h ▸ mem_roots hp0 |>.mp hx⟩
  ext a
  simp only [Pi.bot_apply, Prop.bot_eq_false, mem_roots hp0 |>.not.mp <| by simp [h]]

name: roots_eq_zero_iff_eq_zero_or_isRoot_eq_bot
statement: theorem roots_eq_zero_iff_eq_zero_or_isRoot_eq_bot  : p.roots = 0 ↔ p = 0 ∨ p.IsRoot = ⊥
proof:
rcases eq_or_ne p 0 with rfl | hp0; · simp
  simp [roots_eq_zero_iff_isRoot_eq_bot hp0, hp0]

name: roots_X_sub_C
statement: theorem roots_X_sub_C (r : R) : roots (X - C r) = {r}
proof:
classical
  ext s
  rw [count_roots, rootMultiplicity_X_sub_C, count_singleton]

@[simp]

name: roots_C
statement: theorem roots_C (x : R) : (C x).roots = 0
proof:
classical exact
  if H : x = 0 then by rw [H, C_0, roots_zero]
  else
    Multiset.ext.mpr fun r => (by
      rw [count_roots, count_zero, rootMultiplicity_eq_zero (not_isRoot_C _ _ H)])

@[simp]

name: roots_neg
statement: theorem roots_neg (p : R[X]) : (-p).roots = p.roots
proof:
rw [← neg_one_smul R p, roots_smul_nonzero p (neg_ne_zero.mpr one_ne_zero)]

@[simp]

name: roots_multiset_prod
statement: theorem roots_multiset_prod (m : Multiset R[X]) : (0 : R[X]) ∉ m → m.prod.roots = m.bind roots
proof:
rcases m with ⟨L⟩
  simpa only [Multiset.prod_coe, quot_mk_to_coe''] using roots_list_prod L

name: roots_X_pow
statement: theorem roots_X_pow (n : ℕ) : (X ^ n : R[X]).roots = n • ({0} : Multiset R)
proof: rw [roots_pow, roots_X]

name: roots_prod_X_sub_C
statement: theorem roots_prod_X_sub_C (s : Finset R) : (s.prod fun a => X - C a).roots = s.val
proof:
apply (roots_prod (fun a => X - C a) s ?_).trans
  · simp_rw [roots_X_sub_C]
    rw [Multiset.bind_singleton, Multiset.map_id']
  · refine prod_ne_zero_iff.mpr (fun a _ => X_sub_C_ne_zero a)

@[simp]

name: roots_multiset_prod_X_sub_C
statement: theorem roots_multiset_prod_X_sub_C (s : Multiset R) : (s.map fun a => X - C a).prod.roots = s
proof:
rw [roots_multiset_prod, Multiset.bind_map]
  · simp_rw [roots_X_sub_C]
    rw [Multiset.bind_singleton, Multiset.map_id']
  · rw [Multiset.mem_map]
    rintro ⟨a, -, h⟩
    exact X_sub_C_ne_zero a h

name: roots_ofMultiset
statement: theorem roots_ofMultiset (s : Multiset R) : (ofMultiset s).roots = s
proof:
simp

variable (R) in

name: nthRoots_zero
statement: theorem nthRoots_zero (r : R) : nthRoots 0 r = 0
proof:
simp only [pow_zero, nthRoots, ← C_1, ← C_sub, roots_C]

@[simp]

name: nthRootsFinset_zero
statement: theorem nthRootsFinset_zero (a : R) : nthRootsFinset 0 a = ∅
proof: classical simp [nthRootsFinset_def]

name: one_mem_nthRootsFinset
statement: theorem one_mem_nthRootsFinset (hn : 0 < n) : 1 ∈ nthRootsFinset n (1 : R)
proof: rw [mem_nthRootsFinset hn, one_pow]

name: nthRoots_two_one
statement: theorem nthRoots_two_one  : Polynomial.nthRoots 2 (1 : R) = {-1,1}
proof:
have h₁ : (X ^ 2 - C 1 : R[X]) = (X + C 1) * (X - C 1) := by simp [← sq_sub_sq]
  have h₂ : (X ^ 2 - C 1 : R[X]) ≠ 0 := fun h ↦ by simpa using congrArg (coeff · 0) h
  rw [nthRoots, h₁, roots_mul (h₁ ▸ h₂), roots_X_add_C, roots_X_sub_C]; rfl

end NthRoots

name: preimage_eval_singleton
statement: theorem preimage_eval_singleton (hp : p ≠ C a) : p.eval ⁻¹' {a} = (p - C a).rootSet R
proof: ext; simp [mem_rootSet_of_ne (sub_ne_zero.mpr hp), sub_eq_zero]

name: prod_multiset_X_sub_C_dvd
statement: theorem prod_multiset_X_sub_C_dvd (p : R[X]) : (p.roots.map fun a => X - C a).prod ∣ p
proof:
classical
  rw [← map_dvd_map _ (IsFractionRing.injective R <| FractionRing R)
    (monic_multisetProd_X_sub_C p.roots)]
  rw [prod_multiset_root_eq_finset_root, Polynomial.map_prod]
  refine Finset.prod_dvd_of_coprime (fun a _ b _ h => ?_) fun a _ => ?_
  · simp_rw [Polynomial.map_pow, Polynomial.map_sub, map_C, map_X]
    exact (pairwise_coprime_X_sub_C (IsFractionRing.injective R <| FractionRing R) h).pow
  · exact Polynomial.map_dvd _ (pow_rootMultiplicity_dvd p a)

/-- A Galois connection. -/

name: card_roots_le_one_of_irreducible
statement: theorem card_roots_le_one_of_irreducible (hirr : Irreducible p) : p.roots.card ≤ 1
proof:
obtain hp | ⟨x, hx⟩ := p.roots.empty_or_exists_mem
  · simp [hp]
  convert p.card_roots'
  exact (natDegree_eq_of_degree_eq_some <| degree_eq_one_of_irreducible_of_root hirr <|
    isRoot_of_mem_roots hx).symm

name: zero_modByMonic
statement: theorem zero_modByMonic (p : R[X]) : 0 %ₘ p = 0
proof:
grind [modByMonic, divModByMonicAux]

@[simp]

name: zero_divByMonic
statement: theorem zero_divByMonic (p : R[X]) : 0 /ₘ p = 0
proof:
grind [divByMonic, divModByMonicAux]

@[simp]

name: degree_modByMonic_le_left
statement: theorem degree_modByMonic_le_left  : degree (p %ₘ q) ≤ degree p
proof:
nontriviality R
  by_cases hq : q.Monic
  · cases lt_or_ge (degree p) (degree q)
    · rw [(modByMonic_eq_self_iff hq).mpr ‹_›]
    · exact (degree_modByMonic_le p hq).trans ‹_›
  · rw [modByMonic_eq_of_not_monic p hq]

name: X_dvd_sub_C
statement: theorem X_dvd_sub_C  : X ∣ p - C (p.coeff 0)
proof: simp [X_dvd_iff, coeff_C]

name: self_mul_modByMonic
statement: theorem self_mul_modByMonic (hq : q.Monic) : (q * p) %ₘ q = 0
proof:
rw [modByMonic_eq_zero_iff_dvd hq]
  exact dvd_mul_right q p

name: divByMonic_one
statement: theorem divByMonic_one (p : R[X]) : p /ₘ 1 = p
proof: conv_rhs => rw [← modByMonic_add_div p 1]; simp

name: not_isField
statement: theorem not_isField  : ¬IsField R[X]
proof:
nontriviality R
  intro h
  letI := h.toField
  simpa using congr_arg natDegree (monic_X.eq_one_of_isUnit <| monic_X (R := R).ne_zero.isUnit)

section multiplicity

/-- An algorithm for deciding polynomial divisibility.
Prefer `Classical.dec`, as the algorithm relies on `%ₘ` and so is `noncomputable`.
-/
@[deprecated Classical.dec (since := "2026-02-07")]

name: rootMultiplicity_C
statement: theorem rootMultiplicity_C (r a : R) : rootMultiplicity a (C r) = 0
proof:
cases subsingleton_or_nontrivial R
  · rw [Subsingleton.elim (C r) 0, rootMultiplicity_zero]
  classical
  rw [rootMultiplicity_eq_multiplicity]
  split_ifs with hr
  · rfl
  have h : natDegree (C r) < natDegree (X - C a) := by simp
  simp_rw [multiplicity_eq_zero.mpr ((monic_X_sub_C a).not_dvd_of_natDegree_lt hr h)]

name: X_sub_C_dvd_sub_C_eval
statement: theorem X_sub_C_dvd_sub_C_eval  : X - C a ∣ p - C (p.eval a)
proof:
rw [dvd_iff_isRoot, IsRoot, eval_sub, eval_C, sub_self]

-- TODO: generalize this to Ring. In general, 0 can be replaced by any element in the center of R.

name: modByMonic_X
statement: theorem modByMonic_X (p : R[X]) : p %ₘ X = C (p.eval 0)
proof: rw [← modByMonic_X_sub_C_eq_C_eval, C_0, sub_zero]

name: mul_self_modByMonic
statement: theorem mul_self_modByMonic (hq : q.Monic) : (p * q) %ₘ q = 0
proof:
rw [modByMonic_eq_zero_iff_dvd hq]
  exact dvd_mul_left q p

name: add_modByMonic
statement: theorem add_modByMonic (p₁ p₂ : R[X]) : (p₁ + p₂) %ₘ q = p₁ %ₘ q + p₂ %ₘ q
proof:
by_cases hq : q.Monic
  · rcases subsingleton_or_nontrivial R with hR | hR
    · simp only [eq_iff_true_of_subsingleton]
    · exact
      (div_modByMonic_unique (p₁ /ₘ q + p₂ /ₘ q) _ hq
          ⟨by
            rw [mul_add, add_left_comm, add_assoc, modByMonic_add_div, ← add_assoc,
              add_comm (q * _), modByMonic_add_div],
            (degree_add_le _ _).trans_lt
              (max_lt (degree_modByMonic_lt _ hq) (degree_modByMonic_lt _ hq))⟩).2
  · simp_rw [modByMonic_eq_of_not_monic _ hq]

name: neg_modByMonic
statement: theorem neg_modByMonic (p q : R[X]) : (-p) %ₘ q = -(p %ₘ q)
proof: rw [eq_neg_iff_add_eq_zero, ← add_modByMonic, neg_add_cancel, zero_modByMonic]

name: sub_modByMonic
statement: theorem sub_modByMonic (p₁ p₂ q : R[X]) : (p₁ - p₂) %ₘ q = p₁ %ₘ q - p₂ %ₘ q
proof: simp [sub_eq_add_neg, add_modByMonic, neg_modByMonic]

name: mul_modByMonic
statement: theorem mul_modByMonic (p₁ p₂ q : R[X]) : (p₁ * p₂) %ₘ q = (p₁ %ₘ q) * (p₂ %ₘ q) %ₘ q
proof:
by_cases! h : ¬ q.Monic
  · simp [Polynomial.modByMonic_eq_of_not_monic _ h]
  apply Polynomial.modByMonic_eq_of_dvd_sub h
  have : p₁ * p₂ - p₁ %ₘ q * (p₂ %ₘ q) = (p₁ %ₘ q) * (p₂ - p₂ %ₘ q) + p₂ * (p₁ - p₁ %ₘ q) := by ring
  rw [this]
  apply dvd_add
  all_goals
  · apply dvd_mul_of_dvd_right
    simp [Polynomial.modByMonic_eq_sub_mul_div]

name: sumIDeriv_apply
statement: theorem sumIDeriv_apply (p : R[X]) : sumIDeriv p = ∑ i ∈ range (p.natDegree + 1), derivative^[i] p
proof:
dsimp [sumIDeriv]
  exact Finsupp.sum_of_support_subset _ (by simp) _ (by simp)

name: sumIDeriv_C
statement: theorem sumIDeriv_C (a : R) : sumIDeriv (C a) = C a
proof:
rw [sumIDeriv_apply, natDegree_C, zero_add, sum_range_one, Function.iterate_zero_apply]

@[simp]

name: sumIDeriv_X
statement: theorem sumIDeriv_X  : sumIDeriv X = X + C 1
proof:
rw [sumIDeriv_apply, natDegree_X, sum_range_succ, sum_range_one, Function.iterate_zero_apply,
    Function.iterate_one, derivative_X, eq_natCast, Nat.cast_one]

@[simp]

name: sumIDeriv_derivative
statement: theorem sumIDeriv_derivative (p : R[X]) : sumIDeriv (derivative p) = derivative (sumIDeriv p)
proof:
rw [sumIDeriv_apply_of_le ((natDegree_derivative_le p).trans tsub_le_self), sumIDeriv_apply,
    derivative_sum]
  simp_rw [← Function.iterate_succ_apply, Function.iterate_succ_apply']

name: sumIDeriv_eq_self_add
statement: theorem sumIDeriv_eq_self_add (p : R[X]) : sumIDeriv p = p + derivative (sumIDeriv p)
proof:
rw [sumIDeriv_apply, derivative_sum, sum_range_succ', sum_range_succ,
    add_comm, ← add_zero (Finset.sum _ _)]
  simp_rw [← Function.iterate_succ_apply' derivative, Nat.succ_eq_add_one,
    Function.iterate_zero_apply, iterate_derivative_eq_zero (Nat.lt_succ_self _)]

name: mirror_natDegree
statement: theorem mirror_natDegree  : p.mirror.natDegree = p.natDegree
proof:
by_cases hp : p = 0
  · rw [hp, mirror_zero]
  nontriviality R
  rw [mirror, natDegree_mul', reverse_natDegree, natDegree_X_pow,
    tsub_add_cancel_of_le p.natTrailingDegree_le_natDegree]
  rwa [leadingCoeff_X_pow, mul_one, reverse_leadingCoeff, Ne, trailingCoeff_eq_zero]

name: mirror_natTrailingDegree
statement: theorem mirror_natTrailingDegree  : p.mirror.natTrailingDegree = p.natTrailingDegree
proof:
by_cases hp : p = 0
  · rw [hp, mirror_zero]
  · rw [mirror, natTrailingDegree_mul_X_pow ((mt reverse_eq_zero.mp) hp),
      natTrailingDegree_reverse, zero_add]

name: coeff_mirror
statement: theorem coeff_mirror (n : ℕ) : p.mirror.coeff n = p.coeff (revAt (p.natDegree + p.natTrailingDegree) n)
proof:
by_cases h2 : p.natDegree < n
  · rw [coeff_eq_zero_of_natDegree_lt (by rwa [mirror_natDegree])]
    by_cases h1 : n ≤ p.natDegree + p.natTrailingDegree
    · rw [revAt_le h1, coeff_eq_zero_of_lt_natTrailingDegree]
      exact (tsub_lt_iff_left h1).mpr (Nat.add_lt_add_right h2 _)
    · rw [← revAtFun_eq, revAtFun, if_neg h1, coeff_eq_zero_of_natDegree_lt h2]
  rw [not_lt] at h2
  rw [revAt_le (h2.trans (Nat.le_add_right _ _))]
  by_cases h3 : p.natTrailingDegree ≤ n
  · rw [← tsub_add_eq_add_tsub h2, ← tsub_tsub_assoc h2 h3, mirror, coeff_mul_X_pow', if_pos h3,
      coeff_reverse, revAt_le (tsub_le_self.trans h2)]
  rw [not_le] at h3
  rw [coeff_eq_zero_of_natDegree_lt (lt_tsub_iff_right.mpr (Nat.add_lt_add_left h3 _))]
  exact coeff_eq_zero_of_lt_natTrailingDegree (by rwa [mirror_natTrailingDegree])

--TODO: Extract `Finset.sum_range_rev_at` lemma.

name: mirror_eval_one
statement: theorem mirror_eval_one  : p.mirror.eval 1 = p.eval 1
proof:
simp_rw [eval_eq_sum_range, one_pow, mul_one, mirror_natDegree]
  refine Finset.sum_bij_ne_zero ?_ ?_ ?_ ?_ ?_
  · exact fun n _ _ => revAt (p.natDegree + p.natTrailingDegree) n
  · intro n hn hp
    rw [Finset.mem_range_succ_iff] at *
    rw [revAt_le (hn.trans (Nat.le_add_right _ _))]
    rw [tsub_le_iff_tsub_le, add_comm, add_tsub_cancel_right, ← mirror_natTrailingDegree]
    exact natTrailingDegree_le_of_ne_zero hp
  · exact fun n₁ _ _ _ _ _ h => by rw [← @revAt_invol _ n₁, h, revAt_invol]
  · intro n hn hp
    use revAt (p.natDegree + p.natTrailingDegree) n
    refine ⟨?_, ?_, revAt_invol⟩
    · rw [Finset.mem_range_succ_iff] at *
      rw [revAt_le (hn.trans (Nat.le_add_right _ _))]
      rw [tsub_le_iff_tsub_le, add_comm, add_tsub_cancel_right]
      exact natTrailingDegree_le_of_ne_zero hp
    · change p.mirror.coeff _ ≠ 0
      rwa [coeff_mirror, revAt_invol]
  · exact fun n _ _ => p.coeff_mirror n

name: mirror_trailingCoeff
statement: theorem mirror_trailingCoeff  : p.mirror.trailingCoeff = p.leadingCoeff
proof:
rw [leadingCoeff, trailingCoeff, mirror_natTrailingDegree, coeff_mirror,
    revAt_le (Nat.le_add_left _ _), add_tsub_cancel_right]

@[simp]

name: mirror_leadingCoeff
statement: theorem mirror_leadingCoeff  : p.mirror.leadingCoeff = p.trailingCoeff
proof: rw [← p.mirror_mirror, mirror_trailingCoeff, p.mirror_mirror]

name: coeff_mul_mirror
statement: theorem coeff_mul_mirror  : (p * p.mirror).coeff (p.natDegree + p.natTrailingDegree) = p.sum fun _ => (· ^ 2)
proof:
rw [coeff_mul, Finset.Nat.sum_antidiagonal_eq_sum_range_succ_mk]
  refine
    (Finset.sum_congr rfl fun n hn => ?_).trans
      (p.sum_eq_of_subset (fun _ ↦ (· ^ 2)) (fun _ ↦ zero_pow two_ne_zero) fun n hn ↦
          Finset.mem_range_succ_iff.mpr
            ((le_natDegree_of_mem_supp n hn).trans (Nat.le_add_right _ _))).symm
  rw [coeff_mirror, ← revAt_le (Finset.mem_range_succ_iff.mp hn), revAt_invol, ← sq]

variable [NoZeroDivisors R]

name: natDegree_mul_mirror
statement: theorem natDegree_mul_mirror  : (p * p.mirror).natDegree = 2 * p.natDegree
proof:
by_cases hp : p = 0
  · rw [hp, zero_mul, natDegree_zero, mul_zero]
  rw [natDegree_mul hp (mt mirror_eq_zero.mp hp), mirror_natDegree, two_mul]

name: natTrailingDegree_mul_mirror
statement: theorem natTrailingDegree_mul_mirror  : (p * p.mirror).natTrailingDegree = 2 * p.natTrailingDegree
proof:
by_cases hp : p = 0
  · rw [hp, zero_mul, natTrailingDegree_zero, mul_zero]
  rw [natTrailingDegree_mul hp (mt mirror_eq_zero.mp hp), mirror_natTrailingDegree, two_mul]

name: mirror_mul_of_domain
statement: theorem mirror_mul_of_domain  : (p * q).mirror = p.mirror * q.mirror
proof:
by_cases hp : p = 0
  · rw [hp, zero_mul, mirror_zero, zero_mul]
  by_cases hq : q = 0
  · rw [hq, mul_zero, mirror_zero, mul_zero]
  rw [mirror, mirror, mirror, reverse_mul_of_domain, natTrailingDegree_mul hp hq, pow_add]
  rw [mul_assoc, ← mul_assoc q.reverse, ← X_pow_mul (p := reverse q)]
  repeat' rw [mul_assoc]

name: mirror_smul
statement: theorem mirror_smul (a : R) : (a • p).mirror = a • p.mirror
proof:
rw [← C_mul', ← C_mul', mirror_mul_of_domain, mirror_C]

end Semiring

section Ring

variable {R : Type*} [Ring R] (p q : R[X])

name: mirror_neg
statement: theorem mirror_neg  : (-p).mirror = -p.mirror
proof:
rw [mirror, mirror, reverse_neg, natTrailingDegree_neg, neg_mul_eq_neg_mul]

end Ring

section CommRing

variable {R : Type*} [CommRing R] [NoZeroDivisors R] {f : R[X]}

name: not_isUnit
statement: theorem not_isUnit (hp : p.IsUnitTrinomial) : ¬IsUnit p
proof:
obtain ⟨k, m, n, hkm, hmn, u, v, w, rfl⟩ := hp
  exact fun h =>
    ne_zero_of_lt hmn
      ((trinomial_natDegree hkm hmn w.ne_zero).symm.trans
        (natDegree_eq_of_degree_eq_some (degree_eq_zero_of_isUnit h)))

name: card_support_eq_three
statement: theorem card_support_eq_three (hp : p.IsUnitTrinomial) : #p.support = 3
proof:
obtain ⟨k, m, n, hkm, hmn, u, v, w, rfl⟩ := hp
  exact card_support_trinomial hkm hmn u.ne_zero v.ne_zero w.ne_zero

name: ne_zero
statement: theorem ne_zero (hp : p.IsUnitTrinomial) : p ≠ 0
proof:
rintro rfl
  simpa using hp.card_support_eq_three

name: isUnitTrinomial_iff
statement: theorem isUnitTrinomial_iff  : p.IsUnitTrinomial ↔ #p.support = 3 ∧ ∀ k ∈ p.support, IsUnit (p.coeff k)
proof:
refine ⟨fun hp => ⟨hp.card_support_eq_three, fun k => hp.coeff_isUnit⟩, fun hp => ?_⟩
  obtain ⟨k, m, n, hkm, hmn, x, y, z, hx, hy, hz, rfl⟩ := card_support_eq_three.mp hp.1
  rw [support_trinomial hkm hmn hx hy hz] at hp
  replace hx := hp.2 k (mem_insert_self k {m, n})
  replace hy := hp.2 m (mem_insert_of_mem (mem_insert_self m {n}))
  replace hz := hp.2 n (mem_insert_of_mem (mem_insert_of_mem (mem_singleton_self n)))
  simp_rw [coeff_add, coeff_C_mul, coeff_X_pow_self, mul_one, coeff_X_pow] at hx hy hz
  rw [if_neg hkm.ne, if_neg (hkm.trans hmn).ne] at hx
  rw [if_neg hkm.ne', if_neg hmn.ne] at hy
  rw [if_neg (hkm.trans hmn).ne', if_neg hmn.ne'] at hz
  simp_rw [mul_zero, zero_add, add_zero] at hx hy hz
  exact ⟨k, m, n, hkm, hmn, hx.unit, hy.unit, hz.unit, rfl⟩

name: splits_iff_exists_multiset
statement: theorem splits_iff_exists_multiset  : Splits f ↔ ∃ m : Multiset R, f = C f.leadingCoeff * (m.map (X - C ·)).prod
proof:
refine splits_iff_exists_multiset'.trans ⟨?_, ?_⟩ <;>
    rintro ⟨m, hm⟩ <;> exact ⟨m.map (- ·), by simpa⟩

name: T_add
statement: theorem T_add (m n : ℤ) : (T (m + n) : R[T;T⁻¹]) = T m * T n
proof: simp [T, single_mul_single]

name: leftInverse_trunc_toLaurent
statement: theorem leftInverse_trunc_toLaurent  : Function.LeftInverse (trunc : R[T;T⁻¹] → R[X]) Polynomial.toLaurent
proof:
refine fun f => f.induction_on' ?_ ?_
  · intro f g hf hg
    simp only [hf, hg, map_add]
  · intro n r
    simp only [Polynomial.toLaurent_C_mul_T, trunc_C_mul_T, Int.natCast_nonneg, Int.toNat_natCast,
      if_true]

@[simp]

name: exists_T_pow
statement: theorem exists_T_pow (f : R[T;T⁻¹]) : ∃ (n : ℕ) (f' : R[X]), toLaurent f' = f * T n
proof:
refine f.induction_on' ?_ fun n a => ?_ <;> clear f
  · rintro f g ⟨m, fn, hf⟩ ⟨n, gn, hg⟩
    refine ⟨m + n, fn * X ^ n + gn * X ^ m, ?_⟩
    simp only [hf, hg, add_mul, add_comm (n : ℤ), map_add, map_mul, Polynomial.toLaurent_X_pow,
      mul_T_assoc, Int.natCast_add]
  · rcases n with n | n
    · exact ⟨0, Polynomial.C a * X ^ n, by simp⟩
    · refine ⟨n + 1, Polynomial.C a, ?_⟩
      simp only [Int.negSucc_eq, Polynomial.toLaurent_C, Int.natCast_succ, mul_T_assoc,
        neg_add_cancel, T_zero, mul_one]

/-- This is a version of `exists_T_pow` stated as an induction principle. -/
@[elab_as_elim]

name: toLaurent_support
statement: theorem toLaurent_support (f : R[X]) : f.toLaurent.support = f.support.map Nat.castEmbedding
proof:
generalize hd : f.support = s
  revert f
  refine Finset.induction_on s ?_ ?_ <;> clear s
  · intro f hf
    rw [Finset.map_empty, Finsupp.support_eq_empty, toLaurent_eq_zero]
    exact Polynomial.support_eq_empty.mp hf
  · intro a s as hf f fs
    have : (erase a f).toLaurent.support = s.map Nat.castEmbedding := by
      refine hf (f.erase a) ?_
      simp only [fs, Finset.erase_eq_of_notMem as, Polynomial.support_erase,
        Finset.erase_insert_eq_erase]
    rw [← monomial_add_erase f a, Finset.map_insert, ← this, map_add, Polynomial.toLaurent_C_mul_T,
      support_add_eq, Finset.insert_eq]
    · congr
      exact support_C_mul_T_of_ne_zero (Polynomial.mem_support_iff.mp (by simp [fs])) _
    · rw [this]
      exact Disjoint.mono_left (support_C_mul_T _ _) (by simpa)

end Support

section Degrees

/-- The degree of a Laurent polynomial takes values in `WithBot ℤ`.
If `f : R[T;T⁻¹]` is a Laurent polynomial, then `f.degree` is the maximum of its support of `f`,
or `⊥`, if `f = 0`. -/

name: eval₂_toLaurent
statement: theorem eval₂_toLaurent (p : R[X]) : eval₂ f x (toLaurent p) = Polynomial.eval₂ f x p
proof:
unfold eval₂
  rw [← algebraMap_eq_toLaurent, IsLocalization.lift_eq, coe_eval₂RingHom]

name: eval₂_T_n
statement: theorem eval₂_T_n (n : ℕ) : eval₂ f x (T n) = x ^ n
proof: rw [← Polynomial.toLaurent_X_pow, eval₂_toLaurent, eval₂_X_pow]

name: eval₂_T_neg_n
statement: theorem eval₂_T_neg_n (n : ℕ) : eval₂ f x (T (-n)) = x⁻¹ ^ n
proof:
rw [← mk'_one_X_pow]
  unfold eval₂
  rw [IsLocalization.lift_mk'_spec, map_one, coe_eval₂RingHom, eval₂_X_pow, ← mul_pow,
    Units.mul_inv, one_pow]

@[simp]

name: eval₂_T
statement: theorem eval₂_T (n : ℤ) : eval₂ f x (T n) = (x ^ n).val
proof:
by_cases! hn : 0 ≤ n
  · lift n to ℕ using hn
    apply eval₂_T_n
  · obtain ⟨m, rfl⟩ := Int.exists_eq_neg_ofNat hn.le
    rw [eval₂_T_neg_n, zpow_neg, zpow_natCast, ← inv_pow, Units.val_pow_eq_pow_val]

@[simp]

name: eval₂_C
statement: theorem eval₂_C (r : R) : eval₂ f x (C r) = f r
proof: rw [← toLaurent_C, eval₂_toLaurent, Polynomial.eval₂_C]

name: toLaurent_reverse
statement: theorem toLaurent_reverse (p : R[X]) : toLaurent p.reverse = invert (toLaurent p) * (T p.natDegree)
proof:
nontriviality R
  induction p using Polynomial.recOnHorner with
  | M0 => simp
  | MC _ _ _ _ ih => simp [add_mul, ← ih]
  | MX _ hp => simpa [natDegree_mul_X hp]

end Inversion

section Smeval

section SMulWithZero

variable [Semiring R] [AddCommMonoid S] [SMulWithZero R S] [Monoid S] (f g : R[T;T⁻¹]) (x y : Sˣ)

/-- Evaluate a Laurent polynomial at a unit, using scalar multiplication. -/

name: smeval_zero
statement: theorem smeval_zero  : (0 : R[T;T⁻¹]).smeval x = (0 : S)
proof: simp only [smeval_eq_sum, Finsupp.sum_zero_index]

name: smeval_C
statement: theorem smeval_C (r : R) : (C r).smeval x = r • 1
proof:
rw [← single_eq_C, smeval_single x (0 : ℤ) r, zpow_zero, Units.val_one]

end SMulWithZero

section MulActionWithZero

variable [Semiring R] [AddCommMonoid S] [MulActionWithZero R S] [Monoid S] (f g : R[T;T⁻¹])
  (x y : Sˣ)

@[simp]

name: smeval_one
statement: theorem smeval_one  : (1 : R[T;T⁻¹]).smeval x = 1
proof:
rw [← T_zero, smeval_T_pow 0 x, zpow_zero, Units.val_eq_one]

end MulActionWithZero

section Module

variable [Semiring R] [AddCommMonoid S] [Module R S] [Monoid S] (f g : R[T;T⁻¹]) (x y : Sˣ)

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
@[simp]

name: smeval_add
statement: theorem smeval_add  : (f + g).smeval x = f.smeval x + g.smeval x
proof:
simp only [smeval_eq_sum]
  rw [Finsupp.sum_add_index (fun n _ => zero_smul R (x ^ n).val) (fun n _ r r' => add_smul r r' _)]

@[simp]

name: smeval_C_mul
statement: theorem smeval_C_mul (r : R) : (C r * f).smeval x = r • (f.smeval x)
proof:
induction f using LaurentPolynomial.induction_on' with
  | add p q hp hq =>
    rw [mul_add, smeval_add, smeval_add, smul_add, hp, hq]
  | C_mul_T n s =>
    rw [← mul_assoc, ← map_mul, smeval_C_mul_T_n, smeval_C_mul_T_n, mul_smul]

variable (R) in
/-- Evaluation as an `R`-linear map. -/
@[simps]

name: coeffList_zero
statement: theorem coeffList_zero  : (0 : R[X]).coeffList = []
proof:
simp [coeffList]

/-- Only the zero polynomial has no coefficients. -/
@[simp]

name: coeffList_eq_cons_leadingCoeff
statement: theorem coeffList_eq_cons_leadingCoeff (h : P ≠ 0) : ∃ ls, P.coeffList = P.leadingCoeff :: ls
proof:
simp [coeffList, List.range_succ, withBotSucc_degree_eq_natDegree_add_one h]

@[simp]

name: length_coeffList_eq_withBotSucc_degree
statement: theorem length_coeffList_eq_withBotSucc_degree (P : R[X]) : P.coeffList.length = P.degree.succ
proof:
simp [coeffList]

@[simp]

name: leadingCoeff_cons_eraseLead
statement: theorem leadingCoeff_cons_eraseLead (h : P.nextCoeff ≠ 0) : P.leadingCoeff :: P.eraseLead.coeffList = P.coeffList
proof:
have h₂ := ne_zero_of_natDegree_gt (natDegree_pos_of_nextCoeff_ne_zero h)
  have h₃ := mt nextCoeff_eq_zero_of_eraseLead_eq_zero h
  simpa [natDegree_eraseLead_add_one h, coeffList, withBotSucc_degree_eq_natDegree_add_one h₂,
    withBotSucc_degree_eq_natDegree_add_one h₃, List.range_succ] using
    (Polynomial.eraseLead_coeff_of_ne · ·.ne)

@[simp]

name: coeffList_neg
statement: theorem coeffList_neg  : (-P).coeffList = P.coeffList.map (-·)
proof:
by_cases hp : P = 0
  · rw [hp, coeffList_zero, neg_zero, coeffList_zero, List.map_nil]
  · simp [coeffList]

end Ring

section NoZeroDivisors

variable [Semiring R] [NoZeroDivisors R] (P : R[X])

name: coeff_divX
statement: theorem coeff_divX  : (divX p).coeff n = p.coeff (n + 1)
proof: rw [add_comm]; cases p; rfl

name: divX_one
statement: theorem divX_one  : divX (1 : R[X]) = 0
proof:
ext
  simpa only [coeff_divX, coeff_zero] using coeff_one

@[simp]

name: divX_C_mul
statement: theorem divX_C_mul  : divX (C a * p) = C a * divX p
proof:
ext
  simp

name: divX_X_pow
statement: theorem divX_X_pow  : divX (X ^ n : R[X]) = if (n = 0) then 0 else X ^ (n - 1)
proof:
cases n
  · simp
  · ext n
    simp [coeff_X_pow]

/-- `divX` as an additive homomorphism. -/
noncomputable

name: natDegree_divX_eq_natDegree_tsub_one
statement: theorem natDegree_divX_eq_natDegree_tsub_one  : p.divX.natDegree = p.natDegree - 1
proof:
apply map_natDegree_eq_sub (φ := divX_hom)
  · intro f
    simpa [divX_hom, divX_eq_zero_iff] using eq_C_of_natDegree_eq_zero
  · intro n c c0
    rw [← C_mul_X_pow_eq_monomial, divX_hom_toFun, divX_C_mul, divX_X_pow]
    split_ifs with n0
    · simp [n0]
    · exact natDegree_C_mul_X_pow (n - 1) c c0

name: divX_C_mul_X_pow
statement: theorem divX_C_mul_X_pow  : divX (C a * X ^ n) = if n = 0 then 0 else C a * X ^ (n - 1)
proof: simp only [divX_C_mul, divX_X_pow, mul_ite, mul_zero]

name: degree_divX_lt
statement: theorem degree_divX_lt (hp0 : p ≠ 0) : (divX p).degree < p.degree
proof:
haveI := Nontrivial.of_polynomial_ne hp0
  calc
    degree (divX p) < (divX p * X + C (p.coeff 0)).degree :=
      if h : degree p ≤ 0 then by
        have h' : C (p.coeff 0) ≠ 0 := by rwa [← eq_C_of_degree_le_zero h]
        rw [eq_C_of_degree_le_zero h, divX_C, degree_zero, zero_mul, zero_add]
        exact lt_of_le_of_ne bot_le (Ne.symm (mt degree_eq_bot.1 <| by simpa using h'))
      else by
        have hXp0 : divX p ≠ 0 := by
          simpa [divX_eq_zero_iff, -not_le, degree_le_zero_iff] using h
        have : leadingCoeff (divX p) * leadingCoeff X ≠ 0 := by simpa
        have : degree (C (p.coeff 0)) < degree (divX p * X) :=
          calc
            degree (C (p.coeff 0)) ≤ 0 := degree_C_le
            _ < 1 := by decide
            _ = degree (X : R[X]) := degree_X.symm
            _ ≤ degree (divX p * X) := by
              rw [← zero_add (degree X), degree_mul' this]
              exact add_le_add
                (by rw [zero_le_degree_iff, Ne, divX_eq_zero_iff]
                    exact fun h0 => h (h0.symm ▸ degree_C_le))
                    le_rfl
        rw [degree_add_eq_left_of_degree_lt this]; exact degree_lt_degree_mul_X hXp0
    _ = degree p := congr_arg _ (divX_mul_X_add _)

/-- An induction principle for polynomials, valued in Sort* instead of Prop. -/
@[elab_as_elim]
noncomputable def recOnHorner {M : R[X] → Sort*} (p : R[X]) (M0 : M 0)
    (MC : ∀ p a, coeff p 0 = 0 → a ≠ 0 → M p → M (p + C a))
    (MX : ∀ p, p ≠ 0 → M p → M (p * X)) : M p :=
  letI := Classical.decEq R
  if hp : p = 0 then hp ▸ M0
  else by
    have wf : degree (divX p) < degree p := degree_divX_lt hp
    rw [← divX_mul_X_add p] at *
    exact
      if hcp0 : coeff p 0 = 0 then by
        rw [hcp0, C_0, add_zero]
        exact
          MX _ (fun h : divX p = 0 => by simp [h, hcp0] at hp) (recOnHorner (divX p) M0 MC MX)
      else
        MC _ _ (coeff_mul_X_zero _) hcp0
          (if hpX0 : divX p = 0 then show M (divX p * X) by rw [hpX0, zero_mul]; exact M0
          else MX (divX p) hpX0 (recOnHorner _ M0 MC MX))
termination_by p.degree

/-- A property holds for all polynomials of positive `degree` with coefficients in a semiring `R`
if it holds for
* `a * X`, with `a ∈ R`,
* `p * X`, with `p ∈ R[X]`,
* `p + a`, with `a ∈ R`, `p ∈ R[X]`,

with appropriate restrictions on each term.

See `natDegree_ne_zero_induction_on` for a similar statement involving no explicit multiplication.
-/
@[elab_as_elim]

name: support_eq_empty
statement: theorem support_eq_empty  : p.support = ∅ ↔ p = 0
proof:
rcases p with ⟨⟩
  simp [support]

@[simp] lemma support_nonempty : p.support.Nonempty ↔ p ≠ 0 :=
  Finset.nonempty_iff_ne_empty.trans support_eq_empty.not

name: support_add
statement: theorem support_add  : (p + q).support ⊆ p.support ∪ q.support
proof:
simpa [support] using Finsupp.support_add

/-- `C a` is the constant polynomial `a`.
`C` is provided as a ring homomorphism.
-/

name: C_mul_monomial
statement: theorem C_mul_monomial  : C a * monomial n b = monomial n (a * b)
proof:
simp only [← monomial_zero_left, monomial_mul_monomial, zero_add]

@[simp, grind =]

name: monomial_mul_C
statement: theorem monomial_mul_C  : monomial n a * C b = monomial n (a * b)
proof:
simp only [← monomial_zero_left, monomial_mul_monomial, add_zero]

/-- `X` is the polynomial variable (aka indeterminate). -/

name: monomial_one_right_eq_X_pow
statement: theorem monomial_one_right_eq_X_pow (n : ℕ) : monomial n (1 : R) = X ^ n
proof:
induction n with
  | zero => simp
  | succ n ih => rw [pow_succ, ← ih, ← monomial_one_one_eq_X, monomial_mul_monomial, mul_one]

@[simp]

name: X_mul
statement: theorem X_mul  : X * p = p * X
proof:
rcases p with ⟨⟩
  simp only [X, ← ofFinsupp_single, ← ofFinsupp_mul, ofFinsupp.injEq]
  ext
  simp [AddMonoidAlgebra.mul_apply, add_comm]

name: coeff_injective
statement: theorem coeff_injective  : Injective (coeff : R[X] → ℕ → R)
proof:
rintro ⟨p⟩ ⟨q⟩
  simp only [coeff, DFunLike.coe_fn_eq, imp_self, ofFinsupp.injEq]

@[simp]

name: coeff_monomial
statement: theorem coeff_monomial  : coeff (monomial n a) m = if n = m then a else 0
proof:
simp [coeff, Finsupp.single_apply]

@[simp]

name: coeff_one_zero
statement: theorem coeff_one_zero  : coeff (1 : R[X]) 0 = 1
proof:
simp [coeff_one]

@[simp]

name: mem_support_iff
statement: theorem mem_support_iff  : n ∈ p.support ↔ p.coeff n ≠ 0
proof:
rcases p with ⟨⟩
  simp

name: coeff_C
statement: theorem coeff_C  : coeff (C a) n = ite (n = 0) a 0
proof:
convert coeff_monomial (a := a) (m := n) (n := 0) using 2
  simp [eq_comm]

@[simp]

name: coeff_natCast_ite
statement: theorem coeff_natCast_ite  : (Nat.cast m : R[X]).coeff n = ite (n = 0) m 0
proof:
simp only [← C_eq_natCast, coeff_C, Nat.cast_ite, Nat.cast_zero]

@[simp]

name: coeff_ofNat_succ
statement: theorem coeff_ofNat_succ (a n : ℕ) : coeff (ofNat(a) : R[X]) (n + 1) = 0
proof:
rw [← Nat.cast_ofNat]
  simp [-Nat.cast_ofNat]

name: toFinsupp_C_mul_X
statement: theorem toFinsupp_C_mul_X (a : R) : (C a * X).toFinsupp = .single 1 a
proof:
rw [C_mul_X_eq_monomial, toFinsupp_monomial]

@[grind inj]

name: forall_eq_iff_forall_eq
statement: theorem forall_eq_iff_forall_eq  : (∀ f g : R[X], f = g) ↔ ∀ a b : R, a = b
proof: simpa only [← subsingleton_iff] using subsingleton_iff_subsingleton

name: addSubmonoid_closure_setOf_eq_monomial
statement: theorem addSubmonoid_closure_setOf_eq_monomial  : AddSubmonoid.closure { p : R[X] | ∃ n a, p = monomial n a } = ⊤
proof:
apply top_unique
  rw [← AddSubmonoid.map_equiv_top (toFinsuppIso R).symm.toAddEquiv, ←
    Finsupp.add_closure_setOf_eq_single, AddMonoidHom.map_mclosure]
  refine AddSubmonoid.closure_mono (Set.image_subset_iff.2 ?_)
  rintro _ ⟨n, a, rfl⟩
  exact ⟨n, a, Polynomial.ofFinsupp_single _ _⟩

name: toFinsupp_X_pow
statement: theorem toFinsupp_X_pow (n : ℕ) : (X ^ n).toFinsupp = .single n (1 : R)
proof: rw [X_pow_eq_monomial, toFinsupp_monomial]

name: mul_eq_sum_sum
statement: theorem mul_eq_sum_sum  : p * q = ∑ i ∈ p.support, q.sum fun j a => (monomial (i + j)) (p.coeff i * a)
proof:
apply toFinsupp_injective
  simp_rw [sum, coeff, toFinsupp_sum, support, toFinsupp_mul, toFinsupp_monomial,
    AddMonoidAlgebra.mul_def, Finsupp.sum]

@[simp]

name: sum_C_mul_X_pow_eq
statement: theorem sum_C_mul_X_pow_eq (p : R[X]) : (p.sum fun n a => C a * X ^ n) = p
proof:
simp_rw [C_mul_X_pow_eq_monomial, sum_monomial_eq]

@[elab_as_elim]
protected theorem induction_on {motive : R[X] → Prop} (p : R[X]) (C : ∀ a, motive (C a))
    (add : ∀ p q, motive p → motive q → motive (p + q))
    (monomial : ∀ (n : ℕ) (a : R),
      motive (Polynomial.C a * X ^ n) → motive (Polynomial.C a * X ^ (n + 1))) : motive p := by
  have A : ∀ {n : ℕ} {a}, motive (Polynomial.C a * X ^ n) := by
    intro n a
    induction n with
    | zero => rw [pow_zero, mul_one]; exact C a
    | succ n ih => exact monomial _ _ ih
  have B : ∀ s : Finset ℕ, motive (s.sum fun n : ℕ => Polynomial.C (p.coeff n) * X ^ n) := by
    apply Finset.induction
    · convert C 0
      exact C_0.symm
    · intro n s ns ih
      rw [sum_insert ns]
      exact add _ _ A ih
  rw [← sum_C_mul_X_pow_eq p, Polynomial.sum]
  exact B (support p)

/-- To prove something about polynomials,
it suffices to show the condition is closed under taking sums,
and it holds for monomials.
-/
@[elab_as_elim]
protected theorem induction_on' {motive : R[X] → Prop} (p : R[X])
    (add : ∀ p q, motive p → motive q → motive (p + q))
    (monomial : ∀ (n : ℕ) (a : R), motive (monomial n a)) : motive p :=
  Polynomial.induction_on p (monomial 0) add fun n a _h =>
    by rw [C_mul_X_pow_eq_monomial]; exact monomial _ _

/-- `erase p n` is the polynomial `p` in which the `X^n` term has been erased. -/
irreducible_def erase (n : ℕ) : R[X] → R[X]
  | ⟨p⟩ => ⟨p.erase n⟩

@[simp]

name: coeffs_one
statement: theorem coeffs_one  : coeffs (1 : R[X]) ⊆ {1}
proof:
classical
  simp_rw [coeffs, Finset.image_subset_iff]
  simp_all [coeff_one]

name: monomial_sub
statement: theorem monomial_sub (n : ℕ) : monomial n (a - b) = monomial n a - monomial n b
proof:
rw [sub_eq_add_neg, map_add, monomial_neg, sub_eq_add_neg]

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
@[simp]

name: signVariations_zero
statement: theorem signVariations_zero  : signVariations (0 : R[X]) = 0
proof:
simp [signVariations]

/-- Sign variations of a monomial are always zero. -/
@[simp]

name: signVariations_eraseLead
statement: theorem signVariations_eraseLead (h : SignType.sign P.leadingCoeff = SignType.sign P.nextCoeff) : signVariations P.eraseLead = signVariations P
proof:
by_cases hpz : P = 0
  · simp_all
  · have h₂ : nextCoeff P ≠ 0 := by intro; simp_all
    obtain ⟨_, hl⟩ := coeffList_eq_cons_leadingCoeff (mt nextCoeff_eq_zero_of_eraseLead_eq_zero h₂)
    simp [signVariations, List.destutter, leadingCoeff_eraseLead_eq_nextCoeff h₂, hl, h, h₂,
      coeffList_eraseLead hpz]

/-- If we drop the leading coefficient, the sign changes drop by 0 or 1 depending on whether
the first two nonzero coefficients match. -/

name: signVariations_eraseLead_le
statement: theorem signVariations_eraseLead_le  : signVariations P.eraseLead ≤ signVariations P
proof:
by_cases hpz : P = 0
  · simp [hpz]
  · grind [signVariations_eq_eraseLead_add_ite]

/-- We can only lose at most one sign changes if we drop the leading coefficient. -/

name: signVariations_le_eraseLead_succ
statement: theorem signVariations_le_eraseLead_succ  : signVariations P ≤ signVariations P.eraseLead + 1
proof:
by_cases hpz : P = 0
  · simp [hpz]
  · grind [signVariations_eq_eraseLead_add_ite]

end Semiring

section OrderedRing

variable {R : Type*} [Ring R] [LinearOrder R] [IsOrderedRing R] (P : Polynomial R) {x : R}

/-- The number of sign changes does not change if we negate. -/
@[simp]

name: signVariations_neg
statement: theorem signVariations_neg  : signVariations (-P) = signVariations P
proof:
rw [signVariations, signVariations, coeffList_neg]
  simp only [List.map_map, List.filter_map]
  have hsc : SignType.sign ∘ (fun (x : R) => -x) = (fun x => -x) ∘ SignType.sign := by
    grind [Left.sign_neg]
  have h_neg_destutter (l : List SignType) :
      (l.destutter (¬· = ·)).map (- ·) = (l.map (- ·)).destutter (¬· = ·) := by
    grind [List.map_destutter, neg_inj]
  rw [hsc, List.comp_map, ← h_neg_destutter, List.length_map]
  congr 5
  funext
  simp [SignType.sign]

end OrderedRing

section StrictOrderedRing

variable {R : Type*} [Ring R] [LinearOrder R] [IsStrictOrderedRing R] {P : Polynomial R} {η : R}

/-- The number of sign changes does not change if we multiply by any nonzero scalar. -/
@[simp]

name: roots_countP_pos_le_signVariations
statement: theorem roots_countP_pos_le_signVariations  : P.roots.countP (0 < ·) ≤ signVariations P
proof:
generalize h : P.roots.countP (0 < ·) = num_pos_roots
  induction num_pos_roots generalizing P -- Induct on number of roots.
  · exact zero_le
  rename_i ih
  have hp : P ≠ 0 := by grind [roots_zero, Multiset.countP_zero]
  -- we can take a positive root, η, because the number of roots is positive
  obtain ⟨η, η_root, η_pos⟩ : ∃ x, x ∈ P.roots ∧ 0 < x := by grind [Multiset.countP_pos]
  -- (X - η) divides P(X), so write P(X) = (X - η) * Q(X)
  obtain ⟨Q, rfl⟩ := dvd_iff_isRoot.mpr (isRoot_of_mem_roots η_root)
  -- P has at least num_roots sign variations
  grw [ih Q, succ_signVariations_le_X_sub_C_mul η_pos]
  · exact right_ne_zero_of_mul hp
  · simp [← h, roots_mul (ne_zero_of_mem_roots η_root), η_pos, ← Nat.succ.injEq]

end CommStrictOrderedRing
end Polynomial

name: natDegree_list_sum_le
statement: theorem natDegree_list_sum_le (l : List S[X]) : natDegree l.sum ≤ (l.map natDegree).foldr max 0
proof:
apply List.sum_le_foldr_max natDegree
  · simp
  · exact natDegree_add_le

name: natDegree_sum_le
statement: theorem natDegree_sum_le (f : ι → S[X]) : natDegree (∑ i ∈ s, f i) ≤ s.fold max 0 (natDegree ∘ f)
proof: simpa using natDegree_multiset_sum_le (s.val.map f)

name: degree_list_sum_le
statement: theorem degree_list_sum_le (l : List S[X]) : degree l.sum ≤ (l.map natDegree).maximum
proof:
apply degree_list_sum_le_of_forall_degree_le
  intro p hp
  by_cases h : p = 0
  · subst h
    simp
  · rw [degree_eq_natDegree h]
    apply List.le_maximum_of_mem'
    rw [List.mem_map]
    use p
    simp [hp]

name: natDegree_prod_le
statement: theorem natDegree_prod_le  : (∏ i ∈ s, f i).natDegree ≤ ∑ i ∈ s, (f i).natDegree
proof:
simpa using natDegree_multiset_prod_le (s.1.map f)

/-- The degree of a product of polynomials is at most the sum of the degrees,
where the degree of the zero polynomial is ⊥.
-/

name: degree_prod_le
statement: theorem degree_prod_le  : (∏ i ∈ s, f i).degree ≤ ∑ i ∈ s, (f i).degree
proof:
simpa only [Multiset.map_map] using degree_multiset_prod_le (s.1.map f)

/-- The leading coefficient of a product of polynomials is equal to
the product of the leading coefficients, provided that this product is nonzero.

See `Polynomial.leadingCoeff_multiset_prod` (without the `'`) for a version for integral domains,
where this condition is automatically satisfied.
-/

name: natDegree_multiset_prod_of_monic
statement: theorem natDegree_multiset_prod_of_monic (h : ∀ f ∈ t, Monic f) : t.prod.natDegree = (t.map natDegree).sum
proof:
nontriviality R
  apply natDegree_multiset_prod'
  simp_all

name: coeff_zero_multiset_prod
statement: theorem coeff_zero_multiset_prod  : t.prod.coeff 0 = (t.map fun f => coeff f 0).prod
proof:
refine Multiset.induction_on t ?_ fun a t ht => ?_; · simp
  rw [Multiset.prod_cons, Multiset.map_cons, Multiset.prod_cons, Polynomial.mul_coeff_zero, ht]

name: coeff_zero_prod
statement: theorem coeff_zero_prod  : (∏ i ∈ s, f i).coeff 0 = ∏ i ∈ s, (f i).coeff 0
proof:
simpa using coeff_zero_multiset_prod (s.1.map f)

end CommSemiring

section CommRing

variable [CommRing R]

open Monic

-- Eventually this can be generalized with Vieta's formulas
-- plus the connection between roots and factorization.

name: multiset_prod_X_sub_C_nextCoeff
statement: theorem multiset_prod_X_sub_C_nextCoeff (t : Multiset R) : nextCoeff (t.map fun x => X - C x).prod = -t.sum
proof:
rw [nextCoeff_multiset_prod]
  · simp only [nextCoeff_X_sub_C]
    exact t.sum_hom (-AddMonoidHom.id R)
  · intros
    apply monic_X_sub_C

name: natDegree_multiset_prod_X_sub_C_eq_card
statement: theorem natDegree_multiset_prod_X_sub_C_eq_card (s : Multiset R) : (s.map (X - C ·)).prod.natDegree = Multiset.card s
proof:
rw [natDegree_multiset_prod_of_monic, Multiset.map_map]
  · simp
  · exact Multiset.forall_mem_map_iff.2 fun a _ => monic_X_sub_C a

@[simp] lemma natDegree_finsetProd_X_sub_C_eq_card {α} (s : Finset α) (f : α → R) :
    (∏ a ∈ s, (X - C (f a))).natDegree = s.card := by
  rw [Finset.prod, ← (X - C ·).comp_def f, ← Multiset.map_map,
    natDegree_multiset_prod_X_sub_C_eq_card, Multiset.card_map, Finset.card]

@[deprecated (since := "2026-04-08")]
alias natDegree_finset_prod_X_sub_C_eq_card := natDegree_finsetProd_X_sub_C_eq_card

end CommRing

section NoZeroDivisors

section Semiring

variable [Semiring R] [NoZeroDivisors R]

/-- The degree of a product of polynomials is equal to
the sum of the degrees, where the degree of the zero polynomial is ⊥.
`[Nontrivial R]` is needed, otherwise for `l = []` we have `⊥` in the LHS and `0` in the RHS.
-/

name: natDegree_prod
statement: theorem natDegree_prod (h : ∀ i ∈ s, f i ≠ 0) : (∏ i ∈ s, f i).natDegree = ∑ i ∈ s, (f i).natDegree
proof:
nontriviality R
  apply natDegree_prod'
  rw [prod_ne_zero_iff]
  intro x hx; simp [h x hx]

name: leadingCoeff_multiset_prod
statement: theorem leadingCoeff_multiset_prod  : t.prod.leadingCoeff = (t.map fun f => leadingCoeff f).prod
proof:
rw [← leadingCoeffHom_apply, MonoidHom.map_multiset_prod]
  simp only [leadingCoeffHom_apply]

/-- The leading coefficient of a product of polynomials is equal to
the product of the leading coefficients.

See `Polynomial.leadingCoeff_prod'` (with a `'`) for a version for commutative semirings,
where additionally, the product of the leading coefficients must be nonzero.
-/

name: leadingCoeff_prod
statement: theorem leadingCoeff_prod  : (∏ i ∈ s, f i).leadingCoeff = ∏ i ∈ s, (f i).leadingCoeff
proof:
simpa using leadingCoeff_multiset_prod (s.1.map f)

end CommSemiring

end NoZeroDivisors

end Polynomial

name: smeval_C
statement: theorem smeval_C  : (C r).smeval x = r • x ^ 0
proof:
simp only [smeval_eq_sum, smul_pow, zero_smul, sum_C_index]

@[simp]

name: smeval_monomial
statement: theorem smeval_monomial (n : ℕ) : (monomial n r).smeval x = r • x ^ n
proof: simp only [smeval_eq_sum, smul_pow, zero_smul, sum_monomial_index]

name: eval_eq_smeval
statement: theorem eval_eq_smeval  : p.eval r = p.smeval r
proof:
rw [eval_eq_sum, smeval_eq_sum]
  rfl

name: smeval_zero
statement: theorem smeval_zero  : (0 : R[X]).smeval x = 0
proof:
simp only [smeval_eq_sum, sum_zero_index]

@[simp]

name: smeval_one
statement: theorem smeval_one  : (1 : R[X]).smeval x = 1 • x ^ 0
proof:
rw [← C_1, smeval_C]
  simp only [one_smul]

@[simp]

name: smeval_X
statement: theorem smeval_X  : (X : R[X]).smeval x = x ^ 1
proof:
simp only [smeval_eq_sum, smul_pow, zero_smul, sum_X_index, one_smul]

@[simp]

name: smeval_add
statement: theorem smeval_add  : (p + q).smeval x = p.smeval x + q.smeval x
proof:
simp only [smeval_eq_sum]
  refine sum_add_index p q (smul_pow x) (fun _ ↦ ?_) (fun _ _ _ ↦ ?_)
  · rw [smul_pow, zero_smul]
  · rw [smul_pow, smul_pow, smul_pow, add_smul]

name: smeval_natCast
statement: theorem smeval_natCast (n : ℕ) : (n : R[X]).smeval x = n • x ^ 0
proof:
induction n with
  | zero => simp only [smeval_zero, Nat.cast_zero, zero_smul]
  | succ n ih => rw [n.cast_succ, smeval_add, ih, smeval_one, ← add_nsmul]

@[simp]

name: smeval_smul
statement: theorem smeval_smul (r : R) : (r • p).smeval x = r • p.smeval x
proof:
induction p using Polynomial.induction_on' with
  | add p q ph qh => rw [smul_add, smeval_add, ph, qh, ← smul_add, smeval_add]
  | monomial n a => rw [smul_monomial, smeval_monomial, smeval_monomial, smul_assoc]

/-- `Polynomial.smeval` as a linear map. -/

name: smeval_neg
statement: theorem smeval_neg  : (-p).smeval x = -p.smeval x
proof:
rw [← add_eq_zero_iff_eq_neg, ← smeval_add, neg_add_cancel, smeval_zero]

@[simp]

name: smeval_sub
statement: theorem smeval_sub  : (p - q).smeval x = p.smeval x - q.smeval x
proof: rw [sub_eq_add_neg, smeval_add, smeval_neg, sub_eq_add_neg]

name: smeval_C_mul
statement: theorem smeval_C_mul  : (C r * p).smeval x = r • p.smeval x
proof:
induction p using Polynomial.induction_on' with
  | add p q ph qh => simp only [mul_add, smeval_add, ph, qh, smul_add]
  | monomial n b => simp only [C_mul_monomial, smeval_monomial, mul_smul]

variable [NatPowAssoc S]

name: smeval_at_natCast
statement: theorem smeval_at_natCast (q : ℕ[X]) : ∀ (n : ℕ), q.smeval (n : S) = q.smeval n
proof:
induction q using Polynomial.induction_on' with
  | add p q ph qh =>
    intro n
    simp only [smeval_add, ph, qh, Nat.cast_add]
  | monomial n a =>
    intro n
    rw [smeval_monomial, smeval_monomial, nsmul_eq_mul, smul_eq_mul, Nat.cast_mul, Nat.cast_npow]

name: smeval_at_zero
statement: theorem smeval_at_zero  : p.smeval (0 : S) = (p.coeff 0) • (1 : S)
proof:
induction p using Polynomial.induction_on' with
  | add p q ph qh => simp_all only [smeval_add, coeff_add, add_smul]
  | monomial n a =>
    cases n with
    | zero => simp only [monomial_zero_left, smeval_C, npow_zero, coeff_C_zero]
    | succ n => rw [coeff_monomial_succ, smeval_monomial, npow_add, npow_one, mul_zero, zero_smul,
        smul_zero]

section
variable [SMulCommClass R S S]

name: smeval_X_mul
statement: theorem smeval_X_mul  : (X * p).smeval x = x * p.smeval x
proof:
induction p using Polynomial.induction_on' with
  | add p q ph qh => simp only [smeval_add, ph, qh, mul_add]
  | monomial n a =>
    rw [← monomial_one_one_eq_X, monomial_mul_monomial, smeval_monomial, one_mul, npow_add,
      npow_one, ← mul_smul_comm, smeval_monomial]

name: smeval_X_pow_assoc
statement: theorem smeval_X_pow_assoc (m n : ℕ) : x ^ m * x ^ n * p.smeval x = x ^ m * (x ^ n * p.smeval x)
proof:
induction p using Polynomial.induction_on' with
  | add p q ph qh => simp only [smeval_add, ph, qh, mul_add]
  | monomial n a => simp only [smeval_monomial, mul_smul_comm, npow_mul_assoc]

name: smeval_monomial_mul
statement: theorem smeval_monomial_mul (n : ℕ) : (monomial n r * p).smeval x = r • (x ^ n * p.smeval x)
proof:
induction p using Polynomial.induction_on' with
  | add r s hr hs =>
    simp only [smeval_add]
    rw [← C_mul_X_pow_eq_monomial, mul_assoc, smeval_C_mul, smeval_X_pow_mul, smeval_add]
  | monomial n a =>
    rw [smeval_monomial, monomial_mul_monomial, smeval_monomial, npow_add, mul_smul, mul_smul_comm]

end

variable [IsScalarTower R S S]

name: smeval_mul_X
statement: theorem smeval_mul_X  : (p * X).smeval x = p.smeval x * x
proof:
induction p using Polynomial.induction_on' with
  | add p q ph qh => simp only [add_mul, smeval_add, ph, qh]
  | monomial n a =>
    simp only [← monomial_one_one_eq_X, monomial_mul_monomial, smeval_monomial, mul_one,
      npow_add, smul_mul_assoc, npow_one]

name: smeval_assoc_X_pow
statement: theorem smeval_assoc_X_pow (m n : ℕ) : p.smeval x * x ^ m * x ^ n = p.smeval x * (x ^ m * x ^ n)
proof:
induction p using Polynomial.induction_on' with
  | add p q ph qh => simp only [smeval_add, ph, qh, add_mul]
  | monomial n a =>
    rw [smeval_monomial, smul_mul_assoc, smul_mul_assoc, npow_mul_assoc, ← smul_mul_assoc]

name: smeval_mul
statement: theorem smeval_mul  : (p * q).smeval x = p.smeval x * q.smeval x
proof:
induction p using Polynomial.induction_on' with
  | add r s hr hs => simp only [hr, hs, smeval_add, add_mul]
  | monomial n a =>
    simp only [smeval_monomial, smeval_monomial_mul, smul_mul_assoc]

name: smeval_comp
statement: theorem smeval_comp  : (p.comp q).smeval x = p.smeval (q.smeval x)
proof:
induction p using Polynomial.induction_on' with
  | add r s hr hs => simp [add_comp, hr, hs, smeval_add]
  | monomial n a => simp [smeval_monomial, smeval_C_mul, smeval_pow]

end NatPowAssoc

section Commute

variable (R : Type*) [Semiring R] (p q : R[X]) {S : Type*} [Semiring S]
  [Module R S] [IsScalarTower R S S] [SMulCommClass R S S] {x y : S}

name: smeval_commute_left
statement: theorem smeval_commute_left (hc : Commute x y) : Commute (p.smeval x) y
proof:
induction p using Polynomial.induction_on' with
  | add r s hr hs => exact (smeval_add R r s x) ▸ Commute.add_left hr hs
  | monomial n a => simpa [smeval_monomial] using Commute.smul_left (Commute.pow_left hc _) _

name: smeval_commute
statement: theorem smeval_commute (hc : Commute x y) : Commute (p.smeval x) (q.smeval y)
proof:
induction p using Polynomial.induction_on' with
  | add r s hr hs => exact (smeval_add R r s x) ▸ Commute.add_left hr hs
  | monomial n a =>
    simp only [smeval_monomial]
    refine Commute.smul_left ?_ a
    induction n with
    | zero => simp only [npow_zero, Commute.one_left]
    | succ n ih =>
      refine (commute_iff_eq (x ^ (n + 1)) (q.smeval y)).mpr ?_
      rw [commute_iff_eq (x ^ n) (q.smeval y)] at ih
      have hxq : x * q.smeval y = q.smeval y * x := by
        refine (commute_iff_eq x (q.smeval y)).mp ?_
        exact Commute.symm (smeval_commute_left R q (Commute.symm hc))
      rw [pow_succ, ← mul_assoc, ← ih, mul_assoc, hxq, mul_assoc]

end Commute

section Algebra

name: evalEval_zero
statement: theorem evalEval_zero (x y : R) : (0 : R[X][Y]).evalEval x y = 0
proof:
simp only [evalEval, eval_zero]

@[simp]

name: evalEval_one
statement: theorem evalEval_one (x y : R) : (1 : R[X][Y]).evalEval x y = 1
proof:
simp only [evalEval, eval_one]

@[simp]

name: evalEval_X
statement: theorem evalEval_X (x y : R) : X.evalEval x y = y
proof:
rw [evalEval, eval_X, eval_C]

@[simp]

name: evalEvalRingHom_eq
statement: theorem evalEvalRingHom_eq (x : R) : evalEvalRingHom x = eval₂RingHom (evalRingHom x)
proof: ext <;> simp

name: eval₂_evalRingHom
statement: theorem eval₂_evalRingHom (x : R) : eval₂ (evalRingHom x) = evalEval x
proof: ext1; rw [← coe_evalEvalRingHom, evalEvalRingHom_eq, coe_eval₂RingHom]

name: map_mapRingHom_eval_map
statement: theorem map_mapRingHom_eval_map  : (p.map <| mapRingHom f).eval (q.map f) = (p.eval q).map f
proof: rw [eval_map, ← coe_mapRingHom, eval₂_hom]

name: map_mapRingHom_eval_map_eval
statement: theorem map_mapRingHom_eval_map_eval (r : R) : ((p.map <| mapRingHom f).eval <| q.map f).eval (f r) = f ((p.eval q).eval r)
proof: rw [map_mapRingHom_eval_map, eval_map, eval₂_hom]

name: map_mapRingHom_evalEval
statement: theorem map_mapRingHom_evalEval (x y : R) : (p.map <| mapRingHom f).evalEval (f x) (f y) = f (p.evalEval x y)
proof:
rw [evalEval, ← map_mapRingHom_eval_map_eval, map_C]

end

variable [CommSemiring R] [CommSemiring S]

/-- Two equivalent ways to express the evaluation of a bivariate polynomial over `R`
at a point in the affine plane over an `R`-algebra `S`. -/

name: coe_aevalAeval_eq_evalEval
statement: theorem coe_aevalAeval_eq_evalEval (x y : A) : ⇑(aevalAeval x y) = evalEval x y
proof:
ext
  simp [aeval, aevalEquiv]

name: equivMvPolynomial_C_X
statement: theorem equivMvPolynomial_C_X  : equivMvPolynomial R (C X) = .X 0
proof:
simp [equivMvPolynomial]

@[simp]

name: equivMvPolynomial_X
statement: theorem equivMvPolynomial_X  : equivMvPolynomial R X = .X 1
proof:
simp [equivMvPolynomial]

@[simp]

name: equivMvPolynomial_symm_X_0
statement: theorem equivMvPolynomial_symm_X_0  : (equivMvPolynomial R).symm (.X 0) = C X
proof:
simp [equivMvPolynomial]

@[simp]

name: equivMvPolynomial_symm_X_1
statement: theorem equivMvPolynomial_symm_X_1  : (equivMvPolynomial R).symm (.X 1) = X
proof:
simp [equivMvPolynomial]

@[simp]

name: equivMvPolynomial_symm_C
statement: theorem equivMvPolynomial_symm_C (a : R) : (equivMvPolynomial R).symm (.C a) = C (C a)
proof: simp [equivMvPolynomial]

name: pderiv_one_equivMvPolynomial
statement: theorem pderiv_one_equivMvPolynomial (p : R[X][Y]) : (equivMvPolynomial R p).pderiv 1 = equivMvPolynomial R (derivative p)
proof:
induction p using Polynomial.induction_on' with
  | add p q _ _ => aesop
  | monomial n p =>
  induction p using Polynomial.induction_on' with
  | add p q _ _ => aesop
  | monomial m a =>
    simp_rw [← Polynomial.C_mul_X_pow_eq_monomial]
    simp [derivative_pow]

@[deprecated (since := "2025-12-09")]
alias Polynomial.Bivariate.pderiv_one_equivMvPolynomial := pderiv_one_equivMvPolynomial

end MvPolynomial

end Bivariate

end Polynomial

open Polynomial

namespace AdjoinRoot

variable {R : Type*} [CommRing R] {x y : R} {p : R[X][Y]} (h : p.evalEval x y = 0)

/-- If the evaluation (`evalEval`) of a bivariate polynomial `p : R[X][Y]` at a point (x,y)
is zero, then `Polynomial.evalEval x y` factors through `AdjoinRoot.evalEval`, a ring homomorphism
from `AdjoinRoot p` to `R`. -/
@[simps!] noncomputable def evalEval : AdjoinRoot p →+* R :=
  lift (evalRingHom x) y <| eval₂_evalRingHom x ▸ h

name: evalEval_mk
statement: theorem evalEval_mk (g : R[X][Y]) : evalEval h (mk p g) = g.evalEval x y
proof:
rw [evalEval, lift_mk, eval₂_evalRingHom]

end AdjoinRoot

name: homogenize_zero
statement: theorem homogenize_zero (n : ℕ) : homogenize (0 : R[X]) n = 0
proof:
simp [homogenize]

@[simp]

name: homogenize_one
statement: theorem homogenize_one (n : ℕ) : homogenize (1 : R[X]) n = .X 1 ^ n
proof: simpa using homogenize_C (1 : R) n

name: expand_monomial
statement: theorem expand_monomial (r : R) : expand R p (monomial q r) = monomial (q * p) r
proof: simp_rw [← smul_X_eq_monomial, map_smul, map_pow, expand_X, mul_comm, pow_mul]

name: map_frobenius_expand
statement: theorem map_frobenius_expand (f : R[X]) : map (frobenius R p) (expand R p f) = f ^ p
proof:
refine f.induction_on' (fun a b ha hb => ?_) fun n a => ?_
  · rw [map_add, Polynomial.map_add, ha, hb, add_pow_expChar]
  · rw [expand_monomial, map_monomial, ← C_mul_X_pow_eq_monomial, ← C_mul_X_pow_eq_monomial,
      mul_pow, ← C.map_pow, frobenius_def]
    ring

@[deprecated (since := "2025-12-27")]
alias expand_char := map_frobenius_expand

name: rootMultiplicity_expand_pow
statement: theorem rootMultiplicity_expand_pow  : (expand R (p ^ n) f).rootMultiplicity r = p ^ n * f.rootMultiplicity (r ^ p ^ n)
proof:
obtain rfl | h0 := eq_or_ne f 0; · simp
  obtain ⟨g, hg, ndvd⟩ := f.exists_eq_pow_rootMultiplicity_mul_and_not_dvd h0 (r ^ p ^ n)
  rw [dvd_iff_isRoot, ← eval_X (x := r), ← eval_pow, ← isRoot_comp, ← expand_eq_comp_X_pow] at ndvd
  conv_lhs => rw [hg, map_mul, map_pow, map_sub, expand_X, expand_C, map_pow, ← sub_pow_expChar_pow,
    ← pow_mul, mul_comm, rootMultiplicity_mul_X_sub_C_pow (expand_ne_zero (expChar_pow_pos R p n)
      |>.mpr <| right_ne_zero_of_mul <| hg ▸ h0), rootMultiplicity_eq_zero ndvd, zero_add]

name: rootMultiplicity_expand
statement: theorem rootMultiplicity_expand  : (expand R p f).rootMultiplicity r = p * f.rootMultiplicity (r ^ p)
proof:
rw [← pow_one p, rootMultiplicity_expand_pow]

end rootMultiplicity

section IsDomain

variable (R : Type u) [CommRing R] [IsDomain R]

name: eraseLead_support
statement: theorem eraseLead_support (f : R[X]) : f.eraseLead.support = f.support.erase f.natDegree
proof: simp only [eraseLead, support_erase]

name: eraseLead_coeff
statement: theorem eraseLead_coeff (i : ℕ) : f.eraseLead.coeff i = if i = f.natDegree then 0 else f.coeff i
proof:
simp only [eraseLead, coeff_erase]

@[simp]

name: eraseLead_add_C_mul_X_pow
statement: theorem eraseLead_add_C_mul_X_pow (f : R[X]) : f.eraseLead + C f.leadingCoeff * X ^ f.natDegree = f
proof:
rw [C_mul_X_pow_eq_monomial, eraseLead_add_monomial_natDegree_leadingCoeff]

@[simp]

name: eraseLead_ne_zero
statement: theorem eraseLead_ne_zero (f0 : 2 ≤ #f.support) : eraseLead f ≠ 0
proof:
rw [Ne, ← card_support_eq_zero, eraseLead_support]
  exact
    (zero_lt_one.trans_le <| (tsub_le_tsub_right f0 1).trans Finset.pred_card_le_card_erase).ne.symm

name: eraseLead_support_card_lt
statement: theorem eraseLead_support_card_lt (h : f ≠ 0) : #(eraseLead f).support < #f.support
proof:
rw [eraseLead_support]
  exact card_lt_card (erase_ssubset <| natDegree_mem_support_of_nonzero h)

name: card_support_eraseLead_add_one
statement: theorem card_support_eraseLead_add_one (h : f ≠ 0) : #f.eraseLead.support + 1 = #f.support
proof:
set c := #f.support with hc
  cases h₁ : c
  case zero =>
    by_contra
    exact h (card_support_eq_zero.mp h₁)
  case succ =>
    rw [eraseLead_support, card_erase_of_mem (natDegree_mem_support_of_nonzero h), ← hc, h₁]
    rfl

@[simp]

name: card_support_eraseLead
statement: theorem card_support_eraseLead  : #f.eraseLead.support = #f.support - 1
proof:
by_cases hf : f = 0
  · rw [hf, eraseLead_zero, support_zero, card_empty]
  · rw [← card_support_eraseLead_add_one hf, add_tsub_cancel_right]

name: card_support_le_one_of_eraseLead_eq_zero
statement: theorem card_support_le_one_of_eraseLead_eq_zero (h : f.eraseLead = 0) : #f.support ≤ 1
proof:
by_cases hpz : f = 0
  case pos => simp [hpz]
  case neg => exact le_of_eq (card_support_eq_one_of_eraseLead_eq_zero hpz h)

@[simp]

name: eraseLead_X_pow
statement: theorem eraseLead_X_pow (n : ℕ) : eraseLead (X ^ n : R[X]) = 0
proof:
rw [X_pow_eq_monomial, eraseLead_monomial]

@[simp]

name: eraseLead_C_mul_X
statement: theorem eraseLead_C_mul_X (r : R) : eraseLead (C r * X) = 0
proof: simpa using eraseLead_C_mul_X_pow _ 1

name: natDegree_pos_of_eraseLead_ne_zero
statement: theorem natDegree_pos_of_eraseLead_ne_zero (h : f.eraseLead ≠ 0) : 0 < f.natDegree
proof:
by_contra h₂
  rw [eq_C_of_natDegree_eq_zero (Nat.eq_zero_of_not_pos h₂)] at h
  simp at h

name: eraseLead_natDegree_lt_or_eraseLead_eq_zero
statement: theorem eraseLead_natDegree_lt_or_eraseLead_eq_zero (f : R[X]) : (eraseLead f).natDegree < f.natDegree ∨ f.eraseLead = 0
proof:
by_cases! h : #f.support ≤ 1
  · right
    rw [← C_mul_X_pow_eq_self h]
    simp
  · left
    apply eraseLead_natDegree_lt h

name: eraseLead_natDegree_le
statement: theorem eraseLead_natDegree_le (f : R[X]) : (eraseLead f).natDegree ≤ f.natDegree - 1
proof:
rcases f.eraseLead_natDegree_lt_or_eraseLead_eq_zero with (h | h)
  · exact Nat.le_sub_one_of_lt h
  · simp only [h, natDegree_zero, zero_le]

name: natDegree_eraseLead
statement: theorem natDegree_eraseLead (h : f.nextCoeff ≠ 0) : f.eraseLead.natDegree = f.natDegree - 1
proof:
have := natDegree_pos_of_nextCoeff_ne_zero h
  refine f.eraseLead_natDegree_le.antisymm <| le_natDegree_of_ne_zero ?_
  rwa [eraseLead_coeff_of_ne _ (tsub_lt_self _ _).ne, ← nextCoeff_of_natDegree_pos]
  all_goals positivity

name: natDegree_eraseLead_add_one
statement: theorem natDegree_eraseLead_add_one (h : f.nextCoeff ≠ 0) : f.eraseLead.natDegree + 1 = f.natDegree
proof:
rw [natDegree_eraseLead h, tsub_add_cancel_of_le]
  exact natDegree_pos_of_nextCoeff_ne_zero h

name: natDegree_eraseLead_le_of_nextCoeff_eq_zero
statement: theorem natDegree_eraseLead_le_of_nextCoeff_eq_zero (h : f.nextCoeff = 0) : f.eraseLead.natDegree ≤ f.natDegree - 2
proof:
refine natDegree_le_pred (n := f.natDegree - 1) (eraseLead_natDegree_le f) ?_
  rw [nextCoeff_eq_zero, natDegree_eq_zero] at h
  obtain ⟨a, rfl⟩ | ⟨hf, h⟩ := h
  · simp
  rw [eraseLead_coeff_of_ne _ (tsub_lt_self hf zero_lt_one).ne, ← nextCoeff_of_natDegree_pos hf]
  simp [nextCoeff_eq_zero, h, eq_zero_or_pos]

name: leadingCoeff_eraseLead_eq_nextCoeff
statement: theorem leadingCoeff_eraseLead_eq_nextCoeff (h : f.nextCoeff ≠ 0) : f.eraseLead.leadingCoeff = f.nextCoeff
proof:
have := natDegree_pos_of_nextCoeff_ne_zero h
  rw [leadingCoeff, nextCoeff, natDegree_eraseLead h, if_neg,
    eraseLead_coeff_of_ne _ (tsub_lt_self _ _).ne]
  all_goals positivity

name: nextCoeff_eq_zero_of_eraseLead_eq_zero
statement: theorem nextCoeff_eq_zero_of_eraseLead_eq_zero (h : f.eraseLead = 0) : f.nextCoeff = 0
proof:
by_contra h₂
  exact leadingCoeff_ne_zero.mp (leadingCoeff_eraseLead_eq_nextCoeff h₂ ▸ h₂) h

/-- If we erase the leading coefficient of a `Polynomial.coeffList` like [+,0,...], and then
multiply by a linear term, it's equivalent to erasing the first two coefficients of the product. -/

name: C_add
statement: theorem C_add (x y : R) : (.C (x + y) : QuadraticAlgebra R a b) = .C x + .C y
proof:
ext <;> simp

@[deprecated (since := "2025-12-15")] alias coe_add := C_add

end AddZeroClass

section Neg
variable [Neg R]

instance : Neg (QuadraticAlgebra R a b) where neg z := ⟨-z.re, -z.im⟩

@[simp] theorem re_neg (z : QuadraticAlgebra R a b) : (-z).re = -z.re := rfl

@[simp] theorem im_neg (z : QuadraticAlgebra R a b) : (-z).im = -z.im := rfl

@[simp]

name: C_ofNat
statement: theorem C_ofNat (n : ℕ) : (.C (ofNat(n) : R) : QuadraticAlgebra R a b) = ofNat(n)
proof:
ext <;> rfl

@[deprecated (since := "2025-12-15")] alias coe_ofNat := C_ofNat

@[simp, norm_cast]

name: C_mul
statement: theorem C_mul (x y : R) : .C (x * y) = (.C x * .C y : QuadraticAlgebra R a b)
proof:
ext <;> simp

@[deprecated (since := "2025-12-15")] alias coe_mul := C_mul

end NonUnitalNonAssocSemiring

section NonAssocSemiring
variable [NonAssocSemiring R]

instance instNonAssocSemiring : NonAssocSemiring (QuadraticAlgebra R a b) where
  one_mul _ := by ext <;> simp
  mul_one _ := by ext <;> simp

@[simp]

name: rank_eq_two
statement: theorem rank_eq_two  : Module.rank R (QuadraticAlgebra R a b) = 2
proof: simp [rank_eq_card_basis (basis a b)]

name: finrank_eq_two
statement: theorem finrank_eq_two  : Module.finrank R (QuadraticAlgebra R a b) = 2
proof:
simp [Module.finrank, rank_eq_two]

end Semiring

section CommSemiring
variable [CommSemiring R]

instance instCommSemiring : CommSemiring (QuadraticAlgebra R a b) where
  mul_assoc _ _ _ := by ext <;> simp <;> ring
  mul_comm _ _ := by ext <;> simp <;> ring

instance [CommSemiring S] [Algebra S R] : Algebra S (QuadraticAlgebra R a b) where
  algebraMap.toFun s := .C (algebraMap S R s)
  algebraMap.map_one' := by ext <;> simp
  algebraMap.map_mul' x y := by ext <;> simp
  algebraMap.map_zero' := by ext <;> simp
  algebraMap.map_add' x y := by ext <;> simp
