  commutes' s z := by ext <;> simp [Algebra.commutes]
  smul_def' s x := by ext <;> simp [Algebra.smul_def]

name: det_toLinearMap_eq_norm
statement: theorem det_toLinearMap_eq_norm (z : QuadraticAlgebra R a b) : (DistribSMul.toLinearMap R (QuadraticAlgebra R a b) z).det = z.norm
proof:
rw [← LinearMap.det_toMatrix <| basis ..]
  have : !![z.re, a * z.im; z.im, z.re + b * z.im].det = z.norm := by
    simp [norm]
    ring
  convert this
  apply LinearEquiv.eq_symm_apply _ |>.mp
  ext1 w
  apply basis .. |>.repr.injective
  apply DFunLike.coe_injective'
  rw [LinearMap.toMatrix_symm, Matrix.repr_toLin]
  ext i
  fin_cases i
    <;> simp
    <;> ring

end QuadraticAlgebra

name: omega_mul_omega_eq_mk
statement: theorem omega_mul_omega_eq_mk  : (ω : QuadraticAlgebra R a b) * ω = ⟨a, b⟩
proof: ext <;> simp

name: omega_mul_omega_eq_add
statement: theorem omega_mul_omega_eq_add  : (ω : QuadraticAlgebra R a b) * ω = a • 1 + b • ω
proof:
ext <;> simp

@[simp]

name: omega_mul_mk
statement: theorem omega_mul_mk (x y : R) : (ω : QuadraticAlgebra R a b) * ⟨x, y⟩ = ⟨a * y, x + b * y⟩
proof:
ext <;> simp

@[simp]

name: omega_mul_algebraMap_mul_mk
statement: theorem omega_mul_algebraMap_mul_mk (n x y : R) : (ω : QuadraticAlgebra R a b) * algebraMap _ _ n * ⟨x, y⟩ = ⟨a * n * y, n * x + n * b * y⟩
proof:
ext <;> simp; ring

@[deprecated (since := "2025-12-15")] alias omega_mul_coe_mul_mk := omega_mul_algebraMap_mul_mk

name: mk_eq_add_smul_omega
statement: theorem mk_eq_add_smul_omega (x y : R) : (⟨x, y⟩ : QuadraticAlgebra R a b) = algebraMap _ _ x + y • ω
proof:
ext <;> simp

variable {A : Type*} [Ring A] [Algebra R A]

@[ext]

name: norm_algebraMap
statement: theorem norm_algebraMap (r : R) : norm (algebraMap R (QuadraticAlgebra R a b) r) = r ^ 2
proof:
simp [norm_def, pow_two]

@[deprecated (since := "2025-12-15")] alias norm_coe := norm_algebraMap

@[simp]

name: norm_natCast
statement: theorem norm_natCast (n : ℕ) : norm (n : QuadraticAlgebra R a b) = n ^ 2
proof:
simp [norm_def, pow_two]

@[simp]

name: norm_intCast
statement: theorem norm_intCast (n : ℤ) : norm (n : QuadraticAlgebra R a b) = n ^ 2
proof: simp [norm_def, pow_two]

name: algebraMap_norm_eq_mul_star
statement: theorem algebraMap_norm_eq_mul_star (z : QuadraticAlgebra R a b) : (algebraMap R _ (norm z : R)) = z * star z
proof:
ext <;> simp [norm, star, mul_comm] <;> ring

@[deprecated (since := "2025-12-15")] alias coe_norm_eq_mul_star := algebraMap_norm_eq_mul_star

@[simp]

name: norm_neg
statement: theorem norm_neg (x : QuadraticAlgebra R a b) : (-x).norm = x.norm
proof:
simp [norm]

@[simp]

name: norm_star
statement: theorem norm_star (x : QuadraticAlgebra R a b) : (star x).norm = x.norm
proof:
simp only [norm, MonoidHom.coe_mk, OneHom.coe_mk, re_star, im_star, mul_neg, neg_mul, neg_neg,
    sub_left_inj]
  ring

name: associator_op
statement: theorem associator_op (x y z : Rᵐᵒᵖ) : associator x y z = -op (associator (unop z) (unop y) (unop x))
proof:
simp only [associator_apply, ← unop_mul, ← unop_sub, op_unop, neg_sub]

end NonUnitalNonAssocRing

section NonUnitalRing
variable [NonUnitalRing R]

@[simp]

name: sq_add_sq_mul_sq_add_sq
statement: theorem sq_add_sq_mul_sq_add_sq  : (x₁ ^ 2 + x₂ ^ 2) * (y₁ ^ 2 + y₂ ^ 2) = (x₁ * y₁ - x₂ * y₂) ^ 2 + (x₁ * y₂ + x₂ * y₁) ^ 2
proof:
ring

/-- Brahmagupta's identity, see <https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brahmagupta%27s_identity>
-/

name: pow_four_add_four_mul_pow_four
statement: theorem pow_four_add_four_mul_pow_four  : a ^ 4 + 4 * b ^ 4 = ((a - b) ^ 2 + b ^ 2) * ((a + b) ^ 2 + b ^ 2)
proof:
ring

/-- Sophie Germain's identity, see <https://www.cut-the-knot.org/blue/SophieGermainIdentity.shtml>.
-/

name: range_two_mul
statement: theorem range_two_mul (α) : Set.range (fun x : α ↦ 2 * x) = {a | Even a}
proof:
ext x
  simp [eq_comm, two_mul, Even]

name: isUnit_two_iff_forall_even
statement: theorem isUnit_two_iff_forall_even  : IsUnit (2 : α) ↔ ∀ a : α, Even a
proof:
refine ⟨Even.of_isUnit_two, fun h => ?_⟩
  obtain ⟨a, ha⟩ := h 1
  rw [← two_mul, eq_comm] at ha
  exact ⟨⟨2, a, ha, .trans (Commute.ofNat_right _ _).eq ha⟩, rfl⟩

end Semiring

section Ring
variable [Ring α]

name: odd_add_two
statement: theorem odd_add_two  : Odd (a + 2) ↔ Odd a
proof:
rw [← one_add_one_eq_two, ← add_assoc, odd_add_one, even_add_one]

@[simp]

name: odd_sub_two
statement: theorem odd_sub_two  : Odd (a - 2) ↔ Odd a
proof:
rw [← odd_add_two (a := a - 2), add_comm_sub, sub_self, add_zero]

end Ring

namespace Nat
variable {m n : ℕ}

@[grind =]

name: even_div
statement: theorem even_div  : Even (m / n) ↔ m % (2 * n) / n = 0
proof:
rw [even_iff_two_dvd, dvd_iff_mod_eq_zero, ← Nat.mod_mul_right_div_self, mul_comm]

@[parity_simps] lemma odd_add : Odd (m + n) ↔ (Odd m ↔ Even n) := by grind

name: two_dvd_mul_sub_one
statement: theorem two_dvd_mul_sub_one (k : ℕ) : 2 ∣ k * (k - 1)
proof:
rcases k with rfl | k; · simp
  simpa [mul_comm (k + 1)] using k.two_dvd_mul_add_one

-- Here are examples of how `parity_simps` can be used with `Nat`.

name: neg_one_pow_eq_ite
statement: theorem neg_one_pow_eq_ite  : (-1 : R) ^ n = if Even n then 1 else (-1)
proof:
cases even_or_odd n with
  | inl h => rw [h.neg_one_pow, if_pos h]
  | inr h => rw [h.neg_one_pow, if_neg (by simpa using h)]

name: neg_one_pow_congr
statement: theorem neg_one_pow_congr (h : Even m ↔ Even n) : (-1 : R) ^ m = (-1) ^ n
proof: simp [h, neg_one_pow_eq_ite]

name: neg_one_zpow_eq_ite
statement: theorem neg_one_zpow_eq_ite  : (-1 : α) ^ n = if Even n then 1 else -1
proof:
obtain ⟨n, _⟩ := n.eq_nat_or_neg
  aesop (add safe (by rw [neg_one_pow_eq_ite]))

end DivisionMonoid

section CharTwo

-- We state the following theorems in terms of the slightly more general `2 = 0` hypothesis.

variable {R : Type*} [AddMonoidWithOne R]

private theorem natCast_eq_zero_or_one_of_two_eq_zero' (n : ℕ) (h : (2 : R) = 0) :
    (Even n → (n : R) = 0) ∧ (Odd n → (n : R) = 1) := by
  induction n using Nat.twoStepInduction with
  | zero => simp
  | one => simp
  | more n _ _ => simpa [add_assoc, Nat.even_add_one, Nat.odd_add_one, h]

name: isUnit_iff_mem_nonZeroDivisors_of_finite
statement: theorem isUnit_iff_mem_nonZeroDivisors_of_finite  : IsUnit a ↔ a ∈ nonZeroDivisors R
proof: rw [← isRegular_iff_mem_nonZeroDivisors, isRegular_iff_isUnit_of_finite]

name: dvd_cancel_right_mem_nonZeroDivisors
statement: theorem dvd_cancel_right_mem_nonZeroDivisors (hr : r ∈ R⁰) : x * r ∣ y * r ↔ x ∣ y
proof: simp_rw [← mul_comm r, dvd_cancel_left_mem_nonZeroDivisors hr]

name: toDistrib_injective
statement: theorem toDistrib_injective  : Function.Injective (@toDistrib R)
proof:
intro _ _ h
  ext x y
  · exact congrArg (·.toAdd.add x y) h
  · exact congrArg (·.toMul.mul x y) h

end NonUnitalNonAssocSemiring

/-! ### NonUnitalSemiring -/
namespace NonUnitalSemiring

name: toNonUnitalNonAssocSemiring_injective
statement: theorem toNonUnitalNonAssocSemiring_injective  : Function.Injective (@toNonUnitalNonAssocSemiring R)
proof:
rintro ⟨⟩ ⟨⟩ _; congr

@[ext] theorem ext ⦃inst₁ inst₂ : NonUnitalSemiring R⦄
    (h_add : local_hAdd[R, inst₁] = local_hAdd[R, inst₂])
    (h_mul : local_hMul[R, inst₁] = local_hMul[R, inst₂]) :
    inst₁ = inst₂ :=
  toNonUnitalNonAssocSemiring_injective <|
    NonUnitalNonAssocSemiring.ext h_add h_mul

end NonUnitalSemiring

/-! ### NonAssocSemiring and its ancestors

This section also includes results for `AddMonoidWithOne`, `AddCommMonoidWithOne`, etc.
as these are considered implementation detail of the ring classes.
TODO consider relocating these lemmas.
-/
/- TODO consider relocating these lemmas. -/
@[ext] theorem AddMonoidWithOne.ext ⦃inst₁ inst₂ : AddMonoidWithOne R⦄
    (h_add : local_hAdd[R, inst₁] = local_hAdd[R, inst₂])
    (h_one : (letI := inst₁; One.one : R) = (letI := inst₂; One.one : R)) :
    inst₁ = inst₂ := by
  have h_monoid : inst₁.toAddMonoid = inst₂.toAddMonoid := by ext : 1; exact h_add
  have h_zero' : inst₁.toZero = inst₂.toZero := congrArg (·.toZero) h_monoid
  have h_one' : inst₁.toOne = inst₂.toOne :=
    congrArg One.mk h_one
  have h_natCast : inst₁.toNatCast.natCast = inst₂.toNatCast.natCast := by
    funext n; induction n with
    | zero => rewrite [inst₁.natCast_zero, inst₂.natCast_zero]
              exact congrArg (@Zero.zero R) h_zero'
    | succ n h => rw [inst₁.natCast_succ, inst₂.natCast_succ, h_add]
                  exact congrArg₂ _ h h_one
  rcases inst₁ with @⟨⟨⟩⟩; rcases inst₂ with @⟨⟨⟩⟩
  congr

name: toNonUnitalNonAssocSemiring_injective
statement: theorem toNonUnitalNonAssocSemiring_injective  : Function.Injective (@toNonUnitalNonAssocSemiring R)
proof:
intro _ _ _
  ext <;> congr

end NonAssocSemiring

/-! ### NonUnitalNonAssocRing -/
namespace NonUnitalNonAssocRing

@[ext] theorem ext ⦃inst₁ inst₂ : NonUnitalNonAssocRing R⦄
    (h_add : local_hAdd[R, inst₁] = local_hAdd[R, inst₂])
    (h_mul : local_hMul[R, inst₁] = local_hMul[R, inst₂]) :
    inst₁ = inst₂ := by
  -- Split into `AddCommGroup` instance, `mul` function and properties.
  rcases inst₁ with @⟨_, ⟨⟩⟩; rcases inst₂ with @⟨_, ⟨⟩⟩
  congr; (ext : 1; assumption)

name: toNonUnitalNonAssocSemiring_injective
statement: theorem toNonUnitalNonAssocSemiring_injective  : Function.Injective (@toNonUnitalNonAssocSemiring R)
proof:
intro _ _ h
  -- Use above extensionality lemma to prove injectivity by showing that `h_add` and `h_mul` hold.
  ext x y
  · exact congrArg (·.toAdd.add x y) h
  · exact congrArg (·.toMul.mul x y) h

end NonUnitalNonAssocRing

/-! ### NonUnitalRing -/
namespace NonUnitalRing

@[ext] theorem ext ⦃inst₁ inst₂ : NonUnitalRing R⦄
    (h_add : local_hAdd[R, inst₁] = local_hAdd[R, inst₂])
    (h_mul : local_hMul[R, inst₁] = local_hMul[R, inst₂]) :
    inst₁ = inst₂ := by
  have : inst₁.toNonUnitalNonAssocRing = inst₂.toNonUnitalNonAssocRing := by
    ext : 1 <;> assumption
  -- Split into fields and prove they are equal using the above.
  cases inst₁; cases inst₂
  congr

name: toNonUnitalSemiring_injective
statement: theorem toNonUnitalSemiring_injective  : Function.Injective (@toNonUnitalSemiring R)
proof:
intro _ _ h
  ext x y
  · exact congrArg (·.toAdd.add x y) h
  · exact congrArg (·.toMul.mul x y) h

name: toNonUnitalNonAssocring_injective
statement: theorem toNonUnitalNonAssocring_injective  : Function.Injective (@toNonUnitalNonAssocRing R)
proof:
intro _ _ _
  ext <;> congr

end NonUnitalRing

/-! ### NonAssocRing and its ancestors

This section also includes results for `AddGroupWithOne`, `AddCommGroupWithOne`, etc.
as these are considered implementation detail of the ring classes.
TODO consider relocating these lemmas. -/
@[ext] theorem AddGroupWithOne.ext ⦃inst₁ inst₂ : AddGroupWithOne R⦄
    (h_add : local_hAdd[R, inst₁] = local_hAdd[R, inst₂])
    (h_one : (letI := inst₁; One.one : R) = (letI := inst₂; One.one)) :
    inst₁ = inst₂ := by
  have : inst₁.toAddMonoidWithOne = inst₂.toAddMonoidWithOne :=
    AddMonoidWithOne.ext h_add h_one
  have : inst₁.toNatCast = inst₂.toNatCast := congrArg (·.toNatCast) this
  have h_group : inst₁.toAddGroup = inst₂.toAddGroup := by ext : 1; exact h_add
  -- Extract equality of necessary substructures from h_group
  injection h_group with h_group; injection h_group
  have : inst₁.toIntCast.intCast = inst₂.toIntCast.intCast := by
    funext n; cases n with
    | ofNat n => rewrite [Int.ofNat_eq_natCast, inst₁.intCast_ofNat, inst₂.intCast_ofNat]; congr
    | negSucc n => rewrite [inst₁.intCast_negSucc, inst₂.intCast_negSucc]; congr
  rcases inst₁ with @⟨⟨⟩⟩; rcases inst₂ with @⟨⟨⟩⟩
  congr

@[ext] theorem AddCommGroupWithOne.ext ⦃inst₁ inst₂ : AddCommGroupWithOne R⦄
    (h_add : local_hAdd[R, inst₁] = local_hAdd[R, inst₂])
    (h_one : (letI := inst₁; One.one : R) = (letI := inst₂; One.one)) :
    inst₁ = inst₂ := by
  have : inst₁.toAddCommGroup = inst₂.toAddCommGroup :=
    AddCommGroup.ext h_add
  have : inst₁.toAddGroupWithOne = inst₂.toAddGroupWithOne :=
    AddGroupWithOne.ext h_add h_one
  injection this with _ h_addMonoidWithOne; injection h_addMonoidWithOne
  cases inst₁; cases inst₂
  congr

namespace NonAssocRing

@[ext] theorem ext ⦃inst₁ inst₂ : NonAssocRing R⦄
    (h_add : local_hAdd[R, inst₁] = local_hAdd[R, inst₂])
    (h_mul : local_hMul[R, inst₁] = local_hMul[R, inst₂]) :
    inst₁ = inst₂ := by
  have h₁ : inst₁.toNonUnitalNonAssocRing = inst₂.toNonUnitalNonAssocRing := by
    ext : 1 <;> assumption
  have h₂ : inst₁.toNonAssocSemiring = inst₂.toNonAssocSemiring := by
    ext : 1 <;> assumption
  -- Mathematically non-trivial fact: `intCast` is determined by the rest.
  have h₃ : inst₁.toAddCommGroupWithOne = inst₂.toAddCommGroupWithOne :=
    AddCommGroupWithOne.ext h_add (congrArg (·.toOne.one) h₂)
  cases inst₁; cases inst₂
  congr <;> solve | injection h₁ | injection h₂ | injection h₃

name: toNonAssocSemiring_injective
statement: theorem toNonAssocSemiring_injective  : Function.Injective (@toNonAssocSemiring R)
proof:
intro _ _ h
  ext x y
  · exact congrArg (·.toAdd.add x y) h
  · exact congrArg (·.toMul.mul x y) h

name: toNonUnitalNonAssocring_injective
statement: theorem toNonUnitalNonAssocring_injective  : Function.Injective (@toNonUnitalNonAssocRing R)
proof:
intro _ _ _
  ext <;> congr

end NonAssocRing

/-! ### Semiring -/
namespace Semiring

@[ext] theorem ext ⦃inst₁ inst₂ : Semiring R⦄
    (h_add : local_hAdd[R, inst₁] = local_hAdd[R, inst₂])
    (h_mul : local_hMul[R, inst₁] = local_hMul[R, inst₂]) :
    inst₁ = inst₂ := by
  -- Show that enough substructures are equal.
  have h₀ : inst₁.toAddCommMonoid = inst₂.toAddCommMonoid := by
    ext : 1 <;> assumption
  have h₁ : inst₁.toNonUnitalSemiring = inst₂.toNonUnitalSemiring := by
    ext : 1 <;> assumption
  have h₂ : inst₁.toNonAssocSemiring = inst₂.toNonAssocSemiring := by
    ext : 1 <;> assumption
  have h₃ : (inst₁.toMonoidWithZero).toMonoid = (inst₂.toMonoidWithZero).toMonoid := by
    ext : 1; exact h_mul
  -- Split into fields and prove they are equal using the above.
  cases inst₁; cases inst₂
  congr <;> solve | injection h₁ | injection h₂

name: toNonUnitalSemiring_injective
statement: theorem toNonUnitalSemiring_injective  : Function.Injective (@toNonUnitalSemiring R)
proof:
intro _ _ h
  ext x y
  · exact congrArg (·.toAdd.add x y) h
  · exact congrArg (·.toMul.mul x y) h

name: toNonAssocSemiring_injective
statement: theorem toNonAssocSemiring_injective  : Function.Injective (@toNonAssocSemiring R)
proof:
intro _ _ h
  ext x y
  · exact congrArg (·.toAdd.add x y) h
  · exact congrArg (·.toMul.mul x y) h

end Semiring

/-! ### Ring -/
namespace Ring

@[ext] theorem ext ⦃inst₁ inst₂ : Ring R⦄
    (h_add : local_hAdd[R, inst₁] = local_hAdd[R, inst₂])
    (h_mul : local_hMul[R, inst₁] = local_hMul[R, inst₂]) :
    inst₁ = inst₂ := by
  -- Show that enough substructures are equal.
  have h₁ : inst₁.toSemiring = inst₂.toSemiring := by
    ext : 1 <;> assumption
  have h₂ : inst₁.toNonAssocRing = inst₂.toNonAssocRing := by
    ext : 1 <;> assumption
  /- We prove that the `SubNegMonoid`s are equal because they are one
  field away from `Sub` and `Neg`, enabling use of `injection`. -/
  have h₃ : (inst₁.toAddCommGroup).toAddGroup.toSubNegMonoid
            = (inst₂.toAddCommGroup).toAddGroup.toSubNegMonoid :=
    congrArg (@AddGroup.toSubNegMonoid R) <| by ext : 1; exact h_add
  -- Split into fields and prove they are equal using the above.
  cases inst₁; cases inst₂
  congr <;> solve | injection h₂ | injection h₃

name: toNonUnitalRing_injective
statement: theorem toNonUnitalRing_injective  : Function.Injective (@toNonUnitalRing R)
proof:
intro _ _ h

  · exact congrArg (·.toMul.mul x y) h

name: toNonAssocRing_injective
statement: theorem toNonAssocRing_injective  : Function.Injective (@toNonAssocRing R)
proof:

  ext <;> congr

name: toSemiring_injective
statement: theorem toSemiring_injective  : Function.Injective (@toSemiring R)
proof:
intro _ _ h
  ext x y
  · exact congrArg (·.toAdd.add x y) h
  · exact congrArg (·.toMul.mul x y) h

end Ring

name: neg_right
statement: theorem neg_right (h : SemiconjBy a x y) : SemiconjBy a (-x) (-y)
proof:
simp only [SemiconjBy, h.eq, neg_mul, mul_neg]

@[simp]

name: neg_left
statement: theorem neg_left (h : SemiconjBy a x y) : SemiconjBy (-a) x y
proof:
simp only [SemiconjBy, h.eq, neg_mul, mul_neg]

@[simp]

name: centroid_eq_centralizer_mulLeftRight
statement: theorem centroid_eq_centralizer_mulLeftRight  : RingHom.rangeS (toEndRingHom α) = Subsemiring.centralizer (Set.range L ∪ Set.range R)
proof:
ext T
  refine ⟨?_, fun h ↦ ?_⟩
  · rintro ⟨f, rfl⟩ S (⟨a, rfl⟩ | ⟨b, rfl⟩)
    · exact AddMonoidHom.ext fun b ↦ (map_mul_left f a b).symm
    · exact AddMonoidHom.ext fun a ↦ (map_mul_right f a b).symm
  · rw [Subsemiring.mem_centralizer_iff] at h
    refine ⟨⟨T, fun a b ↦ ?_, fun a b ↦ ?_⟩, rfl⟩
    · exact congr($(h (L a) (.inl ⟨a, rfl⟩)) b).symm
    · exact congr($(h (R b) (.inr ⟨b, rfl⟩)) a).symm

/-- The canonical homomorphism from the center into the center of the centroid -/

name: ite_zero_mul_ite_zero
statement: theorem ite_zero_mul_ite_zero  : ite P a 0 * ite Q b 0 = ite (P ∧ Q) (a * b) 0
proof:
simp only [← ite_and, ite_mul, mul_ite, mul_zero, zero_mul, and_comm]

end MulZeroClass

name: add_mul_self_eq
statement: theorem add_mul_self_eq (a b : α) : (a + b) * (a + b) = a * a + 2 * a * b + b * b
proof: simp only [two_mul, add_mul, mul_add, add_assoc, mul_comm b]

name: add_sq
statement: theorem add_sq (a b : α) : (a + b) ^ 2 = a ^ 2 + 2 * a * b + b ^ 2
proof: simp only [sq, add_mul_self_eq]

name: mul_sub_left_distrib
statement: theorem mul_sub_left_distrib (a b c : α) : a * (b - c) = a * b - a * c
proof:
simpa only [sub_eq_add_neg, neg_mul_eq_mul_neg] using mul_add a b (-c)

alias mul_sub := mul_sub_left_distrib

name: mul_sub_right_distrib
statement: theorem mul_sub_right_distrib (a b c : α) : (a - b) * c = a * c - b * c
proof:
simpa only [sub_eq_add_neg, neg_mul_eq_neg_mul] using add_mul a (-b) c

alias sub_mul := mul_sub_right_distrib

end NonUnitalNonAssocRing

section NonAssocRing

variable [NonAssocRing α]

name: mul_one_sub_mul
statement: theorem mul_one_sub_mul (a b c : α) : a * (1 - b) * c = a * c - a * b * c
proof:
rw [mul_one_sub, sub_mul]

end NonAssocRing

section Ring

variable [Ring α]

-- A (unital, associative) ring is a not-necessarily-unital ring
-- see Note [lower instance priority]
instance (priority := 100) Ring.toNonUnitalRing : NonUnitalRing α :=
  { ‹Ring α› with }

-- A (unital, associative) ring is a not-necessarily-associative ring
-- see Note [lower instance priority]
instance (priority := 100) Ring.toNonAssocRing : NonAssocRing α :=
  { ‹Ring α› with }

end Ring

/-- A non-unital non-associative commutative ring is a `NonUnitalNonAssocRing` with commutative
multiplication. -/

name: sq_sub_sq
statement: theorem sq_sub_sq (h : Commute a b) : a ^ 2 - b ^ 2 = (a + b) * (a - b)
proof:
rw [sq, sq, h.mul_self_sub_mul_self_eq]

protected lemma sub_sq {a b : R} (h : Commute a b) :
    (a - b) ^ 2 = a ^ 2 - 2 * a * b + b ^ 2 := by
  simp [sq, add_mul, sub_mul, mul_sub, two_mul, h.eq, ← sub_add, ← sub_sub]

variable [NoZeroDivisors R]

protected lemma sq_eq_sq_iff_eq_or_eq_neg (h : Commute a b) : a ^ 2 = b ^ 2 ↔ a = b ∨ a = -b := by
  rw [← sub_eq_zero, h.sq_sub_sq, mul_eq_zero, add_eq_zero_iff_eq_neg, sub_eq_zero, or_comm]

end Ring
end Commute

section HasDistribNeg
variable (R)
variable [Monoid R] [HasDistribNeg R]

name: neg_one_pow_mul_eq_zero_iff
statement: theorem neg_one_pow_mul_eq_zero_iff  : (-1) ^ n * a = 0 ↔ a = 0
proof:
rcases neg_one_pow_eq_or R n with h | h <;> simp [h]

@[simp] lemma mul_neg_one_pow_eq_zero_iff : a * (-1) ^ n = 0 ↔ a = 0 := by
  obtain h | h := neg_one_pow_eq_or R n <;> simp [h]

name: neg_one_pow_eq_pow_mod_two
statement: theorem neg_one_pow_eq_pow_mod_two (n : ℕ) : (-1 : R) ^ n = (-1) ^ (n % 2)
proof:
rw [← Nat.mod_add_div n 2, pow_add, pow_mul]; simp [sq]

variable [NoZeroDivisors R]

@[simp] lemma sq_eq_one_iff : a ^ 2 = 1 ↔ a = 1 ∨ a = -1 := by
  rw [← (Commute.one_right a).sq_eq_sq_iff_eq_or_eq_neg, one_pow]

name: sub_sq
statement: theorem sub_sq (a b : R) : (a - b) ^ 2 = a ^ 2 - 2 * a * b + b ^ 2
proof:
rw [sub_eq_add_neg, add_sq, neg_sq, mul_neg, ← sub_eq_add_neg]

alias sub_pow_two := sub_sq

name: sub_sq_comm
statement: theorem sub_sq_comm (a b : R) : (a - b) ^ 2 = (b - a) ^ 2
proof:
rw [sub_sq', mul_right_comm, add_comm, sub_sq']

variable [NoZeroDivisors R] {a b : R}

name: sumSq_toAddSubmonoid
statement: theorem sumSq_toAddSubmonoid  : (sumSq T).toAddSubmonoid = .sumSq T
proof:
simp [sumSq, ← AddSubmonoid.closure_isSquare,
    Subsemigroup.nonUnitalSubsemiringClosure_toAddSubmonoid]

@[simp]

name: closure_isSquare
statement: theorem closure_isSquare  : closure {x : T | IsSquare x} = sumSq T
proof:
simp [sumSq, Subsemigroup.nonUnitalSubsemiringClosure_eq_closure]

end NonUnitalSubsemiring

@[simp, aesop safe]

name: closure_isSquare
statement: theorem closure_isSquare  : closure {x : T | IsSquare x} = sumSq T
proof:
apply_fun toNonUnitalSubsemiring using toNonUnitalSubsemiring_injective
  simp [← Submonoid.square_subsemiringClosure]

end Subsemiring

/-- In a commutative semiring, a finite product of sums of squares is a sum of squares. -/
@[aesop unsafe 50% apply]

name: toNonUnitalRingHomm_comp_symm_toNonUnitalRingHom
statement: theorem toNonUnitalRingHomm_comp_symm_toNonUnitalRingHom (e : R ≃+* S) : e.toNonUnitalRingHom.comp e.symm.toNonUnitalRingHom = NonUnitalRingHom.id _

  simp

variable [NonAssocSemiring R] [NonAssocSemiring S] [NonAssocSemiring S']

name: toRingHom_comp_symm_toRingHom
statement: theorem toRingHom_comp_symm_toRingHom (e : R ≃+* S) : e.toRingHom.comp e.symm.toRingHom = RingHom.id _
proof: simp
