statement: theorem degree_add_le (p q : R[X]) : degree (p + q) ≤ max (degree p) (degree q)
proof:
simpa only [degree, ← support_toFinsupp, toFinsupp_add]
    using AddMonoidAlgebra.sup_support_add_le _ _ _

name: natDegree_add_le
statement: theorem natDegree_add_le (p q : R[X]) : natDegree (p + q) ≤ max (natDegree p) (natDegree q)
proof: rcases le_max_iff.1 (degree_add_le p q) with h | h <;> simp [natDegree_le_natDegree h]

name: leadingCoeff_eq_zero_iff_deg_eq_bot
statement: theorem leadingCoeff_eq_zero_iff_deg_eq_bot  : leadingCoeff p = 0 ↔ degree p = ⊥
proof: rw [leadingCoeff_eq_zero, degree_eq_bot]

name: degree_erase_lt
statement: theorem degree_erase_lt (hp : p ≠ 0) : degree (p.erase (natDegree p)) < degree p
proof:
apply lt_of_le_of_ne (degree_erase_le _ _)
  rw [degree_eq_natDegree hp, degree, support_erase]
  exact fun h => notMem_erase _ _ (mem_of_max h)

name: degree_mul_le
statement: theorem degree_mul_le (p q : R[X]) : degree (p * q) ≤ degree p + degree q
proof: simpa [degree, ← support_toFinsupp] using AddMonoidAlgebra.sup_support_mul_le (by simp) ..

name: leadingCoeff_C_mul_X
statement: theorem leadingCoeff_C_mul_X (a : R) : leadingCoeff (C a * X) = a
proof:
simpa only [pow_one] using leadingCoeff_C_mul_X_pow a 1

@[simp]

name: leadingCoeff_X_pow
statement: theorem leadingCoeff_X_pow (n : ℕ) : leadingCoeff ((X : R[X]) ^ n) = 1
proof: simpa only [C_1, one_mul] using leadingCoeff_C_mul_X_pow (1 : R) n

name: leadingCoeff_X
statement: theorem leadingCoeff_X  : leadingCoeff (X : R[X]) = 1
proof:
simpa only [pow_one] using @leadingCoeff_X_pow R _ 1

@[simp]

name: natDegree_eq_zero_iff_degree_le_zero
statement: theorem natDegree_eq_zero_iff_degree_le_zero  : p.natDegree = 0 ↔ p.degree ≤ 0
proof: rw [← nonpos_iff_eq_zero, natDegree_le_iff_degree_le, Nat.cast_zero]

name: degree_X_pow
statement: theorem degree_X_pow  : degree ((X : R[X]) ^ n) = n
proof:
rw [X_pow_eq_monomial, degree_monomial _ (one_ne_zero' R)]

@[simp]

name: degree_sub_le
statement: theorem degree_sub_le (p q : R[X]) : degree (p - q) ≤ max (degree p) (degree q)
proof: simpa only [degree_neg q] using degree_add_le p (-q)

name: natDegree_sub_le
statement: theorem natDegree_sub_le (p q : R[X]) : natDegree (p - q) ≤ max (natDegree p) (natDegree q)
proof: simpa only [← natDegree_neg q] using natDegree_add_le p (-q)

name: natDegree_comp_eq_of_mul_ne_zero
statement: theorem natDegree_comp_eq_of_mul_ne_zero (h : p.leadingCoeff * q.leadingCoeff ^ p.natDegree ≠ 0) : natDegree (p.comp q) = natDegree p * natDegree q
proof:
by_cases hq : natDegree q = 0
  · exact le_antisymm natDegree_comp_le (by simp [hq])
  apply natDegree_eq_of_le_of_coeff_ne_zero natDegree_comp_le
  rwa [coeff_comp_degree_mul_degree hq]

name: natDegree_le_iff_coeff_eq_zero
statement: theorem natDegree_le_iff_coeff_eq_zero  : p.natDegree ≤ n ↔ ∀ N : ℕ, n < N → p.coeff N = 0
proof: simp_rw [natDegree_le_iff_degree_le, degree_le_iff_coeff_zero, Nat.cast_lt]

name: natDegree_mul_C_eq_of_mul_ne_zero
statement: theorem natDegree_mul_C_eq_of_mul_ne_zero (h : p.leadingCoeff * a ≠ 0) : (p * C a).natDegree = p.natDegree
proof:
refine eq_natDegree_of_le_mem_support (natDegree_mul_C_le p a) ?_
  refine mem_support_iff.mpr ?_
  rwa [coeff_mul_C]

/-- Although not explicitly stated, the assumptions of lemma `natDegree_C_mul_of_mul_ne_zero`
force the polynomial `p` to be non-zero, via `p.leadingCoeff ≠ 0`.
-/

name: natDegree_C_mul_of_mul_ne_zero
statement: theorem natDegree_C_mul_of_mul_ne_zero (h : a * p.leadingCoeff ≠ 0) : (C a * p).natDegree = p.natDegree
proof:
refine eq_natDegree_of_le_mem_support (natDegree_C_mul_le a p) ?_
  refine mem_support_iff.mpr ?_
  rwa [coeff_C_mul]

name: degree_C_mul_of_mul_ne_zero
statement: theorem degree_C_mul_of_mul_ne_zero (h : a * p.leadingCoeff ≠ 0) : (C a * p).degree = p.degree
proof: rw [degree_mul' (by simpa)]; simp [left_ne_zero_of_mul h]

name: natDegree_add_coeff_mul
statement: theorem natDegree_add_coeff_mul (f g : R[X]) : (f * g).coeff (f.natDegree + g.natDegree) = f.coeff f.natDegree * g.coeff g.natDegree
proof: simp only [coeff_natDegree, coeff_mul_degree_add_degree]

name: coeff_pow_of_natDegree_le
statement: theorem coeff_pow_of_natDegree_le (pn : p.natDegree ≤ n) : (p ^ m).coeff (m * n) = p.coeff n ^ m
proof:
induction m with
  | zero => simp
  | succ m hm =>
    rw [pow_succ, pow_succ, ← hm, Nat.succ_mul, coeff_mul_add_eq_of_natDegree_le _ pn]
    refine natDegree_pow_le.trans (le_trans ?_ (le_refl _))
    gcongr

name: coeff_add_eq_right_of_lt
statement: theorem coeff_add_eq_right_of_lt (pn : p.natDegree < n) : (p + q).coeff n = q.coeff n
proof:
rw [add_comm]
  exact coeff_add_eq_left_of_lt pn

open scoped Function -- required for scoped `on` notation

name: natDegree_pos_of_nextCoeff_ne_zero
statement: theorem natDegree_pos_of_nextCoeff_ne_zero (h : p.nextCoeff ≠ 0) : 0 < p.natDegree
proof:
grind [nextCoeff]

end Degree

end Semiring

section Ring

variable [Ring R] {p q : R[X]}

name: natDegree_sub_le_iff_left
statement: theorem natDegree_sub_le_iff_left (qn : q.natDegree ≤ n) : (p - q).natDegree ≤ n ↔ p.natDegree ≤ n
proof:
rw [← natDegree_neg] at qn
  rw [sub_eq_add_neg, natDegree_add_le_iff_left _ _ qn]

name: coeff_sub_eq_left_of_lt
statement: theorem coeff_sub_eq_left_of_lt (dg : q.natDegree < n) : (p - q).coeff n = p.coeff n
proof:
rw [← natDegree_neg] at dg
  rw [sub_eq_add_neg, coeff_add_eq_left_of_lt dg]

name: coeff_sub_eq_neg_right_of_lt
statement: theorem coeff_sub_eq_neg_right_of_lt (df : p.natDegree < n) : (p - q).coeff n = -q.coeff n
proof:
rwa [sub_eq_add_neg, coeff_add_eq_right_of_lt, coeff_neg]

end Ring

section NoZeroDivisors

variable [Semiring R] {p q : R[X]} {a : R}

@[simp]

name: natDegree_eq_one
statement: theorem natDegree_eq_one  : p.natDegree = 1 ↔ ∃ a ≠ 0, ∃ b, C a * X + C b = p
proof:
refine ⟨fun hp ↦ ⟨p.coeff 1, fun h ↦ ?_, p.coeff 0, ?_⟩, ?_⟩
  · rw [← hp, coeff_natDegree, leadingCoeff_eq_zero] at h
    simp_all
  · ext n
    obtain _ | _ | n := n
    · simp
    · simp
    · simp only [coeff_add, coeff_mul_X, coeff_C_succ, add_zero]
      rw [coeff_eq_zero_of_natDegree_lt]
      simp [hp]
  · rintro ⟨a, ha, b, rfl⟩
    simp [ha]

name: degree_mul_C
statement: theorem degree_mul_C (a0 : a ≠ 0) : (p * C a).degree = p.degree
proof: rw [degree_mul, degree_C a0, add_zero]

name: degree_C_mul
statement: theorem degree_C_mul (a0 : a ≠ 0) : (C a * p).degree = p.degree
proof: rw [degree_mul, degree_C a0, zero_add]

name: natDegree_mul_C
statement: theorem natDegree_mul_C (a0 : a ≠ 0) : (p * C a).natDegree = p.natDegree
proof: simp only [natDegree, degree_mul_C a0]

name: natDegree_C_mul
statement: theorem natDegree_C_mul (a0 : a ≠ 0) : (C a * p).natDegree = p.natDegree
proof: simp only [natDegree, degree_C_mul a0]

name: natDegree_comp
statement: theorem natDegree_comp  : natDegree (p.comp q) = natDegree p * natDegree q
proof:
by_cases q0 : q.natDegree = 0
  · rw [degree_le_zero_iff.mp (natDegree_eq_zero_iff_degree_le_zero.mp q0), comp_C, natDegree_C,
      natDegree_C, mul_zero]
  · by_cases p0 : p = 0
    · simp only [p0, zero_comp, natDegree_zero, zero_mul]
    · simp only [Ne, mul_eq_zero, leadingCoeff_eq_zero, p0, natDegree_comp_eq_of_mul_ne_zero,
        ne_zero_of_natDegree_gt (Nat.pos_of_ne_zero q0), not_false_eq_true, pow_ne_zero, or_self]

@[simp]

name: natDegree_iterate_comp
statement: theorem natDegree_iterate_comp (k : ℕ) : (p.comp^[k] q).natDegree = p.natDegree ^ k * q.natDegree
proof:
induction k with
  | zero => simp
  | succ k IH => rw [Function.iterate_succ_apply', natDegree_comp, IH, pow_succ', mul_assoc]

name: leadingCoeff_comp
statement: theorem leadingCoeff_comp (hq : natDegree q ≠ 0) : leadingCoeff (p.comp q) = leadingCoeff p * leadingCoeff q ^ natDegree p
proof:
rw [← coeff_comp_degree_mul_degree hq, ← natDegree_comp, coeff_natDegree]

@[simp]

name: nextCoeff_C_mul
statement: theorem nextCoeff_C_mul  : (C a * p).nextCoeff = a * p.nextCoeff
proof:
by_cases h₀ : a = 0 <;> simp [h₀, nextCoeff, natDegree_C_mul]

@[simp]

name: nextCoeff_mul_C
statement: theorem nextCoeff_mul_C  : (p * C a).nextCoeff = p.nextCoeff * a
proof:
by_cases h₀ : a = 0 <;> simp [h₀, nextCoeff, natDegree_mul_C]

end NoZeroDivisors

@[simp] lemma comp_neg_X_leadingCoeff_eq [Ring R] (p : R[X]) :
    (p.comp (-X)).leadingCoeff = (-1) ^ p.natDegree * p.leadingCoeff := by
  nontriviality R
  by_cases h : p = 0
  · simp [h]
  rw [Polynomial.leadingCoeff, natDegree_comp_eq_of_mul_ne_zero, coeff_comp_degree_mul_degree] <;>
  simp [((Commute.neg_one_left _).pow_left _).eq, h]

@[simp]

name: degree_mul_leadingCoeff_self_inv
statement: theorem degree_mul_leadingCoeff_self_inv (p : K[X]) : degree (p * C (leadingCoeff p)⁻¹) = degree p
proof:
by_cases hp : p = 0
  · simp [hp]
  exact degree_mul_leadingCoeff_inv _ hp

name: degree_add_degree_leadingCoeff_inv
statement: theorem degree_add_degree_leadingCoeff_inv (p : K[X]) : degree p + degree (C (leadingCoeff p)⁻¹) = degree p
proof:
rw [← degree_mul, degree_mul_leadingCoeff_self_inv]

end DivisionRing

end Polynomial

name: coeff_natTrailingDegree_eq_zero
statement: theorem coeff_natTrailingDegree_eq_zero  : coeff p p.natTrailingDegree = 0 ↔ p = 0
proof:
constructor
  · rintro h
    by_contra hp
    obtain ⟨n, hpn, hn⟩ := by simpa using min_mem_image_coe <| support_nonempty.2 hp
    obtain rfl := (trailingDegree_eq_iff_natTrailingDegree_eq hp).1 hn.symm
    exact hpn h
  · rintro rfl
    simp

name: natTrailingDegree_eq_zero
statement: theorem natTrailingDegree_eq_zero  : natTrailingDegree p = 0 ↔ p = 0 ∨ coeff p 0 ≠ 0
proof: simp [natTrailingDegree, or_comm]

name: trailingCoeff_eq_coeff_zero
statement: theorem trailingCoeff_eq_coeff_zero (h : coeff p 0 ≠ 0) : trailingCoeff p = coeff p 0
proof: rw [trailingCoeff, (natTrailingDegree_eq_zero.mpr <| .inr h)]

name: trailingDegree_monomial
statement: theorem trailingDegree_monomial (ha : a ≠ 0) : trailingDegree (monomial n a) = n
proof:
rw [trailingDegree, support_monomial n ha, min_singleton]
  rfl

name: natTrailingDegree_monomial
statement: theorem natTrailingDegree_monomial (ha : a ≠ 0) : natTrailingDegree (monomial n a) = n
proof:
rw [natTrailingDegree, trailingDegree_monomial ha]
  rfl

name: trailingDegree_one_le
statement: theorem trailingDegree_one_le  : (0 : ℕ∞) ≤ trailingDegree (1 : R[X])
proof:
rw [← C_1]
  exact le_trailingDegree_C

@[simp]

name: natTrailingDegree_natCast
statement: theorem natTrailingDegree_natCast (n : ℕ) : natTrailingDegree (n : R[X]) = 0
proof:
simp only [← C_eq_natCast, natTrailingDegree_C]

@[simp]

name: le_trailingDegree_X_pow
statement: theorem le_trailingDegree_X_pow (n : ℕ) : (n : ℕ∞) ≤ trailingDegree (X ^ n : R[X])
proof: simpa only [C_1, one_mul] using le_trailingDegree_C_mul_X_pow n (1 : R)

name: natTrailingDegree_le_natDegree
statement: theorem natTrailingDegree_le_natDegree (p : R[X]) : p.natTrailingDegree ≤ p.natDegree
proof:
by_cases hp : p = 0
  · rw [hp, natDegree_zero, natTrailingDegree_zero]
  · exact le_natDegree_of_ne_zero (mt trailingCoeff_eq_zero.mp hp)

name: le_trailingDegree_mul
statement: theorem le_trailingDegree_mul  : p.trailingDegree + q.trailingDegree ≤ (p * q).trailingDegree
proof:
refine Finset.le_min fun n hn => ?_
  rw [mem_support_iff, coeff_mul] at hn
  obtain ⟨⟨i, j⟩, hij, hpq⟩ := exists_ne_zero_of_sum_ne_zero hn
  refine
    (add_le_add (min_le (mem_support_iff.mpr (left_ne_zero_of_mul hpq)))
          (min_le (mem_support_iff.mpr (right_ne_zero_of_mul hpq)))).trans_eq ?_
  rwa [← WithTop.coe_add, WithTop.coe_eq_coe, ← mem_antidiagonal]

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in

name: le_natTrailingDegree_mul
statement: theorem le_natTrailingDegree_mul (h : p * q ≠ 0) : p.natTrailingDegree + q.natTrailingDegree ≤ (p * q).natTrailingDegree
proof:
have hp : p ≠ 0 := fun hp => h (by rw [hp, zero_mul])
  have hq : q ≠ 0 := fun hq => h (by rw [hq, mul_zero])
  rw [← WithTop.coe_le_coe, WithTop.coe_add, ← Nat.cast_withTop (natTrailingDegree p),
    ← Nat.cast_withTop (natTrailingDegree q), ← Nat.cast_withTop (natTrailingDegree (p * q)),
    ← trailingDegree_eq_natTrailingDegree hp, ← trailingDegree_eq_natTrailingDegree hq,
    ← trailingDegree_eq_natTrailingDegree h]
  exact le_trailingDegree_mul

name: trailingDegree_X_pow
statement: theorem trailingDegree_X_pow (n : ℕ) : (X ^ n : R[X]).trailingDegree = n
proof:
rw [X_pow_eq_monomial, trailingDegree_monomial one_ne_zero]

@[simp]

name: natTrailingDegree_X_pow
statement: theorem natTrailingDegree_X_pow (n : ℕ) : (X ^ n : R[X]).natTrailingDegree = n
proof:
rw [X_pow_eq_monomial, natTrailingDegree_monomial one_ne_zero]

end NonzeroSemiring

section Ring

variable [Ring R]

@[simp]

name: trailingDegree_neg
statement: theorem trailingDegree_neg (p : R[X]) : trailingDegree (-p) = trailingDegree p
proof:
unfold trailingDegree
  rw [support_neg]

@[simp]

name: natTrailingDegree_neg
statement: theorem natTrailingDegree_neg (p : R[X]) : natTrailingDegree (-p) = natTrailingDegree p
proof:
simp [natTrailingDegree]

@[simp]

name: natTrailingDegree_intCast
statement: theorem natTrailingDegree_intCast (n : ℤ) : natTrailingDegree (n : R[X]) = 0
proof:
simp only [← C_eq_intCast, natTrailingDegree_C]

end Ring

section Semiring

variable [Semiring R]

/-- The second-lowest coefficient, or 0 for constants -/

name: nextCoeffUp_C_eq_zero
statement: theorem nextCoeffUp_C_eq_zero (c : R) : nextCoeffUp (C c) = 0
proof:
rw [nextCoeffUp]
  simp

name: eq_X_pow_iff_natDegree_le_natTrailingDegree
statement: theorem eq_X_pow_iff_natDegree_le_natTrailingDegree (h₁ : p.Monic) : p = X ^ p.natDegree ↔ p.natDegree ≤ p.natTrailingDegree
proof:
refine ⟨fun h => ?_, fun h => ?_⟩
  · nontriviality R
    rw [h, natTrailingDegree_X_pow, ← h]
  · ext n
    rw [coeff_X_pow]
    obtain hn | rfl | hn := lt_trichotomy n p.natDegree
    · rw [if_neg hn.ne, coeff_eq_zero_of_lt_natTrailingDegree (hn.trans_le h)]
    · simpa only [if_pos rfl] using h₁.leadingCoeff
    · rw [if_neg hn.ne', coeff_eq_zero_of_natDegree_lt hn]

name: zero_le_degree_iff
statement: theorem zero_le_degree_iff  : 0 ≤ degree p ↔ p ≠ 0
proof: rw [← not_lt, Nat.WithBot.lt_zero_iff, degree_eq_bot]

name: degree_linear
statement: theorem degree_linear (ha : a ≠ 0) : degree (C a * X + C b) = 1
proof: rw [degree_add_eq_left_of_degree_lt <| degree_C_lt_degree_C_mul_X ha, degree_C_mul_X ha]

name: natDegree_linear
statement: theorem natDegree_linear (ha : a ≠ 0) : natDegree (C a * X + C b) = 1
proof:
rw [natDegree_add_C, natDegree_C_mul_X a ha]

@[simp]

name: leadingCoeff_linear
statement: theorem leadingCoeff_linear (ha : a ≠ 0) : leadingCoeff (C a * X + C b) = a
proof:
rw [add_comm, leadingCoeff_add_of_degree_lt (degree_C_lt_degree_C_mul_X ha),
    leadingCoeff_C_mul_X]

name: degree_quadratic_le
statement: theorem degree_quadratic_le  : degree (C a * X ^ 2 + C b * X + C c) ≤ 2
proof:
simpa only [add_assoc] using
    degree_add_le_of_degree_le (degree_C_mul_X_pow_le 2 a)
      (le_trans degree_linear_le <| WithBot.coe_le_coe.mpr one_le_two)

name: degree_linear_lt_degree_C_mul_X_sq
statement: theorem degree_linear_lt_degree_C_mul_X_sq (ha : a ≠ 0) : degree (C b * X + C c) < degree (C a * X ^ 2)
proof:
simpa only [degree_C_mul_X_pow 2 ha] using degree_linear_lt

@[simp]

name: degree_quadratic
statement: theorem degree_quadratic (ha : a ≠ 0) : degree (C a * X ^ 2 + C b * X + C c) = 2
proof:
rw [add_assoc, degree_add_eq_left_of_degree_lt <| degree_linear_lt_degree_C_mul_X_sq ha,
    degree_C_mul_X_pow 2 ha]
  rfl

name: leadingCoeff_quadratic
statement: theorem leadingCoeff_quadratic (ha : a ≠ 0) : leadingCoeff (C a * X ^ 2 + C b * X + C c) = a
proof:
rw [add_assoc, add_comm, leadingCoeff_add_of_degree_lt <| degree_linear_lt_degree_C_mul_X_sq ha,
    leadingCoeff_C_mul_X_pow]

name: degree_cubic_le
statement: theorem degree_cubic_le  : degree (C a * X ^ 3 + C b * X ^ 2 + C c * X + C d) ≤ 3
proof:
simpa only [add_assoc] using
    degree_add_le_of_degree_le (degree_C_mul_X_pow_le 3 a)
      (le_trans degree_quadratic_le <| WithBot.coe_le_coe.mpr <| Nat.le_succ 2)

name: degree_quadratic_lt_degree_C_mul_X_cb
statement: theorem degree_quadratic_lt_degree_C_mul_X_cb (ha : a ≠ 0) : degree (C b * X ^ 2 + C c * X + C d) < degree (C a * X ^ 3)
proof:
simpa only [degree_C_mul_X_pow 3 ha] using degree_quadratic_lt

@[simp]

name: degree_cubic
statement: theorem degree_cubic (ha : a ≠ 0) : degree (C a * X ^ 3 + C b * X ^ 2 + C c * X + C d) = 3
proof:
rw [add_assoc, add_assoc, ← add_assoc (C b * X ^ 2),
    degree_add_eq_left_of_degree_lt <| degree_quadratic_lt_degree_C_mul_X_cb ha,
    degree_C_mul_X_pow 3 ha]
  rfl

name: leadingCoeff_cubic
statement: theorem leadingCoeff_cubic (ha : a ≠ 0) : leadingCoeff (C a * X ^ 3 + C b * X ^ 2 + C c * X + C d) = a
proof:
rw [add_assoc, add_assoc, ← add_assoc (C b * X ^ 2), add_comm,
    leadingCoeff_add_of_degree_lt <| degree_quadratic_lt_degree_C_mul_X_cb ha,
    leadingCoeff_C_mul_X_pow]

end Semiring

end Polynomial

name: monomial_natDegree_leadingCoeff_eq_self
statement: theorem monomial_natDegree_leadingCoeff_eq_self (h : #p.support ≤ 1) : monomial p.natDegree p.leadingCoeff = p
proof:
classical
  rcases card_support_le_one_iff_monomial.1 h with ⟨n, a, rfl⟩
  by_cases ha : a = 0 <;> simp [ha]

name: C_mul_X_pow_eq_self
statement: theorem C_mul_X_pow_eq_self (h : #p.support ≤ 1) : C p.leadingCoeff * X ^ p.natDegree = p
proof:
rw [C_mul_X_pow_eq_monomial, monomial_natDegree_leadingCoeff_eq_self h]

end Semiring

end Polynomial

name: X_smul_of
statement: theorem X_smul_of (m : M) : (X : R[X]) • (of R M a m) = of R M a (a • m)
proof: rw [← of_aeval_smul, aeval_X]

name: of_symm_X_smul
statement: theorem of_symm_X_smul (m : AEval R M a) : (of R M a).symm ((X : R[X]) • m) = a • (of R M a).symm m
proof:
rw [of_symm_smul, aeval_X]

instance instIsScalarTowerOrigPolynomial : IsScalarTower R R[X] <| AEval R M a where
  smul_assoc r f m := by
    apply (of R M a).symm.injective
    rw [of_symm_smul, map_smul, smul_assoc, map_smul, of_symm_smul]

instance instFinitePolynomial [Module.Finite R M] : Module.Finite R[X] <| AEval R M a :=
  Finite.of_restrictScalars_finite R _ _

/-- Construct an `R[X]`-linear map out of `AEval R M a` from an `R`-linear map out of `M`. -/

name: annihilator_eq_ker_aeval
statement: theorem annihilator_eq_ker_aeval  : annihilator R[X] (AEval R M a) = RingHom.ker (aeval a)
proof:
ext p
  simp_rw [mem_annihilator, RingHom.mem_ker]
  change (∀ m : M, aeval a p • m = 0) ↔ _
  exact ⟨fun h ↦ eq_of_smul_eq_smul (α := M) <| by simp [h], fun h ↦ by simp [h]⟩

@[simp]

name: annihilator_top_eq_ker_aeval
statement: theorem annihilator_top_eq_ker_aeval  : (⊤ : Submodule R[X] <| AEval R M a).annihilator = RingHom.ker (aeval a)
proof:
ext p
  simp only [Submodule.mem_annihilator, Submodule.mem_top, forall_true_left, RingHom.mem_ker]
  change (∀ m : M, aeval a p • m = 0) ↔ _
  exact ⟨fun h ↦ eq_of_smul_eq_smul (α := M) <| by simp [h], fun h ↦ by simp [h]⟩

section Submodule

variable (R M)

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
/-- The natural order isomorphism between the two ways to represent invariant submodules. -/
noncomputable def mapSubmodule :
    (Algebra.lsmul R R M a).invtSubmodule ≃o Submodule R[X] (AEval R M a) where
  toFun p :=
    { toAddSubmonoid := (p : Submodule R M).toAddSubmonoid.map (of R M a)
      smul_mem' := by
        rintro f - ⟨m : M, h : m ∈ (p : Submodule R M), rfl⟩
        simp only [AddSubsemigroup.mem_carrier, AddSubmonoid.mem_toSubsemigroup,
          AddSubmonoid.mem_map, Submodule.mem_toAddSubmonoid]
        exact ⟨aeval a f • m, aeval_apply_smul_mem_of_le_comap' h f a p.2, of_aeval_smul a f m⟩ }
  invFun q := ⟨(Submodule.orderIsoMapComap (of R M a)).symm (q.restrictScalars R), fun m hm ↦ by
    simpa [← X_smul_of] using q.smul_mem (X : R[X]) hm⟩
  left_inv p := by ext; simp
  right_inv q := by ext; aesop
  map_rel_iff' {p p'} := ⟨fun h x hx ↦ by aesop (rule_sets := [SetLike!]), fun h x hx ↦ by aesop⟩

@[simp] lemma mem_mapSubmodule_apply {p : (Algebra.lsmul R R M a).invtSubmodule} {m : AEval R M a} :
    m ∈ mapSubmodule R M a p ↔ (of R M a).symm m ∈ (p : Submodule R M) :=
  ⟨fun ⟨_, hm, hm'⟩ ↦ hm'.symm ▸ hm, fun hm ↦ ⟨(of R M a).symm m, hm, rfl⟩⟩

@[simp] lemma mem_mapSubmodule_symm_apply {q : Submodule R[X] (AEval R M a)} {m : M} :
    m ∈ ((mapSubmodule R M a).symm q : Submodule R M) ↔ of R M a m ∈ q :=
  Iff.rfl

variable {R M}
variable (p : Submodule R M) (hp : p ∈ (Algebra.lsmul R R M a).invtSubmodule)

/-- The natural `R`-linear equivalence between the two ways to represent an invariant submodule. -/

name: eval₂_add
statement: theorem eval₂_add  : (p + q).eval₂ f x = p.eval₂ f x + q.eval₂ f x
proof:
simp only [eval₂_eq_sum]
  apply sum_add_index <;> simp [add_mul]

@[simp]

name: eval₂_natCast
statement: theorem eval₂_natCast (n : ℕ) : (n : R[X]).eval₂ f x = n
proof:
induction n with
  | zero => simp only [eval₂_zero, Nat.cast_zero]
  | succ n ih => rw [n.cast_succ, eval₂_add, ih, eval₂_one, n.cast_succ]

@[simp]

name: eval₂_mul_X
statement: theorem eval₂_mul_X  : eval₂ f x (p * X) = eval₂ f x p * x
proof:
refine _root_.trans (eval₂_mul_noncomm _ _ fun k => ?_) (by rw [eval₂_X])
  rcases em (k = 1) with (rfl | hk)
  · simp
  · simp [coeff_X_of_ne_one hk]

@[simp]

name: eval_eq_sum
statement: theorem eval_eq_sum  : p.eval x = p.sum fun e a => a * x ^ e
proof:
rw [eval, eval₂_eq_sum]
  rfl

@[simp]

name: eval_C_mul
statement: theorem eval_C_mul  : (C a * p).eval x = a * p.eval x
proof:
induction p using Polynomial.induction_on' with
  | add p q ph qh => simp only [mul_add, eval_add, ph, qh]
  | monomial n b => simp only [mul_assoc, C_mul_monomial, eval_monomial]

@[simp]

name: eval_mul_C_of_commute
statement: theorem eval_mul_C_of_commute (h : Commute a x) : (p * C a).eval x = p.eval x * a
proof:
rw [eval, eval₂_mul_C'] <;> simp [h]

/-- Polynomial evaluation commutes with `List.sum`. -/

name: comp_X
statement: theorem comp_X  : p.comp X = p
proof:
simp only [comp, eval₂_def, C_mul_X_pow_eq_monomial]
  exact sum_monomial_eq _

@[simp]

name: mul_X_comp
statement: theorem mul_X_comp  : (p * X).comp r = p.comp r * r
proof:
induction p using Polynomial.induction_on' with
  | add p q hp hq => simp only [hp, hq, add_mul, add_comp]
  | monomial n b => simp only [pow_succ, mul_assoc, monomial_mul_X, monomial_comp]

@[simp]

name: C_mul_comp
statement: theorem C_mul_comp  : (C a * p).comp r = C a * p.comp r
proof:
induction p using Polynomial.induction_on' with
  | add p q hp hq => simp [hp, hq, mul_add]
  | monomial n b => simp [mul_assoc]

@[simp]

name: map_mul
statement: theorem map_mul  : (p * q).map f = p.map f * q.map f
proof:
rw [map, eval₂_mul_noncomm]
  exact fun k => (commute_X _).symm

-- `map` is a ring-hom unconditionally, and theoretically the definition could be replaced,
-- but this turns out not to be easy because `p.map f` does not resolve to `Polynomial.map`
-- if `map` is a `RingHom` instead of a plain function; the elaborator does not try to coerce
-- to a function before trying field (dot) notation (this may be technically infeasible);
-- the relevant code is (both lines): https://github.com/leanprover-community/
-- lean/blob/487ac5d7e9b34800502e1ddf3c7c806c01cf9d51/src/frontends/lean/elaborator.cpp#L1876-L1913
/-- `Polynomial.map` as a `RingHom`. -/

name: eval_comp
statement: theorem eval_comp  : (p.comp q).eval x = p.eval (q.eval x)
proof:
induction p using Polynomial.induction_on' with
  | add r s hr hs => simp [add_comp, hr, hs]
  | monomial n a => simp

name: root_mul
statement: theorem root_mul  : IsRoot (p * q) a ↔ IsRoot p a ∨ IsRoot q a
proof: simp_rw [IsRoot, eval_mul, mul_eq_zero]

name: root_X_sub_C
statement: theorem root_X_sub_C  : IsRoot (X - C a) b ↔ a = b
proof:
rw [IsRoot.def, eval_sub, eval_X, eval_C, sub_eq_zero, eq_comm]

@[simp]

name: eval₂_at_zero
statement: theorem eval₂_at_zero  : p.eval₂ f 0 = f (coeff p 0)
proof:
simp +contextual only [eval₂_eq_sum, zero_pow_eq, mul_ite, mul_zero,
    mul_one, sum, Classical.not_not, mem_support_iff, sum_ite_eq', ite_eq_left_iff, map_zero,
    imp_true_iff]

@[simp]

name: coeff_map
statement: theorem coeff_map (n : ℕ) : coeff (p.map f) n = f (coeff p n)
proof:
rw [map, eval₂_def, coeff_sum, sum]
  simp_all

name: hom_eval₂
statement: theorem hom_eval₂ (x : S) : g (p.eval₂ f x) = p.eval₂ (g.comp f) (g x)
proof:
rw [← eval₂_map, eval₂_at_apply, eval_map]

end HomEval₂

end Semiring

section CommSemiring

section Eval

section

variable [Semiring R] {p q : R[X]} {x : R} [Semiring S] (f : R →+* S)

name: map_smul
statement: theorem map_smul (r : R) : (r • p).map f = f r • p.map f
proof:
rw [map, eval₂_smul, RingHom.comp_apply, C_mul']

end Map

end Semiring

end Polynomial

name: degree_map_le
statement: theorem degree_map_le  : degree (p.map f) ≤ degree p
proof:
refine (degree_le_iff_coeff_zero _ _).2 fun m hm => ?_
  rw [degree_lt_iff_coeff_zero] at hm
  simp [hm m le_rfl]

name: int_monic_iff
statement: theorem int_monic_iff  : (P.int R hP).Monic ↔ P.Monic
proof:
rw [Monic, Monic, ← int_leadingCoeff_eq, OneMemClass.coe_eq_one]

@[simp]

name: int_eval₂_eq
statement: theorem int_eval₂_eq (x : L) : eval₂ (algebraMap R L) x (P.int R hP) = aeval x P
proof:
rw [aeval_eq_sum_range, eval₂_eq_sum_range]
  exact Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun n _ => by rw [Algebra.smul_def]; rfl)

end Polynomial

name: op_closure
statement: theorem op_closure (s : Set R) : (closure s).op = closure (MulOpposite.unop ⁻¹' s)
proof:
simp_rw [closure, op_sInf, Set.preimage_setOf_eq, coe_unop]
  congr with a
  exact MulOpposite.unop_surjective.forall

name: unop_closure
statement: theorem unop_closure (s : Set Rᵐᵒᵖ) : (closure s).unop = closure (MulOpposite.op ⁻¹' s)
proof:
rw [← op_inj, op_unop, op_closure]
  simp_rw [Set.preimage_preimage, MulOpposite.op_unop, Set.preimage_id']

/-- Bijection between a subring `S` and its opposite. -/
@[simps!]

name: range_eq_map
statement: theorem range_eq_map (f : R →+* S) : f.range = Subring.map f ⊤
proof:
ext
  simp

name: map_range
statement: theorem map_range  : f.range.map g = (g.comp f).range
proof:
simpa only [range_eq_map] using (⊤ : Subring R).map_map g f

/-- The range of a ring homomorphism is a fintype, if the domain is a fintype.
Note: this instance can form a diamond with `Subtype.fintype` in the
  presence of `Fintype S`. -/
instance fintypeRange [Fintype R] [DecidableEq S] (f : R →+* S) : Fintype (range f) :=
  Set.fintypeRange f

end RingHom

namespace Subring

/-! ## bot -/


instance : Bot (Subring R) :=
  ⟨(Int.castRingHom R).range⟩

instance : Inhabited (Subring R) :=
  ⟨⊥⟩

@[norm_cast]

name: associated_abs_left_iff
statement: theorem associated_abs_left_iff  : Associated |x| y ↔ Associated x y
proof:
obtain h | h := abs_choice x <;>
  simp [h]

@[simp]

name: associated_abs_right_iff
statement: theorem associated_abs_right_iff  : Associated x |y| ↔ Associated x y
proof:
rw [Associated.comm, associated_abs_left_iff, Associated.comm]

alias ⟨_, Associated.abs_left⟩ := associated_abs_left_iff

alias ⟨_, Associated.abs_right⟩ := associated_abs_right_iff

end misc

name: dvd_sub_left
statement: theorem dvd_sub_left (h : a ∣ c) : a ∣ b - c ↔ a ∣ b
proof:
simpa only [← sub_eq_add_neg] using dvd_add_left (dvd_neg.2 h)

/-- If an element `a` divides another element `b` in a ring, `a` divides the difference of `b` and
another element `c` iff `a` divides `c`. -/

name: dvd_sub_right
statement: theorem dvd_sub_right (h : a ∣ b) : a ∣ b - c ↔ a ∣ c
proof: rw [sub_eq_add_neg, dvd_add_right h, dvd_neg]

name: dvd_iff_dvd_of_dvd_sub
statement: theorem dvd_iff_dvd_of_dvd_sub (h : a ∣ b - c) : a ∣ b ↔ a ∣ c
proof: rw [← sub_add_cancel b c, dvd_add_right h]

name: comp_zero
statement: theorem comp_zero (g : β →ₙ+* γ) : g.comp (0 : α →ₙ+* β) = 0
proof:
ext
  simp

@[simp]

name: zero_comp
statement: theorem zero_comp (f : α →ₙ+* β) : (0 : β →ₙ+* γ).comp f = 0
proof:
ext
  rfl

@[simp]

name: toMonoidWithZeroHom_eq_coe
statement: theorem toMonoidWithZeroHom_eq_coe (f : α →+* β) : (f.toMonoidWithZeroHom : α → β) = f
proof:
rfl

@[simp]

name: coe_addMonoidHom_mkRingHomOfMulSelfOfTwoNeZero
statement: theorem coe_addMonoidHom_mkRingHomOfMulSelfOfTwoNeZero (h h_two h_one) : (f.mkRingHomOfMulSelfOfTwoNeZero h h_two h_one : β →+ α) = f
proof:
ext
  rfl

end AddMonoidHom

name: one_eq_closure
statement: theorem one_eq_closure  : (1 : AddSubmonoid R) = closure {1}
proof: rw [closure_singleton_eq, one_eq_mrange]; congr 1; ext; simp

name: closure_mul_closure
statement: theorem closure_mul_closure (S T : Set R) : closure S * closure T = closure (S * T)
proof:
apply le_antisymm
  · refine mul_le.2 fun a ha b hb => ?_
    rw [← AddMonoidHom.mulRight_apply, ← AddSubmonoid.mem_comap]
    refine (closure_le.2 fun a' ha' => ?_) ha
    change b ∈ (closure (S * T)).comap (AddMonoidHom.mulLeft a')
    refine (closure_le.2 fun b' hb' => ?_) hb
    change a' * b' ∈ closure (S * T)
    exact subset_closure (Set.mul_mem_mul ha' hb')
  · rw [closure_le]
    rintro _ ⟨a, ha, b, hb, rfl⟩
    exact mul_mem_mul (subset_closure ha) (subset_closure hb)

name: mul_eq_closure_mul_set
statement: theorem mul_eq_closure_mul_set (M N : AddSubmonoid R) : M * N = closure (M * N : Set R)
proof:
rw [← closure_mul_closure, closure_eq, closure_eq]

@[simp] lemma mul_bot (S : AddSubmonoid R) : S * ⊥ = ⊥ := addSubmonoid_smul_bot S

-- need `zero_smul` to generalize to `SMul`
@[simp]

name: op_closure
statement: theorem op_closure (s : Set R) : (closure s).op = closure (MulOpposite.unop ⁻¹' s)
proof:
simp_rw [closure, op_sInf, Set.preimage_setOf_eq, coe_unop]
  congr with a
  exact MulOpposite.unop_surjective.forall

name: unop_closure
statement: theorem unop_closure (s : Set Rᵐᵒᵖ) : (closure s).unop = closure (MulOpposite.op ⁻¹' s)
proof:
rw [← op_inj, op_unop, op_closure]
  simp_rw [Set.preimage_preimage, MulOpposite.op_unop, Set.preimage_id']

/-- Bijection between a subsemiring `S` and its opposite. -/
@[simps!]

name: rangeS_eq_map
statement: theorem rangeS_eq_map (f : R →+* S) : f.rangeS = (⊤ : Subsemiring R).map f
proof:
ext
  simp

name: map_rangeS
statement: theorem map_rangeS  : f.rangeS.map g = (g.comp f).rangeS
proof:

variable {f} in

name: rangeS_eq_top
statement: theorem rangeS_eq_top  : f.rangeS = ⊤ ↔ Function.Surjective f
proof:

/-- The range of a morphism of semirings is a fintype, if the domain is a fintype.
Note: this instance can form a diamond with `Subtype.fintype` in the
  presence of `Fintype S`. -/
instance fintypeRangeS [Fintype R] [DecidableEq S] (f : R →+* S) : Fintype (rangeS f) :=
  Set.fintypeRange f

instance : Bot (Subsemiring R) :=

instance : Inhabited (Subsemiring R) :=

@[norm_cast]

name: subsemiringClosure_toNonUnitalSubsemiring
statement: theorem subsemiringClosure_toNonUnitalSubsemiring (M : Submonoid R) : M.subsemiringClosure.toNonUnitalSubsemiring = .closure M
proof:
refine Eq.symm (NonUnitalSubsemiring.closure_eq_of_le ?_ fun _ hx ↦ ?_)
  · simp [Submonoid.subsemiringClosure_coe]
  · simp only [Subsemiring.mem_toNonUnitalSubsemiring, subsemiringClosure_mem] at hx
    induction hx using AddSubmonoid.closure_induction <;> aesop

/-- The `Subsemiring` generated by a multiplicative submonoid coincides with the
`Subsemiring.closure` of the submonoid itself . -/

name: subsemiringClosure_eq_closure
statement: theorem subsemiringClosure_eq_closure  : M.subsemiringClosure = Subsemiring.closure (M : Set R)
proof:
ext
  refine
    ⟨fun hx => ?_, fun hx =>
      (Subsemiring.mem_closure.mp hx) M.subsemiringClosure fun s sM => ?_⟩
  <;> rintro - ⟨H1, rfl⟩
  <;> rintro - ⟨H2, rfl⟩
  · exact AddSubmonoid.mem_closure.mp hx H1.toAddSubmonoid H2
  · exact H2 sM

end Submonoid

name: coe_closure_eq

proof: rw [sub_eq_add_neg, p.add_mem_iff_left (p.neg_mem hy)]

name: sub_mem_iff_right
statement: theorem sub_mem_iff_right (hx : x ∈ p) : x - y ∈ p ↔ y ∈ p
proof:

instance addCommGroup : AddCommGroup p := AddSubgroupClass.toAddCommGroup p
