open Monoid Coprod

namespace MulEquiv

section MulOneClass

variable {M N M' N' : Type*} [MulOneClass M] [MulOneClass N] [MulOneClass M']
  [MulOneClass N']

/-- Lift two monoid equivalences `e : M ≃* N` and `e' : M' ≃* N'` to a monoid equivalence
`(M ∗ M') ≃* (N ∗ N')`. -/
@[to_additive (attr := simps! -fullyApplied) /-- Lift two additive monoid
equivalences `e : M ≃+ N` and `e' : M' ≃+ N'` to an additive monoid equivalence
`(AddMonoid.Coprod M M') ≃+ (AddMonoid.Coprod N N')`. -/]

name: minOrder_eq_top
statement: theorem minOrder_eq_top  : minOrder G = ⊤
proof:
simpa [minOrder] using fun _ ↦ not_isOfFinOrder_of_isMulTorsionFree

end Group

section CommGroup
variable [CommGroup G] {s : Subgroup G}

@[to_additive (attr := simp)]

name: minOrder_eq_top_iff
statement: theorem minOrder_eq_top_iff  : minOrder G = ⊤ ↔ IsMulTorsionFree G
proof:
simp [minOrder, isMulTorsionFree_iff_not_isOfFinOrder]

end CommGroup
end Monoid

open AddMonoid AddSubgroup Nat Set

namespace ZMod

@[simp]
protected lemma minOrder {n : ℕ} (hn : n ≠ 0) (hn₁ : n ≠ 1) : minOrder (ZMod n) = n.minFac := by
  have : Fact (1 < n) := ⟨one_lt_iff_ne_zero_and_ne_one.mpr ⟨hn, hn₁⟩⟩
  classical
  have : (↑(n / n.minFac) : ZMod n) ≠ 0 := by
    rw [Ne, ringChar.spec, ringChar.eq (ZMod n) n]
    exact
      not_dvd_of_pos_of_lt (Nat.div_pos (minFac_le hn.bot_lt) n.minFac_pos)
        (div_lt_self hn.bot_lt (minFac_prime hn₁).one_lt)
  refine ((minOrder_le_natCard (zmultiples_eq_bot.not.2 this) <| toFinite _).trans ?_).antisymm <|
    le_minOrder_iff_forall_addSubgroup.2 fun s hs _ ↦ ?_
  · rw [Nat.card_zmultiples, ZMod.addOrderOf_coe _ hn,
      gcd_eq_right (div_dvd_of_dvd n.minFac_dvd), Nat.div_div_self n.minFac_dvd hn]
  · haveI : Nontrivial s := s.bot_or_nontrivial.resolve_left hs
    exact WithTop.coe_le_coe.2 <| minFac_le_of_dvd Finite.one_lt_card <|
      (card_addSubgroup_dvd_card _).trans n.card_zmod.dvd

@[simp]

name: leftInv_leftInv_le
statement: theorem leftInv_leftInv_le  : S.leftInv.leftInv ≤ S
proof:
rintro x ⟨⟨y, z, h₁⟩, h₂ : x * y = 1⟩
  convert z.prop
  rw [← mul_one x, ← h₁, ← mul_assoc, h₂, one_mul]

@[to_additive]

name: leftInv_leftInv_eq
statement: theorem leftInv_leftInv_eq (hS : S ≤ IsUnit.submonoid M) : S.leftInv.leftInv = S
proof:
refine le_antisymm S.leftInv_leftInv_le ?_
  intro x hx
  have : x = ((hS hx).unit⁻¹⁻¹ : Mˣ) := by
    rw [inv_inv (hS hx).unit]
    rfl
  rw [this]
  exact S.leftInv.unit_mem_leftInv _ (S.unit_mem_leftInv _ hx)

/-- The function from `S.leftInv` to `S` sending an element to its right inverse in `S`.
This is a `MonoidHom` when `M` is commutative. -/
@[to_additive
/-- The function from `S.leftAdd` to `S` sending an element to its right additive
inverse in `S`. This is an `AddMonoidHom` when `M` is commutative. -/]
noncomputable def fromLeftInv : S.leftInv → S := fun x ↦ x.prop.choose

@[to_additive (attr := simp)]

name: fromLeftInv_mul
statement: theorem fromLeftInv_mul (x : S.leftInv) : (S.fromLeftInv x : M) * x = 1
proof:
rw [mul_comm, mul_fromLeftInv]

@[to_additive]

name: leftInvEquiv_mul
statement: theorem leftInvEquiv_mul (x : S.leftInv) : (S.leftInvEquiv hS x : M) * x = 1
proof:
simpa only [leftInvEquiv_apply, fromCommLeftInv] using fromLeftInv_mul S x

@[to_additive]

name: mul_leftInvEquiv
statement: theorem mul_leftInvEquiv (x : S.leftInv) : (x : M) * S.leftInvEquiv hS x = 1
proof:
simp only [leftInvEquiv_apply, fromCommLeftInv, mul_fromLeftInv]

@[to_additive (attr := simp)]

name: leftInvEquiv_symm_mul
statement: theorem leftInvEquiv_symm_mul (x : S) : ((S.leftInvEquiv hS).symm x : M) * x = 1
proof:
convert S.mul_leftInvEquiv hS ((S.leftInvEquiv hS).symm x)
  simp

@[to_additive (attr := simp)]

name: mul_leftInvEquiv_symm
statement: theorem mul_leftInvEquiv_symm (x : S) : (x : M) * (S.leftInvEquiv hS).symm x = 1
proof:
convert S.leftInvEquiv_mul hS ((S.leftInvEquiv hS).symm x)
  simp

end CommMonoid

section Group

variable [Group M] (S : Submonoid M)

open scoped Pointwise

@[to_additive]

name: fromLeftInv_eq_inv
statement: theorem fromLeftInv_eq_inv (x : S.leftInv) : (S.fromLeftInv x : M) = (x : M)⁻¹
proof:
rw [← mul_right_inj (x : M), mul_inv_cancel, mul_fromLeftInv]

end Group

section CommGroup

variable [CommGroup M] (S : Submonoid M) (hS : S ≤ IsUnit.submonoid M)

@[to_additive (attr := simp)]

name: leftInvEquiv_symm_eq_inv
statement: theorem leftInvEquiv_symm_eq_inv (x : S) : ((S.leftInvEquiv hS).symm x : M) = (x : M)⁻¹
proof:
rw [← mul_right_inj (x : M), mul_inv_cancel, mul_leftInvEquiv_symm]

end CommGroup

end Submonoid

name: example_0
statement: example  : center.commMonoid.toMonoid = Submonoid.toMonoid (center M)
proof:
with_reducible_and_instances rfl

@[to_additive]

name: rightHom_inl
statement: theorem rightHom_inl (n : N) : S.rightHom (S.inl n) = 1
proof:
rw [← MonoidHom.mem_ker, ← S.range_inl_eq_ker_rightHom, MonoidHom.mem_range]
  exact exists_apply_eq_apply S.inl n

@[to_additive (attr := simp)]

name: rightHom_comp_inl
statement: theorem rightHom_comp_inl  : S.rightHom.comp S.inl = 1
proof:
ext n
  rw [MonoidHom.one_apply, MonoidHom.comp_apply]
  exact S.rightHom_inl n

/-- `E` acts on `N` by conjugation. -/
noncomputable def conjAct : E →* MulAut N where
  toFun e := (MonoidHom.ofInjective S.inl_injective).trans <|
    (MulAut.conjNormal e).trans (MonoidHom.ofInjective S.inl_injective).symm
  map_one' := by
    ext _
    simp only [map_one, MulEquiv.trans_apply, MulAut.one_apply, MulEquiv.symm_apply_apply]
  map_mul' _ _ := by
    ext _
    simp only [map_mul, MulEquiv.trans_apply, MulAut.mul_apply, MulEquiv.apply_symm_apply]

/-- The inclusion and a conjugation commute. -/

name: rightHom_comp_splitting
statement: theorem rightHom_comp_splitting  : S.rightHom.comp s = MonoidHom.id G
proof:
ext g
  simp only [MonoidHom.comp_apply, MonoidHom.id_apply, MonoidHom.coe_coe, rightHom_splitting]

end Splitting

/-- A splitting of an extension `S` is `N`-conjugate to another iff there exists `n : N` such that
the section homomorphism is a conjugate of the other section homomorphism by `S.inl n`. -/
@[to_additive
/-- A splitting of an extension `S` is `N`-conjugate to another iff there exists `n : N` such
that the section homomorphism is a conjugate of the other section homomorphism by `S.inl n`. -/]

name: mul_inv_mem_range_inl
statement: theorem mul_inv_mem_range_inl  : σ g * (σ' g)⁻¹ ∈ S.inl.range
proof:
simp only [S.range_inl_eq_ker_rightHom, MonoidHom.mem_ker, map_mul, map_inv, rightHom_section,
    mul_inv_cancel]

@[to_additive]

name: inv_mul_mem_range_inl
statement: theorem inv_mul_mem_range_inl  : (σ g)⁻¹ * σ' g ∈ S.inl.range
proof:
simp only [S.range_inl_eq_ker_rightHom, MonoidHom.mem_ker, map_mul, map_inv, rightHom_section,
    inv_mul_cancel]

@[to_additive]

name: exists_eq_inl_mul
statement: theorem exists_eq_inl_mul  : ∃ n : N, σ g = S.inl n * σ' g
proof:
obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := mul_inv_mem_range_inl σ σ' g
  exact ⟨n, by rw [hn, inv_mul_cancel_right]⟩

@[to_additive]

name: exists_eq_mul_inl
statement: theorem exists_eq_mul_inl  : ∃ n : N, σ g = σ' g * S.inl n
proof:
obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := inv_mul_mem_range_inl σ' σ g
  exact ⟨n, by rw [hn, mul_inv_cancel_left]⟩

@[to_additive]

name: mul_mul_mul_inv_mem_range_inl
statement: theorem mul_mul_mul_inv_mem_range_inl  : σ g₁ * σ g₂ * (σ (g₁ * g₂))⁻¹ ∈ S.inl.range
proof:
simp only [S.range_inl_eq_ker_rightHom, MonoidHom.mem_ker, map_mul, map_inv, rightHom_section,
    mul_inv_cancel]

@[to_additive]

name: mul_inv_mul_mul_mem_range_inl
statement: theorem mul_inv_mul_mul_mem_range_inl  : (σ (g₁ * g₂))⁻¹ * σ g₁ * σ g₂ ∈ S.inl.range
proof:
simp only [S.range_inl_eq_ker_rightHom, MonoidHom.mem_ker, map_mul, map_inv, rightHom_section,
    mul_assoc, inv_mul_cancel]

@[to_additive]

name: exists_mul_eq_inl_mul_mul
statement: theorem exists_mul_eq_inl_mul_mul  : ∃ n : N, σ (g₁ * g₂) = S.inl n * σ g₁ * σ g₂
proof:
obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := mul_mul_mul_inv_mem_range_inl σ g₁ g₂
  use n⁻¹
  rw [mul_assoc, map_inv, eq_inv_mul_iff_mul_eq, ← eq_mul_inv_iff_mul_eq, hn]

@[to_additive]

name: exists_mul_eq_mul_mul_inl
statement: theorem exists_mul_eq_mul_mul_inl  : ∃ n : N, σ (g₁ * g₂) = σ g₁ * σ g₂ * S.inl n
proof:
obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := mul_inv_mul_mul_mem_range_inl σ g₁ g₂
  use n⁻¹
  rw [map_inv, eq_mul_inv_iff_mul_eq, ← eq_inv_mul_iff_mul_eq, ← mul_assoc, hn]

initialize_simps_projections AddGroupExtension.Section (toFun → apply)
initialize_simps_projections Section (toFun → apply)

/-- The composition of an isomorphism between equivalent group extensions and a section -/
@[to_additive (attr := simps!)
/-- The composition of an isomorphism between equivalent additive group extensions and a section -/]

name: right_splitting
statement: theorem right_splitting (g : G) : (s g).right = g
proof:
rw [← rightHom_eq_right, ← toGroupExtension_rightHom, s.rightHom_splitting]

end SemidirectProduct

name: pow_two
statement: theorem pow_two  : t ^ 2 = 1
proof:
rcases ht with ⟨w, i, rfl⟩
  simp

name: mul_self
statement: theorem mul_self  : t * t = 1
proof:
rcases ht with ⟨w, i, rfl⟩
  simp

name: inv
statement: theorem inv  : t⁻¹ = t
proof:
rcases ht with ⟨w, i, rfl⟩
  simp [mul_assoc]

name: odd_length
statement: theorem odd_length  : Odd (ℓ t)
proof:
suffices cs.lengthParity t = Multiplicative.ofAdd 1 by
    simpa [lengthParity_eq_ofAdd_length, ZMod.natCast_eq_one_iff_odd]
  rcases ht with ⟨w, i, rfl⟩
  simp [lengthParity_simple]

name: length_mul_left_ne
statement: theorem length_mul_left_ne (w : W) : ℓ (w * t) ≠ ℓ w
proof:
suffices cs.lengthParity (w * t) ≠ cs.lengthParity w by
    contrapose this
    simp only [lengthParity_eq_ofAdd_length, this]
  rcases ht with ⟨w, i, rfl⟩
  simp [lengthParity_simple]

name: length_mul_right_ne
statement: theorem length_mul_right_ne (w : W) : ℓ (t * w) ≠ ℓ w
proof:
suffices cs.lengthParity (t * w) ≠ cs.lengthParity w by
    contrapose this
    simp only [lengthParity_eq_ofAdd_length, this]
  rcases ht with ⟨w, i, rfl⟩
  simp [lengthParity_simple]

name: conj
statement: theorem conj (w : W) : cs.IsReflection (w * t * w⁻¹)
proof:
obtain ⟨u, i, rfl⟩ := ht
  use w * u, i
  group

end IsReflection

@[simp]

name: isReflection_conj_iff
statement: theorem isReflection_conj_iff (w t : W) : cs.IsReflection (w * t * w⁻¹) ↔ cs.IsReflection t
proof:
constructor
  · intro h
    simpa [← mul_assoc] using h.conj w⁻¹
  · exact IsReflection.conj (w := w)

/-- The proposition that `t` is a right inversion of `w`; i.e., `t` is a reflection and
$\ell (w t) < \ell(w)$. -/

name: leftInvSeq_eq_reverse_rightInvSeq_reverse
statement: theorem leftInvSeq_eq_reverse_rightInvSeq_reverse (ω : List B) : lis ω = (ris ω.reverse).reverse
proof:
induction ω with
  | nil => simp
  | cons i ω ih =>
    rw [leftInvSeq, reverse_cons, ← concat_eq_append, rightInvSeq_concat, ih]
    simp [map_reverse]

name: rightInvSeq_reverse
statement: theorem rightInvSeq_reverse (ω : List B) : ris (ω.reverse) = (lis ω).reverse
proof: simp [leftInvSeq_eq_reverse_rightInvSeq_reverse]

name: leftInvSeq_reverse
statement: theorem leftInvSeq_reverse (ω : List B) : lis (ω.reverse) = (ris ω).reverse
proof:
simp [leftInvSeq_eq_reverse_rightInvSeq_reverse]

@[simp] theorem length_rightInvSeq (ω : List B) : (ris ω).length = ω.length := by
  induction ω with
  | nil => simp
  | cons i ω ih => simpa [rightInvSeq]

@[simp] theorem length_leftInvSeq (ω : List B) : (lis ω).length = ω.length := by
  simp [leftInvSeq_eq_reverse_rightInvSeq_reverse]

name: prod_rightInvSeq
statement: theorem prod_rightInvSeq (ω : List B) : prod (ris ω) = (π ω)⁻¹
proof:
induction ω with
  | nil => simp
  | cons i ω ih => simp [rightInvSeq, ih, wordProd_cons]

name: prod_leftInvSeq
statement: theorem prod_leftInvSeq (ω : List B) : prod (lis ω) = (π ω)⁻¹
proof:
simp only [leftInvSeq_eq_reverse_rightInvSeq_reverse, prod_reverse_noncomm, inv_inj]
  have : List.map (fun x ↦ x⁻¹) (ris ω.reverse) = ris ω.reverse := calc
    List.map (fun x ↦ x⁻¹) (ris ω.reverse)
    _ = List.map id (ris ω.reverse) := by
        apply List.map_congr_left
        intro t ht
        exact (cs.isReflection_of_mem_rightInvSeq _ ht).inv
    _ = ris ω.reverse := map_id _
  rw [this]
  nth_rw 2 [← reverse_reverse ω]
  rw [wordProd_reverse]
  exact cs.prod_rightInvSeq _

name: exists_word_with_prod
statement: theorem exists_word_with_prod (w : W) : ∃ n ω, n = ω.length ∧ π ω = w
proof:
rcases cs.wordProd_surjective w with ⟨ω, rfl⟩
  use ω.length, ω

open scoped Classical in
/-- The length of `w`; i.e., the minimum number of simple reflections that
must be multiplied to form `w`. -/
@[no_expose] noncomputable def length (w : W) : ℕ := Nat.find (cs.exists_word_with_prod w)

local prefix:100 "ℓ " => cs.length

/-- The proposition that `ω` is reduced; that is, it has minimal length among all words that
represent the same element of `W`. -/

name: exists_isReduced
statement: theorem exists_isReduced (w : W) : ∃ ω : List B, cs.IsReduced ω ∧ w = π ω
proof:
classical
  obtain ⟨ω, hω, rfl⟩ := Nat.find_spec (cs.exists_word_with_prod w)
  exact ⟨ω, hω, rfl⟩

@[deprecated (since := "2026-03-25")] alias exists_reduced_word := exists_isReduced
@[deprecated (since := "2026-03-25")] alias exists_reduced_word' := exists_isReduced

open scoped Classical in

name: length_inv
statement: theorem length_inv (w : W) : ℓ (w⁻¹) = ℓ w
proof:
apply Nat.le_antisymm
  · rcases cs.exists_isReduced w with ⟨ω, hω, rfl⟩
    have := cs.length_wordProd_le ω.reverse
    rwa [wordProd_reverse, length_reverse, ← hω] at this
  · rcases cs.exists_isReduced w⁻¹ with ⟨ω, hω, h'ω⟩
    have := cs.length_wordProd_le ω.reverse
    rwa [wordProd_reverse, length_reverse, ← h'ω, ← hω, inv_inv, ← h'ω] at this

name: length_mul_le
statement: theorem length_mul_le (w₁ w₂ : W) : ℓ (w₁ * w₂) ≤ ℓ w₁ + ℓ w₂
proof:
rcases cs.exists_isReduced w₁ with ⟨ω₁, hω₁, rfl⟩
  rcases cs.exists_isReduced w₂ with ⟨ω₂, hω₂, rfl⟩
  have := cs.length_wordProd_le (ω₁ ++ ω₂)
  simpa [hω₁.eq, hω₂.eq, wordProd_append] using this

name: length_le_length_mul_add_left
statement: theorem length_le_length_mul_add_left (w₁ w₂ : W) : ℓ w₂ ≤ ℓ (w₁ * w₂) + ℓ w₁
proof: simpa [add_comm] using cs.length_mul_le w₁⁻¹ (w₁ * w₂)

name: length_le_length_mul_add_right
statement: theorem length_le_length_mul_add_right (w₁ w₂ : W) : ℓ w₁ ≤ ℓ (w₁ * w₂) + ℓ w₂
proof:
simpa using cs.length_mul_le (w₁ * w₂) w₂⁻¹

@[deprecated length_le_length_mul_add_right (since := "2026-03-25")]

name: length_mul_ge_length_sub_length
statement: theorem length_mul_ge_length_sub_length (w₁ w₂ : W) : ℓ w₁ - ℓ w₂ ≤ ℓ (w₁ * w₂)
proof:
rw [Nat.sub_le_iff_le_add]; exact length_le_length_mul_add_right ..

@[deprecated length_le_length_mul_add_left (since := "2026-03-25")]

name: lengthParity_eq_ofAdd_length
statement: theorem lengthParity_eq_ofAdd_length (w : W) : cs.lengthParity w = Multiplicative.ofAdd (↑(ℓ w))
proof:
rcases cs.exists_isReduced w with ⟨ω, hω, rfl⟩
  rw [hω, wordProd, map_list_prod, List.map_map, lengthParity_comp_simple, map_const',
    prod_replicate, ← ofAdd_nsmul, nsmul_one]

name: length_mul_mod_two
statement: theorem length_mul_mod_two (w₁ w₂ : W) : ℓ (w₁ * w₂) % 2 = (ℓ w₁ + ℓ w₂) % 2
proof:
rw [← ZMod.natCast_eq_natCast_iff', Nat.cast_add]
  simpa only [lengthParity_eq_ofAdd_length, ofAdd_add] using map_mul cs.lengthParity w₁ w₂

@[simp]

name: length_simple
statement: theorem length_simple (i : B) : ℓ (s i) = 1
proof:
apply Nat.le_antisymm
  · simpa using cs.length_wordProd_le [i]
  · by_contra! length_lt_one
    have : cs.lengthParity (s i) = Multiplicative.ofAdd 0 := by
      rw [lengthParity_eq_ofAdd_length, Nat.lt_one_iff.mp length_lt_one, Nat.cast_zero]
    have : Multiplicative.ofAdd (0 : ZMod 2) = Multiplicative.ofAdd 1 :=
      this.symm.trans (cs.lengthParity_simple i)
    contradiction

name: isReduced_reverse_iff
statement: theorem isReduced_reverse_iff (ω : List B) : cs.IsReduced (ω.reverse) ↔ cs.IsReduced ω
proof: simp [IsReduced]

name: simple_mul_simple_self
statement: theorem simple_mul_simple_self (i : B) : s i * s i = 1
proof:
have : (FreeGroup.of i) * (FreeGroup.of i) ∈ M.relationsSet := ⟨(i, i), by simp [relation]⟩
  have : (PresentedGroup.mk _ (FreeGroup.of i * FreeGroup.of i) : M.Group) = 1 :=
    (QuotientGroup.eq_one_iff _).mpr (Subgroup.subset_normalClosure this)
  unfold simple
  rw [← map_mul, PresentedGroup.of, map_mul]
  exact map_mul_eq_one cs.mulEquiv.symm this

@[simp]

name: simple_mul_simple_pow
statement: theorem simple_mul_simple_pow (i i' : B) : (s i * s i') ^ M i i' = 1
proof:
have : (FreeGroup.of i * FreeGroup.of i') ^ M i i' ∈ M.relationsSet := ⟨(i, i'), rfl⟩
  have : (PresentedGroup.mk _ ((FreeGroup.of i * FreeGroup.of i') ^ M i i') : M.Group) = 1 :=
    (QuotientGroup.eq_one_iff _).mpr (Subgroup.subset_normalClosure this)
  unfold simple
  rw [← map_mul, ← map_pow]
  exact (MulEquiv.map_eq_one_iff cs.mulEquiv.symm).mpr this

@[simp] theorem simple_mul_simple_pow' (i i' : B) : (s i' * s i) ^ M i i' = 1 :=
  M.symmetric i' i ▸ cs.simple_mul_simple_pow i' i

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
/-- The simple reflections of `W` generate `W` as a group. -/

name: subgroup_closure_range_simple
statement: theorem subgroup_closure_range_simple  : Subgroup.closure (range cs.simple) = ⊤
proof:
have : cs.simple = cs.mulEquiv.symm ∘ PresentedGroup.of := rfl
  rw [this, Set.range_comp, ← MulEquiv.coe_toMonoidHom, ← MonoidHom.map_closure,
    PresentedGroup.closure_range_of, ← MonoidHom.range_eq_map]
  exact MonoidHom.range_eq_top.2 (MulEquiv.surjective _)

/-- The simple reflections of `W` generate `W` as a monoid. -/

name: submonoid_closure_range_simple
statement: theorem submonoid_closure_range_simple  : Submonoid.closure (range cs.simple) = ⊤
proof:
have : range cs.simple = range cs.simple ∪ (range cs.simple)⁻¹ := by
    simp_rw [inv_range, inv_simple, union_self]
  rw [this, ← Subgroup.closure_toSubmonoid, subgroup_closure_range_simple, Subgroup.top_toSubmonoid]

/-! ### Induction principles for Coxeter systems -/

/-- If `p : W → Prop` holds for all simple reflections, it holds for the identity, and it is
preserved under multiplication, then it holds for all elements of `W`. -/

name: simple_determines_coxeterSystem
statement: theorem simple_determines_coxeterSystem  : Injective (simple : CoxeterSystem M W → B → W)
proof:
intro cs1 cs2 h
  apply CoxeterSystem.ext
  apply MulEquiv.toMonoidHom_injective
  apply cs1.ext_simple
  nth_rw 2 [h]
  simp [simple]

/-! ### Words -/

/-- The product of the simple reflections of `W` corresponding to the indices in `ω`. -/

name: wordProd_reverse
statement: theorem wordProd_reverse (ω : List B) : π (reverse ω) = (π ω)⁻¹
proof:
induction ω with
  | nil => simp
  | cons x ω' ih => simpa [wordProd_cons, wordProd_append] using ih

name: wordProd_surjective
statement: theorem wordProd_surjective  : Surjective cs.wordProd
proof:
intro w
  apply cs.simple_induction_left w
  · use []
    rw [wordProd_nil]
  · rintro _ i ⟨ω, rfl⟩
    use i :: ω
    rw [wordProd_cons]

/-- The word of length `m` that alternates between `i` and `i'`, ending with `i'`. -/

name: wordProd_braidWord_eq
statement: theorem wordProd_braidWord_eq (i i' : B) : π (braidWord M i i') = π (braidWord M i' i)
proof:
have := cs.prod_alternatingWord_eq_prod_alternatingWord_sub i i' (M i i')
    (Nat.le_mul_of_pos_right _ (by simp))
  rw [tsub_eq_of_eq_add (mul_two (M i i'))] at this
  nth_rw 2 [M.symmetric i i'] at this
  exact this

end CoxeterSystem

name: commutator_subset_ker
statement: theorem commutator_subset_ker  : commutator G ≤ f.ker
proof:
rw [commutator_eq_closure, Subgroup.closure_le]
  rintro x ⟨p, q, rfl⟩
  simp [MonoidHom.mem_ker, mul_right_comm (f p) (f q), commutatorElement_def]

/-- If `f : G → A` is a group homomorphism to an abelian group, then `lift f` is the unique map
  from the abelianization of a `G` to `A` that factors through `f`. -/

name: rank_commutator_le_card
statement: theorem rank_commutator_le_card  : Group.rank (_root_.commutator G) ≤ Nat.card (commutatorSet G)
proof:
rw [Subgroup.rank_congr (commutator_eq_closure G)]
  apply Subgroup.rank_closure_finite_le_nat_card

variable [Group.FG G]

instance finiteIndex_center : FiniteIndex (center G) := by
  obtain ⟨S, -, hS⟩ := Group.rank_spec G
  exact ⟨mt (Finite.card_eq_zero_of_embedding (quotientCenterEmbedding hS)) Finite.card_pos.ne'⟩

variable (G) in

name: index_center_le_pow
statement: theorem index_center_le_pow  : (center G).index ≤ Nat.card (commutatorSet G) ^ Group.rank G
proof:
obtain ⟨S, hS1, hS2⟩ := Group.rank_spec G
  rw [← hS1, ← Fintype.card_coe, ← Nat.card_eq_fintype_card, ← Finset.coe_sort_coe, ← Nat.card_fun]
  exact Finite.card_le_of_embedding (quotientCenterEmbedding hS2)

end Subgroup

section commutatorRepresentatives

open Subgroup

name: card_commutatorSet_closureCommutatorRepresentatives
statement: theorem card_commutatorSet_closureCommutatorRepresentatives  : Nat.card (commutatorSet (closureCommutatorRepresentatives G)) = Nat.card (commutatorSet G)
proof:
rw [← image_commutatorSet_closureCommutatorRepresentatives G]
  exact Nat.card_congr (Equiv.Set.image _ _ (subtype_injective _))

name: card_commutator_closureCommutatorRepresentatives
statement: theorem card_commutator_closureCommutatorRepresentatives  : Nat.card (commutator (closureCommutatorRepresentatives G)) = Nat.card (commutator G)
proof:
rw [commutator_eq_closure G, ← image_commutatorSet_closureCommutatorRepresentatives, ←
    MonoidHom.map_closure, ← commutator_eq_closure]
  exact Nat.card_congr (Equiv.Set.image _ _ (subtype_injective _))

variable [Finite (commutatorSet G)]

instance : Finite (commutatorRepresentatives G) := Set.finite_coe_iff.mpr (Set.finite_range _)

instance closureCommutatorRepresentatives_fg : Group.FG (closureCommutatorRepresentatives G) :=
  Group.closure_finite_fg _

variable (G) in

name: rank_closureCommutatorRepresentatives_le
statement: theorem rank_closureCommutatorRepresentatives_le  : Group.rank (closureCommutatorRepresentatives G) ≤ 2 * Nat.card (commutatorSet G)
proof:
rw [two_mul]
  exact
    (Subgroup.rank_closure_finite_le_nat_card _).trans
      ((Set.card_union_le _ _).trans
        (add_le_add ((Finite.card_image_le _).trans (Finite.card_range_le _))
          ((Finite.card_image_le _).trans (Finite.card_range_le _))))

instance : Finite (commutatorSet (closureCommutatorRepresentatives G)) := by
  apply Nat.finite_of_card_ne_zero
  rw [card_commutatorSet_closureCommutatorRepresentatives]
  exact Finite.card_pos.ne'

end commutatorRepresentatives

name: commutatorElement_eq_one_iff_mul_comm
statement: theorem commutatorElement_eq_one_iff_mul_comm  : ⁅g₁, g₂⁆ = 1 ↔ g₁ * g₂ = g₂ * g₁
proof:
rw [commutatorElement_def, mul_inv_eq_one, mul_inv_eq_iff_eq_mul]

@[to_additive]

name: commutatorElement_inv
statement: theorem commutatorElement_inv  : ⁅g₁, g₂⁆⁻¹ = ⁅g₂, g₁⁆
proof:
simp_rw [commutatorElement_def, mul_inv_rev, inv_inv, mul_assoc]

@[to_additive (attr := simp)]

name: map_commutatorElement
statement: theorem map_commutatorElement  : (f ⁅g₁, g₂⁆ : G') = ⁅f g₁, f g₂⁆
proof:
simp_rw [commutatorElement_def, map_mul f, map_inv f]

@[to_additive]

name: conjugate_commutatorElement
statement: theorem conjugate_commutatorElement  : g₃ * ⁅g₁, g₂⁆ * g₃⁻¹ = ⁅g₃ * g₁ * g₃⁻¹, g₃ * g₂ * g₃⁻¹⁆
proof:
simp [mul_assoc, commutatorElement_def]

namespace Subgroup

/-- The commutator of two subgroups `H₁` and `H₂`. -/
@[to_additive /-- The commutator of two additive subgroups `H₁` and `H₂`. -/]
instance commutator : Bracket (Subgroup G) (Subgroup G) :=
  ⟨fun H₁ H₂ => closure { g | ∃ g₁ ∈ H₁, ∃ g₂ ∈ H₂, ⁅g₁, g₂⁆ = g }⟩

@[to_additive]

name: commutator_eq_bot_iff_le_centralizer
statement: theorem commutator_eq_bot_iff_le_centralizer  : ⁅H₁, H₂⁆ = ⊥ ↔ H₁ ≤ centralizer H₂
proof:
rw [eq_bot_iff, commutator_le]
  refine forall_congr' fun p =>
    forall_congr' fun _hp => forall_congr' fun q => forall_congr' fun hq => ?_
  rw [mem_bot, commutatorElement_eq_one_iff_mul_comm, eq_comm]

/-- **The Three Subgroups Lemma** (via the Hall-Witt identity) -/
@[to_additive /-- **The Three Subgroups Lemma** (via the Hall-Witt identity) -/]

name: commutator_top_left_le_iff
statement: theorem commutator_top_left_le_iff  : ⁅(⊤ : Subgroup G), H⁆ ≤ H ↔ H.Normal
proof:
refine ⟨fun hle ↦ ⟨fun h hh g ↦ ?_⟩, fun h ↦ commutator_le_right ⊤ H⟩
  exact (H.mul_mem_cancel_right <| H.inv_mem hh).mp <| commutator_le.mp hle g trivial h hh

@[to_additive]

name: map_commutator
statement: theorem map_commutator (f : G →* G') : map f ⁅H₁, H₂⁆ = ⁅map f H₁, map f H₂⁆
proof:
simp_rw [le_antisymm_iff, map_le_iff_le_comap, commutator_le, mem_comap, map_commutatorElement]
  constructor
  · intro p hp q hq
    exact commutator_mem_commutator (mem_map_of_mem _ hp) (mem_map_of_mem _ hq)
  · rintro _ ⟨p, hp, rfl⟩ _ ⟨q, hq, rfl⟩
    rw [← map_commutatorElement]
    exact mem_map_of_mem _ (commutator_mem_commutator hp hq)

variable {H₁ H₂}

@[to_additive]

name: commutator_prod_prod
statement: theorem commutator_prod_prod (K₁ K₂ : Subgroup G') : ⁅H₁.prod K₁, H₂.prod K₂⁆ = ⁅H₁, H₂⁆.prod ⁅K₁, K₂⁆
proof:
apply le_antisymm
  · rw [commutator_le]
    rintro ⟨p₁, p₂⟩ ⟨hp₁, hp₂⟩ ⟨q₁, q₂⟩ ⟨hq₁, hq₂⟩
    exact ⟨commutator_mem_commutator hp₁ hq₁, commutator_mem_commutator hp₂ hq₂⟩
  · rw [prod_le_iff]
    constructor <;>
      · rw [map_commutator]
        apply commutator_mono <;>
          simp [le_prod_iff, map_map, MonoidHom.fst_comp_inl, MonoidHom.snd_comp_inl,
            MonoidHom.fst_comp_inr, MonoidHom.snd_comp_inr]

/-- The commutator of direct product is contained in the direct product of the commutators.

See `commutator_pi_pi_of_finite` for equality given `Fintype η`.
-/
@[to_additive /-- The commutator of direct product is contained in the direct product of the
commutators. See `commutator_pi_pi_of_finite` for equality given `Fintype η`. -/]

name: commutator_eq_closure
statement: theorem commutator_eq_closure  : commutator G = Subgroup.closure (commutatorSet G)
proof:
simp [commutator, Subgroup.commutator_def, commutatorSet]

@[to_additive]

name: commutator_eq_normalClosure
statement: theorem commutator_eq_normalClosure  : commutator G = Subgroup.normalClosure (commutatorSet G)
proof:
simp [commutator, Subgroup.commutator_def', commutatorSet]

variable {G} in
@[to_additive]

name: commutator_eq_bot_iff_center_eq_top
statement: theorem commutator_eq_bot_iff_center_eq_top  : commutator G = ⊥ ↔ Subgroup.center G = ⊤
proof:
simp [commutator, Subgroup.commutator_eq_bot_iff_le_centralizer]

@[to_additive]

name: commutator_eq_bot_iff
statement: theorem commutator_eq_bot_iff  : commutator G = ⊥ ↔ IsMulCommutative G
proof:
rw [commutator_eq_bot_iff_center_eq_top, center_eq_top_iff]

@[to_additive]

name: commutator_centralizer_commutator_le_center
statement: theorem commutator_centralizer_commutator_le_center  : ⁅centralizer (commutator G : Set G), centralizer (commutator G)⁆ ≤ Subgroup.center G
proof:
rw [← Subgroup.centralizer_univ, ← Subgroup.coe_top, ←
    Subgroup.commutator_eq_bot_iff_le_centralizer]
  suffices ⁅⁅⊤, centralizer (commutator G : Set G)⁆, centralizer (commutator G : Set G)⁆ = ⊥ by
    refine Subgroup.commutator_commutator_eq_bot_of_rotate ?_ this
    rwa [Subgroup.commutator_comm (centralizer (commutator G : Set G))]
  rw [Subgroup.commutator_comm, Subgroup.commutator_eq_bot_iff_le_centralizer]
  exact Set.centralizer_subset (Subgroup.commutator_mono le_top le_top)

/-- If `g` is conjugate to `g ^ 2`, then `g` is a commutator -/
@[to_additive /-- If g is conjugate to `2 • g`, then `g` is an additive commutator -/]

name: normalCore_eq_ker
statement: theorem normalCore_eq_ker  : H.normalCore = (MulAction.toPermHom G (G ⧸ H)).ker
proof:
apply le_antisymm
  · intro g hg
    apply Equiv.Perm.ext
    refine fun q ↦ QuotientGroup.induction_on q ?_
    refine fun g' => (MulAction.Quotient.smul_mk H g g').trans (QuotientGroup.eq.mpr ?_)
    rw [smul_eq_mul, mul_inv_rev, ← inv_inv g', inv_inv]
    exact H.normalCore.inv_mem hg g'⁻¹
  · refine (Subgroup.normal_le_normalCore.mpr fun g hg => ?_)
    rw [← H.inv_mem_iff, ← mul_one g⁻¹, ← QuotientGroup.eq, ← mul_one g]
    exact (MulAction.Quotient.smul_mk H g 1).symm.trans (Equiv.Perm.ext_iff.mp hg (1 : G))

open QuotientGroup

/-- Cosets of the centralizer of an element embed into the set of commutators. -/
noncomputable def quotientCentralizerEmbedding (g : G) :
    G ⧸ centralizer {g} ↪ commutatorSet G :=
  ((MulAction.orbitEquivQuotientStabilizer (ConjAct G) g).trans
            (quotientEquivOfEq (ConjAct.stabilizer_eq_centralizer g))).symm.toEmbedding.trans
    ⟨fun x =>
      ⟨x * g⁻¹,
        let ⟨_, x, rfl⟩ := x
        ⟨x, g, rfl⟩⟩,
      fun _ _ => Subtype.ext ∘ mul_right_cancel ∘ Subtype.ext_iff.mp⟩

name: is_one_preprimitive_iff
statement: theorem is_one_preprimitive_iff  : IsMultiplyPreprimitive M α 1 ↔ IsPreprimitive M α
proof:
constructor
  · intro H1
    rw [← isPreprimitive_of_fixingSubgroup_empty_iff]
    apply H1.isPreprimitive_ofFixingSubgroup (by simp)
  · intro h
    rw [isMultiplyPreprimitive_iff]
    constructor
    · exact is_one_pretransitive_iff.mpr h.toIsPretransitive
    · simpa using isPreprimitive_of_fixingSubgroup_empty_iff.mpr h

/-- The action of `stabilizer M a` is one-less preprimitive. -/
@[to_additive /-- The action of `stabilizer M a` is one-less preprimitive. -/]

name: id_apply
statement: theorem id_apply (x : A) : MulDistribMulActionHom.id M x = x
proof:
rfl

variable {M C ψ χ}

instance _root_.DistriMulActionHom.instZero {A : Type*} [AddMonoid A] [DistribMulAction M A]
    {B : Type*} [AddMonoid B] [DistribMulAction N B] : Zero (A →ₑ+[φ] B) :=
  ⟨{ (0 : A →+ B) with map_smul' := fun m _ => by simp }⟩

@[to_additive (dont_translate := M)]
instance : One (A →*[M] A) :=
  ⟨MulDistribMulActionHom.id M⟩

@[simp]

name: isPreprimitive_of_is_two_pretransitive
statement: theorem isPreprimitive_of_is_two_pretransitive (h2 : IsMultiplyPretransitive G α 2) : IsPreprimitive G α
proof:
have : IsPretransitive G α := isPretransitive_of_is_two_pretransitive
  apply IsPreprimitive.mk
  intro B hB
  rcases B.subsingleton_or_nontrivial with h | h
  · left
    exact h
  · right
    obtain ⟨a, ha, b, hb, h⟩ := h
    rw [← top_eq_univ, eq_top_iff]
    intro c _
    by_cases h' : a = c
    · rw [← h']; exact ha
    · rw [is_two_pretransitive_iff] at h2
      obtain ⟨g, hga, hgb⟩ := h2 h h'
      rw [MulAction.isBlock_iff_smul_eq_of_mem] at hB
      rw [← hB (g := g) ha (by rw [hga]; exact ha), ← hgb]
      exact smul_mem_smul_set hb

end Two

section Higher

variable (G α) in
/-- The natural equivariant map from `n ↪ α` to `m ↪ α` given by an embedding
`e : m ↪ n`. -/
@[to_additive
/-- The natural equivariant map from `n ↪ α` to `m ↪ α` given by an embedding `e : m ↪ n`. -/]

name: isMultiplyPretransitive
statement: theorem isMultiplyPretransitive (n : ℕ) : IsMultiplyPretransitive (Perm α) α n
proof:
rw [isMultiplyPretransitive_iff]
  exact exists_smul_eq_embedding

/-- The action of the permutation group of `α` on `α` is preprimitive -/
instance : IsPreprimitive (Perm α) α :=
  isPreprimitive_of_is_two_pretransitive (isMultiplyPretransitive _ _)

-- This is optimal, `AlternatingGroup α` is `Nat.card α - 2`-pretransitive.
/-- A subgroup of `Perm α` is `⊤` if(f) it is `(Nat.card α - 1)`-pretransitive. -/

name: isMultiplyPretransitive
statement: theorem isMultiplyPretransitive  : IsMultiplyPretransitive (alternatingGroup α) α (Nat.card α - 2)
proof:
rcases lt_or_ge (Nat.card α) 2 with h2 | h2
  · rw [Nat.sub_eq_zero_of_le (le_of_lt h2)]
    apply is_zero_pretransitive
  have h2le : Nat.card α - 2 ≤ Nat.card α := sub_le (Nat.card α) 2
  have := Equiv.Perm.isMultiplyPretransitive α (Nat.card α)
  have : IsMultiplyPretransitive (Equiv.Perm α) α (Nat.card α - 2) :=
    MulAction.isMultiplyPretransitive_of_le h2le le_rfl
  refine ⟨fun x y ↦ ?_⟩
  obtain ⟨g, hg⟩ := exists_smul_eq (Equiv.Perm α) x y
  rcases Int.units_eq_one_or (Equiv.Perm.sign g) with h | h
  · exact ⟨⟨g, h⟩, hg⟩
  · have : (Finset.univ.image x)ᶜ.card = 2 := by
      rw [Finset.card_compl, Finset.univ.card_image_of_injective (by exact x.2), Finset.card_univ,
        ← Nat.card_eq_fintype_card, Fintype.card_fin, tsub_tsub_cancel_of_le h2]
    obtain ⟨a, b, hab, hs⟩ := Finset.card_eq_two.mp this
    refine ⟨⟨g * Equiv.swap a b, by simp [h, hab]⟩, ?_⟩
    ext i
    have h : x i ∈ Finset.univ.image x := Finset.mem_image.mpr ⟨i, Finset.mem_univ i, rfl⟩
    rw [← Finset.notMem_compl, hs, Finset.mem_insert, Finset.mem_singleton, not_or] at h
    simp [Equiv.swap_apply_of_ne_of_ne h.1 h.2, ← hg]

/-- A subgroup of `Equiv.Perm α` which is (card α - 2)-pretransitive
contains `alternatingGroup α`. -/

name: isPretransitive_of_three_le_card
statement: theorem isPretransitive_of_three_le_card (h : 3 ≤ Nat.card α) : IsPretransitive (alternatingGroup α) α
proof:
rw [← is_one_pretransitive_iff]
  letI := isMultiplyPretransitive α
  apply isMultiplyPretransitive_of_le (n := Nat.card α - 2) _ (sub_le _ _)
  rwa [← add_le_add_iff_right 2, Nat.sub_add_cancel (le_trans (by norm_num) h)]

/-- The action of the alternating group has trivial blocks.

This holds for any `α`, even when `Nat.card α ≤ 2` and the action
is not preprimitive, because it is not pretransitive. -/

name: card_comm_eq_card_conjClasses_mul_card
statement: theorem card_comm_eq_card_conjClasses_mul_card (G : Type*) : Nat.card { p : G × G // Commute p.1 p.2 } = Nat.card (ConjClasses G) * Nat.card G
proof:
classical
  rcases fintypeOrInfinite G; swap
  · rw [mul_comm, Nat.card_eq_zero_of_infinite, Nat.card_eq_zero_of_infinite, zero_mul]
  simp only [Nat.card_eq_fintype_card]
  calc card { p : G × G // Commute p.1 p.2 }
      _ = card ((a : G) × { b // Commute a b }) :=
            card_congr (Equiv.subtypeProdEquivSigmaSubtype Commute)
      _ = ∑ i, card { b // Commute i b } := card_sigma
      _ = ∑ x, card (MulAction.fixedBy G x) :=
            sum_equiv ConjAct.toConjAct.toEquiv (fun a ↦ card { b // Commute a b })
              (fun g ↦ card (MulAction.fixedBy G g))
              fun g ↦ card_congr' <| congr_arg _ <| funext fun h ↦ mul_inv_eq_iff_eq_mul.symm.eq
      _ = card (Quotient (MulAction.orbitRel (ConjAct G) G)) * card (ConjAct G) :=
             MulAction.sum_card_fixedBy_eq_card_orbits_mul_card_group _ _
      _ = card (ConjClasses G) * card G := by
             congr 1; apply card_congr'; congr; ext
             exact (Setoid.comm' _).trans isConj_iff.symm

name: fixedBy_inv
statement: theorem fixedBy_inv (g : G) : fixedBy α g⁻¹ = fixedBy α g
proof:
ext
  rw [mem_fixedBy, mem_fixedBy, inv_smul_eq_iff, eq_comm]

@[to_additive]

name: fixedBy_mul
statement: theorem fixedBy_mul (m₁ m₂ : M) : fixedBy α m₁ ∩ fixedBy α m₂ ⊆ fixedBy α (m₁ * m₂)
proof:
intro a ⟨h₁, h₂⟩
  rw [mem_fixedBy, mul_smul, h₂, h₁]

variable (α) in
@[to_additive]

name: smul_fixedBy
statement: theorem smul_fixedBy (g h : G) : h • fixedBy α g = fixedBy α (h * g * h⁻¹)
proof:
ext a
  simp_rw [Set.mem_smul_set_iff_inv_smul_mem, mem_fixedBy, mul_smul, smul_eq_iff_eq_inv_smul h]

name: toConjAct_inv_smul
statement: theorem toConjAct_inv_smul (g h : G) : toConjAct g⁻¹ • h = g⁻¹ * h * g
proof:
rw [toConjAct_smul, inv_inv]

-- todo: this file is not in good order; I will refactor this after the PR

instance : MulDistribMulAction (ConjAct G) G where
  smul_mul := by simp [smul_def]
  smul_one := by simp [smul_def]
  one_smul := by simp [smul_def]
  mul_smul := by simp [smul_def, mul_assoc]

instance smulCommClass [SMul α G] [SMulCommClass α G G] [IsScalarTower α G G] :
    SMulCommClass α (ConjAct G) G where
  smul_comm a ug g := by rw [smul_def, smul_def, mul_smul_comm, smul_mul_assoc]

instance smulCommClass' [SMul α G] [SMulCommClass G α G] [IsScalarTower α G G] :
    SMulCommClass (ConjAct G) α G :=
  haveI := SMulCommClass.symm G α G
  SMulCommClass.symm _ _ _

name: fixedPoints_eq_center
statement: theorem fixedPoints_eq_center  : fixedPoints (ConjAct G) G = center G
proof:
ext x
  simp [mem_center_iff, smul_def, mul_inv_eq_iff_eq_mul]

@[simp]

name: orbit_eq_carrier_conjClasses
statement: theorem orbit_eq_carrier_conjClasses (g : G) : orbit (ConjAct G) g = (ConjClasses.mk g).carrier
proof:
ext h
  rw [ConjClasses.mem_carrier_iff_mk_eq, ConjClasses.mk_eq_mk_iff_isConj, mem_orbit_conjAct]

name: orbitRel_of_subMul
statement: theorem orbitRel_of_subMul (p : SubMulAction R M) : MulAction.orbitRel R p = (MulAction.orbitRel R M).comap Subtype.val
proof:
refine Setoid.ext_iff.2 (fun x y ↦ ?_)
  rw [Setoid.comap_rel]
  exact mem_orbit_subMul_iff

/-- Stabilizers in group SubMulAction coincide with stabilizers in the ambient space -/
@[to_additive]

name: neg_mem
statement: theorem neg_mem (hx : x ∈ p) : -x ∈ p
proof:
rw [← neg_one_smul R]
  exact p.smul_mem _ hx

@[simp]

name: image_inclusion
statement: theorem image_inclusion (s : SubMulAction M α) : Set.range s.inclusion = s.carrier
proof:
rw [inclusion.coe_eq]
  exact Subtype.range_coe

@[to_additive]

name: fixedPoints_of_subsingleton
statement: theorem fixedPoints_of_subsingleton  : fixedPoints G α = .univ
proof:
apply Set.eq_univ_of_forall
  simp only [mem_fixedPoints]
  intro x hx
  apply Subsingleton.elim ..

/-- If a group acts nontrivially, then the type is nontrivial -/
@[to_additive /-- If a subgroup acts nontrivially, then the type is nontrivial. -/]

name: orbitRel_subgroupOf
statement: theorem orbitRel_subgroupOf (H K : Subgroup G) : orbitRel (H.subgroupOf K) α = orbitRel (H ⊓ K : Subgroup G) α
proof:
rw [← Subgroup.subgroupOf_map_subtype]
  ext x
  simp_rw [orbitRel_apply]
  refine ⟨fun h ↦ ?_, fun h ↦ ?_⟩
  · rcases h with ⟨⟨gv, gp⟩, rfl⟩
    simp only
    refine mem_orbit _ (⟨gv, ?_⟩ : Subgroup.map K.subtype (H.subgroupOf K))
    simpa using gp
  · rcases h with ⟨⟨gv, gp⟩, rfl⟩
    simp only
    simp only [Subgroup.subgroupOf_map_subtype, Subgroup.mem_inf] at gp
    refine mem_orbit _ (⟨⟨gv, ?_⟩, ?_⟩ : H.subgroupOf K)
    · exact gp.2
    · simp only [Subgroup.mem_subgroupOf]
      exact gp.1

variable (G α)

/-- An action is pretransitive if and only if the quotient by `MulAction.orbitRel` is a
subsingleton. -/
@[to_additive /-- An additive action is pretransitive if and only if the quotient by
`AddAction.orbitRel` is a subsingleton. -/]

name: pretransitive_iff_subsingleton_quotient
statement: theorem pretransitive_iff_subsingleton_quotient  : IsPretransitive G α ↔ Subsingleton (orbitRel.Quotient G α)
proof:
refine ⟨fun _ ↦ ⟨fun a b ↦ ?_⟩, fun _ ↦ ⟨fun a b ↦ ?_⟩⟩
  · refine Quot.inductionOn a (fun x ↦ ?_)
    exact Quot.inductionOn b (fun y ↦ Quot.sound <| exists_smul_eq G y x)
  · have h : Quotient.mk (orbitRel G α) b = ⟦a⟧ := Subsingleton.elim _ _
    exact Quotient.eq''.mp h

/-- If `α` is non-empty, an action is pretransitive if and only if the quotient has exactly one
element. -/
@[to_additive /-- If `α` is non-empty, an additive action is pretransitive if and only if the
quotient has exactly one element. -/]

name: pretransitive_iff_unique_quotient_of_nonempty
statement: theorem pretransitive_iff_unique_quotient_of_nonempty  : IsPretransitive G α ↔ Nonempty (Unique <| orbitRel.Quotient G α)
proof:
rw [unique_iff_subsingleton_and_nonempty, pretransitive_iff_subsingleton_quotient, iff_self_and]
  exact fun _ ↦ (nonempty_quotient_iff _).mpr inferInstance

variable {G α}

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
@[to_additive]
instance (x : orbitRel.Quotient G α) : IsPretransitive G x.orbit where
  exists_smul_eq := by
    induction x using Quotient.inductionOn'
    rintro ⟨y, yh⟩ ⟨z, zh⟩
    rw [orbitRel.Quotient.mem_orbit, Quotient.eq''] at yh zh
    rcases yh with ⟨g, rfl⟩
    rcases zh with ⟨h, rfl⟩
    refine ⟨h * g⁻¹, ?_⟩
    ext
    simp [mul_smul]

@[to_additive]

name: stabilizerEquivStabilizer_one
statement: theorem stabilizerEquivStabilizer_one  : stabilizerEquivStabilizer (one_smul G a).symm = MulEquiv.refl (stabilizer G a)
proof: ext; simp [stabilizerEquivStabilizer_apply]
