statement: theorem dualTensorHomEquivOfBasis_symm_cancel_left (x : Module.Dual R M ⊗[R] N) : (dualTensorHomEquivOfBasis b).symm (dualTensorHom R M N x) = x
proof:
rw [← dualTensorHomEquivOfBasis_apply b,
    LinearEquiv.symm_apply_apply <| dualTensorHomEquivOfBasis (N := N) b]

@[simp]

name: dualTensorHomEquivOfBasis_symm_cancel_right
statement: theorem dualTensorHomEquivOfBasis_symm_cancel_right (x : M →ₗ[R] N) : dualTensorHom R M N ((dualTensorHomEquivOfBasis b).symm x) = x
proof:
rw [← dualTensorHomEquivOfBasis_apply b, LinearEquiv.apply_symm_apply]

variable (R M N P Q)
variable [Module.Free R M] [Module.Finite R M]

/-- If `M` is finite free, the natural map $M^* ⊗ N → Hom(M, N)$ is an
equivalence. -/
@[simp]
noncomputable def dualTensorHomEquiv : Module.Dual R M ⊗[R] N ≃ₗ[R] M →ₗ[R] N :=
  dualTensorHomEquivOfBasis (Module.Free.chooseBasis R M)

end CommSemiring

end Contraction

section HomTensorHom

open TensorProduct

open Module TensorProduct LinearMap

section CommSemiring
variable [CommSemiring R]
variable [AddCommMonoid M] [AddCommMonoid N] [AddCommMonoid P] [AddCommMonoid Q]
variable [Module R M] [Module R N] [Module R P] [Module R Q]
variable [Free R M] [Module.Finite R M] [Free R N] [Module.Finite R N]

/-- When `M` is a finite free module, the map `lTensorHomToHomLTensor` is an equivalence. Note
that `lTensorHomEquivHomLTensor` is not defined directly in terms of
`lTensorHomToHomLTensor`, but the equivalence between the two is given by
`lTensorHomEquivHomLTensor_toLinearMap` and `lTensorHomEquivHomLTensor_apply`. -/
noncomputable def lTensorHomEquivHomLTensor : P ⊗[R] (M →ₗ[R] Q) ≃ₗ[R] M →ₗ[R] P ⊗[R] Q :=
  congr (LinearEquiv.refl R P) (dualTensorHomEquiv R M Q).symm ≪≫ₗ
      TensorProduct.leftComm R P _ Q ≪≫ₗ
    dualTensorHomEquiv R M _

/-- When `M` is a finite free module, the map `rTensorHomToHomRTensor` is an equivalence. Note
that `rTensorHomEquivHomRTensor` is not defined directly in terms of
`rTensorHomToHomRTensor`, but the equivalence between the two is given by
`rTensorHomEquivHomRTensor_toLinearMap` and `rTensorHomEquivHomRTensor_apply`. -/
noncomputable def rTensorHomEquivHomRTensor : (M →ₗ[R] P) ⊗[R] Q ≃ₗ[R] M →ₗ[R] P ⊗[R] Q :=
  congr (dualTensorHomEquiv R M P).symm (LinearEquiv.refl R Q) ≪≫ₗ TensorProduct.assoc R _ P Q ≪≫ₗ
    dualTensorHomEquiv R M _

attribute [-ext] AlgebraTensorModule.curry_injective in
@[simp]

name: lTensorHomEquivHomLTensor_toLinearMap
statement: theorem lTensorHomEquivHomLTensor_toLinearMap  : (lTensorHomEquivHomLTensor R M P Q).toLinearMap = lTensorHomToHomLTensor (.id R) M P Q
proof:
let e := congr (LinearEquiv.refl R P) (dualTensorHomEquiv R M Q)
  have h : Function.Surjective e.toLinearMap := e.surjective
  refine (cancel_right h).1 ?_
  ext f q m
  simp only [e, lTensorHomEquivHomLTensor, dualTensorHomEquiv, LinearEquiv.comp_coe, compr₂ₛₗ_apply,
    mk_apply, LinearEquiv.coe_coe, LinearEquiv.trans_apply, congr_tmul, LinearEquiv.refl_apply,
    dualTensorHomEquivOfBasis_apply, dualTensorHomEquivOfBasis_symm_cancel_left, leftComm_tmul,
    dualTensorHom_apply, coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, lTensorHomToHomLTensor_apply, tmul_smul]

attribute [-ext] AlgebraTensorModule.curry_injective in
@[simp]

name: rTensorHomEquivHomRTensor_toLinearMap
statement: theorem rTensorHomEquivHomRTensor_toLinearMap  : (rTensorHomEquivHomRTensor R M P Q).toLinearMap = rTensorHomToHomRTensor (.id R) M P Q
proof:
let e := congr (dualTensorHomEquiv R M P) (LinearEquiv.refl R Q)
  have h : Function.Surjective e.toLinearMap := e.surjective
  refine (cancel_right h).1 ?_
  ext f p q m
  simp only [e, rTensorHomEquivHomRTensor, dualTensorHomEquiv, compr₂ₛₗ_apply, mk_apply, coe_comp,
    LinearEquiv.coe_toLinearMap, Function.comp_apply,
    dualTensorHomEquivOfBasis_apply, LinearEquiv.trans_apply, congr_tmul,
    dualTensorHomEquivOfBasis_symm_cancel_left, LinearEquiv.refl_apply, assoc_tmul,
    dualTensorHom_apply, rTensorHomToHomRTensor_apply, smul_tmul']

variable {R M N P Q}

@[simp]

name: homTensorHomEquiv_toLinearMap
statement: theorem homTensorHomEquiv_toLinearMap  : (homTensorHomEquiv R M N P Q).toLinearMap = homTensorHomMap (.id R) M N P Q
proof:
ext m n
  simp only [homTensorHomEquiv, compr₂ₛₗ_apply, mk_apply, LinearEquiv.coe_toLinearMap,
    LinearEquiv.trans_apply, lift.equiv_apply, LinearEquiv.arrowCongr_apply, LinearEquiv.refl_symm,
    LinearEquiv.refl_apply, rTensorHomEquivHomRTensor_apply, lTensorHomEquivHomLTensor_apply,
    lTensorHomToHomLTensor_apply, rTensorHomToHomRTensor_apply, homTensorHomMap_apply,
    map_tmul]

variable {R M N P Q}

@[simp]

name: mem_unitaryGroup_iff
statement: theorem mem_unitaryGroup_iff  : A ∈ Matrix.unitaryGroup n α ↔ A * star A = 1
proof:
refine ⟨And.right, fun hA => ⟨?_, hA⟩⟩
  simpa only [mul_eq_one_comm] using hA

name: transpose_inv_eq_map_star
statement: theorem transpose_inv_eq_map_star (U : unitaryGroup n α) : (UnitaryGroup.transpose U)⁻¹ = map_star U
proof:
simp [← map_star_inv_eq_transpose]

end UnitaryGroup

section specialUnitaryGroup

variable (n) (α)

/-- `Matrix.specialUnitaryGroup` is the group of unitary `n` by `n` matrices where the determinant
is 1. (This definition is only correct if 2 is invertible.) -/

name: det_eq_one
statement: theorem det_eq_one (u : SpecialLinearGroup R V) : LinearMap.det (u : V →ₗ[R] V) = 1
proof:
simp [← LinearEquiv.coe_det, u.prop]

/-- The coercion from `SpecialLinearGroup R V` to the function type `V → V` -/
instance : CoeFun (SpecialLinearGroup R V) (fun _ ↦ V → V) where
  coe u x := u.val x

name: ext_iff
statement: theorem ext_iff (u v : SpecialLinearGroup R V) : u = v ↔ ∀ x : V, u x = v x
proof:
simp only [← Subtype.coe_inj, LinearEquiv.ext_iff]

@[ext]

name: congr_linearEquiv_refl
statement: theorem congr_linearEquiv_refl  : congr_linearEquiv (LinearEquiv.refl R V) = MulEquiv.refl _
proof:
rfl

end SpecialLinearGroup

namespace Matrix.SpecialLinearGroup

variable {n : Type*} [Fintype n] [DecidableEq n] (b : Module.Basis n R V)

/-- The canonical isomorphism from `SL(n, R)` to the special linear group of the module `n → R`. -/

name: center_eq_bot_of_finrank_le_one
statement: theorem center_eq_bot_of_finrank_le_one (h : Module.finrank R V ≤ 1) : Subgroup.center (SpecialLinearGroup R V) = ⊥
proof:
nontriviality R
  let b := Module.Free.chooseBasis R V
  have : Subsingleton (Module.Free.ChooseBasisIndex R V) := by
    rwa [← Finite.card_le_one_iff_subsingleton,
      Nat.card_eq_fintype_card, ← Module.finrank_eq_card_basis b]
  have : Subsingleton (Subgroup.center
    (Matrix.SpecialLinearGroup (Module.Free.ChooseBasisIndex R V) R)) := by
    infer_instance
  rw [Equiv.subsingleton_congr
    (Subgroup.centerCongr (Matrix.SpecialLinearGroup.toLin_equiv b)).toEquiv] at this
  exact Subgroup.eq_bot_of_subsingleton _

name: preReflection_apply
statement: theorem preReflection_apply  : preReflection x f y = y - (f y) • x
proof:
simp [preReflection]

variable {x f}

name: preReflection_apply_self
statement: theorem preReflection_apply_self (h : f x = 2) : preReflection x f x = -x
proof: rw [preReflection_apply, h, two_smul]; abel

name: inverse_domain
statement: theorem inverse_domain  : (inverse f).domain = LinearMap.range f.toFun
proof:
rw [inverse, Submodule.toLinearPMap_domain, ← graph_map_snd_eq_range,
    ← LinearEquiv.fst_comp_prodComm, Submodule.map_comp]

variable (hf : f.toFun.ker = ⊥)
include hf

/-- The graph of the inverse generates a `LinearPMap`. -/

name: inverse_graph
statement: theorem inverse_graph  : (inverse f).graph = f.graph.map (LinearEquiv.prodComm R E F : (E × F) →ₗ[R] (F × E))
proof: rw [inverse, Submodule.toLinearPMap_graph_eq _ (mem_inverse_graph_snd_eq_zero hf)]

name: inverse_range
statement: theorem inverse_range  : LinearMap.range (inverse f).toFun = f.domain
proof:
rw [inverse, Submodule.toLinearPMap_range _ (mem_inverse_graph_snd_eq_zero hf),
    ← graph_map_fst_eq_domain, ← LinearEquiv.snd_comp_prodComm, Submodule.map_comp]

name: mem_inverse_graph
statement: theorem mem_inverse_graph (x : f.domain) : (f x, (x : E)) ∈ (inverse f).graph
proof:
simp only [inverse_graph hf, Submodule.mem_map, mem_graph_iff, Subtype.exists, exists_and_left,
    exists_eq_left, LinearEquiv.coe_coe, LinearEquiv.prodComm_apply, Prod.exists, Prod.swap_prod_mk,
    Prod.mk.injEq]
  exact ⟨(x : E), f x, ⟨x.2, Eq.refl _⟩, Eq.refl _, Eq.refl _⟩

name: range_projection
statement: theorem range_projection (hpq : IsCompl p q) : range (p.projection q hpq) = p
proof: simp [projection, range_comp]

name: ker_projection
statement: theorem ker_projection (hpq : IsCompl p q) : ker (p.projection q hpq) = q
proof: simp [projection, ker_comp]

name: linearProjOfIsCompl_apply_left
statement: theorem linearProjOfIsCompl_apply_left (x : F) : linearProjOfIsCompl q i hi h (i x) = x
proof:
obtain ⟨ix, rfl⟩ := (LinearEquiv.ofInjective i hi).symm.surjective x
  simp [linearProjOfIsCompl]

name: linearProjOfIsCompl_apply_right
statement: theorem linearProjOfIsCompl_apply_right (x : q) : linearProjOfIsCompl q i hi h x = 0
proof:
simp [LinearMap.linearProjOfIsCompl]

@[simp]

name: ker_linearProjOfIsCompl
statement: theorem ker_linearProjOfIsCompl  : ker (linearProjOfIsCompl q i hi h) = q
proof:
simp [LinearMap.linearProjOfIsCompl]

end

/-- Given linear maps `φ` and `ψ` from complement submodules, `LinearMap.ofIsCompl` is
the induced linear map over the entire module. -/

name: ofIsCompl_subtype_zero_eq
statement: theorem ofIsCompl_subtype_zero_eq (hpq : IsCompl p q) : ofIsCompl hpq p.subtype 0 = p.projection q hpq
proof: simp [ofIsCompl_eq_add, projection]

name: isProj_range_iff_isIdempotentElem
statement: theorem isProj_range_iff_isIdempotentElem (f : M →ₗ[S] M) : IsProj (range f) f ↔ IsIdempotentElem f
proof:
refine ⟨fun ⟨h1, h2⟩ => ?_, fun hf =>
    ⟨fun x => mem_range_self f x, fun x ⟨y, hy⟩ => by rw [← hy, ← Module.End.mul_apply, hf.eq]⟩⟩
  ext x
  exact h2 (f x) (h1 x)

alias ⟨_, IsIdempotentElem.isProj_range⟩ := isProj_range_iff_isIdempotentElem

name: isProj_iff_isIdempotentElem
statement: theorem isProj_iff_isIdempotentElem (f : M →ₗ[S] M) : (∃ p : Submodule S M, IsProj p f) ↔ IsIdempotentElem f
proof:
refine ⟨fun ⟨p, hp⟩ => ?_, fun h => ⟨_, IsIdempotentElem.isProj_range _ h⟩⟩
  ext x
  exact hp.map_id (f x) (hp.map_mem x)

namespace IsProj

variable {p m}

name: range_mem_invtSubmodule_iff
statement: theorem range_mem_invtSubmodule_iff (hf : IsIdempotentElem f) : range f ∈ Module.End.invtSubmodule T ↔ f ∘ₗ T ∘ₗ f = T ∘ₗ f
proof:
rw [hf.comp_eq_right_iff, range_comp, Module.End.mem_invtSubmodule_iff_map_le]

alias ⟨conj_eq_of_range_mem_invtSubmodule, range_mem_invtSubmodule⟩ := range_mem_invtSubmodule_iff

name: ker_mem_invtSubmodule_iff
statement: theorem ker_mem_invtSubmodule_iff (hf : IsIdempotentElem f) : ker f ∈ Module.End.invtSubmodule T ↔ f ∘ₗ T ∘ₗ f = f ∘ₗ T
proof:
rw [← comp_assoc, hf.comp_eq_left_iff, ker_comp, Module.End.mem_invtSubmodule]

alias ⟨conj_eq_of_ker_mem_invtSubmodule, ker_mem_invtSubmodule⟩ := ker_mem_invtSubmodule_iff

/-- An idempotent operator `f` commutes with a linear operator `T` if and only if
both `range f` and `ker f` are invariant under `T`. -/

name: commute_iff
statement: theorem commute_iff (hf : IsIdempotentElem f) : Commute f T ↔ (range f ∈ Module.End.invtSubmodule T ∧ ker f ∈ Module.End.invtSubmodule T)
proof:
simp_rw [hf.range_mem_invtSubmodule_iff, hf.ker_mem_invtSubmodule_iff, ← Module.End.mul_eq_comp]
  exact ⟨fun h => (by simp [← h.eq, ← mul_assoc, hf.eq]), fun ⟨h1, h2⟩ => h2.symm.trans h1⟩

/-- An idempotent operator `f` commutes with a unit operator `T` if and only if
`T (range f) = range f` and `T (ker f) = ker f`. -/

name: surjective_piEquiv_apply_iff
statement: theorem surjective_piEquiv_apply_iff (v : ι → M) : Surjective (piEquiv ι R M v) ↔ span R (range v) = ⊤
proof:
rw [← LinearMap.range_eq_top, range_piEquiv]

end Module

namespace Module.Free

variable {ι : Type*} (R : Type*) (M : Type*) [Semiring R] [AddCommMonoid M] [Module R M]

/-- The product of finitely many free modules is free. -/
instance _root_.Module.Free.pi (M : ι → Type*) [Finite ι] [∀ i : ι, AddCommMonoid (M i)]
    [∀ i : ι, Module R (M i)] [∀ i : ι, Module.Free R (M i)] : Module.Free R (∀ i, M i) :=
  let ⟨_⟩ := nonempty_fintype ι
  .of_basis <| Pi.basis fun i => Module.Free.chooseBasis R (M i)

variable (ι) in
/-- The product of finitely many free modules is free (non-dependent version to help with typeclass
search). -/
instance _root_.Module.Free.function [Finite ι] [Module.Free R M] : Module.Free R (ι → M) :=
  Free.pi _ _

end Module.Free

name: range_inl
statement: theorem range_inl  : range (inl R M M₂) = ker (snd R M M₂)
proof:
ext x
  simp only [mem_ker, mem_range]
  constructor
  · rintro ⟨y, rfl⟩
    rfl
  · intro h
    exact ⟨x.fst, Prod.ext rfl h.symm⟩

name: range_inr
statement: theorem range_inr  : range (inr R M M₂) = ker (fst R M M₂)
proof:
ext x
  simp only [mem_ker, mem_range]
  constructor
  · rintro ⟨y, rfl⟩
    rfl
  · intro h
    exact ⟨x.snd, Prod.ext h.symm rfl⟩

name: coprod_inl_inr
statement: theorem coprod_inl_inr  : coprod (inl R M M₂) (inr R M M₂) = LinearMap.id
proof:
ext <;>
    simp only [Prod.mk_add_mk, add_zero, id_apply, coprod_apply, inl_apply, inr_apply, zero_add]

name: coprod_comp_inl_inr
statement: theorem coprod_comp_inl_inr (f : M × M₂ →ₗ[R] M₃) : (f.comp (inl R M M₂)).coprod (f.comp (inr R M M₂)) = f
proof:
rw [← comp_coprod, coprod_inl_inr, comp_id]

/-- Taking the product of two maps with the same codomain is equivalent to taking the product of
their domains.

See note [bundled maps over different rings] for why separate `R` and `S` semirings are used. -/
@[simps]

name: isCompl_range_inl_inr
statement: theorem isCompl_range_inl_inr  : IsCompl (range <| inl R M M₂) (range <| inr R M M₂)
proof:
constructor
  · rw [disjoint_def]
    rintro ⟨_, _⟩ ⟨x, hx⟩ ⟨y, hy⟩
    simp only [Prod.ext_iff, inl_apply, inr_apply] at hx hy ⊢
    exact ⟨hy.1.symm, hx.2.symm⟩
  · rw [codisjoint_iff_le_sup]
    rintro ⟨x, y⟩ -
    simp only [mem_sup, mem_range]
    refine ⟨(x, 0), ⟨x, rfl⟩, (0, y), ⟨y, rfl⟩, ?_⟩
    simp

name: disjoint_inl_inr
statement: theorem disjoint_inl_inr  : Disjoint (range <| inl R M M₂) (range <| inr R M M₂)
proof: simp +contextual [disjoint_def, @eq_comm M 0]

name: map_inl
statement: theorem map_inl  : p.map (inl R M M₂) = prod p ⊥
proof:
ext ⟨x, y⟩
  simp only [and_left_comm, eq_comm, mem_map, Prod.mk_inj, inl_apply, mem_bot, exists_eq_left',
    mem_prod]

@[simp]

name: map_inr
statement: theorem map_inr  : q.map (inr R M M₂) = prod ⊥ q
proof:
ext ⟨x, y⟩; simp [and_left_comm, eq_comm, and_comm]

@[simp]

name: prod_map_fst
statement: theorem prod_map_fst  : (prod p q).map (fst R M M₂) = p
proof:
ext x; simp [(⟨0, zero_mem _⟩ : ∃ x, x ∈ q)]

@[simp]

name: prod_map_snd
statement: theorem prod_map_snd  : (prod p q).map (snd R M M₂) = q
proof:
ext x; simp [(⟨0, zero_mem _⟩ : ∃ x, x ∈ p)]

@[simp]

name: fst_map_fst
statement: theorem fst_map_fst  : (Submodule.fst R M M₂).map (LinearMap.fst R M M₂) = ⊤
proof: aesop

name: fst_map_snd
statement: theorem fst_map_snd  : (Submodule.fst R M M₂).map (LinearMap.snd R M M₂) = ⊥
proof:
aesop (add simp fst)

/-- `N` as a submodule of `M × N`. -/

name: snd_map_fst
statement: theorem snd_map_fst  : (Submodule.snd R M M₂).map (LinearMap.fst R M M₂) = ⊥
proof: aesop (add simp snd)

name: snd_map_snd
statement: theorem snd_map_snd  : (Submodule.snd R M M₂).map (LinearMap.snd R M M₂) = ⊤
proof: aesop

name: fst_sup_snd
statement: theorem fst_sup_snd  : Submodule.fst R M M₂ ⊔ Submodule.snd R M M₂ = ⊤
proof:
rw [eq_top_iff]
  rintro ⟨m, n⟩ -
  rw [show (m, n) = (m, 0) + (0, n) by simp]
  apply Submodule.add_mem (Submodule.fst R M M₂ ⊔ Submodule.snd R M M₂)
  · exact Submodule.mem_sup_left (Submodule.mem_comap.mpr (by simp))
  · exact Submodule.mem_sup_right (Submodule.mem_comap.mpr (by simp))

name: fst_inf_snd
statement: theorem fst_inf_snd  : Submodule.fst R M M₂ ⊓ Submodule.snd R M M₂ = ⊥
proof: aesop

name: fst_comp_prodComm
statement: theorem fst_comp_prodComm  : (LinearMap.fst R M₂ M).comp (prodComm R M M₂).toLinearMap = (LinearMap.snd R M M₂)
proof: ext <;> simp

name: snd_comp_prodComm
statement: theorem snd_comp_prodComm  : (LinearMap.snd R M₂ M).comp (prodComm R M M₂).toLinearMap = (LinearMap.fst R M M₂)
proof:
ext <;> simp

@[simp]

name: graph_eq_ker_coprod
statement: theorem graph_eq_ker_coprod  : g.graph = ker ((-g).coprod LinearMap.id)
proof:
ext x
  change _ = _ ↔ -g x.1 + x.2 = _
  rw [add_comm, add_neg_eq_zero]

name: graph_eq_range_prod
statement: theorem graph_eq_range_prod  : f.graph = range (LinearMap.id.prod f)
proof:
ext x
  exact ⟨fun hx => ⟨x.1, Prod.ext rfl hx.symm⟩, fun ⟨u, hu⟩ => hu ▸ rfl⟩

end Graph

end LinearMap

section LineTest

open Set Function

variable {R S G H I : Type*}
  [Semiring R] [Semiring S] {σ : R →+* S} [RingHomSurjective σ]
  [AddCommMonoid G] [Module R G]
  [AddCommMonoid H] [Module S H]
  [AddCommMonoid I] [Module S I]

/-- **Vertical line test** for linear maps.

Let `f : G → H × I` be a linear (or semilinear) map to a product. Assume that `f` is surjective on
the first factor and that the image of `f` intersects every "vertical line" `{(h, i) | i : I}` at
most once. Then the image of `f` is the graph of some linear map `f' : H → I`. -/

name: trace_eq_matrix_trace
statement: theorem trace_eq_matrix_trace (f : M →ₗ[R] M) : trace R M f = Matrix.trace (LinearMap.toMatrix b b f)
proof:
classical
  rw [trace_eq_matrix_trace_of_finset R b.reindexFinsetRange, ← traceAux_def, ← traceAux_def,
    traceAux_eq R b b.reindexFinsetRange]

variable {R} in
@[simp] theorem _root_.Matrix.trace_toLin_eq (A : Matrix ι ι R) (b : Basis ι R M) :
    LinearMap.trace R _ (Matrix.toLin b b A) = A.trace := by
  simp [trace_eq_matrix_trace R b]

variable {R} in
@[simp] theorem _root_.Matrix.trace_toLin'_eq (A : Matrix ι ι R) :
    LinearMap.trace R _ A.toLin' = A.trace :=
  A.trace_toLin_eq (Pi.basisFun R ι)

name: trace_mul_comm
statement: theorem trace_mul_comm (f g : M →ₗ[R] M) : trace R M (f * g) = trace R M (g * f)
proof:
classical
  by_cases H : ∃ s : Finset M, Nonempty (Basis s R M)
  · let ⟨s, ⟨b⟩⟩ := H
    simp_rw [trace_eq_matrix_trace R b, LinearMap.toMatrix_mul]
    apply Matrix.trace_mul_comm
  · rw [trace, dif_neg H, LinearMap.zero_apply, LinearMap.zero_apply]

name: trace_mul_cycle
statement: theorem trace_mul_cycle (f g h : M →ₗ[R] M) : trace R M (f * g * h) = trace R M (h * f * g)
proof: rw [LinearMap.trace_mul_comm, ← mul_assoc]

name: trace_eq_contract_apply
statement: theorem trace_eq_contract_apply (x : Module.Dual R M ⊗[R] M) : (LinearMap.trace R M) ((dualTensorHom R M M) x) = contractLeft R M x
proof:
rw [← comp_apply, trace_eq_contract]

/-- When `M` is finite free, the trace of a linear map corresponds to the contraction pairing under
the isomorphism `End(M) ≃ M* ⊗ M`. -/

name: trace_one
statement: theorem trace_one  : trace R M 1 = (finrank R M : R)
proof:
cases subsingleton_or_nontrivial R
  · simp [eq_iff_true_of_subsingleton]
  have b := Module.Free.chooseBasis R M
  rw [trace_eq_matrix_trace R b, toMatrix_one, finrank_eq_card_chooseBasisIndex]
  simp

/-- The trace of the identity endomorphism is the dimension of the free module. -/
@[simp]

name: trace_transpose
statement: theorem trace_transpose  : trace R (Module.Dual R M) ∘ₗ Module.Dual.transpose = trace R M
proof:
let e := dualTensorHomEquiv R M M
  have h : Function.Surjective e.toLinearMap := e.surjective
  refine (cancel_right h).1 ?_
  ext f m; simp [e]

name: trace_comp_comm
statement: theorem trace_comp_comm  : compr₂ (llcomp R M N M) (trace R M) = compr₂ (llcomp R N M N).flip (trace R N)
proof:
apply
    (compl₁₂_inj (show Surjective (dualTensorHom R N M) from (dualTensorHomEquiv R N M).surjective)
        (show Surjective (dualTensorHom R M N) from (dualTensorHomEquiv R M N).surjective)).1
  ext g m f n
  simp only [AlgebraTensorModule.curry_apply, TensorProduct.curry_apply,
    coe_restrictScalars, compl₁₂_apply, compr₂_apply, flip_apply, llcomp_apply',
    comp_dualTensorHom, LinearMapClass.map_smul, trace_eq_contract_apply,
    contractLeft_apply, smul_eq_mul, mul_comm]

variable {R M N P}

@[simp]

name: sameRay_neg_iff
statement: theorem sameRay_neg_iff  : SameRay R (-x) (-y) ↔ SameRay R x y
proof:
simp only [SameRay, neg_eq_zero, smul_neg, neg_inj]

alias ⟨SameRay.of_neg, SameRay.neg⟩ := sameRay_neg_iff

name: exists_eq_smul_add
statement: theorem exists_eq_smul_add (h : SameRay R v₁ v₂) : ∃ a b : R, 0 ≤ a ∧ 0 ≤ b ∧ a + b = 1 ∧ v₁ = a • (v₁ + v₂) ∧ v₂ = b • (v₁ + v₂)
proof:
rcases h with (rfl | rfl | ⟨r₁, r₂, h₁, h₂, H⟩)
  · use 0, 1
    simp
  · use 1, 0
    simp
  · have h₁₂ : 0 < r₁ + r₂ := add_pos h₁ h₂
    refine
      ⟨r₂ / (r₁ + r₂), r₁ / (r₁ + r₂), div_nonneg h₂.le h₁₂.le, div_nonneg h₁.le h₁₂.le, ?_, ?_, ?_⟩
    · rw [← add_div, add_comm, div_self h₁₂.ne']
    · rw [div_eq_inv_mul, mul_smul, smul_add, ← H, ← add_smul, add_comm r₂, inv_smul_smul₀ h₁₂.ne']
    · rw [div_eq_inv_mul, mul_smul, smul_add, H, ← add_smul, add_comm r₂, inv_smul_smul₀ h₁₂.ne']

/-- If vectors `v₁` and `v₂` are on the same ray, then they are nonnegative multiples of the same
vector. Actually, this vector can be assumed to be `v₁ + v₂`, see `SameRay.exists_eq_smul_add`. -/

name: exists_pos_left_iff_sameRay_and_ne_zero
statement: theorem exists_pos_left_iff_sameRay_and_ne_zero (hx : x ≠ 0) : (∃ r : R, 0 < r ∧ r • x = y) ↔ SameRay R x y ∧ y ≠ 0
proof:
constructor
  · rintro ⟨r, hr, rfl⟩
    simp [hx, hr.le, hr.ne']
  · rintro ⟨hxy, hy⟩
    exact (exists_pos_left_iff_sameRay hx hy).2 hxy

name: exists_nonneg_left_iff_sameRay
statement: theorem exists_nonneg_left_iff_sameRay (hx : x ≠ 0) : (∃ r : R, 0 ≤ r ∧ r • x = y) ↔ SameRay R x y
proof:
refine ⟨fun h => ?_, fun h => h.exists_nonneg_left hx⟩
  rcases h with ⟨r, hr, rfl⟩
  exact SameRay.sameRay_nonneg_smul_right x hr

name: exists_pos_right_iff_sameRay_and_ne_zero
statement: theorem exists_pos_right_iff_sameRay_and_ne_zero (hy : y ≠ 0) : (∃ r : R, 0 < r ∧ x = r • y) ↔ SameRay R x y ∧ x ≠ 0
proof:
rw [SameRay.sameRay_comm]
  simp_rw [eq_comm (a := x)]
  exact exists_pos_left_iff_sameRay_and_ne_zero hy

name: exists_nonneg_right_iff_sameRay
statement: theorem exists_nonneg_right_iff_sameRay (hy : y ≠ 0) : (∃ r : R, 0 ≤ r ∧ x = r • y) ↔ SameRay R x y
proof:
rw [SameRay.sameRay_comm]
  simp_rw [eq_comm (a := x)]
  exact exists_nonneg_left_iff_sameRay (R := R) hy

end LinearOrderedField

name: nonempty_lifts
statement: theorem nonempty_lifts (x : ⨂[R] i, s i) : Set.Nonempty (lifts x)
proof:
existsi Quot.out x
  simp [lifts, ← AddCon.quot_mk_eq_coe]

instance (x : ⨂[R] i, s i) : Nonempty ↑x.lifts := nonempty_subtype.mpr (nonempty_lifts x)

/-- The empty list lifts the element `0` of `⨂[R] i, s i`.
-/

name: lifts_zero
statement: theorem lifts_zero  : 0 ∈ lifts (0 : ⨂[R] i, s i)
proof:
rw [mem_lifts_iff, FreeAddMonoid.toList_zero, List.map_nil, List.sum_nil]

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
/-- If elements `p,q` of `FreeAddMonoid (R × Π i, s i)` lift elements `x,y` of `⨂[R] i, s i`
respectively, then `p + q` lifts `x + y`.
-/

name: map_comp
statement: theorem map_comp  : map (fun (i : ι) ↦ g i ∘ₗ f i) = map g ∘ₗ map f
proof:
ext
  simp only [LinearMap.compMultilinearMap_apply, map_tprod, LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply]

name: lift_comp_map
statement: theorem lift_comp_map (h : MultilinearMap R t E) : lift h ∘ₗ map f = lift (h.compLinearMap f)
proof:
ext
  simp only [LinearMap.compMultilinearMap_apply, LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply,
    map_tprod, lift.tprod, MultilinearMap.compLinearMap_apply]

attribute [local ext high] ext

@[simp]

name: map_id
statement: theorem map_id  : map (fun i ↦ (LinearMap.id : s i →ₗ[R] s i)) = .id
proof:
ext
  simp only [LinearMap.compMultilinearMap_apply, map_tprod, LinearMap.id_coe, id_eq]

@[simp]
protected theorem map_one : map (fun (i : ι) ↦ (1 : s i →ₗ[R] s i)) = 1 :=
  map_id

protected theorem map_mul (f₁ f₂ : Π i, s i →ₗ[R] s i) :
    map (fun i ↦ f₁ i * f₂ i) = map f₁ * map f₂ :=
  map_comp f₁ f₂

/-- Upgrading `PiTensorProduct.map` to a `MonoidHom` when `s = t`. -/
@[simps]

name: piTensorHomMap_tprod_eq_map
statement: theorem piTensorHomMap_tprod_eq_map (f : Π i, s i →ₗ[R] t i) : piTensorHomMap (tprod R f) = map f
proof:
ext; simp

/-- If `s i` and `t i` are linearly equivalent for every `i` in `ι`, then `⨂[R] i, s i` and
`⨂[R] i, t i` are linearly equivalent.

This is the n-ary version of `TensorProduct.congr`
-/
noncomputable def congr (f : Π i, s i ≃ₗ[R] t i) :
    (⨂[R] i, s i) ≃ₗ[R] ⨂[R] i, t i :=
  .ofLinear
    (map (fun i ↦ f i))
    (map (fun i ↦ (f i).symm))
    (by ext; simp)
    (by ext; simp)

@[simp]

name: piTensorHomMapFun₂_add
statement: theorem piTensorHomMapFun₂_add (φ ψ : ⨂[R] i, s i →ₗ[R] t i →ₗ[R] t' i) : piTensorHomMapFun₂ (φ + ψ) = piTensorHomMapFun₂ φ + piTensorHomMapFun₂ ψ
proof:
dsimp [piTensorHomMapFun₂]; ext; simp only [map_add, LinearMap.compMultilinearMap_apply,
    lift.tprod, add_apply, LinearMap.add_apply]

name: reindex_symm
statement: theorem reindex_symm (e : ι ≃ ι₂) : (reindex R (fun _ ↦ M) e).symm = reindex R (fun _ ↦ M) e.symm
proof:
ext x
  simp [reindex]

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
@[simp]

name: reindex_refl
statement: theorem reindex_refl  : reindex R s (Equiv.refl ι) = LinearEquiv.refl R _
proof:
ext
  simp [reindex, domDomCongrLinearEquiv']

variable {t : ι → Type*}
variable [∀ i, AddCommMonoid (t i)] [∀ i, Module R (t i)]

/-- Re-indexing the components of the tensor product by an equivalence `e` is compatible
with `PiTensorProduct.map`. -/

name: det_zero
statement: theorem det_zero  : LinearMap.det (0 : M →ₗ[A] M) = (0 : A) ^ Module.finrank A M
proof: simp only [← zero_smul A (1 : M →ₗ[A] M), det_smul, mul_one, map_one]

name: det_ring
statement: theorem det_ring (f : R →ₗ[R] R) : f.det = f 1
proof: simp [← det_toMatrix (Basis.singleton Unit R)]

name: det_lsmul
statement: theorem det_lsmul (x : R) : LinearMap.det (lsmul R R S x) = x ^ finrank R S
proof:
rw [lsmul_eq_smul_one, LinearMap.det_smul, map_one, mul_one]

end Algebra

namespace LinearEquiv

/-- On a `LinearEquiv`, the domain of `LinearMap.det` can be promoted to `Rˣ`. -/
protected def det : (M ≃ₗ[R] M) →* Rˣ :=
  (Units.map (LinearMap.det : (M →ₗ[R] M) →* R)).comp
    (LinearMap.GeneralLinearGroup.generalLinearEquiv R M).symm.toMonoidHom

@[simp]

name: det_isEmpty
statement: theorem det_isEmpty  : e.det = AlternatingMap.constOfIsEmpty R M ι 1
proof:
ext v
  exact Matrix.det_isEmpty

/-- `Basis.det` is not the zero map. -/

name: det_mul_det
statement: theorem det_mul_det (b b' b'' : Basis ι A M) : b.det b' * b'.det b'' = b.det b''
proof:
have : b'' = (b'.equiv b'' (Equiv.refl ι)).toLinearMap ∘ b' := by
    ext; simp
  conv_rhs =>
    rw [this, Basis.det_comp, det_basis, mul_comm]

name: det_unitsSMul_self
statement: theorem det_unitsSMul_self (w : ι → Rˣ) : e.det (e.unitsSMul w) = ∏ i, (w i : R)
proof:
simp [det_apply]

/-- The determinant of a basis constructed by `isUnitSMul` is the product of the given units. -/
@[simp]

name: goursatFst_toAddSubgroup
statement: theorem goursatFst_toAddSubgroup  : (goursatFst L).toAddSubgroup = L.toAddSubgroup.goursatFst
proof:
ext x
  simp [goursatFst, AddSubgroup.mem_goursatFst]

name: goursatSnd_toAddSubgroup
statement: theorem goursatSnd_toAddSubgroup  : (goursatSnd L).toAddSubgroup = L.toAddSubgroup.goursatSnd
proof:
ext x
  simp [goursatSnd, AddSubgroup.mem_goursatSnd]

variable (L) in

name: goursatFst_prod_goursatSnd_le
statement: theorem goursatFst_prod_goursatSnd_le  : L.goursatFst.prod L.goursatSnd ≤ L
proof:
simpa only [← toAddSubgroup_le, goursatFst_toAddSubgroup, goursatSnd_toAddSubgroup]
    using L.toAddSubgroup.goursatFst_prod_goursatSnd_le

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
include hL₁ hL₂ in
/-- **Goursat's lemma** for a submodule of a product with surjective projections.

If `L` is a submodule of `M × N` which projects fully on both factors, then there exist submodules
`M' ≤ M` and `N' ≤ N` such that `M' × N' ≤ L` and the image of `L` in `(M ⧸ M') × (N ⧸ N')` is the
graph of an isomorphism of `R`-modules `(M ⧸ M') ≃ (N ⧸ N')`.

`M` and `N` can be explicitly constructed as `L.goursatFst` and `L.goursatSnd` respectively. -/

name: one_left
statement: theorem one_left  : (1 : Submodule R S).LinearDisjoint N
proof:
rw [linearDisjoint_iff, ← Algebra.toSubmodule_bot, mulMap_one_left_eq]
  exact N.injective_subtype.comp N.lTensorOne.injective

/-- The image of `R` in `S` is linearly disjoint with any other submodules. -/

name: one_right
statement: theorem one_right  : M.LinearDisjoint (1 : Submodule R S)
proof:
rw [linearDisjoint_iff, ← Algebra.toSubmodule_bot, mulMap_one_right_eq]
  exact M.injective_subtype.comp M.rTensorOne.injective

/-- If for any finitely generated submodules `M'` of `M`, `M'` and `N` are linearly disjoint,
then `M` and `N` themselves are linearly disjoint. -/

name: span_singleton_annIdealGenerator
statement: theorem span_singleton_annIdealGenerator (a : A) : Ideal.span {annIdealGenerator 𝕜 a} = annIdeal 𝕜 a
proof:
by_cases h : annIdealGenerator 𝕜 a = 0
  · rw [h, annIdealGenerator_eq_zero_iff.mp h, Set.singleton_zero, Ideal.span_zero]
  · rw [annIdealGenerator, Ideal.span_singleton_mul_right_unit, Ideal.span_singleton_generator]
    apply Polynomial.isUnit_C.mpr
    apply IsUnit.mk0
    apply inv_eq_zero.not.mpr
    apply Polynomial.leadingCoeff_eq_zero.not.mpr
    apply (mul_ne_zero_iff.mp h).1

/-- The annihilating ideal generator is a member of the annihilating ideal. -/

name: annIdealGenerator_eq_minpoly
statement: theorem annIdealGenerator_eq_minpoly (a : A) : annIdealGenerator 𝕜 a = minpoly 𝕜 a
proof:
by_cases h : annIdealGenerator 𝕜 a = 0
  · rw [h, minpoly.eq_zero]
    rintro ⟨p, p_monic, hp : aeval a p = 0⟩
    refine p_monic.ne_zero (Ideal.mem_bot.mp ?_)
    simpa only [annIdealGenerator_eq_zero_iff.mp h] using mem_annIdeal_iff_aeval_eq_zero.mpr hp
  · exact minpoly.unique _ _ (monic_annIdealGenerator _ _ h) (annIdealGenerator_aeval_eq_zero _ _)
      fun q q_monic hq =>
        degree_annIdealGenerator_le_of_mem a q (mem_annIdeal_iff_aeval_eq_zero.mpr hq)
          q_monic.ne_zero

/-- If a monic generates the annihilating ideal, it must match our choice
of the annihilating ideal generator. -/

name: J_transpose
statement: theorem J_transpose  : (J l R)ᵀ = -J l R
proof:
rw [J, fromBlocks_transpose, ← neg_one_smul R (fromBlocks _ _ _ _ : Matrix (l ⊕ l) (l ⊕ l) R),
    fromBlocks_smul, Matrix.transpose_zero, Matrix.transpose_one, transpose_neg]
  simp [fromBlocks]

variable [Fintype l]

name: J_squared
statement: theorem J_squared  : J l R * J l R = -1
proof:
rw [J, fromBlocks_multiply]
  simp only [Matrix.zero_mul, Matrix.neg_mul, zero_add, neg_zero, Matrix.one_mul, add_zero]
  rw [← neg_zero, ← Matrix.fromBlocks_neg, ← fromBlocks_one]

name: J_inv
statement: theorem J_inv  : (J l R)⁻¹ = -J l R
proof:
refine Matrix.inv_eq_right_inv ?_
  rw [Matrix.mul_neg, J_squared]
  exact neg_neg 1

name: J_det_mul_J_det
statement: theorem J_det_mul_J_det  : det (J l R) * det (J l R) = 1
proof:
rw [← det_mul, J_squared, ← one_smul R (-1 : Matrix _ _ R), smul_neg, ← neg_smul, det_smul,
    Fintype.card_sum, det_one, mul_one]
  apply Even.neg_one_pow
  exact Even.add_self _

name: J_mem
statement: theorem J_mem  : J l R ∈ symplecticGroup l R
proof:
rw [mem_iff, J, fromBlocks_multiply, fromBlocks_transpose, fromBlocks_multiply]
  simp

/-- The canonical skew-symmetric matrix as an element in the symplectic group. -/

name: neg_mem
statement: theorem neg_mem (h : A ∈ symplecticGroup l R) : -A ∈ symplecticGroup l R
proof:
rw [mem_iff] at h ⊢
  simp [h]

name: symplectic_det
statement: theorem symplectic_det (hA : A ∈ symplecticGroup l R) : IsUnit <| det A
proof:
rw [isUnit_iff_exists_inv]
  use A.det
  refine (isUnit_det_J l R).mul_left_cancel ?_
  rw [mul_one]
  rw [mem_iff] at hA
  apply_fun det at hA
  simp only [det_mul, det_transpose] at hA
  rw [mul_comm A.det, mul_assoc] at hA
  exact hA

name: transpose_mem
statement: theorem transpose_mem (hA : A ∈ symplecticGroup l R) : Aᵀ ∈ symplecticGroup l R
proof:
rw [mem_iff] at hA ⊢
  rw [transpose_transpose]
  have huA := symplectic_det hA
  have huAT : IsUnit Aᵀ.det := by
    rw [Matrix.det_transpose]
    exact huA
  calc
    Aᵀ * J l R * A = (-Aᵀ) * (J l R)⁻¹ * A := by
      rw [J_inv]
      simp
    _ = (-Aᵀ) * (A * J l R * Aᵀ)⁻¹ * A := by rw [hA]
    _ = -(Aᵀ * (Aᵀ⁻¹ * (J l R)⁻¹)) * A⁻¹ * A := by
      simp only [Matrix.mul_inv_rev, Matrix.mul_assoc, Matrix.neg_mul]
    _ = -(J l R)⁻¹ := by
      rw [mul_nonsing_inv_cancel_left _ _ huAT, nonsing_inv_mul_cancel_right _ _ huA]
    _ = J l R := by simp [J_inv]

@[simp]

name: exists_isNilpotent_isSemisimple
statement: theorem exists_isNilpotent_isSemisimple  : ∃ᵉ (n ∈ adjoin K {f}) (s ∈ adjoin K {f}), IsNilpotent n ∧ IsSemisimple s ∧ f = n + s
proof:
obtain ⟨g, k, sep, -, nil⟩ := exists_squarefree_dvd_pow_of_ne_zero (minpoly.ne_zero_of_finite K f)
  rw [← PerfectField.separable_iff_squarefree] at sep
  exact exists_isNilpotent_isSemisimple_of_separable_of_dvd_pow sep nil

/-- **Uniqueness of Jordan-Chevalley-Dunford decomposition**: if `n₁ + s₁ = n₂ + s₂` with `nᵢ`
nilpotent, `sᵢ` semisimple, and `nᵢ`, `sᵢ` commuting, then `n₁ = n₂` and `s₁ = s₂`. -/

name: iSup_range_single
statement: theorem iSup_range_single  : ⨆ i, range (single R φ i) = ⊤
proof:
cases nonempty_fintype ι
  convert top_unique (iInf_emptyset.ge.trans <| iInf_ker_proj_le_iSup_range_single R φ _)
  · rename_i i
    exact ((@iSup_pos _ _ _ fun _ => range <| single R φ i) <| Finset.mem_univ i).symm
  · rw [Finset.coe_univ, Set.union_empty]

name: single_eq_pi_diag
statement: theorem single_eq_pi_diag (i : ι) : single R φ i = pi (diag i)
proof:
ext x j
  convert (update_apply 0 x i j _).symm
  rfl

name: proj_comp_single
statement: theorem proj_comp_single (i j : ι) : (proj i).comp (single R φ j) = diag j i
proof:
rw [single_eq_pi_diag, proj_pi]

end

/-- A linear map `f` applied to `x : ι → R` can be computed using the image under `f` of elements
of the canonical basis. -/

name: biInf_comap_proj
statement: theorem biInf_comap_proj  : ⨅ i ∈ I, comap (proj i : ((i : ι) → φ i) →ₗ[R] φ i) (p i) = pi I p
proof:
ext x
  simp

name: iInf_comap_proj
statement: theorem iInf_comap_proj  : ⨅ i, comap (proj i : ((i : ι) → φ i) →ₗ[R] φ i) (p i) = pi Set.univ p
proof:
ext x
  simp

name: iSup_eq_range_dfinsupp_lsum
statement: theorem iSup_eq_range_dfinsupp_lsum (p : ι → Submodule R N) : iSup p = LinearMap.range (DFinsupp.lsum ℕ fun i => (p i).subtype)
proof:
apply le_antisymm
  · apply iSup_le _
    intro i y hy
    simp only [LinearMap.mem_range, lsum_apply_apply]
    exact ⟨DFinsupp.single i ⟨y, hy⟩, DFinsupp.sumAddHom_single _ _ _⟩
  · rintro x ⟨v, rfl⟩
    exact dfinsuppSumAddHom_mem _ v _ fun i _ => (le_iSup p i : p i ≤ _) (v i).2

/-- The bounded supremum of a family of commutative additive submonoids is equal to the range of
`DFinsupp.sumAddHom` composed with `DFinsupp.filter_add_monoid_hom`; that is, every element in the
bounded `iSup` can be produced from taking a finite number of non-zero elements from the `S i` that
satisfy `p i`, coercing them to `γ`, and summing them. -/

name: vandermonde_eq_projVandermonde
statement: theorem vandermonde_eq_projVandermonde (v : Fin n → R) : vandermonde v = projVandermonde v 1
proof:
simp [projVandermonde, rectVandermonde, vandermonde]

/-- We don't mark this as `@[simp]` because the RHS is not simp-nf,
and simplifying the RHS gives a bothersome `Nat` subtraction. -/

name: det_projVandermonde_of_field
statement: theorem det_projVandermonde_of_field (v w : Fin n → K) : (projVandermonde v w).det = ∏ i : Fin n, ∏ j ∈ Finset.Ioi i, (v j * w i - v i * w j)
proof:
induction n with
  | zero => simp
  | succ n ih =>
  /- We can assume not all `w i` are zero, and therefore that `w 0 ≠ 0`,
  since otherwise we can swap row `0` with another nonzero row. -/
  wlog h0 : w 0 ≠ 0 generalizing v w with aux
  · obtain h0' | ⟨i₀, hi₀ : w i₀ ≠ 0⟩ := forall_or_exists_not (w · = 0)
    · obtain rfl | hne := eq_or_ne n 0
      · simp [projVandermonde_apply]
      rw [det_eq_zero_of_column_eq_zero 0 (fun i ↦ by simpa [projVandermonde_apply, h0']),
        Finset.prod_sigma', Finset.prod_eq_zero (i := ⟨0, Fin.last n⟩) (by simpa) (by simp [h0'])]
    rw [← mul_right_inj' (a := ((Equiv.swap 0 i₀).sign : K))
      (by simp [show 0 ≠ i₀ by rintro rfl; contradiction]), ← det_permute, ← projVandermonde_comp,
      aux _ _ (by simpa), ← (Equiv.swap 0 i₀).prod_Ioi_comp_eq_sign_mul_prod (by simp)]
    rfl
  /- Let `W` be obtained from the matrix by subtracting `r = (v 0) / (w 0)` times each column
  from the next column, starting from the penultimate column. This doesn't change the determinant.-/
  set r := v 0 / w 0 with hr
  set W : Matrix (Fin (n + 1)) (Fin (n + 1)) K := .of fun i ↦ (cons (projVandermonde v w i 0)
    (fun j ↦ projVandermonde v w i j.succ - r * projVandermonde v w i j.castSucc))
  -- deleting the first row and column of `W` gives a row-scaling of a Vandermonde matrix.
  have hW_eq : (W.submatrix succ succ) = .of fun i j ↦ (v (succ i) - r * w (succ i)) *
      projVandermonde (v ∘ succ) (w ∘ succ) i j := by
    ext i j
    simp only [projVandermonde_apply, val_zero, rev_zero, val_last, val_succ,
      val_castSucc, submatrix_apply, Function.comp_apply, rev_succ,
      W, r, rev_castSucc]
    simp
    ring
  /- The first row of `W` is `[(w 0)^n, 0, ..., 0]` - take a cofactor expansion along this row,
  and apply induction. -/
  rw [det_eq_of_forall_col_eq_smul_add_pred (B := W) (c := fun _ ↦ r) (by simp [W])
    (fun i j ↦ by simp [W, r, projVandermonde_apply]), det_succ_row_zero,
    Finset.sum_eq_single 0 _ (by simp)]
  · rw [succAbove_zero, hW_eq, det_mul_column, ih]
    simp only [Nat.succ_eq_add_one, coe_ofNat_eq_mod, Nat.zero_mod,
      pow_zero, show W 0 0 = w 0 ^ n by simp [W, projVandermonde_apply], one_mul, hr]
    field_simp
    simp only [Finset.prod_div_distrib, Finset.prod_const, Finset.card_fin, Function.comp_apply]
    field_simp
    simp only [prod_univ_succ, Ioi_zero_eq_map, Finset.prod_map, coe_succEmb, prod_Ioi_succ]
  intro j _ hj0
  obtain ⟨j, rfl⟩ := j.eq_succ_of_ne_zero hj0
  rw [mul_eq_zero, mul_eq_zero]
  refine .inl (.inr ?_)
  simp only [of_apply, projVandermonde_apply_of_ne_zero h0, val_succ, val_castSucc, cons_succ, W, r]
  ring

/-- The formula for the determinant of a projective Vandermonde matrix. -/

name: det_vandermonde
statement: theorem det_vandermonde (v : Fin n → R) : det (vandermonde v) = ∏ i : Fin n, ∏ j ∈ Ioi i, (v j - v i)
proof: simp [vandermonde_eq_projVandermonde, det_projVandermonde]

name: det_vandermonde_id_eq_superFactorial
statement: theorem det_vandermonde_id_eq_superFactorial (n : ℕ) : (vandermonde fun i : Fin (n + 1) ↦ (i : R)).det = n.superFactorial
proof:
induction n with
  | zero => simp
  | succ n hn =>
    rw [Nat.superFactorial, det_vandermonde, Fin.prod_univ_succAbove _ 0]
    push_cast
    congr
    · simp only [Fin.val_zero, Nat.cast_zero, sub_zero]
      norm_cast
      simp [Fin.prod_univ_eq_prod_range (fun i ↦ (↑i + 1)) (n + 1)]
    · rw [det_vandermonde] at hn
      simp [hn]

private lemma of_eval_descPochhammer_eq_mul_of_choose {n : ℕ} (v : Fin n → ℕ) :
    (of fun i j : Fin n => (descPochhammer ℤ j).eval (v i : ℤ)).det =
    (∏ i : Fin n, Nat.factorial i) *
      (of fun i j : Fin n => (Nat.choose (v i) j : ℤ)).det := by
  convert det_mul_row (fun (i : Fin n) => ((Nat.factorial (i : ℕ)) : ℤ)) _
  · rw [of_apply, descPochhammer_eval_eq_descFactorial ℤ _ _]
    congr
    exact Nat.descFactorial_eq_factorial_mul_choose _ _
  · rw [Nat.cast_prod]

name: isSemisimple_iff
statement: theorem isSemisimple_iff  : f.IsSemisimple ↔ ∀ p ∈ invtSubmodule f, ∃ q ∈ invtSubmodule f, IsCompl p q
proof:
simp [isSemisimple_iff']

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in

name: isSemisimple_zero
statement: theorem isSemisimple_zero  : IsSemisimple (0 : Module.End R M)
proof:
simpa [isSemisimple_iff] using exists_isCompl

@[simp]

name: isSemisimple_id
statement: theorem isSemisimple_id  : IsSemisimple (LinearMap.id : Module.End R M)
proof:
simpa [isSemisimple_iff] using exists_isCompl

@[simp] lemma isSemisimple_neg : (-f).IsSemisimple ↔ f.IsSemisimple := by
  simp [isSemisimple_iff, mem_invtSubmodule]

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
variable (f) in
protected lemma _root_.LinearEquiv.isSemisimple_iff {M₂ : Type*} [AddCommGroup M₂] [Module R M₂]
    (g : End R M₂) (e : M ≃ₗ[R] M₂) (he : e ∘ₗ f = g ∘ₗ e) :
    f.IsSemisimple ↔ g.IsSemisimple := by
  let e : AEval' f ≃ₗ[R[X]] AEval' g := LinearEquiv.ofAEval _ (e.trans (AEval'.of g)) fun x ↦ by
    simpa [AEval'.X_smul_of] using LinearMap.congr_fun he x
  simp_rw [IsSemisimple, isSemisimpleModule_iff,
    (Submodule.orderIsoMapComap e).complementedLattice_iff]

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in

name: isFinitelySemisimple_iff_isSemisimple
statement: theorem isFinitelySemisimple_iff_isSemisimple  : f.IsFinitelySemisimple ↔ f.IsSemisimple
proof:
refine ⟨fun hf ↦ isSemisimple_iff.mpr fun p hp ↦ ?_, IsSemisimple.isFinitelySemisimple⟩
  obtain ⟨q, -, hq₁, hq₂, hq₃⟩ :=
    isFinitelySemisimple_iff.mp hf ⊤ (invtSubmodule.top_mem f) inferInstance p hp le_top
  exact ⟨q, hq₁, hq₂, codisjoint_iff.mpr hq₃⟩

@[simp]

name: compl₂_id
statement: theorem compl₂_id (h : M →ₛₗ[σ₁₅] N →ₛₗ[σ₂₃] P) : h.compl₂ LinearMap.id = h
proof:
ext
  rw [compl₂_apply, id_coe, _root_.id]

name: restrictScalarsRange_apply
statement: theorem restrictScalarsRange_apply (m : M') : k (restrictScalarsRange i k hk f hf m) = f (i m)
proof:
have : k (restrictScalarsRange i k hk f hf m) =
      (k ∘ₗ ((f.restrictScalars S).comp i).codLift k hk hf) m :=
    rfl
  rw [this, comp_codLift, comp_apply, restrictScalars_apply]

@[simp]

name: restrictScalarsRange_apply_eq_zero_iff
statement: theorem restrictScalarsRange_apply_eq_zero_iff (m : M') : restrictScalarsRange i k hk f hf m = 0 ↔ f (i m) = 0
proof:
rw [← hk.eq_iff, restrictScalarsRange_apply, map_zero]

end restrictScalarsRange

section restrictScalarsRange₂

variable {R S M N P M' N' P' : Type*}
  [CommSemiring R] [CommSemiring S] [SMul S R]
  [AddCommMonoid M] [Module R M] [AddCommMonoid N] [Module R N] [AddCommMonoid P] [Module R P]
  [Module S M] [Module S N] [Module S P]
  [IsScalarTower S R M] [IsScalarTower S R N] [IsScalarTower S R P]
  [AddCommMonoid M'] [Module S M'] [AddCommMonoid N'] [Module S N'] [AddCommMonoid P'] [Module S P']
  [SMulCommClass R S P]

variable (i : M' →ₗ[S] M) (j : N' →ₗ[S] N) (k : P' →ₗ[S] P) (hk : Injective k)
  (B : M →ₗ[R] N →ₗ[R] P) (hB : ∀ m n, B (i m) (j n) ∈ LinearMap.range k)

/-- Restrict the scalars, domains, and range of a bilinear map. -/
noncomputable def restrictScalarsRange₂ :
    M' →ₗ[S] N' →ₗ[S] P' :=
  (((LinearMap.restrictScalarsₗ S R _ _ _).comp
    (B.restrictScalars S)).compl₁₂ i j).codRestrict₂ k hk hB

@[simp] lemma restrictScalarsRange₂_apply (m : M') (n : N') :
    k (restrictScalarsRange₂ i j k hk B hB m n) = B (i m) (j n) := by
  simp [restrictScalarsRange₂]

@[simp]

name: comap_leq_ker_subToSupQuotient
statement: theorem comap_leq_ker_subToSupQuotient (p p' : Submodule R M) : comap (Submodule.subtype p) (p ⊓ p') ≤ ker (subToSupQuotient p p')
proof:
rw [LinearMap.ker_comp, Submodule.inclusion, comap_codRestrict, ker_mkQ, map_comap_subtype]
  exact comap_mono (inf_le_inf_right _ le_sup_left)

/-- Canonical linear map from the quotient `p/(p ∩ p')` to `(p+p')/p'`, mapping `x + (p ∩ p')`
to `x + p'`, where `p` and `p'` are submodules of an ambient module.

Note that in the following declaration the type of the domain is expressed using
`comap p.subtype p ⊓ comap p.subtype p'`
instead of
`comap p.subtype (p ⊓ p')`
because the former is the simp normal form (see also `Submodule.comap_inf`). -/

name: quotientInfEquivSupQuotient_injective
statement: theorem quotientInfEquivSupQuotient_injective (p p' : Submodule R M) : Function.Injective (quotientInfToSupQuotient p p')
proof:
rw [← ker_eq_bot, quotientInfToSupQuotient, ker_liftQ_eq_bot]
  rw [ker_comp, ker_mkQ]
  exact fun ⟨x, hx1⟩ hx2 => ⟨hx1, hx2⟩

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in

name: quotientInfEquivSupQuotient_surjective
statement: theorem quotientInfEquivSupQuotient_surjective (p p' : Submodule R M) : Function.Surjective (quotientInfToSupQuotient p p')
proof:
rw [← range_eq_top, quotientInfToSupQuotient, range_liftQ, eq_top_iff']
  rintro ⟨x, hx⟩; rcases mem_sup.1 hx with ⟨y, hy, z, hz, rfl⟩
  use ⟨y, hy⟩; apply (Submodule.Quotient.eq _).2
  simp only [mem_comap, map_sub, coe_subtype, coe_inclusion, sub_add_cancel_left, neg_mem_iff, hz]

/--
Second Isomorphism Law : the canonical map from `p/(p ∩ p')` to `(p+p')/p'` as a linear isomorphism.

Note that in the following declaration the type of the domain is expressed using
`comap p.subtype p ⊓ comap p.subtype p'`
instead of
`comap p.subtype (p ⊓ p')`
because the former is the simp normal form (see also `Submodule.comap_inf`). -/
noncomputable def quotientInfEquivSupQuotient (p p' : Submodule R M) :
    (p ⧸ comap p.subtype p ⊓ comap p.subtype p') ≃ₗ[R] _ ⧸ comap (p ⊔ p').subtype p' :=
  LinearEquiv.ofBijective (quotientInfToSupQuotient p p')
    ⟨quotientInfEquivSupQuotient_injective p p', quotientInfEquivSupQuotient_surjective p p'⟩

@[simp]

name: basisDivisor_self
statement: theorem basisDivisor_self  : basisDivisor x x = 0
proof: simp only [basisDivisor, sub_self, inv_zero, map_zero, zero_mul]

name: basisDivisor_inj
statement: theorem basisDivisor_inj (hxy : basisDivisor x y = 0) : x = y
proof:
simp_rw [basisDivisor, mul_eq_zero, X_sub_C_ne_zero, or_false, C_eq_zero, inv_eq_zero,
    sub_eq_zero] at hxy
  exact hxy

@[simp]

name: basisDivisor_ne_zero_iff
statement: theorem basisDivisor_ne_zero_iff  : basisDivisor x y ≠ 0 ↔ x ≠ y
proof: rw [Ne, basisDivisor_eq_zero_iff]

name: degree_basisDivisor_of_ne
statement: theorem degree_basisDivisor_of_ne (hxy : x ≠ y) : (basisDivisor x y).degree = 1
proof:
rw [basisDivisor, degree_mul, degree_X_sub_C, degree_C, zero_add]
  exact inv_ne_zero (sub_ne_zero_of_ne hxy)

@[simp]

name: degree_basisDivisor_self
statement: theorem degree_basisDivisor_self  : (basisDivisor x x).degree = ⊥
proof: rw [basisDivisor_self, degree_zero]

name: natDegree_basisDivisor_self
statement: theorem natDegree_basisDivisor_self  : (basisDivisor x x).natDegree = 0
proof: rw [basisDivisor_self, natDegree_zero]

name: eval_basisDivisor_right
statement: theorem eval_basisDivisor_right  : eval y (basisDivisor x y) = 0
proof: simp only [basisDivisor, eval_mul, eval_C, eval_sub, eval_X, sub_self, mul_zero]

name: eval_basisDivisor_left_of_ne
statement: theorem eval_basisDivisor_left_of_ne (hxy : x ≠ y) : eval x (basisDivisor x y) = 1
proof:
simp only [basisDivisor, eval_mul, eval_C, eval_sub, eval_X]
  exact inv_mul_cancel₀ (sub_ne_zero_of_ne hxy)

end BasisDivisor

section Basis

variable {F : Type*} [Field F] {ι : Type*} [DecidableEq ι]
variable {s : Finset ι} {v : ι → F} {i j : ι}

open Finset

/-- Lagrange basis polynomials indexed by `s : Finset ι`, defined at nodes `v i` for a
map `v : ι → F`. For `i, j ∈ s`, `basis s v i` evaluates to 0 at `v j` for `i ≠ j`. When
`v` is injective on `s`, `basis s v i` evaluates to 1 at `v i`. -/
protected def basis (s : Finset ι) (v : ι → F) (i : ι) : F[X] :=
  ∏ j ∈ s.erase i, basisDivisor (v i) (v j)

@[simp]

name: basis_singleton
statement: theorem basis_singleton (i : ι) : Lagrange.basis {i} v i = 1
proof:
rw [Lagrange.basis, erase_singleton, prod_empty]

@[simp]

name: basis_pair_left
statement: theorem basis_pair_left (hij : i ≠ j) : Lagrange.basis {i, j} v i = basisDivisor (v i) (v j)
proof:
simp only [Lagrange.basis, hij, erase_insert_eq_erase, erase_eq_of_notMem, mem_singleton,
    not_false_iff, prod_singleton]

@[simp]

name: basis_pair_right
statement: theorem basis_pair_right (hij : i ≠ j) : Lagrange.basis {i, j} v j = basisDivisor (v j) (v i)
proof:
rw [pair_comm]
  exact basis_pair_left hij.symm

name: interpolate_singleton
statement: theorem interpolate_singleton  : interpolate {i} v r = C (r i)
proof: rw [interpolate_apply, sum_singleton, basis_singleton, mul_one]

name: degree_interpolate_le
statement: theorem degree_interpolate_le (hvs : Set.InjOn v s) : (interpolate s v r).degree ≤ ↑(#s - 1)
proof:
refine (degree_sum_le _ _).trans ?_
  rw [Finset.sup_le_iff]
  intro i hi
  rw [degree_mul, degree_basis hvs hi]
  by_cases hr : r i = 0
  · simpa only [hr, map_zero, degree_zero, WithBot.bot_add] using bot_le
  · rw [degree_C hr, zero_add]

name: degree_interpolate_lt
statement: theorem degree_interpolate_lt (hvs : Set.InjOn v s) : (interpolate s v r).degree < #s
proof:
rw [Nat.cast_withBot]
  rcases eq_empty_or_nonempty s with (rfl | h)
  · rw [interpolate_empty, degree_zero, card_empty]
    exact WithBot.bot_lt_coe _
  · refine lt_of_le_of_lt (degree_interpolate_le _ hvs) ?_
    rw [Nat.cast_withBot, WithBot.coe_lt_coe]
    exact Nat.sub_lt (Nonempty.card_pos h) zero_lt_one

name: nodal_empty
statement: theorem nodal_empty  : nodal ∅ v = 1
proof:
rfl

@[simp]

name: natDegree_nodal
statement: theorem natDegree_nodal  : (nodal s v).natDegree = #s
proof:
simp_rw [nodal, natDegree_prod_of_monic (h := fun i _ => monic_X_sub_C (v i)),
    natDegree_X_sub_C, sum_const, smul_eq_mul, mul_one]

name: nodal_ne_zero
statement: theorem nodal_ne_zero  : nodal s v ≠ 0
proof:
rcases s.eq_empty_or_nonempty with (rfl | h)
  · exact one_ne_zero
  · apply ne_zero_of_natDegree_gt (n := 0)
    simp only [natDegree_nodal, h.card_pos]

@[simp]

name: degree_nodal
statement: theorem degree_nodal  : (nodal s v).degree = #s
proof: simp_rw [degree_eq_natDegree nodal_ne_zero, natDegree_nodal]

name: derivative_nodal
statement: theorem derivative_nodal  : derivative (nodal s v) = ∑ i ∈ s, nodal (s.erase i) v
proof:
refine s.induction_on ?_ fun i t hit IH => ?_
  · rw [nodal_empty, derivative_one, sum_empty]
  · rw [nodal_insert_eq_nodal hit, derivative_mul, IH, derivative_sub, derivative_X, derivative_C,
      sub_zero, one_mul, sum_insert hit, mul_sum, erase_insert hit, add_right_inj]
    refine sum_congr rfl fun j hjt => ?_
    rw [t.erase_insert_of_ne (ne_of_mem_of_not_mem hjt hit).symm,
      nodal_insert_eq_nodal (mem_of_mem_erase.mt hit)]

name: nodalWeight_eq_eval_nodal_erase_inv
statement: theorem nodalWeight_eq_eval_nodal_erase_inv  : nodalWeight s v i = (eval (v i) (nodal (s.erase i) v))⁻¹
proof: rw [eval_nodal, nodalWeight, prod_inv_distrib]

name: nodal_erase_eq_nodal_div
statement: theorem nodal_erase_eq_nodal_div (hi : i ∈ s) : nodal (s.erase i) v = nodal s v / (X - C (v i))
proof:
rw [nodal_eq_mul_nodal_erase hi, mul_div_cancel_left₀]
  exact X_sub_C_ne_zero _

name: nodalWeight_eq_eval_derivative_nodal
statement: theorem nodalWeight_eq_eval_derivative_nodal (hi : i ∈ s) : nodalWeight s v i = (eval (v i) (Polynomial.derivative (nodal s v)))⁻¹
proof: rw [eval_nodal_derivative_eval_node_eq hi, nodalWeight_eq_eval_nodal_erase_inv]

name: basis_eq_prod_sub_inv_mul_nodal_div
statement: theorem basis_eq_prod_sub_inv_mul_nodal_div (hi : i ∈ s) : Lagrange.basis s v i = C (nodalWeight s v i) * (nodal s v / (X - C (v i)))
proof:
simp_rw [Lagrange.basis, basisDivisor, nodalWeight, prod_mul_distrib, map_prod, ←
    nodal_erase_eq_nodal_div hi, nodal]

name: strongRankCondition_iff_forall_not_injective
statement: theorem strongRankCondition_iff_forall_not_injective  : StrongRankCondition R ↔ ∀ n (f : (ℕ →₀ R) →ₗ[R] Fin n → R), ¬ Injective f
proof:
rw [strongRankCondition_iff_succ, ← not_iff_not]; push Not
  constructor <;> refine fun ⟨n, f, inj⟩ ↦ ⟨n, ?_⟩
  · exact f.exists_finsupp_nat_of_fin_fun_injective inj
  · exact ⟨f ∘ₗ Finsupp.lmapDomain R R (↑) ∘ₗ (Finsupp.linearEquivFunOnFinite ..).symm.toLinearMap,
      inj.comp <| by simpa using Finsupp.mapDomain_injective Fin.val_injective⟩

end

section

variable (R : Type u) [Semiring R] [InvariantBasisNumber R]

name: nontrivial_of_invariantBasisNumber
statement: theorem nontrivial_of_invariantBasisNumber  : Nontrivial R
proof:
by_contra! h
  exact zero_ne_one (eq_of_fin_equiv R <| .ofSubsingleton ..)

end

section

variable (R : Type u) [Ring R] [Nontrivial R] [IsNoetherianRing R]

/-- Any nontrivial Noetherian ring satisfies the strong rank condition,
    since it satisfies Orzech property. -/
instance (priority := 100) IsNoetherianRing.strongRankCondition : StrongRankCondition R :=
  inferInstance

end

/-!
  We want to show that nontrivial commutative rings have invariant basis number. The idea is to
  take a maximal ideal `I` of `R` and use an isomorphism `R^n ≃ R^m` of `R` modules to produce an
  isomorphism `(R/I)^n ≃ (R/I)^m` of `R/I`-modules, which will imply `n = m` since `R/I` is a field
  and we know that fields have invariant basis number.

  We construct the isomorphism in two steps:
  1. We construct the ring `R^n/I^n`, show that it is an `R/I`-module and show that there is an
     isomorphism of `R/I`-modules `R^n/I^n ≃ (R/I)^n`. This isomorphism is called
    `Ideal.piQuotEquiv` and is located in the file `Mathlib/RingTheory/Ideal/Quotient/Basic.lean`.
  2. We construct an isomorphism of `R/I`-modules `R^n/I^n ≃ R^m/I^m` using the isomorphism
     `R^n ≃ R^m`.
-/


section

variable {R : Type u} [CommRing R] (I : Ideal R) {ι : Type v} [Fintype ι] {ι' : Type w}

/-- An `R`-linear map `R^n → R^m` induces a function `R^n/I^n → R^m/I^m`. -/
private def induced_map (I : Ideal R) (e : (ι → R) →ₗ[R] ι' → R) :
    (ι → R) ⧸ Ideal.pi (fun _ ↦ I) → (ι' → R) ⧸ Ideal.pi fun _ ↦ I := fun x =>
  Quotient.liftOn' x (fun y => Ideal.Quotient.mk _ (e y))
    fun a b hab => Ideal.Quotient.eq.2 fun h => by
      rw [Submodule.quotientRel_def] at hab
      rw [← map_sub]
      exact Ideal.map_pi _ _ hab e h

/-- An isomorphism of `R`-modules `R^n ≃ R^m` induces an isomorphism of `R/I`-modules
    `R^n/I^n ≃ R^m/I^m`. -/
private def inducedEquiv [Fintype ι'] (I : Ideal R) (e : (ι → R) ≃ₗ[R] ι' → R) :
    ((ι → R) ⧸ Ideal.pi fun _ ↦ I) ≃ₗ[R ⧸ I] (ι' → R) ⧸ Ideal.pi fun _ ↦ I where
  toFun := induced_map I e
  invFun := induced_map I e.symm
  map_add' := by rintro ⟨a⟩ ⟨b⟩; exact congr_arg _ (map_add ..)
  map_smul' := by rintro ⟨a⟩ ⟨b⟩; exact congr_arg _ (map_smul ..)
  left_inv := by rintro ⟨a⟩; exact congr_arg _ (e.left_inv ..)
  right_inv := by rintro ⟨a⟩; exact congr_arg _ (e.right_inv ..)

end

section

attribute [local instance] Ideal.Quotient.field

/-- Nontrivial commutative rings have the invariant basis number property.

There are two stronger results in mathlib: `commRing_strongRankCondition`, which says that any
nontrivial commutative ring satisfies the strong rank condition, and
`rankCondition_of_nontrivial_of_commSemiring`, which says that any nontrivial commutative semiring
satisfies the rank condition.

We prove this instance separately to avoid dependency on
`Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/Charpoly/Basic.lean` or `Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/Matrix/ToLin.lean`. -/
instance (priority := 100) invariantBasisNumber_of_nontrivial_of_commRing {R : Type u} [CommRing R]
    [Nontrivial R] : InvariantBasisNumber R :=
  ⟨fun e =>
    let ⟨I, _hI⟩ := Ideal.exists_maximal R
    eq_of_fin_equiv (R ⧸ I)
      ((Ideal.piQuotEquiv _ _).symm ≪≫ₗ inducedEquiv _ e ≪≫ₗ Ideal.piQuotEquiv _ _)⟩

end

name: zero_compLinearMap
statement: theorem zero_compLinearMap (g : M₂ →ₗ[R] M) : (0 : M [⋀^ι]→ₗ[R] N).compLinearMap g = 0
proof:
ext
  simp only [compLinearMap_apply, zero_apply]

@[simp]

name: lift_ι_apply
statement: theorem lift_ι_apply (a : M) : lift f (ι R M a) = f a
proof:
simp [lift, ι, algHom]

@[simp]

name: lift_ι
statement: theorem lift_ι  : lift (ι R M) = .id R (SymmetricAlgebra R M)
proof:
apply algHom_ext
  rw [lift_comp_ι]
  ext
  simp

/-- The left-inverse of `algebraMap`. -/

name: bot
statement: theorem bot  : x ≡ y [SMOD (⊥ : Submodule R M)] ↔ x = y
proof:
rw [SModEq.def, Submodule.Quotient.eq, mem_bot, sub_eq_zero]

@[gcongr, mono]

name: neg
statement: theorem neg (hxy : x ≡ y [SMOD U]) : -x ≡ - y [SMOD U]
proof:
simpa only [SModEq.def, Quotient.mk_neg, neg_inj]

@[gcongr]

name: independent_iff
statement: theorem independent_iff  : Independent f ↔ LinearIndependent K (Projectivization.rep ∘ f)
proof:
refine ⟨?_, fun h => ?_⟩
  · rintro ⟨ff, hff, hh⟩
    choose a ha using fun i : ι => exists_smul_eq_mk_rep K (ff i) (hff i)
    convert hh.units_smul a
    ext i
    exact (ha i).symm
  · convert Independent.mk _ _ h
    · simp only [mk_rep, Function.comp_apply]
    · intro i
      apply rep_nonzero

/-- A family of points in projective space is independent if and only if the family of
submodules which the points determine is independent in the lattice-theoretic sense. -/

name: independent_iff_iSupIndep
statement: theorem independent_iff_iSupIndep  : Independent f ↔ iSupIndep fun i => (f i).submodule
proof:
refine ⟨?_, fun h => ?_⟩
  · rintro ⟨f, hf, hi⟩
    simp only [submodule_mk]
    exact (iSupIndep_iff_linearIndependent_of_ne_zero (R := K) hf).mpr hi
  · rw [independent_iff]
    refine h.linearIndependent (Projectivization.submodule ∘ f) (fun i => ?_) fun i => ?_
    · simpa only [Function.comp_apply, submodule_eq] using Submodule.mem_span_singleton_self _
    · exact rep_nonzero (f i)

/-- A linearly dependent family of nonzero vectors gives a dependent family of points
in projective space. -/

name: dependent_iff
statement: theorem dependent_iff  : Dependent f ↔ ¬LinearIndependent K (Projectivization.rep ∘ f)
proof:
refine ⟨?_, fun h => ?_⟩
  · rintro ⟨ff, hff, hh1⟩
    contrapose hh1
    choose a ha using fun i : ι => exists_smul_eq_mk_rep K (ff i) (hff i)
    convert hh1.units_smul a⁻¹
    ext i
    simp only [← ha, inv_smul_smul, Pi.smul_apply', Pi.inv_apply, Function.comp_apply]
  · convert Dependent.mk _ _ h
    · simp only [mk_rep, Function.comp_apply]
    · exact fun i => rep_nonzero (f i)

/-- Dependence is the negation of independence. -/

name: dependent_iff_not_independent
statement: theorem dependent_iff_not_independent  : Dependent f ↔ ¬Independent f
proof:
rw [dependent_iff, independent_iff]

/-- Independence is the negation of dependence. -/

name: independent_iff_not_dependent
statement: theorem independent_iff_not_dependent  : Independent f ↔ ¬Dependent f
proof:
rw [dependent_iff_not_independent, Classical.not_not]

/-- Two points in a projective space are dependent if and only if they are equal. -/
@[simp]

name: dependent_pair_iff_eq
statement: theorem dependent_pair_iff_eq (u v : ℙ K V) : Dependent ![u, v] ↔ u = v
proof:
rw [dependent_iff_not_independent, independent_iff, linearIndependent_fin2]
  dsimp only [Function.comp_def, Matrix.cons_val]
  simp only [not_and, not_forall, not_not, ← mk_eq_mk_iff' K _ _ (rep_nonzero u) (rep_nonzero v),
    mk_rep, Classical.imp_iff_right_iff]
  exact Or.inl (rep_nonzero v)

/-- Two points in a projective space are independent if and only if the points are not equal. -/
@[simp]

name: independent_pair_iff_ne
statement: theorem independent_pair_iff_ne (u v : ℙ K V) : Independent ![u, v] ↔ u ≠ v
proof:
rw [independent_iff_not_dependent, dependent_pair_iff_eq u v]

end Projectivization

name: finite_iff_of_finite
statement: theorem finite_iff_of_finite  : Finite (ℙ k V) ↔ Finite V
proof:
classical
  refine ⟨fun h ↦ ?_, fun h ↦ inferInstance⟩
  let e := nonZeroEquivProjectivizationProdUnits k V
  have : Finite { v : V // v ≠ 0 } := Finite.of_equiv _ e.symm
  let eq : { v : V // v ≠ 0 } ⊕ Unit ≃ V :=
    ⟨(Sum.elim Subtype.val (fun _ ↦ 0)), fun v ↦ if h : v = 0 then Sum.inr () else Sum.inl ⟨v, h⟩,
      by intro x; aesop, by intro x; aesop⟩
  exact Finite.of_equiv _ eq

/-- Fraction free cardinality formula for the points of `ℙ k V` if `k` and `V` are finite
(for silly reasons the formula also holds when `k` and `V` are infinite).
See `Projectivization.card'` and `Projectivization.card''` for other spellings of the formula. -/

name: card
statement: theorem card  : Nat.card V - 1 = Nat.card (ℙ k V) * (Nat.card k - 1)
proof:
nontriviality V
  cases finite_or_infinite k with
  | inr h =>
    have : Infinite V := Module.Free.infinite k V
    simp
  | inl h =>
  cases finite_or_infinite V with
  | inr h =>
    have := not_iff_not.mpr (finite_iff_of_finite k V)
    push Not at this
    have : Infinite (ℙ k V) := by rwa [this]
    simp
  | inl h =>
  classical
  haveI : Fintype V := Fintype.ofFinite V
  haveI : Fintype (ℙ k V) := Fintype.ofFinite (ℙ k V)
  haveI : Fintype k := Fintype.ofFinite k
  have hV : Fintype.card { v : V // v ≠ 0 } = Fintype.card V - 1 := by simp
  simp_rw [← Fintype.card_eq_nat_card, ← Fintype.card_units (α := k), ← hV]
  rw [Fintype.card_congr (nonZeroEquivProjectivizationProdUnits k V), Fintype.card_prod]

/-- Cardinality formula for the points of `ℙ k V` if `k` and `V` are finite with less
natural subtraction. -/

name: span_univ
statement: theorem span_univ  : span (Set.univ : Set (ℙ K V)) = ⊤
proof:
rw [eq_top_iff, SetLike.le_def]
  intro x _hx
  exact subset_span _ (Set.mem_univ x)

/-- The span of a set of points is contained in a subspace if and only if the set of points is
contained in the subspace. -/

name: submodule_eq
statement: theorem submodule_eq (v : ℙ K V) : v.submodule = K ∙ v.rep
proof:
conv_lhs => rw [← v.mk_rep]
  rfl

name: finrank_submodule
statement: theorem finrank_submodule (v : ℙ K V) : finrank K v.submodule = 1
proof:
rw [submodule_eq]
  exact finrank_span_singleton v.rep_nonzero

instance (v : ℙ K V) : FiniteDimensional K v.submodule := by
  rw [← v.mk_rep]
  change FiniteDimensional K (K ∙ v.rep)
  infer_instance

name: map_id
statement: theorem map_id  : map (LinearMap.id : V →ₗ[K] V) (LinearEquiv.refl K V).injective = id
proof:
ext ⟨v⟩
  rfl

@[simp]

name: linearIndepOn_pair
statement: theorem linearIndepOn_pair (D D' : ℙ K V) : LinearIndepOn K id {D.rep, D'.rep}
proof:
by_cases h : D = D'
  · simpa [h] using D'.rep_nonzero
  rw [← ne_eq, ← linearIndependent_pair_iff_ne, LinearIndependent.pair_symm_iff,
    ← linearIndepOn_id_range_iff] at h
  · simpa using h
  · simpa [injective_pair_iff_ne, injective_pair_iff_ne, ne_eq] using h.injective

end linearIndependent

end Projectivization

name: ιMulti_span
statement: theorem ιMulti_span  : Submodule.span R (Set.range (ιMulti R n)) = (⊤ : Submodule R (⋀[R]^n M))
proof:
apply LinearMap.map_injective (Submodule.ker_subtype (⋀[R]^n M))
  rw [LinearMap.map_span, ← Set.image_univ, Set.image_image]
  simp only [Submodule.coe_subtype, ιMulti_apply_coe, Set.image_univ, Submodule.map_top,
    Submodule.range_subtype]
  exact ExteriorAlgebra.ιMulti_span_fixedDegree R n

open Set Submodule in
/-- A version of `ιMulti_span_fixedDegree_of_span_eq_top` that works in the exterior power. -/

name: alternatingMapLinearEquiv_comp_ιMulti
statement: theorem alternatingMapLinearEquiv_comp_ιMulti (f : M [⋀^Fin n]→ₗ[R] N) : (alternatingMapLinearEquiv f).compAlternatingMap (ιMulti R n) = f
proof:
obtain ⟨φ, rfl⟩ := alternatingMapLinearEquiv.symm.surjective f
  dsimp [alternatingMapLinearEquiv]
  simp only [LinearEquiv.symm_apply_apply]
  rfl

@[simp]

name: alternatingMapLinearEquiv_symm_map
statement: theorem alternatingMapLinearEquiv_symm_map (f : M →ₗ[R] N) : alternatingMapLinearEquiv.symm (map n f) = (ιMulti R n).compLinearMap f
proof:
simp only [map, LinearEquiv.symm_apply_apply]

@[simp]

name: map_comp_ιMulti
statement: theorem map_comp_ιMulti (f : M →ₗ[R] N) : (map n f).compAlternatingMap (ιMulti R n) = (ιMulti R n).compLinearMap f
proof:
simp only [map, alternatingMapLinearEquiv_comp_ιMulti]

@[simp]

name: zeroEquiv_ιMulti
statement: theorem zeroEquiv_ιMulti (f : Fin 0 → M) : zeroEquiv R M (ιMulti _ _ f) = 1
proof: simp [zeroEquiv]

name: oneEquiv_ιMulti
statement: theorem oneEquiv_ιMulti (f : Fin 1 → M) : oneEquiv R M (ιMulti _ _ f) = f 0
proof: simp [oneEquiv]

name: pairingDual_apply_apply_eq_one
statement: theorem pairingDual_apply_apply_eq_one (a : Fin n ↪o ι) : pairingDual R M n (ιMulti _ _ (f ∘ a)) (ιMulti _ _ (x ∘ a)) = 1
proof:
simp only [pairingDual_ιMulti_ιMulti, Function.comp_apply]
  rw [← Matrix.det_one (n := Fin n)]
  congr
  ext i j
  dsimp
  by_cases hij : i = j
  · subst hij
    simp only [h₁, Matrix.one_apply_eq]
  · rw [h₀ (by simpa using Ne.symm hij), Matrix.one_apply_ne hij]

include h₀ in

name: restrict_aux
statement: theorem restrict_aux  : Bijective (p.compl₁₂ i j)
proof:
refine ⟨LinearMap.ker_eq_bot.mp <| eq_bot_iff.mpr fun m hm ↦ ?_, fun f ↦ ?_⟩
  · replace hm : i m ∈ j.range.dualAnnihilator.map (p.toPerfPair.symm : Dual R N →ₗ[R] M) := by
      simp only [Submodule.mem_map, Submodule.mem_dualAnnihilator]
      refine ⟨p.toPerfPair (i m), ?_, LinearEquiv.symm_apply_apply _ _⟩
      rintro - ⟨n, rfl⟩
      simpa using LinearMap.congr_fun hm n
    suffices i m ∈ (⊥ : Submodule R M) by simpa [hi] using this
    simpa only [← hij.isCompl_left.inf_eq_bot, Submodule.mem_inf]
      using ⟨LinearMap.mem_range_self i m, hm⟩
  · set F : Module.Dual R N := f ∘ₗ j.linearProjOfIsCompl _ hj hij.isCompl_right with hF
    have hF (n : N') : F (j n) = f n := by simp [hF]
    set m : M := p.toPerfPair.symm F with hm
    obtain ⟨-, y, ⟨m₀, rfl⟩, hy, hm'⟩ :=
      Submodule.codisjoint_iff_exists_add_eq.mp hij.isCompl_left.codisjoint m
    refine ⟨m₀, LinearMap.ext fun n ↦ ?_⟩
    replace hy : (p y) (j n) = 0 := by
      simp only [Submodule.mem_map, Submodule.mem_dualAnnihilator] at hy
      obtain ⟨g, hg, rfl⟩ := hy
      simpa using hg _ (LinearMap.mem_range_self j n)
    rw [hm, ← LinearEquiv.symm_apply_eq, map_add, LinearEquiv.symm_symm] at hm'
    simpa [← hF, ← LinearMap.congr_fun hm' (j n)]

/-- The restriction of a perfect pairing to submodules is a perfect pairing. -/

name: left_top_iff
statement: theorem left_top_iff  : p.IsPerfectCompl ⊤ V ↔ V = ⊤
proof:
refine ⟨fun h ↦ ?_, fun h ↦ ?_⟩
  · exact eq_top_of_isCompl_bot <| by simpa using h.isCompl_right
  · rw [h]
    exact
      { isCompl_left := by simpa using isCompl_top_bot
        isCompl_right := by simpa using isCompl_top_bot }

@[simp]

name: right_top_iff
statement: theorem right_top_iff  : p.IsPerfectCompl U ⊤ ↔ U = ⊤
proof:
rw [← IsPerfectCompl.flip_iff]
  exact left_top_iff

end IsPerfectCompl

end LinearMap

variable [IsReflexive R M]

variable (e : N ≃ₗ[R] Dual R M)

namespace LinearEquiv

/-- For a reflexive module `M`, an equivalence `N ≃ₗ[R] Dual R M` naturally yields an equivalence
`M ≃ₗ[R] Dual R N`. Such equivalences are known as perfect pairings. -/

name: trans_dualMap_symm_flip
statement: theorem trans_dualMap_symm_flip  : e.trans e.flip.symm.dualMap = Dual.eval R N
proof:
ext; simp [symm_flip]

include e in
/-- If `N` is in perfect pairing with `M`, then it is reflexive. -/

name: isReflexive_of_equiv_dual_of_isReflexive
statement: theorem isReflexive_of_equiv_dual_of_isReflexive  : IsReflexive R N
proof:
constructor
  rw [← trans_dualMap_symm_flip e]
  exact LinearEquiv.bijective _

@[simp] lemma flip_flip (h : IsReflexive R N := isReflexive_of_equiv_dual_of_isReflexive e) :
    e.flip.flip = e := by
  ext; rfl

instance : e.toLinearMap.IsPerfPair where
  bijective_left := e.bijective
  bijective_right := e.flip.bijective

end LinearEquiv

namespace Submodule

open LinearEquiv

omit [IsReflexive R M] in
@[simp]

name: collinear_empty
statement: theorem collinear_empty  : Collinear k (∅ : Set P)
proof:
rw [collinear_iff_rank_le_one, vectorSpan_empty]
  simp

variable {P}

/-- A single point is collinear. -/

name: collinear_singleton
statement: theorem collinear_singleton (p : P) : Collinear k ({p} : Set P)
proof:
rw [collinear_iff_rank_le_one, vectorSpan_singleton]
  simp

variable {k}

/-- Given a point `p₀` in a set of points, that set is collinear if and
only if the points can all be expressed as multiples of the same
vector, added to `p₀`. -/

name: collinear_iff_exists_forall_eq_smul_vadd
statement: theorem collinear_iff_exists_forall_eq_smul_vadd (s : Set P) : Collinear k s ↔ ∃ (p₀ : P) (v : V), ∀ p ∈ s, ∃ r : k, p = r • v +ᵥ p₀
proof:
rcases Set.eq_empty_or_nonempty s with (rfl | ⟨⟨p₁, hp₁⟩⟩)
  · simp [collinear_empty]
  · rw [collinear_iff_of_mem hp₁]
    constructor
    · exact fun h => ⟨p₁, h⟩
    · rintro ⟨p, v, hv⟩
      use v
      intro p₂ hp₂
      rcases hv p₂ hp₂ with ⟨r, rfl⟩
      rcases hv p₁ hp₁ with ⟨r₁, rfl⟩
      use r - r₁
      simp [vadd_vadd, ← add_smul]

variable (k) in
/-- Two points are collinear. -/

name: collinear_pair
statement: theorem collinear_pair (p₁ p₂ : P) : Collinear k ({p₁, p₂} : Set P)
proof:
rw [collinear_iff_exists_forall_eq_smul_vadd]
  use p₁, p₂ -ᵥ p₁
  intro p hp
  rw [Set.mem_insert_iff, Set.mem_singleton_iff] at hp
  rcases hp with hp | hp
  · use 0
    simp [hp]
  · use 1
    simp [hp]

/-- Three points are affinely independent if and only if they are not
collinear. -/

name: slope_neg_fun
statement: theorem slope_neg_fun (f : k → E) : slope (-f) = -slope f
proof: ext x y; exact slope_neg f x y

name: slope_nonneg_iff_of_le
statement: theorem slope_nonneg_iff_of_le (hxy : x ≤ y) : 0 ≤ slope f x y ↔ f x ≤ f y
proof:
by_cases hxeqy : x = y
  · simp [hxeqy]
  refine ⟨fun h ↦ ?_, fun h ↦ smul_nonneg (inv_nonneg.2 (sub_nonneg.2 hxy)) ?_⟩
  · have := smul_nonneg (sub_nonneg.2 hxy) h
    rwa [slope, ← mul_smul, mul_inv_cancel₀ (mt sub_eq_zero.1 (Ne.symm hxeqy)), one_smul,
      vsub_eq_sub, sub_nonneg] at this
  · rwa [vsub_eq_sub, sub_nonneg]

name: slope_nonpos_iff_of_le
statement: theorem slope_nonpos_iff_of_le (hxy : x ≤ y) : slope f x y ≤ 0 ↔ f y ≤ f x
proof: simpa using slope_nonneg_iff_of_le (f := -f) hxy

name: slope_pos_iff_of_le
statement: theorem slope_pos_iff_of_le (hxy : x ≤ y) : 0 < slope f x y ↔ f x < f y
proof: simp_rw [lt_iff_le_and_ne, slope_nonneg_iff_of_le hxy, Ne, eq_comm, slope_eq_zero_iff]

name: slope_neg_iff_of_le
statement: theorem slope_neg_iff_of_le (hxy : x ≤ y) : slope f x y < 0 ↔ f y < f x
proof: simpa using slope_pos_iff_of_le (f := -f) hxy

name: centroid_univ
statement: theorem centroid_univ (s : Finset P) : univ.centroid k ((↑) : s → P) = s.centroid k id
proof:
rw [centroid, centroid, ← s.attach_affineCombination_coe]
  congr
  ext
  simp

/-- The centroid of a single point. -/
@[simp]

name: coord_reindex
statement: theorem coord_reindex (i : ι') : (b.reindex e).coord i = b.coord (e.symm i)
proof:
ext
  classical simp [AffineBasis.coord]

@[simp]

name: coord_apply_eq
statement: theorem coord_apply_eq (i : ι) : b.coord i (b i) = 1
proof:
simp only [coord, Basis.coe_sumCoords, map_zero, sub_zero,
    AffineMap.coe_mk, Finsupp.sum_zero_index, vsub_self]

@[simp]

name: coord_apply_ne
statement: theorem coord_apply_ne (h : i ≠ j) : b.coord i (b j) = 0
proof:
rw [coord, AffineMap.coe_mk, ← Subtype.coe_mk (p := (· ≠ i)) j h.symm, ← b.basisOf_apply,
    Basis.sumCoords_self_apply, sub_self]

name: toMatrix_self
statement: theorem toMatrix_self  : b.toMatrix b = (1 : Matrix ι ι k)

name: toMatrix_vecMul_coords
statement: theorem toMatrix_vecMul_coords (x : P) : b₂.coords x ᵥ* b.toMatrix b₂ = b.coords x
proof:
ext j
  change _ = b.coord j x
  conv_rhs => rw [← b₂.affineCombination_coord_eq_self x]
  rw [Finset.map_affineCombination _ _ _ (b₂.sum_coord_apply_eq_one x)]
  simp [Matrix.vecMul, dotProduct, toMatrix_apply, coords]

variable [DecidableEq ι]

name: toMatrix_mul_toMatrix
statement: theorem toMatrix_mul_toMatrix  : b.toMatrix b₂ * b₂.toMatrix b = 1
proof:
ext l m
  change (b.coords (b₂ l) ᵥ* b₂.toMatrix b) m = _
  rw [toMatrix_vecMul_coords, coords_apply, ← toMatrix_apply, toMatrix_self]

name: toMatrix_inv_vecMul_toMatrix
statement: theorem toMatrix_inv_vecMul_toMatrix (x : P) : b.coords x ᵥ* (b.toMatrix b₂)⁻¹ = b₂.coords x
proof:
have hu := b.isUnit_toMatrix b₂
  rw [Matrix.isUnit_iff_isUnit_det] at hu
  rw [← b.toMatrix_vecMul_coords b₂, Matrix.vecMul_vecMul, Matrix.mul_nonsing_inv _ hu,
    Matrix.vecMul_one]

/-- If we fix a background affine basis `b`, then for any other basis `b₂`, we can characterise
the barycentric coordinates provided by `b₂` in terms of determinants relative to `b`. -/

name: det_smul_coords_eq_cramer_coords
statement: theorem det_smul_coords_eq_cramer_coords (x : P) : (b.toMatrix b₂).det • b₂.coords x = (b.toMatrix b₂)ᵀ.cramer (b.coords x)
proof:
have hu := b.isUnit_toMatrix b₂
  rw [Matrix.isUnit_iff_isUnit_det] at hu
  rw [← b.toMatrix_inv_vecMul_toMatrix, Matrix.det_smul_inv_vecMul_eq_cramer_transpose _ _ hu]

end CommRing

end AffineBasis

name: pointwise_vadd_bot
statement: theorem pointwise_vadd_bot (v : V) : v +ᵥ (⊥ : AffineSubspace k P) = ⊥
proof:
ext; simp [pointwise_vadd_eq_map, map_bot]

@[simp] lemma pointwise_vadd_top (v : V) : v +ᵥ (⊤ : AffineSubspace k P) = ⊤ := by
  ext; simp [pointwise_vadd_eq_map, vadd_eq_iff_eq_neg_vadd]

name: smul_bot
statement: theorem smul_bot (a : M) : a • (⊥ : AffineSubspace k V) = ⊥
proof: ext; simp [smul_eq_map, map_bot]

name: smul_top
statement: theorem smul_top (ha : IsUnit a) : a • (⊤ : AffineSubspace k V) = ⊤
proof:
ext x; simpa [smul_eq_map, map_top] using ⟨ha.unit⁻¹ • x, smul_inv_smul ha.unit _⟩

end MulAction

end Ring

section Field
variable [Field k] [AddCommGroup V] [Module k V] {a : k}

@[simp]

name: midpoint_comm
statement: theorem midpoint_comm (x y : P) : midpoint R x y = midpoint R y x
proof: rw [midpoint, ← lineMap_apply_one_sub, one_sub_invOf_two, midpoint]

name: midpoint_vsub_right
statement: theorem midpoint_vsub_right (p₁ p₂ : P) : midpoint R p₁ p₂ -ᵥ p₂ = (⅟2 : R) • (p₁ -ᵥ p₂)
proof:
rw [midpoint_comm, midpoint_vsub_left]

@[simp]

name: right_vsub_midpoint
statement: theorem right_vsub_midpoint (p₁ p₂ : P) : p₂ -ᵥ midpoint R p₁ p₂ = (⅟2 : R) • (p₂ -ᵥ p₁)
proof: rw [midpoint_comm, left_vsub_midpoint]

name: midpoint_vsub
statement: theorem midpoint_vsub (p₁ p₂ p : P) : midpoint R p₁ p₂ -ᵥ p = (⅟2 : R) • (p₁ -ᵥ p) + (⅟2 : R) • (p₂ -ᵥ p)
proof:
rw [← vsub_sub_vsub_cancel_right p₁ p p₂, smul_sub, sub_eq_add_neg, ← smul_neg,
    neg_vsub_eq_vsub_rev, add_assoc, invOf_two_smul_add_invOf_two_smul, ← vadd_vsub_assoc,
    midpoint_comm, midpoint, lineMap_apply]

name: vsub_midpoint
statement: theorem vsub_midpoint (p₁ p₂ p : P) : p -ᵥ midpoint R p₁ p₂ = (⅟2 : R) • (p -ᵥ p₁) + (⅟2 : R) • (p -ᵥ p₂)
proof:
rw [← neg_vsub_eq_vsub_rev, midpoint_vsub, neg_add, ← smul_neg, ← smul_neg, neg_vsub_eq_vsub_rev,
    neg_vsub_eq_vsub_rev]

@[simp]

name: midpoint_eq_smul_add
statement: theorem midpoint_eq_smul_add (x y : V) : midpoint R x y = (⅟2 : R) • (x + y)
proof:
rw [midpoint_eq_iff, pointReflection_apply, vsub_eq_sub, vadd_eq_add, sub_add_eq_add_sub, ←
    two_smul R, smul_smul, mul_invOf_self, one_smul, add_sub_cancel_left]

@[simp]

name: midpoint_self_neg
statement: theorem midpoint_self_neg (x : V) : midpoint R x (-x) = 0
proof:
rw [midpoint_eq_smul_add, add_neg_cancel, smul_zero]

@[simp]

name: midpoint_sub_add
statement: theorem midpoint_sub_add (x y : V) : midpoint R (x - y) (x + y) = x
proof:
rw [sub_eq_add_neg, ← vadd_eq_add, ← vadd_eq_add, ← midpoint_vadd_midpoint]; simp

@[simp]

name: midpoint_add_sub
statement: theorem midpoint_add_sub (x y : V) : midpoint R (x + y) (x - y) = x
proof: rw [midpoint_comm]; simp

name: midpoint_vsub_midpoint_same_left
statement: theorem midpoint_vsub_midpoint_same_left (p₁ p₂ p₃ : P) : midpoint R p₁ p₂ -ᵥ midpoint R p₁ p₃ = (⅟2 : R) • (p₂ -ᵥ p₃)
proof: rw [midpoint_vsub_midpoint, vsub_self, midpoint_eq_smul_add, zero_add]

name: midpoint_vsub_midpoint_same_right
statement: theorem midpoint_vsub_midpoint_same_right (p₁ p₂ p₃ : P) : midpoint R p₁ p₃ -ᵥ midpoint R p₂ p₃ = (⅟2 : R) • (p₁ -ᵥ p₂)
proof:
rw [midpoint_vsub_midpoint, vsub_self, midpoint_eq_smul_add, add_zero]

end

namespace AddMonoidHom

variable (R R' : Type*) {E F : Type*} [Ring R] [Invertible (2 : R)] [AddCommGroup E] [Module R E]
  [Ring R'] [Invertible (2 : R')] [AddCommGroup F] [Module R' F]

/-- A map `f : E → F` sending zero to zero and midpoints to midpoints is an `AddMonoidHom`. -/

name: linear_eq_zero_iff_exists_const
statement: theorem linear_eq_zero_iff_exists_const (f : P1 →ᵃ[k] P2) : f.linear = 0 ↔ ∃ q, f = const k P1 q
proof:
refine ⟨fun h => ?_, fun h => ?_⟩
  · use f (Classical.arbitrary P1)
    ext
    rw [coe_const, Function.const_apply, ← @vsub_eq_zero_iff_eq V2, ← f.linearMap_vsub, h,
      LinearMap.zero_apply]
  · rcases h with ⟨q, rfl⟩
    exact const_linear k P1 q

instance nonempty : Nonempty (P1 →ᵃ[k] P2) :=
  (AddTorsor.nonempty : Nonempty P2).map <| const k P1

/-- Construct an affine map by verifying the relation between the map and its linear part at one
base point. Namely, this function takes a map `f : P₁ → P₂`, a linear map `f' : V₁ →ₗ[k] V₂`, and
a point `p` such that for any other point `p'` we have `f p' = f' (p' -ᵥ p) +ᵥ f p`. -/

name: linear_injective_iff
statement: theorem linear_injective_iff (f : P1 →ᵃ[k] P2) : Function.Injective f.linear ↔ Function.Injective f
proof:
obtain ⟨p⟩ := (inferInstance : Nonempty P1)
  have h : ⇑f.linear = (Equiv.vaddConst (f p)).symm ∘ f ∘ Equiv.vaddConst p := by
    ext v
    simp [f.map_vadd]
  rw [h, Equiv.comp_injective, Equiv.injective_comp]

@[simp]

name: linear_surjective_iff
statement: theorem linear_surjective_iff (f : P1 →ᵃ[k] P2) : Function.Surjective f.linear ↔ Function.Surjective f
proof:
obtain ⟨p⟩ := (inferInstance : Nonempty P1)
  have h : ⇑f.linear = (Equiv.vaddConst (f p)).symm ∘ f ∘ Equiv.vaddConst p := by
    ext v
    simp [f.map_vadd]
  rw [h, Equiv.comp_surjective, Equiv.surjective_comp]

@[simp]

name: lineMap_apply_zero
statement: theorem lineMap_apply_zero (p₀ p₁ : P1) : lineMap p₀ p₁ (0 : k) = p₀
proof:
simp [lineMap_apply]

@[simp]

name: lineMap_apply_one
statement: theorem lineMap_apply_one (p₀ p₁ : P1) : lineMap p₀ p₁ (1 : k) = p₁
proof:
simp [lineMap_apply]

@[simp]

name: lineMap_symm
statement: theorem lineMap_symm (p₀ p₁ : P1) : lineMap p₀ p₁ = (lineMap p₁ p₀).comp (lineMap (1 : k) (0 : k))
proof:
simp

@[simp]

name: decomp
statement: theorem decomp (f : V1 →ᵃ[k] V2) : (f : V1 → V2) = ⇑f.linear + fun _ => f 0
proof:
ext x
  calc
    f x = f.linear x +ᵥ f 0 := by rw [← f.map_vadd, vadd_eq_add, add_zero]
    _ = (f.linear + fun _ : V1 => f 0) x := rfl

/-- Decomposition of an affine map in the special case when the point space and vector space
are the same. -/

name: pi_eq_zero
statement: theorem pi_eq_zero  : pi fv = 0 ↔ ∀ i, fv i = 0
proof:
simp only [AffineMap.ext_iff, funext_iff, pi_apply]
  exact forall_comm

name: pi_zero
statement: theorem pi_zero  : pi (fun _ ↦ 0 : (i : ι) → P1 →ᵃ[k] φv i) = 0
proof: ext; rfl

name: homothety_one
statement: theorem homothety_one (c : P1) : homothety c (1 : k) = id k P1
proof:
ext p
  simp [homothety_apply]

@[simp]

name: homothety_zero
statement: theorem homothety_zero (c : P1) : homothety c (0 : k) = const k P1 c
proof:
ext p
  simp [homothety_apply]

@[simp]

name: toAffineMap_injective
statement: theorem toAffineMap_injective  : Injective (toAffineMap : (P₁ ≃ᵃ[k] P₂) → P₁ →ᵃ[k] P₂)
proof:
rintro ⟨e, el, h⟩ ⟨e', el', h'⟩ H
  simp_all

@[simp]

name: ofLinearEquiv_refl
statement: theorem ofLinearEquiv_refl (p : P) : ofLinearEquiv (.refl k V) p p = .refl k P
proof:
ext x
  simp [ofLinearEquiv_apply]

@[simp]

name: homothety_neg_one_apply
statement: theorem homothety_neg_one_apply (c p : P₁) : homothety c (-1 : R') p = pointReflection R' c p
proof:
simp [homothety_apply, Equiv.pointReflection_apply]

end AffineMap

name: lineMap_lt_lineMap_iff_of_lt
statement: theorem lineMap_lt_lineMap_iff_of_lt (h : r < r') : lineMap a b r < lineMap a b r' ↔ a < b
proof:
simp only [lineMap_apply_module]
  rw [← lt_sub_iff_add_lt, add_sub_assoc, ← sub_lt_iff_lt_add', ← sub_smul, ← sub_smul,
    sub_sub_sub_cancel_left, smul_lt_smul_iff_of_pos_left (sub_pos.2 h)]

name: left_le_lineMap_iff_nonneg
statement: theorem left_le_lineMap_iff_nonneg (h : a < b) : a ≤ lineMap a b r ↔ 0 ≤ r
proof: rw [← lineMap_le_lineMap_iff_of_lt' h, lineMap_apply_zero]

name: lineMap_le_left_iff_nonpos
statement: theorem lineMap_le_left_iff_nonpos (h : a < b) : lineMap a b r ≤ a ↔ r ≤ 0
proof: rw [← lineMap_le_lineMap_iff_of_lt' h, lineMap_apply_zero]

name: right_le_lineMap_iff_one_le
