statement: theorem right_le_lineMap_iff_one_le (h : a < b) : b ≤ lineMap a b r ↔ 1 ≤ r
proof: rw [← lineMap_le_lineMap_iff_of_lt' h, lineMap_apply_one]

name: lineMap_le_right_iff_le_one
statement: theorem lineMap_le_right_iff_le_one (h : a < b) : lineMap a b r ≤ b ↔ r ≤ 1
proof: rw [← lineMap_le_lineMap_iff_of_lt' h, lineMap_apply_one]

name: left_lt_lineMap_iff_pos
statement: theorem left_lt_lineMap_iff_pos (h : a < b) : a < lineMap a b r ↔ 0 < r
proof: rw [← lineMap_lt_lineMap_iff_of_lt' h, lineMap_apply_zero]

name: lineMap_lt_left_iff_neg
statement: theorem lineMap_lt_left_iff_neg (h : a < b) : lineMap a b r < a ↔ r < 0
proof: rw [← lineMap_lt_lineMap_iff_of_lt' h, lineMap_apply_zero]

name: right_lt_lineMap_iff_one_lt
statement: theorem right_lt_lineMap_iff_one_lt (h : a < b) : b < lineMap a b r ↔ 1 < r
proof: rw [← lineMap_lt_lineMap_iff_of_lt' h, lineMap_apply_one]

name: lineMap_lt_right_iff_lt_one
statement: theorem lineMap_lt_right_iff_lt_one (h : a < b) : lineMap a b r < b ↔ r < 1
proof: rw [← lineMap_lt_lineMap_iff_of_lt' h, lineMap_apply_one]

name: lineMap_le_lineMap_iff_of_lt
statement: theorem lineMap_le_lineMap_iff_of_lt (h : r < r') : lineMap a b r ≤ lineMap a b r' ↔ a ≤ b
proof:
simp only [lineMap_apply_module]
  rw [← le_sub_iff_add_le, add_sub_assoc, ← sub_le_iff_le_add', ← sub_smul, ← sub_smul,
    sub_sub_sub_cancel_left, smul_le_smul_iff_of_pos_left (sub_pos.2 h)]

name: exists_affineIndependent
statement: theorem exists_affineIndependent (s : Set P) : ∃ t ⊆ s, affineSpan k t = affineSpan k s ∧ AffineIndependent k ((↑) : t → P)
proof:
rcases s.eq_empty_or_nonempty with (rfl | ⟨p, hp⟩)
  · exact ⟨∅, Set.empty_subset ∅, rfl, affineIndependent_of_subsingleton k _⟩
  obtain ⟨b, hb₁, hb₂, hb₃⟩ := exists_linearIndependent k ((Equiv.vaddConst p).symm '' s)
  have hb₀ : ∀ v : V, v ∈ b → v ≠ 0 := fun v hv => hb₃.ne_zero (⟨v, hv⟩ : b)
  rw [linearIndependent_set_iff_affineIndependent_vadd_union_singleton k hb₀ p] at hb₃
  refine ⟨{p} ∪ Equiv.vaddConst p '' b, ?_, ?_, hb₃⟩
  · apply Set.union_subset (Set.singleton_subset_iff.mpr hp)
    rwa [← (Equiv.vaddConst p).subset_symm_image b s]
  · rw [Equiv.coe_vaddConst_symm, ← vectorSpan_eq_span_vsub_set_right k hp] at hb₂
    apply AffineSubspace.ext_of_direction_eq
    · have : Submodule.span k b = Submodule.span k (insert 0 b) := by simp
      simp only [direction_affineSpan, ← hb₂, Equiv.coe_vaddConst, Set.singleton_union,
        vectorSpan_eq_span_vsub_set_right k (Set.mem_insert p _), this]
      congr
      change (Equiv.vaddConst p).symm '' insert p (Equiv.vaddConst p '' b) = _
      rw [Set.image_insert_eq, ← Set.image_comp]
      simp
    · use p
      simp only [Equiv.coe_vaddConst, Set.singleton_union, Set.mem_inter_iff]
      exact ⟨mem_affineSpan k (Set.mem_insert p _), mem_affineSpan k hp⟩

variable {V}

/-- Two different points are affinely independent. -/

name: algebraMap₀_eq_smul_one
statement: theorem algebraMap₀_eq_smul_one (r : R) : (algebraMap₀ r : (⨂[R]^0) M) = r • ₜ1
proof: simp [algebraMap₀]; congr

name: algebraMap₀_mul_algebraMap₀
statement: theorem algebraMap₀_mul_algebraMap₀ (r s : R) : cast R M (add_zero _) (algebraMap₀ r ₜ* algebraMap₀ s) = algebraMap₀ (r * s)
proof:
rw [← smul_eq_mul, map_smul]
  exact algebraMap₀_mul r (@algebraMap₀ R M _ _ _ s)

instance gsemiring : DirectSum.GSemiring fun i => ⨂[R]^i M :=
  { TensorPower.gmonoid with
    mul_zero := fun _ => map_zero _
    zero_mul := fun _ => LinearMap.map_zero₂ _ _
    mul_add := fun _ _ _ => map_add _ _ _
    add_mul := fun _ _ _ => LinearMap.map_add₂ _ _ _ _
    natCast := fun n => algebraMap₀ (n : R)
    natCast_zero := by simp only [Nat.cast_zero, map_zero]
    natCast_succ := fun n => by simp only [Nat.cast_succ, map_add, algebraMap₀_one] }

name: finsuppTensorFinsuppRid_self
statement: theorem finsuppTensorFinsuppRid_self  : finsuppTensorFinsuppRid R R ι κ = finsuppTensorFinsupp' R ι κ
proof:
rw [finsuppTensorFinsupp', finsuppTensorFinsuppLid, finsuppTensorFinsuppRid,
    TensorProduct.lid_eq_rid]

end

name: of_left_eq_zero
statement: theorem of_left_eq_zero (v : V) : transvection (0 : Dual R V) v = id
proof:
ext
  simp [transvection]

@[simp]

name: of_right_eq_zero
statement: theorem of_right_eq_zero (f : Dual R V) : transvection f 0 = id
proof:
ext
  simp [transvection]

name: transvections_subset_dilatransvections
statement: theorem transvections_subset_dilatransvections  : transvections R V ⊆ dilatransvections R V
proof:
rintro e ⟨f, v, hfv, rfl⟩
  exact ⟨f, v, by simp⟩

@[simp]

name: involute_comp_involute
statement: theorem involute_comp_involute  : involute.comp involute = AlgHom.id R (CliffordAlgebra Q)
proof: ext; simp

name: evenOdd_map_reverse
statement: theorem evenOdd_map_reverse (n : ZMod 2) : (evenOdd Q n).map (reverse : CliffordAlgebra Q →ₗ[R] CliffordAlgebra Q) = evenOdd Q n
proof:
simp_rw [evenOdd, Submodule.map_iSup, submodule_map_pow_reverse, ι_range_map_reverse]

@[simp]

name: evenOdd_comap_reverse
statement: theorem evenOdd_comap_reverse (n : ZMod 2) : (evenOdd Q n).comap (reverse : CliffordAlgebra Q →ₗ[R] CliffordAlgebra Q) = evenOdd Q n
proof:
rw [← submodule_map_reverse_eq_comap, evenOdd_map_reverse]

end Reverse

@[simp]

name: contractLeft_ι
statement: theorem contractLeft_ι (x : M) : d⌋ι Q x = algebraMap R _ (d x)
proof:
-- Porting note: Lean cannot figure out anymore the third argument
  refine (foldr'_ι _ _ ?_ _ _).trans <| by
    simp_rw [contractLeftAux_apply_apply, mul_zero, sub_zero,
      Algebra.algebraMap_eq_smul_one]
  exact fun m x fx ↦ contractLeftAux_contractLeftAux Q d m x fx

@[simp]

name: contractRight_ι
statement: theorem contractRight_ι (x : M) : ι Q x⌊d = algebraMap R _ (d x)
proof:
rw [contractRight_eq, reverse_ι, contractLeft_ι, reverse.commutes]

@[simp]

name: contractLeft_algebraMap
statement: theorem contractLeft_algebraMap (r : R) : d⌋algebraMap R (CliffordAlgebra Q) r = 0
proof:
-- Porting note: Lean cannot figure out anymore the third argument
  refine (foldr'_algebraMap _ _ ?_ _ _).trans <| smul_zero _
  exact fun m x fx ↦ contractLeftAux_contractLeftAux Q d m x fx

@[simp]

name: contractRight_algebraMap
statement: theorem contractRight_algebraMap (r : R) : algebraMap R (CliffordAlgebra Q) r⌊d = 0
proof:
rw [contractRight_eq, reverse.commutes, contractLeft_algebraMap, map_zero]

@[simp]

name: contractLeft_one
statement: theorem contractLeft_one  : d⌋(1 : CliffordAlgebra Q) = 0
proof:
simpa only [map_one] using contractLeft_algebraMap Q d 1

@[simp]

name: contractRight_one
statement: theorem contractRight_one  : (1 : CliffordAlgebra Q)⌊d = 0

name: contractLeft_contractLeft
statement: theorem contractLeft_contractLeft (x : CliffordAlgebra Q) : d⌋(d⌋x) = 0
proof:
induction x using CliffordAlgebra.left_induction with
  | algebraMap => simp_rw [contractLeft_algebraMap, map_zero]
  | add _ _ hx hy => rw [map_add, map_add, hx, hy, add_zero]
  | ι_mul _ _ hx =>
    rw [contractLeft_ι_mul, map_sub, contractLeft_ι_mul, hx, map_smul, mul_zero, sub_zero, sub_self]

/-- This is [grinberg_clifford_2016] Theorem 13 -/

name: contractRight_contractRight
statement: theorem contractRight_contractRight (x : CliffordAlgebra Q) : x⌊d⌊d = 0
proof:
rw [contractRight_eq, contractRight_eq, reverse_reverse, contractLeft_contractLeft, map_zero]

/-- This is [grinberg_clifford_2016] Theorem 8 -/

name: contractLeft_comm
statement: theorem contractLeft_comm (x : CliffordAlgebra Q) : d⌋(d'⌋x) = -(d'⌋(d⌋x))
proof:
induction x using CliffordAlgebra.left_induction with
  | algebraMap => simp_rw [contractLeft_algebraMap, map_zero, neg_zero]
  | add _ _ hx hy => rw [map_add, map_add, map_add, map_add, hx, hy, neg_add]
  | ι_mul _ _ hx =>
    simp only [contractLeft_ι_mul, map_sub, map_smul]
    rw [neg_sub, sub_sub_eq_add_sub, hx, mul_neg, ← sub_eq_add_neg]

/-- This is [grinberg_clifford_2016] Theorem 14 -/

name: contractRight_comm
statement: theorem contractRight_comm (x : CliffordAlgebra Q) : x⌊d⌊d' = -(x⌊d'⌊d)
proof:
rw [contractRight_eq, contractRight_eq, contractRight_eq, contractRight_eq, reverse_reverse,
    reverse_reverse, contractLeft_comm, map_neg]

/- TODO:

name: changeForm_one
statement: theorem changeForm_one  : changeForm h (1 : CliffordAlgebra Q) = 1
proof:
simpa using changeForm_algebraMap h (1 : R)

@[simp]

name: changeForm_ι_mul_ι
statement: theorem changeForm_ι_mul_ι (m₁ m₂ : M) : changeForm h (ι Q m₁ * ι Q m₂) = ι Q' m₁ * ι Q' m₂ - algebraMap _ _ (B m₁ m₂)
proof:
rw [changeForm_ι_mul, changeForm_ι, contractLeft_ι]

/-- Theorem 23 of [grinberg_clifford_2016] -/

name: changeForm_changeForm
statement: theorem changeForm_changeForm (x : CliffordAlgebra Q) : changeForm h' (changeForm h x) = changeForm (changeForm.add_proof h h') x
proof:
induction x using CliffordAlgebra.left_induction with
  | algebraMap => simp_rw [changeForm_algebraMap]
  | add _ _ hx hy => rw [map_add, map_add, map_add, hx, hy]
  | ι_mul _ _ hx => rw [changeForm_ι_mul, map_sub, changeForm_ι_mul, changeForm_ι_mul, hx, sub_sub,
      LinearMap.add_apply, map_add, LinearMap.add_apply, changeForm_contractLeft, hx,
      add_comm (_ : CliffordAlgebra Q'')]

name: one_le_evenOdd_zero
statement: theorem one_le_evenOdd_zero  : 1 ≤ evenOdd Q 0
proof:
refine le_trans ?_ (le_iSup _ ⟨0, Nat.cast_zero⟩)
  exact (pow_zero _).ge

name: range_ι_le_evenOdd_one
statement: theorem range_ι_le_evenOdd_one  : LinearMap.range (ι Q) ≤ evenOdd Q 1
proof:
refine le_trans ?_ (le_iSup _ ⟨1, Nat.cast_one⟩)
  exact (pow_one _).ge

name: evenOdd_mul_le
statement: theorem evenOdd_mul_le (i j : ZMod 2) : evenOdd Q i * evenOdd Q j ≤ evenOdd Q (i + j)
proof:
simp_rw [evenOdd, Submodule.iSup_eq_span, Submodule.span_mul_span]
  apply Submodule.span_mono
  simp_rw [Set.iUnion_mul, Set.mul_iUnion, Set.iUnion_subset_iff, Set.mul_subset_iff]
  rintro ⟨xi, rfl⟩ ⟨yi, rfl⟩ x hx y hy
  refine Set.mem_iUnion.mpr ⟨⟨xi + yi, Nat.cast_add _ _⟩, ?_⟩
  simp only [pow_add]
  exact Submodule.mul_mem_mul hx hy

instance evenOdd.gradedMonoid : SetLike.GradedMonoid (evenOdd Q) where
  one_mem := Submodule.one_le.mp (one_le_evenOdd_zero Q)
  mul_mem _i _j _p _q hp hq := Submodule.mul_le.mp (evenOdd_mul_le Q _ _) _ hp _ hq

/-- A version of `CliffordAlgebra.ι` that maps directly into the graded structure. This is
primarily an auxiliary construction used to provide `CliffordAlgebra.gradedAlgebra`. -/
protected def GradedAlgebra.ι : M →ₗ[R] ⨁ i : ZMod 2, evenOdd Q i :=
  DirectSum.lof R (ZMod 2) (fun i => ↥(evenOdd Q i)) 1 ∘ₗ (ι Q).codRestrict _ (ι_mem_evenOdd_one Q)

name: iSup_ι_range_eq_top
statement: theorem iSup_ι_range_eq_top  : ⨆ i : ℕ, LinearMap.range (ι Q) ^ i = ⊤
proof:
rw [← (DirectSum.Decomposition.isInternal (evenOdd Q)).submodule_iSup_eq_top, eq_comm]
  calc
    -- Porting note: needs extra annotations, no longer unifies against the goal in the face of
    -- ambiguity
    ⨆ (i : ZMod 2) (j : { n : ℕ // ↑n = i }), LinearMap.range (ι Q) ^ (j : ℕ) =
        ⨆ i : Σ i : ZMod 2, { n : ℕ // ↑n = i }, LinearMap.range (ι Q) ^ (i.2 : ℕ) := by
      rw [iSup_sigma]
    _ = ⨆ i : ℕ, LinearMap.range (ι Q) ^ i :=
      Function.Surjective.iSup_congr (fun i => i.2) (fun i => ⟨⟨_, i, rfl⟩, rfl⟩) fun _ => rfl

name: toComplex_involute
statement: theorem toComplex_involute (c : CliffordAlgebra Q) : toComplex (involute c) = conj (toComplex c)
proof:
have : toComplex (involute (ι Q 1)) = conj (toComplex (ι Q 1)) := by
    simp only [involute_ι, toComplex_ι, map_neg, one_smul, Complex.conj_I]
  suffices toComplex.comp involute = Complex.conjAe.toAlgHom.comp toComplex by
    exact AlgHom.congr_fun this c
  ext : 2
  exact this

/-- Intermediate result for `CliffordAlgebraComplex.equiv`: `ℂ` can be converted to
`CliffordAlgebraComplex.Q` above can be converted to. -/

name: toComplex_comp_ofComplex
statement: theorem toComplex_comp_ofComplex  : toComplex.comp ofComplex = AlgHom.id ℝ ℂ
proof:
ext1
  dsimp only [AlgHom.comp_apply, Subtype.coe_mk, AlgHom.id_apply]
  rw [ofComplex_I, toComplex_ι, one_smul]

@[simp]

name: ofComplex_comp_toComplex
statement: theorem ofComplex_comp_toComplex  : ofComplex.comp toComplex = AlgHom.id ℝ (CliffordAlgebra Q)
proof:
ext
  dsimp only [LinearMap.comp_apply, Subtype.coe_mk, AlgHom.id_apply, AlgHom.toLinearMap_apply,
    AlgHom.comp_apply]
  rw [toComplex_ι, one_smul, ofComplex_I]

@[simp]

name: reverse_apply
statement: theorem reverse_apply (x : CliffordAlgebra Q) : x.reverse = x
proof:
induction x using CliffordAlgebra.induction with
  | algebraMap r => exact reverse.commutes _
  | ι x => rw [reverse_ι]
  | mul x₁ x₂ hx₁ hx₂ => rw [reverse.map_mul, mul_comm, hx₁, hx₂]
  | add x₁ x₂ hx₁ hx₂ => rw [reverse.map_add, hx₁, hx₂]

@[simp]

name: ofQuaternion_comp_toQuaternion
statement: theorem ofQuaternion_comp_toQuaternion  : ofQuaternion.comp toQuaternion = AlgHom.id R (CliffordAlgebra (Q c₁ c₂))
proof:
ext : 2 <;> (ext; simp)

@[simp]

name: toQuaternion_comp_ofQuaternion
statement: theorem toQuaternion_comp_ofQuaternion  : toQuaternion.comp ofQuaternion = AlgHom.id R ℍ[R,c₁,0,c₂]
proof:
ext : 1 <;> simp

@[simp]

name: ι_mul_ι
statement: theorem ι_mul_ι (r₁ r₂) : ι (0 : QuadraticForm R R) r₁ * ι (0 : QuadraticForm R R) r₂ = 0
proof:
rw [← mul_one r₁, ← mul_one r₂, ← smul_eq_mul r₁, ← smul_eq_mul r₂, map_smul, map_smul,
    smul_mul_smul_comm, ι_sq_scalar, QuadraticMap.zero_apply, map_zero, smul_zero]

/-- The clifford algebra over a 1-dimensional vector space with 0 quadratic form is isomorphic to
the dual numbers. -/
protected def equiv : CliffordAlgebra (0 : QuadraticForm R R) ≃ₐ[R] R[ε] :=
  AlgEquiv.ofAlgHom
    (CliffordAlgebra.lift (0 : QuadraticForm R R) ⟨inrHom R _, fun m => inr_mul_inr _ m m⟩)
    (DualNumber.lift ⟨
      (Algebra.ofId _ _, ι (R := R) _ 1),
      ι_mul_ι (1 : R) 1,
      fun _ => (Algebra.commutes _ _).symm⟩)
    (by ext : 1; simp) (by ext : 2; simp)

@[simp]

name: toProd_ι_tmul_one
statement: theorem toProd_ι_tmul_one (m₁ : M₁) : toProd Q₁ Q₂ (ι _ m₁ ᵍ⊗ₜ 1) = ι _ (m₁, 0)
proof:
rw [toProd, GradedTensorProduct.lift_tmul, map_one, mul_one, map_apply_ι,
    QuadraticMap.Isometry.inl_apply]

@[simp]

name: toProd_one_tmul_ι
statement: theorem toProd_one_tmul_ι (m₂ : M₂) : toProd Q₁ Q₂ (1 ᵍ⊗ₜ ι _ m₂) = ι _ (0, m₂)
proof:
rw [toProd, GradedTensorProduct.lift_tmul, map_one, one_mul, map_apply_ι,
    QuadraticMap.Isometry.inr_apply]

name: toProd_comp_ofProd
statement: theorem toProd_comp_ofProd  : (toProd Q₁ Q₂).comp (ofProd Q₁ Q₂) = AlgHom.id _ _
proof:
ext m <;> dsimp
  · rw [ofProd_ι_mk, map_add, toProd_one_tmul_ι, toProd_ι_tmul_one, Prod.mk_zero_zero,
      map_zero, add_zero]
  · rw [ofProd_ι_mk, map_add, toProd_one_tmul_ι, toProd_ι_tmul_one, Prod.mk_zero_zero,
      map_zero, zero_add]

name: ofProd_comp_toProd
statement: theorem ofProd_comp_toProd  : (ofProd Q₁ Q₂).comp (toProd Q₁ Q₂) = AlgHom.id _ _
proof:
ext <;> simp

/-- The Clifford algebra over an orthogonal direct sum of quadratic vector spaces is isomorphic
as an algebra to the graded tensor product of the Clifford algebras of each space.

This is `CliffordAlgebra.toProd` and `CliffordAlgebra.ofProd` as an equivalence. -/
@[simps!]

name: toBaseChange_comp_ofBaseChange
statement: theorem toBaseChange_comp_ofBaseChange (Q : QuadraticForm R V) : (toBaseChange A Q).comp (ofBaseChange A Q) = AlgHom.id _ _
proof:
ext v
  simp

@[simp] theorem toBaseChange_ofBaseChange (Q : QuadraticForm R V) (x : A ⊗[R] CliffordAlgebra Q) :
    toBaseChange A Q (ofBaseChange A Q x) = x :=
  AlgHom.congr_fun (toBaseChange_comp_ofBaseChange A Q :) x

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in

name: ofBaseChange_comp_toBaseChange
statement: theorem ofBaseChange_comp_toBaseChange (Q : QuadraticForm R V) : (ofBaseChange A Q).comp (toBaseChange A Q) = AlgHom.id _ _
proof:
ext x
  change ofBaseChange A Q (toBaseChange A Q (ι (Q.baseChange A) (1 ⊗ₜ[R] x)))
    = ι (Q.baseChange A) (1 ⊗ₜ[R] x)
  rw [toBaseChange_ι, ofBaseChange_tmul_ι]

@[simp] theorem ofBaseChange_toBaseChange
    (Q : QuadraticForm R V) (x : CliffordAlgebra (Q.baseChange A)) :
    ofBaseChange A Q (toBaseChange A Q x) = x :=
  AlgHom.congr_fun (ofBaseChange_comp_toBaseChange A Q :) x

/-- Base-changing the vector space of a clifford algebra is isomorphic as an A-algebra to
base-changing the clifford algebra itself; $<|Cℓ(A ⊗_R V, Q_A) ≅ A ⊗_R Cℓ(V, Q)<|$.

This is `CliffordAlgebra.toBaseChange` and `CliffordAlgebra.ofBaseChange` as an equivalence. -/
@[simps!]

name: star_algebraMap
statement: theorem star_algebraMap (r : R) : star (algebraMap R (CliffordAlgebra Q) r) = algebraMap R (CliffordAlgebra Q) r
proof:
rw [star_def, involute.commutes, reverse.commutes]

end CliffordAlgebra

name: aux_mul
statement: theorem aux_mul (x y : even Q) : aux f (x * y) = aux f x * aux f y
proof:
obtain ⟨x, x_property⟩ := x
  cases y
  refine (congr_arg Prod.fst (foldr_mul _ _ _ _ _ _)).trans ?_
  dsimp only
  induction x, x_property using even_induction Q with
  | algebraMap r =>
    generalize_proofs at ⊢
    simpa using Algebra.smul_def r _
  | add x y hx hy ihx ihy =>
    rw [map_add, Prod.fst_add]
    simp [ihx, ihy, ← add_mul, ← map_add]
  | ι_mul_ι_mul m₁ m₂ x hx ih =>
    simp [aux_apply, ih, ← mul_assoc]

end even.lift

open even.lift

variable (Q)

/-- Every algebra morphism from the even subalgebra is in one-to-one correspondence with a
bilinear map that sends duplicate arguments to the quadratic form, and contracts across
multiplication. -/
@[simps! symm_apply_bilin]

name: ι_sq_scalar
statement: theorem ι_sq_scalar (m : M) : ι Q m * ι Q m = algebraMap R _ (Q m)
proof:
rw [ι]
  erw [LinearMap.comp_apply]
  rw [AlgHom.toLinearMap_apply]
  erw [← map_mul (RingQuot.mkAlgHom R (Rel Q))]
  rw [RingQuot.mkAlgHom_rel R (Rel.of m), AlgHom.commutes]
  rfl

variable {Q} {A : Type*} [Semiring A] [Algebra R A]

@[simp]

name: lift_comp_ι
statement: theorem lift_comp_ι (g : CliffordAlgebra Q →ₐ[R] A) : lift Q ⟨g.toLinearMap.comp (ι Q), comp_ι_sq_scalar _⟩ = g
proof:
exact (lift Q : _ ≃ (CliffordAlgebra Q →ₐ[R] A)).apply_symm_apply g

/-- See note [partially-applied ext lemmas]. -/
@[ext high]

name: adjoin_range_ι
statement: theorem adjoin_range_ι  : Algebra.adjoin R (Set.range (ι Q)) = ⊤
proof:
refine top_unique fun x hx => ?_; clear hx
  induction x using induction with
  | algebraMap => exact algebraMap_mem _ _
  | add x y hx hy => exact add_mem hx hy
  | mul x y hx hy => exact mul_mem hx hy
  | ι x => exact Algebra.subset_adjoin (Set.mem_range_self _)

@[simp]

name: ι_mul_ι_mul_ι
statement: theorem ι_mul_ι_mul_ι (a b : M) : ι Q a * ι Q b * ι Q a = ι Q (QuadraticMap.polar Q a b • a - Q a • b)
proof:
rw [ι_mul_ι_comm, sub_mul, mul_assoc, ι_sq_scalar, ← Algebra.smul_def, ← Algebra.commutes, ←
    Algebra.smul_def, ← map_smul, ← map_smul, ← map_sub]

@[simp]

name: map_id
statement: theorem map_id  : map (QuadraticMap.Isometry.id Q₁) = AlgHom.id R (CliffordAlgebra Q₁)
proof:
ext m; exact map_apply_ι _ m

@[simp]

name: equivOfIsometry_refl
statement: theorem equivOfIsometry_refl  : (equivOfIsometry <| QuadraticMap.IsometryEquiv.refl Q₁) = AlgEquiv.refl
proof:
ext x
  exact AlgHom.congr_fun (map_id Q₁) x

end Map

end CliffordAlgebra

namespace TensorAlgebra

variable {Q}

/-- The canonical image of the `TensorAlgebra` in the `CliffordAlgebra`, which maps
`TensorAlgebra.ι R x` to `CliffordAlgebra.ι Q x`. -/

name: toClifford_ι
statement: theorem toClifford_ι (m : M) : toClifford (TensorAlgebra.ι R m) = CliffordAlgebra.ι Q m
proof:
simp [toClifford]

end TensorAlgebra

name: neg_e0_mul_v
statement: theorem neg_e0_mul_v (m : M) : -(e0 Q * v Q m) = v Q m * e0 Q
proof:
refine neg_eq_of_add_eq_zero_right ((ι_mul_ι_add_swap _ _).trans ?_)
  dsimp [QuadraticMap.polar]
  simp only [add_zero, mul_zero, mul_one, zero_add, neg_zero,
    add_sub_cancel_right, sub_self, map_zero]

name: neg_v_mul_e0
statement: theorem neg_v_mul_e0 (m : M) : -(v Q m * e0 Q) = e0 Q * v Q m
proof:
rw [neg_eq_iff_eq_neg]
  exact (neg_e0_mul_v _ m).symm

@[simp]

name: e0_mul_v_mul_e0
statement: theorem e0_mul_v_mul_e0 (m : M) : e0 Q * v Q m * e0 Q = v Q m
proof:
rw [← neg_v_mul_e0, ← neg_mul, mul_assoc, e0_mul_e0, mul_neg_one, neg_neg]

@[simp]

name: toEven_ι
statement: theorem toEven_ι (m : M) : (toEven Q (ι Q m) : CliffordAlgebra (Q' Q)) = e0 Q * v Q m
proof:
simp only [toEven, CliffordAlgebra.lift_ι_apply, ← even_toSubmodule]
  rw [LinearMap.codRestrict_apply, LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, LinearMap.mulLeft_apply]

/-- The embedding from the even subalgebra with an extra dimension into the original algebra. -/

name: card_eq_card_quotient_mul_card
statement: theorem card_eq_card_quotient_mul_card (S : Submodule R M) : Nat.card M = Nat.card S * Nat.card (M ⧸ S)
proof:
rw [mul_comm, ← Nat.card_prod]
  exact Nat.card_congr AddSubgroup.addGroupEquivQuotientProdAddSubgroup

end Submodule

name: mk_surjective
statement: theorem mk_surjective  : Function.Surjective (@mk _ _ _ _ _ p)
proof:
rintro ⟨x⟩
  exact ⟨x, rfl⟩

universe u in
instance {R M : Type*} [Ring R] [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] {N : Submodule R M} [Small.{u} M] :
    Small.{u} (M ⧸ N) :=
  small_of_surjective (Submodule.Quotient.mk_surjective _)

end Quotient

section

variable {M₂ : Type*} [AddCommGroup M₂] [Module R M₂]

name: mkQ_surjective
statement: theorem mkQ_surjective  : Function.Surjective p.mkQ
proof:
rintro ⟨x⟩; exact ⟨x, rfl⟩

end

variable {R₂ M₂ : Type*} [Ring R₂] [AddCommGroup M₂] [Module R₂ M₂] {τ₁₂ : R →+* R₂}

/-- Two `LinearMap`s from a quotient module are equal if their compositions with
`submodule.mkQ` are equal.

See note [partially-applied ext lemmas]. -/
@[ext high] -- Increase priority so this applies before `LinearMap.ext`

name: right_inv
statement: theorem right_inv  : Function.RightInverse (invFun p) (toFun p)
proof:
dsimp only [toFun, invFun]
  rw [Function.rightInverse_iff_comp, ← coe_comp, ← @id_coe R]
  congr
  refine pi_ext fun i x ↦ ?_
  induction x using Submodule.Quotient.induction_on with | _ x'
  refine funext fun j ↦ ?_
  rw [comp_apply, piQuotientLift_single, mapQ_apply,
    quotientPiLift_mk, id_apply]
  by_cases hij : i = j <;> simp only [mkQ_apply, coe_single]
  · subst hij
    rw [Pi.single_eq_same, Pi.single_eq_same]
  · rw [Pi.single_eq_of_ne (Ne.symm hij), Pi.single_eq_of_ne (Ne.symm hij), Quotient.mk_zero]

end quotientPi_aux

open quotientPi_aux in
/-- The quotient of a direct sum is the direct sum of quotients. -/
@[simps!]

name: mkQ_map_self
statement: theorem mkQ_map_self  : map p.mkQ p = ⊥
proof:
rw [eq_bot_iff, map_le_iff_le_comap, comap_bot, ker_mkQ]

@[simp]

name: map_mkQ_eq_top
statement: theorem map_mkQ_eq_top  : map p.mkQ p' = ⊤ ↔ p ⊔ p' = ⊤
proof:
simp only [LinearMap.map_eq_top_iff p.range_mkQ, sup_comm, ker_mkQ]

variable (q : Submodule R₂ M₂)

/-- The map from the quotient of `M` by submodule `p` to the quotient of `M₂` by submodule `q` along
`f : M → M₂` is linear. -/

name: factor_comp_mk
statement: theorem factor_comp_mk (H : p ≤ p') : (factor H).comp (mkQ p) = mkQ p'
proof:
ext x
  rw [LinearMap.comp_apply, factor_mk]

@[simp]

name: factor_surjective
statement: theorem factor_surjective (H : p ≤ p') : Function.Surjective (factor H)
proof:
intro x
  use Quotient.mk x.out
  exact Quotient.out_eq x

end

/-- The correspondence theorem for modules: there is an order isomorphism between submodules of the
quotient of `M` by `p`, and submodules of `M` larger than `p`. -/

name: linearIndependent_iff_injective_fintypeLinearCombination
statement: theorem linearIndependent_iff_injective_fintypeLinearCombination  : LinearIndependent R v ↔ Injective (Fintype.linearCombination R v)
proof:
simp [← Finsupp.linearCombination_eq_fintype_linearCombination, LinearIndependent]

alias ⟨LinearIndependent.fintypeLinearCombination_injective, _⟩ :=
  linearIndependent_iff_injective_fintypeLinearCombination

name: linearIndepOn_iff_linearCombinationOnₛ
statement: theorem linearIndepOn_iff_linearCombinationOnₛ  : LinearIndepOn R v s ↔ Injective (Finsupp.linearCombinationOn ι M R v s)
proof:
rw [← linearIndependent_restrict_iff]
  simp [LinearIndependent, Finsupp.linearCombination_restrict]

end Indexed

section repr

/-- Canonical isomorphism between linear combinations and the span of linearly independent vectors.
-/
@[simps (rhsMd := default) apply_coe symm_apply]

name: linearIndependent_neg_iff
statement: theorem linearIndependent_neg_iff  : LinearIndependent R (-v) ↔ LinearIndependent R v
proof:
refine ⟨fun h ↦ ?_, LinearIndependent.neg⟩
  simpa using h.neg

name: linearIndependent_iff
statement: theorem linearIndependent_iff  : LinearIndependent R v ↔ ∀ l, Finsupp.linearCombination R v l = 0 → l = 0
proof:
simp [linearIndependent_iff_ker, LinearMap.ker_eq_bot']

/-- A version of `linearIndependent_iff` where the linear combination is a `Finset` sum. -/

name: not_linearIndependent_iff_linearCombination
statement: theorem not_linearIndependent_iff_linearCombination  : ¬LinearIndependent R v ↔ ∃ l, (Finsupp.linearCombination R v) l = 0 ∧ l ≠ 0
proof: simp [linearIndependent_iff_ker, LinearMap.ker_eq_bot']

name: not_linearIndependent_iff_finsupp
statement: theorem not_linearIndependent_iff_finsupp  : ¬LinearIndependent R v ↔ ∃ (f : ι →₀ R), f.sum (fun x r ↦ r • v x) = 0 ∧ f ≠ 0
proof: simp [linearIndependent_iff_ker, LinearMap.ker_eq_bot', Finsupp.linearCombination]

name: linearIndependent_iff_notMem_span
statement: theorem linearIndependent_iff_notMem_span  : LinearIndependent K v ↔ ∀ i, v i ∉ span K (v '' (univ \ {i}))
proof:
apply linearIndependent_iff_eq_zero_of_smul_mem_span.trans
  constructor
  · intro h i h_in_span
    apply one_ne_zero (h i 1 (by simp [h_in_span]))
  · intro h i a ha
    by_contra ha'
    exact False.elim (h _ ((smul_mem_iff _ ha').1 ha))

name: linearIndepOn_iff_notMem_span
statement: theorem linearIndepOn_iff_notMem_span  : LinearIndepOn K v s ↔ ∀ i ∈ s, v i ∉ span K (v '' (s \ {i}))
proof:
rw [LinearIndepOn, linearIndependent_iff_notMem_span, ← Function.comp_def]
  simp_rw [Set.image_comp]
  simp [Set.image_diff Subtype.val_injective]

end Module

name: exists_maximal_linearIndepOn
statement: theorem exists_maximal_linearIndepOn (v : ι → M) : ∃ s : Set ι, (LinearIndepOn R v s) ∧ ∀ i ∉ s, ∃ a : R, a ≠ 0 ∧ a • v i ∈ span R (v '' s)
proof:
classical
    rcases exists_maximal_linearIndepOn' R v with ⟨I, hIlinind, hImaximal⟩
    use I, hIlinind
    intro i hi
    specialize hImaximal (I ∪ {i}) (by simp)
    set J := I ∪ {i} with hJ
    have memJ : ∀ {x}, x ∈ J ↔ x = i ∨ x ∈ I := by simp [hJ]
    have hiJ : i ∈ J := by simp [J]
    have h := by
      refine mt hImaximal ?_
      · intro h2
        rw [h2] at hi
        exact absurd hiJ hi
    obtain ⟨f, supp_f, sum_f, f_ne⟩ := linearDepOn_iff.mp h
    have hfi : f i ≠ 0 := by
      contrapose hIlinind
      refine linearDepOn_iff.mpr ⟨f, ?_, sum_f, f_ne⟩
      simp only [Finsupp.mem_supported, hJ] at supp_f ⊢
      rintro x hx
      refine (memJ.mp (supp_f hx)).resolve_left ?_
      rintro rfl
      exact (Finsupp.mem_support_iff.mp hx) hIlinind
    use f i, hfi
    have hfi' : i ∈ f.support := Finsupp.mem_support_iff.mpr hfi
    rw [← Finset.insert_erase hfi', Finset.sum_insert (Finset.notMem_erase _ _),
      add_eq_zero_iff_eq_neg] at sum_f
    rw [sum_f]
    refine neg_mem (sum_mem fun c hc => smul_mem _ _ (subset_span ⟨c, ?_, rfl⟩))
    exact (memJ.mp (supp_f (Finset.erase_subset _ _ hc))).resolve_left (Finset.ne_of_mem_erase hc)

@[stacks 0CKM]

name: mem_span_insert_exchange
statement: theorem mem_span_insert_exchange  : x ∈ span K (insert y s) → x ∉ span K s → y ∈ span K (insert x s)
proof:
simp only [mem_span_insert, forall_exists_index, and_imp]
  rintro a z hz rfl h
  refine ⟨a⁻¹, -a⁻¹ • z, smul_mem _ _ hz, ?_⟩
  have a0 : a ≠ 0 := by
    rintro rfl
    simp_all
  match_scalars <;> simp [a0]

protected theorem LinearIndepOn.insert {s : Set ι} {x : ι} (hs : LinearIndepOn K v s)
    (hx : v x ∉ span K (v '' s)) : LinearIndepOn K v (insert x s) := by
  rw [← union_singleton]
  have x0 : v x ≠ 0 := fun h => hx (h ▸ zero_mem _)
  apply hs.union (LinearIndepOn.singleton x0)
  rwa [image_singleton, disjoint_span_singleton' x0]

protected theorem LinearIndepOn.id_insert (hs : LinearIndepOn K id s) (hx : x ∉ span K s) :
    LinearIndepOn K id (insert x s) :=
  hs.insert ((image_id s).symm ▸ hx)

name: exists_linearIndependent
statement: theorem exists_linearIndependent  : ∃ b ⊆ t, span K b = span K t ∧ LinearIndependent K ((↑) : b → V)
proof:
obtain ⟨b, hb₁, -, hb₂, hb₃⟩ :=
    exists_linearIndepOn_id_extension (linearIndependent_empty K V) (Set.empty_subset t)
  exact ⟨b, hb₁, (span_eq_of_le _ hb₂ (Submodule.span_mono hb₁)).symm, hb₃⟩

/-- Indexed version of `exists_linearIndependent`. -/

name: linearIndependent_unique_iff
statement: theorem linearIndependent_unique_iff  : LinearIndependent R v ↔ v default ≠ 0
proof:
refine ⟨?_, .of_subsingleton _⟩
  simpa [linearIndependent_iff, Finsupp.linearCombination_unique, Finsupp.ext_iff,
    Unique.forall_iff, or_imp] using fun h hv ↦ by simpa using h (.single default 1) hv

variable (R) in
@[simp]

name: free_and_finite
statement: theorem free_and_finite  : Module.Free R (MultilinearMap R M₁ M₂) ∧ Module.Finite R (MultilinearMap R M₁ M₂)
proof:
cases nonempty_fintype ι
  have := @free_and_finite_fin R M₂ _ _ _ _ _ (Fintype.card ι)
    (fun x => M₁ ((Fintype.equivFin ι).symm x))
  obtain ⟨l, r⟩ := this
  have e := domDomCongrLinearEquiv' R R M₁ M₂ (Fintype.equivFin ι)
  exact ⟨Module.Free.of_equiv e.symm, Module.Finite.equiv e.symm⟩

instance _root_.Module.Finite.multilinearMap : Module.Finite R (MultilinearMap R M₁ M₂) :=
  free_and_finite.2

instance _root_.Module.Free.multilinearMap : Module.Free R (MultilinearMap R M₁ M₂) :=
  free_and_finite.1

end MultilinearMap

name: uncurrySum_currySum
statement: theorem uncurrySum_currySum (f : MultilinearMap R N M₂) : uncurrySum (currySum f) = f
proof:
ext
  simp only [uncurrySum_apply, currySum_apply]
  congr
  ext (_ | _) <;> simp

@[simp]

name: piFamily_zero
statement: theorem piFamily_zero  : piFamily (0 : Π (p : Π i, κ i), MultilinearMap R (fun i ↦ M i (p i)) (N p)) = 0
proof:
ext; simp

@[simp]

name: dfinsuppFamily_zero
statement: theorem dfinsuppFamily_zero  : dfinsuppFamily (0 : Π (p : Π i, κ i), MultilinearMap R (fun i ↦ M i (p i)) (N p)) = 0
proof:
ext; simp

@[simp]

name: map_zero
statement: theorem map_zero  : f 0 = 0
proof:
obtain ⟨i, _⟩ : ∃ i : ι, i ∈ Set.univ := Set.exists_mem_of_nonempty ι
  exact map_coord_zero f i rfl

instance : Add (MultilinearMap R M₁ M₂) :=
  ⟨fun f f' =>
    ⟨fun x => f x + f' x, fun m i x y => by simp [add_left_comm, add_assoc], fun m i c x => by
      simp [smul_add]⟩⟩

@[simp]

name: mkPiRing_zero
statement: theorem mkPiRing_zero  : MultilinearMap.mkPiRing R ι (0 : M₂) = 0
proof: ext; rw [mkPiRing_apply, smul_zero, MultilinearMap.zero_apply]

name: finTwoProd_zero
statement: theorem finTwoProd_zero (R : Type*) : Basis.finTwoProd R 0 = (1, 0)
proof:
simp [Basis.finTwoProd, LinearEquiv.finTwoArrow]

@[simp]

name: finTwoProd_one
statement: theorem finTwoProd_one (R : Type*) : Basis.finTwoProd R 1 = (0, 1)
proof:
simp [Basis.finTwoProd, LinearEquiv.finTwoArrow]

@[simp]

name: repr_self_apply
statement: theorem repr_self_apply (j) : b.repr (b i) j = if i = j then 1 else 0
proof:
rw [repr_self, Finsupp.single_apply]

@[simp]

name: repr_linearCombination
statement: theorem repr_linearCombination (v) : b.repr (Finsupp.linearCombination _ b v) = v
proof:
rw [← b.coe_repr_symm]
  exact b.repr.apply_symm_apply v

@[simp]

name: linearCombination_repr
statement: theorem linearCombination_repr  : Finsupp.linearCombination _ b (b.repr x) = x
proof:
rw [← b.coe_repr_symm]
  exact b.repr.symm_apply_apply x

end repr

section Map

variable (f : M ≃ₗ[R] M')

/-- Apply the linear equivalence `f` to the basis vectors. -/
@[simps]
protected def map : Basis ι R M' :=
  ofRepr (f.symm.trans b.repr)

@[simp]

name: reindex_refl
statement: theorem reindex_refl  : b.reindex (Equiv.refl ι) = b
proof:
simp [reindex]

/-- `simp` can prove this as `Basis.coe_reindex` + `EquivLike.range_comp` -/

name: range_reindex
statement: theorem range_reindex  : Set.range (b.reindex e) = Set.range b
proof:
simp [coe_reindex, range_comp]

end Reindex

end Basis

section Fintype

open Basis

open Fintype

/-- A module over `R` with a finite basis is linearly equivalent to functions from its basis to `R`.
-/

name: reindexRange_apply
statement: theorem reindexRange_apply (x : range b) : b.reindexRange x = x
proof:
rcases x with ⟨bi, ⟨i, rfl⟩⟩
  exact b.reindexRange_self i

name: reindexFinsetRange_apply
statement: theorem reindexFinsetRange_apply (x : Finset.univ.image b) : b.reindexFinsetRange x = x
proof:
rcases x with ⟨bi, hbi⟩
  rcases Finset.mem_image.mp hbi with ⟨i, -, rfl⟩
  exact b.reindexFinsetRange_self i

name: coe_sumCoords_of_fintype
statement: theorem coe_sumCoords_of_fintype  : (b.sumCoords : M → R) = ∑ i, b.coord i
proof:
ext m
  simp only [sumCoords, Finsupp.sum_fintype, LinearMap.id_coe, LinearEquiv.coe_coe, coord_apply,
    id, Fintype.sum_apply, imp_true_iff, Finsupp.coe_lsum, LinearMap.coe_comp, comp_apply,
    LinearMap.coe_sum]

@[simp]

name: sumCoords_self_apply
statement: theorem sumCoords_self_apply  : b.sumCoords (b i) = 1
proof:
simp only [Basis.sumCoords, LinearMap.id_coe, LinearEquiv.coe_coe, id, Basis.repr_self,
    Function.comp_apply, Finsupp.coe_lsum, LinearMap.coe_comp, Finsupp.sum_single_index]

name: coe_sumCoords_eq_finsum
statement: theorem coe_sumCoords_eq_finsum  : (b.sumCoords : M → R) = fun m => ∑ᶠ i, b.coord i m
proof:
ext m
  simp only [Basis.sumCoords, Basis.coord, Finsupp.lapply_apply, LinearMap.id_coe,
    LinearEquiv.coe_coe, Function.comp_apply, Finsupp.coe_lsum, LinearMap.coe_comp,
    finsum_eq_sum _ (b.repr m).hasFiniteSupport, Finsupp.sum, Finset.finite_toSet_toFinset, id,
    Finsupp.fun_support_eq]

variable (e : ι ≃ ι')

@[simp]

name: sumCoords_reindex
statement: theorem sumCoords_reindex  : (b.reindex e).sumCoords = b.sumCoords
proof:
ext x
  simp only [coe_sumCoords, repr_reindex]
  exact Finsupp.sum_mapDomain_index (fun _ => rfl) fun _ _ _ => rfl

variable (S : Type*) [Semiring S] [Module S M']
variable [SMulCommClass R S M']

name: prod_apply
statement: theorem prod_apply (i) : b.prod b' i = Sum.elim (LinearMap.inl R M M' ∘ b) (LinearMap.inr R M M' ∘ b') i
proof:
ext <;> cases i <;>
    simp only [prod_apply_inl_fst, Sum.elim_inl, LinearMap.inl_apply, prod_apply_inr_fst,
      Sum.elim_inr, LinearMap.inr_apply, prod_apply_inl_snd, prod_apply_inr_snd, Function.comp]

end Prod

end Basis

namespace Free

variable (R M N : Type*) [Semiring R] [AddCommMonoid M] [Module R M] [AddCommMonoid N] [Module R N]
instance prod [Module.Free R M] [Module.Free R N] : Module.Free R (M × N) :=
  .of_basis <| (Module.Free.chooseBasis R M).prod (Module.Free.chooseBasis R N)

end Free

end Module

name: restrictScalars_apply
statement: theorem restrictScalars_apply (i : ι) : (b.restrictScalars R i : M) = b i
proof:
simp only [Basis.restrictScalars, Basis.span_apply]

@[simp]

name: mem_span_iff_repr_mem
statement: theorem mem_span_iff_repr_mem (m : M) : m ∈ span R (Set.range b) ↔ ∀ i, b.repr m i ∈ Set.range (algebraMap R S)
proof:
refine
    ⟨fun hm i => ⟨(b.restrictScalars R).repr ⟨m, hm⟩ i, b.restrictScalars_repr_apply R ⟨m, hm⟩ i⟩,
      fun h => ?_⟩
  rw [← b.linearCombination_repr m, Finsupp.linearCombination_apply S _]
  refine sum_mem fun i _ => ?_
  obtain ⟨_, h⟩ := h i
  simp_rw [← h, algebraMap_smul]
  exact smul_mem _ _ (subset_span (Set.mem_range_self i))

end RestrictScalars

section AddSubgroup

variable {M R : Type*} [Ring R] [Nontrivial R] [IsAddTorsionFree R]
  [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] (A : AddSubgroup M) {ι : Type*} (b : Basis ι R M)

/--
Let `A` be a subgroup of an additive commutative group `M` that is also an `R`-module.
Construct a basis of `A` as a `ℤ`-basis from an `R`-basis of `E` that generates `A`.
-/
noncomputable def addSubgroupOfClosure (h : A = .closure (Set.range b)) :
    Basis ι ℤ A.toIntSubmodule :=
  (b.restrictScalars ℤ).map <|
    LinearEquiv.ofEq _ _
      (by rw [h, ← Submodule.span_int_eq_addSubgroupClosure, toAddSubgroup_toIntSubmodule])

@[simp]

name: repr_range
statement: theorem repr_range  : LinearMap.range (b.repr : M →ₗ[R] ι →₀ R) = Finsupp.supported R R univ
proof: rw [LinearEquiv.range, Finsupp.supported_univ]

name: index_nonempty
statement: theorem index_nonempty (b : Basis ι R M) : Nonempty ι
proof:
obtain ⟨x, y, ne⟩ : ∃ x y : M, x ≠ y := Nontrivial.exists_pair_ne
  obtain ⟨i, _⟩ := not_forall.mp (mt b.ext_elem_iff.2 ne)
  exact ⟨i⟩

protected theorem linearIndependent : LinearIndependent R b :=
  fun x y hxy => by
    rw [← b.repr_linearCombination x, hxy, b.repr_linearCombination y]

protected lemma linearIndepOn (s : Set ι) : LinearIndepOn R b s :=
  b.linearIndependent.linearIndepOn s

protected theorem ne_zero [Nontrivial R] (i) : b i ≠ 0 :=
  b.linearIndependent.ne_zero i

name: span_apply
statement: theorem span_apply (i : ι) : Basis.span hli i = ⟨v i, Submodule.subset_span <| mem_range_self _⟩
proof:
ext
  exact congr_arg ((↑) : span R (range v) → M) <| Basis.mk_apply _ _ _

protected theorem coe_span_apply (i : ι) : (Basis.span hli i : M) = v i := by simp

@[simp]
protected theorem span_repr_eq_single (i : ι)
    (hi : v i ∈ span R (range v) := subset_span <| mem_range_self i) :
    (Basis.span hli).repr ⟨v i, hi⟩ = single i 1 := by
  rw [← LinearEquiv.eq_symm_apply]
  simp [Basis.span]

name: range_extend
statement: theorem range_extend (hs : LinearIndepOn K id s) : range (Basis.extend hs) = hs.extend (subset_univ _)
proof:
rw [coe_extend, Subtype.range_coe_subtype, setOf_mem_eq]

/-- Auxiliary definition: the index for the new basis vectors in `Basis.sumExtend`.

The specific value of this definition should be considered an implementation detail. -/

name: range_ofSpan
statement: theorem range_ofSpan (hs : ⊤ ≤ span K s) : range (ofSpan hs) = (linearIndepOn_empty K id).extend (empty_subset s)
proof: rw [coe_ofSpan, Subtype.range_coe_subtype, setOf_mem_eq]

name: ofVectorSpace_apply_self
statement: theorem ofVectorSpace_apply_self (x : ofVectorSpaceIndex K V) : ofVectorSpace K V x = x
proof:
unfold ofVectorSpace
  exact Basis.mk_apply _ _ _

@[simp]

name: atom_iff_nonzero_span
statement: theorem atom_iff_nonzero_span (W : Submodule K V) : IsAtom W ↔ ∃ v ≠ 0, W = span K {v}
proof:
refine ⟨fun h => ?_, fun h => ?_⟩
  · obtain ⟨hbot, h⟩ := h
    rcases (Submodule.ne_bot_iff W).1 hbot with ⟨v, ⟨hW, hv⟩⟩
    refine ⟨v, ⟨hv, ?_⟩⟩
    by_contra heq
    specialize h (span K {v})
    rw [span_singleton_eq_bot, lt_iff_le_and_ne] at h
    exact hv (h ⟨(span_singleton_le_iff_mem v W).2 hW, Ne.symm heq⟩)
  · rcases h with ⟨v, ⟨hv, rfl⟩⟩
    exact nonzero_span_atom v hv

/-- The lattice of submodules of a module over a division ring is atomistic. -/
instance : IsAtomistic (Submodule K V) :=
  CompleteLattice.isAtomistic_iff.2 fun W => by
    refine ⟨_, submodule_eq_sSup_le_nonzero_spans W, ?_⟩
    rintro _ ⟨w, ⟨_, ⟨hw, rfl⟩⟩⟩
    exact nonzero_span_atom w hw

end AtomsOfSubmoduleLattice

variable {K V}

name: ker_flip
statement: theorem ker_flip (H : B.IsRefl) : B.flip.ker = B.ker
proof:
ext x
  simp [LinearMap.ext_iff, H.eq_iff]

name: ker_eq_bot_iff_ker_flip_eq_bot
statement: theorem ker_eq_bot_iff_ker_flip_eq_bot (H : B.IsRefl) : LinearMap.ker B = ⊥ ↔ LinearMap.ker B.flip = ⊥
proof:
rwa [ker_flip]

end IsRefl

end Reflexive

/-! ### Symmetric bilinear forms -/


section Symmetric

variable [CommSemiring R] [AddCommMonoid M] [Module R M] {I : R →+* R} {B : M →ₛₗ[I] M →ₗ[R] R}

/-- The proposition that a sesquilinear form is symmetric -/

name: isRefl
statement: theorem isRefl (H : B.IsAlt) : B.IsRefl
proof:
intro x y h
  rw [← neg H, h, neg_zero]

name: isAdjointPair_iff_comp_eq_compl₂
statement: theorem isAdjointPair_iff_comp_eq_compl₂  : IsAdjointPair B B' f g ↔ B'.comp f = B.compl₂ g
proof:
constructor <;> intro h
  · ext x y
    rw [comp_apply, compl₂_apply]
    exact h x y
  · intro _ _
    rw [← compl₂_apply, ← comp_apply, h]

name: mem_skewAdjointSubmodule
statement: theorem mem_skewAdjointSubmodule (f : Module.End R M) : f ∈ B.skewAdjointSubmodule ↔ B.IsSkewAdjoint f
proof:
rw [isSkewAdjoint_iff_neg_self_adjoint]
  exact Iff.rfl

end AddCommGroup

end SelfadjointPair

/-! ### Nondegenerate bilinear maps -/


section Nondegenerate

section CommSemiring

variable [CommSemiring R] [AddCommMonoid M] [Module R M] [CommSemiring R₁] [AddCommMonoid M₁]
  [Module R₁ M₁] [CommSemiring R₂] [AddCommMonoid M₂] [Module R₂ M₂]
  {I₁ : R₁ →+* R} {I₂ : R₂ →+* R} {I₁' : R₁ →+* R}

/-- A bilinear map is called left-separating if
the only element that is left-orthogonal to every other element is `0`; i.e.,
for every nonzero `x` in `M₁`, there exists `y` in `M₂` with `B x y ≠ 0`. -/

name: toTensorAlgebra_gOne
statement: theorem toTensorAlgebra_gOne  : TensorPower.toTensorAlgebra (@GradedMonoid.GOne.one _ (fun n => ⨂[R]^n M) _ _) = 1
proof:
simp [GradedMonoid.GOne.one, TensorPower.toTensorAlgebra_tprod]

@[simp]

name: ofDirectSum_comp_toDirectSum
statement: theorem ofDirectSum_comp_toDirectSum  : ofDirectSum.comp toDirectSum = AlgHom.id R (TensorAlgebra R M)
proof:
ext
  simp [tprod_apply]

@[simp]

name: toDirectSum_comp_ofDirectSum
statement: theorem toDirectSum_comp_ofDirectSum  : toDirectSum.comp ofDirectSum = AlgHom.id R (⨁ n, ⨂[R]^n M)
proof:
ext
  simp [DirectSum.lof_eq_of, -tprod_apply, toDirectSum_tensorPower_tprod]

@[simp]

name: ringQuot_mkAlgHom_freeAlgebra_ι_eq_ι
statement: theorem ringQuot_mkAlgHom_freeAlgebra_ι_eq_ι (m : M) : RingQuot.mkAlgHom R (Rel R M) (FreeAlgebra.ι R m) = ι R m
proof:
rw [ι]
  rfl

/-- Given a linear map `f : M → A` where `A` is an `R`-algebra, `lift R f` is the unique lift
of `f` to a morphism of `R`-algebras `TensorAlgebra R M → A`.
-/
@[simps symm_apply]

name: toTensor_ι
statement: theorem toTensor_ι (m : M) : FreeAlgebra.toTensor (FreeAlgebra.ι R m) = TensorAlgebra.ι R m
proof:
simp [toTensor]

end FreeAlgebra

name: ι_apply
statement: theorem ι_apply (x : ⨁ i, A i) : ⟨Quot.mk (Rel <| rel R A) (TensorAlgebra.ι R x)⟩ = ι' R A x
proof:
aesop (add simp [ι', mkAlgHom, RingQuot.mkAlgHom, mkRingHom])

/-- The injection into the free product of any `1 : A i` is the 1 of the free product. -/

name: identify_one
statement: theorem identify_one (i : I) : ι' R A (DirectSum.lof R I A i 1) = 1
proof:
suffices ι' R A (DirectSum.lof R I A i 1) = mkAlgHom R A 1 by simpa
  exact RingQuot.mkAlgHom_rel R <| rel_id R A (i := i)

/-- Multiplication in the free product of the injections of any two `aᵢ aᵢ': A i` for
the same `i` is just the injection of multiplication `aᵢ * aᵢ'` in `A i`. -/

name: lof_map_one
statement: theorem lof_map_one (i : I) : lof R A i 1 = 1
proof:
rw [lof]; dsimp [mkAlgHom]; exact identify_one R A i

/-- The `i`th canonical injection, from `A i` to the free product -/
irreducible_def ι (i : I) : A i →ₐ[R] FreeProduct R A :=
  AlgHom.ofLinearMap (ι' R A ∘ₗ DirectSum.lof R I A i)
    (lof_map_one R A i) (mul_injections R A · · |>.symm)

/-- The family of canonical injection maps, with `i` left implicit -/
irreducible_def of {i : I} : A i →ₐ[R] FreeProduct R A := ι R A i


/-- Universal property of the free product of algebras:
for every `R`-algebra `B`, every family of maps `maps : (i : I) → (A i →ₐ[R] B)` lifts
to a unique arrow `π` from `FreeProduct R A` such that  `π ∘ ι i = maps i`. -/
@[simps] def lift : ({i : I} → A i →ₐ[R] B) ≃ (FreeProduct R A →ₐ[R] B) where
  toFun maps :=
    RingQuot.liftAlgHom R ⟨
        TensorAlgebra.lift R <|
          DirectSum.toModule R I B <|
            (@maps · |>.toLinearMap),
        fun x y r ↦ by
          cases r with
          | id => simp
          | prod => simp⟩
  invFun π i := π ∘ₐ ι R A i
  left_inv π := by
    ext i aᵢ
    aesop (add simp [ι, ι'])
  right_inv maps := by
    ext i a
    aesop (add simp [ι, ι'])

/-- Universal property of the free product of algebras, property:
for every `R`-algebra `B`, every family of maps `maps : (i : I) → (A i →ₐ[R] B)` lifts
to a unique arrow `π` from `FreeProduct R A` such that  `π ∘ ι i = maps i`. -/
@[simp↓] theorem lift_comp_ι : lift R A maps ∘ₐ ι R A i = maps := by
  ext a
  simp [lift_apply, ι]

@[simp↓] theorem lift_algebraMap (r : R) : lift R A maps (algebraMap R _ r) = algebraMap R _ r := by
  rw [lift_apply, AlgHom.commutes]

@[aesop safe destruct] theorem lift_unique
    (f : FreeProduct R A →ₐ[R] B) (h : ∀ i, f ∘ₐ ι R A i = maps) :
    f = lift R A maps := by
  ext i a; simp_rw [AlgHom.ext_iff] at h; specialize h i a
  simp [h.symm, ι]

end LinearAlgebra.FreeProduct

name: tensorTensorTensorComm_trans_tensorTensorTensorComm
statement: theorem tensorTensorTensorComm_trans_tensorTensorTensorComm  : tensorTensorTensorComm R M N P Q ≪≫ₗ tensorTensorTensorComm R M P N Q = .refl R _
proof:
rw [← tensorTensorTensorComm_symm]
  exact LinearEquiv.symm_trans_self _

name: span_tmul_eq_top
statement: theorem span_tmul_eq_top  : Submodule.span R { t : M ⊗[R] N | ∃ m n, m ⊗ₜ n = t } = ⊤
proof:
ext t; simp only [Submodule.mem_top, iff_true]
  refine t.induction_on ?_ ?_ ?_
  · exact Submodule.zero_mem _
  · intro m n
    apply Submodule.subset_span
    use m, n
  · intro t₁ t₂ ht₁ ht₂
    exact Submodule.add_mem _ ht₁ ht₂

@[simp]

name: map₂_mk_top_top_eq_top
statement: theorem map₂_mk_top_top_eq_top  : Submodule.map₂ (mk R M N) ⊤ ⊤ = ⊤
proof:
rw [← top_le_iff, ← span_tmul_eq_top, Submodule.map₂_eq_span_image2]
  exact Submodule.span_mono fun _ ⟨m, n, h⟩ => ⟨m, trivial, n, trivial, h⟩

name: exists_multiset
statement: theorem exists_multiset (x : M ⊗[R] N) : ∃ S : Multiset (M × N), x = (S.map fun i ↦ i.1 ⊗ₜ[R] i.2).sum
proof:
induction x with
  | zero => exact ⟨0, by simp⟩
  | tmul x y => exact ⟨{(x, y)}, by simp⟩
  | add x y hx hy =>
    obtain ⟨Sx, hx⟩ := hx
    obtain ⟨Sy, hy⟩ := hy
    exact ⟨Sx + Sy, by rw [Multiset.map_add, Multiset.sum_add, hx, hy]⟩

/-- For any element `x` of `M ⊗[R] N`, there exists a finite subset `{ (m_i, n_i) }`
of `M × N` such that each `m_i` is distinct (we represent it as an element of `M →₀ N`),
such that `x` is equal to the sum of `m_i ⊗ₜ[R] n_i`. -/

name: exists_finsupp_left
statement: theorem exists_finsupp_left (x : M ⊗[R] N) : ∃ S : M →₀ N, x = S.sum fun m n ↦ m ⊗ₜ[R] n
proof:
induction x with
  | zero => exact ⟨0, by simp⟩
  | tmul x y => exact ⟨Finsupp.single x y, by simp⟩
  | add x y hx hy =>
    obtain ⟨Sx, hx⟩ := hx
    obtain ⟨Sy, hy⟩ := hy
    use Sx + Sy
    rw [hx, hy]
    exact (Finsupp.sum_add_index' (by simp) TensorProduct.tmul_add).symm

/-- For any element `x` of `M ⊗[R] N`, there exists a finite subset `{ (m_i, n_i) }`
of `M × N` such that each `n_i` is distinct (we represent it as an element of `N →₀ M`),
such that `x` is equal to the sum of `m_i ⊗ₜ[R] n_i`. -/

name: exists_finsupp_right
statement: theorem exists_finsupp_right (x : M ⊗[R] N) : ∃ S : N →₀ M, x = S.sum fun n m ↦ m ⊗ₜ[R] n
proof:
obtain ⟨S, h⟩ := exists_finsupp_left (TensorProduct.comm R M N x)
  refine ⟨S, (TensorProduct.comm R M N).injective ?_⟩
  simp_rw [h, Finsupp.sum, map_sum, comm_tmul]

/-- For any element `x` of `M ⊗[R] N`, there exists a finite subset `{ (m_i, n_i) }`
of `M × N`, such that `x` is equal to the sum of `m_i ⊗ₜ[R] n_i`. -/

name: exists_finset
statement: theorem exists_finset (x : M ⊗[R] N) : ∃ S : Finset (M × N), x = S.sum fun i ↦ i.1 ⊗ₜ[R] i.2
proof:
obtain ⟨S, h⟩ := exists_finsupp_left x
  use S.graph
  rw [h, Finsupp.sum]
  apply Finset.sum_nbij' (fun m ↦ ⟨m, S m⟩) Prod.fst <;> simp

/-- For a finite subset `s` of `M ⊗[R] N`, there are finitely generated
submodules `M'` and `N'` of `M` and `N`, respectively, such that `s` is contained in the image
of `M' ⊗[R] N'` in `M ⊗[R] N`.

In particular, every element of a tensor product lies in the tensor product of some finite
submodules. -/

name: exists_sum_tmul_eq
statement: theorem exists_sum_tmul_eq (x : M ⊗[R] N) : ∃ (k : ℕ) (m : Fin k → M) (n : Fin k → N), x = ∑ j, m j ⊗ₜ n j
proof:
induction x with
  | zero => exact ⟨0, IsEmpty.elim inferInstance, IsEmpty.elim inferInstance, by simp⟩
  | tmul x y => exact ⟨1, fun _ ↦ x, fun _ ↦ y, by simp⟩
  | add x y hx hy =>
    obtain ⟨kx, mx, nx, rfl⟩ := hx
    obtain ⟨ky, my, ny, rfl⟩ := hy
    use kx + ky, Fin.addCases mx my, Fin.addCases nx ny
    simp [Fin.sum_univ_add]

end TensorProduct

name: sum_tmul_eq_zero_of_vanishesTrivially
statement: theorem sum_tmul_eq_zero_of_vanishesTrivially (hmn : VanishesTrivially R m n) : ∑ i, m i ⊗ₜ n i = (0 : M ⊗[R] N)
proof:
obtain ⟨k, a, y, h₁, h₂⟩ := hmn
  simp_rw [h₁, tmul_sum, tmul_smul]
  rw [Finset.sum_comm]
  simp_rw [← tmul_smul, ← smul_tmul, ← sum_tmul, h₂, zero_tmul, Finset.sum_const_zero]

/-- **Equational criterion for vanishing**
[A. Altman and S. Kleiman, *A term of commutative algebra* (Lemma 8.16)][altman2021term],
forward direction.

Assume that the $m_i$ generate $M$. If the expression $\sum_i m_i \otimes n_i$
vanishes, then it vanishes trivially. -/

name: mulMap_range
statement: theorem mulMap_range  : LinearMap.range (mulMap M N) = M * N
proof:
refine le_antisymm ?_ (mul_le.2 fun m hm n hn ↦ ⟨⟨m, hm⟩ ⊗ₜ[R] ⟨n, hn⟩, rfl⟩)
  rintro _ ⟨x, rfl⟩
  induction x with
  | zero => rw [map_zero]; exact zero_mem _
  | tmul a b => exact mul_mem_mul a.2 b.2
  | add a b ha hb => rw [map_add]; exact add_mem ha hb

/-- If `M` and `N` are submodules in an algebra `S` over `R`, there is the natural `R`-linear map
`M ⊗[R] N →ₗ[R] M * N` induced by multiplication in `S`,
which is surjective (`Submodule.mulMap'_surjective`). -/

name: comm_trans_lTensorOne
statement: theorem comm_trans_lTensorOne  : (TensorProduct.comm R _ _).trans M.lTensorOne = M.rTensorOne
proof:
refine LinearEquiv.toLinearMap_injective <| TensorProduct.ext' fun m r ↦ ?_
  obtain ⟨x, h⟩ := Algebra.mem_bot.1 r.2
  replace h : algebraMap R _ x = r := Subtype.val_injective h
  rw [← h]; simp

@[simp]

name: comm_trans_rTensorOne
statement: theorem comm_trans_rTensorOne  : (TensorProduct.comm R _ _).trans M.rTensorOne = M.lTensorOne
proof:
refine LinearEquiv.toLinearMap_injective <| TensorProduct.ext' fun r m ↦ ?_
  obtain ⟨x, h⟩ := Algebra.mem_bot.1 r.2
  replace h : algebraMap R _ x = r := Subtype.val_injective h
  rw [← h]; simp

variable {M} in

name: map_id
statement: theorem map_id  : map (id : M →ₗ[R] M) (id : N →ₗ[R] N) = .id
proof:
ext
  simp only [mk_apply, id_coe, compr₂ₛₗ_apply, _root_.id, map_tmul]

@[simp]
protected theorem map_one : map (1 : M →ₗ[R] M) (1 : N →ₗ[R] N) = 1 :=
  map_id

protected theorem map_mul (f₁ f₂ : M →ₗ[R] M) (g₁ g₂ : N →ₗ[R] N) :
    map (f₁ * f₂) (g₁ * g₂) = map f₁ g₁ * map f₂ g₂ :=
  map_comp ..

@[simp]
protected theorem map_pow (f : M →ₗ[R] M) (g : N →ₗ[R] N) (n : ℕ) :
    map f g ^ n = map (f ^ n) (g ^ n) := by
  induction n with
  | zero => simp only [pow_zero, TensorProduct.map_one]
  | succ n ih => simp only [pow_succ', ih, TensorProduct.map_mul]

name: lTensor_inj_iff_rTensor_inj
statement: theorem lTensor_inj_iff_rTensor_inj  : Function.Injective (lTensor M f) ↔ Function.Injective (rTensor M f)
proof:
simp [← comm_comp_rTensor_comp_comm_eq]

/-- Given a linear map `f : N → P`, `f ⊗ M` is surjective if and only if `M ⊗ f` is surjective. -/

name: lTensor_surj_iff_rTensor_surj
statement: theorem lTensor_surj_iff_rTensor_surj  : Function.Surjective (lTensor M f) ↔ Function.Surjective (rTensor M f)
proof:
simp [← comm_comp_rTensor_comp_comm_eq]

/-- Given a linear map `f : N → P`, `f ⊗ M` is bijective if and only if `M ⊗ f` is bijective. -/

name: lTensor_bij_iff_rTensor_bij
statement: theorem lTensor_bij_iff_rTensor_bij  : Function.Bijective (lTensor M f) ↔ Function.Bijective (rTensor M f)
proof:
simp [← comm_comp_rTensor_comp_comm_eq]

variable {M} in

name: lTensor_comp
statement: theorem lTensor_comp  : (g.comp f).lTensor M = (g.lTensor M).comp (f.lTensor M)
proof:
ext m n
  simp only [compr₂ₛₗ_apply, mk_apply, comp_apply, lTensor_tmul]

name: rTensor_comp
statement: theorem rTensor_comp  : (g.comp f).rTensor M = (g.rTensor M).comp (f.rTensor M)
proof:
ext m n
  simp only [compr₂ₛₗ_apply, mk_apply, comp_apply, rTensor_tmul]

name: lTensor_id_apply
statement: theorem lTensor_id_apply (x : M ⊗[R] N) : (LinearMap.id : N →ₗ[R] N).lTensor M x = x
proof:
rw [lTensor_id, id_coe, _root_.id]

@[simp]

name: rTensor_id_apply
statement: theorem rTensor_id_apply (x : N ⊗[R] M) : (LinearMap.id : N →ₗ[R] N).rTensor M x = x
proof:
rw [rTensor_id, id_coe, _root_.id]

@[simp]

name: lTensor_sub
statement: theorem lTensor_sub (f g : N →ₗ[R] P) : (f - g).lTensor M = f.lTensor M - g.lTensor M
proof:
simp_rw [← coe_lTensorHom]
  exact (lTensorHom (R := R) (N := N) (P := P) M).map_sub f g

@[simp]

name: rTensor_sub
statement: theorem rTensor_sub (f g : N →ₗ[R] P) : (f - g).rTensor Q = f.rTensor Q - g.rTensor Q
proof:
simp only [← coe_rTensorHom]
  exact (rTensorHom (R := R) (N := N) (P := P) Q).map_sub f g

@[simp]

name: lTensor_neg
statement: theorem lTensor_neg (f : N →ₗ[R] P) : (-f).lTensor M = -f.lTensor M
proof:
simp only [← coe_lTensorHom]
  exact (lTensorHom (R := R) (N := N) (P := P) M).map_neg f

@[simp]

name: rTensor_neg
statement: theorem rTensor_neg (f : N →ₗ[R] P) : (-f).rTensor Q = -f.rTensor Q
proof:
simp only [← coe_rTensorHom]
  exact (rTensorHom (R := R) (N := N) (P := P) Q).map_neg f

end LinearMap

end Ring

namespace Equiv
variable {R A A' B B' : Type*} [CommSemiring R]
  [AddCommMonoid A'] [AddCommMonoid B'] [Module R A'] [Module R B']

variable (R) in
open TensorProduct in

name: mulMap_comm
statement: theorem mulMap_comm  : mulMap B A = (mulMap A B).comp (Algebra.TensorProduct.comm R B A)
proof: ext <;> simp

name: mulMap_range
statement: theorem mulMap_range  : (A.mulMap B).range = A ⊔ B
proof: simp_rw [mulMap, Algebra.TensorProduct.productMap_range, Subalgebra.range_val]

name: cancelBaseChange_self_eq_lid
statement: theorem cancelBaseChange_self_eq_lid  : cancelBaseChange R A A A N = TensorProduct.lid A (A ⊗[R] N)
proof:
ext x
  induction x using TensorProduct.induction_on with
  | zero => simp only [map_zero]
  | tmul b y =>
    induction y using TensorProduct.induction_on with
    | zero => simp
    | tmul a m =>
      simp only [cancelBaseChange_tmul, lid_tmul, smul_tmul', smul_eq_mul, mul_comm]
    | add x y hx hy =>
      simp only [tmul_add, map_add, lid_tmul, hx, hy]
  | add x y hx hy => simp [hx, hy]

end cancelBaseChange

section leftComm
variable [Module R P] [IsScalarTower R A P]

/-- Heterobasic version of `TensorProduct.leftComm` -/

name: baseChange_add
statement: theorem baseChange_add  : (f + g).baseChange A = f.baseChange A + g.baseChange A
proof:
ext
  simp [baseChange_eq_ltensor, -baseChange_tmul]

@[simp]

name: baseChange_zero
statement: theorem baseChange_zero  : baseChange A (0 : M →ₗ[R] N) = 0
proof:
ext
  simp

@[simp]

name: baseChange_smul
statement: theorem baseChange_smul  : (r • f).baseChange A = r • f.baseChange A
proof:
ext
  simp

@[simp]

name: baseChange_id
statement: theorem baseChange_id  : (.id : M →ₗ[R] M).baseChange A = .id
proof: ext; simp

name: baseChange_comp
statement: theorem baseChange_comp (g : N →ₗ[R] P) : (g ∘ₗ f).baseChange A = g.baseChange A ∘ₗ f.baseChange A
proof:
ext; simp

open AlgebraTensorModule in

name: baseChange_mul
statement: theorem baseChange_mul (f g : Module.End R M) : (f * g).baseChange A = f.baseChange A * g.baseChange A
proof:
ext; simp

variable (R A M N)

/-- `baseChange` as a linear map.

When `M = N`, this is true more strongly as `Module.End.baseChangeHom`. -/
@[simps]

name: baseChange_sub
statement: theorem baseChange_sub  : (f - g).baseChange A = f.baseChange A - g.baseChange A
proof:
ext
  simp [tmul_sub]

@[simp]

name: baseChange_neg
statement: theorem baseChange_neg  : (-f).baseChange A = -f.baseChange A
proof:
ext
  simp [tmul_neg]

end Ring

end LinearMap

namespace Submodule

open TensorProduct

variable {R M : Type*} (A : Type*) [CommSemiring R] [Semiring A] [Algebra R A]
  [AddCommMonoid M] [Module R M] (p q : Submodule R M)

/-- If `A` is an `R`-algebra, any `R`-submodule `p` of an `R`-module `M` may be pushed forward to
an `A`-submodule of `A ⊗ M`.

This "base change" operation is also known as "extension of scalars". -/

name: baseChange_eq_span
statement: theorem baseChange_eq_span  : p.baseChange A = span A (p.map (TensorProduct.mk R A M 1))
proof:
refine le_antisymm ?_ ?_
  · rw [baseChange, LinearMap.range_le_iff_comap, eq_top_iff,
      ← span_eq_top_of_span_eq_top R A _ (span_tmul_eq_top R ..), span_le]
    refine fun _ ⟨a, m, h⟩ ↦ ?_
    rw [← h, SetLike.mem_coe, mem_comap, LinearMap.baseChange_tmul, ← mul_one a, ← smul_eq_mul,
      ← smul_tmul']
    exact smul_mem _ a (subset_span ⟨m, m.2, rfl⟩)
  · refine span_le.2 fun _ ⟨m, hm, h⟩ ↦ h ▸ ⟨1 ⊗ₜ[R] ⟨m, hm⟩, rfl⟩

@[simp]

name: baseChange_top
statement: theorem baseChange_top  : (⊤ : Submodule R M).baseChange A = ⊤
proof:
rw [eq_top_iff, ← span_eq_top_of_span_eq_top R A _ (span_tmul_eq_top R ..)]
  exact span_le.2 fun _ ⟨a, m, h⟩ ↦ h ▸ tmul_mem_baseChange_of_mem _ trivial

variable {p q} in

name: baseChange_mono
statement: theorem baseChange_mono (h : p ≤ q) : p.baseChange A ≤ q.baseChange A
proof:
rw [baseChange, LinearMap.baseChange, ← subtype_comp_inclusion p q h,
    ← LinearMap.id_comp LinearMap.id, AlgebraTensorModule.map_comp]
  apply LinearMap.range_comp_le_range

@[simp]

name: baseChange_span
statement: theorem baseChange_span (s : Set M) : (span R s).baseChange A = span A (TensorProduct.mk R A M 1 '' s)
proof:
rw [baseChange_eq_span, map_span, span_span_of_tower]

/-- Given an `R`-submodule `p` of `M`, and `R`-algebra `A`, we obtain an `A`-submodule of
`A ⊗[R] M` by `p.baseChange A`. This is then the surjective `A`-linear map
`A ⊗[R] M → p.baseChange A`. -/

name: lift_mk_compr₂ₛₗ
statement: theorem lift_mk_compr₂ₛₗ (g : M ⊗ N →ₛₗ[σ₁₂] P₂) : lift ((mk R M N).compr₂ₛₗ g) = g
proof: rw [lift_compr₂ₛₗ g, lift_mk, LinearMap.comp_id]

name: lift_mk_compr₂
statement: theorem lift_mk_compr₂ (f : M ⊗ N →ₗ[R] P) : lift ((mk R M N).compr₂ f) = f
proof:
rw [lift_compr₂ f, lift_mk, LinearMap.comp_id]

/-- This used to be an `@[ext]` lemma, but it fails very slowly when the `ext` tactic tries to apply
it in some cases, notably when one wants to show equality of two linear maps. The `@[ext]`
attribute is now added locally where it is needed. Using this as the `@[ext]` lemma instead of
`TensorProduct.ext'` allows `ext` to apply lemmas specific to `M →ₗ _` and `N →ₗ _`.

See note [partially-applied ext lemmas]. -/

name: comm_trans_comm
statement: theorem comm_trans_comm  : TensorProduct.comm R N M ≪≫ₗ TensorProduct.comm R M N = .refl _ _
proof: apply LinearEquiv.toLinearMap_injective; ext; rfl

name: comm_comp_comm
statement: theorem comm_comp_comm  : (TensorProduct.comm R N M).toLinearMap ∘ₗ (TensorProduct.comm R M N).toLinearMap = .id
proof:
simp

@[simp]

name: comm_comp_comm_assoc
statement: theorem comm_comp_comm_assoc (f : P →ₗ[R] M ⊗[R] N) : (TensorProduct.comm R N M).toLinearMap ∘ₗ (TensorProduct.comm R M N).toLinearMap ∘ₗ f = f
proof:
rw [← LinearMap.comp_assoc, comm_comp_comm, LinearMap.id_comp]

@[simp] theorem comm_comm (x) :
    TensorProduct.comm R M N (TensorProduct.comm R N M x) = x :=
  congr($(comm_trans_comm _ _ _) x)

end

section CompatibleSMul

variable (R) (A S M N : Type*) [AddCommMonoid M] [AddCommMonoid N] [Module R M]
  [Module R N] [CommSemiring A] [Module A M] [Module A N] [SMulCommClass R A M]
  [CommSemiring S] [Module S M] [SMulCommClass R S M] [SMulCommClass A S M]
  [CompatibleSMul R A M N]

/-- If M and N are both R- and A-modules and their actions on them commute,
and if the A-action on `M ⊗[R] N` can switch between the two factors, then there is a
canonical S-linear map from `M ⊗[A] N` to `M ⊗[R] N`,
where `S` is any other ring acting on `M` and whose action commutes with the `A` and `R`-actions. -/

name: le_comap_range_lTensor
statement: theorem le_comap_range_lTensor (q : Q) : LinearMap.range g ≤ (LinearMap.range (lTensor Q g)).comap (TensorProduct.mk R Q P q)
proof:
rintro x ⟨n, rfl⟩
  exact ⟨q ⊗ₜ[R] n, rfl⟩

name: lTensor_exact
statement: theorem lTensor_exact  : Exact (lTensor Q f) (lTensor Q g)
proof:
rw [exact_iff, ← Submodule.ker_mkQ (p := range (lTensor Q f)),
    ← lTensor.inverse_comp_lTensor Q hfg hg]
  apply symm
  apply LinearMap.ker_comp_of_ker_eq_bot
  rw [LinearMap.ker_eq_bot]
  exact (lTensor.equiv Q hfg hg).symm.injective

/-- Right-exactness of tensor product -/

name: lTensor_mkQ
statement: theorem lTensor_mkQ (N : Submodule R M) : ker (lTensor Q N.mkQ) = range (lTensor Q N.subtype)
proof:
rw [← exact_iff]
  exact lTensor_exact Q (LinearMap.exact_subtype_mkQ N) (Submodule.mkQ_surjective N)

/-- The direct map in `rTensor.equiv` -/
noncomputable def rTensor.toFun (hfg : Exact f g) :
    N ⊗[R] Q ⧸ range (rTensor Q f) →ₗ[R] P ⊗[R] Q :=
  Submodule.liftQ _ (rTensor Q g) <| by
    rw [range_le_iff_comap, ← ker_comp, ← rTensor_comp,
      hfg.linearMap_comp_eq_zero, rTensor_zero, ker_zero]

/-- The inverse map in `rTensor.equiv_of_rightInverse` (computably, given a right inverse) -/
noncomputable def rTensor.inverse_of_rightInverse {h : P → N} (hfg : Exact f g)
    (hgh : Function.RightInverse h g) :
    P ⊗[R] Q →ₗ[R] N ⊗[R] Q ⧸ LinearMap.range (rTensor Q f) :=
  TensorProduct.lift {
    toFun := fun p ↦ Submodule.mkQ _ ∘ₗ TensorProduct.mk R _ _ (h p)
    map_add' := fun p p' => LinearMap.ext fun q => (Submodule.Quotient.eq _).mpr <| by
      change h (p + p') ⊗ₜ[R] q - (h p ⊗ₜ[R] q + h p' ⊗ₜ[R] q) ∈ range (rTensor Q f)
      rw [← TensorProduct.add_tmul, ← TensorProduct.sub_tmul]
      apply le_comap_range_rTensor f
      rw [exact_iff] at hfg
      simp only [← hfg, mem_ker, map_sub, map_add, hgh _, sub_self]
    map_smul' := fun r p => LinearMap.ext fun q => (Submodule.Quotient.eq _).mpr <| by
      change h (r • p) ⊗ₜ[R] q - r • h p ⊗ₜ[R] q ∈ range (rTensor Q f)
      rw [TensorProduct.smul_tmul', ← TensorProduct.sub_tmul]
      apply le_comap_range_rTensor f
      rw [exact_iff] at hfg
      simp only [← hfg, mem_ker, map_sub, map_smul, hgh _, sub_self] }

name: rTensor_exact
statement: theorem rTensor_exact  : Exact (rTensor Q f) (rTensor Q g)
proof:
rw [rTensor_exact_iff_lTensor_exact]
  exact lTensor_exact Q hfg hg

/-- Right-exactness of tensor product (`rTensor`) -/

name: rTensor_mkQ
statement: theorem rTensor_mkQ (N : Submodule R M) : ker (rTensor Q N.mkQ) = range (rTensor Q N.subtype)
proof:
rw [← exact_iff]
  exact rTensor_exact Q (LinearMap.exact_subtype_mkQ N) (Submodule.mkQ_surjective N)

open Submodule LinearEquiv in

name: eval_charpoly
statement: theorem eval_charpoly (t : R) : f.charpoly.eval t = (algebraMap _ _ t - f).det
proof:
rw [charpoly, Matrix.eval_charpoly, ← LinearMap.det_toMatrix (chooseBasis R M), map_sub,
    scalar_apply, toMatrix_algebraMap, scalar_apply]

@[simp]

name: charpoly_zero
statement: theorem charpoly_zero  : (0 : M →ₗ[R] M).charpoly = X ^ Module.finrank R M
proof: simp [charpoly, Module.finrank_eq_card_chooseBasisIndex]

name: charpoly_one
statement: theorem charpoly_one  : (1 : M →ₗ[R] M).charpoly = (X - 1) ^ Module.finrank R M
proof: simp [charpoly, Module.finrank_eq_card_chooseBasisIndex, Matrix.charpoly_one]

name: charpoly_natDegree
statement: theorem charpoly_natDegree  : natDegree (charpoly f) = finrank R M
proof:
haveI := nontrivial_of_invariantBasisNumber
  rw [charpoly, Matrix.charpoly_natDegree_eq_dim, finrank_eq_card_chooseBasisIndex]

end Coeff

section CayleyHamilton

/-- The **Cayley-Hamilton Theorem**, that the characteristic polynomial of a linear map, applied
to the linear map itself, is zero.

See `Matrix.aeval_self_charpoly` for the equivalent statement about matrices. -/

name: aeval_self_charpoly
statement: theorem aeval_self_charpoly  : aeval f f.charpoly = 0
proof:
apply (LinearEquiv.map_eq_zero_iff (algEquivMatrix (chooseBasis R M)).toLinearEquiv).1
  rw [AlgEquiv.toLinearEquiv_apply, ← AlgEquiv.coe_algHom, ← Polynomial.aeval_algHom_apply _ _ _,
    charpoly_def]
  exact Matrix.aeval_self_charpoly _

name: pow_eq_aeval_mod_charpoly
statement: theorem pow_eq_aeval_mod_charpoly (k : ℕ) : f ^ k = aeval f (X ^ k %ₘ f.charpoly)
proof:
rw [← aeval_eq_aeval_mod_charpoly, map_pow, aeval_X]

variable {f}

name: basis_apply
statement: theorem basis_apply (s : Finset I) : b.ExteriorAlgebra s = ιMulti_family R s.card b (prodEquiv.symm s).2
proof: simp [Basis.ExteriorAlgebra]

name: basis_apply_powersetCard
statement: theorem basis_apply_powersetCard  : b.ExteriorAlgebra s = ιMulti_family R m b s
proof: simp [basis_apply_ofCard]

name: basis_eq_coe_basis
statement: theorem basis_eq_coe_basis  : b.ExteriorAlgebra s = (b.exteriorPower m s : ExteriorAlgebra R M)
proof: rw [basis_apply_powersetCard, exteriorPower.basis_apply, ιMulti_family_apply_coe]

name: basis_mul_of_not_disjoint
statement: theorem basis_mul_of_not_disjoint (h : ¬Disjoint s.val t.val) : b.ExteriorAlgebra s * b.ExteriorAlgebra t = 0
proof: simpa only [basis_apply_powersetCard] using ιMulti_family_mul_of_not_disjoint R b s t h

name: ιMulti_span
statement: theorem ιMulti_span  : Submodule.span R (Set.range fun x : Σ n, (Fin n → M) => ιMulti R x.1 x.2) = ⊤
proof:
rw [Submodule.eq_top_iff']
  intro x
  induction x using DirectSum.Decomposition.inductionOn fun i => ⋀[R]^i M with
  | zero => exact Submodule.zero_mem _
  | add _ _ hm hm' => exact Submodule.add_mem _ hm hm'
  | homogeneous hm =>
    let ⟨m, hm⟩ := hm
    apply Set.mem_of_mem_of_subset hm
    rw [← ιMulti_span_fixedDegree]
    refine Submodule.span_mono fun _ hx ↦ ?_
    obtain ⟨y, rfl⟩ := hx
    exact ⟨⟨_, y⟩, rfl⟩

end ExteriorAlgebra

name: ιMulti_zero_apply
statement: theorem ιMulti_zero_apply (v : Fin 0 → M) : ιMulti R 0 v = 1
proof:
simp [ιMulti]

@[simp]

name: ιMulti_span_fixedDegree
statement: theorem ιMulti_span_fixedDegree (n : ℕ) : Submodule.span R (Set.range (ιMulti R n)) = ⋀[R]^n M
proof:
refine le_antisymm (Submodule.span_le.2 (ιMulti_range R n)) ?_
  rw [exteriorPower, Submodule.pow_eq_span_pow_set, Submodule.span_le]
  refine fun u hu ↦ Submodule.subset_span ?_
  obtain ⟨f, rfl⟩ := Set.mem_pow.mp hu
  refine ⟨fun i => ιInv (f i).1, ?_⟩
  rw [ιMulti_apply]
  congr with i
  obtain ⟨v, hv⟩ := (f i).prop
  rw [← hv, ι_leftInverse]

/-- Given a linearly ordered family `v` of vectors of `M` and a natural number `n`, produce the
family of `n`fold exterior products of elements of `v`, seen as members of the exterior algebra. -/
abbrev ιMulti_family (n : ℕ) {I : Type*} [LinearOrder I] (v : I → M)
    (s : Set.powersetCard I n) : ExteriorAlgebra R M :=
  ιMulti R n (v ∘ (Set.powersetCard.ofFinEmbEquiv.symm s))

open Set Set.powersetCard

name: ιInv_comp_map
statement: theorem ιInv_comp_map (f : M →ₗ[R] N) : ιInv.comp (map f).toLinearMap = f.comp ιInv
proof:
letI : Module Rᵐᵒᵖ M := Module.compHom _ ((RingHom.id R).fromOpposite mul_comm)
  haveI : IsCentralScalar R M := ⟨fun r m => rfl⟩
  letI : Module Rᵐᵒᵖ N := Module.compHom _ ((RingHom.id R).fromOpposite mul_comm)
  haveI : IsCentralScalar R N := ⟨fun r m => rfl⟩
  unfold ιInv
  conv_lhs => rw [LinearMap.comp_assoc, ← AlgHom.comp_toLinearMap, toTrivSqZeroExt_comp_map,
                AlgHom.comp_toLinearMap, ← LinearMap.comp_assoc, TrivSqZeroExt.sndHom_comp_map]
  rfl

open Function in
/-- For a linear map `f` from `M` to `N`,
`ExteriorAlgebra.map g` is a retraction of `ExteriorAlgebra.map f` iff
`g` is a retraction of `f`. -/
@[simp]

name: toExterior_ι
statement: theorem toExterior_ι (m : M) : TensorAlgebra.toExterior (TensorAlgebra.ι R m) = ExteriorAlgebra.ι R m
proof:
simp [toExterior]

end TensorAlgebra

name: orthogonal_top_eq_ker
statement: theorem orthogonal_top_eq_ker (hB : B.IsRefl) : B.orthogonal ⊤ = LinearMap.ker B
proof: ext; simp [LinearMap.BilinForm.IsOrtho, LinearMap.ext_iff, hB.eq_iff]

name: isCompl_orthogonal_iff_disjoint
statement: theorem isCompl_orthogonal_iff_disjoint (hB₀ : B.IsRefl) : IsCompl W (B.orthogonal W) ↔ Disjoint W (B.orthogonal W)
proof:
refine ⟨IsCompl.disjoint, fun h ↦ ⟨h, ?_⟩⟩
  rw [codisjoint_iff]
  apply (eq_top_of_finrank_eq <| (finrank_le _).antisymm _)
  calc
    finrank K V ≤ finrank K V + finrank K ↥(W ⊓ B.orthogonal ⊤) := le_self_add
    _ ≤ finrank K ↥(W ⊔ B.orthogonal W) + finrank K ↥(W ⊓ B.orthogonal W) := ?_
    _ ≤ finrank K ↥(W ⊔ B.orthogonal W) := by simp [h.eq_bot]
  rw [finrank_sup_add_finrank_inf_eq, finrank_add_finrank_orthogonal hB₀ W]

/-- A subspace is complement to its orthogonal complement with respect to some
reflexive bilinear form if that bilinear form restricted on to the subspace is nondegenerate. -/

name: compLeft_id
statement: theorem compLeft_id (B : BilinForm R M) : B.compLeft LinearMap.id = B
proof:
ext
  rfl

@[simp]

name: compRight_id
statement: theorem compRight_id (B : BilinForm R M) : B.compRight LinearMap.id = B
proof:
ext
  rfl

-- Shortcut for `comp_id_{left,right}` followed by `comp{Right,Left}_id`,
-- Needs higher priority to be applied
@[simp high]

name: comp_id_id
statement: theorem comp_id_id (B : BilinForm R M) : B.comp LinearMap.id LinearMap.id = B
proof:
ext
  rfl

name: congr_symm
statement: theorem congr_symm (e : M ≃ₗ[R] M') : (congr e).symm = congr e.symm
proof:
rfl

@[simp]

name: sum_repr_mul_repr_mul
statement: theorem sum_repr_mul_repr_mul (x y : M) : ((b.repr x).sum fun i xi => (b.repr y).sum fun j yj => xi • yj • B (b i) (b j)) = B x y
proof:
conv_rhs => rw [← b.linearCombination_repr x, ← b.linearCombination_repr y]
  simp_rw [Finsupp.linearCombination_apply, Finsupp.sum, sum_left, sum_right, smul_left, smul_right,
    smul_eq_mul]

end Basis

end BilinForm

end LinearMap

name: flip_flip
statement: theorem flip_flip  : flipHom.trans flipHom = LinearEquiv.refl R (BilinForm R M)
proof:
ext A
  simp

/-- The `flip` of a bilinear form over a commutative ring, obtained by exchanging the left and
right arguments. -/
abbrev flip (B : BilinForm R M) :=
  flipHom B

end flip

/-- The restriction of a bilinear form on a submodule. -/
@[simps! apply]

name: isSymm_iff
statement: theorem isSymm_iff  : IsSymm B ↔ LinearMap.IsSymm B
proof:
simp [isSymm_def, LinearMap.isSymm_def]

namespace IsSymm

name: repr_comp_embedding_eq_smul
statement: theorem repr_comp_embedding_eq_smul  : snf.bM.repr m ∘ snf.f = snf.a • (snf.bN.repr m : Fin n → R)
proof:
ext i
  simp [Pi.smul_apply (snf.a i)]

@[simp] lemma coord_apply_embedding_eq_smul_coord {i : Fin n} :
    snf.bM.coord (snf.f i) ∘ₗ N.subtype = snf.a i • snf.bN.coord i := by
  ext m
  simp [Pi.smul_apply (snf.a i)]

/-- Given a Smith-normal-form pair of bases for `N ⊆ M`, and a linear endomorphism `f` of `M`
that preserves `N`, the diagonal of the matrix of the restriction `f` to `N` does not depend on
which of the two bases for `N` is used. -/
@[simp]

name: natCard_eq
statement: theorem natCard_eq  : Nat.card (ModN G n) = n ^ Module.finrank ℤ G
proof:
simp [Nat.card_congr (basis <| Module.Free.chooseBasis ℤ G).repr.toEquiv,
    Nat.card_eq_fintype_card, Module.finrank_eq_card_chooseBasisIndex]

end ModN

name: finrank_real_complex
statement: theorem finrank_real_complex  : finrank ℝ ℂ = 2
proof:
rw [finrank_eq_card_basis basisOneI, Fintype.card_fin]

@[simp]

name: det_conjAe
statement: theorem det_conjAe  : conjAe.toLinearEquiv.toLinearMap.det = -1
proof:
rw [← LinearMap.det_toMatrix basisOneI, toMatrix_conjAe, Matrix.det_fin_two_of]
  simp

/-- The determinant of `conjAe`, as a linear equiv. -/
@[simp]

name: toMatrix_conjAe
statement: theorem toMatrix_conjAe  : conjAe.toLinearEquiv.toLinearMap.toMatrix basisOneI basisOneI = !![1, 0; 0, -1]
proof:
ext i j
  fin_cases i <;> fin_cases j <;> simp [LinearMap.toMatrix_apply]

/-- The identity and the complex conjugation are the only two `ℝ`-algebra homomorphisms of `ℂ`. -/

name: real_algHom_eq_id_or_conj
statement: theorem real_algHom_eq_id_or_conj (f : ℂ →ₐ[ℝ] ℂ) : f = AlgHom.id ℝ ℂ ∨ f = conjAe
proof:
refine
      (eq_or_eq_neg_of_sq_eq_sq (f I) I <| by rw [← map_pow, I_sq, map_neg, map_one]).imp ?_ ?_ <;>
    refine fun h => algHom_ext ?_
  exacts [h, conj_I.symm ▸ h]

/-- The natural `LinearEquiv` from `ℂ` to `ℝ × ℝ`. -/
@[simps! +simpRhs apply symm_apply_re symm_apply_im]

name: adjoin_I
statement: theorem adjoin_I  : ℝ[I] = ⊤
proof:
refine top_unique fun x hx => ?_; clear hx
  rw [← x.re_add_im, ← smul_eq_mul, ← Complex.coe_algebraMap]
  exact add_mem (algebraMap_mem _ _) (Subalgebra.smul_mem _ (subset_adjoin <| by simp) _)

@[simp]

name: realPart_apply_coe
statement: theorem realPart_apply_coe (a : A) : (ℜ a : A) = (2 : ℝ)⁻¹ • (a + star a)
proof:
unfold realPart
  simp only [selfAdjointPart_apply_coe, invOf_eq_inv]

name: imaginaryPart_apply_coe
statement: theorem imaginaryPart_apply_coe (a : A) : (ℑ a : A) = -I • (2 : ℝ)⁻¹ • (a - star a)
proof:
unfold imaginaryPart
  simp only [LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, skewAdjoint.negISMul_apply_coe,
    skewAdjointPart_apply_coe, invOf_eq_inv, neg_smul]

/-- The standard decomposition of `ℜ a + Complex.I • ℑ a = a` of an element of a star module over
`ℂ` into a linear combination of self adjoint elements. -/

name: realPart_add_I_smul_imaginaryPart
statement: theorem realPart_add_I_smul_imaginaryPart (a : A) : (ℜ a : A) + I • (ℑ a : A) = a
proof:
simpa only [smul_smul, realPart_apply_coe, imaginaryPart_apply_coe, neg_smul, I_mul_I, one_smul,
    neg_sub, add_add_sub_cancel, smul_sub, smul_add, neg_sub_neg, invOf_eq_inv] using
    invOf_two_smul_add_invOf_two_smul ℝ a

@[simp]

name: realPart_I_smul
statement: theorem realPart_I_smul (a : A) : ℜ (I • a) = -ℑ a
proof:
ext
  simp [realPart_apply_coe, imaginaryPart_apply_coe, smul_comm I, sub_eq_add_neg, add_comm]

@[simp]

name: imaginaryPart_I_smul
statement: theorem imaginaryPart_I_smul (a : A) : ℑ (I • a) = ℜ a
proof:
ext
  simp [realPart_apply_coe, imaginaryPart_apply_coe, smul_comm I (2⁻¹ : ℝ), smul_smul I]

name: skewAdjointPart_eq_I_smul_imaginaryPart
statement: theorem skewAdjointPart_eq_I_smul_imaginaryPart (x : A) : (skewAdjointPart ℝ x : A) = I • (imaginaryPart x : A)
proof: simp [imaginaryPart_apply_coe, smul_smul]

name: imaginaryPart_comp_subtype_selfAdjoint
statement: theorem imaginaryPart_comp_subtype_selfAdjoint  : imaginaryPart.comp (selfAdjoint.submodule ℝ A).subtype = 0
proof:
rw [imaginaryPart, LinearMap.comp_assoc, skewAdjointPart_comp_subtype_selfAdjoint,
    LinearMap.comp_zero]

@[simp]

name: ker_imaginaryPart
statement: theorem ker_imaginaryPart  : imaginaryPart.ker = selfAdjoint.submodule ℝ A
proof:
ext x
  simp [selfAdjoint.submodule, selfAdjoint.mem_iff, imaginaryPart, Subtype.ext_iff]
  grind

@[simp]

name: span_selfAdjoint
statement: theorem span_selfAdjoint  : span ℂ (selfAdjoint A : Set A) = ⊤
proof:
refine eq_top_iff'.mpr fun x ↦ ?_
  rw [← realPart_add_I_smul_imaginaryPart x]
  exact add_mem (subset_span (ℜ x).property) <|
    SMulMemClass.smul_mem _ <| subset_span (ℑ x).property

end AddCommGroup

open scoped ComplexStarModule

/-- The natural `ℝ`-linear equivalence between `selfAdjoint ℂ` and `ℝ`. -/
@[simps apply symm_apply]

name: realPart_ofReal
statement: theorem realPart_ofReal (r : ℝ) : (ℜ (r : ℂ) : ℂ) = r
proof:
rw [realPart_apply_coe, star_def, conj_ofReal, ← two_smul ℝ (r : ℂ)]
  simp

@[simp]

name: imaginaryPart_ofReal
statement: theorem imaginaryPart_ofReal (r : ℝ) : ℑ (r : ℂ) = 0
proof:
ext1; simp [imaginaryPart_apply_coe, conj_ofReal]

set_option linter.style.whitespace false in -- manual alignment is not recognised

name: example_14
statement: example  : (Semiring.toNatAlgebra : Algebra ℕ ℂ) = Complex.instAlgebraOfReal
proof: with_reducible_and_instances rfl

name: example_15
statement: example  : (Ring.toIntAlgebra ℂ : Algebra ℤ ℂ) = Complex.instAlgebraOfReal
proof: with_reducible_and_instances rfl

name: example_16
statement: example  : Module.restrictScalars ℝ ℂ ℂ = Complex.instModule
proof:
with_reducible_and_instances rfl

section

variable {A : Type*} [Semiring A] [Algebra ℝ A]

/-- We need this lemma since `Complex.coe_algebraMap` diverts the simp-normal form away from
`AlgHom.commutes`. -/
@[simp]

name: radical_le_ker_polarBilin
statement: theorem radical_le_ker_polarBilin  : Q.radical ≤ Q.polarBilin.ker
proof:
intro m
  simp +contextual [mem_radical_iff', LinearMap.ext_iff, QuadraticMap.polar]

/--
A quadratic map is said to be **nondegenerate** if its radical is 0,
and the radical of its associated polar form has rank ≤ 1.
(The second condition is automatic if 2 is invertible in `R`, but not in general.)

See [elman-karpenko-merkurjev-2008], Chapter II, §7.
-/

name: radical_eq_ker_polarBilin
statement: theorem radical_eq_ker_polarBilin  : Q.radical = Q.polarBilin.ker
proof:
ext m
  simp only [mem_radical_iff', LinearMap.mem_ker, LinearMap.ext_iff, LinearMap.zero_apply,
    QuadraticMap.polarBilin_apply_apply, QuadraticMap.polar]
  refine ⟨by simp +contextual, fun h ↦ ?_⟩
  suffices Q m = 0 by grind
  specialize h m
  rwa [← two_smul R, QuadraticMap.map_smul, sub_sub, ← two_smul R, mul_smul, ← smul_sub,
    (isUnit_of_invertible 2).smul_eq_zero, two_smul, add_sub_cancel_right] at h

/-- If `2` is invertible in the coefficient ring,
the radical of a quadratic map is the kernel of its associated bilinear map. -/

name: radical_eq_ker_associated
statement: theorem radical_eq_ker_associated  : Q.radical = (QuadraticMap.associated Q).ker
proof:
rw [radical_eq_ker_polarBilin]
  ext m
  simp [LinearMap.ext_iff, QuadraticMap.polar, -smul_eq_mul, invOf_smul_eq_iff]

/--
If `2` is invertible in the coefficient ring,
a quadratic map is nondegenerate iff its radical is 0.
(Use `QuadraticMap.Nondegenerate.radical_eq_bot`
for the one-way implication in all characteristics.)
-/

name: nondegenerate_iff_radical_eq_bot
statement: theorem nondegenerate_iff_radical_eq_bot  : Q.Nondegenerate ↔ Q.radical = ⊥
proof:
refine ⟨Nondegenerate.radical_eq_bot, fun h ↦ ⟨h, ?_⟩⟩
  rw [← QuadraticMap.radical_eq_ker_polarBilin, h]
  nontriviality R
  simp only [rank_subsingleton', zero_le]

/-- If `2` is invertible in the coefficient ring,
a quadratic map is nondegenerate
iff its associated bilinear map is nondegenerate. -/

name: nondegenerate_associated_iff
statement: theorem nondegenerate_associated_iff  : (QuadraticMap.associated Q).Nondegenerate ↔ Q.Nondegenerate
proof:
rw [nondegenerate_iff_radical_eq_bot, radical_eq_ker_associated,
    LinearMap.IsRefl.nondegenerate_iff_separatingLeft, LinearMap.separatingLeft_iff_ker_eq_bot]
  exact fun x y ↦ (congr_arg (· x y) (associated_flip R Q)).trans

/-- If `2` is invertible in the coefficient ring,
a quadratic map is nondegenerate
iff its polar bilinear map is nondegenerate. -/

name: nondegenerate_polar_iff
statement: theorem nondegenerate_polar_iff  : (QuadraticMap.polarBilin Q).Nondegenerate ↔ Q.Nondegenerate
proof:
rw [nondegenerate_iff_radical_eq_bot, radical_eq_ker_polarBilin,
    LinearMap.IsRefl.nondegenerate_iff_separatingLeft, LinearMap.separatingLeft_iff_ker_eq_bot]
  exact fun x y ↦ (polar_comm Q y x).trans

end InvertibleTwo

end QuadraticMap

namespace QuadraticForm
variable {𝕜 ι : Type*} [Field 𝕜] [NeZero (2 : 𝕜)] [Fintype ι] {w : ι → 𝕜}

/-- Over a field of characteristic different from `2`,
the radical of a weighted-sum-of-squares quadratic form is the number of zero weights. -/

name: radical_weightedSumSquares
statement: theorem radical_weightedSumSquares  : radical (weightedSumSquares 𝕜 w) = Pi.spanSubset 𝕜 {i | w i = 0}
proof:
classical
  ext v
  simp only [mem_radical_iff', weightedSumSquares_apply, ← pow_two, smul_eq_mul, Pi.add_apply,
    add_sq, mul_add, sum_add_distrib, add_eq_right, Pi.mem_spanSubset_iff]
  constructor
  · rintro ⟨hv, hvv'⟩ i
    simpa [hv, Pi.single_apply, NeZero.ne, or_iff_not_imp_left] using hvv' (Pi.single i 1)
  · simpa only [← sum_add_distrib]
      using fun h ↦ ⟨sum_eq_zero (by grind), fun v ↦ sum_eq_zero (by grind)⟩

/-- If the quadratic form `Q` is equivalent to a weighted sum of squares with weights `w`, then
the rank of `Q.radical` is equal to the number of zero weights. -/

name: separatingLeft_dualProd
statement: theorem separatingLeft_dualProd  : (dualProd R M).SeparatingLeft ↔ Function.Injective (Module.Dual.eval R M)
proof:
classical
  rw [separatingLeft_iff_ker_eq_bot, ker_eq_bot]
