name: mem_pairSelfAdjointMatricesSubmodule
statement: theorem mem_pairSelfAdjointMatricesSubmodule  : A₁ ∈ pairSelfAdjointMatricesSubmodule J J₂ ↔ Matrix.IsAdjointPair J J₂ A₁ A₁
proof:
simp only [pairSelfAdjointMatricesSubmodule, Submodule.mem_map_equiv,
    mem_isPairSelfAdjointSubmodule, toMatrix'_symm, ← isAdjointPair_toLinearMap₂',
    IsPairSelfAdjoint, toLin'_apply']

/-- The submodule of self-adjoint matrices with respect to the bilinear form corresponding to
the matrix `J`. -/

name: mem_selfAdjointMatricesSubmodule
statement: theorem mem_selfAdjointMatricesSubmodule  : A₁ ∈ selfAdjointMatricesSubmodule J ↔ J.IsSelfAdjoint A₁
proof:
rw [selfAdjointMatricesSubmodule, mem_pairSelfAdjointMatricesSubmodule, Matrix.IsSelfAdjoint]

/-- The submodule of skew-adjoint matrices with respect to the bilinear form corresponding to
the matrix `J`. -/

name: mem_skewAdjointMatricesSubmodule
statement: theorem mem_skewAdjointMatricesSubmodule  : A₁ ∈ skewAdjointMatricesSubmodule J ↔ J.IsSkewAdjoint A₁
proof:
rw [skewAdjointMatricesSubmodule, mem_pairSelfAdjointMatricesSubmodule]
  simp [Matrix.IsSkewAdjoint, Matrix.IsAdjointPair]

end MatrixAdjoints

namespace LinearMap

/-! ### Nondegenerate bilinear forms -/

open Matrix

variable [CommRing R] [DecidableEq m] [Fintype m] [DecidableEq n] [Fintype n]
  {M : Matrix m n R}

section StandardBasis

variable {B : (m → R) →ₗ[R] (n → R) →ₗ[R] R}

/-!
Lemmas transferring nondegeneracy (or left/right separating) between a matrix and its associated
bilinear form (for the standard basis)
-/

name: separatingLeft_iff_det_ne_zero
statement: theorem separatingLeft_iff_det_ne_zero  : B.SeparatingLeft ↔ (toMatrix₂ b b B).det ≠ 0
proof: rw [← Matrix.separatingLeft_iff_det_ne_zero, separatingLeft_toMatrix₂_iff]

name: separatingRight_iff_det_ne_zero
statement: theorem separatingRight_iff_det_ne_zero  : B.SeparatingRight ↔ (toMatrix₂ b b B).det ≠ 0
proof: rw [← Matrix.separatingRight_iff_det_ne_zero, separatingRight_toMatrix₂_iff]

name: nondegenerate_iff_det_ne_zero
statement: theorem nondegenerate_iff_det_ne_zero  : B.Nondegenerate ↔ (toMatrix₂ b b B).det ≠ 0
proof: rw [← Matrix.nondegenerate_iff_det_ne_zero, nondegenerate_toMatrix₂_iff]

name: inv_mul
statement: theorem inv_mul (t : TransvectionStruct n R) : t.inv.toMatrix * t.toMatrix = 1
proof:
rcases t with ⟨_, _, t_hij⟩
  simp [toMatrix, transvection_mul_transvection_same, t_hij]

name: mul_inv
statement: theorem mul_inv (t : TransvectionStruct n R) : t.toMatrix * t.inv.toMatrix = 1
proof:
rcases t with ⟨_, _, t_hij⟩
  simp [toMatrix, transvection_mul_transvection_same, t_hij]

name: toMatrix_sumInl
statement: theorem toMatrix_sumInl (t : TransvectionStruct n R) : (t.sumInl p).toMatrix = fromBlocks t.toMatrix 0 0 1
proof:
cases t
  ext a b
  rcases a with a | a <;> rcases b with b | b
  · by_cases h : a = b <;> simp [TransvectionStruct.sumInl, transvection, h, single]
  · simp [TransvectionStruct.sumInl, transvection]
  · simp [TransvectionStruct.sumInl, transvection]
  · by_cases h : a = b <;> simp [TransvectionStruct.sumInl, transvection, h]

@[simp]

name: listTransvecCol_mul_last_row
statement: theorem listTransvecCol_mul_last_row (i : Fin r ⊕ Unit) : ((listTransvecCol M).prod * M) (inr unit) i = M (inr unit) i
proof:
simpa using listTransvecCol_mul_last_row_drop M i zero_le

/-- Multiplying by all the matrices in `listTransvecCol M` kills all the coefficients in the
last column but the last one. -/

name: mul_listTransvecRow_last_col
statement: theorem mul_listTransvecRow_last_col (i : Fin r ⊕ Unit) : (M * (listTransvecRow M).prod) i (inr unit) = M i (inr unit)
proof:
have A : (listTransvecRow M).length = r := by simp [listTransvecRow]
  rw [← List.take_length (l := listTransvecRow M), A]
  simpa using mul_listTransvecRow_last_col_take M i le_rfl

/-- Multiplying by all the matrices in `listTransvecRow M` kills all the coefficients in the
last row but the last one. -/

name: range_vecMulLinear
statement: theorem range_vecMulLinear (M : Matrix m n R) : LinearMap.range M.vecMulLinear = span R (range M.row)
proof:
letI := Classical.decEq m
  simp_rw [range_eq_map, ← iSup_range_single, Submodule.map_iSup, range_eq_map, ←
    Ideal.span_singleton_one, Ideal.span, Submodule.map_span, image_image, image_singleton,
    Matrix.vecMulLinear_apply, iSup_span, range_eq_iUnion, iUnion_singleton_eq_range,
    LinearMap.single, LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk, row_def]
  unfold vecMul
  simp_rw [single_dotProduct, one_mul]

name: smulTower_leftMulMatrix_algebraMap
statement: theorem smulTower_leftMulMatrix_algebraMap (x : S) : leftMulMatrix (b.smulTower c) (algebraMap _ _ x) = blockDiagonal fun _ ↦ leftMulMatrix b x
proof:
ext ⟨i, k⟩ ⟨j, k'⟩
  rw [smulTower_leftMulMatrix, AlgHom.commutes, blockDiagonal_apply, algebraMap_matrix_apply]
  split_ifs with h <;> simp only at h <;> simp

name: linearMap_apply
statement: theorem linearMap_apply (ij : ι₂ × ι₁) : (b₁.linearMap b₂ ij) = (Matrix.toLin b₁ b₂) (Matrix.stdBasis R ι₂ ι₁ ij)
proof: simp [linearMap]

name: isStablyFiniteRing_iff_isDedekindFiniteMonoid_moduleEnd
statement: theorem isStablyFiniteRing_iff_isDedekindFiniteMonoid_moduleEnd  : IsStablyFiniteRing A ↔ ∀ n, IsDedekindFiniteMonoid (Module.End A (Fin n → A))
proof:
simp_rw [isStablyFiniteRing_iff, MulEquivClass.isDedekindFiniteMonoid_iff
    (matrixRingEquivEndVecMulOpposite (ι := Fin _) (A := A)),
    MulOpposite.isDedekindFiniteMonoid_iff]

instance (ι) [Finite ι] [IsStablyFiniteRing A] : IsStablyFiniteRing (Module.End A (ι → A)) := by
  have := Fintype.ofFinite ι
  classical rw [← MulOpposite.isStablyFiniteRing_iff,
    ← RingEquiv.isStablyFiniteRing_iff (matrixRingEquivEndVecMulOpposite (ι := ι) (A := A))]
  infer_instance

open Function

name: isStablyFiniteRing_iff_injective_of_surjective
statement: theorem isStablyFiniteRing_iff_injective_of_surjective  : IsStablyFiniteRing A ↔ ∀ n (f : Module.End A (Fin n → A)), Surjective f → Injective f
proof:
simp_rw [isStablyFiniteRing_iff_isDedekindFiniteMonoid_moduleEnd, isDedekindFiniteMonoid_iff]
  refine ⟨fun h n f surj ↦ ?_, fun h n f g eq ↦ ?_⟩
  · have ⟨g, eq⟩ := Module.projective_lifting_property _ .id surj
    exact injective_of_comp_eq_id _ _ (h _ eq)
  · have surj := surjective_of_comp_eq_id _ _ eq
    have := (LinearEquiv.ofBijective f ⟨h _ _ surj, surj⟩).symm_comp
    rwa [← left_inv_eq_right_inv this eq]

/-- `Module.End.injective_of_surjective` is the more general version for finite free `A`-modules
not necessarily of the form `Fin n → A`, but this version requires less imports. -/

name: toMatrix_self
statement: theorem toMatrix_self  : e.toMatrix e = 1
proof:
unfold Basis.toMatrix
  ext i j
  simp [Matrix.one_apply, Finsupp.single_apply, eq_comm]

name: sum_toMatrix_smul_self
statement: theorem sum_toMatrix_smul_self  : ∑ i : ι, e.toMatrix v i j • e i = v j
proof: simp_rw [e.toMatrix_apply, e.sum_repr]

name: map_id

proof:
ext
  rfl

@[simp]

name: transpose_transpose
statement: theorem transpose_transpose (M : Matrix m n α) : Mᵀᵀ = M
proof:
ext
  rfl

variable (n α) in

name: coe_T_zpow
statement: theorem coe_T_zpow (n : ℤ) : (T ^ n).1 = !![1, n; 0, 1]
proof:
induction n with
  | zero => rw [zpow_zero, coe_one, Matrix.one_fin_two]
  | succ n h =>
    simp_rw [zpow_add, zpow_one, coe_mul, h, coe_T, Matrix.mul_fin_two]
    congrm !![_, ?_; _, _]
    rw [mul_one, mul_one, add_comm]
  | pred n h =>
    simp_rw [zpow_sub, zpow_one, coe_mul, h, coe_T_inv, Matrix.mul_fin_two]
    congrm !![?_, ?_; _, _] <;> ring

@[simp]

name: S_mul_S_eq
statement: theorem S_mul_S_eq  : (S : Matrix (Fin 2) (Fin 2) ℤ) * S = -1
proof:
simp only [S, Int.reduceNeg, cons_mul, Nat.succ_eq_add_one, Nat.reduceAdd,
    vecMul_cons, head_cons, zero_smul, tail_cons, neg_smul, one_smul, neg_cons, neg_zero, neg_empty,
    empty_vecMul, add_zero, zero_add, empty_mul, Equiv.symm_apply_apply]
  exact Eq.symm (eta_fin_two (-1))

name: T_S_rel
statement: theorem T_S_rel  : S • S • S • T • S • T • S = T⁻¹
proof:
ext i j
  fin_cases i <;> fin_cases j <;> rfl

end ModularGroup

name: swap_comm
statement: theorem swap_comm (i j : n) : swap R i j = swap R j i
proof:
simp only [swap, Equiv.swap_comm]

@[simp]

name: transpose_swap
statement: theorem transpose_swap (i j : n) : (swap R i j).transpose = swap R i j
proof: simp [swap]

name: swap_mul_self
statement: theorem swap_mul_self (i j : n) : swap R i j * swap R i j = 1
proof:
simp only [swap]
  rw [← Equiv.swap_inv, Equiv.Perm.inv_def]
  simp [← PEquiv.toMatrix_trans, ← Equiv.toPEquiv_trans]

end

namespace GeneralLinearGroup
variable (R : Type*) {n : Type*} [CommRing R] [DecidableEq n] [Fintype n]

/-- `Matrix.swap` as an element of `GL n R`. -/
@[simps]

name: map_swap
statement: theorem map_swap (i j : n) : (swap R i j).map f = swap S i j
proof:
ext : 1
  simp [swap]

end GeneralLinearGroup

end Matrix

name: isUnit_iff_isUnit_det
statement: theorem isUnit_iff_isUnit_det  : IsUnit A ↔ IsUnit A.det
proof:
simp only [← nonempty_invertible_iff_isUnit, (invertibleEquivDetInvertible A).nonempty_congr]

@[simp]

name: isUnit_det_transpose
statement: theorem isUnit_det_transpose (h : IsUnit A.det) : IsUnit Aᵀ.det
proof:
rw [det_transpose]
  exact h

/-! ### A noncomputable `Inv` instance  -/


/-- The inverse of a square matrix, when it is invertible (and zero otherwise). -/
noncomputable instance inv : Inv (Matrix n n α) :=
  ⟨fun A => A.det⁻¹ʳ • A.adjugate⟩

name: nonsing_inv_apply_not_isUnit
statement: theorem nonsing_inv_apply_not_isUnit (h : ¬IsUnit A.det) : A⁻¹ = 0
proof: rw [inv_def, Ring.inverse_non_unit _ h, zero_smul]

name: nonsing_inv_apply
statement: theorem nonsing_inv_apply (h : IsUnit A.det) : A⁻¹ = (↑h.unit⁻¹ : α) • A.adjugate
proof:
rw [inv_def, ← Ring.inverse_unit h.unit, IsUnit.unit_spec]

/-- The nonsingular inverse is the same as `invOf` when `A` is invertible. -/
@[simp]

name: invOf_eq_nonsing_inv
statement: theorem invOf_eq_nonsing_inv  : ⅟A = A⁻¹
proof:
letI := detInvertibleOfInvertible A
  rw [inv_def, Ring.inverse_invertible, invOf_eq]

/-- Coercing the result of `Units.instInv` is the same as coercing first and applying the
nonsingular inverse. -/
@[simp, norm_cast]

name: nonsing_inv_eq_ringInverse
statement: theorem nonsing_inv_eq_ringInverse  : A⁻¹ = A⁻¹ʳ
proof:
by_cases h_det : IsUnit A.det
  · cases (A.isUnit_iff_isUnit_det.mpr h_det).nonempty_invertible
    rw [← invOf_eq_nonsing_inv, Ring.inverse_invertible]
  · have h := mt A.isUnit_iff_isUnit_det.mp h_det
    rw [Ring.inverse_non_unit _ h, nonsing_inv_apply_not_isUnit A h_det]

name: transpose_nonsing_inv
statement: theorem transpose_nonsing_inv  : A⁻¹ᵀ = Aᵀ⁻¹
proof: rw [inv_def, inv_def, transpose_smul, det_transpose, adjugate_transpose]

name: conjTranspose_nonsing_inv
statement: theorem conjTranspose_nonsing_inv  : A⁻¹ᴴ = Aᴴ⁻¹
proof:
rw [inv_def, inv_def, conjTranspose_smul, det_conjTranspose, adjugate_conjTranspose,
    Ring.inverse_star]

/-- The `nonsing_inv` of `A` is a right inverse. -/
@[simp]

name: mul_nonsing_inv
statement: theorem mul_nonsing_inv (h : IsUnit A.det) : A * A⁻¹ = 1
proof:
cases (A.isUnit_iff_isUnit_det.mpr h).nonempty_invertible
  rw [← invOf_eq_nonsing_inv, mul_invOf_self]

/-- The nonsingular inverse of `A` is a left inverse. -/
@[simp]

name: nonsing_inv_mul
statement: theorem nonsing_inv_mul (h : IsUnit A.det) : A⁻¹ * A = 1
proof:
cases (A.isUnit_iff_isUnit_det.mpr h).nonempty_invertible
  rw [← invOf_eq_nonsing_inv, invOf_mul_self]

instance [Invertible A] : Invertible A⁻¹ := by
  rw [← invOf_eq_nonsing_inv]
  infer_instance

@[simp]

name: inv_inv_of_invertible
statement: theorem inv_inv_of_invertible  : A⁻¹⁻¹ = A
proof:
simp only [← invOf_eq_nonsing_inv, invOf_invOf]

@[simp]

name: nonsing_inv_cancel_or_zero
statement: theorem nonsing_inv_cancel_or_zero  : A⁻¹ * A = 1 ∧ A * A⁻¹ = 1 ∨ A⁻¹ = 0
proof:
by_cases h : IsUnit A.det
  · exact Or.inl ⟨nonsing_inv_mul _ h, mul_nonsing_inv _ h⟩
  · exact Or.inr (nonsing_inv_apply_not_isUnit _ h)

name: det_nonsing_inv_mul_det
statement: theorem det_nonsing_inv_mul_det (h : IsUnit A.det) : A⁻¹.det * A.det = 1
proof:
rw [← det_mul, A.nonsing_inv_mul h, det_one]

@[simp]

name: det_nonsing_inv
statement: theorem det_nonsing_inv  : A⁻¹.det = A.det⁻¹ʳ
proof:
by_cases h : IsUnit A.det
  · cases h.nonempty_invertible
    letI := invertibleOfDetInvertible A
    rw [Ring.inverse_invertible, ← invOf_eq_nonsing_inv, det_invOf]
  cases isEmpty_or_nonempty n
  · rw [det_isEmpty, det_isEmpty, Ring.inverse_one]
  · rw [Ring.inverse_non_unit _ h, nonsing_inv_apply_not_isUnit _ h, det_zero ‹_›]

name: unitOfDetInvertible_eq_nonsingInvUnit
statement: theorem unitOfDetInvertible_eq_nonsingInvUnit  : unitOfDetInvertible A = nonsingInvUnit A (isUnit_of_invertible _)
proof:
ext
  rfl

variable {A} {B}

/-- If matrix A is left invertible, then its inverse equals its left inverse. -/

name: inv_zero
statement: theorem inv_zero  : (0 : Matrix n n α)⁻¹ = 0
proof:
rcases subsingleton_or_nontrivial α with ht | ht
  · simp [eq_iff_true_of_subsingleton]
  rcases (Fintype.card n).zero_le.eq_or_lt with hc | hc
  · rw [eq_comm, Fintype.card_eq_zero_iff] at hc
    subsingleton
  · have hn : Nonempty n := Fintype.card_pos_iff.mp hc
    refine nonsing_inv_apply_not_isUnit _ ?_
    simp [det]

noncomputable instance : InvOneClass (Matrix n n α) :=
  { Matrix.one, Matrix.inv with inv_one := inv_eq_left_inv (by simp) }

name: inv_diagonal
statement: theorem inv_diagonal (v : n → α) : (diagonal v)⁻¹ = diagonal v⁻¹ʳ
proof:
rw [nonsing_inv_eq_ringInverse]
  by_cases h : IsUnit v
  · have := isUnit_diagonal.mpr h
    cases this.nonempty_invertible
    cases h.nonempty_invertible
    rw [Ring.inverse_invertible, Ring.inverse_invertible, invOf_diagonal_eq]
  · have := isUnit_diagonal.not.mpr h
    rw [Ring.inverse_non_unit _ h, Pi.zero_def, diagonal_zero, Ring.inverse_non_unit _ this]

end Diagonal

/-- The inverse of a 1×1 or 0×0 matrix is always diagonal.

While we could write this as `of fun _ _ => (A default default)⁻¹ʳ` on the RHS, this is
less useful because:

* It wouldn't work for 0×0 matrices.
* More things are true about diagonal matrices than constant matrices, and so more lemmas exist.

`Matrix.diagonal_unique` can be used to reach this form, while `Ring.inverse_eq_inv` can be used
to replace `Ring.inverse` with `⁻¹`.
-/
@[simp]

name: inv_inv_inv
statement: theorem inv_inv_inv (A : Matrix n n α) : A⁻¹⁻¹⁻¹ = A⁻¹
proof:
by_cases h : IsUnit A.det
  · rw [nonsing_inv_nonsing_inv _ h]
  · simp [nonsing_inv_apply_not_isUnit _ h]

/-- The `Matrix` version of `inv_add_inv'` -/

name: mul_inv_rev
statement: theorem mul_inv_rev (A B : Matrix n n α) : (A * B)⁻¹ = B⁻¹ * A⁻¹
proof:
simp only [inv_def]
  rw [Matrix.smul_mul, Matrix.mul_smul, smul_smul, det_mul, adjugate_mul_distrib,
    Ring.mul_inverse_rev]

/-- A version of `List.prod_inv_reverse` for `Matrix.inv`. -/

name: zero_zpow_eq
statement: theorem zero_zpow_eq (n : ℤ) : (0 : M) ^ n = if n = 0 then 1 else 0
proof:
split_ifs with h
  · rw [h, zpow_zero]
  · rw [zero_zpow _ h]

name: zpow_neg_one
statement: theorem zpow_neg_one (A : M) : A ^ (-1 : ℤ) = A⁻¹
proof:
convert DivInvMonoid.zpow_neg' 0 A
  simp only [zpow_one, Int.ofNat_zero, Int.natCast_succ, zpow_eq_pow, zero_add]

@[simp]

name: permanent_one
statement: theorem permanent_one  : permanent (1 : Matrix n n R) = 1
proof: rw [← diagonal_one]; simp [-diagonal_one]

name: permanent_transpose
statement: theorem permanent_transpose (M : Matrix n n R) : Mᵀ.permanent = M.permanent
proof:
refine sum_bijective _ inv_involutive.bijective _ _ ?_
  intro σ
  apply Fintype.prod_equiv σ
  simp

/-- Permuting the columns does not change the permanent. -/

name: trace_one
statement: theorem trace_one  : trace (1 : Matrix n n R) = Fintype.card n
proof:
simp_rw [trace, diag_one, Pi.one_def, Finset.sum_const, nsmul_one, Finset.card_univ]

end One

section Mul

@[simp]

name: trace_single_eq_of_ne
statement: theorem trace_single_eq_of_ne (h : i ≠ j) : trace (single i j c) = 0
proof:
simp [trace, h]

@[simp]

name: trace_single_eq_same
statement: theorem trace_single_eq_same  : trace (single i i c) = c
proof: simp [trace]

name: detp_one_diagonal
statement: theorem detp_one_diagonal (d : n → R) : detp 1 (diagonal d) = ∏ i, d i
proof:
rw [detp, sum_eq_single_of_mem 1]
  · simp
  · simp [ofSign]
  · rintro σ - hσ1
    obtain ⟨i, hi⟩ := not_forall.mp (mt Perm.ext_iff.mpr hσ1)
    exact prod_eq_zero (mem_univ i) (diagonal_apply_ne' _ hi)

@[simp]

name: detp_one_one
statement: theorem detp_one_one  : detp 1 (1 : Matrix n n R) = 1
proof:
rw [← diagonal_one, detp_one_diagonal, prod_const_one]

@[simp]

name: detp_neg_one_diagonal
statement: theorem detp_neg_one_diagonal (d : n → R) : detp (-1) (diagonal d) = 0
proof:
rw [detp, sum_eq_zero]
  intro σ hσ
  have hσ1 : σ ≠ 1 := by
    contrapose hσ
    rw [hσ, mem_ofSign, sign_one]
    decide
  obtain ⟨i, hi⟩ := not_forall.mp (mt Perm.ext_iff.mpr hσ1)
  exact prod_eq_zero (mem_univ i) (diagonal_apply_ne' _ hi)

@[simp]

name: detp_neg_one_one
statement: theorem detp_neg_one_one  : detp (-1) (1 : Matrix n n R) = 0
proof:
rw [← diagonal_one, detp_neg_one_diagonal]

/-- The adjugate matrix, but only the terms of a given sign. -/

name: mul_adjp_apply_eq
statement: theorem mul_adjp_apply_eq  : (A * adjp s A) i i = detp s A
proof:
have key := sum_fiberwise_eq_sum_filter (ofSign s) univ (· i) fun σ ↦ ∏ k, A k (σ k)
  simp_rw [mem_univ, filter_true] at key
  simp_rw [mul_apply, adjp_apply, mul_sum, detp, ← key]
  refine sum_congr rfl fun x hx ↦ sum_congr rfl fun σ hσ ↦ ?_
  rw [← prod_mul_prod_compl ({i} : Finset n), prod_singleton, (mem_filter.mp hσ).2]

name: mul_adjp_apply_ne
statement: theorem mul_adjp_apply_ne (h : i ≠ j) : (A * adjp 1 A) i j = (A * adjp (-1) A) i j
proof:
simp_rw [mul_apply, adjp_apply, mul_sum, sum_sigma']
  let f : (Σ x : n, Perm n) → (Σ x : n, Perm n) := fun ⟨x, σ⟩ ↦ ⟨σ i, σ * swap i j⟩
  let t s : Finset (Σ x : n, Perm n) := univ.sigma fun x ↦ (ofSign s).filter fun σ ↦ σ j = x
  have hf {s} : ∀ p ∈ t s, f (f p) = p := by
    intro ⟨x, σ⟩ hp
    rw [mem_sigma, mem_filter, mem_ofSign] at hp
    simp_rw [f, Perm.mul_apply, swap_apply_left, hp.2.2, mul_swap_mul_self]
  refine sum_bij' (fun p _ ↦ f p) (fun p _ ↦ f p) ?_ ?_ hf hf ?_
  · intro ⟨x, σ⟩ hp
    rw [mem_sigma, mem_filter, mem_ofSign] at hp ⊢
    rw [Perm.mul_apply, sign_mul, hp.2.1, sign_swap h, swap_apply_right]
    exact ⟨mem_univ (σ i), rfl, rfl⟩
  · intro ⟨x, σ⟩ hp
    rw [mem_sigma, mem_filter, mem_ofSign] at hp ⊢
    rw [Perm.mul_apply, sign_mul, hp.2.1, sign_swap h, swap_apply_right]
    exact ⟨mem_univ (σ i), rfl, rfl⟩
  · intro ⟨x, σ⟩ hp
    rw [mem_sigma, mem_filter, mem_ofSign] at hp
    have key : ({j}ᶜ : Finset n) = disjUnion ({i} : Finset n) ({i, j} : Finset n)ᶜ (by simp) := by
      rw [singleton_disjUnion, cons_eq_insert, compl_insert, insert_erase]
      rwa [mem_compl, mem_singleton]
    simp_rw [key, prod_disjUnion, prod_singleton, f, Perm.mul_apply, swap_apply_left, ← mul_assoc]
    rw [mul_comm (A i x) (A i (σ i)), hp.2.2]
    refine congr_arg _ (prod_congr rfl fun x hx ↦ ?_)
    rw [mem_compl, mem_insert, mem_singleton, not_or] at hx
    rw [swap_apply_of_ne_of_ne hx.1 hx.2]

name: mul_adjp_add_detp
statement: theorem mul_adjp_add_detp  : A * adjp 1 A + detp (-1) A • 1 = A * adjp (-1) A + detp 1 A • 1
proof:
ext i j
  rcases eq_or_ne i j with rfl | h <;> simp_rw [add_apply, smul_apply, smul_eq_mul]
  · simp_rw [mul_adjp_apply_eq, one_apply_eq, mul_one, add_comm]
  · simp_rw [mul_adjp_apply_ne A i j h, one_apply_ne h, mul_zero]

variable {A B}

name: detp_smul_add_adjp
statement: theorem detp_smul_add_adjp (hAB : A * B = 1) : detp 1 B • A + adjp (-1) B = detp (-1) B • A + adjp 1 B
proof:
have key := congr(A * $(mul_adjp_add_detp B))
  simp_rw [mul_add, ← mul_assoc, hAB, one_mul, Matrix.mul_smul, mul_one] at key
  rwa [add_comm, eq_comm, add_comm]

name: rank_zero
statement: theorem rank_zero  : rank (0 : Matrix m n R) = 0
proof:
rw [rank, mulVecLin_zero, LinearMap.range_zero, finrank_bot]

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
@[simp]

name: cRank_toNat_eq_rank
statement: theorem cRank_toNat_eq_rank (A : Matrix m n R) : A.cRank.toNat = A.rank
proof:
rw [cRank_toNat_eq_finrank, ← rank_eq_finrank_span_cols]

@[simp]

name: eRank_toNat_eq_rank
statement: theorem eRank_toNat_eq_rank (A : Matrix m n R) : A.eRank.toNat = A.rank
proof:
rw [eRank_toNat_eq_finrank, ← rank_eq_finrank_span_cols]

end CommRing

section Field

variable [Field R]

/-- The rank of a diagonal matrix is the count of non-zero elements on its main diagonal -/

name: ker_mulVecLin_conjTranspose_mul_self
statement: theorem ker_mulVecLin_conjTranspose_mul_self (A : Matrix m n R) : LinearMap.ker (Aᴴ * A).mulVecLin = LinearMap.ker (mulVecLin A)
proof:
ext x
  simp only [LinearMap.mem_ker, mulVecLin_apply, conjTranspose_mul_self_mulVec_eq_zero]

name: rank_conjTranspose_mul_self
statement: theorem rank_conjTranspose_mul_self (A : Matrix m n R) : (Aᴴ * A).rank = A.rank
proof:
dsimp only [rank]
  refine add_left_injective (finrank R (LinearMap.ker (mulVecLin A))) ?_
  dsimp only
  trans finrank R { x // x ∈ LinearMap.range (mulVecLin (Aᴴ * A)) } +
    finrank R { x // x ∈ LinearMap.ker (mulVecLin (Aᴴ * A)) }
  · rw [ker_mulVecLin_conjTranspose_mul_self]
  · simp only [LinearMap.finrank_range_add_finrank_ker]

-- this follows the proof here https://math.stackexchange.com/a/81903/1896
/-- TODO: prove this in greater generality. -/
@[simp]

name: rank_self_mul_conjTranspose
statement: theorem rank_self_mul_conjTranspose (A : Matrix m n R) : (A * Aᴴ).rank = A.rank
proof:
simpa only [rank_conjTranspose, conjTranspose_conjTranspose] using
    rank_conjTranspose_mul_self Aᴴ

end StarOrderedField

section LinearOrderedField

variable [Fintype m] [Field R] [LinearOrder R] [IsStrictOrderedRing R]

name: ker_mulVecLin_transpose_mul_self
statement: theorem ker_mulVecLin_transpose_mul_self (A : Matrix m n R) : LinearMap.ker (Aᵀ * A).mulVecLin = LinearMap.ker (mulVecLin A)
proof:
ext x
  simp only [LinearMap.mem_ker, mulVecLin_apply, ← mulVec_mulVec]
  constructor
  · intro h
    replace h := congr_arg (dotProduct x) h
    rwa [dotProduct_mulVec, dotProduct_zero, vecMul_transpose, dotProduct_self_eq_zero] at h
  · intro h
    rw [h, mulVec_zero]

name: rank_transpose_mul_self
statement: theorem rank_transpose_mul_self (A : Matrix m n R) : (Aᵀ * A).rank = A.rank
proof:
dsimp only [rank]
  refine add_left_injective (finrank R <| LinearMap.ker A.mulVecLin) ?_
  dsimp only
  trans finrank R { x // x ∈ LinearMap.range (mulVecLin (Aᵀ * A)) } +
    finrank R { x // x ∈ LinearMap.ker (mulVecLin (Aᵀ * A)) }
  · rw [ker_mulVecLin_transpose_mul_self]
  · simp only [LinearMap.finrank_range_add_finrank_ker]

end LinearOrderedField

@[simp]

name: separatingRight_def
statement: theorem separatingRight_def  : M.SeparatingRight ↔ (∀ w, (∀ v, v ⬝ᵥ M *ᵥ w = 0) → w = 0)
proof:
refine forall_congr' fun w ↦ ⟨fun hM hw ↦ hM ?_, fun hM hw ↦ hM ?_⟩ <;>
  convert hw

name: separatingLeft_def
statement: theorem separatingLeft_def  : M.SeparatingLeft ↔ (∀ v, (∀ w, v ⬝ᵥ M *ᵥ w = 0) → v = 0)
proof:
refine forall_congr' fun v ↦ ⟨fun hM hv ↦ hM ?_, fun hM hv ↦ hM ?_⟩ <;>
  convert hv

name: nondegenerate_of_det_ne_zero
statement: theorem nondegenerate_of_det_ne_zero (hM : M.det ≠ 0) : Nondegenerate M
proof:
refine nondegenerate_def.mpr ⟨fun v h ↦ ?_, fun w h ↦ ?_⟩
  · ext i
    specialize h (M.cramer (Pi.single i 1))
    simp_all
  · ext i
    contrapose! h
    use Pi.single i 1 ᵥ* M.adjugate
    rw [dotProduct_mulVec, vecMul_vecMul, adjugate_mul]
    simp_all [dotProduct, smul_apply, smul_eq_mul, Matrix.one_apply]

name: reps_zero_empty
statement: theorem reps_zero_empty  : reps 0 = ∅
proof:
rw [reps, Set.eq_empty_iff_forall_notMem]
  rintro A ⟨h₁, h₂, -, h₄⟩
  suffices |A.1 0 1| < 0 by linarith [abs_nonneg (A.1 0 1)]
  have := A_c_eq_zero h₁
  simp_all [h₂.ne']

noncomputable instance repsFintype (k : ℤ) : Fintype (reps k) := by
  let H := Finset.Icc (-|k|) |k|
  let H4 := Fin 2 → Fin 2 → H
  apply Fintype.ofInjective (β := H4) (f := fun M i j ↦ ⟨M.1.1 i j, reps_entries_le_m' M.2 i j⟩)
  intro M N h
  ext i j
  simpa only [Subtype.mk.injEq] using congrFun₂ h i j

@[simp]

name: S_smul_four
statement: theorem S_smul_four (A : Δ m) : S • S • S • S • A = A
proof:
simp only [smul_def, ← mul_assoc, S_mul_S_eq, neg_mul, one_mul, mul_neg, neg_neg, Subtype.coe_eta]

@[simp]

name: T_S_rel_smul
statement: theorem T_S_rel_smul (A : Δ m) : S • S • S • T • S • T • S • A = T⁻¹ • A
proof: simp_rw [← T_S_rel, ← smul_assoc]

name: circulant_injective
statement: theorem circulant_injective  : Injective (circulant : (n → α) → Matrix n n α)
proof:
intro v w h
  ext k
  rw [← circulant_col_zero_eq v, ← circulant_col_zero_eq w, h]

name: nondegenerate_iff_ker_eq_bot
statement: theorem nondegenerate_iff_ker_eq_bot  : B.Nondegenerate ↔ B.ker = ⊥
proof:
refine ⟨Nondegenerate.ker_eq_bot, fun h ↦ .ofSeparatingLeft ?_⟩
  rwa [separatingLeft_iff_ker_eq_bot]

end LeftRight

end BilinForm

end LinearMap

name: det_permutation
statement: theorem det_permutation  : det (σ.permMatrix R) = Perm.sign σ
proof:
rw [← Matrix.mul_one (σ.permMatrix R), PEquiv.toMatrix_toPEquiv_mul,
    det_permute, det_one, mul_one]

/-- The trace of a permutation matrix equals the number of fixed points. -/

name: trace_permutation
statement: theorem trace_permutation  : trace (σ.permMatrix R) = (Function.fixedPoints σ).ncard
proof:
delta trace
  simp [toPEquiv_apply, ← Set.ncard_coe_finset, Function.fixedPoints, Function.IsFixedPt]

name: permMatrix_mul
statement: theorem permMatrix_mul  : (σ * τ).permMatrix R = τ.permMatrix R * σ.permMatrix R
proof:
rw [Perm.permMatrix, Perm.mul_def, toPEquiv_trans, PEquiv.toMatrix_trans]

/-- `permMatrix` as a homomorphism. -/
@[simps]

name: mem_rowStochastic_iff_sum
statement: theorem mem_rowStochastic_iff_sum  : M ∈ rowStochastic R n ↔ (∀ i j, 0 ≤ M i j) ∧ (∀ i, ∑ j, M i j = 1)
proof:
simp [funext_iff, rowStochastic, mulVec, dotProduct]

/-- Every entry of a row stochastic matrix is nonnegative. -/

name: convex_rowStochastic
statement: theorem convex_rowStochastic  : Convex R (rowStochastic R n : Set (Matrix n n R))
proof:
intro x hx y hy a b ha hb h
  simp only [SetLike.mem_coe, mem_rowStochastic_iff_sum] at hx hy ⊢
  simp [add_nonneg, ha, hb, mul_nonneg, hx, hy, sum_add_distrib, ← mul_sum, h]

/-- Any permutation matrix is row stochastic. -/
@[simp, grind ←]

name: convex_colStochastic
statement: theorem convex_colStochastic  : Convex R (colStochastic R n : Set (Matrix n n R))
proof:
intro x hx y hy a b ha hb h
  simp only [SetLike.mem_coe, mem_colStochastic_iff_sum] at hx hy ⊢
  simp [add_nonneg, ha, hb, mul_nonneg, hx, hy, sum_add_distrib, ← mul_sum, h]

/-- Any permutation matrix is column stochastic. -/
@[simp, grind ←]

name: transpose_mem_rowStochastic_iff_mem_colStochastic
statement: theorem transpose_mem_rowStochastic_iff_mem_colStochastic  : Mᵀ ∈ rowStochastic R n ↔ M ∈ colStochastic R n
proof:
simp only [mem_colStochastic_iff_sum, mem_rowStochastic_iff_sum, transpose_apply,
    and_congr_left_iff]
  exact fun _ ↦ forall_comm

/-- The transpose of a matrix is column stochastic matrix if it is row stochastic. -/
@[grind =]

name: transpose_mem_colStochastic_iff_mem_rowStochastic
statement: theorem transpose_mem_colStochastic_iff_mem_rowStochastic  : Mᵀ ∈ colStochastic R n ↔ M ∈ rowStochastic R n
proof:
simp only [mem_colStochastic_iff_sum, mem_rowStochastic_iff_sum, transpose_apply,
    and_congr_left_iff]
  exact fun _ ↦ forall_comm

/-- Reindexing a matrix preserves row-stochasticity. -/
@[aesop safe apply]

name: matrix_bot
statement: theorem matrix_bot  : (⊥ : Ideal R).matrix n = ⊥
proof:
ext M
  simp only [mem_matrix, mem_bot]
  constructor
  · intro H; ext; apply H
  · intro H; simp [H]

@[simp]

name: matrix_top
statement: theorem matrix_top  : (⊤ : Ideal R).matrix n = ⊤
proof:
ext; simp

end Ideal

/-! ### Jacobson radicals of left ideals in a matrix ring -/

namespace Ideal
open Matrix

variable {R : Type*} [Ring R] {n : Type*} [Fintype n] [DecidableEq n]

/-- A standard basis matrix is in $J(Mₙ(I))$
as long as its one possibly non-zero entry is in $J(I)$. -/

name: single_mem_jacobson_matrix
statement: theorem single_mem_jacobson_matrix (I : Ideal R) : ∀ x ∈ I.jacobson, ∀ (i j : n), single i j x ∈ (I.matrix n).jacobson
proof:
-- Proof generalized from example 8 in
  -- https://ysharifi.wordpress.com/2022/08/16/the-jacobson-radical-basic-examples/
  simp_rw [Ideal.mem_jacobson_iff]
  intro x xIJ p q M
  have ⟨z, zMx⟩ := xIJ (M q p)
  let N : Matrix n n R := 1 - ∑ i, single i q (if i = q then 1 - z else (M i p) * x * z)
  use N
  intro i j
  obtain rfl | qj := eq_or_ne q j
  · by_cases iq : i = q
    · simp [iq, N, zMx, single, mul_apply, sum_apply, ite_and, sub_mul]
    · convert I.mul_mem_left (-M i p * x) zMx
      simp [iq, N, single, mul_apply, sum_apply, ite_and, sub_mul]
      simp [sub_add, mul_add, mul_sub, mul_assoc]
  · simp [N, qj, sum_apply, mul_apply]

/-- For any left ideal $I ≤ R$, we have $Mₙ(J(I)) ≤ J(Mₙ(I))$. -/

name: matrix_jacobson_le
statement: theorem matrix_jacobson_le (I : Ideal R) : I.jacobson.matrix n ≤ (I.matrix n).jacobson
proof:
intro M MI
  rw [matrix_eq_sum_single M]
  apply sum_mem
  intro i _
  apply sum_mem
  intro j _
  apply single_mem_jacobson_matrix I _ (MI i j)

end Ideal

/-! ### Two-sided ideals in a matrix ring -/

namespace RingCon
variable {R n : Type*}

section NonUnitalNonAssocSemiring
variable [NonUnitalNonAssocSemiring R] [Fintype n]
variable (n)

/-- The ring congruence of matrices with entries related by `c`. -/

name: jacobson_matrix_le
statement: theorem jacobson_matrix_le (I : TwoSidedIdeal R) : (I.matrix n).jacobson ≤ I.jacobson.matrix n
proof:
-- Proof generalized from example 8 in
  -- https://ysharifi.wordpress.com/2022/08/16/the-jacobson-radical-basic-examples/
  intro M Mmem p q
  simp only [zero_apply, ← mem_iff]
  rw [mem_jacobson_iff]
  replace Mmem := mul_mem_right _ _ (single q p 1) Mmem
  rw [mem_jacobson_iff] at Mmem
  intro y
  specialize Mmem (y • single p p 1)
  have ⟨N, NxMI⟩ := Mmem
  use N p p
  simpa [mul_apply, single, ite_and] using NxMI p p

/-- For any two-sided ideal $I ≤ R$, we have $J(Mₙ(I)) = Mₙ(J(I))$. -/

name: jacobson_matrix
statement: theorem jacobson_matrix (I : TwoSidedIdeal R) : (I.matrix n).jacobson = I.jacobson.matrix n
proof:
apply le_antisymm
  · apply jacobson_matrix_le
  · change asIdeal (I.matrix n).jacobson ≥ asIdeal (I.jacobson.matrix n)
    simp [asIdeal_jacobson, asIdeal_matrix, Ideal.matrix_jacobson_le]

name: replicateCol_injective
statement: theorem replicateCol_injective  : Function.Injective (replicateCol ι : (m → α) → Matrix m ι α)
proof:
inhabit ι
  exact fun _x _y h => funext fun i => congr_fun₂ h i default

@[simp] theorem replicateCol_inj [Nonempty ι] {v w : m → α} :
    replicateCol ι v = replicateCol ι w ↔ v = w :=
  replicateCol_injective.eq_iff

@[simp] theorem replicateCol_zero [Zero α] : replicateCol ι (0 : m → α) = 0 := rfl

@[simp] theorem replicateCol_eq_zero [Zero α] [Nonempty ι] (v : m → α) :
    replicateCol ι v = 0 ↔ v = 0 :=
  replicateCol_inj

@[simp]

name: replicateRow_injective
statement: theorem replicateRow_injective  : Function.Injective (replicateRow ι : (n → α) → Matrix ι n α)
proof:
inhabit ι
  exact fun _x _y h => funext fun j => congr_fun₂ h default j

@[simp] theorem replicateRow_inj [Nonempty ι] {v w : n → α} :
    replicateRow ι v = replicateRow ι w ↔ v = w :=
  replicateRow_injective.eq_iff

@[simp] theorem replicateRow_zero [Zero α] : replicateRow ι (0 : n → α) = 0 := rfl

@[simp] theorem replicateRow_eq_zero [Zero α] [Nonempty ι] (v : n → α) :
    replicateRow ι v = 0 ↔ v = 0 :=
  replicateRow_inj

@[simp]

name: transpose_replicateCol
statement: theorem transpose_replicateCol (v : m → α) : (replicateCol ι v)ᵀ = replicateRow ι v
proof:
ext
  rfl

@[simp]

name: transpose_replicateRow
statement: theorem transpose_replicateRow (v : m → α) : (replicateRow ι v)ᵀ = replicateCol ι v
proof:
ext
  rfl

@[simp]

name: updateRow_transpose
statement: theorem updateRow_transpose  : updateRow Mᵀ j c = (updateCol M j c)ᵀ
proof:
ext
  rw [transpose_apply, updateRow_apply, updateCol_apply]
  rfl

name: updateCol_transpose
statement: theorem updateCol_transpose  : updateCol Mᵀ i b = (updateRow M i b)ᵀ
proof:
ext
  rw [transpose_apply, updateRow_apply, updateCol_apply]
  rfl

name: iInf_ker_lapply_le_bot
statement: theorem iInf_ker_lapply_le_bot  : ⨅ a, ker (lapply a : (α →₀ M) →ₗ[R] M) ≤ ⊥
proof:
simp only [SetLike.le_def, mem_iInf, mem_ker, mem_bot, lapply_apply]
  exact fun a h => Finsupp.ext h

name: iSup_lsingle_range
statement: theorem iSup_lsingle_range  : ⨆ a, LinearMap.range (lsingle a : M →ₗ[R] α →₀ M) = ⊤
proof:
refine eq_top_iff.2 <| SetLike.le_def.2 fun f _ => ?_
  rw [← sum_single f]
  exact sum_mem fun a _ => Submodule.mem_iSup_of_mem a ⟨_, rfl⟩

name: span_single_eq_top
statement: theorem span_single_eq_top  : span R {single i x | (i : α) (x : M)} = ⊤
proof:
refine eq_top_iff'.mpr fun x ↦ ?_
  induction x using Finsupp.induction_linear with
  | zero => exact Submodule.zero_mem ..
  | add f g f_in g_in => exact add_mem f_in g_in
  | single a b => exact mem_span_of_mem ⟨a, b, rfl⟩

end Finsupp

open Finsupp

namespace Submodule

section Semiring

variable {R : Type*} {M : Type*} {N : Type*}
variable [Semiring R] [AddCommMonoid M] [Module R M] [AddCommMonoid N] [Module R N]

name: linearCombination_zero_apply
statement: theorem linearCombination_zero_apply (x : α →₀ R) : (linearCombination R (0 : α → M)) x = 0
proof:
simp [linearCombination_apply]

variable (α M)

@[simp]

name: linearCombination_linear_comp
statement: theorem linearCombination_linear_comp (f : M →ₗ[R] M') : linearCombination R (f ∘ v) = f ∘ₗ linearCombination R v
proof:
ext
  simp [linearCombination_apply]

name: linearCombination_surjective
statement: theorem linearCombination_surjective (h : Function.Surjective v) : Function.Surjective (linearCombination R v)
proof:
intro x
  obtain ⟨y, hy⟩ := h x
  exact ⟨single y 1, by simp [hy]⟩

name: range_linearCombination
statement: theorem range_linearCombination  : LinearMap.range (linearCombination R v) = span R (range v)
proof:
ext x
  constructor
  · intro hx
    rw [LinearMap.mem_range] at hx
    rcases hx with ⟨l, hl⟩
    rw [← hl]
    rw [linearCombination_apply]
    exact sum_mem fun i _ => Submodule.smul_mem _ _ (subset_span (mem_range_self i))
  · apply span_le.2
    intro x hx
    rcases hx with ⟨i, hi⟩
    rw [SetLike.mem_coe, LinearMap.mem_range]
    use single i 1
    simp [hi]

name: linearCombination_comp_lmapDomain
statement: theorem linearCombination_comp_lmapDomain (f : α → α') : (linearCombination R v').comp (Finsupp.lmapDomain R R f) = linearCombination R (v' ∘ f)
proof:
ext
  simp

@[simp]

name: span_eq_range_linearCombination
statement: theorem span_eq_range_linearCombination (s : Set M) : span R s = LinearMap.range (linearCombination R ((↑) : s → M))
proof: rw [range_linearCombination, Subtype.range_coe_subtype, Set.setOf_mem_eq]

name: span_image_eq_map_linearCombination
statement: theorem span_image_eq_map_linearCombination (s : Set α) : span R (v '' s) = Submodule.map (linearCombination R v) (supported R R s)
proof:
apply span_eq_of_le
  · intro x hx
    rw [Set.mem_image] at hx
    apply Exists.elim hx
    intro i hi
    exact ⟨_, Finsupp.single_mem_supported R 1 hi.1, by simp [hi.2]⟩
  · refine map_le_iff_le_comap.2 fun z hz => ?_
    have : ∀ i, z i • v i ∈ span R (v '' s) := by
      intro c
      haveI := Classical.decPred fun x => x ∈ s
      by_cases h : c ∈ s
      · exact smul_mem _ _ (subset_span (Set.mem_image_of_mem _ h))
      · simp [(Finsupp.mem_supported' R _).1 hz _ h]
    rw [mem_comap, linearCombination_apply]
    refine sum_mem ?_
    simp [this]

name: linearCombination_fin_zero
statement: theorem linearCombination_fin_zero (f : Fin 0 → M) : linearCombination R f = 0
proof:
ext i
  apply finZeroElim i

variable (α) (M) (v)

/-- `Finsupp.linearCombinationOn M v s` interprets `p : α →₀ R` as a linear combination of a
subset of the vectors in `v`, mapping it to the span of those vectors.

The subset is indicated by a set `s : Set α` of indices.
-/

name: linearCombinationOn_range
statement: theorem linearCombinationOn_range (s : Set α) : LinearMap.range (linearCombinationOn α M R v s) = ⊤
proof:
rw [linearCombinationOn, LinearMap.range_eq_map, LinearMap.map_codRestrict,
    ← LinearMap.range_le_iff_comap, range_subtype, Submodule.map_top, LinearMap.range_comp,
    range_subtype]
  exact (span_image_eq_map_linearCombination _ _).le

name: linearCombination_comp
statement: theorem linearCombination_comp (f : α' → α) : linearCombination R (v ∘ f) = (linearCombination R v).comp (lmapDomain R R f)
proof:
ext
  simp [linearCombination_apply]

name: mem_supported_support
statement: theorem mem_supported_support (p : α →₀ M) : p ∈ Finsupp.supported M R (p.support : Set α)
proof: rw [Finsupp.mem_supported]

name: supported_eq_span_single
statement: theorem supported_eq_span_single (s : Set α) : supported R R s = span R ((fun i => single i 1) '' s)
proof:
refine (span_eq_of_le _ ?_ (SetLike.le_def.2 fun l hl => ?_)).symm
  · rintro _ ⟨_, hp, rfl⟩
    exact single_mem_supported R 1 hp
  · rw [← l.sum_single]
    refine sum_mem fun i il => ?_
    rw [show single i (l i) = l i • single i 1 by simp]
    exact smul_mem _ (l i) (subset_span (mem_image_of_mem _ (hl il)))

name: restrictDom_comp_subtype
statement: theorem restrictDom_comp_subtype (s : Set α) : (restrictDom M R s).comp (Submodule.subtype _) = LinearMap.id
proof:
ext l a
  by_cases h : a ∈ s
  · simp [h]
  simpa [h] using ((mem_supported' R l.1).1 l.2 a h).symm

name: supported_union
statement: theorem supported_union (s t : Set α) : supported M R (s ∪ t) = supported M R s ⊔ supported M R t
proof: rw [Set.union_eq_iUnion, supported_iUnion, iSup_bool_eq, cond_true, cond_false]

name: supported_inter
statement: theorem supported_inter (s t : Set α) : supported M R (s ∩ t) = supported M R s ⊓ supported M R t
proof: rw [Set.inter_eq_iInter, supported_iInter, iInf_bool_eq]; rfl

name: fst_prodOfFinsuppNat
statement: theorem fst_prodOfFinsuppNat (x : ℕ →₀ P) : (prodOfFinsuppNat f x).1 = x 0
proof:
simp_rw [prodOfFinsuppNat, coe_lsum, sum, Prod.fst_sum]
  rw [Finset.sum_eq_single 0 (fun n _ hn ↦ ?_) (by simp)]
  · simp
  obtain ⟨n, rfl⟩ := n.exists_eq_succ_of_ne_zero hn
  simp [pow_succ']

name: prodOfFinsuppNat_injective
statement: theorem prodOfFinsuppNat_injective (inj : Injective f) : Injective (prodOfFinsuppNat f)
proof:
intro x y
  let s := x.support ∪ y.support
  obtain eq | ne := s.eq_empty_or_nonempty
  · simp_all [s]
  set n := s.max' ne with hn
  clear_value n; revert x y
  induction n using Nat.strong_induction_on with | h n ih =>
  intro x y s _ hn eq
  rw [← x.single_add_erase 0, ← y.single_add_erase 0]
  simp_rw [← mapDomain_comapDomain_nat_add_one, ← f.fst_prodOfFinsuppNat, eq]
  congr 2
  by_contra ne
  apply ne (ih _ _ _ rfl (inj _))
  · contrapose! ne; simp_all [-comapDomain_support]
  · simp +contextual [hn, s, ← Nat.succ_le_iff, Finset.le_max']
  simp_rw [← snd_prodOfFinsuppNat, eq]

name: linearMap_id
statement: theorem linearMap_id  : linearMap R M (_root_.id : X → X) = .id
proof:
classical
  aesop

name: rank_zero
statement: theorem rank_zero  : rank (0 : V →ₗ[K] V') = 0
proof:
rw [rank, LinearMap.range_zero, rank_bot]

variable [AddCommGroup V''] [Module K V'']

name: rank_zero_iff_forall_zero
statement: theorem rank_zero_iff_forall_zero  : Module.rank R M = 0 ↔ ∀ x : M, x = 0
proof:
simp_rw [rank_eq_zero_iff, smul_eq_zero, and_or_left, not_and_self_iff, false_or,
    exists_and_right, and_iff_right (exists_ne (0 : R))]

/-- See `rank_subsingleton` for the reason that `Nontrivial R` is needed.
Also see `rank_eq_zero_iff` for the version without `NoZeroSMulDivisor R M`. -/

name: rank_pos_iff_exists_ne_zero
statement: theorem rank_pos_iff_exists_ne_zero  : 0 < Module.rank R M ↔ ∃ x : M, x ≠ 0
proof: contrapose!; rw [nonpos_iff_eq_zero]; exact rank_zero_iff_forall_zero

name: finite_finsupp_self_iff
statement: theorem finite_finsupp_self_iff  : Module.Finite R (ι →₀ R) ↔ Subsingleton R ∨ Finite ι
proof:
simp only [finite_finsupp_iff, Finite.self, true_and, or_iff_right_iff_imp]
  exact fun _ ↦ .inr inferInstance

end Module

name: finrank_subsingleton
statement: theorem finrank_subsingleton  : finrank R M = 1
proof: rw [finrank, rank_subsingleton, map_one]

name: one_lt_rank_of_one_lt_finrank
statement: theorem one_lt_rank_of_one_lt_finrank (h : 1 < finrank R M) : 1 < Module.rank R M
proof: simpa using lt_rank_of_lt_finrank h

name: finrank_eq
statement: theorem finrank_eq (f : M ≃ₗ[R] N) : finrank R M = finrank R N
proof:
unfold finrank
  rw [← Cardinal.toNat_lift, f.lift_rank_eq, Cardinal.toNat_lift]

/-- Pushforwards of finite-dimensional submodules along a `LinearEquiv` have the same finrank. -/

name: finrank_top
statement: theorem finrank_top  : finrank R (⊤ : Submodule R M) = finrank R M
proof:
unfold finrank
  simp

namespace Algebra

/-- If `S₀ / R₀` and `S₁ / R₁` are algebras, `i : R₀ ≃+* R₁` and `j : S₀ ≃+* S₁` are
ring isomorphisms, such that `R₀ → R₁ → S₁` and `R₀ → S₀ → S₁` commute,
then the `finrank` of `S₀ / R₀` is equal to the finrank of `S₁ / R₁`. -/

name: max_aleph0_card_le_rank_fun_nat
statement: theorem max_aleph0_card_le_rank_fun_nat  : max ℵ₀ #K ≤ Module.rank K (ℕ → K)
proof:
have aleph0_le : ℵ₀ ≤ Module.rank K (ℕ → K) := (rank_finsupp_self K ℕ).symm.trans_le
    (Finsupp.lcoeFun.rank_le_of_injective <| by exact DFunLike.coe_injective)
  refine max_le aleph0_le ?_
  obtain card_K | card_K := le_or_gt #K ℵ₀
  · exact card_K.trans aleph0_le
  by_contra!
  obtain ⟨⟨ιK, bK⟩⟩ := Module.Free.exists_basis (R := K) (M := ℕ → K)
  let L := Subfield.closure (Set.range (fun i : ιK × ℕ ↦ bK i.1 i.2))
  have hLK : #L < #K := by
    refine (Subfield.cardinalMk_closure_le_max _).trans_lt
      (max_lt_iff.mpr ⟨mk_range_le.trans_lt ?_, card_K⟩)
    rwa [mk_prod, ← aleph0, lift_uzero, bK.mk_eq_rank'', mul_aleph0_eq aleph0_le]
  letI := Module.compHom K (RingHom.op L.subtype)
  obtain ⟨⟨ιL, bL⟩⟩ := Module.Free.exists_basis (R := Lᵐᵒᵖ) (M := K)
  have card_ιL : ℵ₀ ≤ #ιL := by
    contrapose! hLK
    haveI := @Fintype.ofFinite _ (lt_aleph0_iff_finite.mp hLK)
    rw [bL.repr.toEquiv.cardinal_eq, mk_finsupp_of_fintype,
        ← MulOpposite.opEquiv.cardinal_eq] at card_K ⊢
    apply power_nat_le
    contrapose! card_K
    exact (power_lt_aleph0 card_K natCast_lt_aleph0).le
  obtain ⟨e⟩ := lift_mk_le'.mp (card_ιL.trans_eq (lift_uzero #ιL).symm)
  have rep_e := bK.linearCombination_repr (bL ∘ e)
  rw [Finsupp.linearCombination_apply, Finsupp.sum] at rep_e
  set c := bK.repr (bL ∘ e)
  set s := c.support
  let f i (j : s) : L := ⟨bK j i, Subfield.subset_closure ⟨(j, i), rfl⟩⟩
  have : ¬LinearIndependent Lᵐᵒᵖ f := fun h ↦ by
    have := h.cardinal_lift_le_rank
    rw [lift_uzero, (LinearEquiv.piCongrRight fun _ ↦ MulOpposite.opLinearEquiv Lᵐᵒᵖ).rank_eq,
        rank_fun'] at this
    exact natCast_lt_aleph0.not_ge this
  obtain ⟨t, g, eq0, i, hi, hgi⟩ := not_linearIndependent_iff.mp this
  refine hgi (linearIndependent_iff'.mp (bL.linearIndependent.comp e e.injective) t g ?_ i hi)
  clear_value c s
  simp_rw [← rep_e, Finset.sum_apply, Pi.smul_apply, Finset.smul_sum]
  rw [Finset.sum_comm]
  refine Finset.sum_eq_zero fun i hi ↦ ?_
  replace eq0 := congr_arg L.subtype (congr_fun eq0 ⟨i, hi⟩)
  rw [Finset.sum_apply, map_sum] at eq0
  have : SMulCommClass Lᵐᵒᵖ K K := ⟨fun _ _ _ ↦ mul_assoc _ _ _⟩
  simp_rw [smul_comm _ (c i), ← Finset.smul_sum]
  erw [eq0, smul_zero]

variable {K}

open Function in

name: nontrivial_of_hasRankNullity
statement: theorem nontrivial_of_hasRankNullity  : Nontrivial R
proof:
refine (subsingleton_or_nontrivial R).resolve_left fun H ↦ ?_
  have := rank_quotient_add_rank (R := R) (M := PUnit) ⊥
  simp [one_add_one_eq_two] at this

attribute [local instance] nontrivial_of_hasRankNullity

name: finrank_ulift
statement: theorem finrank_ulift  : finrank R (ULift M) = finrank R M
proof:
simp_rw [finrank, rank_ulift, toNat_lift]

end ULift

section Prod

variable (R M M')
variable [Module R M₁] [Module R M']

name: rank_add_rank_le_rank_prod
statement: theorem rank_add_rank_le_rank_prod  : Module.rank R M + Module.rank R M₁ ≤ Module.rank R (M × M₁)
proof:
conv_lhs => simp only [Module.rank_def]
  rw [Cardinal.ciSup_add_ciSup _ bddAbove_of_small _ bddAbove_of_small]
  exact ciSup_le fun ⟨s, hs⟩ ↦ ciSup_le fun ⟨t, ht⟩ ↦
    (linearIndependent_inl_union_inr' hs ht).cardinal_le_rank

name: lift_rank_add_lift_rank_le_rank_prod
statement: theorem lift_rank_add_lift_rank_le_rank_prod  : lift.{v'} (Module.rank R M) + lift.{v} (Module.rank R M') ≤ Module.rank R (M × M')
proof:
rw [← rank_ulift, ← rank_ulift]
  exact (rank_add_rank_le_rank_prod R _).trans_eq
    (ULift.moduleEquiv.prodCongr ULift.moduleEquiv).rank_eq

variable {R M M'}
variable [StrongRankCondition R] [Module.Free R M] [Module.Free R M'] [Module.Free R M₁]

open Module.Free

/-- If `M` and `M'` are free, then the rank of `M × M'` is
`(Module.rank R M).lift + (Module.rank R M').lift`. -/
@[simp]

name: rank_finsupp
statement: theorem rank_finsupp (ι : Type w) : Module.rank R (ι →₀ M) = Cardinal.lift.{v} #ι * Cardinal.lift.{w} (Module.rank R M)
proof:
obtain ⟨⟨_, bs⟩⟩ := Module.Free.exists_basis (R := R) (M := M)
  rw [← bs.mk_eq_rank'', ← (Finsupp.basis fun _ : ι => bs).mk_eq_rank'', Cardinal.mk_sigma,
    Cardinal.sum_const]

name: rank_finsupp_self
statement: theorem rank_finsupp_self (ι : Type w) : Module.rank R (ι →₀ R) = Cardinal.lift.{u} #ι
proof:
simp

/-- If `R` and `ι` lie in the same universe, the rank of `(ι →₀ R)` is `# ι`. -/

name: rank_span_le
statement: theorem rank_span_le (s : Set M) : Module.rank R (span R s) ≤ #s
proof:
rw [Finsupp.span_eq_range_linearCombination, ← lift_strictMono.le_iff_le]
  refine (lift_rank_range_le _).trans ?_
  rw [rank_finsupp_self]
  simp only [lift_lift, le_refl]

name: rank_span_finset_le
statement: theorem rank_span_finset_le (s : Finset M) : Module.rank R (span R (s : Set M)) ≤ s.card
proof: simpa using rank_span_le (s : Set M)

name: subalgebra_top_rank_eq_submodule_top_rank
statement: theorem subalgebra_top_rank_eq_submodule_top_rank  : Module.rank F (⊤ : Subalgebra F E) = Module.rank F (⊤ : Submodule F E)
proof:
rw [← Algebra.top_toSubmodule]
  rfl

name: subalgebra_top_finrank_eq_submodule_top_finrank
statement: theorem subalgebra_top_finrank_eq_submodule_top_finrank  : finrank F (⊤ : Subalgebra F E) = finrank F (⊤ : Submodule F E)
proof:
rw [← Algebra.top_toSubmodule]
  rfl

name: sumQuot_inl
statement: theorem sumQuot_inl (i : m) : sumQuot bW bQ (Sum.inl i) = bW i
proof:
simp [sumQuot]

@[simp]

name: sumQuot_inr
statement: theorem sumQuot_inr (j : n) : Submodule.Quotient.mk (sumQuot bW bQ (Sum.inr j)) = bQ j
proof:
simpa only [sumQuot, Basis.coe_mk, Sum.elim_inr, Function.comp_apply, ← W.mkQ_apply]
    using Function.rightInverse_surjInv W.mkQ_surjective _

@[simp]

name: sumQuot_repr_left
statement: theorem sumQuot_repr_left (i : m) : (sumQuot bW bQ).repr (bW i) = Finsupp.single (Sum.inl i) 1
proof: rw [← Module.Basis.apply_eq_iff, sumQuot_inl]

name: mk_eq_rank
statement: theorem mk_eq_rank (v : Basis ι R M) : Cardinal.lift.{v} #ι = Cardinal.lift.{w} (Module.rank R M)
proof:
haveI := nontrivial_of_invariantBasisNumber R
  rw [← v.mk_range_eq_rank, Cardinal.mk_range_eq_of_injective v.injective]

name: rank_pos_iff_of_free
statement: theorem rank_pos_iff_of_free  : 0 < Module.rank R M ↔ Nontrivial M
proof:
refine ⟨fun h ↦ ?_, fun _ ↦ rank_pos_of_free⟩
  rw [← not_subsingleton_iff_nontrivial]
  intro h'
  simp only [rank_subsingleton', lt_self_iff_false] at h

name: rank_zero_iff_of_free
statement: theorem rank_zero_iff_of_free  : Module.rank R M = 0 ↔ Subsingleton M
proof:
rw [← not_nontrivial_iff_subsingleton, iff_not_comm,
    ← Module.rank_pos_iff_of_free (R := R), pos_iff_ne_zero]

name: finrank_eq_nat_card_basis
statement: theorem finrank_eq_nat_card_basis (h : Basis ι R M) : finrank R M = Nat.card ι
proof:
rw [Nat.card, ← toNat_lift.{v}, h.mk_eq_rank, toNat_lift, finrank]

/-- If a vector space (or module) has a finite basis, then its dimension (or rank) is equal to the
cardinality of the basis. -/

name: rank_self
statement: theorem rank_self  : Module.rank R R = 1
proof:
rw [← Cardinal.lift_inj, ← (Basis.singleton PUnit R).mk_eq_rank, Cardinal.mk_punit]

/-- A ring satisfying `StrongRankCondition` (such as a `DivisionRing`) is one-dimensional as a
module over itself. -/
@[simp]

name: rank_lt_aleph0
statement: theorem rank_lt_aleph0  : Module.rank R M < ℵ₀
proof:
simp only [Module.rank_def]
  obtain ⟨S, hS⟩ := Module.finite_def.mp ‹_›
  exact (ciSup_le' fun i => linearIndependent_le_span_finset _ i.prop S hS).trans_lt
    natCast_lt_aleph0

noncomputable instance {R M : Type*} [DivisionRing R] [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M]
    {s t : Set M} [Module.Finite R (span R t)]
    (hs : LinearIndepOn R id s) (hst : s ⊆ t) :
    Fintype (hs.extend hst) := by
  refine Classical.choice (Cardinal.lt_aleph0_iff_fintype.1 ?_)
  rw [← rank_span_set (hs.linearIndepOn_extend hst), hs.span_extend_eq_span]
  exact Module.rank_lt_aleph0 ..

/-- If `M` is finite, `finrank M = rank M`. -/
@[simp]

name: finrank_eq_rank
statement: theorem finrank_eq_rank  : ↑(finrank R M) = Module.rank R M
proof: rw [Module.finrank, cast_toNat_of_lt_aleph0 (rank_lt_aleph0 R M)]

name: strongRankCondition_iff_forall_zero_lt_finrank
statement: theorem strongRankCondition_iff_forall_zero_lt_finrank  : StrongRankCondition R ↔ ∀ n > 0, 0 < finrank R (Fin n → R)
proof:
rw [strongRankCondition_iff_forall_rank_lt_aleph0, ← not_iff_not]
  push Not
  simp_rw [finrank, Nat.le_zero, toNat_eq_zero]
  refine ⟨fun ⟨n, le⟩ ↦ ⟨n + 1, n.succ_pos, ?_⟩, fun ⟨n, pos, eq⟩ ↦ ⟨n, ?_⟩⟩
  · exact .inr <| le.trans <| LinearMap.rank_le_of_injective
      (ExtendByZero.linearMap R _) <| extend_injective (Fin.castSucc_injective n) _
  · rw [or_iff_not_imp_left, ← Ne, ← Cardinal.one_le_iff_ne_zero, one_le_rank_iff] at eq
    rw [← n.succ_pred_eq_of_pos pos] at eq ⊢
    exact eq ⟨.single R (fun _ ↦ _) 0, Pi.single_injective (M := fun _ ↦ _) _⟩

/-- If each `Rⁿ` is a Noetherian `R`-module, then `R` satisfies the strong rank condition.
Not an instance for performance reasons.

If `R` is a ring, the assumption is equivalent to `R` being a left-Noetherian ring, but this
is not necessarily the case for semirings: `ℕ` is a Noetherian semiring but `ℕ²` is not a
Noetherian `ℕ`-module. (`ℕ` does satisfy the strong rank condition.) -/

name: rank_le_one_iff
statement: theorem rank_le_one_iff  : Module.rank K V ≤ 1 ↔ ∃ v₀ : V, ∀ v, ∃ r : K, r • v₀ = v
proof:
obtain ⟨κ, b⟩ := Module.Free.exists_basis (R := K) (M := V)
  constructor
  · intro hd
    rw [← b.mk_eq_rank'', le_one_iff_subsingleton] at hd
    rcases isEmpty_or_nonempty κ with hb | ⟨⟨i⟩⟩
    · use 0
      have h' : ∀ v : V, v = 0 := by
        simpa [range_eq_empty, Submodule.eq_bot_iff] using b.span_eq.symm
      intro v
      simp [h' v]
    · use b i
      have h' : K ∙ b i = ⊤ :=
        (subsingleton_range b).eq_singleton_of_mem (mem_range_self i) ▸ b.span_eq
      intro v
      have hv : v ∈ (⊤ : Submodule K V) := mem_top
      rwa [← h', mem_span_singleton] at hv
  · rintro ⟨v₀, hv₀⟩
    have h : K ∙ v₀ = ⊤ := by
      ext
      simp [mem_span_singleton, hv₀]
    rw [← rank_top, ← h]
    refine (rank_span_le _).trans_eq ?_
    simp

/-- A vector space has dimension `1` if and only if there is a
single non-zero vector of which all vectors are multiples. -/

name: rank_eq_one_iff
statement: theorem rank_eq_one_iff  : Module.rank K V = 1 ↔ ∃ v₀ : V, v₀ ≠ 0 ∧ ∀ v, ∃ r : K, r • v₀ = v
proof:
haveI := nontrivial_of_invariantBasisNumber K
  refine ⟨fun h ↦ ?_, fun ⟨v₀, h, hv⟩ ↦ (rank_le_one_iff.2 ⟨v₀, hv⟩).antisymm ?_⟩
  · obtain ⟨v₀, hv⟩ := rank_le_one_iff.1 h.le
    refine ⟨v₀, fun hzero ↦ ?_, hv⟩
    simp_rw [hzero, smul_zero, exists_const] at hv
    haveI : Subsingleton V := .intro fun _ _ ↦ by simp_rw [← hv]
    exact one_ne_zero (h ▸ rank_subsingleton' K V)
  · by_contra H
    rw [not_le, Cardinal.lt_one_iff] at H
    obtain ⟨κ, b⟩ := Module.Free.exists_basis (R := K) (M := V)
    haveI := mk_eq_zero_iff.1 (H ▸ b.mk_eq_rank'')
    haveI := b.repr.toEquiv.subsingleton
    exact h (Subsingleton.elim _ _)

/-- A submodule has dimension at most `1` if and only if there is a
single vector in the submodule such that the submodule is contained in
its span. -/

name: rank_submodule_le_one_iff
statement: theorem rank_submodule_le_one_iff (s : Submodule K V) : Module.rank K s ≤ 1 ↔ ∃ v₀ ∈ s, s ≤ K ∙ v₀
proof:
simp_rw [rank_le_one_iff, le_span_singleton_iff]
  simp

/-- A submodule has dimension `1` if and only if there is a
single non-zero vector in the submodule such that the submodule is contained in
its span. -/

name: rank_submodule_eq_one_iff
statement: theorem rank_submodule_eq_one_iff (s : Submodule K V) : Module.rank K s = 1 ↔ ∃ v₀ ∈ s, v₀ ≠ 0 ∧ s ≤ K ∙ v₀
proof:
simp_rw [rank_eq_one_iff, le_span_singleton_iff]
  refine ⟨fun ⟨⟨v₀, hv₀⟩, H, h⟩ ↦ ⟨v₀, hv₀, fun h' ↦ by
    simp only [h', ne_eq] at H; exact H rfl, fun v hv ↦ ?_⟩,
    fun ⟨v₀, hv₀, H, h⟩ ↦ ⟨⟨v₀, hv₀⟩,
      fun h' ↦ H (by rwa [AddSubmonoid.mk_eq_zero] at h'), fun ⟨v, hv⟩ ↦ ?_⟩⟩
  · obtain ⟨r, hr⟩ := h ⟨v, hv⟩
    exact ⟨r, by rwa [Subtype.ext_iff, coe_smul] at hr⟩
  · obtain ⟨r, hr⟩ := h v hv
    exact ⟨r, by rwa [Subtype.ext_iff, coe_smul]⟩

/-- A submodule has dimension at most `1` if and only if there is a
single vector, not necessarily in the submodule, such that the
submodule is contained in its span. -/

name: eq_bot_of_rank_le_one
statement: theorem eq_bot_of_rank_le_one (h : Module.rank F S ≤ 1) : S = ⊥
proof:
nontriviality E
  obtain ⟨κ, b⟩ := Module.Free.exists_basis (R := F) (M := S)
  by_cases h1 : Module.rank F S = 1
  · refine bot_unique fun x hx ↦ Algebra.mem_bot.2 ?_
    rw [← b.mk_eq_rank'', eq_one_iff_unique, ← unique_iff_subsingleton_and_nonempty] at h1
    obtain ⟨h1⟩ := h1
    obtain ⟨y, hy⟩ := (bijective_algebraMap_of_linearEquiv (b.repr ≪≫ₗ
      Finsupp.uniqueLinearEquiv _ _ default).symm).surjective ⟨x, hx⟩
    exact ⟨y, congr(Subtype.val $(hy))⟩
  haveI := mk_eq_zero_iff.1 (b.mk_eq_rank''.symm ▸ Cardinal.lt_one_iff.1 (h.lt_of_ne h1))
  haveI := b.repr.toEquiv.subsingleton
  exact False.elim <| one_ne_zero congr(S.val $(Subsingleton.elim 1 0))

name: eq_bot_of_finrank_one
statement: theorem eq_bot_of_finrank_one (h : finrank F S = 1) : S = ⊥
proof:
refine Subalgebra.eq_bot_of_rank_le_one ?_
  rw [finrank, toNat_eq_one] at h
  rw [h]

@[simp]

name: rank_quotient_add_rank_of_divisionRing
statement: theorem rank_quotient_add_rank_of_divisionRing (p : Submodule K V) : Module.rank K (V ⧸ p) + Module.rank K p = Module.rank K V
proof:
classical
    let ⟨f⟩ := quotient_prod_linearEquiv p
    exact rank_prod'.symm.trans f.rank_eq

instance DivisionRing.hasRankNullity : HasRankNullity.{u₀} K where
  rank_quotient_add_rank := rank_quotient_add_rank_of_divisionRing
  exists_set_linearIndependent V _ _ := by
    let b := Module.Free.chooseBasis K V
    refine ⟨range b, ?_, b.linearIndependent.linearIndepOn_id⟩
    rw [← lift_injective.eq_iff, mk_range_eq_of_injective b.injective,
      Module.Free.rank_eq_card_chooseBasisIndex]

section

variable [AddCommGroup V₂] [Module K V₂]
variable [AddCommGroup V₃] [Module K V₃]

open LinearMap

/-- This is mostly an auxiliary lemma for `Submodule.rank_sup_add_rank_inf_eq`. -/

name: rank_subsingleton
statement: theorem rank_subsingleton  : Module.rank R M = 1
proof:
rw [Module.rank_def, ciSup_eq_of_forall_le_of_forall_lt_exists_gt]
  · have := Module.subsingleton R M
    simp [Set.subsingleton_of_subsingleton]
  · intro w hw
    exact ⟨⟨{0}, LinearIndepOn.of_subsingleton⟩, hw.trans_eq (Cardinal.mk_singleton _).symm⟩

name: rank_range_le
statement: theorem rank_range_le (f : M →ₗ[R] M₁) : Module.rank R (LinearMap.range f) ≤ Module.rank R M
proof: simpa using lift_rank_range_le f

name: exists_set_linearIndependent_of_isDomain
statement: theorem exists_set_linearIndependent_of_isDomain  : ∃ s : Set M, #s = Module.rank R M ∧ LinearIndepOn R id s
proof:
obtain ⟨w, hw⟩ :=
    IsLocalizedModule.linearIndependent_lift R⁰ (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap R⁰ M) <|
      Module.Free.chooseBasis (FractionRing R) (LocalizedModule R⁰ M)
        |>.linearIndependent.restrict_scalars' _
  refine ⟨Set.range w, ?_, (linearIndepOn_id_range_iff hw.injective).mpr hw⟩
  apply Cardinal.lift_injective.{max uR uM}
  rw [Cardinal.mk_range_eq_of_injective hw.injective, ← Module.Free.rank_eq_card_chooseBasisIndex,
    IsLocalization.rank_eq (FractionRing R) R⁰ le_rfl,
    IsLocalizedModule.lift_rank_eq R⁰ (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap R⁰ M) le_rfl]

/-- The **rank-nullity theorem** for commutative domains. Also see `rank_quotient_add_rank`. -/

name: lift_rank_eq_of_le_nonZeroDivisors
statement: theorem lift_rank_eq_of_le_nonZeroDivisors  : Cardinal.lift.{uM} (Module.rank T P) = Cardinal.lift.{uP} (Module.rank R M)
proof:
rw [← lift_inj.{_, max uS uT uN}, lift_lift, lift_lift]
  let ST := S ⊗[R] T
  conv_rhs => rw [← lift_lift.{uN, max uS uT uP}, ← IsLocalizedModule.lift_rank_eq p f hp,
    ← IsLocalization.rank_eq S p hp, lift_lift, ← lift_lift.{max uS uT, max uM uP},
    ← rank_baseChange (R := ST), ← lift_id'.{max uS uT, max uS uT uN} (Module.rank ..),
    lift_lift, ← lift_lift.{max uS uT uP, uM}]
  let _ : Algebra T ST := Algebra.TensorProduct.rightAlgebra
  set pT := Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid T p
  rw [← lift_lift.{max uS uT, max uM uN}, ← lift_umax.{uP},
    ← IsLocalizedModule.lift_rank_eq pT (mk T ST P 1) hpT,
    ← IsLocalization.rank_eq ST pT hpT, lift_id'.{uP, max uS uT},
    ← lift_id'.{max uS uT, max uS uT uP} (Module.rank ..), lift_lift,
    ← lift_lift.{max uS uT uN, uM}, lift_inj]
  exact LinearEquiv.lift_rank_eq <| AlgebraTensorModule.congr (.refl ST ST) bc.equiv.symm ≪≫ₗ
    AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange .. ≪≫ₗ (AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange ..).symm ≪≫ₗ
    AlgebraTensorModule.congr (.refl ..) ((isLocalizedModule_iff_isBaseChange p S f).mp ‹_›).equiv

name: finrank_eq_of_le_nonZeroDivisors
statement: theorem finrank_eq_of_le_nonZeroDivisors  : finrank T P = finrank R M
proof:
simpa using congr_arg toNat (lift_rank_eq_of_le_nonZeroDivisors S f hp hpT bc)

omit bc

name: lift_rank_eq
statement: theorem lift_rank_eq  : Cardinal.lift.{uM} (Module.rank T P) = Cardinal.lift.{uP} (Module.rank R M)
proof:
have inj := FaithfulSMul.algebraMap_injective R T
  have := inj.noZeroDivisors _ (map_zero _) (map_mul _)
  cases subsingleton_or_nontrivial R
  · have := (algebraMap R T).codomain_trivial; simp only [rank_subsingleton, lift_one]
  have := (isDomain_iff_noZeroDivisors_and_nontrivial T).mpr
    ⟨‹_›, (FaithfulSMul.algebraMap_injective R T).nontrivial⟩
  let FR := FractionRing R
  let FT := FractionRing T
  replace inj : Function.Injective (algebraMap R FT) := (IsFractionRing.injective T _).comp inj
  let g := TensorProduct.mk T FT P 1
  have : IsLocalizedModule R⁰ (TensorProduct.mk R FR FT 1) := inferInstance
  let _ : Algebra FT (FR ⊗[R] FT) := Algebra.TensorProduct.rightAlgebra
  let _ := isLocalizedModule_iff_isLocalization.mp this |>.atUnits _ _ ?_ |>.symm.isField
    (Field.toIsField FT) |>.toField
  on_goal 2 => rintro _ ⟨_, mem, rfl⟩; exact (map_ne_zero_of_mem_nonZeroDivisors _ inj mem).isUnit
  have := bc.comp_iff.2 ((isLocalizedModule_iff_isBaseChange T⁰ FT g).1 inferInstance)
  rw [← lift_inj.{_, max uT uP}, lift_lift, lift_lift, ← lift_lift.{max uT uP, uM},
    ← IsLocalizedModule.lift_rank_eq T⁰ g le_rfl, lift_lift, ← lift_lift.{uM},
    ← IsLocalization.rank_eq FT T⁰ le_rfl,
    lift_rank_eq_of_le_nonZeroDivisors FR (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap R⁰ M) le_rfl
      (map_le_nonZeroDivisors_of_injective _ inj le_rfl) this, lift_lift]

name: nonempty_oreSet_of_strongRankCondition
statement: theorem nonempty_oreSet_of_strongRankCondition  : Nonempty (OreLocalization.OreSet R⁰)
proof:
by_contra! h
  have := aleph0_le_rank_of_isEmpty_oreSet h
  rw [rank_self] at this
  exact this.not_gt one_lt_aleph0

end Ring

name: rank_le_iff_exists_linearMap
statement: theorem rank_le_iff_exists_linearMap  : Module.rank R M ≤ Module.rank R M₁ ↔ ∃ f : M →ₗ[R] M₁, Function.Injective f
proof: simp [← lift_rank_le_iff_exists_linearMap]

name: subsingleton_of_rank_zero
statement: theorem subsingleton_of_rank_zero (h : Module.rank R M = 0) : Subsingleton M
proof:
rw [← Basis.mk_eq_rank'' (Module.Free.chooseBasis R M), Cardinal.mk_eq_zero_iff] at h
  exact (Module.Free.chooseBasis R M).repr.subsingleton

/-- See `rank_lt_aleph0` for the inverse direction without `Module.Free R M`. -/

name: rank_lt_aleph0_iff
statement: theorem rank_lt_aleph0_iff  : Module.rank R M < ℵ₀ ↔ Module.Finite R M
proof:
rw [Free.rank_eq_card_chooseBasisIndex, mk_lt_aleph0_iff]
  exact ⟨fun h ↦ Finite.of_basis (Free.chooseBasis R M),
    fun I ↦ Finite.of_fintype (Free.ChooseBasisIndex R M)⟩

name: finrank_of_not_finite
statement: theorem finrank_of_not_finite (h : ¬Module.Finite R M) : finrank R M = 0
proof:
rw [finrank, toNat_eq_zero, ← not_lt, Module.rank_lt_aleph0_iff]
  exact .inr h

name: finite_of_finrank_pos
statement: theorem finite_of_finrank_pos (h : 0 < finrank R M) : Module.Finite R M
proof:
contrapose h
  simp [finrank_of_not_finite h]

name: nonempty_linearEquiv_of_finrank_eq_one
statement: theorem nonempty_linearEquiv_of_finrank_eq_one (d1 : Module.finrank R M = 1) : Nonempty (R ≃ₗ[R] M)
proof:
let ⟨ι, b⟩ := (Module.Free.exists_basis R M).some
  have : Unique ι := (b.nonempty_unique_index_of_finrank_eq_one d1).some
  exact ⟨((b.equivFun).trans (LinearEquiv.funUnique ι R R)).symm⟩

@[simp]

name: span_insert_zero
statement: theorem span_insert_zero  : span R (insert (0 : M) s) = span R s
proof:
refine le_antisymm ?_ (Submodule.span_mono (Set.subset_insert 0 s))
  rw [span_le, Set.insert_subset_iff]
  exact ⟨by simp only [SetLike.mem_coe, Submodule.zero_mem], Submodule.subset_span⟩

@[simp]

name: span_sdiff_singleton_zero
statement: theorem span_sdiff_singleton_zero  : span R (s \ {0}) = span R s
proof:
by_cases h : 0 ∈ s
  · rw [← span_insert_zero, show insert 0 (s \ {0}) = s by simp [h]]
  · simp [h]

name: span_nat_eq_addSubmonoidClosure
statement: theorem span_nat_eq_addSubmonoidClosure (s : Set M) : (span ℕ s).toAddSubmonoid = AddSubmonoid.closure s
proof:
refine Eq.symm (AddSubmonoid.closure_eq_of_le subset_span ?_)
  apply (OrderIso.to_galoisConnection (AddSubmonoid.toNatSubmodule (M := M)).symm).l_le
     (a := span ℕ s) (b := AddSubmonoid.closure s)
  rw [span_le]
  exact AddSubmonoid.subset_closure

@[simp]

name: span_nat_eq
statement: theorem span_nat_eq (s : AddSubmonoid M) : (span ℕ (s : Set M)).toAddSubmonoid = s
proof: rw [span_nat_eq_addSubmonoidClosure, s.closure_eq]

name: span_eq_iSup_of_singleton_spans
statement: theorem span_eq_iSup_of_singleton_spans (s : Set M) : span R s = ⨆ x ∈ s, R ∙ x
proof: simp only [← span_iUnion, Set.biUnion_of_singleton s]

name: codisjoint_iff_exists_add_eq
statement: theorem codisjoint_iff_exists_add_eq  : Codisjoint p p' ↔ ∀ z, ∃ x y, x ∈ p ∧ y ∈ p' ∧ x + y = z
proof:
rw [codisjoint_iff, eq_top_iff']
  exact forall_congr' (fun z => mem_sup.trans <| by simp)

variable (p p')

name: coe_sup
statement: theorem coe_sup  : ↑(p ⊔ p') = (p + p' : Set M)
proof:
ext
  rw [SetLike.mem_coe, mem_sup, Set.mem_add]
  simp

name: sup_toAddSubmonoid
statement: theorem sup_toAddSubmonoid  : (p ⊔ p').toAddSubmonoid = p.toAddSubmonoid ⊔ p'.toAddSubmonoid
proof:
ext x
  rw [mem_toAddSubmonoid, mem_sup, AddSubmonoid.mem_sup]
  rfl

name: sup_eq_top_iff
statement: theorem sup_eq_top_iff  : p ⊔ p' = ⊤ ↔ ∀ m : M, ∃ u ∈ p, ∃ v ∈ p', m = u + v
proof:
rw [eq_top_iff']
  refine forall_congr' fun m ↦ ?_
  rw [mem_sup]
  tauto

variable {P : M → Prop}

@[simp high]

name: forall_mem_sup
statement: theorem forall_mem_sup  : (∀ x ∈ p ⊔ p', P x) ↔ (∀ x₁ ∈ p, ∀ x₂ ∈ p', P (x₁ + x₂))
proof:
simp [mem_sup]
  aesop

@[simp high]

name: exists_mem_sup
statement: theorem exists_mem_sup  : (∃ x ∈ p ⊔ p', P x) ↔ (∃ x₁ ∈ p, ∃ x₂ ∈ p', P (x₁ + x₂))
proof:
simp [mem_sup]

end

@[simp]

name: span_singleton_eq_top_iff
statement: theorem span_singleton_eq_top_iff (x : M) : R ∙ x = ⊤ ↔ ∀ v, ∃ r : R, r • x = v
proof:
rw [eq_top_iff, le_span_singleton_iff]
  tauto

@[simp]

name: span_zero_singleton
statement: theorem span_zero_singleton  : R ∙ (0 : M) = ⊥
proof:
ext
  simp [mem_span_singleton, eq_comm]

name: submodule_eq_sSup_le_nonzero_spans
statement: theorem submodule_eq_sSup_le_nonzero_spans (p : Submodule R M) : p = sSup { T : Submodule R M | ∃ m ∈ p, m ≠ 0 ∧ T = span R {m} }
proof:
let S := { T : Submodule R M | ∃ m ∈ p, m ≠ 0 ∧ T = span R {m} }
  apply le_antisymm
  · intro m hm
    by_cases h : m = 0
    · rw [h]
      simp
    · exact @le_sSup _ _ S _ ⟨m, ⟨hm, ⟨h, rfl⟩⟩⟩ m (mem_span_singleton_self m)
  · rw [sSup_le_iff]
    rintro S ⟨_, ⟨_, ⟨_, rfl⟩⟩⟩
    rwa [span_singleton_le_iff_mem]

name: sup_toAddSubgroup
statement: theorem sup_toAddSubgroup (p p' : Submodule R M) : (p ⊔ p').toAddSubgroup = p.toAddSubgroup ⊔ p'.toAddSubgroup
proof:
ext x
  rw [mem_toAddSubgroup, mem_sup, AddSubgroup.mem_sup]
  rfl

name: injective_inclusionSpan
statement: theorem injective_inclusionSpan  : Injective (p.inclusionSpan S)
proof:
intro x y hxy
  rw [Subtype.ext_iff] at hxy
  simpa using hxy

name: span_range_inclusionSpan
statement: theorem span_range_inclusionSpan  : span S (range <| p.inclusionSpan S) = ⊤
proof:
have : (span S (p : Set M)).subtype '' range (inclusionSpan S p) = p := by
    ext; simpa [Subtype.ext_iff] using fun h ↦ subset_span h
  apply map_injective_of_injective (span S (p : Set M)).injective_subtype
  rw [map_subtype_top, map_span, this]

variable (R s)

/-- If `R` is "smaller" ring than `S` then the span by `R` is smaller than the span by `S`. -/

name: singleton_span_isCompactElement
statement: theorem singleton_span_isCompactElement (x : M) : IsCompactElement (span R {x} : Submodule R M)
proof:
rw [CompleteLattice.isCompactElement_iff_le_of_directed_sSup_le]
  intro d hemp hdir hsup
  have : x ∈ (sSup d) := (SetLike.le_def.mp hsup) (mem_span_singleton_self x)
  obtain ⟨y, ⟨hyd, hxy⟩⟩ := (mem_sSup_of_directed hemp hdir).mp this
  exact ⟨y, ⟨hyd, by simpa only [span_le, singleton_subset_iff] ⟩⟩

/-- The span of a finite subset is compact in the lattice of submodules. -/

name: finset_span_isCompactElement
statement: theorem finset_span_isCompactElement (S : Finset M) : IsCompactElement (span R S : Submodule R M)
proof:
rw [span_eq_iSup_of_singleton_spans]
  simp only [Finset.mem_coe]
  rw [← Finset.sup_eq_iSup]
  exact
    CompleteLattice.isCompactElement_finsetSup S fun x _ => singleton_span_isCompactElement x

/-- The span of a finite subset is compact in the lattice of submodules. -/

name: prod_sup_prod
statement: theorem prod_sup_prod  : prod p q₁ ⊔ prod p' q₁' = prod (p ⊔ p') (q₁ ⊔ q₁')
proof:
refine le_antisymm
    (sup_le (prod_mono le_sup_left le_sup_left) (prod_mono le_sup_right le_sup_right)) ?_
  simp only [SetLike.le_def, mem_prod, and_imp, Prod.forall]; intro xx yy hxx hyy
  rcases mem_sup.1 hxx with ⟨x, hx, x', hx', rfl⟩
  rcases mem_sup.1 hyy with ⟨y, hy, y', hy', rfl⟩
  exact mem_sup.2 ⟨(x, y), ⟨hx, hy⟩, (x', y'), ⟨hx', hy'⟩, rfl⟩

/-- If a bilinear map takes values in a submodule along two sets, then the same is true along
the span of these sets. -/

name: span_neg_eq_neg
statement: theorem span_neg_eq_neg (s : Set M) : span R (-s) = -span R s
proof:
apply le_antisymm
  · rw [span_le, coe_set_neg, ← Set.neg_subset, neg_neg]
    exact subset_span
  · rw [neg_le, span_le, coe_set_neg, ← Set.neg_subset]
    exact subset_span

variable {R M : Type*} [Ring R] [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M]

name: disjoint_span_singleton
statement: theorem disjoint_span_singleton  : Disjoint s (K ∙ x) ↔ x ∈ s → x = 0
proof:
simpa +contextual [disjoint_span_singleton'', or_iff_not_imp_left, forall_comm (β := ¬_),
    s.smul_mem_iff] using ⟨fun h ↦ h _ one_ne_zero, fun h _ _ ↦ h⟩

name: disjoint_span_singleton_of_notMem
statement: theorem disjoint_span_singleton_of_notMem (hx : x ∉ s) : Disjoint s (K ∙ x)
proof: simp [disjoint_span_singleton, hx]

name: toSpanSingleton_zero
statement: theorem toSpanSingleton_zero  : toSpanSingleton R M 0 = 0
proof:
ext
  simp

name: toSpanSingleton_add
statement: theorem toSpanSingleton_add (x y : M) : toSpanSingleton R M (x + y) = toSpanSingleton R M x + toSpanSingleton R M y
proof: ext; simp

name: coord_self
statement: theorem coord_self  : (coord R M x h) (⟨x, Submodule.mem_span_singleton_self x⟩ : R ∙ x) = 1
proof: rw [← toSpanNonzeroSingleton_one R M x h, LinearEquiv.symm_apply_apply]

name: surjective_tensorToSpan
statement: theorem surjective_tensorToSpan  : Surjective (p.tensorToSpan A)
proof:
intro v
  obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := (Finsupp.mem_span_iff_linearCombination _ _ _).mp v.property
  use f.sum fun x a ↦ a ⊗ₜ x
  rw [map_finsuppSum, Subtype.ext_iff, ← Submodule.subtype_apply, map_finsuppSum]
  simpa using hf

variable [Algebra.IsEpi R A] [Module.Flat R A]

open Module.Flat LinearMap in

name: injective_tensorToSpan
statement: theorem injective_tensorToSpan  : Injective (p.tensorToSpan A)
proof:
let f : A ⊗[R] (span A (p : Set M)) →ₗ[A] span A (p : Set M) :=
    AlgebraTensorModule.lift <| (restrictScalarsₗ R A _ _ A) ∘ₗ lsmul A (span A (p : Set M))
  let g : A ⊗[R] p →ₗ[R] A ⊗[R] span A (p : Set M) := (p.inclusionSpan A).lTensor A
  have hf : Injective f := Algebra.injective_lift_lsmul R A _
  have hg : Injective g := lTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap _ (p.injective_inclusionSpan A)
  have : p.tensorToSpan A = f.restrictScalars R ∘ₗ g := by ext; simp [tensorToSpan, f, g]
  rw [← LinearMap.coe_restrictScalars R, this, coe_comp]
  exact hf.comp hg

/-- If `A` is a flat epi `R`-algebra and `p` is an `R`-submodule of an `A`-module `M` then the
natural surjection from `A ⊗[R] p` to `span A p` is an equivalence. -/
noncomputable def tensorEquivSpan : A ⊗[R] p ≃ₗ[A] span A (p : Set M) :=
  .ofBijective (p.tensorToSpan A) ⟨p.injective_tensorToSpan A, p.surjective_tensorToSpan A⟩

@[simp] lemma tensorEquivSpan_apply_tmul (a : A) (x : p) :
    p.tensorEquivSpan A (a ⊗ₜ x) = a • (x : M) :=
  rfl

variable (R) in
/-- If `A` is a flat epi `R`-algebra and `s` is a subset of an `A`-module `M` then the natural
surjection from `A ⊗[R] span R s` to `span A s` is an equivalence. -/
noncomputable def tensorSpanEquivSpan (s : Set M) : A ⊗[R] span R s ≃ₗ[A] span A s :=
  ((span R s).tensorEquivSpan A).trans <| .ofEq _ _ <| span_span_of_tower R A s

@[simp] lemma coe_tensorSpanEquivSpan_apply_tmul {s : Set M} (a : A) (x : span R s) :
    tensorSpanEquivSpan R A s (a ⊗ₜ x) = a • (x : M) :=
  rfl

end CommSemiring

section CommRing

open Module

variable [CommRing R] [CommRing A] [Nontrivial A]
  [Algebra R A] [Algebra.IsEpi R A] [Module.Flat R A]
  [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [Module A M] [IsScalarTower R A M]
  (p : Submodule R M) [Free R p] [Module.Finite R p]

@[simp] lemma finrank_span_eq_finrank :
    finrank A (span A (p : Set M)) = finrank R p := by
  rcases subsingleton_or_nontrivial R; · simp [Algebra.subsingleton R A]
  let ι := Free.ChooseBasisIndex R p
  let b₁ : Basis ι R p := Free.chooseBasis R p
  let b₂ : Basis ι A (span A (p : Set M)) := (b₁.baseChange A).map <| p.tensorEquivSpan A
  rw [finrank_eq_card_basis b₁, finrank_eq_card_basis b₂]

variable (R) in

name: finrank_maxGenEigenspace_zero_eq
statement: theorem finrank_maxGenEigenspace_zero_eq (φ : Module.End K M) : finrank K (φ.maxGenEigenspace 0) = natTrailingDegree (φ.charpoly)
proof:
set V := φ.maxGenEigenspace 0
  have hV : V = ⨆ (n : ℕ), ker (φ ^ n) := by
    simp [V, ← Module.End.iSup_genEigenspace_eq, Module.End.genEigenspace_nat]
  let W := ⨅ (n : ℕ), LinearMap.range (φ ^ n)
  have hVW : IsCompl V W := by
    rw [hV]
    exact LinearMap.isCompl_iSup_ker_pow_iInf_range_pow φ
  have hφV : ∀ x ∈ V, φ x ∈ V := by
    simp only [V, Module.End.mem_maxGenEigenspace, zero_smul, sub_zero,
      forall_exists_index]
    intro x n hx
    use n
    rw [← Module.End.mul_apply, ← pow_succ, pow_succ', Module.End.mul_apply, hx, map_zero]
  have hφW : ∀ x ∈ W, φ x ∈ W := by
    simp only [W, Submodule.mem_iInf, mem_range]
    intro x H n
    obtain ⟨y, rfl⟩ := H n
    use φ y
    rw [← Module.End.mul_apply, ← pow_succ, pow_succ', Module.End.mul_apply]
  let F := φ.restrict hφV
  let G := φ.restrict hφW
  let ψ := F.prodMap G
  let e := Submodule.prodEquivOfIsCompl V W hVW
  let bV := chooseBasis K V
  let bW := chooseBasis K W
  let b := bV.prod bW
  have hψ : ψ = e.symm.conj φ := by
    apply b.ext
    simp only [Basis.prod_apply, coe_inl, coe_inr, prodMap_apply, LinearEquiv.conj_apply,
      LinearEquiv.symm_symm, Submodule.coe_prodEquivOfIsCompl, coe_comp, LinearEquiv.coe_coe,
      Function.comp_apply, coprod_apply, Submodule.coe_subtype, map_add, Sum.forall, Sum.elim_inl,
      map_zero, ZeroMemClass.coe_zero, add_zero, LinearEquiv.eq_symm_apply, and_self,
      Submodule.coe_prodEquivOfIsCompl', coe_restrict_apply, implies_true, Sum.elim_inr, zero_add,
      e, V, W, ψ, F, G, b]
  rw [← e.symm.charpoly_conj φ, ← hψ, charpoly_prodMap,
    natTrailingDegree_mul (charpoly_monic _).ne_zero (charpoly_monic _).ne_zero]
  have hG : natTrailingDegree (charpoly G) = 0 := by
    apply Polynomial.natTrailingDegree_eq_zero_of_constantCoeff_ne_zero
    apply ((not_hasEigenvalue_zero_tfae G).out 2 5).mpr
    intro x hx
    apply Subtype.ext
    suffices x.1 ∈ V ⊓ W by rwa [hVW.inf_eq_bot, Submodule.mem_bot] at this
    suffices x.1 ∈ V from ⟨this, x.2⟩
    simp only [Module.End.mem_maxGenEigenspace, zero_smul, sub_zero, V]
    use 1
    rw [pow_one]
    rwa [Subtype.ext_iff] at hx
  rw [hG, add_zero, eq_comm]
  apply ((charpoly_nilpotent_tfae F).out 2 3).mp
  simp only [Subtype.forall, Module.End.mem_maxGenEigenspace, zero_smul, sub_zero, V, F]
  rintro x ⟨n, hx⟩
  use n
  apply Subtype.ext
  rw [ZeroMemClass.coe_zero]
  refine .trans ?_ hx
  generalize_proofs h'
  clear hx
  induction n <;> simp [pow_succ', *]

name: aeval_apply_of_hasEigenvector
statement: theorem aeval_apply_of_hasEigenvector (h : f.HasEigenvector μ x) : aeval f p x = p.eval μ • x
proof:
refine p.induction_on ?_ ?_ ?_
  · intro a; simp [Module.algebraMap_end_apply]
  · intro p q hp hq; simp [hp, hq, add_smul]
  · intro n a hna
    rw [mul_comm, pow_succ', mul_assoc, map_mul, Module.End.mul_apply, mul_comm, hna]
    simp only [mem_eigenspace_iff.1 h.1, smul_smul, aeval_X, eval_mul, eval_C, eval_pow, eval_X,
      map_smulₛₗ, RingHom.id_apply, mul_comm]

/-- A variant of `Module.End.aeval_apply_of_hasEigenvector` which does not require `x ≠ 0`. -/

name: aeval_apply_of_mem_apply_eq_smul
statement: theorem aeval_apply_of_mem_apply_eq_smul (hx : f x = μ • x) : aeval f p x = p.eval μ • x
proof:
rcases eq_or_ne x 0 with rfl | hne
  · simp
  · exact aeval_apply_of_hasEigenvector ⟨mem_eigenspace_iff.mpr hx, hne⟩

name: finite_hasEigenvalue
statement: theorem finite_hasEigenvalue  : Set.Finite f.HasEigenvalue
proof:
have h : minpoly R f ≠ 0 := minpoly.ne_zero (Algebra.IsIntegral.isIntegral (R := R) f)
  convert (minpoly R f).rootSet_finite R
  ext μ
  change f.HasEigenvalue μ ↔ _
  rw [hasEigenvalue_iff_isRoot, mem_rootSet_of_ne h, IsRoot, coe_aeval_eq_eval]

/-- An endomorphism of a finite-dimensional vector space has finitely many eigenvalues. -/
noncomputable instance : Fintype f.Eigenvalues :=
  Set.Finite.fintype f.finite_hasEigenvalue

end IsDomain

end CommRing

section Field

variable {K : Type v} {V : Type w} [Field K] [AddCommGroup V] [Module K V]

name: iSup_eigenspace_toLin_diagonal_eq_top
statement: theorem iSup_eigenspace_toLin_diagonal_eq_top  : ⨆ μ, eigenspace ((diagonal d).toLin b b) μ = ⊤
proof:
refine (Submodule.eq_top_iff_forall_basis_mem b).mpr fun j ↦ ?_
  exact Submodule.mem_iSup_of_mem (d j) <| by simp [diagonal_apply]

@[simp]

name: maxGenEigenspace_toLin_diagonal_eq_eigenspace
statement: theorem maxGenEigenspace_toLin_diagonal_eq_eigenspace  : maxGenEigenspace ((diagonal d).toLin b b) μ = eigenspace ((diagonal d).toLin b b) μ
proof:
refine le_antisymm (fun x hx ↦ ?_) eigenspace_le_maxGenEigenspace
  obtain ⟨k, hk⟩ := (mem_maxGenEigenspace _ _ _).mp hx
  replace hk (j : n) : b.repr x j = 0 ∨ d j = μ ∧ k ≠ 0 := by
    have aux : (diagonal d).toLin b b - μ • 1 = (diagonal (d - μ • 1)).toLin b b := by
      rw [Pi.sub_def, ← diagonal_sub]; simp [one_eq_id]
    rw [aux, ← toLin_pow, diagonal_pow, toLin_apply_eq_zero_iff] at hk
    simpa [mulVec_eq_sum, diagonal_apply, sub_eq_zero] using hk j
  have aux (j : n) : (b.repr x j * d j) • b j = μ • (b.repr x j • b j) := by
    rcases hk j with hj | hj
    · simp [hj]
    · rw [← hj.1, mul_comm, mul_smul]
  simp [toLin_apply, mulVec_eq_sum, diagonal_apply, aux, ← Finset.smul_sum]

@[simp]

name: eigenspace_zero
statement: theorem eigenspace_zero (f : End R M) : f.eigenspace 0 = LinearMap.ker f
proof:
simp only [eigenspace, ← Nat.cast_one (R := ℕ∞), genEigenspace_zero_nat, pow_one]

/-- A nonzero element of an eigenspace is an eigenvector. (Def 5.8 of [axler2024]) -/
abbrev HasEigenvector (f : End R M) (μ : R) (x : M) : Prop :=
  HasUnifEigenvector f μ 1 x

name: alternatizeUncurryFin_add
statement: theorem alternatizeUncurryFin_add (f g : M →ₗ[R] M [⋀^Fin n]→ₗ[R] N) : alternatizeUncurryFin (f + g) = alternatizeUncurryFin f + alternatizeUncurryFin g
proof:
ext
  simp [alternatizeUncurryFin_apply, Finset.sum_add_distrib]

@[simp]

name: range_mkOfPoint_points
statement: theorem range_mkOfPoint_points (p : P) : Set.range (mkOfPoint k p).points = {p}
proof:
simp

variable {k}

/-- Two simplices are equal if they have the same points. -/
@[ext]

name: interior_eq_empty
statement: theorem interior_eq_empty (s : Simplex k P 0) : s.interior = ∅
proof:
ext p
  simp only [Simplex.interior, Simplex.setInterior, Nat.reduceAdd, univ_unique, Fin.default_eq_zero,
    Fin.isValue, sum_singleton, Set.mem_Ioo, Set.mem_setOf_eq, Set.mem_empty_iff_false, iff_false,
    not_exists, not_and]
  intro w h hi
  simpa [h] using hi 0

@[simp] lemma closedInterior_eq_singleton [ZeroLEOneClass k] (s : Simplex k P 0) :
    s.closedInterior = {s.points 0} := by
  ext p
  simp only [Simplex.closedInterior, Simplex.setInterior, Nat.reduceAdd, univ_unique,
    Fin.default_eq_zero, Fin.isValue, sum_singleton, Set.mem_Icc, Set.mem_setOf_eq,
    Set.mem_singleton_iff]
  constructor
  · rintro ⟨w, h0, hi, rfl⟩
    simp [affineCombination_apply, h0]
  · rintro rfl
    exact ⟨1, by simp [affineCombination_apply]⟩

omit [PartialOrder k] in

name: vectorSpan_empty
statement: theorem vectorSpan_empty  : vectorSpan k (∅ : Set P) = (⊥ : Submodule k V)
proof:
rw [vectorSpan_def, vsub_empty, Submodule.span_empty]

/-- The `vectorSpan` of a single point is `⊥`. -/
@[simp]

name: spanPoints_nonempty
statement: theorem spanPoints_nonempty (s : Set P) : (spanPoints k s).Nonempty ↔ s.Nonempty
proof:
constructor
  · contrapose
    rw [Set.not_nonempty_iff_eq_empty, Set.not_nonempty_iff_eq_empty]
    intro h
    simp [h, spanPoints]
  · exact fun h => h.mono (subset_spanPoints _ _)

/-- Adding a point in the affine span and a vector in the spanning submodule produces a point in the
affine span. -/

name: toAffineSubspace_direction
statement: theorem toAffineSubspace_direction (s : Submodule k V) : s.toAffineSubspace.direction = s
proof:
ext x; simp [← s.toAffineSubspace.vadd_mem_iff_mem_direction _ s.zero_mem]

end Submodule

section affineSpan

variable (k : Type*) {V : Type*} {P : Type*} [Ring k] [AddCommGroup V] [Module k V]
  [AffineSpace V P]

/-- The affine span of a set of points is the smallest affine subspace containing those points.
(Actually defined here in terms of spans in modules.) -/

name: direction_affineSpan
statement: theorem direction_affineSpan (s : Set P) : (affineSpan k s).direction = vectorSpan k s
proof:
apply le_antisymm
  · refine Submodule.span_le.2 ?_
    rintro v ⟨p₁, ⟨p₂, hp₂, v₁, hv₁, hp₁⟩, p₃, ⟨p₄, hp₄, v₂, hv₂, hp₃⟩, rfl⟩
    simp only [SetLike.mem_coe]
