      simp only [transpose_apply, isSStronglyConnected_iff, not_and, not_forall, not_exists,
        not_lt, nonpos_iff_eq_zero]
      intro a; simp_all only [implies_true, not_true_eq_false]
    have : ¬ A.IsIrreducible := by
      rw [isIrreducible_iff]; simp_all only [not_forall, not_le, isSStronglyConnected_iff,
      not_and, not_exists, not_lt, nonpos_iff_eq_zero, isEmpty_Prop, IsEmpty.forall_iff]
    simp_all only [not_forall, not_le]

end Matrix

name: card_GL_field
statement: theorem card_GL_field  : Nat.card (GL (Fin n) 𝔽) = ∏ i : (Fin n), (q ^ n - q ^ (i : ℕ))
proof:
rw [Nat.card_congr (equiv_GL_linearindependent n), card_linearIndependent,
    Module.finrank_fintype_fun_eq_card, Fintype.card_fin]
  simp only [Module.finrank_fintype_fun_eq_card, Fintype.card_fin, le_refl]

end field

end Matrix

name: isParabolic_conj_iff
statement: theorem isParabolic_conj_iff  : (g.val * m * g.val⁻¹).IsParabolic ↔ IsParabolic m
proof:
simp_rw [IsParabolic, discr_conj, Set.mem_range, ← Matrix.coe_units_inv,
    Units.eq_mul_inv_iff_mul_eq, scalar_apply, ← smul_eq_diagonal_mul, smul_eq_mul_diagonal,
    Units.mul_right_inj]

@[simp] lemma isParabolic_conj'_iff : (g.val⁻¹ * m * g.val).IsParabolic ↔ m.IsParabolic := by
  simpa using isParabolic_conj_iff g⁻¹

name: sub_scalar_sq_eq_discr
statement: theorem sub_scalar_sq_eq_discr  : (m - scalar _ (m.trace / 2)) ^ 2 = scalar _ (m.discr / 4)
proof:
simp only [scalar_apply, trace_fin_two, discr_fin_two, trace_fin_two,
    det_fin_two, sq, (by norm_num : (4 : K) = 2 * 2)]
  ext i j
  fin_cases i <;>
  fin_cases j <;>
  · simp [Matrix.mul_apply]
    field

variable (m) in
/-- The unique eigenvalue of a parabolic matrix (junk if `m` is not parabolic). -/

name: isParabolic_iff_exists
statement: theorem isParabolic_iff_exists  : m.IsParabolic ↔ ∃ a n, m = scalar _ a + n ∧ n ≠ 0 ∧ n ^ 2 = 0
proof:
constructor
  · exact fun hm ↦ ⟨_, _, (add_sub_cancel ..).symm, sub_ne_zero.mpr fun h ↦ hm.1 ⟨_, h.symm⟩,
      hm.sub_eigenvalue_sq_eq_zero⟩
  · rintro ⟨a, n, hm, hn0, hnsq⟩
    constructor
    · refine fun ⟨b, hb⟩ ↦ hn0 ?_
      rw [← sub_eq_iff_eq_add'] at hm
      simpa only [← hm, ← hb, ← map_sub, ← map_pow, ← map_zero (scalar (Fin 2)), scalar_inj,
        sq_eq_zero_iff] using hnsq
    · suffices scalar (Fin 2) (m.discr / 4) = 0 by
        rw [← map_zero (scalar (Fin 2)), scalar_inj, div_eq_zero_iff] at this
        have : (4 : K) ≠ 0 := by simpa [show (4 : K) = 2 ^ 2 by norm_num] using NeZero.ne _
        tauto
      rw [← sub_scalar_sq_eq_discr, hm, trace_add, scalar_apply, trace_diagonal]
      simp [mul_div_cancel_left₀ _ (NeZero.ne (2 : K)),
        (Matrix.isNilpotent_trace_of_isNilpotent ⟨2, hnsq⟩).eq_zero, hnsq]

end Field

section Preorder

variable {R : Type*} [CommRing R] [Preorder R] (m : Matrix (Fin 2) (Fin 2) R) (g : GL (Fin 2) R)

/-- A `2 × 2` matrix is *hyperbolic* if its discriminant is strictly positive. -/

name: isHyperbolic_conj_iff
statement: theorem isHyperbolic_conj_iff  : (g.val * m * g.val⁻¹).IsHyperbolic ↔ m.IsHyperbolic
proof: simp [IsHyperbolic]

name: isElliptic_conj_iff
statement: theorem isElliptic_conj_iff  : (g.val * m * g.val⁻¹).IsElliptic ↔ m.IsElliptic
proof: simp [IsElliptic]

name: isHyperbolic_neg_iff
statement: theorem isHyperbolic_neg_iff  : (-m).IsHyperbolic ↔ m.IsHyperbolic
proof:
simp [IsHyperbolic, discr_fin_two, det_neg]

protected alias ⟨IsHyperbolic.of_neg, IsHyperbolic.neg⟩ := isHyperbolic_neg_iff

@[simp]

name: isElliptic_neg_iff
statement: theorem isElliptic_neg_iff  : (-m).IsElliptic ↔ m.IsElliptic
proof:
simp [IsElliptic, discr_fin_two, det_neg]

protected alias ⟨IsElliptic.of_neg, IsElliptic.neg⟩ := isElliptic_neg_iff

end Preorder

section LinearOrder

variable {R : Type*} [CommRing R] [LinearOrder R] [IsOrderedRing R] {m : Matrix (Fin 2) (Fin 2) R}

name: det_scalar
statement: theorem det_scalar (u : Rˣ) : det (scalar n u) = u ^ Fintype.card n
proof:
ext
  simp

/-- The groups `GL n R` (notation for `Matrix.GeneralLinearGroup n R`) and
`LinearMap.GeneralLinearGroup R (n → R)` are multiplicatively equivalent -/

name: map_one
statement: theorem map_one  : map f (1 : GL n R) = 1
proof:
simp only [map_one]

protected lemma map_mul (g h : GL n R) : map f (g * h) = map f g * map f h := by
  simp only [map_mul]

protected lemma map_inv (g : GL n R) : map f g⁻¹ = (map f g)⁻¹ := by
  simp only [map_inv]

protected lemma map_det (g : GL n R) : Matrix.GeneralLinearGroup.det (map f g) =
    Units.map f (Matrix.GeneralLinearGroup.det g) := by
  ext
  simp only [map,
    Matrix.GeneralLinearGroup.val_det_apply, Units.coe_map, MonoidHom.coe_coe]
  exact Eq.symm (RingHom.map_det f g.1)

name: map_mul_map_inv
statement: theorem map_mul_map_inv (g : GL n R) : map f g * map f g⁻¹ = 1
proof: simp only [map_inv, mul_inv_cancel]

name: map_inv_mul_map
statement: theorem map_inv_mul_map (g : GL n R) : map f g⁻¹ * map f g = 1
proof:
simp only [map_inv, inv_mul_cancel]

@[simp]

name: coe_map_mul_map_inv
statement: theorem coe_map_mul_map_inv (g : GL n R) : g.val.map f * g.val⁻¹.map f = 1
proof:
rw [← Matrix.map_mul]
  simp only [isUnits_det_units, mul_nonsing_inv, map_zero, map_one, Matrix.map_one]

@[simp]

name: coe_map_inv_mul_map
statement: theorem coe_map_inv_mul_map (g : GL n R) : g.val⁻¹.map f * g.val.map f = 1
proof:
rw [← Matrix.map_mul]
  simp only [isUnits_det_units, nonsing_inv_mul, map_zero, map_one, Matrix.map_one]

section kronecker
variable {R m : Type*} [CommSemiring R] [Fintype m] [DecidableEq m]

open scoped Kronecker

/-- The invertible kronecker matrix of invertible matrices. -/
protected def kronecker (x : GL n R) (y : GL m R) : GL (n × m) R where
  val := x ⊗ₖ y
  inv := ↑x⁻¹ ⊗ₖ ↑y⁻¹
  val_inv := by simp only [← mul_kronecker_mul, Units.mul_inv, one_kronecker_one]
  inv_val := by simp only [← mul_kronecker_mul, Units.inv_mul, one_kronecker_one]

name: det_mapGL
statement: theorem det_mapGL (g : SpecialLinearGroup n R) : (mapGL S g).det = 1
proof:
simp [mapGL]

end SpecialLinearGroup

section

variable {n : Type u} {R : Type v} [DecidableEq n] [Fintype n]
  [CommRing R] [LinearOrder R] [IsStrictOrderedRing R]

section

variable (n R)

/-- This is the subgroup of `nxn` matrices with entries over a
linear ordered ring and positive determinant. -/

name: mk_scalar
statement: theorem mk_scalar (u : Rˣ) : mk (.scalar n u) = 1
proof:
rw [← MonoidHom.mem_ker, ker_mk, GeneralLinearGroup.center_eq_range_scalar]
  simp

@[elab_as_elim, cases_eliminator]

name: signDet_mk
statement: theorem signDet_mk (g : GL n R) : signDet (mk g) = Units.map signHom.toMonoidHom g.det
proof:
rfl

@[simp]

name: val_signDet_mk
statement: theorem val_signDet_mk (g : GL n R) : (signDet (mk g) : SignType) = .sign g.det.val
proof:
rfl

end ProjGenLinGroup

end Matrix

name: center_eq_range_scalar
statement: theorem center_eq_range_scalar  : Subgroup.center (GL n R) = (scalar n).range
proof:
ext g
  constructor
  · -- previous lemma shows the underlying matrix is scalar, but now need to show
    -- the scalar is a unit; so we apply argument both to `g` and `g⁻¹`
    intro hg
    cases isEmpty_or_nonempty n with
    | inl hn => simp [nontriviality]
    | inr hn =>
      obtain ⟨a, ha⟩ := mem_center_iff_val_mem_range_scalar.mp hg
      obtain ⟨b, hb⟩ := mem_center_iff_val_mem_range_scalar.mp (Subgroup.inv_mem _ hg)
      have hab : a * b = 1 := by
        simpa [-mul_inv_cancel, ← ha, ← hb, ← diagonal_one, Units.ext_iff] using mul_inv_cancel g
      refine ⟨⟨a, b, hab, mul_comm a b ▸ hab⟩, ?_⟩
      simp [Units.ext_iff, ← ha]
  · rintro ⟨a, rfl⟩
    exact mem_center_iff_val_mem_range_scalar.mpr ⟨a, rfl⟩

@[deprecated (since := "2026-02-08")]
alias center_eq_range_units := center_eq_range_scalar

end Center

end GeneralLinearGroup

end Matrix

name: rank_eq_zero_iff_isTorsion
statement: theorem rank_eq_zero_iff_isTorsion  : Module.rank R M = 0 ↔ Module.IsTorsion R M
proof:
rw [Module.IsTorsion, rank_eq_zero_iff]
  simp [mem_nonZeroDivisors_iff_ne_zero]

/-- The `StrongRankCondition` is automatic. See `commRing_strongRankCondition`. -/

name: conjugate_eq
statement: theorem conjugate_eq  : q = p / (p - 1)
proof:
convert inv_inv q ▸ congr($(h.symm.inv_sub_inv_eq_inv.symm)⁻¹) using 1
  field [h.ne_zero]

name: mul_eq_add
statement: theorem mul_eq_add  : p * q = p + q
proof: simpa only [sub_mul, sub_eq_iff_eq_add, one_mul] using h.sub_one_mul_conj

name: div_conj_eq_sub_one
statement: theorem div_conj_eq_sub_one  : p / q = p - 1
proof:
field_simp [h.symm.ne_zero]
  linear_combination -h.sub_one_mul_conj

name: inv_add_inv_ennreal
statement: theorem inv_add_inv_ennreal  : (ENNReal.ofReal p)⁻¹ + (ENNReal.ofReal q)⁻¹ = 1
proof:
rw [← ENNReal.ofReal_one, ← ENNReal.ofReal_inv_of_pos h.pos,
    ← ENNReal.ofReal_inv_of_pos h.symm.pos, ← ENNReal.ofReal_add h.inv_nonneg h.symm.inv_nonneg,
    h.inv_add_inv_eq_one]

end

name: inv_sub_inv_eq_inv
statement: theorem inv_sub_inv_eq_inv  : r⁻¹ - q⁻¹ = p⁻¹
proof:
have := h.symm.inv_lt_inv.le
  exact_mod_cast h.coe.inv_sub_inv_eq_inv

name: conjugate_eq
statement: theorem conjugate_eq  : q = p / (p - 1)
proof:
have : ((1 : ℝ≥0) : ℝ) ≤ p := h.coe.lt.le
  exact_mod_cast NNReal.coe_sub this ▸ coe_one ▸ h.coe.conjugate_eq

name: mul_eq_add
statement: theorem mul_eq_add  : p * q = p + q
proof: simpa [mul_add, add_mul, h.ne_zero, h.symm.ne_zero, add_comm q] using congr(p * $(h.inv_eq) * q)

name: div_conj_eq_sub_one
statement: theorem div_conj_eq_sub_one  : p / q = p - 1
proof:
field_simp [h.symm.ne_zero]
  linear_combination -h.sub_one_mul_conj

name: holderConjugate_iff_eq_conjExponent
statement: theorem holderConjugate_iff_eq_conjExponent (hp : 1 < p) : p.HolderConjugate q ↔ q = p / (p - 1)
proof:
rw [← holderConjugate_coe_iff, Real.holderConjugate_iff_eq_conjExponent (by exact_mod_cast hp),
    ← coe_one, ← NNReal.coe_sub hp.le]
  exact_mod_cast Iff.rfl

name: of_toReal
statement: theorem of_toReal (h : Real.HolderTriple p.toReal q.toReal r.toReal) : HolderTriple p q r
proof:
have hp := h.pos
  have hq := h.symm.pos
  have hr := h.pos'
  rw [toReal_pos_iff] at hp hq hr
  simpa [hp.2.ne, hq.2.ne, hr.2.ne] using h.ennrealOfReal

variable (r) in

name: of_toReal
statement: theorem of_toReal (h : p.toReal.HolderConjugate q.toReal) : p.HolderConjugate q
proof:
rw [Real.HolderConjugate] at h
  exact HolderTriple.of_toReal (toReal_one ▸ h)

name: toReal_iff
statement: theorem toReal_iff (hp : 1 < p.toReal) : p.toReal.HolderConjugate q.toReal ↔ p.HolderConjugate q
proof:
refine ⟨of_toReal, fun h ↦ ?_⟩
  have hq : 0 < q.toReal := by
    rw [toReal_pos_iff]
    refine ⟨pos q p, lt_top_iff_one_lt q p |>.mpr ?_⟩
    contrapose! hp
    exact toReal_mono one_ne_top hp
  simpa using HolderTriple.toReal 1 (zero_lt_one.trans hp) hq

name: toNNReal_iff
statement: theorem toNNReal_iff (hp : 1 < p.toNNReal) : NNReal.HolderConjugate p.toNNReal q.toNNReal ↔ HolderConjugate p q
proof:
simp_rw [← NNReal.holderTriple_coe_iff, coe_toNNReal_eq_toReal]
  apply toReal_iff ?_
  all_goals simpa [← coe_toNNReal_eq_toReal]

name: mul_eq_add
statement: theorem mul_eq_add  : p * q = p + q
proof:
obtain rfl | hp := eq_or_ne p ∞
  · simp [ne_zero q ∞]
  obtain rfl | hq := eq_or_ne q ∞
  · simp [ne_zero p ∞]
  simpa [add_comm p, mul_add, add_mul, hp, hq, ne_zero p q, ne_zero q p, ENNReal.mul_inv_cancel,
    ENNReal.inv_mul_cancel_right] using congr(p * $((inv_add_inv_eq_one p q).symm) * q)

name: div_conj_eq_sub_one
statement: theorem div_conj_eq_sub_one  : p / q = p - 1
proof:
obtain rfl | hq := eq_or_ne q ∞
  · obtain rfl := unique ∞ p 1
    simp
  refine ENNReal.eq_sub_of_add_eq one_ne_top ?_
  rw [← ENNReal.div_self (ne_zero q p) hq, ← ENNReal.add_div, ← h.mul_eq_add, mul_div_assoc,
    ENNReal.div_self (ne_zero q p) hq, mul_one]

end

protected lemma inv_inv (hab : a + b = 1) : a⁻¹.HolderConjugate b⁻¹ where
  inv_add_inv_eq_inv := by simpa [inv_inv] using hab

name: ratLt_bddAbove
statement: theorem ratLt_bddAbove (x : M) : BddAbove (ratLt x)
proof:
obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := Archimedean.arch x zero_lt_one
  use n
  rw [ratLt, mem_upperBounds]
  intro ⟨num, den, _, _⟩
  rw [Rat.le_iff]
  suffices num • 1 < den • x → num ≤ n * den by simpa using this
  intro h
  exact num_le_nat_mul_den h.le (by simpa using hn)

name: ratLt_nonempty
statement: theorem ratLt_nonempty (x : M) : (ratLt x).Nonempty
proof:
obtain hneg | rfl | hxpos := lt_trichotomy x 0
  · obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := Archimedean.arch (-x - x) zero_lt_one
    use Rat.ofInt (-n)
    suffices -(n • 1) < x by simpa using this
    exact neg_lt.mpr (lt_of_lt_of_le (by simpa using hneg) hn)
  · exact ⟨Rat.ofInt (-1), by simp⟩
  · obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := Archimedean.arch 1 hxpos
    use Rat.mk' 1 (n + 1) (by simp) (by simp)
    simpa using hn.trans_lt <| (nsmul_lt_nsmul_iff_left hxpos).mpr (by simp)

open scoped Pointwise in

name: ratLt_add
statement: theorem ratLt_add (x y : M) : ratLt (x + y) = ratLt x + ratLt y
proof:
ext a
  rw [Set.mem_add]
  constructor
  · /- Given `a ∈ ratLt 1 (x + y)`, find `u ∈ ratLt 1 x`, `v ∈ ratLt 1 y`
      such that `u + v = a`.
      In a naive attempt, one can take the denominator `d` of `a`,
      and find the largest `u = p / d < x / 1`.
      However, `d` could be too "coarse", and `v = a - u` could be 1/d too large than `y / 1`.
      To ensure a large enough denominator, we take `d * k`, where
      `1 + 1 ≤ k • (d • (x + y) - a.num • 1)`. -/
    intro h
    rw [Set.mem_setOf_eq] at h
    obtain ⟨k, hk⟩ := Archimedean.arch (1 + 1) <| sub_pos.mpr h
    have hk0 : k ≠ 0 := by
      contrapose! hk
      simp [hk]
    have hka0 : k * a.den ≠ 0 := mul_ne_zero hk0 a.den_ne_zero
    obtain ⟨m, ⟨hm1, hm2⟩, _⟩ := existsUnique_add_zsmul_mem_Ico zero_lt_one 0 (k • a.den • x - 1)
    refine ⟨mkRat m (k * a.den), ?_, mkRat (k * a.num - m) (k * a.den), ?_, ?_⟩
    · rw [mkRat_mem_ratLt hka0, ← smul_smul]
      simpa using hm2
    · have hk' : 1 + (k • a.num • 1 - k • a.den • y) ≤ k • a.den • x - 1 := by
        rw [smul_add, smul_sub, smul_add, le_sub_iff_add_le, ← sub_le_iff_le_add] at hk
        rw [le_sub_iff_add_le]
        convert hk using 1
        abel
      have : k • a.num • 1 - k • a.den • y < m • 1 :=
        lt_of_lt_of_le (lt_add_of_pos_left _ zero_lt_one) (by simpa using hk'.trans hm1)
      rw [mkRat_mem_ratLt hka0, sub_smul, sub_lt_comm, ← smul_smul, ← smul_smul, natCast_zsmul]
      exact this
    · rw [Rat.mkRat_add_mkRat_of_den _ _ hka0]
      rw [add_sub_cancel, Rat.mkRat_mul_left hk0, Rat.mkRat_num_den']
  · -- `u ∈ ratLt 1 x`, `v ∈ ratLt 1 y` → `u + v ∈ ratLt 1 (x + y)`
    intro ⟨u, hu, v, hv, huv⟩
    rw [← huv]
    rw [Set.mem_setOf_eq] at hu hv ⊢
    exact num_smul_one_lt_den_smul_add hu hv

name: embedRealFun_zero
statement: theorem embedRealFun_zero  : embedRealFun (0 : M) = 0
proof:
apply le_antisymm
  · apply csSup_le (ratLt'_nonempty 0)
    intro x
    unfold ratLt' ratLt
    suffices ∀ (y : ℚ), y.num • (1 : M) < 0 → y = x → x ≤ 0 by simpa using this
    intro y hy hyx
    rw [← hyx, Rat.cast_nonpos, ← Rat.num_nonpos]
    exact (neg_of_smul_neg_right hy zero_le_one).le
  · rw [le_csSup_iff (ratLt'_bddAbove (0 : M)) (ratLt'_nonempty 0)]
    intro x
    rw [mem_upperBounds]
    suffices (∀ (y : ℚ), y.num • (1 : M) < 0 → y ≤ x) → 0 ≤ x by simpa using this
    intro h
    have h' (y : ℚ) (hy : y < 0) : y ≤ x := by
      exact h _ <| (smul_neg_iff_of_neg_left (by simpa using hy)).mpr zero_lt_one
    contrapose! h'
    obtain ⟨y, hxy, hy⟩ := exists_rat_btwn h'
    exact ⟨y, by simpa using hy, hxy⟩

name: embedRealFun_add
statement: theorem embedRealFun_add (x y : M) : embedRealFun (x + y) = embedRealFun x + embedRealFun y
proof:
rw [embedRealFun, ratLt'_add, csSup_add (ratLt'_nonempty x) (ratLt'_bddAbove x)
    (ratLt'_nonempty y) (ratLt'_bddAbove y)]

variable (M) in

name: embedReal_one
statement: theorem embedReal_one  : (embedReal M) 1 = 1
proof:
rw [embedReal_apply]
  apply le_antisymm
  · apply csSup_le (ratLt'_nonempty 1)
    suffices ∀ (x : ℚ), x.num • (1 : M) < (x.den : ℤ) • (1 : M) → (x : ℝ) ≤ 1 by simpa using this
    intro x hx
    suffices x ≤ 1 by norm_cast
    simpa [Rat.le_iff] using ((smul_lt_smul_iff_of_pos_right zero_lt_one).mp hx).le
  · rw [le_csSup_iff (ratLt'_bddAbove (1 : M)) (ratLt'_nonempty 1)]
    simp_rw [mem_upperBounds]
    suffices ∀ (x : ℝ), (∀ (y : ℚ), y.num • (1 : M) < (y.den : ℤ) • 1 → y ≤ x) → 1 ≤ x by
      simpa using this
    intro x h
    have h' (y : ℚ) (hy : y < 1) : y ≤ x :=
      h _ ((smul_lt_smul_iff_of_pos_right zero_lt_one).mpr (by simpa using (Rat.lt_iff _ _).mp hy))
    contrapose! h'
    obtain ⟨y, hxy, hy⟩ := exists_rat_btwn h'
    exact ⟨y, (by norm_cast at hy), hxy⟩

omit [One M] [ZeroLEOneClass M] [NeZero (1 : M)] in
variable (M) in

name: exists_orderAddMonoidHom_real_injective
statement: theorem exists_orderAddMonoidHom_real_injective  : ∃ f : M →+o ℝ, Function.Injective f
proof:
cases subsingleton_or_nontrivial M
  · exact ⟨0, Function.injective_of_subsingleton _⟩
  · obtain ⟨a, ha⟩ := exists_ne (0 : M)
    let one : One M := ⟨|a|⟩
    have : ZeroLEOneClass M := ⟨abs_nonneg a⟩
    have : NeZero (1 : M) := ⟨abs_ne_zero.mpr ha⟩
    exact ⟨embedReal M, embedReal_injective M⟩

end Archimedean

name: sInf_univ
statement: theorem sInf_univ  : sInf (@Set.univ ℝ) = 0
proof:
simp [sInf_def]

@[simp] lemma iSup_of_isEmpty [IsEmpty ι] (f : ι → ℝ) : ⨆ i, f i = 0 := by
  dsimp [iSup]
  convert Real.sSup_empty
  rw [Set.range_eq_empty_iff]
  infer_instance

@[simp]

name: iSup_const_zero
statement: theorem iSup_const_zero  : ⨆ _ : ι, (0 : ℝ) = 0
proof:
cases isEmpty_or_nonempty ι
  · exact Real.iSup_of_isEmpty _
  · exact ciSup_const

name: iInf_const_zero
statement: theorem iInf_const_zero  : ⨅ _ : ι, (0 : ℝ) = 0
proof:
cases isEmpty_or_nonempty ι
  · exact Real.iInf_of_isEmpty _
  · exact ciInf_const

name: sSup_neg
statement: theorem sSup_neg (s : Set ℝ) : sSup (-s) = -sInf s
proof:
obtain rfl | hn := s.eq_empty_or_nonempty; · simp
  by_cases hb : BddBelow s
  · rw [csSup_neg hn hb]
  · rw [csInf_of_not_bddBelow hb, Real.sInf_empty, csSup_of_not_bddAbove (bddAbove_neg.not.2 hb),
      Real.sSup_empty, neg_zero]

@[simp]

name: sInf_neg
statement: theorem sInf_neg (s : Set ℝ) : sInf (-s) = -sSup s
proof:
rw [← neg_eq_iff_eq_neg, ← Real.sSup_neg, neg_neg]

/-- As `sSup s = 0` when `s` is an empty set of reals, it suffices to show that all elements of `s`
are at most some nonnegative number `a` to show that `sSup s ≤ a`.

See also `csSup_le`. -/
protected lemma sSup_le (hs : ∀ x ∈ s, x ≤ a) (ha : 0 ≤ a) : sSup s ≤ a := by
  obtain rfl | hs' := s.eq_empty_or_nonempty
  exacts [sSup_empty.trans_le ha, csSup_le hs' hs]

/-- As `⨆ i, f i = 0` when the domain of the real-valued function `f` is empty, it suffices to show
that all values of `f` are at most some nonnegative number `a` to show that `⨆ i, f i ≤ a`.

See also `ciSup_le`. -/
protected lemma iSup_le (hf : ∀ i, f i ≤ a) (ha : 0 ≤ a) : ⨆ i, f i ≤ a :=
  Real.sSup_le (Set.forall_mem_range.2 hf) ha

/-- As `sInf s = 0` when `s` is an empty set of reals, it suffices to show that all elements of `s`
are at least some nonpositive number `a` to show that `a ≤ sInf s`.

See also `le_csInf`. -/
protected lemma le_sInf (hs : ∀ x ∈ s, a ≤ x) (ha : a ≤ 0) : a ≤ sInf s := by
  obtain rfl | hs' := s.eq_empty_or_nonempty
  exacts [ha.trans_eq sInf_empty.symm, le_csInf hs' hs]

/-- As `⨅ i, f i = 0` when the domain of the real-valued function `f` is empty, it suffices to show
that all values of `f` are at least some nonpositive number `a` to show that `a ≤ ⨅ i, f i`.

See also `le_ciInf`. -/
protected lemma le_iInf (hf : ∀ i, a ≤ f i) (ha : a ≤ 0) : a ≤ ⨅ i, f i :=
  Real.le_sInf (Set.forall_mem_range.2 hf) ha

/-- As `sSup s = 0` when `s` is an empty set of reals, it suffices to show that all elements of `s`
are nonpositive to show that `sSup s ≤ 0`. -/

name: sSup_nonneg
statement: theorem sSup_nonneg (hs : ∀ x ∈ s, 0 ≤ x) : 0 ≤ sSup s
proof:
obtain rfl | ⟨x, hx⟩ := s.eq_empty_or_nonempty
  · exact sSup_empty.ge
  · exact sSup_nonneg' ⟨x, hx, hs _ hx⟩

/-- As `⨆ i, f i = 0` when the domain of the real-valued function `f` is empty or unbounded above,
it suffices to show that all values of `f` are nonnegative to show that `0 ≤ ⨆ i, f i`. -/

name: sInf_nonpos
statement: theorem sInf_nonpos (hs : ∀ x ∈ s, x ≤ 0) : sInf s ≤ 0
proof:
obtain rfl | ⟨x, hx⟩ := s.eq_empty_or_nonempty
  · exact sInf_empty.le
  · exact sInf_nonpos' ⟨x, hx, hs _ hx⟩

/-- As `⨅ i, f i = 0` when the domain of the real-valued function `f` is empty or unbounded below,
it suffices to show that all values of `f` are nonpositive to show that `0 ≤ ⨅ i, f i`. -/

name: cauSeq_converges
statement: theorem cauSeq_converges (f : CauSeq ℝ abs) : ∃ x, f ≈ const abs x
proof:
let s := {x : ℝ | const abs x < f}
  have lb : ∃ x, x ∈ s := exists_lt f
  have ub' : ∀ x, f < const abs x → ∀ y ∈ s, y ≤ x := fun x h y yS =>
    le_of_lt <| const_lt.1 <| CauSeq.lt_trans yS h
  have ub : ∃ x, ∀ y ∈ s, y ≤ x := (exists_gt f).imp ub'
  refine ⟨sSup s, ((lt_total _ _).resolve_left fun h => ?_).resolve_right fun h => ?_⟩
  · rcases h with ⟨ε, ε0, i, ih⟩
    refine (csSup_le lb (ub' _ ?_)).not_gt (sub_lt_self _ (half_pos ε0))
    refine ⟨_, half_pos ε0, i, fun j ij => ?_⟩
    rw [sub_apply, const_apply, sub_right_comm, le_sub_iff_add_le, add_halves]
    exact ih _ ij
  · rcases h with ⟨ε, ε0, i, ih⟩
    refine (le_csSup ub ?_).not_gt ((lt_add_iff_pos_left _).2 (half_pos ε0))
    refine ⟨_, half_pos ε0, i, fun j ij => ?_⟩
    rw [sub_apply, const_apply, add_comm, ← sub_sub, le_sub_iff_add_le, add_halves]
    exact ih _ ij

instance : CauSeq.IsComplete ℝ abs :=
  ⟨cauSeq_converges⟩

open Set

name: not_bddAbove_coe
statement: theorem not_bddAbove_coe  : ¬ (BddAbove <| range (fun (x : ℚ) ↦ (x : ℝ)))
proof:
dsimp only [BddAbove, upperBounds]
  rw [Set.not_nonempty_iff_eq_empty]
  ext
  simpa using exists_rat_gt _

name: not_bddBelow_coe
statement: theorem not_bddBelow_coe  : ¬ (BddBelow <| range (fun (x : ℚ) ↦ (x : ℝ)))
proof:
dsimp only [BddBelow, lowerBounds]
  rw [Set.not_nonempty_iff_eq_empty]
  ext
  simpa using exists_rat_lt _

name: iUnion_Iic_rat
statement: theorem iUnion_Iic_rat  : ⋃ r : ℚ, Iic (r : ℝ) = univ
proof: exact iUnion_Iic_of_not_bddAbove_range not_bddAbove_coe

name: iInter_Iic_rat
statement: theorem iInter_Iic_rat  : ⋂ r : ℚ, Iic (r : ℝ) = ∅
proof:
exact iInter_Iic_eq_empty_iff.mpr not_bddBelow_coe

/-- Exponentiation is eventually larger than linear growth. -/

name: exists_natCast_add_one_lt_pow_of_one_lt
statement: theorem exists_natCast_add_one_lt_pow_of_one_lt (ha : 1 < a) : ∃ m : ℕ, (m + 1 : ℝ) < a ^ m
proof:
obtain ⟨k, posk, hk⟩ : ∃ k : ℕ, 0 < k ∧ 1 / k + 1 < a := by
    contrapose! ha
    refine le_of_forall_lt_rat_imp_le ?_
    intro q hq
    refine (ha q.den (by positivity)).trans ?_
    rw [← le_sub_iff_add_le, div_le_iff₀ (by positivity), sub_mul, one_mul]
    norm_cast at hq ⊢
    rw [← q.num_div_den, one_lt_div (by positivity)] at hq
    rw [q.mul_den_eq_num]
    norm_cast at hq ⊢
    lia
  use 2 * k ^ 2
  calc
    ((2 * k ^ 2 : ℕ) + 1 : ℝ) ≤ 2 ^ (2 * k) := mod_cast Nat.two_mul_sq_add_one_le_two_pow_two_mul _
    _ = (1 / k * k + 1 : ℝ) ^ (2 * k) := by simp [posk.ne']; norm_num
    _ ≤ ((1 / k + 1) ^ k : ℝ) ^ (2 * k) := by gcongr; exact mul_add_one_le_add_one_pow (by simp) _
    _ = (1 / k + 1 : ℝ) ^ (2 * k ^ 2) := by rw [← pow_mul, mul_left_comm, sq]
    _ < a ^ (2 * k ^ 2) := by gcongr

name: sign_apply_eq
statement: theorem sign_apply_eq (r : ℝ) : sign r = -1 ∨ sign r = 0 ∨ sign r = 1
proof:
obtain hn | rfl | hp := lt_trichotomy r (0 : ℝ)
  · exact Or.inl <| sign_of_neg hn
  · exact Or.inr <| Or.inl <| sign_zero
  · exact Or.inr <| Or.inr <| sign_of_pos hp

/-- This lemma is useful for working with `ℝˣ` -/

name: sign_intCast
statement: theorem sign_intCast (z : ℤ) : sign (z : ℝ) = ↑(Int.sign z)
proof:
obtain hn | rfl | hp := lt_trichotomy z (0 : ℤ)
  · rw [sign_of_neg (Int.cast_lt_zero.mpr hn), Int.sign_eq_neg_one_of_neg hn, Int.cast_neg,
      Int.cast_one]
  · rw [Int.cast_zero, sign_zero, Int.sign_zero, Int.cast_zero]
  · rw [sign_of_pos (Int.cast_pos.mpr hp), Int.sign_eq_one_of_pos hp, Int.cast_one]

name: sign_mul_nonneg
statement: theorem sign_mul_nonneg (r : ℝ) : 0 ≤ sign r * r
proof:
obtain hn | rfl | hp := lt_trichotomy r (0 : ℝ)
  · rw [sign_of_neg hn]
    exact mul_nonneg_of_nonpos_of_nonpos (by simp) hn.le
  · rw [mul_zero]
  · rw [sign_of_pos hp, one_mul]
    exact hp.le

name: inv_sign
statement: theorem inv_sign (r : ℝ) : (sign r)⁻¹ = sign r
proof:
obtain hn | hz | hp := sign_apply_eq r
  · rw [hn]
    simp
  · rw [hz]
    exact inv_zero
  · rw [hp]
    exact inv_one

@[simp]

name: sign_inv
statement: theorem sign_inv (r : ℝ) : sign r⁻¹ = sign r
proof:
obtain hn | rfl | hp := lt_trichotomy r (0 : ℝ)
  · rw [sign_of_neg hn, sign_of_neg (inv_lt_zero.mpr hn)]
  · rw [sign_zero, inv_zero, sign_zero]
  · rw [sign_of_pos hp, sign_of_pos (inv_pos.mpr hp)]

end Real

name: ofCauchy_sub
statement: theorem ofCauchy_sub (a b) : (⟨a - b⟩ : ℝ) = ⟨a⟩ - ⟨b⟩
proof:
rw [sub_eq_add_neg, ofCauchy_add, ofCauchy_neg]
  rfl

name: zero_lt_one
statement: theorem zero_lt_one  : (0 : ℝ) < 1
proof:
convert ratCast_lt.2 zero_lt_one <;> simp [← ofCauchy_ratCast, ofCauchy_one, ofCauchy_zero]

instance instNontrivial : Nontrivial ℝ where
  exists_pair_ne := ⟨0, 1, Real.zero_lt_one.ne⟩

instance instZeroLEOneClass : ZeroLEOneClass ℝ where
  zero_le_one := le_of_lt Real.zero_lt_one

instance instIsOrderedAddMonoid : IsOrderedAddMonoid ℝ where
  add_le_add_left := by
    simp only [le_iff_eq_or_lt]
    rintro a b ⟨rfl, h⟩
    · simp only [lt_self_iff_false, or_false, forall_const]
    · refine fun c => Or.inr ?_
      induction a using Real.ind_mk with | _ a =>
      induction b using Real.ind_mk with | _ b =>
      induction c using Real.ind_mk with | _ c =>
      simp only [mk_lt, ← mk_add] at *
      change Pos _ at *
      rwa [add_sub_add_right_eq_sub]

instance instIsStrictOrderedRing : IsStrictOrderedRing ℝ :=
  .of_mul_pos fun a b ↦ by
    induction a using Real.ind_mk
    induction b using Real.ind_mk
    simpa only [mk_lt, mk_pos, ← mk_mul] using CauSeq.mul_pos

instance instIsOrderedRing : IsOrderedRing ℝ :=
  inferInstance

instance instIsOrderedCancelAddMonoid : IsOrderedCancelAddMonoid ℝ :=
  inferInstance

set_option backward.privateInPublic true in
private irreducible_def sup : ℝ → ℝ → ℝ
  | ⟨x⟩, ⟨y⟩ => ⟨Quotient.map₂ (· ⊔ ·) (fun _ _ hx _ _ hy => sup_equiv_sup hx hy) x y⟩

set_option backward.privateInPublic true in
set_option backward.privateInPublic.warn false in
instance : Max ℝ :=
  ⟨sup⟩

name: ofCauchy_div
statement: theorem ofCauchy_div (f g) : (⟨f / g⟩ : ℝ) = (⟨f⟩ : ℝ) / (⟨g⟩ : ℝ)
proof:
simp_rw [div_eq_mul_inv, ofCauchy_mul, ofCauchy_inv]

noncomputable instance instField : Field ℝ where
  mul_inv_cancel := by
    rintro ⟨a⟩ h
    rw [mul_comm]
    simp only [← ofCauchy_inv, ← ofCauchy_mul, ← ofCauchy_one, ← ofCauchy_zero,
      Ne, ofCauchy.injEq] at *
    exact CauSeq.Completion.inv_mul_cancel h
  inv_zero := by simp [← ofCauchy_zero, ← ofCauchy_inv]
  nnqsmul := _
  nnqsmul_def := fun _ _ => rfl
  qsmul := _
  qsmul_def := fun _ _ => rfl
  nnratCast_def q := by
    rw [← ofCauchy_nnratCast, NNRat.cast_def, ofCauchy_div, ofCauchy_natCast, ofCauchy_natCast]
  ratCast_def q := by
    rw [← ofCauchy_ratCast, Rat.cast_def, ofCauchy_div, ofCauchy_natCast, ofCauchy_intCast]

-- Extra instances to short-circuit type class resolution
noncomputable instance : DivisionRing ℝ := by infer_instance

noncomputable instance decidableLT (a b : ℝ) : Decidable (a < b) := by infer_instance

noncomputable instance decidableLE (a b : ℝ) : Decidable (a ≤ b) := by infer_instance

noncomputable instance decidableEq (a b : ℝ) : Decidable (a = b) := by infer_instance

/-- Show an underlying Cauchy sequence for real numbers.

The representative chosen is the one passed in the VM to `Quot.mk`, so two Cauchy sequences
converging to the same number may be printed differently.
-/
unsafe instance : Repr ℝ where
  reprPrec r p := Repr.addAppParen ("Real.ofCauchy " ++ repr r.cauchy) p

name: sqrt_mul_le_max
statement: theorem sqrt_mul_le_max  : sqrt (x * y) ≤ max x y
proof: rw [sqrt_le_iff_le_sq, sq]; gcongr <;> simp

name: sqrt_mul
statement: theorem sqrt_mul (x y : ℝ≥0) : sqrt (x * y) = sqrt x * sqrt y
proof:
rw [sqrt_eq_iff_eq_sq, mul_pow, sq_sqrt, sq_sqrt]

/-- `NNReal.sqrt` as a `MonoidWithZeroHom`. -/
noncomputable def sqrtHom : ℝ≥0 →*₀ ℝ≥0 :=
  ⟨⟨sqrt, sqrt_zero⟩, sqrt_one, sqrt_mul⟩

name: map_sqrt_atTop
statement: theorem map_sqrt_atTop  : map (√·) atTop = atTop
proof:
unfold sqrt
  simp_rw [← Function.comp_def]
  simp [← map_map]

@[simp]

name: comap_sqrt_atTop
statement: theorem comap_sqrt_atTop  : comap (√·) atTop = atTop
proof:
unfold sqrt
  simp_rw [← Function.comp_def]
  simp [← comap_comap]

name: sqrt_nonneg
statement: theorem sqrt_nonneg (x : ℝ) : 0 ≤ √x
proof:
unfold sqrt
  exact NNReal.coe_nonneg _

@[simp]

name: mul_self_sqrt
statement: theorem mul_self_sqrt (h : 0 ≤ x) : √x * √x = x
proof:
rw [Real.sqrt, ← NNReal.coe_mul, NNReal.mul_self_sqrt, Real.coe_toNNReal _ h]

@[simp]

name: sqrt_eq_cases
statement: theorem sqrt_eq_cases  : √x = y ↔ y * y = x ∧ 0 ≤ y ∨ x < 0 ∧ y = 0
proof:
constructor
  · rintro rfl
    rcases le_or_gt 0 x with hle | hlt
    · exact Or.inl ⟨mul_self_sqrt hle, sqrt_nonneg x⟩
    · exact Or.inr ⟨hlt, sqrt_eq_zero_of_nonpos hlt.le⟩
  · rintro (⟨rfl, hy⟩ | ⟨hx, rfl⟩)
    exacts [sqrt_mul_self hy, sqrt_eq_zero_of_nonpos hx.le]

name: sqrt_eq_iff_mul_self_eq_of_pos
statement: theorem sqrt_eq_iff_mul_self_eq_of_pos (h : 0 < y) : √x = y ↔ y * y = x
proof:
simp [sqrt_eq_cases, h.ne', h.le]

@[simp]

name: sqrt_mul_self_eq_abs
statement: theorem sqrt_mul_self_eq_abs (x : ℝ) : √(x * x) = |x|
proof: rw [← abs_mul_abs_self x, sqrt_mul_self (abs_nonneg _)]

name: sqrt_le_sqrt_iff
statement: theorem sqrt_le_sqrt_iff (hy : 0 ≤ y) : √x ≤ √y ↔ x ≤ y
proof:
rw [Real.sqrt, Real.sqrt, NNReal.coe_le_coe, NNReal.sqrt_le_sqrt, toNNReal_le_toNNReal_iff hy]

@[simp]

name: sqrt_lt_sqrt_iff_of_pos
statement: theorem sqrt_lt_sqrt_iff_of_pos (hy : 0 < y) : √x < √y ↔ x < y
proof:
rw [Real.sqrt, Real.sqrt, NNReal.coe_lt_coe, NNReal.sqrt_lt_sqrt, toNNReal_lt_toNNReal_iff hy]

@[bound]

name: sqrt_le_sqrt
statement: theorem sqrt_le_sqrt (h : x ≤ y) : √x ≤ √y
proof:
rw [Real.sqrt, Real.sqrt, NNReal.coe_le_coe, NNReal.sqrt_le_sqrt]
  exact toNNReal_le_toNNReal h

@[gcongr]

name: sqrt_le_left
statement: theorem sqrt_le_left (hy : 0 ≤ y) : √x ≤ y ↔ x ≤ y ^ 2
proof:
rw [sqrt, ← Real.le_toNNReal_iff_coe_le hy, NNReal.sqrt_le_iff_le_sq, sq, ← Real.toNNReal_mul hy,
    Real.toNNReal_le_toNNReal_iff (mul_self_nonneg y), sq]

name: sqrt_le_iff
statement: theorem sqrt_le_iff  : √x ≤ y ↔ 0 ≤ y ∧ x ≤ y ^ 2
proof:
rw [← and_iff_right_of_imp fun h => (sqrt_nonneg x).trans h, and_congr_right_iff]
  exact sqrt_le_left

name: abs_le_sqrt
statement: theorem abs_le_sqrt (h : x ^ 2 ≤ y) : |x| ≤ √y
proof: rw [← sqrt_sq_eq_abs]; exact sqrt_le_sqrt h

name: sq_le
statement: theorem sq_le (h : 0 ≤ y) : x ^ 2 ≤ y ↔ -√y ≤ x ∧ x ≤ √y
proof:
constructor
  · simpa only [abs_le] using abs_le_sqrt
  · rw [← abs_le, ← sq_abs]
    exact (le_sqrt (abs_nonneg x) h).mp

name: one_le_sqrt
statement: theorem one_le_sqrt  : 1 ≤ √x ↔ 1 ≤ x
proof:
rw [← sqrt_one, sqrt_le_sqrt_iff' zero_lt_one, sqrt_one]

@[simp] lemma sqrt_le_one : √x ≤ 1 ↔ x ≤ 1 := by
  rw [← sqrt_one, sqrt_le_sqrt_iff zero_le_one, sqrt_one]

@[simp] lemma isSquare_iff : IsSquare x ↔ 0 ≤ x :=
  ⟨(·.nonneg), (⟨√x, mul_self_sqrt · |>.symm⟩)⟩

end Real

namespace Mathlib.Meta.Positivity

open Lean Meta Qq Function

/-- Extension for the `positivity` tactic: a square root of a strictly positive nonnegative real is
positive. -/
@[positivity NNReal.sqrt _]
meta def evalNNRealSqrt : PositivityExt where eval {u α} _zα _pα e := do
  match u, α, e with
  | 0, ~q(NNReal), ~q(NNReal.sqrt $a) =>
    let ra ← core q(inferInstance) q(inferInstance) a
    assertInstancesCommute
    match ra with
    | .positive pa => pure (.positive q(NNReal.sqrt_pos_of_pos $pa))
    | _ => failure -- this case is dealt with by generic nonnegativity of nnreals
  | _, _, _ => throwError "not NNReal.sqrt"

/-- Extension for the `positivity` tactic: a square root is nonnegative, and is strictly positive if
its input is. -/
@[positivity √_]
meta def evalSqrt : PositivityExt where eval {u α} _zα _pα e := do
  match u, α, e with
  | 0, ~q(ℝ), ~q(√$a) =>
    let ra ← catchNone <| core q(inferInstance) q(inferInstance) a
    assertInstancesCommute
    match ra with
    | .positive pa => pure (.positive q(Real.sqrt_pos_of_pos $pa))
    | _ => pure (.nonnegative q(Real.sqrt_nonneg $a))
  | _, _, _ => throwError "not Real.sqrt"

end Mathlib.Meta.Positivity

namespace Real

name: sqrt_two_lt_three_halves
statement: theorem sqrt_two_lt_three_halves  : √2 < 3 / 2
proof:
rw [← sq_lt_sq₀ (by positivity) (by positivity)]
  grind

name: inv_sqrt_two_sub_one
statement: theorem inv_sqrt_two_sub_one  : (√2 - 1)⁻¹ = √2 + 1
proof:
grind

@[simp]

name: sqrt_inv
statement: theorem sqrt_inv (x : ℝ) : √x⁻¹ = (√x)⁻¹
proof:
rw [Real.sqrt, Real.toNNReal_inv, NNReal.sqrt_inv, NNReal.coe_inv, Real.sqrt]

@[simp]

name: div_sqrt
statement: theorem div_sqrt  : x / √x = √x
proof: grind

name: lt_sqrt
statement: theorem lt_sqrt (hx : 0 ≤ x) : x < √y ↔ x ^ 2 < y
proof: rw [← sqrt_lt_sqrt_iff (sq_nonneg _), sqrt_sq hx]

name: sq_lt
statement: theorem sq_lt  : x ^ 2 < y ↔ -√y < x ∧ x < √y
proof: rw [← abs_lt, ← sq_abs, lt_sqrt (abs_nonneg _)]

name: lt_sq_of_sqrt_lt
statement: theorem lt_sq_of_sqrt_lt (h : √x < y) : x < y ^ 2
proof:
have hy := x.sqrt_nonneg.trans_lt h
  rwa [← sqrt_lt_sqrt_iff_of_pos (sq_pos_of_pos hy), sqrt_sq hy.le]

/-- The natural square root is at most the real square root -/

name: sqrt_one_add_le
statement: theorem sqrt_one_add_le (h : -1 ≤ x) : √(1 + x) ≤ 1 + x / 2
proof:
refine sqrt_le_iff.mpr ⟨by linarith, ?_⟩
  calc 1 + x
    _ ≤ 1 + x + (x / 2) ^ 2 := le_add_of_nonneg_right <| sq_nonneg _
    _ = _ := by ring

name: mem_primeFactors
statement: theorem mem_primeFactors  : p ∈ n.primeFactors ↔ p.Prime ∧ p ∣ n ∧ n ≠ 0
proof: simp_rw [← toFinset_factors, List.mem_toFinset, mem_primeFactorsList']

name: mem_primeFactors_of_ne_zero
statement: theorem mem_primeFactors_of_ne_zero (hn : n ≠ 0) : p ∈ n.primeFactors ↔ p.Prime ∧ p ∣ n
proof: simp [hn]

name: mem_primeFactors_iff_mem_primeFactorsList
statement: theorem mem_primeFactors_iff_mem_primeFactorsList  : p ∈ n.primeFactors ↔ p ∈ n.primeFactorsList
proof: simp only [primeFactors, List.mem_toFinset]

name: primeFactors_zero
statement: theorem primeFactors_zero  : primeFactors 0 = ∅
proof:
ext
  simp

@[simp] lemma primeFactors_one : primeFactors 1 = ∅ := by
  ext
  simpa using Prime.ne_one

@[simp] lemma primeFactors_eq_empty : n.primeFactors = ∅ ↔ n = 0 ∨ n = 1 := by
  constructor
  · contrapose!
    rintro hn
    obtain ⟨p, hp, hpn⟩ := exists_prime_and_dvd hn.2
    exact ⟨_, mem_primeFactors.2 ⟨hp, hpn, hn.1⟩⟩
  · rintro (rfl | rfl) <;> simp

@[simp]

name: primeFactors_pow_succ
statement: theorem primeFactors_pow_succ (n k : ℕ) : (n ^ (k + 1)).primeFactors = n.primeFactors
proof:
rcases eq_or_ne n 0 with (rfl | hn)
  · simp
  induction k with
  | zero => simp
  | succ k ih => rw [pow_succ', primeFactors_mul hn (pow_ne_zero _ hn), ih, Finset.union_idempotent]

name: find_eq_iff
statement: theorem find_eq_iff (h : ∃ n : ℕ, p n) : Nat.find h = m ↔ p m ∧ ∀ n < m, ¬p n
proof:
constructor
  · grind [Nat.find_min]
  · rintro ⟨hm, hlt⟩
    have := Nat.find_min' h hm
    grind

@[simp] lemma find_lt_iff (h : ∃ n : ℕ, p n) (n : ℕ) : Nat.find h < n ↔ ∃ m < n, p m :=
  ⟨fun h2 ↦ ⟨Nat.find h, h2, Nat.find_spec h⟩,
    fun ⟨_, hmn, hm⟩ ↦ Nat.lt_of_le_of_lt (Nat.find_min' h hm) hmn⟩

@[simp] lemma find_le_iff (h : ∃ n : ℕ, p n) (n : ℕ) : Nat.find h ≤ n ↔ ∃ m ≤ n, p m := by
  simp only [← Nat.lt_succ_iff, find_lt_iff]

@[simp] lemma le_find_iff (h : ∃ n : ℕ, p n) (n : ℕ) : n ≤ Nat.find h ↔ ∀ m < n, ¬p m := by
  simp only [← not_lt, find_lt_iff, not_exists, not_and]

@[simp] lemma lt_find_iff (h : ∃ n : ℕ, p n) (n : ℕ) : n < Nat.find h ↔ ∀ m ≤ n, ¬p m := by
  simp only [← succ_le_iff, le_find_iff, succ_le_succ_iff]

@[simp] lemma find_eq_zero (h : ∃ n : ℕ, p n) : Nat.find h = 0 ↔ p 0 := by simp [find_eq_iff]

/-- If a predicate `q` holds at some `x` and implies `p` up to that `x`, then
the earliest `xq` such that `q xq` is at least the smallest `xp` where `p xp`.
The stronger version of `Nat.find_mono`, since this one needs
implication only up to `Nat.find _` while the other requires `q` implying `p` everywhere. -/

name: findGreatest_eq_iff
statement: theorem findGreatest_eq_iff  : Nat.findGreatest P k = m ↔ m ≤ k ∧ (m ≠ 0 → P m) ∧ ∀ ⦃n⦄, m < n → n ≤ k → ¬P n
proof:
induction k generalizing m with
  | zero =>
    rw [eq_comm, Iff.comm]
    simp only [Nat.le_zero, ne_eq, findGreatest_zero, and_iff_left_iff_imp]
    rintro rfl
    exact ⟨fun h ↦ (h rfl).elim, fun n hlt heq ↦ by lia⟩
  | succ k ihk =>
    by_cases hk : P (k + 1)
    · rw [findGreatest_eq hk]
      constructor
      · rintro rfl
        exact ⟨le_refl _, fun _ ↦ hk, fun n hlt hle ↦ by lia⟩
      · rintro ⟨hle, h0, hm⟩
        rcases Decidable.lt_or_eq_of_le hle with hlt | rfl
        exacts [(hm hlt (le_refl _) hk).elim, rfl]
    · rw [findGreatest_of_not hk, ihk]
      grind

name: findGreatest_eq_zero_iff
statement: theorem findGreatest_eq_zero_iff  : Nat.findGreatest P k = 0 ↔ ∀ ⦃n⦄, 0 < n → n ≤ k → ¬P n
proof:
simp [findGreatest_eq_iff]

@[simp] lemma findGreatest_pos : 0 < Nat.findGreatest P k ↔ ∃ n, 0 < n ∧ n ≤ k ∧ P n := by
  rw [Nat.pos_iff_ne_zero, Ne, findGreatest_eq_zero_iff]; push Not; rfl

name: minSqFac_has_prop
statement: theorem minSqFac_has_prop (n : ℕ) : MinSqFacProp n (minSqFac n)
proof:
dsimp only [minSqFac]; split_ifs with d2 d4
  · exact ⟨prime_two, (dvd_div_iff_mul_dvd d2).1 d4, fun p pp _ => pp.two_le⟩
  · rcases Nat.eq_zero_or_pos n with rfl | n0
    · cases d4 (by decide)
    refine minSqFacProp_div _ prime_two d2 (mt (dvd_div_iff_mul_dvd d2).2 d4) ?_
    refine minSqFacAux_has_prop 3 (Nat.div_pos (le_of_dvd n0 d2) (by decide)) 0 rfl ?_
    refine fun p pp dp => succ_le_of_lt (lt_of_le_of_ne pp.two_le ?_)
    rintro rfl
    contradiction
  · rcases Nat.eq_zero_or_pos n with rfl | n0
    · cases d2 (by decide)
    refine minSqFacAux_has_prop _ n0 0 rfl ?_
    refine fun p pp dp => succ_le_of_lt (lt_of_le_of_ne pp.two_le ?_)
    rintro rfl
    contradiction

name: squarefree_two
statement: theorem squarefree_two  : Squarefree 2
proof: rw [squarefree_iff_nodup_primeFactorsList] <;> simp

name: sq_mul_squarefree
statement: theorem sq_mul_squarefree (n : ℕ) : ∃ a b : ℕ, b ^ 2 * a = n ∧ Squarefree a
proof:
rcases n with - | n
  · exact ⟨1, 0, by simp, squarefree_one⟩
  · obtain ⟨a, b, -, -, h₁, h₂⟩ := sq_mul_squarefree_of_pos (succ_pos n)
    exact ⟨a, b, h₁, h₂⟩

/-- `Squarefree` is multiplicative. Note that the → direction does not require `hmn`
and generalizes to arbitrary commutative monoids. See `Squarefree.of_mul_left` and
`Squarefree.of_mul_right` above for auxiliary lemmas. -/

name: prod_primeFactors_of_squarefree
statement: theorem prod_primeFactors_of_squarefree (hn : Squarefree n) : ∏ p ∈ n.primeFactors, p = n
proof:
rw [← toFinset_factors, List.prod_toFinset _ hn.nodup_primeFactorsList,
    List.map_id', Nat.prod_primeFactorsList hn.ne_zero]

name: primeFactors_prod
statement: theorem primeFactors_prod (hs : ∀ p ∈ s, p.Prime) : primeFactors (∏ p ∈ s, p) = s
proof:
have hn : ∏ p ∈ s, p ≠ 0 := prod_ne_zero_iff.2 fun p hp ↦ (hs _ hp).ne_zero
  ext p
  rw [mem_primeFactors_of_ne_zero hn, and_congr_right (fun hp ↦ hp.prime.dvd_finsetProd_iff _)]
  refine ⟨?_, fun hp ↦ ⟨hs _ hp, _, hp, dvd_rfl⟩⟩
  rintro ⟨hp, q, hq, hpq⟩
  rwa [← ((hs _ hq).dvd_iff_eq hp.ne_one).1 hpq]

name: dist_add_add_right
statement: theorem dist_add_add_right (n k m : ℕ) : dist (n + k) (m + k) = dist n m
proof: unfold Nat.dist; lia

name: dist_add_add_left
statement: theorem dist_add_add_left (k n m : ℕ) : dist (k + n) (k + m) = dist n m
proof: unfold Nat.dist; lia

name: dist_mul_right
statement: theorem dist_mul_right (n k m : ℕ) : dist (n * k) (m * k) = dist n m * k
proof: rw [dist, dist, right_distrib, tsub_mul n, tsub_mul m]

name: dist_mul_left
statement: theorem dist_mul_left (k n m : ℕ) : dist (k * n) (k * m) = k * dist n m
proof: rw [mul_comm k n, mul_comm k m, dist_mul_right, mul_comm]

name: zero_union_range_succ
statement: theorem zero_union_range_succ  : {0} ∪ range succ = univ
proof:
ext n
  cases n <;> simp

@[simp]
protected theorem range_succ : range succ = { i | 0 < i } := by
  ext (_ | i) <;> simp

variable {α : Type*}

name: range_of_succ
statement: theorem range_of_succ (f : ℕ → α) : {f 0} ∪ range (f ∘ succ) = range f
proof: rw [← image_singleton, range_comp, ← image_union, zero_union_range_succ, image_univ]

name: totient_eq_card_lt_and_coprime
statement: theorem totient_eq_card_lt_and_coprime (n : ℕ) : φ n = Nat.card { m | m < n ∧ n.Coprime m }

name: filter_coprime_Ico_eq_totient
statement: theorem filter_coprime_Ico_eq_totient (a n : ℕ) : #{x ∈ Ico n (n + a) | a.Coprime x} = totient a
proof:
rw [totient, filter_Ico_card_eq_of_periodic, count_eq_card_filter_range]
  exact periodic_coprime a

name: sum_totient
statement: theorem sum_totient (n : ℕ) : n.divisors.sum φ = n
proof:
rcases n.eq_zero_or_pos with (rfl | hn)
  · simp
  rw [← sum_div_divisors n φ]
  have : n = ∑ d ∈ n.divisors, #{k ∈ range n | n.gcd k = d} := by
    nth_rw 1 [← card_range n]
    refine card_eq_sum_card_fiberwise fun x _ => mem_divisors.2 ⟨?_, hn.ne'⟩
    apply gcd_dvd_left
  nth_rw 3 [this]
  exact sum_congr rfl fun x hx => totient_div_of_dvd (dvd_of_mem_divisors hx)

name: prime_iff_card_units
statement: theorem prime_iff_card_units (p : ℕ) : p.Prime ↔ Fintype.card (ZMod p)ˣ = p - 1
proof:
rcases eq_zero_or_neZero p with rfl | hp
  · simp [ZMod, not_prime_zero, zero_tsub]
  rw [ZMod.card_units_eq_totient, Nat.totient_eq_iff_prime <| NeZero.pos p]

@[simp]

name: totient_coprime_totient_iff
statement: theorem totient_coprime_totient_iff (m n : ℕ) : (φ m).Coprime (φ n) ↔ (m = 1 ∨ m = 2) ∨ (n = 1 ∨ n = 2)
proof:
constructor
  · rw [← not_imp_not]
    simp_rw [← odd_totient_iff, not_or, not_odd_iff_even, even_iff_two_dvd]
    exact fun h ↦ Nat.not_coprime_of_dvd_of_dvd one_lt_two h.1 h.2
  · simp_rw [← totient_eq_one_iff]
    rintro (h | h) <;> rw [h]
    exacts [Nat.coprime_one_left _, Nat.coprime_one_right _]

/-! ### Euler's product formula for the totient function

We prove several different statements of this formula. -/


/-- Euler's product formula for the totient function. -/

name: totient_mul_prod_primeFactors
statement: theorem totient_mul_prod_primeFactors (n : ℕ) : (φ n * ∏ p ∈ n.primeFactors, p) = n * ∏ p ∈ n.primeFactors, (p - 1)
proof:
by_cases hn : n = 0; · simp [hn]
  rw [totient_eq_prod_factorization hn]
  nth_rw 3 [← prod_factorization_pow_eq_self hn]
  simp only [prod_primeFactors_prod_factorization, ← Finsupp.prod_mul]
  refine Finsupp.prod_congr (M := ℕ) (N := ℕ) fun p hp => ?_
  rw [Finsupp.mem_support_iff, ← zero_lt_iff] at hp
  rw [mul_comm, ← mul_assoc, ← pow_succ', Nat.sub_one, Nat.succ_pred_eq_of_pos hp]

/-- Euler's product formula for the totient function. -/

name: totient_eq_div_primeFactors_mul
statement: theorem totient_eq_div_primeFactors_mul (n : ℕ) : φ n = (n / ∏ p ∈ n.primeFactors, p) * ∏ p ∈ n.primeFactors, (p - 1)
proof:
rw [← mul_div_left n.totient, totient_mul_prod_primeFactors, mul_comm,
    Nat.mul_div_assoc _ (prod_primeFactors_dvd n), mul_comm]
  exact prod_pos (fun p => pos_of_mem_primeFactors)

/-- Euler's product formula for the totient function. -/

name: totient_eq_mul_prod_factors
statement: theorem totient_eq_mul_prod_factors (n : ℕ) : (φ n : ℚ) = n * ∏ p ∈ n.primeFactors, (1 - (p : ℚ)⁻¹)
proof:
by_cases hn : n = 0
  · simp [hn]
  have hn' : (n : ℚ) ≠ 0 := by simp [hn]
  have hpQ : (∏ p ∈ n.primeFactors, (p : ℚ)) ≠ 0 := by
    rw [← cast_prod, cast_ne_zero, ← zero_lt_iff, prod_primeFactors_prod_factorization]
    exact prod_pos fun p hp => pos_of_mem_primeFactors hp
  simp only [totient_eq_div_primeFactors_mul n, prod_primeFactors_dvd n, cast_mul, cast_prod,
    cast_div_charZero, mul_comm_div, mul_right_inj' hn', div_eq_iff hpQ, ← prod_mul_distrib]
  refine prod_congr rfl fun p hp => ?_
  have hp := pos_of_mem_primeFactorsList (List.mem_toFinset.mp hp)
  have hp' : (p : ℚ) ≠ 0 := cast_ne_zero.mpr hp.ne.symm
  rw [sub_mul, one_mul, mul_comm, mul_inv_cancel₀ hp', cast_pred hp]

name: totient_gcd_mul_totient_mul
statement: theorem totient_gcd_mul_totient_mul (a b : ℕ) : φ (a.gcd b) * φ (a * b) = φ a * φ b * a.gcd b
proof:
have shuffle :
    ∀ a1 a2 b1 b2 c1 c2 : ℕ,
      b1 ∣ a1 → b2 ∣ a2 → a1 / b1 * c1 * (a2 / b2 * c2) = a1 * a2 / (b1 * b2) * (c1 * c2) := by
    intro a1 a2 b1 b2 c1 c2 h1 h2
    calc
      a1 / b1 * c1 * (a2 / b2 * c2) = a1 / b1 * (a2 / b2) * (c1 * c2) := by apply mul_mul_mul_comm
      _ = a1 * a2 / (b1 * b2) * (c1 * c2) := by
        congr 1
        exact div_mul_div_comm h1 h2
  simp only [totient_eq_div_primeFactors_mul]
  rw [shuffle, shuffle]
  rotate_left
  repeat' apply prod_primeFactors_dvd
  simp only [prod_primeFactors_gcd_mul_prod_primeFactors_mul]
  rw [eq_comm, mul_comm, ← mul_assoc, ← Nat.mul_div_assoc]
  exact mul_dvd_mul (prod_primeFactors_dvd a) (prod_primeFactors_dvd b)

name: totient_super_multiplicative
statement: theorem totient_super_multiplicative (a b : ℕ) : φ a * φ b ≤ φ (a * b)
proof:
let d := a.gcd b
  rcases eq_zero_or_pos a with (rfl | ha0)
  · simp
  have hd0 : 0 < d := Nat.gcd_pos_of_pos_left _ ha0
  apply le_of_mul_le_mul_right _ hd0
  grw [← totient_gcd_mul_totient_mul a b, mul_comm, d.totient_le]

@[gcongr]

name: bitIndices_two_mul_add_one
statement: theorem bitIndices_two_mul_add_one (n : ℕ) : bitIndices (2 * n + 1) = 0 :: (bitIndices n).map (· + 1)
proof:
rw [← bitIndices_bit_true, bit_true]

@[simp] theorem bitIndices_two_mul (n : ℕ) :
    bitIndices (2 * n) = (bitIndices n).map (· + 1) := by
  rw [← bitIndices_bit_false, bit_false]

@[simp] theorem bitIndices_sorted {n : ℕ} : n.bitIndices.SortedLT := by
  induction n using binaryRec with
  | zero => simp [sortedLT_iff_pairwise]
  | bit b n hs =>
    suffices List.Pairwise (fun a b ↦ a < b) n.bitIndices by
      cases b <;> simpa [sortedLT_iff_pairwise, bit_false, bit_true, List.pairwise_map]
    exact List.Pairwise.imp (by simp) hs.pairwise

@[simp] theorem bitIndices_nodup {n : ℕ} : n.bitIndices.Nodup := bitIndices_sorted.pairwise.nodup

@[simp] theorem bitIndices_two_pow_mul (k n : ℕ) :
    bitIndices (2 ^ k * n) = (bitIndices n).map (· + k) := by
  induction k with
  | zero => simp
  | succ k ih =>
    rw [add_comm, pow_add, pow_one, mul_assoc, bitIndices_two_mul, ih, List.map_map, comp_add_right]
    simp [add_comm (a := 1)]

@[simp] theorem bitIndices_two_pow (k : ℕ) : bitIndices (2 ^ k) = [k] := by
  rw [← mul_one (a := 2 ^ k), bitIndices_two_pow_mul]; simp

@[simp] theorem sum_map_two_pow_bitIndices (n : ℕ) :
    (n.bitIndices.map (fun i ↦ 2 ^ i)).sum = n := by
  induction n using binaryRec with
  | zero => simp
  | bit b n hs =>
    have hrw : (fun i ↦ 2 ^ i) ∘ (fun x ↦ x + 1) = fun i ↦ 2 * 2 ^ i := by
      ext i; simp [pow_add, mul_comm]
    cases b
    · simpa [hrw, List.sum_map_mul_left]
    simp [hrw, List.sum_map_mul_left, hs, add_comm (a := 1)]

@[deprecated (since := "2026-05-15")] alias twoPowSum_bitIndices := sum_map_two_pow_bitIndices

@[simp] theorem mem_bitIndices {i n : ℕ} : i ∈ n.bitIndices ↔ n.testBit i := by
  induction n using Nat.binaryRec generalizing i with
  | zero => simp
  | bit b n ih => cases b <;> cases i <;> simp_all [Nat.testBit_add_one, Nat.mul_add_div]

/--
Together with `Nat.sum_map_bitIndices_two_pow`, this implies a bijection between `ℕ` and `Finset ℕ`.
See `Finset.equivBitIndices` for this bijection.
-/

name: maxPowDvdDiv_one_right
statement: theorem maxPowDvdDiv_one_right (p : ℕ) : maxPowDvdDiv p 1 = (0, 1)
proof:
rcases eq_or_ne p 1 with rfl | hp <;> simp [maxPowDvdDiv_of_not_dvd, *]

@[simp]

name: divMaxPow_mul_pow_padicValNat
statement: theorem divMaxPow_mul_pow_padicValNat (p n : ℕ) : divMaxPow n p * p ^ padicValNat p n = n
proof:
unfold divMaxPow padicValNat
  fun_cases maxPowDvdDiv with
  | case1 h => exact go_spec h |>.1
  | case2 h => simp

@[simp]

name: pow_padicValNat_mul_divMaxPow
statement: theorem pow_padicValNat_mul_divMaxPow (p n : ℕ) : p ^ padicValNat p n * divMaxPow n p = n
proof: rw [Nat.mul_comm, divMaxPow_mul_pow_padicValNat]

name: dvd_choose_pow_iff
statement: theorem dvd_choose_pow_iff (hp : Prime p) : p ∣ (p ^ n).choose k ↔ k ≠ 0 ∧ k ≠ p ^ n
proof:
refine ⟨fun h => ⟨?_, ?_⟩, fun h => dvd_choose_pow hp h.1 h.2⟩ <;> rintro rfl <;>
    simp [hp.ne_one] at h

end Prime

name: emultiplicity_two_factorial_lt
statement: theorem emultiplicity_two_factorial_lt  : ∀ {n : ℕ} (_ : n ≠ 0), emultiplicity 2 n ! < n
proof:
have h2 := prime_two.prime
  refine binaryRec ?_ ?_
  · exact fun h => False.elim <| h rfl
  · intro b n ih h
    by_cases hn : n = 0
    · subst hn
      simp only [ne_eq, bit_eq_zero_iff, true_and, Bool.not_eq_false] at h
      simp only [bit, h, cond_true, mul_zero, zero_add, factorial_one]
      rw [Prime.emultiplicity_one]
      · exact zero_lt_one
      · decide
    have : emultiplicity 2 (2 * n)! < (2 * n : ℕ) := by
      rw [prime_two.emultiplicity_factorial_mul]
      rw [two_mul]
      push_cast
      apply WithTop.add_lt_add_right _ (ih hn)
      exact Ne.symm nofun
    cases b
    · simpa
    · suffices emultiplicity 2 (2 * n + 1) + emultiplicity 2 (2 * n)! < ↑(2 * n) + 1 by
        simpa [emultiplicity_mul, h2, prime_two, bit, factorial]
      rw [emultiplicity_eq_zero.2 (two_not_dvd_two_mul_add_one n), zero_add]
      refine this.trans ?_
      exact mod_cast lt_succ_self _

end Nat

name: bitwise_zero_left
statement: theorem bitwise_zero_left (m : Nat) : bitwise f 0 m = if f false true then m else 0
proof:
simp [bitwise]

@[simp]

name: bitwise_zero_right
statement: theorem bitwise_zero_right (n : Nat) : bitwise f n 0 = if f true false then n else 0
proof:
unfold bitwise
  simp only [ite_self, Nat.zero_div, ite_true, ite_eq_right_iff]
  rintro ⟨⟩
  split_ifs <;> rfl

name: bitwise_zero
statement: theorem bitwise_zero  : bitwise f 0 0 = 0
proof: simp only [bitwise_zero_right, ite_self]

name: bit_mod_two_eq_zero_iff
statement: theorem bit_mod_two_eq_zero_iff (a x) : bit a x % 2 = 0 ↔ !a
proof: simp

name: bit_mod_two_eq_one_iff
statement: theorem bit_mod_two_eq_one_iff (a x) : bit a x % 2 = 1 ↔ a
proof:
simp

@[simp]

name: testBit_eq_inth
statement: theorem testBit_eq_inth (n i : ℕ) : n.testBit i = n.bits.getI i
proof:
induction i generalizing n with
  | zero =>
    simp only [testBit, shiftRight_zero, one_and_eq_mod_two, mod_two_of_bodd,
      bodd_eq_bits_head, List.getI_zero_eq_headI]
    cases List.headI (bits n) <;> rfl
  | succ i ih =>
    conv_lhs => rw [← bit_bodd_div2 n]
    rw [testBit_bit_succ, ih n.div2, div2_bits_eq_tail]
    cases n.bits <;> simp

name: and_two_pow
statement: theorem and_two_pow (n i : ℕ) : n &&& 2 ^ i = (n.testBit i).toNat * 2 ^ i
proof:
refine eq_of_testBit_eq fun j => ?_
  obtain rfl | hij := Decidable.eq_or_ne i j <;> cases h : n.testBit i
  · simp [h]
  · simp [h]
  · simp [testBit_two_pow_of_ne hij]
  · simp [testBit_two_pow_of_ne hij]

name: two_pow_and
statement: theorem two_pow_and (n i : ℕ) : 2 ^ i &&& n = 2 ^ i * (n.testBit i).toNat
proof:
rw [mul_comm, land_comm, and_two_pow]

/-- Proving associativity of bitwise operations in general essentially boils down to a huge case
    distinction, so it is shorter to use this tactic instead of proving it in the general case. -/
macro "bitwise_assoc_tac" : tactic => set_option hygiene false in `(tactic| (
  induction n using Nat.binaryRec generalizing m k with | zero => simp | bit b n hn => ?_
  induction m using Nat.binaryRec with | zero => simp | bit b' m hm => ?_
  induction k using Nat.binaryRec <;>
    simp [hn, Bool.or_assoc, Bool.and_assoc, Bool.bne_eq_xor]))

name: two_mul_odd_div_two
statement: theorem two_mul_odd_div_two (hn : n % 2 = 1) : 2 * (n / 2) = n - 1

/-- A natural number `m` divides the sum `m + n` if and only if `m` divides `n`. -/
@[simp] protected lemma dvd_add_self_left : m ∣ m + n ↔ m ∣ n := Nat.dvd_add_right (Nat.dvd_refl m)

/-- A natural number `m` divides the sum `n + m` if and only if `m` divides `n`. -/
@[simp] protected lemma dvd_add_self_right : m ∣ n + m ↔ m ∣ n := Nat.dvd_add_left (Nat.dvd_refl m)

/-- Two natural numbers are equal if and only if they have the same multiples. -/

name: log_div_base
statement: theorem log_div_base (b n : ℕ) : log b (n / b) = log b n - 1
proof:
rcases le_or_gt b 1 with hb | hb
  · rw [log_of_left_le_one hb, log_of_left_le_one hb, Nat.zero_sub]
  rcases lt_or_ge n b with h | h
  · rw [div_eq_of_lt h, log_of_lt h, log_zero_right]
  rw [log_of_one_lt_of_le hb h, Nat.add_sub_cancel_right]

name: log_div_base_pow
statement: theorem log_div_base_pow (b n k : ℕ) : log b (n / b ^ k) = log b n - k
proof:
induction k with
  | zero => grind
  | succ k hk => rw [Nat.pow_succ, ← Nat.div_div_eq_div_mul, log_div_base, hk, sub_add_eq]

@[simp]

name: log_div_mul_self
statement: theorem log_div_mul_self (b n : ℕ) : log b (n / b * b) = log b n
proof:
rcases le_or_gt b 1 with hb | hb
  · rw [log_of_left_le_one hb, log_of_left_le_one hb]
  rcases lt_or_ge n b with h | h
  · rw [div_eq_of_lt h, Nat.zero_mul, log_zero_right, log_of_lt h]
  rw [log_mul_base hb (Nat.div_pos h (by lia)).ne', log_div_base,
    Nat.sub_add_cancel (succ_le_iff.2 <| log_pos hb h)]

name: log_pow_left
statement: theorem log_pow_left (b k n : ℕ) : log (b ^ k) n = log b n / k
proof:
rcases eq_or_ne n 0 with rfl | hn
  · simp
  · rcases k.eq_zero_or_pos with rfl | hk
    · simp
    · rcases Nat.lt_or_ge 1 b with hb | hb
      · refine eq_of_forall_le_iff fun c ↦ ?_
        rw [le_log_iff_pow_le (Nat.one_lt_pow (Nat.ne_of_gt hk) hb) hn, Nat.le_div_iff_mul_le hk,
          le_log_iff_pow_le hb hn, Nat.pow_mul']
      · rw [log_of_left_le_one hb, Nat.zero_div, log_of_left_le_one]
        rwa [Nat.pow_le_one_iff (Nat.ne_of_gt hk)]

/-! ### Ceil logarithm -/


/-- `clog b n`, is the upper logarithm of natural number `n` in base `b`. It returns the smallest
`k : ℕ` such that `n ≤ b^k`, so if `b^k = n`, it returns exactly `k`. -/
@[pp_nodot]

name: log_le_clog
statement: theorem log_le_clog (b n : ℕ) : log b n ≤ clog b n
proof:
obtain hb | hb := le_or_gt b 1
  · rw [log_of_left_le_one hb]
    exact zero_le _
  cases n with
  | zero =>
    rw [log_zero_right]
    exact zero_le _
  | succ n =>
    exact (Nat.pow_le_pow_iff_right hb).1
      ((pow_log_le_self b n.succ_ne_zero).trans <| le_pow_clog hb _)

name: clog_pow_left
statement: theorem clog_pow_left (b k n : ℕ) : clog (b ^ k) n = (clog b n + (k - 1)) / k
proof:
rcases k.eq_zero_or_pos with rfl | hk
  · simp
  · rcases Nat.lt_or_ge 1 b with hb | hb
    · refine eq_of_forall_lt_iff fun c ↦ ?_
      rw [lt_clog_iff_pow_lt (Nat.one_lt_pow (Nat.ne_of_gt hk) hb), Nat.lt_div_iff_mul_lt hk,
        Nat.add_sub_cancel, lt_clog_iff_pow_lt hb, Nat.pow_mul']
    · suffices (k - 1) / k = 0 by grind [clog_of_left_le_one, Nat.pow_le_one_iff]
      apply Nat.div_eq_of_lt
      grind

end Nat

name: count_nth_zero
statement: theorem count_nth_zero  : count p (nth p 0) = 0

  exact fun n h₁ h₂ => (mem_range.1 h₁).not_ge (Nat.sInf_le h₂)

name: filter_range_nth_subset_insert
statement: theorem filter_range_nth_subset_insert (k : ℕ) : {n ∈ range (nth p (k + 1)) | p n} ⊆ insert (nth p k) {n ∈ range (nth p k) | p n}
proof:
intro a ha
  simp only [mem_insert, mem_filter, mem_range] at ha ⊢
  exact (le_nth_of_lt_nth_succ ha.1 ha.2).eq_or_lt.imp_right fun h => ⟨h, ha.2⟩

name: nth_count_eq_sInf
statement: theorem nth_count_eq_sInf (n : ℕ) : nth p (count p n) = sInf {i : ℕ | p i ∧ n ≤ i}
proof:
refine (nth_eq_sInf _ _).trans (congr_arg sInf ?_)
  refine Set.ext fun a => and_congr_right fun hpa => ?_
  refine ⟨fun h => not_lt.1 fun ha => ?_, fun hn k hk => lt_of_lt_of_le (nth_lt_of_lt_count hk) hn⟩
  have hn : nth p (count p a) < a := h _ (count_strict_mono hpa ha)
  rwa [nth_count hpa, lt_self_iff_false] at hn

  rw [← shiftRight_add, Nat.add_comm] at this

    x ^ (a - 1) = x ^ a / x := by
  rw [← Nat.pow_div (one_le_iff_ne_zero.mpr ha) (Nat.pos_iff_ne_zero.mpr hx), Nat.pow_one]

/-!
### Recursion and induction principles

This section is here due to dependencies -- the lemmas here require some of the lemmas
proved above, and some of the results in later sections depend on the definitions in this section.
-/

name: two_mul_sq_add_one_le_two_pow_two_mul
statement: theorem two_mul_sq_add_one_le_two_pow_two_mul (k : ℕ) : 2 * k ^ 2 + 1 ≤ 2 ^ (2 * k)
proof:
obtain rfl | hk : k = 0 ∨ k > 0 := k.eq_zero_or_pos
  · decide
  · have hk0 : 0 < 2 * k ^ 2 := Nat.mul_pos Nat.two_pos (Nat.pow_pos hk)
    calc 2 * k ^ 2
      _ < 2 * k ^ 2 + 2 * k ^ 2 := Nat.lt_add_of_pos_left hk0
      _ = (2 * k) ^ 2 := by rw [Nat.mul_pow, ← Nat.add_mul]
      _ ≤ (2 ^ k) ^ 2 := Nat.pow_le_pow_left (Nat.mul_le_pow (by decide : 2 ≠ 1) _) 2
      _ = 2 ^ (2 * k) := (Nat.pow_mul' _ _ _).symm

end Nat
