name: gcd_eq
statement: theorem gcd_eq (h : a ≡ b [MOD m]) : gcd a m = gcd b m
proof:
have h1 := gcd_dvd_right a m
  have h2 := gcd_dvd_right b m
  exact
    dvd_antisymm (dvd_gcd ((h.dvd_iff h1).mp (gcd_dvd_left a m)) h1)
      (dvd_gcd ((h.dvd_iff h2).mpr (gcd_dvd_left b m)) h2)

name: pair_unpair
statement: theorem pair_unpair (n : ℕ) : pair (unpair n).1 (unpair n).2 = n
proof:
dsimp only [unpair]; let s := sqrt n
  have sm : s * s + (n - s * s) = n := Nat.add_sub_cancel' (sqrt_le _)
  split_ifs with h
  · simp [s, pair, h, sm]
  · have hl : n - s * s - s ≤ s := Nat.sub_le_iff_le_add.2
      (Nat.sub_le_iff_le_add'.2 <| by rw [← Nat.add_assoc]; apply sqrt_le_add)
    simp [s, pair, hl.not_gt, Nat.add_assoc, Nat.add_sub_cancel' (le_of_not_gt h), sm]

name: unpair_pair
statement: theorem unpair_pair (a b : ℕ) : unpair (pair a b) = (a, b)
proof:
dsimp only [pair]; split_ifs with h
  · show unpair (b * b + a) = (a, b)
    have be : sqrt (b * b + a) = b := sqrt_add_eq _ (le_trans (le_of_lt h) (Nat.le_add_left _ _))
    simp [unpair, be, Nat.add_sub_cancel_left, h]
  · show unpair (a * a + a + b) = (a, b)
    have ae : sqrt (a * a + (a + b)) = a := by
      rw [sqrt_add_eq]
      exact Nat.add_le_add_left (le_of_not_gt h) _
    simp [unpair, ae, Nat.add_assoc, Nat.add_sub_cancel_left]

/-- An equivalence between `ℕ × ℕ` and `ℕ`. -/
@[simps -fullyApplied]

name: unpair_zero
statement: theorem unpair_zero  : unpair 0 = 0
proof:
rw [unpair]
  simp

name: right_le_pair
statement: theorem right_le_pair (a b : ℕ) : b ≤ pair a b
proof:
by_cases h : a < b
  · simpa [pair, h] using le_trans (le_mul_self _) (Nat.le_add_right _ _)
  · simp [pair, h]

name: unpair_right_le
statement: theorem unpair_right_le (n : ℕ) : (unpair n).2 ≤ n
proof: simpa using right_le_pair n.unpair.1 n.unpair.2

name: pair_lt_max_add_one_sq
statement: theorem pair_lt_max_add_one_sq (m n : ℕ) : pair m n < (max m n + 1) ^ 2
proof:
simp only [pair, Nat.pow_two, Nat.mul_add, Nat.add_mul, Nat.mul_one, Nat.one_mul, Nat.add_assoc]
  split_ifs <;> simp [Nat.le_of_lt, not_lt.1, *] <;> lia

name: max_sq_add_min_le_pair
statement: theorem max_sq_add_min_le_pair (m n : ℕ) : max m n ^ 2 + min m n ≤ pair m n
proof:
rw [pair]
  rcases lt_or_ge m n with h | h
  · rw [if_pos h, max_eq_right h.le, min_eq_left h.le, Nat.pow_two]
  rw [if_neg h.not_gt, max_eq_left h, min_eq_right h, Nat.pow_two, Nat.add_assoc,
    Nat.add_le_add_iff_left]
  exact Nat.le_add_left _ _

name: add_le_pair
statement: theorem add_le_pair (m n : ℕ) : m + n ≤ pair m n
proof:
simp only [pair, Nat.add_assoc]
  split_ifs
  · have := le_mul_self n
    lia
  · exact Nat.le_add_left _ _

name: lt_size_self
statement: theorem lt_size_self (n : ℕ) : n < 2 ^ size n
proof:
induction n using binaryRec' with
  | zero => simp
  | bit b n h IH =>
    rw [← Nat.bit_ne_zero_iff] at h
    rwa [size_bit h, bit_lt_two_pow_succ_iff]

name: size_eq_bits_len
statement: theorem size_eq_bits_len (n : ℕ) : n.bits.length = n.size
proof:
induction n using Nat.binaryRec' with
  | zero => simp
  | bit _ _ h ih =>
    rw [size_bit, bits_append_bit _ _ h]
    · simp [ih]
    · simpa [bit_eq_zero_iff]

end Nat

name: psub_add
statement: theorem psub_add (m n k) : psub m (n + k) = (do psub (← psub m n) k)
proof:
induction k with
    | zero => simp
    | succ n ih => simp only [ih, add_succ, psub_succ, bind_assoc]

/-- Same as `psub`, but with a more efficient implementation. -/
@[inline]

name: sqrt_isSqrt
statement: theorem sqrt_isSqrt (n : ℕ) : IsSqrt n (sqrt n)
proof:
match n with
  | 0 => simp [IsSqrt, sqrt]
  | 1 => simp [IsSqrt, sqrt]
  | n + 2 =>
    have h : ¬ (n + 2) ≤ 1 := by simp
    simp only [IsSqrt, sqrt, h, ite_false]
    refine ⟨sqrt.iter_sq_le _ _, sqrt.lt_iter_succ_sq _ _ ?_⟩
    simp only [Nat.mul_add, Nat.add_mul, Nat.one_mul, Nat.mul_one, ← Nat.add_assoc]
    rw [Nat.lt_add_one_iff, Nat.add_assoc, ← Nat.mul_two]
    refine le_trans (Nat.le_of_eq (div_add_mod' (n + 2) 2).symm) ?_
    rw [show (n + 2) / 2 * 2 + (n + 2) % 2 = n + 2 by grind]
    simp only [shiftLeft_eq, Nat.one_mul]
    refine Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_trans lt_log2_self (le_add_right_of_le ?_))
    rw [← Nat.pow_add]
    grind

name: sqrt_le_add
statement: theorem sqrt_le_add (n : ℕ) : n ≤ sqrt n * sqrt n + sqrt n + sqrt n
proof: rw [← succ_mul]; exact le_of_lt_succ (lt_succ_sqrt n)

name: not_bddAbove_setOf_prime
statement: theorem not_bddAbove_setOf_prime  : ¬BddAbove { p | Prime p }
proof:
rw [not_bddAbove_iff]
  intro n
  obtain ⟨p, hi, hp⟩ := exists_infinite_primes n.succ
  exact ⟨p, hp, hi⟩

end Infinite

end Nat

name: minFac_one
statement: theorem minFac_one  : minFac 1 = 1
proof:
simp [minFac, minFacAux]

@[simp]

name: minFac_two
statement: theorem minFac_two  : minFac 2 = 2
proof: simp [minFac]

name: minFac_pos
statement: theorem minFac_pos (n : ℕ) : 0 < minFac n
proof:
by_cases n1 : n = 1
  · simp [n1]
  · exact (minFac_prime n1).pos

name: decidablePrime_csimp
statement: theorem decidablePrime_csimp  : @decidablePrime = @decidablePrime'
proof: subsingleton

name: minFac_eq_two_iff
statement: theorem minFac_eq_two_iff (n : ℕ) : minFac n = 2 ↔ 2 ∣ n
proof:
constructor
  · intro h
    rw [← h]
    exact minFac_dvd n
  · intro h
    have ub := minFac_le_of_dvd (le_refl 2) h
    have lb := minFac_pos n
    refine ub.eq_or_lt.resolve_right fun h' => ?_
    suffices n.minFac = 1 by simp_all
    exact (le_antisymm (Nat.succ_le_of_lt lb) (Nat.lt_succ_iff.mp h')).symm

name: coprime_two_left
statement: theorem coprime_two_left  : Coprime 2 n ↔ Odd n
proof:
rw [prime_two.coprime_iff_not_dvd, ← not_even_iff_odd, even_iff_two_dvd]

@[simp] lemma coprime_two_right : n.Coprime 2 ↔ Odd n := coprime_comm.trans coprime_two_left

protected alias ⟨Coprime.odd_of_left, _root_.Odd.coprime_two_left⟩ := coprime_two_left
protected alias ⟨Coprime.odd_of_right, _root_.Odd.coprime_two_right⟩ := coprime_two_right

name: pow_sub_one_mod_pow_sub_one
statement: theorem pow_sub_one_mod_pow_sub_one (a b c : ℕ) : (a ^ c - 1) % (a ^ b - 1) = a ^ (c % b) - 1
proof:
rcases eq_zero_or_pos a with rfl | ha0
  · simp [zero_pow_eq]; split_ifs <;> simp
  rcases Nat.eq_or_lt_of_le ha0 with rfl | ha1
  · simp
  rcases eq_zero_or_pos b with rfl | hb0
  · simp
  rcases lt_or_ge c b with h | h
  · rw [mod_eq_of_lt, mod_eq_of_lt h]
    rwa [Nat.sub_lt_sub_iff_right (one_le_pow c a ha0), Nat.pow_lt_pow_iff_right ha1]
  · suffices a ^ (c - b + b) - 1 = a ^ (c - b) * (a ^ b - 1) + (a ^ (c - b) - 1) by
      rw [← Nat.sub_add_cancel h, add_mod_right, this, add_mod, mul_mod, mod_self,
        mul_zero, zero_mod, zero_add, mod_mod, pow_sub_one_mod_pow_sub_one]
    rw [← Nat.add_sub_assoc (one_le_pow (c - b) a ha0), ← mul_add_one, pow_add,
      Nat.sub_add_cancel (one_le_pow b a ha0)]

@[simp]

name: pow_sub_one_gcd_pow_sub_one
statement: theorem pow_sub_one_gcd_pow_sub_one (a b c : ℕ) : gcd (a ^ b - 1) (a ^ c - 1) = a ^ gcd b c - 1
proof:
rcases eq_zero_or_pos b with rfl | hb
  · simp
  replace hb : c % b < b := mod_lt c hb
  rw [gcd_rec, pow_sub_one_mod_pow_sub_one, pow_sub_one_gcd_pow_sub_one, ← gcd_rec]

/-! ### `lcm` and divisibility -/

name: coprime_add_mul_right_right
statement: theorem coprime_add_mul_right_right (m n k : ℕ) : Coprime m (n + k * m) ↔ Coprime m n
proof:
rw [Coprime, Coprime, gcd_add_mul_right_right]

@[simp]

name: coprime_add_mul_left_right
statement: theorem coprime_add_mul_left_right (m n k : ℕ) : Coprime m (n + m * k) ↔ Coprime m n
proof:
rw [Coprime, Coprime, gcd_add_mul_left_right]

@[simp]

name: coprime_mul_right_add_right
statement: theorem coprime_mul_right_add_right (m n k : ℕ) : Coprime m (k * m + n) ↔ Coprime m n
proof:
rw [Coprime, Coprime, gcd_mul_right_add_right]

@[simp]

name: coprime_mul_left_add_right
statement: theorem coprime_mul_left_add_right (m n k : ℕ) : Coprime m (m * k + n) ↔ Coprime m n
proof:
rw [Coprime, Coprime, gcd_mul_left_add_right]

@[simp]

name: coprime_add_mul_right_left
statement: theorem coprime_add_mul_right_left (m n k : ℕ) : Coprime (m + k * n) n ↔ Coprime m n
proof:
rw [Coprime, Coprime, gcd_add_mul_right_left]

@[simp]

name: coprime_add_mul_left_left
statement: theorem coprime_add_mul_left_left (m n k : ℕ) : Coprime (m + n * k) n ↔ Coprime m n
proof:
rw [Coprime, Coprime, gcd_add_mul_left_left]

@[simp]

name: coprime_mul_right_add_left
statement: theorem coprime_mul_right_add_left (m n k : ℕ) : Coprime (k * n + m) n ↔ Coprime m n
proof:
rw [Coprime, Coprime, gcd_mul_right_add_left]

@[simp]

name: coprime_mul_left_add_left
statement: theorem coprime_mul_left_add_left (m n k : ℕ) : Coprime (n * k + m) n ↔ Coprime m n
proof: rw [Coprime, Coprime, gcd_mul_left_add_left]

name: add_coprime_iff_left
statement: theorem add_coprime_iff_left (h : c ∣ b) : Coprime (a + b) c ↔ Coprime a c
proof: obtain ⟨n, rfl⟩ := h; simp

name: add_coprime_iff_right
statement: theorem add_coprime_iff_right (h : c ∣ a) : Coprime (a + b) c ↔ Coprime b c
proof: obtain ⟨n, rfl⟩ := h; simp

name: coprime_add_iff_left
statement: theorem coprime_add_iff_left (h : a ∣ c) : Coprime a (b + c) ↔ Coprime a b
proof: obtain ⟨n, rfl⟩ := h; simp

name: coprime_add_iff_right
statement: theorem coprime_add_iff_right (h : a ∣ b) : Coprime a (b + c) ↔ Coprime a c
proof:
obtain ⟨n, rfl⟩ := h; simp

-- TODO: Replace `Nat.Coprime.coprime_dvd_left`

name: gcd_mul_gcd_eq_iff_dvd_mul_of_coprime
statement: theorem gcd_mul_gcd_eq_iff_dvd_mul_of_coprime (hcop : Coprime n m) : gcd x n * gcd x m = x ↔ x ∣ n * m
proof:
refine ⟨fun h ↦ ?_, (dvd_antisymm ?_ <| dvd_gcd_mul_gcd_iff_dvd_mul.mpr ·)⟩
  refine h ▸ Nat.mul_dvd_mul ?_ ?_ <;> exact x.gcd_dvd_right _
  refine (hcop.gcd_both x x).mul_dvd_of_dvd_of_dvd ?_ ?_ <;> exact x.gcd_dvd_left _

name: choose_le_pow_div
statement: theorem choose_le_pow_div (r n : ℕ) : (n.choose r : α) ≤ (n ^ r : α) / r !
proof:
rw [le_div_iff₀']
  · norm_cast
    rw [← Nat.descFactorial_eq_factorial_mul_choose]
    exact n.descFactorial_le_pow r
  exact mod_cast r.factorial_pos

name: choose_le_descFactorial
statement: theorem choose_le_descFactorial (n k : ℕ) : n.choose k ≤ n.descFactorial k
proof:
rw [choose_eq_descFactorial_div_factorial]
  exact Nat.div_le_self _ _

name: pow_le_choose
statement: theorem pow_le_choose (r n : ℕ) : ((n + 1 - r : ℕ) ^ r : α) / r ! ≤ n.choose r
proof:
rw [div_le_iff₀']
  · norm_cast
    rw [← Nat.descFactorial_eq_factorial_mul_choose]
    exact n.pow_sub_le_descFactorial r
  exact mod_cast r.factorial_pos

name: choose_succ_le_two_pow
statement: theorem choose_succ_le_two_pow (n k : ℕ) : (n + 1).choose k ≤ 2 ^ n
proof:
by_cases lt : n + 1 < k
  · simp [choose_eq_zero_of_lt lt]
  · cases n with
    | zero => cases k <;> simp_all
    | succ n =>
      rcases k with - | k
      · rw [choose_zero_right]
        exact Nat.one_le_two_pow
      · rw [choose_succ_succ', two_pow_succ]
        exact Nat.add_le_add (choose_succ_le_two_pow n k) (choose_succ_le_two_pow n (k + 1))

name: choose_le_two_pow
statement: theorem choose_le_two_pow (n k : ℕ) : n.choose k ≤ 2 ^ n
proof:
obtain (rfl | hn) := eq_zero_or_pos n
  · cases k <;> simp
  · exact (Nat.choose_lt_two_pow _ _ hn).le

end Nat

name: choose_modEq_choose_mod_mul_choose_div
statement: theorem choose_modEq_choose_mod_mul_choose_div  : choose n k ≡ choose (n % p) (k % p) * choose (n / p) (k / p) [ZMOD p]
proof:
have decompose : ((X : (ZMod p)[X]) + 1) ^ n = (X + 1) ^ (n % p) * (X ^ p + 1) ^ (n / p) := by
    simpa using add_pow_eq_mul_pow_add_pow_div_char (X : (ZMod p)[X]) 1 p _
  simp only [← ZMod.intCast_eq_intCast_iff,
    ← coeff_X_add_one_pow _ n k, ← eq_intCast (Int.castRingHom (ZMod p)), ← coeff_map,
    Polynomial.map_pow, Polynomial.map_add, Polynomial.map_one, map_X, decompose]
  simp only [add_pow, one_pow, mul_one, ← pow_mul, sum_mul_sum]
  conv_lhs =>
    enter [1, 2, k, 2, k']
    rw [← mul_assoc, mul_right_comm _ _ (X ^ (p * k')), ← pow_add, mul_assoc, ← cast_mul]
  have h_iff : ∀ x ∈ range (n % p + 1) ×ˢ range (n / p + 1),
      k = x.1 + p * x.2 ↔ (k % p, k / p) = x := by
    intro ⟨x₁, x₂⟩ hx
    rw [Prod.mk.injEq]
    constructor <;> intro h
    · simp only [mem_product, mem_range] at hx
      have h' : x₁ < p := lt_of_lt_of_le hx.left <| mod_lt _ Fin.pos'
      rw [h, add_mul_mod_self_left, add_mul_div_left _ _ Fin.pos', eq_comm (b := x₂)]
      exact ⟨mod_eq_of_lt h', right_eq_add.mpr (div_eq_of_lt h')⟩
    · rw [← h.left, ← h.right, mod_add_div]
  simp only [finsetSum_coeff, coeff_mul_natCast, coeff_X_pow, ite_mul, zero_mul, ← cast_mul]
  rw [← sum_product', sum_congr rfl (fun a ha ↦ if_congr (h_iff a ha) rfl rfl), sum_ite_eq]
  split_ifs with h
  · simp
  · rw [mem_product, mem_range, mem_range, not_and_or, Nat.lt_succ_iff, not_le, not_lt] at h
    cases h <;> simp [choose_eq_zero_of_lt (by tauto)]

/-- For primes `p`, `choose n k` is congruent to `choose (n % p) (k % p) * choose (n / p) (k / p)`
modulo `p`. Also see `choose_modEq_choose_mod_mul_choose_div` for the version with `ZMOD`. -/

name: choose_modEq_choose_mod_mul_choose_div_nat
statement: theorem choose_modEq_choose_mod_mul_choose_div_nat  : choose n k ≡ choose (n % p) (k % p) * choose (n / p) (k / p) [MOD p]
proof:
rw [← Int.natCast_modEq_iff]
  exact_mod_cast choose_modEq_choose_mod_mul_choose_div

/-- For primes `p`, `choose n k` is congruent to the product of `choose (⌊n / p ^ i⌋ % p)
(⌊k / p ^ i⌋ % p)` over i < a, multiplied by `choose (⌊n / p ^ a⌋) (⌊k / p ^ a⌋)`, modulo `p`. -/

name: choose_mul_mul_modEq_choose
statement: theorem choose_mul_mul_modEq_choose  : choose (p * a) (p * b) ≡ choose a b [ZMOD p]
proof:
grw [choose_modEq_choose_mod_mul_choose_div]
  simp [NeZero.pos, Int.ModEq.refl]

/-- For primes `p`, `choose (p * a) (p * b)` is congruent to `choose a b` modulo `p`.
Also see `choose_mul_mul_modEq_choose` for the version with `ZMOD`. -/

name: choose_mul_mul_modEq_choose_nat
statement: theorem choose_mul_mul_modEq_choose_nat  : choose (p * a) (p * b) ≡ choose a b [MOD p]
proof:
rw [← Int.natCast_modEq_iff]
  exact_mod_cast choose_mul_mul_modEq_choose

/-- For primes `p`, `choose (p ^ k * a) (p ^ k * b)` is congruent to `choose a b` modulo `p`.
Also see `choose_pow_mul_pow_mul_modEq_choose_nat` for the version with `MOD`. -/

name: choose_pow_mul_pow_mul_modEq_choose
statement: theorem choose_pow_mul_pow_mul_modEq_choose  : choose (p ^ k * a) (p ^ k * b) ≡ choose a b [ZMOD p]
proof:
induction k with
  | zero => simp [Int.ModEq.refl]
  | succ k ih =>
    grw [Nat.pow_succ', mul_assoc, mul_assoc, choose_mul_mul_modEq_choose, ih]

/-- For primes `p`, `choose (p ^ k * a) (p ^ k * b)` is congruent to `choose a b` modulo `p`.
Also see `choose_pow_mul_pow_mul_modEq_choose` for the version with `ZMOD`. -/

name: choose_pow_mul_pow_mul_modEq_choose_nat
statement: theorem choose_pow_mul_pow_mul_modEq_choose_nat  : choose (p ^ k * a) (p ^ k * b) ≡ choose a b [MOD p]
proof:
rw [← Int.natCast_modEq_iff]
  exact_mod_cast choose_pow_mul_pow_mul_modEq_choose

end Choose

name: centralBinom_le_four_pow
statement: theorem centralBinom_le_four_pow (n : ℕ) : centralBinom n ≤ 4 ^ n
proof: grw [show 4 = 2 ^ 2 by rfl, ← pow_mul, centralBinom_eq_two_mul_choose, choose_le_two_pow]

name: two_dvd_centralBinom_succ
statement: theorem two_dvd_centralBinom_succ (n : ℕ) : 2 ∣ centralBinom (n + 1)
proof:
use (n + 1 + n).choose n
  rw [centralBinom_eq_two_mul_choose, two_mul, ← add_assoc,
      choose_succ_succ' (n + 1 + n) n, choose_symm_add, ← two_mul]

name: succ_dvd_centralBinom
statement: theorem succ_dvd_centralBinom (n : ℕ) : n + 1 ∣ n.centralBinom
proof:
have h_s : (n + 1).Coprime (2 * n + 1) := by
    rw [two_mul, add_assoc, coprime_add_self_right, coprime_self_add_left]
    exact coprime_one_left n
  apply h_s.dvd_of_dvd_mul_left
  apply Nat.dvd_of_mul_dvd_mul_left zero_lt_two
  rw [← mul_assoc, ← succ_mul_centralBinom_succ, mul_comm]
  exact mul_dvd_mul_left _ (two_dvd_centralBinom_succ n)

end Nat

name: sum_range_choose
statement: theorem sum_range_choose (n : ℕ) : (∑ m ∈ range (n + 1), n.choose m) = 2 ^ n
proof:
have := (add_pow 1 1 n).symm
  simpa [one_add_one_eq_two] using this

name: choose_middle_le_pow
statement: theorem choose_middle_le_pow (n : ℕ) : (2 * n + 1).choose n ≤ 4 ^ n
proof:
have t : (2 * n + 1).choose n ≤ ∑ i ∈ range (n + 1), (2 * n + 1).choose i :=
    single_le_sum (fun x _ ↦ by lia) (self_mem_range_succ n)
  simpa [sum_range_choose_halfway n] using t

name: sum_Icc_choose
statement: theorem sum_Icc_choose (n k : ℕ) : ∑ m ∈ Icc k n, m.choose k = (n + 1).choose (k + 1)
proof:
rcases lt_or_ge n k with h | h
  · rw [choose_eq_zero_of_lt (by lia), Icc_eq_empty_of_lt h, sum_empty]
  · induction n, h using le_induction with
    | base => simp
    | succ n _ ih =>
      rw [← Ico_insert_right (by lia), sum_insert (by simp), Ico_add_one_right_eq_Icc, ih,
        choose_succ_succ' (n + 1)]

/-- **Zhu Shijie's identity** aka hockey-stick identity, version with `range`.
Summing `(i + k).choose k` for `i ∈ [0, n]` gives `(n + k + 1).choose (k + 1)`.

Combinatorial interpretation: `(i + k).choose k` is the number of decompositions of `[0, i)` in
`k + 1` (possibly empty) intervals (this follows from a stars and bars description). In particular,
`(n + k + 1).choose (k + 1)` corresponds to decomposing `[0, n)` into `k + 2` intervals.
By putting away the last interval (of some length `n - i`),
we have to decompose the remaining interval `[0, i)` into `k + 1` intervals, hence the sum. -/

name: sum_range_add_choose
statement: theorem sum_range_add_choose (n k : ℕ) : ∑ i ∈ Finset.range (n + 1), (i + k).choose k = (n + k + 1).choose (k + 1)
proof:
rw [← sum_Icc_choose, range_eq_Ico]
  convert (sum_map _ (addRightEmbedding k) (·.choose k)).symm using 2
  rw [map_add_right_Ico, zero_add, add_right_comm, Ico_add_one_right_eq_Icc]

/-- Summing `i * (n.choose i)` for `i ∈ [0, n]` gives `n * 2 ^ (n - 1)`. -/

name: sum_range_mul_choose
statement: theorem sum_range_mul_choose (n : ℕ) : ∑ i ∈ Finset.range (n + 1), i * (n.choose i) = n * 2 ^ (n - 1)
proof:
by_cases h : n = 0
  · simp [h]
  apply (mul_right_inj' two_ne_zero).1
  calc
    2 * ∑ i ∈ Finset.range (n + 1), i * n.choose i
      = ∑ i ∈ Finset.range (n + 1), (n - i) * n.choose (n - i)
        + ∑ i ∈ Finset.range (n + 1), i * n.choose i := by
      rw [two_mul, ← sum_flip]
    _ = ∑ i ∈ Finset.range (n + 1), (n - i) * n.choose i
        + ∑ i ∈ Finset.range (n + 1), i * n.choose i := by
      congr! 2 with _ h'
      rw [choose_symm (mem_range_succ_iff.mp h')]
    _ = ∑ i ∈ Finset.range (n + 1), n * n.choose i := by
      rw [← sum_add_distrib]
      congr! 1 with _ h'
      rw [← add_mul, Nat.sub_add_cancel (mem_range_succ_iff.mp h')]
    _ = n * 2 ^ n := by
      rw [← mul_sum, Nat.sum_range_choose]
    _ = 2 * (n * 2 ^ (n - 1)) := by
      rw [← mul_assoc, mul_comm 2 n, mul_assoc, mul_pow_sub_one h]

name: sum_range_multichoose
statement: theorem sum_range_multichoose (n k : ℕ) : ∑ i ∈ Finset.range (n + 1), k.multichoose i = (n + k).choose k
proof:
cases k with
  | zero => simp [Finset.sum_range_succ']
  | succ k => grind [multichoose_eq, choose_symm_of_eq_add, sum_range_add_choose]

end Nat

name: sum_antidiagonal_choose_add
statement: theorem sum_antidiagonal_choose_add (d n : ℕ) : (∑ ij ∈ antidiagonal n, (d + ij.2).choose d) = (d + n + 1).choose (d + 1)
proof:
induction n with
  | zero => simp
  | succ n hn => rw [Nat.sum_antidiagonal_succ, hn, Nat.choose_succ_succ (d + (n + 1)), ← add_assoc]

end Finset

name: cast_choose_two
statement: theorem cast_choose_two (a : ℕ) : (a.choose 2 : K) = a * (a - 1) / 2
proof:
rw [← cast_descFactorial_two, descFactorial_eq_factorial_mul_choose, factorial_two, mul_comm,
    cast_mul, cast_two, eq_div_iff_mul_eq two_ne_zero]

end DivisionRing
end Nat

name: factorization_choose_le_log
statement: theorem factorization_choose_le_log  : (choose n k).factorization p ≤ log p n
proof:
by_cases h : (choose n k).factorization p = 0
  · simp [h]
  have hp : p.Prime := Not.imp_symm (choose n k).factorization_eq_zero_of_not_prime h
  have hkn : k ≤ n := by
    refine le_of_not_gt fun hnk => h ?_
    simp [choose_eq_zero_of_lt hnk]
  rw [factorization_choose hp hkn (Nat.lt_add_one _)]
  exact (card_filter_le ..).trans_eq (Nat.card_Ico _ _)

/-- A `pow` form of `Nat.factorization_choose_le` -/

name: factorization_choose_le_one
statement: theorem factorization_choose_le_one (p_large : n < p ^ 2) : (choose n k).factorization p ≤ 1
proof:
apply factorization_choose_le_log.trans
  rcases eq_or_ne n 0 with (rfl | hn0); · simp
  exact Nat.lt_succ_iff.1 (log_lt_of_lt_pow hn0 p_large)

name: factorization_factorial_eq_zero_of_lt
statement: theorem factorization_factorial_eq_zero_of_lt (h : n < p) : (factorial n).factorization p = 0
proof:
induction n with
  | zero => simp
  | succ n hn =>
    rw [factorial_succ, factorization_mul n.succ_ne_zero n.factorial_ne_zero, Finsupp.coe_add,
      Pi.add_apply, hn (lt_of_succ_lt h), add_zero, factorization_eq_zero_of_lt h]

name: factorization_choose_eq_zero_of_lt
statement: theorem factorization_choose_eq_zero_of_lt (h : n < p) : (choose n k).factorization p = 0
proof:
by_cases! hnk : n < k; · simp [choose_eq_zero_of_lt hnk]
  rw [choose_eq_factorial_div_factorial hnk,
    factorization_div (factorial_mul_factorial_dvd_factorial hnk), Finsupp.coe_tsub,
    Pi.sub_apply, factorization_factorial_eq_zero_of_lt h, zero_tsub]

/-- If a prime `p` has positive multiplicity in the `n`th central binomial coefficient,
`p` is no more than `2 * n` -/

name: prod_pow_factorization_centralBinom
statement: theorem prod_pow_factorization_centralBinom (n : ℕ) : (∏ p ∈ Finset.range (2 * n + 1), p ^ (centralBinom n).factorization p) = centralBinom n
proof:
apply prod_pow_factorization_choose
  lia

end Nat

name: multinomial_single
statement: theorem multinomial_single  : multinomial s (Pi.single a n) = 1
proof:
rw [← Nat.mul_right_inj (prod_ne_zero_iff.mpr (fun _ _ ↦ factorial_ne_zero _)), mul_one,
    multinomial_spec, sum_pi_single']
  split_ifs with ha
  · rw [Finset.prod_eq_single a (by simp_all) (by simp_all), Pi.single_eq_same]
  · rw [eq_comm, factorial_zero]
    apply Finset.prod_eq_one
    intro _ hb
    rw [Pi.single_apply, if_neg (ne_of_mem_of_not_mem hb ha), factorial_zero]

/-! ### Connection to binomial coefficients

When `Nat.multinomial` is applied to a `Finset` of two elements `{a, b}`, the
result a binomial coefficient. We use `binomial` in the names of lemmas that
involves `Nat.multinomial {a, b}`.
-/

name: multinomial_univ_two
statement: theorem multinomial_univ_two (a b : ℕ) : multinomial Finset.univ ![a, b] = (a + b)! / (a ! * b !)
proof:
rw [multinomial, Fin.sum_univ_two, Fin.prod_univ_two]
  dsimp only [Matrix.cons_val]

name: multinomial_univ_three
statement: theorem multinomial_univ_three (a b c : ℕ) : multinomial Finset.univ ![a, b, c] = (a + b + c)! / (a ! * b ! * c !)
proof:
rw [multinomial, Fin.sum_univ_three, Fin.prod_univ_three]
  rfl

end Nat

/-! ### Alternative definitions -/

namespace Finsupp

variable {α : Type*}

/-- Alternative multinomial definition based on a finsupp, using the support
  for the big operations
-/

name: countPerms_zero
statement: theorem countPerms_zero  : countPerms (0 : Multiset α) = 1
proof:
simp [countPerms, Finsupp.multinomial]

end Multiset

namespace Finset
open _root_.Nat

/-! ### Multinomial theorem -/

variable {α R : Type*} [DecidableEq α]

section Semiring
variable [Semiring R]

open scoped Function -- required for scoped `on` notation

-- TODO: Can we prove one of the following two from the other one?
/-- The **multinomial theorem**. -/

name: multinomial_two_mul_le_mul_multinomial
statement: theorem multinomial_two_mul_le_mul_multinomial  : multinomial s (fun i ↦ 2 * f i) ≤ ((∑ i ∈ s, f i) ^ ∑ i ∈ s, f i) * multinomial s f
proof:
rw [multinomial, multinomial, ← mul_sum,
    ← Nat.mul_div_assoc _ (prod_factorial_dvd_factorial_sum ..)]
  refine Nat.div_le_div_of_mul_le_mul (by positivity)
    ((prod_factorial_dvd_factorial_sum ..).trans (Nat.dvd_mul_left ..)) ?_
  calc
    (2 * ∑ i ∈ s, f i)! * ∏ i ∈ s, (f i)!
      ≤ ((2 * ∑ i ∈ s, f i) ^ (∑ i ∈ s, f i) * (∑ i ∈ s, f i)!) * ∏ i ∈ s, (f i)! := by
      gcongr; exact Nat.factorial_two_mul_le _
    _ = ((∑ i ∈ s, f i) ^ ∑ i ∈ s, f i) * (∑ i ∈ s, f i)! * ∏ i ∈ s, 2 ^ f i * (f i)! := by
      rw [mul_pow, ← prod_pow_eq_pow_sum, prod_mul_distrib]; ring
    _ ≤ ((∑ i ∈ s, f i) ^ ∑ i ∈ s, f i) * (∑ i ∈ s, f i)! * ∏ i ∈ s, (2 * f i)! := by
      gcongr
      rw [← doubleFactorial_two_mul]
      exact doubleFactorial_le_factorial _

end Nat

namespace Sym

variable {n : ℕ} {α : Type*} [DecidableEq α]

name: multinomial_singleton
statement: theorem multinomial_singleton (n : ℕ) : Multiset.multinomial {n} = 1
proof: simp [← cons_zero, multinomial_cons]

name: multinomial_add
statement: theorem multinomial_add (m m' : Multiset ℕ) : (m + m').multinomial = Nat.choose (m + m').sum m.sum * m.multinomial * m'.multinomial
proof:
induction m using Multiset.induction_on with
  | empty => simp
  | cons x m hind =>
    simp only [cons_add, sum_cons, sum_add, multinomial_cons, hind, ← mul_assoc]
    congr 2
    rw [← Nat.choose_symm (Nat.le_add_right _ _), add_tsub_cancel_left, eq_comm,
      Nat.choose_mul (Nat.le_add_right _ _), ← Nat.choose_symm (Nat.le_add_right x _)]
    simp [add_tsub_cancel_left]

name: multinomial_nsmul_singleton
statement: theorem multinomial_nsmul_singleton (k n : ℕ) : (k • {n} : Multiset ℕ).multinomial = Nat.multinomial (Finset.range k) (fun _ ↦ n)
proof:
simp [multinomial_nsmul]

end Multiset

name: choose_self
statement: theorem choose_self (n : ℕ) : choose n n = 1
proof:
induction n <;> grind

@[simp]

name: triangle_succ
statement: theorem triangle_succ (n : ℕ) : (n + 1) * (n + 1 - 1) / 2 = n * (n - 1) / 2 + n
proof:
rw [← add_mul_div_left, Nat.mul_comm 2 n, ← Nat.mul_add, Nat.add_sub_cancel, Nat.mul_comm]
  cases n <;> rfl; apply zero_lt_succ

/-- `choose n 2` is the `n`-th triangle number. -/

name: choose_two_right
statement: theorem choose_two_right (n : ℕ) : choose n 2 = n * (n - 1) / 2
proof:
induction n with
  | zero => simp
  | succ n ih =>
    rw [triangle_succ n, choose, ih]
    simp [Nat.add_comm]

name: add_choose
statement: theorem add_choose (i j : ℕ) : (i + j).choose j = (i + j)! / (i ! * j !)
proof:
rw [choose_eq_factorial_div_factorial (Nat.le_add_left j i), Nat.add_sub_cancel_right,
    Nat.mul_comm]

name: add_choose_mul_factorial_mul_factorial
statement: theorem add_choose_mul_factorial_mul_factorial (i j : ℕ) : (i + j).choose j * i ! * j ! = (i + j)!
proof:
rw [← choose_mul_factorial_mul_factorial (Nat.le_add_left _ _), Nat.add_sub_cancel_right,
    Nat.mul_right_comm]

name: factorial_mul_factorial_dvd_factorial_add
statement: theorem factorial_mul_factorial_dvd_factorial_add (i j : ℕ) : i ! * j ! ∣ (i + j)!
proof:
suffices i ! * (i + j - i)! ∣ (i + j)! by
    rwa [Nat.add_sub_cancel_left i j] at this
  exact factorial_mul_factorial_dvd_factorial (Nat.le_add_right _ _)

@[simp]

name: choose_symm_half
statement: theorem choose_symm_half (m : ℕ) : choose (2 * m + 1) (m + 1) = choose (2 * m + 1) m
proof:
apply choose_symm_of_eq_add
  rw [Nat.add_comm m 1, Nat.add_assoc 1 m m, Nat.add_comm (2 * m) 1, Nat.two_mul m]

name: choose_succ_right_eq
statement: theorem choose_succ_right_eq (n k : ℕ) : choose n (k + 1) * (k + 1) = choose n k * (n - k)
proof:
have e : (n + 1) * choose n k = choose n (k + 1) * (k + 1) + choose n k * (k + 1) := by
    rw [← Nat.add_mul, Nat.add_comm (choose _ _), ← choose_succ_succ, add_one_mul_choose_eq]
  rw [← Nat.sub_eq_of_eq_add e, Nat.mul_comm, ← Nat.mul_sub_left_distrib, Nat.add_sub_add_right]

@[simp, grind =]

name: choose_mul_succ_eq
statement: theorem choose_mul_succ_eq (n k : ℕ) : n.choose k * (n + 1) = (n + 1).choose k * (n + 1 - k)
proof:
cases k with
  | zero => simp
  | succ k =>
    obtain hk | hk := le_or_gt (k + 1) (n + 1)
    · rw [choose_succ_succ, Nat.add_mul, succ_sub_succ, ← choose_succ_right_eq, ← succ_sub_succ,
        Nat.mul_sub_left_distrib, Nat.add_sub_cancel' (Nat.mul_le_mul_left _ hk)]
    · rw [choose_eq_zero_of_lt hk, choose_eq_zero_of_lt (n.lt_succ_self.trans hk), Nat.zero_mul,
        Nat.zero_mul]

name: ascFactorial_eq_factorial_mul_choose
statement: theorem ascFactorial_eq_factorial_mul_choose (n k : ℕ) : (n + 1).ascFactorial k = k ! * (n + k).choose k
proof:
rw [Nat.mul_comm]
  apply Nat.mul_right_cancel (n + k - k).factorial_pos
  rw [choose_mul_factorial_mul_factorial <| Nat.le_add_left k n, Nat.add_sub_cancel_right,
    ← factorial_mul_ascFactorial, Nat.mul_comm]

name: choose_eq_asc_factorial_div_factorial
statement: theorem choose_eq_asc_factorial_div_factorial (n k : ℕ) : (n + k).choose k = (n + 1).ascFactorial k / k !
proof:
apply Nat.mul_left_cancel k.factorial_pos
  rw [← ascFactorial_eq_factorial_mul_choose]
  exact (Nat.mul_div_cancel' <| factorial_dvd_ascFactorial _ _).symm

name: descFactorial_eq_factorial_mul_choose
statement: theorem descFactorial_eq_factorial_mul_choose (n k : ℕ) : n.descFactorial k = k ! * n.choose k
proof:
obtain h | h := Nat.lt_or_ge n k
  · rw [descFactorial_eq_zero_iff_lt.2 h, choose_eq_zero_of_lt h, Nat.mul_zero]
  rw [Nat.mul_comm]
  apply Nat.mul_right_cancel (n - k).factorial_pos
  rw [choose_mul_factorial_mul_factorial h, ← factorial_mul_descFactorial h, Nat.mul_comm]

name: choose_le_middle
statement: theorem choose_le_middle (r n : ℕ) : choose n r ≤ choose n (n / 2)
proof:
rcases le_or_gt r n with b | b
  · rcases le_or_gt r (n / 2) with a | h
    · apply choose_le_middle_of_le_half_left a
    · rw [← choose_symm b]
      apply choose_le_middle_of_le_half_left
      lia
  · rw [choose_eq_zero_of_lt b]
    apply zero_le

/-! #### Inequalities about increasing the first argument -/

name: choose_le_succ
statement: theorem choose_le_succ (a c : ℕ) : choose a c ≤ choose a.succ c
proof: cases c <;> grind

name: choose_le_add
statement: theorem choose_le_add (a b c : ℕ) : choose a c ≤ choose (a + b) c
proof:
induction b with
  | zero => simp
  | succ b_n b_ih => exact b_ih.trans (choose_le_succ (a + b_n) c)

@[gcongr]

name: multichoose_succ_succ
statement: theorem multichoose_succ_succ (n k : ℕ) : multichoose (n + 1) (k + 1) = multichoose n (k + 1) + multichoose (n + 1) k
proof:
simp [multichoose]

@[simp]

name: multichoose_one
statement: theorem multichoose_one (k : ℕ) : multichoose 1 k = 1
proof:
induction k with
  | zero => simp
  | succ k IH => simp [multichoose_succ_succ 0 k, IH]

@[simp]

name: multichoose_two
statement: theorem multichoose_two (k : ℕ) : multichoose 2 k = k + 1
proof:
induction k with
  | zero => simp
  | succ k IH => rw [multichoose, IH]; simp [Nat.add_comm]

@[simp]

name: multichoose_one_right
statement: theorem multichoose_one_right (n : ℕ) : multichoose n 1 = n
proof:
induction n with
  | zero => simp
  | succ n IH => simp [multichoose_succ_succ n 0, IH]

name: add_choose_eq
statement: theorem add_choose_eq (m n k : ℕ) : (m + n).choose k = ∑ ij ∈ antidiagonal k, m.choose ij.1 * n.choose ij.2
proof:
calc
    (m + n).choose k = ((X + 1) ^ (m + n)).coeff k := by rw [coeff_X_add_one_pow, cast_id]
    _ = ((X + 1) ^ m * (X + 1) ^ n).coeff k := by rw [pow_add]
    _ = ∑ ij ∈ antidiagonal k, m.choose ij.1 * n.choose ij.2 := by
      rw [coeff_mul, Finset.sum_congr rfl]
      simp only [coeff_X_add_one_pow, cast_id, imp_true_iff]

/-- The sum of entries squared in a row of Pascal's triangle -/

name: sum_range_choose_sq
statement: theorem sum_range_choose_sq (n : ℕ) : ∑ i ∈ Finset.range (n + 1), (n.choose i) ^ 2 = (2 * n).choose n
proof:
rw [two_mul, add_choose_eq, sum_antidiagonal_eq_sum_range_succ_mk]
  congr! 1 with _ h
  rw [choose_symm (Finset.mem_range_succ_iff.mp h), sq]

end Nat

name: greatestFib_sub_fib_greatestFib_le_greatestFib
statement: theorem greatestFib_sub_fib_greatestFib_le_greatestFib (hn : n ≠ 0) : greatestFib (n - fib (greatestFib n)) ≤ greatestFib n - 2
proof:
rw [← Nat.lt_succ_iff, greatestFib_lt, tsub_lt_iff_right n.fib_greatestFib_le, Nat.sub_succ,
    succ_pred, ← fib_add_one]
  · exact n.lt_fib_greatestFib_add_one
  · simpa
  · simpa [← succ_le_iff, tsub_eq_zero_iff_le] using hn.bot_lt

private lemma zeckendorf_aux (hm : 0 < m) : m - fib (greatestFib m) < m :=
tsub_lt_self hm <| fib_pos.2 <| findGreatest_pos.2 ⟨1, zero_lt_one, le_add_self, hm⟩

/-- The Zeckendorf representation of a natural number.

Note: For unfolding, you should use the equational lemmas `Nat.zeckendorf_zero` and
`Nat.zeckendorf_of_pos` instead of the autogenerated one. -/

name: sum_zeckendorf_fib
statement: theorem sum_zeckendorf_fib (n : ℕ) : (n.zeckendorf.map fib).sum = n
proof:
induction n using zeckendorf.induct <;> simp_all [fib_greatestFib_le]

/-- **Zeckendorf's Theorem** as an equivalence between natural numbers and Zeckendorf
representations. Every natural number can be written uniquely as a sum of non-consecutive Fibonacci
numbers (if we forget about the first two terms `F₀ = 0`, `F₁ = 1`). -/

name: fib_add_two_strictMono
statement: theorem fib_add_two_strictMono  : StrictMono fun n => fib (n + 2)
proof:
refine strictMono_nat_of_lt_succ fun n => ?_
  rw [add_right_comm]
  exact fib_lt_fib_succ (self_le_add_left _ _)

name: fib_coprime_fib_succ
statement: theorem fib_coprime_fib_succ (n : ℕ) : Nat.Coprime (fib n) (fib (n + 1))
proof:
induction n with
  | zero => simp
  | succ n ih => simp only [fib_add_two, coprime_add_self_right, Coprime, ih.symm]

/-- See https://proofwiki.org/wiki/Fibonacci_Number_in_terms_of_Smaller_Fibonacci_Numbers -/

name: fib_add
statement: theorem fib_add (m n : ℕ) : fib (m + n + 1) = fib m * fib n + fib (m + 1) * fib (n + 1)
proof:
induction n generalizing m with
  | zero => simp
  | succ n ih =>
    specialize ih (m + 1)
    rw [add_assoc m 1 n, add_comm 1 n] at ih
    simp only [fib_add_two, ih]
    ring

name: fib_two_mul
statement: theorem fib_two_mul (n : ℕ) : fib (2 * n) = fib n * (2 * fib (n + 1) - fib n)
proof:
cases n
  · simp
  · rw [two_mul, ← add_assoc, fib_add, fib_add_two, two_mul]
    simp only [← add_assoc, add_tsub_cancel_right]
    ring

name: fib_two_mul_add_one
statement: theorem fib_two_mul_add_one (n : ℕ) : fib (2 * n + 1) = fib (n + 1) ^ 2 + fib n ^ 2
proof:
rw [two_mul, fib_add]
  ring

name: fib_two_mul_add_two
statement: theorem fib_two_mul_add_two (n : ℕ) : fib (2 * n + 2) = fib (n + 1) * (2 * fib n + fib (n + 1))
proof:
rw [fib_add_two, fib_two_mul, fib_two_mul_add_one]
  have : fib n ≤ 2 * fib (n + 1) :=
    le_trans fib_le_fib_succ (mul_comm 2 _ ▸ Nat.le_mul_of_pos_right _ two_pos)
  zify [this]
  ring

/-- Computes `(Nat.fib n, Nat.fib (n + 1))` using the binary representation of `n`.
Supports `Nat.fastFib`. -/

name: fastFibAux_eq
statement: theorem fastFibAux_eq (n : ℕ) : fastFibAux n = (fib n, fib (n + 1))
proof:
refine Nat.binaryRec ?_ ?_ n
  · simp [fastFibAux]
  · rintro (_ | _) n' ih <;>
      simp only [fastFibAux_bit_false, fastFibAux_bit_true, congr_arg Prod.fst ih,
        congr_arg Prod.snd ih, Prod.mk_inj] <;>
      simp [bit, fib_two_mul, fib_two_mul_add_one, fib_two_mul_add_two]

@[deprecated (since := "2026-02-04")] alias fast_fib_aux_eq := fastFibAux_eq

name: gcd_fib_add_self
statement: theorem gcd_fib_add_self (m n : ℕ) : gcd (fib m) (fib (n + m)) = gcd (fib m) (fib n)
proof:
rcases Nat.eq_zero_or_pos n with rfl | h
  · simp
  replace h := Nat.succ_pred_eq_of_pos h; rw [← h, succ_eq_add_one]
  calc
    gcd (fib m) (fib (n.pred + 1 + m)) =
        gcd (fib m) (fib n.pred * fib m + fib (n.pred + 1) * fib (m + 1)) := by
        rw [← fib_add n.pred _]
        ring_nf
    _ = gcd (fib m) (fib (n.pred + 1) * fib (m + 1)) := by
        rw [add_comm, gcd_add_mul_right_right (fib m) _ (fib n.pred)]
    _ = gcd (fib m) (fib (n.pred + 1)) :=
      Coprime.gcd_mul_right_cancel_right (fib (n.pred + 1)) (Coprime.symm (fib_coprime_fib_succ m))

name: fib_gcd
statement: theorem fib_gcd (m n : ℕ) : fib (gcd m n) = gcd (fib m) (fib n)
proof:
induction m, n using Nat.gcd.induction with
  | H0 => simp
  | H1 m n _ h' =>
    rw [← gcd_rec m n] at h'
    conv_rhs => rw [← mod_add_div' n m]
    rwa [gcd_fib_add_mul_self m (n % m) (n / m), gcd_comm (fib m) _]

name: fib_succ_eq_succ_sum
statement: theorem fib_succ_eq_succ_sum (n : ℕ) : fib (n + 1) = (∑ k ∈ Finset.range n, fib k) + 1
proof:
induction n with
  | zero => simp
  | succ n ih =>
    calc
      fib (n + 2) = fib n + fib (n + 1) := fib_add_two
      _ = (fib n + ∑ k ∈ Finset.range n, fib k) + 1 := by rw [ih, add_assoc]
      _ = (∑ k ∈ Finset.range (n + 1), fib k) + 1 := by simp [Finset.range_add_one]

end Nat

name: cast_one
statement: theorem cast_one  : ((1 : ℕ) : R) = 1
proof:
rw [cast_succ, Nat.cast_zero, zero_add]

@[simp, norm_cast]

name: one_add_one_eq_two
statement: theorem one_add_one_eq_two  : 1 + 1 = (2 : R)
proof:
rw [← Nat.cast_one, ← Nat.cast_add]
  apply congrArg
  decide

name: two_add_one_eq_three
statement: theorem two_add_one_eq_three  : 2 + 1 = (3 : R)
proof:
rw [← one_add_one_eq_two, ← Nat.cast_one, ← Nat.cast_add, ← Nat.cast_add]
  apply congrArg
  decide

name: three_add_one_eq_four
statement: theorem three_add_one_eq_four  : 3 + 1 = (4 : R)
proof:
rw [← two_add_one_eq_three, ← one_add_one_eq_two, ← Nat.cast_one,
    ← Nat.cast_add, ← Nat.cast_add, ← Nat.cast_add]
  apply congrArg
  decide

name: two_add_two_eq_four
statement: theorem two_add_two_eq_four  : 2 + 2 = (4 : R)
proof:
simp [← one_add_one_eq_two, ← Nat.cast_one, ← three_add_one_eq_four,
    ← two_add_one_eq_three, add_assoc]

section nsmul

@[simp] lemma nsmul_one {A} [AddMonoidWithOne A] : ∀ n : ℕ, n • (1 : A) = n
  | 0 => by simp [zero_nsmul]
  | n + 1 => by simp [succ_nsmul, nsmul_one n]

end nsmul

name: cast_div_charZero
statement: theorem cast_div_charZero (hnm : n ∣ m) : (↑(m / n) : K) = m / n
proof: obtain rfl | hn := eq_or_ne n 0 <;> simp [*]

name: image_cast_int_Iic
statement: theorem image_cast_int_Iic (a : ℕ) : (↑) '' Iic a = Icc (0 : ℤ) a
proof: rw [← Icc_bot, image_cast_int_Icc]; rfl

name: image_cast_int_Iio
statement: theorem image_cast_int_Iio (a : ℕ) : (↑) '' Iio a = Ico (0 : ℤ) a
proof: rw [← Ico_bot, image_cast_int_Ico]; rfl

name: cast_mul
statement: theorem cast_mul (m n : ℕ) : ((m * n : ℕ) : α) = m * n
proof:
induction n <;> simp [mul_add, *]

variable (α) in
/-- `Nat.cast : ℕ → α` as a `RingHom` -/
@[implicit_reducible]

name: factorizationLCMLeft_zero_left
statement: theorem factorizationLCMLeft_zero_left  : factorizationLCMLeft 0 b = 1
proof:
simp [factorizationLCMLeft]

@[simp] lemma factorizationLCMLeft_zero_right : factorizationLCMLeft a 0 = 1 := by
  simp [factorizationLCMLeft]

@[simp] lemma factorizationLCRight_zero_left : factorizationLCMRight 0 b = 1 := by
  simp [factorizationLCMRight]

@[simp] lemma factorizationLCMRight_zero_right : factorizationLCMRight a 0 = 1 := by
  simp [factorizationLCMRight]

name: factorizationLCMLeft_pos
statement: theorem factorizationLCMLeft_pos  : 0 < factorizationLCMLeft a b
proof:
apply Nat.pos_of_ne_zero
  rw [factorizationLCMLeft, Finsupp.prod_ne_zero_iff]
  intro p _ H
  by_cases h : b.factorization p ≤ a.factorization p
  · simp only [h, reduceIte, pow_eq_zero_iff', ne_eq] at H
    simpa [H.1] using H.2
  · simp only [h, reduceIte, one_ne_zero] at H

name: factorizationLCMRight_pos
statement: theorem factorizationLCMRight_pos  : 0 < factorizationLCMRight a b
proof:
apply Nat.pos_of_ne_zero
  rw [factorizationLCMRight, Finsupp.prod_ne_zero_iff]
  intro p _ H
  by_cases h : b.factorization p ≤ a.factorization p
  · simp only [h, reduceIte, reduceCtorEq] at H
  · simp only [h, ↓reduceIte, pow_eq_zero_iff', ne_eq] at H
    simpa [H.1] using H.2

name: coprime_factorizationLCMLeft_factorizationLCMRight
statement: theorem coprime_factorizationLCMLeft_factorizationLCMRight  : (factorizationLCMLeft a b).Coprime (factorizationLCMRight a b)
proof:
rw [factorizationLCMLeft, factorizationLCMRight]
  refine coprime_prod_left_iff.mpr fun p hp ↦ coprime_prod_right_iff.mpr fun q hq ↦ ?_
  dsimp only; split_ifs with h h'
  any_goals simp only [coprime_one_right_eq_true, coprime_one_left_eq_true]
  refine coprime_pow_primes _ _ (prime_of_mem_primeFactors hp) (prime_of_mem_primeFactors hq) ?_
  contrapose h'; rwa [← h']

variable {a b}

name: factorizationLCMLeft_dvd_left
statement: theorem factorizationLCMLeft_dvd_left  : factorizationLCMLeft a b ∣ a
proof:
rcases eq_or_ne a 0 with rfl | ha
  · simp only [dvd_zero]
  rcases eq_or_ne b 0 with rfl | hb
  · simp [factorizationLCMLeft]
  nth_rewrite 2 [← prod_factorization_pow_eq_self ha]
  rw [prod_of_support_subset (s := (lcm a b).factorization.support)]
  · apply prod_dvd_prod_of_dvd; rintro p -; dsimp only; split_ifs with le
    · rw [factorization_lcm ha hb]; apply pow_dvd_pow; exact sup_le le_rfl le
    · apply one_dvd
  · intro p hp; rw [mem_support_iff] at hp ⊢
    rw [factorization_lcm ha hb]; exact (lt_sup_iff.mpr <| .inl <| Nat.pos_of_ne_zero hp).ne'
  · intros; rw [pow_zero]

name: factorizationLCMRight_dvd_right
statement: theorem factorizationLCMRight_dvd_right  : factorizationLCMRight a b ∣ b
proof:
rcases eq_or_ne a 0 with rfl | ha
  · simp [factorizationLCMRight]
  rcases eq_or_ne b 0 with rfl | hb
  · simp only [dvd_zero]
  nth_rewrite 2 [← prod_factorization_pow_eq_self hb]
  rw [prod_of_support_subset (s := (lcm a b).factorization.support)]
  · apply Finset.prod_dvd_prod_of_dvd; rintro p -; dsimp only; split_ifs with le
    · apply one_dvd
    · rw [factorization_lcm ha hb]; apply pow_dvd_pow; exact sup_le (not_le.1 le).le le_rfl
  · intro p hp; rw [mem_support_iff] at hp ⊢
    rw [factorization_lcm ha hb]; exact (lt_sup_iff.mpr <| .inr <| Nat.pos_of_ne_zero hp).ne'
  · intros; rw [pow_zero]


end Nat

name: factorization_eq_primeFactorsList_multiset
statement: theorem factorization_eq_primeFactorsList_multiset (n : ℕ) : n.factorization = Multiset.toFinsupp (n.primeFactorsList : Multiset ℕ)
proof:
ext p
  simp

name: factorization_eq_zero_iff
statement: theorem factorization_eq_zero_iff (n p : ℕ) : n.factorization p = 0 ↔ ¬p.Prime ∨ ¬p ∣ n ∨ n = 0
proof:
simp_rw [← notMem_support_iff, support_factorization, mem_primeFactors, not_and_or, not_ne_iff]

@[simp]

name: factorization_pow
statement: theorem factorization_pow (n k : ℕ) : factorization (n ^ k) = k • n.factorization
proof:
induction k with
  | zero => simp
  | succ k ih =>
    rcases eq_or_ne n 0 with (rfl | hn)
    · simp
    rw [Nat.pow_succ, mul_comm, factorization_mul hn (pow_ne_zero _ hn), ih,
      add_smul, one_smul, add_comm]

/-! ## Lemmas about factorizations of primes and prime powers -/


/-- The only prime factor of prime `p` is `p` itself, with multiplicity `1` -/
@[simp]
protected theorem Prime.factorization {p : ℕ} (hp : Prime p) : p.factorization = single p 1 := by
  ext q
  rw [← primeFactorsList_count_eq, primeFactorsList_prime hp, single_apply, count_singleton',
    if_congr eq_comm] <;> rfl

/-- For prime `p` the only prime factor of `p^k` is `p` with multiplicity `k` -/

name: prod_pow_factorization_eq_self
statement: theorem prod_pow_factorization_eq_self (hf : ∀ p ∈ f.support, Prime p) : (f.prod (· ^ ·)).factorization = f
proof:
rw [Finsupp.prod, factorization_prod (pow_ne_zero _ <| hf · · |>.ne_zero),
    sum_congr rfl (hf · · |>.factorization_pow)]
  exact sum_single f

name: prod_pow_dvd_of_le_factorization
statement: theorem prod_pow_dvd_of_le_factorization (hf : f ≤ n.factorization) : f.prod (· ^ ·) ∣ n
proof:
rcases eq_or_ne n 0 with (rfl | hn)
  · simp
  rwa [← factorization_le_iff_dvd ?_ hn, factorization_prod_pow_eq_self_of_le_factorization hf]
  refine f.prod_ne_zero_iff.mpr fun _ hp ↦ ?_
  exact pow_ne_zero _ (prime_of_mem_primeFactors <| support_mono hf hp).ne_zero

name: ordProj_dvd
statement: theorem ordProj_dvd (n p : ℕ) : ordProj[p] n ∣ n
proof:
if hp : p.Prime then ?_ else simp [hp]
  rw [← primeFactorsList_count_eq]
  apply dvd_of_primeFactorsList_subperm (pow_ne_zero _ hp.ne_zero)
  rw [hp.primeFactorsList_pow, List.subperm_ext_iff]
  intro q hq
  simp [List.eq_of_mem_replicate hq]

name: floorRoot_def
statement: theorem floorRoot_def  : floorRoot n a = if n = 0 ∨ a = 0 then 0 else (a.factorization ⌊/⌋ n).prod (· ^ ·)
proof:
unfold floorRoot; split_ifs with h <;> simp [Finsupp.floorDiv_def, prod_mapRange_index pow_zero]

@[simp] lemma floorRoot_zero_left (a : ℕ) : floorRoot 0 a = 0 := by simp [floorRoot]
@[simp] lemma floorRoot_zero_right (n : ℕ) : floorRoot n 0 = 0 := by simp [floorRoot]
@[simp] lemma floorRoot_one_left (a : ℕ) : floorRoot 1 a = a := by
  simp [floorRoot]; split_ifs <;> simp [*]
@[simp] lemma floorRoot_one_right (hn : n ≠ 0) : floorRoot n 1 = 1 := by simp [floorRoot, hn]

@[simp] lemma floorRoot_pow_self (hn : n ≠ 0) (a : ℕ) : floorRoot n (a ^ n) = a := by
  simp [floorRoot_def, pos_iff_ne_zero.2, hn]; split_ifs <;> simp [*]

name: floorRoot_ne_zero
statement: theorem floorRoot_ne_zero  : floorRoot n a ≠ 0 ↔ n ≠ 0 ∧ a ≠ 0
proof:
simp +contextual [floorRoot, not_or]

@[simp] lemma floorRoot_eq_zero : floorRoot n a = 0 ↔ n = 0 ∨ a = 0 :=
  floorRoot_ne_zero.not_right.trans <| by simp only [not_and_or, ne_eq, not_not]

@[simp] lemma factorization_floorRoot (n a : ℕ) :
    (floorRoot n a).factorization = a.factorization ⌊/⌋ n := by
  rw [floorRoot_def]
  split_ifs with h
  · obtain rfl | rfl := h <;> simp
  apply a.factorization_prod_pow_eq_self_of_le_factorization
  exact le_self_nsmul (by simp) (by lia) |>.trans <| smul_floorDiv_le (by lia)

/-- Galois connection between `a ↦ a ^ n : ℕ → ℕ` and `floorRoot n : ℕ → ℕ` where `ℕ` is ordered
by divisibility. -/

name: pow_dvd_iff_dvd_floorRoot
statement: theorem pow_dvd_iff_dvd_floorRoot  : a ^ n ∣ b ↔ a ∣ floorRoot n b
proof:
obtain rfl | hn := eq_or_ne n 0
  · simp
  obtain rfl | hb := eq_or_ne b 0
  · simp
  obtain rfl | ha := eq_or_ne a 0
  · simp [hn]
  rw [← factorization_le_iff_dvd (pow_ne_zero _ ha) hb,
    ← factorization_le_iff_dvd ha (floorRoot_ne_zero.2 ⟨hn, hb⟩), factorization_pow,
    factorization_floorRoot, le_floorDiv_iff_smul_le (β := ℕ →₀ ℕ) (pos_iff_ne_zero.2 hn)]

name: ceilRoot_def
statement: theorem ceilRoot_def  : ceilRoot n a = if n = 0 ∨ a = 0 then 0 else (a.factorization ⌈/⌉ n).prod (· ^ ·)
proof:
unfold ceilRoot
  split_ifs with h <;>
    simp [Finsupp.ceilDiv_def, prod_mapRange_index pow_zero, Nat.ceilDiv_eq_add_pred_div]

@[simp] lemma ceilRoot_zero_left (a : ℕ) : ceilRoot 0 a = 0 := by simp [ceilRoot]
@[simp] lemma ceilRoot_zero_right (n : ℕ) : ceilRoot n 0 = 0 := by simp [ceilRoot]
@[simp] lemma ceilRoot_one_left (a : ℕ) : ceilRoot 1 a = a := by
  simp [ceilRoot]; split_ifs <;> simp [*]
@[simp] lemma ceilRoot_one_right (hn : n ≠ 0) : ceilRoot n 1 = 1 := by simp [ceilRoot, hn]

@[simp] lemma ceilRoot_pow_self (hn : n ≠ 0) (a : ℕ) : ceilRoot n (a ^ n) = a := by
  simp [ceilRoot_def, pos_iff_ne_zero.2, hn]; split_ifs <;> simp [*]

name: ceilRoot_ne_zero
statement: theorem ceilRoot_ne_zero  : ceilRoot n a ≠ 0 ↔ n ≠ 0 ∧ a ≠ 0
proof:
simp +contextual [ceilRoot_def, not_or]

@[simp] lemma ceilRoot_eq_zero : ceilRoot n a = 0 ↔ n = 0 ∨ a = 0 :=
  ceilRoot_ne_zero.not_right.trans <| by simp only [not_and_or, ne_eq, not_not]

@[simp] lemma factorization_ceilRoot (n a : ℕ) :
    (ceilRoot n a).factorization = a.factorization ⌈/⌉ n := by
  rw [ceilRoot_def]
  split_ifs with h
  · obtain rfl | rfl := h <;> simp
  refine prod_pow_factorization_eq_self fun p hp ↦ ?_
  have : p.Prime ∧ p ∣ a ∧ ¬a = 0 := by simpa using support_ceilDiv_subset hp
  exact this.1

/-- Galois connection between `ceilRoot n : ℕ → ℕ` and `a ↦ a ^ n : ℕ → ℕ` where `ℕ` is ordered
by divisibility.

Note that this cannot possibly hold for `n = 0`, regardless of the value of `ceilRoot 0 a`, because
the statement reduces to `a = 1 ↔ ceilRoot 0 a ∣ b`, which is false for e.g. `a = 0`,
`b = ceilRoot 0 a`. -/

name: dvd_pow_iff_ceilRoot_dvd
statement: theorem dvd_pow_iff_ceilRoot_dvd (hn : n ≠ 0) : a ∣ b ^ n ↔ ceilRoot n a ∣ b
proof:
obtain rfl | ha := eq_or_ne a 0
  · aesop
  obtain rfl | hb := eq_or_ne b 0
  · simp [hn]
  rw [← factorization_le_iff_dvd ha (pow_ne_zero _ hb),
    ← factorization_le_iff_dvd (ceilRoot_ne_zero.2 ⟨hn, ha⟩) hb, factorization_pow,
    factorization_ceilRoot, ceilDiv_le_iff_le_smul (β := ℕ →₀ ℕ) (pos_iff_ne_zero.2 hn)]

name: Iic_factorization_prod_pow_injective
statement: theorem Iic_factorization_prod_pow_injective (n : ℕ) : (·.val.prod (· ^ ·) : Finset.Iic n.factorization → _).Injective
proof: grind [Function.Injective, factorization_prod_pow_eq_self_of_le_factorization]

name: Iio_factorization_prod_pow_injective
statement: theorem Iio_factorization_prod_pow_injective (n : ℕ) : (·.val.prod (· ^ ·) : Finset.Iio n.factorization → _).Injective
proof: grind [Function.Injective, factorization_prod_pow_eq_self_of_le_factorization]

name: exponent_eq_exponent_mul_factorization_of_prime_pow_eq_base_pow
statement: theorem exponent_eq_exponent_mul_factorization_of_prime_pow_eq_base_pow  : m = n * a.factorization p
proof:
have := congrArg Nat.factorization h
  rw [Nat.Prime.factorization_pow hp, Nat.factorization_pow] at this
  simpa using congr($this p)

name: exists_base_eq_prime_pow_of_prime_pow_eq_base_pow
statement: theorem exists_base_eq_prime_pow_of_prime_pow_eq_base_pow  : ∃ k, a = p ^ k
proof:
rcases exponent_dvd_of_prime_pow_eq_pow hp h with ⟨k, m_eq⟩
  rw [m_eq, pow_mul'] at h
  use k
  exact Nat.pow_left_injective hn h.symm

end PrimePowEqPow

end Nat

name: ordProj_pos
statement: theorem ordProj_pos (n p : ℕ) : 0 < ordProj[p] n
proof: if pp : p.Prime then simp [Nat.pow_pos pp.pos] else simp [pp]

name: ordCompl_mul
statement: theorem ordCompl_mul (a b p : ℕ) : ordCompl[p] (a * b) = ordCompl[p] a * ordCompl[p] b
proof:
if ha : a = 0 then simp [ha] else
  if hb : b = 0 then simp [hb] else
  simp only [ordProj_mul p ha hb]
  rw [div_mul_div_comm (ordProj_dvd a p) (ordProj_dvd b p)]

/-! ### Factorization and divisibility -/

/-- A crude upper bound on `n.factorization p` -/

name: factorization_ordCompl
statement: theorem factorization_ordCompl (n p : ℕ) : (ordCompl[p] n).factorization = n.factorization.erase p
proof:
if hn : n = 0 then simp [hn] else
  if pp : p.Prime then ?_ else
    simp [pp]
  ext q
  rcases eq_or_ne q p with (rfl | hqp)
  · simp only [Finsupp.erase_same, factorization_eq_zero_iff, not_dvd_ordCompl pp hn]
    simp
  · rw [Finsupp.erase_ne hqp, factorization_div (ordProj_dvd n p)]
    simp [pp.factorization, hqp.symm]

name: ordCompl_dvd_ordCompl_iff_dvd
statement: theorem ordCompl_dvd_ordCompl_iff_dvd (a b : ℕ) : (∀ p : ℕ, ordCompl[p] a ∣ ordCompl[p] b) ↔ a ∣ b
proof:
refine ⟨fun h => ?_, fun hab p => ordCompl_dvd_ordCompl_of_dvd hab p⟩
  rcases eq_or_ne b 0 with (rfl | hb0)
  · simp
  if pa : a.Prime then ?_ else simpa [pa] using h a
  if pb : b.Prime then ?_ else simpa [pb] using h b
  rw [prime_dvd_prime_iff_eq pa pb]
  by_contra hab
  apply pa.ne_one
  rw [← Nat.dvd_one, ← Nat.mul_dvd_mul_iff_left hb0.bot_lt, mul_one]
  simpa [Prime.factorization_self pb, Prime.factorization pa, hab] using h b

name: dvd_iff_prime_pow_dvd_dvd
statement: theorem dvd_iff_prime_pow_dvd_dvd (n d : ℕ) : d ∣ n ↔ ∀ p k : ℕ, Prime p → p ^ k ∣ d → p ^ k ∣ n
proof:
rcases eq_or_ne n 0 with (rfl | hn)
  · simp
  rcases eq_or_ne d 0 with (rfl | hd)
  · simp only [zero_dvd_iff, hn, false_iff, not_forall]
    exact ⟨2, n, prime_two, dvd_zero _, mt (le_of_dvd hn.bot_lt) (n.lt_two_pow_self).not_ge⟩
  refine ⟨fun h p k _ hpkd => dvd_trans hpkd h, ?_⟩
  rw [← factorization_prime_le_iff_dvd hd hn]
  intro h p pp
  simp_rw [← pp.pow_dvd_iff_le_factorization hn]
  exact h p _ pp (ordProj_dvd _ _)

name: prod_primeFactors_dvd
statement: theorem prod_primeFactors_dvd (n : ℕ) : ∏ p ∈ n.primeFactors, p ∣ n
proof:
by_cases hn : n = 0
  · subst hn
    simp
  · simpa [prod_primeFactorsList hn] using (n.primeFactorsList : Multiset ℕ).toFinset_prod_dvd_prod

name: prod_pow_primeFactors_factorization
statement: theorem prod_pow_primeFactors_factorization (hn : n ≠ 0) : n = ∏ (p : n.primeFactors), (p : ℕ) ^ (n.factorization p)
proof:
nth_rw 1 [← prod_factorization_pow_eq_self hn]
  rw [prod_factorization_eq_prod_primeFactors _]
  exact prod_subtype n.primeFactors (fun _ ↦ Iff.rfl) fun a ↦ a ^ n.factorization a

name: pairwise_coprime_pow_primeFactors_factorization
statement: theorem pairwise_coprime_pow_primeFactors_factorization  : Pairwise (Function.onFun Nat.Coprime fun (p : n.primeFactors) ↦ p ^ n.factorization p)
proof:
intro p1 p2 hp
  refine Nat.Coprime.pow (n.factorization p1) (n.factorization p2) ?_
  refine (Nat.coprime_primes ?_ ?_).mpr <| Subtype.coe_ne_coe.mpr hp
  · exact Nat.prime_of_mem_primeFactors p1.2
  · exact Nat.prime_of_mem_primeFactors p2.2

/-! ### Lemmas about factorizations of particular functions -/

/-- Exactly `n / p` naturals in `[1, n]` are multiples of `p`.
See `Nat.card_multiples'` for an alternative spelling of the statement. -/

name: card_multiples
statement: theorem card_multiples (n p : ℕ) : #{e ∈ range n | p ∣ e + 1} = n / p
proof:
induction n with
  | zero => simp
  | succ n hn =>
    simp [Nat.succ_div, add_ite, add_zero, Finset.range_add_one, filter_insert, apply_ite card,
      card_insert_of_notMem, hn]

/-- Exactly `n / p` naturals in `(0, n]` are multiples of `p`. -/

name: Ioc_filter_dvd_card_eq_div
statement: theorem Ioc_filter_dvd_card_eq_div (n p : ℕ) : #{x ∈ Ioc 0 n | p ∣ x} = n / p
proof:
induction n <;> simp [Nat.succ_div, add_ite, ← insert_Ioc_right_eq_Ioc_add_one, filter_insert,
    apply_ite card, *]

/-- There are exactly `⌊N/n⌋` positive multiples of `n` that are `≤ N`.
See `Nat.card_multiples` for a "shifted-by-one" version. -/

name: digits_zero
statement: theorem digits_zero (b : ℕ) : digits b 0 = []
proof: rcases b with (_ | ⟨_ | ⟨_⟩⟩) <;> simp [digits, digitsAux0, digitsAux1]

name: digits_add_two_add_one
statement: theorem digits_add_two_add_one (b n : ℕ) : digits (b + 2) (n + 1) = ((n + 1) % (b + 2)) :: digits (b + 2) ((n + 1) / (b + 2))
proof:
simp [digits, digitsAux_def]

@[simp]

name: ofDigits_digits
statement: theorem ofDigits_digits (b n : ℕ) : ofDigits b (digits b n) = n
proof:
rcases b with - | b
  · rcases n with - | n
    · rfl
    · simp
  · rcases b with - | b
    · induction n with
      | zero => rfl
      | succ n ih =>
        rw [Nat.zero_add] at ih ⊢
        simp only [ih, add_comm 1, ofDigits_one_cons, Nat.cast_id, digits_one_succ]
    · induction n using Nat.strongRecOn with | ind n h => ?_
      cases n
      · rw [digits_zero]
        rfl
      · simp only [digits_add_two_add_one]
        dsimp [ofDigits]
        rw [h _ (Nat.div_lt_self' _ b)]
        rw [Nat.mod_add_div]

name: ofDigits_one
statement: theorem ofDigits_one (L : List ℕ) : ofDigits 1 L = L.sum
proof:
induction L with
  | nil => rfl
  | cons _ _ ih => simp [ofDigits, List.sum_cons, ih]

/-!
### Properties

This section contains various lemmas of properties relating to `digits` and `ofDigits`.
-/

name: digit_sum_le
statement: theorem digit_sum_le (p n : ℕ) : List.sum (digits p n) ≤ n
proof:
induction n with
  | zero => exact digits_zero _ ▸ Nat.le_refl (List.sum [])
  | succ n =>
    induction p with
    | zero => rw [digits_zero_succ, List.sum_cons, List.sum_nil, add_zero]
    | succ p =>
      nth_rw 2 [← ofDigits_digits p.succ (n + 1)]
      rw [← ofDigits_one <| digits p.succ n.succ]
      exact ofDigits_monotone (digits p.succ n.succ) <| Nat.succ_pos p

/-- Interpreting as a base `p` number and dividing by `p` is the same as interpreting the tail.
-/

name: repr_length
statement: theorem repr_length (n e : Nat) : 0 < e → n < 10 ^ e → (Nat.repr n).length ≤ e
proof:
simpa [Nat.repr] using toDigits_length _ _ _

end Nat

name: length_digits_le_length_digits_succ
statement: theorem length_digits_le_length_digits_succ (b n : ℕ) : (digits b n).length ≤ (digits b (n + 1)).length
proof:
rcases Decidable.eq_or_ne n 0 with (rfl | hn)
  · simp
  rcases le_or_gt b 1 with hb | hb
  · interval_cases b <;> simp +arith [digits_zero_succ', hn]
  simpa [length_digits, hb, hn] using log_mono_right (le_succ _)

@[deprecated (since := "2026-03-18")]
alias digits_len_le_digits_len_succ := length_digits_le_length_digits_succ

name: digits_two_eq_bits
statement: theorem digits_two_eq_bits (n : ℕ) : digits 2 n = n.bits.map fun b => cond b 1 0
proof:
induction n using Nat.binaryRecFromOne with
  | zero => simp
  | one => simp
  | bit b n h ih =>
    rw [bits_append_bit _ _ fun hn => absurd hn h]
    cases b
    · rw [digits_def' one_lt_two]
      · simpa [Nat.bit]
      · simpa [Nat.bit, pos_iff_ne_zero]
    · simpa [Nat.bit, add_comm, digits_add 2 one_lt_two 1 n, Nat.add_mul_div_left]

/-! ### Modular Arithmetic -/


-- This is really a theorem about polynomials.

name: modEq_eleven_digits_sum
statement: theorem modEq_eleven_digits_sum (n : ℕ) : n ≡ ((digits 10 n).map fun n : ℕ => (n : ℤ)).alternatingSum [ZMOD 11]
proof:
have t := zmodeq_ofDigits_digits 11 10 (-1 : ℤ) (by unfold Int.ModEq; rfl) n
  rwa [ofDigits_neg_one] at t

/-! ## Divisibility  -/

name: eleven_dvd_iff
statement: theorem eleven_dvd_iff  : 11 ∣ n ↔ (11 : ℤ) ∣ ((digits 10 n).map fun n : ℕ => (n : ℤ)).alternatingSum
proof:
have t := dvd_iff_dvd_ofDigits 11 10 (-1 : ℤ) (by simp) n
  rw [ofDigits_neg_one] at t
  exact t

name: cast_descFactorial_two
statement: theorem cast_descFactorial_two  : (a.descFactorial 2 : S) = a * (a - 1)
proof:
rw [descFactorial]
  cases a
  · simp
  · simp [mul_add, add_mul]

end Ring

end Nat

name: natCast_of_isNilpotent_of_coprime
statement: theorem natCast_of_isNilpotent_of_coprime (h : p.Coprime n) : IsUnit (n : A)
proof:
obtain ⟨m, hm⟩ := hp
  suffices ∃ a b : A, p ^ m * a + n * b = 1 by
    obtain ⟨a, b, h⟩ := this
    refine .of_mul_eq_one b ?_
    simpa [hm] using h
  refine ⟨(p ^ m).gcdA n, (p ^ m).gcdB n, ?_⟩
  norm_cast
  rw [← Nat.cast_one, ← Int.cast_natCast 1, ← (h.pow_left m).gcd_eq_one, Nat.gcd_eq_gcd_ab]

name: factorial_lt
statement: theorem factorial_lt (hn : 0 < n) : n ! < m ! ↔ n < m
proof:
refine ⟨fun h => not_le.mp fun hmn => Nat.not_le_of_gt h (factorial_le hmn), fun h => ?_⟩
  have : ∀ {n}, 0 < n → n ! < (n + 1)! := by
    intro k hk
    rw [factorial_succ, succ_mul, Nat.lt_add_left_iff_pos]
    exact Nat.mul_pos hk k.factorial_pos
  induction h generalizing hn with
  | refl => exact this hn
  | step hnk ih => exact lt_trans (ih hn) <| this <| lt_trans hn <| lt_of_succ_le hnk

@[gcongr]

name: factorial_eq_one
statement: theorem factorial_eq_one  : n ! = 1 ↔ n ≤ 1
proof:
constructor
  · intro h
    rw [← not_lt, ← one_lt_factorial, h]
    apply lt_irrefl
  · rintro (_ | _ | _) <;> rfl

name: factorial_inj
statement: theorem factorial_inj (hn : 1 < n) : n ! = m ! ↔ n = m
proof:
refine ⟨fun h => ?_, congr_arg _⟩
  obtain hnm | rfl | hnm := lt_trichotomy n m
  · rw [← factorial_lt <| lt_of_succ_lt hn, h] at hnm
    cases lt_irrefl _ hnm
  · rfl
  rw [← one_lt_factorial, h, one_lt_factorial] at hn
  rw [← factorial_lt <| lt_of_succ_lt hn, h] at hnm
  cases lt_irrefl _ hnm

name: ascFactorial_mul_ascFactorial
statement: theorem ascFactorial_mul_ascFactorial (n l k : ℕ) : n.ascFactorial l * (n + l).ascFactorial k = n.ascFactorial (l + k)
proof:
cases n with
  | zero =>
    cases l
    · simp only [ascFactorial_zero, Nat.add_zero, Nat.one_mul, Nat.zero_add]
    · simp only [Nat.add_right_comm, zero_ascFactorial, Nat.zero_add, Nat.zero_mul]
  | succ n' =>
    apply Nat.mul_left_cancel (factorial_pos n')
    simp only [Nat.add_assoc, ← Nat.mul_assoc, factorial_mul_ascFactorial]
    rw [Nat.add_comm 1 l, ← Nat.add_assoc, factorial_mul_ascFactorial, Nat.add_assoc]

/-- Avoid in favor of `Nat.factorial_mul_ascFactorial` if you can. ℕ-division isn't worth it. -/

name: zero_descFactorial_succ
statement: theorem zero_descFactorial_succ (k : ℕ) : (0 : ℕ).descFactorial (k + 1) = 0
proof: rw [descFactorial_succ, Nat.zero_sub, Nat.zero_mul]

name: factorial_two_mul_le
statement: theorem factorial_two_mul_le (n : ℕ) : (2 * n)! ≤ (2 * n) ^ n * n !
proof:
rw [Nat.two_mul, ← factorial_mul_ascFactorial, Nat.mul_comm]
  exact Nat.mul_le_mul_right _ (ascFactorial_le_pow_add _ _)

name: two_pow_mul_factorial_le_factorial_two_mul
statement: theorem two_pow_mul_factorial_le_factorial_two_mul (n : ℕ) : 2 ^ n * n ! ≤ (2 * n)!
proof:
obtain _ | n := n
  · simp
  rw [Nat.mul_comm, Nat.two_mul]
  calc
    _ ≤ (n + 1)! * (n + 2) ^ (n + 1) :=
      Nat.mul_le_mul_left _ (Nat.pow_le_pow_left (le_add_left _ _) _)
    _ ≤ _ := Nat.factorial_mul_pow_le_factorial


/-!
### Factorial via binary splitting.

We prove this is equal to the standard factorial and mark it `@[csimp]`.

We could proceed further, with either Legendre or Luschny methods.
-/

/-!
This is the highest factorial I can `#eval` using the naive implementation without a stack overflow:
```
/-- info: 114716 -/
#guard_msgs in
#eval 9718 ! |>.log2
```

Similarly, evaluation of `ascFactorial 100 15000` fails with the naive implementation
but works with the binary recursion.

We could implement a tail-recursive version (or just use `Nat.fold`),
but instead let's jump straight to binary splitting.
-/

/-- `ascFactorial` implemented using binary splitting.

While this still performs the same number of multiplications,
the big-integer operands to each are much smaller. -/

name: ascFactorial_eq_ascFactorialBinary
statement: theorem ascFactorial_eq_ascFactorialBinary  : ascFactorial = ascFactorialBinary
proof:
ext n k
  fun_induction ascFactorialBinary with
  | case1 => simp
  | case2 => simp [ascFactorial]
  | case3 n k ih₁ ih₂ => grind [ascFactorial_mul_ascFactorial]

/-- Factorial implemented using binary splitting.

While this still performs the same number of multiplications,
the big-integer operands to each are much smaller. -/

name: factorial_eq_factorialBinarySplitting
statement: theorem factorial_eq_factorialBinarySplitting  : @factorial = @factorialBinarySplitting
proof:
ext n
  simp [factorialBinarySplitting, ← ascFactorial_eq_ascFactorialBinary]

/-- `descFactorial` implemented using binary splitting. -/

name: descFactorial_eq_descFactorialBinary
statement: theorem descFactorial_eq_descFactorialBinary  : descFactorial = descFactorialBinary
proof:
ext n k
  rw [descFactorialBinary]
  split_ifs with h
  · rw [descFactorial_of_lt h]
  · rw [← ascFactorial_eq_ascFactorialBinary, ← add_descFactorial_eq_ascFactorial']
    grind

/-!
We are now limited by time, not stack space,
and this is much faster than even the tail-recursive version.

```
#time -- Less than 1s. (Tail-recursive version takes longer for `(10^5) !`.)
#eval (10^6) ! |>.log2
```
-/


end Nat

name: prod_factorial_dvd_factorial_sum
statement: theorem prod_factorial_dvd_factorial_sum  : (∏ i ∈ s, (f i)!) ∣ (∑ i ∈ s, f i)!
proof:
induction s using Finset.cons_induction_on with
  | empty => simp
  | cons a s has ih =>
    rw [prod_cons, Finset.sum_cons]
    exact (mul_dvd_mul_left _ ih).trans (Nat.factorial_mul_factorial_dvd_factorial_add _ _)

name: neg_cast_eq_cast
statement: theorem neg_cast_eq_cast  : (-m : R) = n ↔ m = 0 ∧ n = 0
proof:
simp [neg_eq_iff_add_eq_zero, ← cast_add]

@[simp, norm_cast] lemma cast_eq_neg_cast : (m : R) = -n ↔ m = 0 ∧ n = 0 := by
  simp [eq_neg_iff_add_eq_zero, ← cast_add]

end PartialOrderedRing

end Nat

name: cast_add_one_pos
statement: theorem cast_add_one_pos (n : ℕ) : 0 < (n : α) + 1
proof:
apply zero_lt_one.trans_le
  convert (@mono_cast α _).imp (?_ : 1 ≤ n + 1)
  <;> simp

/-- See also `Nat.cast_pos`, specialised for an `OrderedSemiring`. -/
@[simp low]

name: cast_lt_one
statement: theorem cast_lt_one  : (n : α) < 1 ↔ n = 0
proof:
rw [← cast_one, cast_lt, Nat.lt_succ_iff, le_zero]

@[simp, norm_cast]

name: Ico_filter_dvd_card
statement: theorem Ico_filter_dvd_card  : #{x ∈ Ico a b | r ∣ x} = max (⌈b / (r : ℚ)⌉ - ⌈a / (r : ℚ)⌉) 0
proof:
rw [Ico_filter_dvd_eq _ _ hr, card_map, card_Ico, toNat_eq_max]

/-- There are `⌊b / r⌋ - ⌊a / r⌋` multiples of `r` in `(a, b]`, if `a ≤ b`. -/

name: Ioc_filter_dvd_card
statement: theorem Ioc_filter_dvd_card  : #{x ∈ Ioc a b | r ∣ x} = max (⌊b / (r : ℚ)⌋ - ⌊a / (r : ℚ)⌋) 0
proof:
rw [Ioc_filter_dvd_eq _ _ hr, card_map, card_Ioc, toNat_eq_max]

/-- There are `⌈(b - v) / r⌉ - ⌈(a - v) / r⌉` numbers congruent to `v` mod `r` in `[a, b)`,
if `a ≤ b`. -/

name: Ico_filter_modEq_card
statement: theorem Ico_filter_modEq_card (v : ℤ) : #{x ∈ Ico a b | x ≡ v [ZMOD r]} = max (⌈(b - v) / (r : ℚ)⌉ - ⌈(a - v) / (r : ℚ)⌉) 0
proof:
simp [Ico_filter_modEq_eq, Ico_filter_dvd_eq, hr]

/-- There are `⌊(b - v) / r⌋ - ⌊(a - v) / r⌋` numbers congruent to `v` mod `r` in `(a, b]`,
if `a ≤ b`. -/

name: Ioc_filter_modEq_card
statement: theorem Ioc_filter_modEq_card (v : ℤ) : #{x ∈ Ioc a b | x ≡ v [ZMOD r]} = max (⌊(b - v) / (r : ℚ)⌋ - ⌊(a - v) / (r : ℚ)⌋) 0
proof:
simp [Ioc_filter_modEq_eq, Ioc_filter_dvd_eq, hr]

end Int

namespace Nat

variable (a b : ℕ) {r : ℕ}

name: Ico_filter_modEq_card
statement: theorem Ico_filter_modEq_card (v : ℕ) : #{x ∈ Ico a b | x ≡ v [MOD r]} = max (⌈(b - v) / (r : ℚ)⌉ - ⌈(a - v) / (r : ℚ)⌉) 0
proof:
simp_rw [← Ico_filter_modEq_cast _ _ ▸ card_map _,
    Int.Ico_filter_modEq_card _ _ (cast_lt.mpr hr), Int.cast_natCast]

/-- There are `⌊(b - v) / r⌋ - ⌊(a - v) / r⌋` numbers congruent to `v` mod `r` in `(a, b]`,
if `a ≤ b`. `Nat` version of `Int.Ioc_filter_modEq_card`. -/

name: Ioc_filter_modEq_card
statement: theorem Ioc_filter_modEq_card (v : ℕ) : #{x ∈ Ioc a b | x ≡ v [MOD r]} = max (⌊(b - v) / (r : ℚ)⌋ - ⌊(a - v) / (r : ℚ)⌋) 0
proof:
simp_rw [← Ioc_filter_modEq_cast _ _ ▸ card_map _,
    Int.Ioc_filter_modEq_card _ _ (cast_lt.mpr hr), Int.cast_natCast]

/-- There are `⌈(b - v % r) / r⌉` numbers in `[0, b)` congruent to `v` mod `r`. -/

name: count_modEq_card_eq_ceil
statement: theorem count_modEq_card_eq_ceil (v : ℕ) : b.count (· ≡ v [MOD r]) = ⌈(b - (v % r : ℕ)) / (r : ℚ)⌉
proof:
have hr' : 0 < (r : ℚ) := by positivity
  rw [count_eq_card_filter_range, ← Ico_zero_eq_range, Ico_filter_modEq_card _ _ hr,
    max_eq_left (sub_nonneg.mpr <| by gcongr; positivity)]
  conv_lhs =>
    rw [← div_add_mod v r, cast_add, cast_mul, add_comm]
    tactic => simp_rw [← sub_sub, sub_div (_ - _), mul_div_cancel_left₀ _ hr'.ne',
      Int.ceil_sub_natCast]
    rw [sub_sub_sub_cancel_right, cast_zero, zero_sub]
  rw [sub_eq_self, ceil_eq_zero_iff, Set.mem_Ioc, div_le_iff₀ hr', lt_div_iff₀ hr', neg_one_mul,
    zero_mul, neg_lt_neg_iff, cast_lt]
  exact ⟨mod_lt _ hr, by simp⟩

/-- There are `b / r + [v % r < b % r]` numbers in `[0, b)` congruent to `v` mod `r`,
where `[·]` is the Iverson bracket. -/

name: count_modEq_card
statement: theorem count_modEq_card (v : ℕ) : b.count (· ≡ v [MOD r]) = b / r + if v % r < b % r then 1 else 0
proof:
have hr' : 0 < (r : ℚ) := by positivity
  rw [← ofNat_inj, count_modEq_card_eq_ceil _ hr, cast_add]
  conv_lhs => rw [← div_add_mod b r, cast_add, cast_mul, ← add_sub, _root_.add_div,
    mul_div_cancel_left₀ _ hr'.ne', add_comm, Int.ceil_add_natCast, add_comm]
  rw [add_right_inj]
  split_ifs with h
  · rw [← cast_sub h.le, Int.ceil_eq_iff, div_le_iff₀ hr', lt_div_iff₀ hr', cast_one, Int.cast_one,
      sub_self, zero_mul, cast_pos, tsub_pos_iff_lt, one_mul, cast_le, tsub_le_iff_right]
    exact ⟨h, ((mod_lt _ hr).trans_le (by simp)).le⟩
  · rw [cast_zero, ceil_eq_zero_iff, Set.mem_Ioc, div_le_iff₀ hr', lt_div_iff₀ hr', zero_mul,
      tsub_nonpos, ← neg_eq_neg_one_mul, neg_lt_sub_iff_lt_add, ← cast_add, cast_lt, cast_le]
    exact ⟨(mod_lt _ hr).trans_le (by simp), not_lt.mp h⟩

end Nat

name: card_Icc_of_le
statement: theorem card_Icc_of_le (h : a ≤ b + 1) : (#(Icc a b) : ℤ) = b + 1 - a
proof: rw [card_Icc, toNat_sub_of_le h]

name: card_Ico_of_le
statement: theorem card_Ico_of_le (h : a ≤ b) : (#(Ico a b) : ℤ) = b - a
proof: rw [card_Ico, toNat_sub_of_le h]

name: card_Ioc_of_le
statement: theorem card_Ioc_of_le (h : a ≤ b) : (#(Ioc a b) : ℤ) = b - a
proof: rw [card_Ioc, toNat_sub_of_le h]

name: card_Ioo_of_lt
statement: theorem card_Ioo_of_lt (h : a < b) : (#(Ioo a b) : ℤ) = b - a - 1
proof: rw [card_Ioo, sub_sub, toNat_sub_of_le h]

name: Icc_eq_pair
statement: theorem Icc_eq_pair  : Finset.Icc a (a + 1) = {a, a + 1}
proof:
ext
  simp
  omega

name: card_fintype_Icc_of_le
statement: theorem card_fintype_Icc_of_le (h : a ≤ b + 1) : (Fintype.card (Set.Icc a b) : ℤ) = b + 1 - a
proof: simp [h]

name: card_fintype_Ico_of_le
statement: theorem card_fintype_Ico_of_le (h : a ≤ b) : (Fintype.card (Set.Ico a b) : ℤ) = b - a
proof: simp [h]

name: card_fintype_Ioc_of_le
statement: theorem card_fintype_Ioc_of_le (h : a ≤ b) : (Fintype.card (Set.Ioc a b) : ℤ) = b - a
proof: simp [h]

name: card_fintype_Ioo_of_lt
statement: theorem card_fintype_Ioo_of_lt (h : a < b) : (Fintype.card (Set.Ioo a b) : ℤ) = b - a - 1
proof: simp [h]

name: le_natCast_sub
statement: theorem le_natCast_sub (m n : ℕ) : (m - n : ℤ) ≤ ↑(m - n : ℕ)
proof:
lia

/-! ### `succ` and `pred` -/

name: div2_bit
statement: theorem div2_bit (b n) : div2 (bit b n) = n
proof:
rw [bit_val, div2_val, add_comm, Int.add_mul_ediv_left, (_ : (_ / 2 : ℤ) = 0), zero_add]
  cases b
  · decide
  · change ofNat _ = _
    rw [Nat.div_eq_of_lt] <;> simp
  · decide

/-- Like `Int.ediv_emod_unique`, but permitting negative `b`. -/

name: bodd_subNatNat
statement: theorem bodd_subNatNat (m n : ℕ) : bodd (subNatNat m n) = xor m.bodd n.bodd
proof:
apply subNatNat_elim m n fun m n i => bodd i = xor m.bodd n.bodd <;>
  intro i j <;>
  simp only [Int.bodd, Nat.bodd_add] <;>
  cases Nat.bodd i <;> simp

@[simp]

name: bodd_negOfNat
statement: theorem bodd_negOfNat (n : ℕ) : bodd (negOfNat n) = n.bodd
proof:
cases n <;> simp +decide
  rfl

@[simp]

name: bodd_neg
statement: theorem bodd_neg (n : ℤ) : bodd (-n) = bodd n
proof:
cases n <;> simp only [← negOfNat_eq, bodd_negOfNat, neg_negSucc] <;> simp [bodd]

@[simp]

name: bodd_add
statement: theorem bodd_add (m n : ℤ) : bodd (m + n) = xor (bodd m) (bodd n)
proof:
rcases m with m | m <;>
  rcases n with n | n <;>
  simp only [ofNat_eq_natCast, ofNat_add_negSucc, negSucc_add_ofNat,
             negSucc_add_negSucc, bodd_subNatNat, ← Nat.cast_add] <;>
  simp [bodd, Bool.xor_comm]

@[simp]

name: bodd_mul
statement: theorem bodd_mul (m n : ℤ) : bodd (m * n) = (bodd m && bodd n)
proof:
rcases m with m | m <;> rcases n with n | n <;>
  simp only [ofNat_eq_natCast, ofNat_mul_negSucc, negSucc_mul_ofNat, ofNat_mul_ofNat,
             negSucc_mul_negSucc] <;>
  simp only [negSucc_eq, ← Int.natCast_succ, bodd_neg, bodd_coe, Nat.bodd_mul]

name: bit_val
statement: theorem bit_val (b n) : bit b n = 2 * n + cond b 1 0
proof:
cases b
  · apply (add_zero _).symm
  · rfl

name: bodd_bit
statement: theorem bodd_bit (b n) : bodd (bit b n) = b
proof:
rw [bit_val]
  cases b <;> cases bodd n <;> simp [(show bodd 2 = false by rfl)]

@[simp]

name: bitwise_or
statement: theorem bitwise_or  : bitwise or = lor
proof:
funext m n
  rcases m with m | m <;> rcases n with n | n <;> try {rfl}
    <;> simp only [bitwise, natBitwise, Bool.not_false, Bool.or_true, cond_true, lor, Nat.ldiff,
      negSucc.injEq, Bool.true_or]
  · rw [Nat.bitwise_swap, Function.swap]
    congr
    funext x y
    cases x <;> cases y <;> rfl
  · simp
  · congr
    simp

-- Porting note: Was `bitwise_tac` in mathlib

name: bitwise_and
statement: theorem bitwise_and  : bitwise and = land
proof:
funext m n
  rcases m with m | m <;> rcases n with n | n <;> try {rfl}
    <;> simp only [bitwise, natBitwise, Bool.not_false,
      cond_false, cond_true, Bool.and_true,
      Bool.and_false]
  · rw [Nat.bitwise_swap, Function.swap]
    congr
    funext x y
    cases x <;> cases y <;> rfl
  · congr
    simp

-- Porting note: Was `bitwise_tac` in mathlib

name: bitwise_diff
statement: theorem bitwise_diff  : (bitwise fun a b => a && not b) = ldiff
proof:
funext m n
  rcases m with m | m <;> rcases n with n | n <;> try {rfl}
    <;> simp only [bitwise, natBitwise, Bool.not_false,
      cond_false, cond_true, Nat.ldiff, Bool.and_true, negSucc.injEq,
      Bool.and_false, Bool.not_true, ldiff]
  · congr
    simp
  · congr
    simp
  · rw [Nat.bitwise_swap, Function.swap]
    congr
    funext x y
    cases x <;> cases y <;> rfl

-- Porting note: Was `bitwise_tac` in mathlib

name: bitwise_xor
statement: theorem bitwise_xor  : bitwise xor = Int.xor
proof:
funext m n
  rcases m with m | m <;> rcases n with n | n <;> try {rfl}
    <;> simp only [bitwise, natBitwise, Bool.not_false, Bool.bne_eq_xor,
      cond_false, cond_true, negSucc.injEq, Bool.false_xor,
      Bool.true_xor, Bool.not_true,
      Int.xor, HXor.hXor, XorOp.xor, Nat.xor] <;> simp

@[simp]

name: lor_bit
statement: theorem lor_bit (a m b n) : lor (bit a m) (bit b n) = bit (a || b) (lor m n)
proof:
rw [← bitwise_or, bitwise_bit]

@[simp]

name: land_bit
statement: theorem land_bit (a m b n) : land (bit a m) (bit b n) = bit (a && b) (land m n)
proof:
rw [← bitwise_and, bitwise_bit]

@[simp]

name: ldiff_bit
statement: theorem ldiff_bit (a m b n) : ldiff (bit a m) (bit b n) = bit (a && not b) (ldiff m n)
proof:
rw [← bitwise_diff, bitwise_bit]

@[simp]

name: lxor_bit
statement: theorem lxor_bit (a m b n) : Int.xor (bit a m) (bit b n) = bit (xor a b) (Int.xor m n)
proof:
rw [← bitwise_xor, bitwise_bit]

@[simp]

name: testBit_lor
statement: theorem testBit_lor (m n k) : testBit (lor m n) k = (testBit m k || testBit n k)
proof:
rw [← bitwise_or, testBit_bitwise]

/-! ### toNat -/

/-
The following lemma is non-confluent with
```
simp only [*, @Int.lt_toNat, CharP.cast_eq_zero, @Nat.cast_pred, Int.ofNat_toNat]
```
from the default simp set, which simplifies the LHS to `max i 0 - 1`.
Therefore we mark this lemma as `@[simp high]`.
-/
@[simp high]

name: xgcdAux_val
statement: theorem xgcdAux_val (x y) : xgcdAux x 1 0 y 0 1 = (gcd x y, xgcd x y)
proof: rw [xgcd, ← xgcdAux_fst x y 1 0 0 1]

name: xgcd_val
statement: theorem xgcd_val (x y) : xgcd x y = (gcdA x y, gcdB x y)
proof:
unfold gcdA gcdB; constructor

section

variable (x y : ℕ)

private def P : ℕ × ℤ × ℤ → Prop
  | (r, s, t) => (r : ℤ) = x * s + y * t

private theorem xgcdAux_P {r r'} :
    ∀ {s t s' t'}, P x y (r, s, t) → P x y (r', s', t') → P x y (xgcdAux r s t r' s' t') := by
  induction r, r' using gcd.induction with
  | H0 => simp
  | H1 a b h IH =>
    intro s t s' t' p p'
    rw [xgcdAux_rec h]; refine IH ?_ p; dsimp [P] at *
    rw [Int.emod_def]; generalize (b / a : ℤ) = k
    rw [p, p', Int.mul_sub, sub_add_eq_add_sub, Int.mul_sub, Int.add_mul, mul_comm k t,
      mul_comm k s, ← mul_assoc, ← mul_assoc, add_comm (x * s * k), ← add_sub_assoc, sub_sub]

/-- **Bézout's lemma**: given `x y : ℕ`, `gcd x y = x * a + y * b`, where `a = gcd_a x y` and
`b = gcd_b x y` are computed by the extended Euclidean algorithm.
-/

name: gcd_eq_gcd_ab
statement: theorem gcd_eq_gcd_ab  : (gcd x y : ℤ) = x * gcdA x y + y * gcdB x y
proof:
have := @xgcdAux_P x y x y 1 0 0 1 (by simp [P]) (by simp [P])
  rwa [xgcdAux_val, xgcd_val] at this

end

name: pow_eq_one_iff_of_coprime
statement: theorem pow_eq_one_iff_of_coprime (hmn : m.Coprime n) : a ^ m = 1 ∧ a ^ n = 1 ↔ a = 1

simp [← pow_gcd_eq_one, hmn]

variable {α : Type*}

section GroupWithZero
variable [GroupWithZero α] {a b : α} {m n : ℕ}

protected lemma Commute.pow_eq_pow_iff_of_coprime (hab : Commute a b) (hmn : m.Coprime n) :
    a ^ m = b ^ n ↔ ∃ c, a = c ^ n ∧ b = c ^ m := by
  refine ⟨fun h ↦ ?_, by rintro ⟨c, rfl, rfl⟩; rw [← pow_mul, ← pow_mul']⟩
  by_cases m = 0; · simp_all
  by_cases n = 0; · simp_all
  by_cases hb : b = 0; · exact ⟨0, by simp_all⟩
  by_cases ha : a = 0; · exact ⟨0, by have := h.symm; simp_all⟩
  refine ⟨a ^ Nat.gcdB m n * b ^ Nat.gcdA m n, ?_, ?_⟩ <;>
  · refine (pow_one _).symm.trans ?_
    conv_lhs => rw [← zpow_natCast, ← hmn, Nat.gcd_eq_gcd_ab]
    simp only [zpow_add₀ ha, zpow_add₀ hb, ← zpow_natCast, (hab.zpow_zpow₀ _ _).mul_zpow,
      ← zpow_mul, mul_comm (Nat.gcdB m n), mul_comm (Nat.gcdA m n)]
    simp only [zpow_mul, zpow_natCast, h]
    exact ((Commute.pow_pow (by aesop) _ _).zpow_zpow₀ _ _).symm

end GroupWithZero

section CommGroupWithZero
variable [CommGroupWithZero α] {a b : α} {m n : ℕ}

name: strongRec_of_ge
statement: theorem strongRec_of_ge  : ∀ hn : m ≤ n, m.strongRec lt ge n = ge n hn fun k _ ↦ m.strongRec lt ge k
proof:
refine m.strongRec (fun n hnm hmn ↦ (Int.not_lt.mpr hmn hnm).elim) (fun n _ ih hn ↦ ?_) n
  rw [Int.strongRec, dif_neg (Int.not_lt.mpr hn)]
  congr; revert ih
  refine n.inductionOn' m (fun _ ↦ ?_) (fun k hmk ih' ih ↦ ?_) (fun k hkm ih' _ ↦ ?_) <;> ext l hl
  · rw [inductionOn'_self, strongRec_of_lt hl]
  · rw [inductionOn'_add_one hmk]; split_ifs with hlm
    · rw [strongRec_of_lt hlm]
    · rw [ih' fun l hl ↦ ih l (Int.lt_trans hl k.lt_succ), ih _ hl]
  · rw [inductionOn'_sub_one hkm, ih']
    exact fun l hlk hml ↦ (Int.not_lt.mpr hkm <| Int.lt_of_le_of_lt hml hlk).elim

end strongRec
